name: 1-2-3 the First
description: The eldest midwayer of the primary order on Urantia who stood loyal to Gabriel during the Caligastia rebellion and led efforts to reduce rebel casualties among his order. He currently serves on Jerusem as a member of the twenty-four counselors and has previously functioned as governor general of Urantia.

A fundamental number in the domain of life, representing one of three primal life plans after the order of the three Paradise Sources and Centers. In Nebadon, these three basic forms of life are segregated on three different types of planets.

A number that is basic to the central universe and the spiritual system of inherent transmissions of character. The number seven reflects the sevenfold associative possibilities of the three persons of Deity and is fundamental to universe organization, appearing in the chemical domains, life design patterns, and throughout the structure of reality.
The decimal system that is inherent in energy, matter, and the physical universe but not in the spiritual realm.

A fundamental number in the domain of life, with twelve distinct and divine concepts of transmissible life originally existing. This number, with its subdivisions and multiples, runs throughout all basic life patterns of the seven superuniverses.
A number that has been regarded as evil or unlucky since antiquity, contrasting with the lucky numbers three and seven that came from later revelations.

name: 606 of Satania
description: The numerical designation of Urantia in the planetary system of Satania, being the 606th inhabited world in this local system. Satania currently contains 619 inhabited worlds with more than two hundred additional planets evolving toward habitation.

name: A-B-C the First
description: The designation for the first secondary midwayer of the first subgroup among eight subgroups of Adamic midwayers. Each subgroup consists of 248 members who are children of the same parents.

name: a priori assumptions
description: The three fundamental cosmic mind insights of causation, duty, and worship that constitute the basic assumptions enabling humans to function as rational and self-conscious personalities in the realms of science, philosophy, and religion.

name: Aaron (Old Testament)
description: The older brother of Moses who was involved in the golden calf incident, where the Israelites created an idol to represent Yahweh. Peter preached about Aaron and the Golden Calf during his early ministry in Rimmon.

name: Aaron the stone mason
aliases: a man with a withered hand
description: Aaron was a stone mason healed by Jesus of his withered hand, who later told a paralytic about Jesus' healing powers, leading to the dramatic roof incident where the paralytic was lowered through the ceiling to reach Jesus.

name: Abaddon
description: Abaddon was the chief of staff of Caligastia who followed his master into rebellion and has served as chief executive of the Urantia rebels ever since.

name: abandonters
description: Abandonters are the permanent citizens of Uversa headquarters spheres, created by unrevealed agents of the Ancients of Days and the seven Reflective Spirits. They are highly skilled artistically and socially adapted beings who administer routine affairs under the supervision of Son-fused mortals.

name: Abel
description: Abel was the first child of Adam and Eve born in the second garden. He chose to become a herder and was slain by his older brother Cain after taunting him about their different parentage and the preference shown for Abel's animal sacrifices.

name: Abila
description: Abila was one of the cities in the Decapolis where Jesus and his apostles preached during multiple evangelistic tours, including the first labor of the twelve and both preaching tours through the region.

name: ability
description: Ability is an inherent gift or capacity that cannot be exceeded, distinguished from skill which is acquired through practice. The three sources of special human ability are natural aptitude, Thought Adjuster guidance, and assistance from spirit artisans.

name: Abimelech
description: Abimelech was a leader whose people Abraham converted to his teachings, though they retained many superstitions including the practice of sacrificing first-born sons.

name: Abiram
description: Abiram was the first-born son of a petty king in Palestine who was sacrificed alive in the foundation of Jericho's walls, along with his younger brother Segub who was placed in the gate foundations.

name: Abner
description: Abner was the chief of John the Baptist's loyal supporters and former head of the Engedi Nazarite colony. After John's death, he became head of seventy gospel messengers and later led the Philadelphia church, remaining faithful to Jesus' original teachings throughout his 89-year life.

name: abodes
description: Abodes refer to the many dwelling places or stopping-places in the Father's universe that Jesus promised his followers, including other inhabited planets and architectural worlds where ascending mortals continue their eternal progression toward Paradise.

name: aborigines (Australian)
description: Indigenous peoples of Australia who remain at primitive stages of human development. They have made limited progress since ancient hunting days, perpetuating fear of ghosts, ancestor veneration, and clan-centered religion. These Aboriginal Australians represent remains of ancient antisocial peoples who never developed tribal government or used slaves.

name: aborigines (Indian)
description: Indigenous peoples of India who are not representative of the primitive peoples who originally populated India, but rather form the most inferior southern and eastern fringe that was never fully absorbed by either the early Andites or their later appearing Aryan cousins.

name: abortion
description: A practice that became very common after the establishment of taboos on childbirth among the unmarried. Many races learned techniques of abortion, and among primitive clans, this practice along with infanticide threatened group survival, though maternal affection typically prevented destruction of children after they had been suckled.

name: Abraham (Old Testament)
description: The Hebrew patriarch originally named Abram who changed his name to Abraham when his covenant with Melchizedek took on a formal aspect. He was born at Ur but left due to sun worship, became a chosen individual responsible for keeping alive the truth of one God, and was an ancestor of Joseph, the father of Jesus. Abraham was a wealthy and efficient businessman who attended the Salem school and became one of Melchizedek's most brilliant pupils.

name: Abraham the Pharisee
description: A young and influential member of the Sanhedrin who publicly espoused the teachings of Jesus and was baptized in the pool of Siloam by Abner. His conversion caused great excitement in Jerusalem and he gave all his worldly goods to the apostolic treasury, which enabled the immediate sending forth of newly trained evangelists.

name: Abram
description: The original name of the Hebrew patriarch who later changed his name to Abraham when his covenant with Melchizedek took on a formal aspect.

name: Absalom
description: One of the sons of King David whose mother was Canaanite. He was an aspirant to the throne that Solomon finally occupied and would today be called a demagogue. Absalom was among the half dozen contenders for David's throne.

name: absent landlord (parable)
description: One of the parables told by Jesus to the chief Pharisees, scribes, and listening multitude, following the story of the two sons. This parable appears in the Gospel accounts of Mark, Matthew, and Luke.

name: absoluta
description: The term used on Uversa for space potency, which is the unquestioned free space presence of the Unqualified Absolute. This represents the universe force-space potential that is prereality and responsive only to the personal grasp of the Universal Father, being the domain from which all cosmic force and energy manifestations originate.

name: Absolute
description: A concept that evolved in Buddhist philosophy as the Absolute Buddha or Eternal One of infinite existence, representing the highest Buddhist approximation of divine reality. In universe philosophy, the Absolute is understood as the impersonal and coordinate reaction of the Trinity to all basic space situations, though in infinity there is ultimately one Absolute while on subinfinite levels there are three Absolutes.

name: Absolute Buddha
description: A Buddhist concept referring to the ultimate, unconditioned state of enlightenment or awakening beyond all relative existence.

name: Absolute Deity
description: The absolute aspect of Deity, representing the unlimited and unconditioned nature of divine reality.

name: absolute level
description: The highest level of reality characterized by unlimited, unconditioned existence beyond time and space.

name: absolute mind (mind)
description: The unconditioned, unlimited aspect of mind that transcends all finite and experiential forms of consciousness.

name: Absolute Mind (person)
description: The Infinite Spirit as the personification of absolute mind, representing the unlimited mental aspect of deity.

name: Absolute Person
description: The Eternal Son as the absolute expression of divine personality, representing perfect and unlimited personal reality.

name: Absolutes
description: The seven eternal, coordinate realities that constitute the foundation of all existence: the Universal Father, Eternal Son, Infinite Spirit, Isle of Paradise, Deity Absolute, Universal Absolute, and Unqualified Absolute. These seven Absolutes are unqualifiedly eternal and represent the premise of all reality, functioning as the basic foundations for all universe reality.

name: absolutism
description: The state of being bound by unlimited, unconditioned perfection, from which the Universal Father is liberated through the personalization of the Son, Spirit, and Trinity, allowing for diverse expressions of divine will and personality.

name: absolutum
description: The unique material substance of Paradise, described as a homogeneous organization of space potency that is neither dead nor alive. It represents the original nonspiritual expression of the First Source and Center and exists nowhere else in the universe of universes.

name: absonite level
description: A transcendent level of reality that exists beyond the finite but below the absolute, characterized by supertime and transcended space.

name: absoniters
description: An order of transcendental beings with absonite nature, related to the Transcendentalers.

name: absonites
description: Beings or entities characterized by absonite nature, existing at a transcendental level of reality between the finite and absolute levels.

name: absonity
description: A transcendental level of reality that exists between the finite and absolute levels, characterized by the ability to transcend time and space. Absonite beings are neither finite nor infinite, neither infinity nor absoluteness, representing a progressive course toward divinity that Paradise arrivals must study.

name: abstraction
description: A philosophical concept or practice that can become problematic when divorced from spiritual reality. Philosophers commit grave error when misled by the fallacy of abstraction, focusing on one isolated aspect as the whole truth, while purely subjective philosophical abstraction is devoid of love.

name: accidents
description: Seemingly random or chance occurrences that primitive peoples viewed as intentional spiritual interventions. While believers are not immune to the accidents of time or natural catastrophes, blind and unforeseen accidents do not occur in the cosmos, and all such events eventually work out for the welfare and progress of the universes.

name: Achaia
description: A Roman province in ancient Greece where Corinth served as the capital. The apostle Andrew was crucified in Patrae in Achaia, and Luke wrote his gospel there in 82 A.D.

name: achievement
description: The realization of goals through personal effort, imagination, and divine partnership. True achievement is the child of imaginative adventure, grows out of divine capacity for action, and requires wisdom, judgment, and perseverance, with recognition that all beings are held responsible only for accomplishments within their abilities.

name: acknowledgments
description: Formal recognition of sources and assistance used by authors of The Urantia Papers. The midwayer author of Jesus' life narrative extensively acknowledges drawing from human sources, concepts, and expressions from over two thousand individuals who lived from Jesus' time to the inditing of the revelations.

name: Acropolis
description: The ancient hilltop citadel of Athens, Greece, under whose shadow the Apostle Paul preached to the Athenians about the Unknown God at the council of the Areopagus.

name: Act of God
description: A divine designation referring specifically to the children of the Infinite Spirit, who are truly the Act of God, while the divine Sons are the Word of God.

name: action
description: A concept fundamental to spiritual and evolutionary progress, representing the essential link between faith and experience. Action is required to transform spiritual potentials into actuals, make decisions meaningful, and achieve personal growth and cosmic advancement.

name: Actium
description: The site of a decisive naval battle where Emperor Augustus defeated Mark Antony, commemorated by Augustus founding the nearby city of Nicopolis, meaning 'city of victory.'

name: actors
description: Celestial artisans who serve as dramatic performers in heavenly pageants and spectacular presentations, often collaborating with reversion directors in morontia and spirit assemblies.

name: Acts, Book of
description: The second book planned by Luke, the physician of Antioch, as part of his three-volume work on Christ and Christianity. Luke died in A.D. 90 before completing this work about the apostles' activities.

name: acts of God
description: A term used to describe inexplicable natural phenomena and powerful forces that primitive humans could not comprehend, such as storms, earthquakes, and other mysterious manifestations.

name: actual
description: One of the three phases of reality, representing realities existing in fullness of expression, in contrast to potential realities which carry undisclosed capacity for growth.

name: Actual, the
description: The triodity of actuality consisting of the Eternal Son, Infinite Spirit, and Paradise Isle - the union of the three Absolutes of actuality that constitutes the actual revelation of the First Source and Center's originality.

name: Adam
description: The Planetary Adam of Urantia, a Material Son from Jerusem who, along with his consort Eve, was tasked with biologically uplifting the human races. Despite their default and subsequent mortality, they made significant contributions to human civilization and racial improvement.

name: Adam (original of Satania)
description: The supervising head and original Material Son of Satania, after whom all subsequent Material Sons are named. Unlike their progeny, this original Adam and his consort Eve possess inherent immortality.

name: Adam (village)
description: A village east of the Jordan River where John the Baptist made his memorable attack on Herod Antipas and first spoke of 'another one who is to come after me,' referring to Jesus.

name: Adamic dispensation
description: A planetary epoch that begins with the arrival of a Material Son and Daughter on an evolutionary world, marking a new phase in planetary spiritual and biological development.

name: Adamic Sons
description: Material Sons who serve as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds, working alongside Material Daughters to improve the physical and genetic heritage of planetary races.

name: Adamites
description: The violet race founded by Adam and Eve on Urantia, characterized by fair complexions, light hair, and blue eyes. They were the ninth human race to appear on Urantia and served as biologic uplifters, contributing their superior life plasm to the evolutionary races through careful interbreeding programs.

name: Adamson
description: The first-born child of Adam and Eve on Urantia who founded a secondary center of violet race civilization north of the second Eden. He lived 396 years and, together with his wife Ratta, became the progenitor of the secondary midwayers and established a high culture that influenced later civilizations.

name: Adamsonites
description: The descendants of Adamson and Ratta who maintained a high culture for almost seven thousand years in their headquarters east of the Caspian Sea. They were the discoverers of bronze and eventually migrated to Greece, becoming the ancestors of the Greeks and contributing to European civilization.

name: Aden
description: A preacher who taught in Jesus' name and claimed to cast out devils, despite never having been with the apostles. When John forbade his activities, Jesus instructed that he should not be hindered, teaching the principle that "he who is not against us is for us."

name: Adirondack Mountains
description: An ancient mountain range in North America that forms part of a primitive fossil-bearing rock ridge extending from Pennsylvania through the Great Lakes region to Minnesota.

name: adjudication
description: The highest function of any government involving the rendering of verdicts and judgments. Those entrusted with adjudication should be chosen from the most experienced, understanding, and noble individuals available.

name: Adjuster-expression
description: The manifestation or communication of divine guidance through the indwelling Thought Adjuster's influence on mortal consciousness and decision-making.

name: Adjuster-fused mortals
description: Ascending mortals who have achieved eternal fusion with their indwelling Thought Adjusters, representing one of the three orders of fusion that characterize the survival careers of evolutionary beings.

name: Adjuster fusion
description: The process by which a mortal's soul permanently unites with their indwelling Thought Adjuster, achieving spiritual immortality and becoming an agondonter of the finaliter corps.

name: adjutant mind-spirits
description: Seven mind-spirits created by the Divine Minister of each local universe that minister to material minds from animal to human levels. Named intuition, understanding, courage, knowledge, counsel, worship, and wisdom, they guide evolutionary development and prepare minds for spiritual ministry, with the first five functioning in animals and all seven in human intellect.

name: adjutants of Havona circuits
aliases: Twelvefold Adjutants of the Havona Circuits
description: Twelve helper beings who serve each of the seven Circuit Spirits of Havona. Ascending mortals must pass tests before these twelve adjutants of their superuniverse's Circuit Spirit to advance from one Havona circle to the next.

name: administrative assistants
description: Able seraphim of the fourth order who serve as immediate assistants to System Sovereigns and Planetary Princes. They execute administrative details, serve as personal agents for system rulers, and travel between worlds on missions for system welfare. One thousand of this order currently assist Urantia's acting ruler.

name: administrative enactors
description: One of seven groups of Heavenly Reproducers who specialize in depicting the significance of governmental philosophy and administrative technique. They are celestial dramatists of sovereignty who reproduce supreme governmental values on the morontia level.

name: administrator seraphim
description: The fourth order of seraphim assigned to administrative duties in local systems. Native to system capitals but stationed throughout mansion worlds and inhabited planets, they possess unusual administrative ability and assist in universe government affairs. Many were lost during the Lucifer rebellion, with about half of Urantia's administrator seraphim joining the revolt.

name: Adonai
description: One of the appellations used by the Semites to avoid speaking the name of their Deity, which they considered taboo. Along with other titles like The Lord, The Almighty, and The Most High, it allowed reverent reference to God without pronouncing the sacred name.

name: Adonia
description: The commercial metropolis of central Asia, located near present-day Ashkhabad. It served as a major trade center where migrating Andites established commerce in stone, metal, wood, and pottery, preferring urban manufacturing to herding as climatic conditions made hunting unprofitable.

name: adoption
description: An ancient practice involving ceremonial blood drinking or saliva exchange to gain clan membership and friendship. Originally used to avoid killing strangers, adoption ensured someone would provide post-death offerings for the soul and was practiced in various forms including the adoption of children into superior households.

name: adultery
description: Recognized in ancient times as a form of stealing and infringement of the husband's property rights. Women were held to stricter sexual accountability than men because marital infidelity affected descent and inheritance. Various cultures developed tests and punishments for adultery, and it was prohibited in numerous ancient legal codes.

name: adventure
description: Adventure represents the quest for new experience and discovery, both material and spiritual. The Andites contributed this trait to human evolution through their roving dispositions and extensive explorations. Adventure encompasses the supreme human experience of seeking and finding God, as well as the eternal progression that begins with death and continues through endless spiritual discovery and growth.

name: advice
description: Advice should only be given when requested, according to both celestial protocol and human wisdom. High celestial beings, including Trinity Sons and other Paradise personalities, never proffer counsel unless asked. Similarly, Jesus seldom offered advice unless specifically requested, following the principle that wisdom should be shared only when the recipient genuinely desires guidance.

name: advisers regarding conquest of predatory animals
description: Members of the Caligastia one hundred who provided guidance on controlling and managing dangerous animals during the early development of human civilization.

name: Advisory Commission on Ethics and Self-government
aliases: Advisory Commission of Interuniverse Ethics and Self-government
description: A Paradise organization composed of reserve Faithfuls of Days who serve as the Advisory Commission of Interuniverse Ethics and Self-government. These high Trinity-origin personalities provide guidance on ethical matters and governmental principles across the universes.

name: aesthetics
description: The appreciation of beauty and artistic expression, which represents one of the cosmologic levels of thought. While aesthetic appreciation and love of the beautiful are important aspects of spiritual development, sound religious philosophy recognizes that the aesthetic cult of pure wonder cannot serve as a substitute for genuine religion.

name: affectation
description: The ridiculous effort of the ignorant to appear wise or the attempt of the barren soul to appear rich. Jesus was completely free from affectation, being always refreshingly genuine and sincere without any pretense or shamming.

name: affection
description: Personal affection serves as the spiritual bond that holds families together and creates effective human relationships. While enduring marriages cannot be founded on biological affection alone, genuine personal affection between individuals, whether of opposite or same sex, forms the basis for lasting human associations and friendships.

name: Afghanistan
description: A mountainous region in Asia where Badonan, a descendant of the first human beings Andon and Fonta, founded a primitive center of culture that persisted for over half a million years. This area served as an important focal point for early human development and cultural evolution.

name: Africa
description: The continent where early human races, particularly the secondary Sangik peoples including the green, orange, and indigo races, migrated and established various civilizations. Africa played a significant role in geological evolution, race migrations, cultural development, and later became a field for gospel teaching through various missionaries and teachers.

name: Agaman
description: A widow from Damascus who was among the ten women commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom. She served in the women's corps that provided parallel ministry to the twelve apostles and was present at Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance in Jerusalem.

name: Agni
description: Agni was the ancient Aryan fire god, often exalted as the head of the Vedic pantheon. He was depicted as a three-headed deity who served as lord of the earth and represented a vestigial symbol of earlier Trinity concepts that had degenerated from the original monotheistic teachings brought to India by Sethite priests around 16,000 BC.

name: agondonters
description: Agondonters are ascendant evolutionary will creatures who can believe without seeing, persevere when isolated, and triumph over insuperable difficulties even when alone. These beings from quarantined worlds occupy their own residential sector on Jerusem and maintain this functional grouping throughout their ascension until reaching Paradise, where they join the Corps of the Mortal Finality.

name: agriculture
description: Agriculture is the cultivation of plants and soil, representing the highest type of material civilization and the most human of all activities. It appeared during the age of the Planetary Prince and was taught by both Caligastia and Adam, with the growing of plants exerting an ennobling influence that instills patience, quiet, and peace in all races of mankind.

name: Ahab
description: Ahab was a king of Ephraim who was reproved by the prophet Elijah for his moral transgressions. He murdered Naboth to seize his land, with his Phoenician wife forging papers to confiscate Naboth's property on charges of blasphemy, and later resisted King Shalmaneser III at the battle of Karkar.

name: Ahura-Mazda
description: Ahura-Mazda was the supreme god at the head of Zoroaster's constellation of seven deities. This concept later degenerated into Sol Invictus, the sun god of Mithraism, representing the evolution and corruption of Zoroastrian religious ideas.

name: Ai
description: Ai was an ancient city that Joshua completely destroyed in a barbaric massacre, killing all inhabitants including men, women, and children, with only the king spared to gratify the conqueror's vanity. This attack exemplified the brutal warfare practices before the more merciful custom of enslaving prisoners of war developed.

name: Akkad
description: Akkad was one of the prominent city-states of ancient Mesopotamia that once held sovereignty over the region. It was part of the Kish confederation and later fell to northern invaders around 2500 BC, after which the Mesopotamian Andites passed from history.

name: Alabama
description: Alabama is a North American state whose geological record contains thirty-five distinct coal layers, indicating multiple cycles of land subsidence and uplift throughout Earth's history. These coal beds contain both fresh-water and salt-water fossils.

name: Alaska
description: Alaska is the northwesternmost region of North America where the red race first arrived over 80,000 years ago. It forms part of the geological ridge system extending from Alaska to Mexico and was volcanically active during ancient mountain-forming periods.

name: alchemy
description: Alchemy was an early proto-scientific practice that sought to transform base materials. Through honest and fearless search for true causes, alchemy evolved into the modern science of chemistry, just as astrology became astronomy and magic became medicine.

name: Alexander (brother of Philo)
description: A Jewish banker from Alexandria and brother of the famous philosopher Philo, with whom Gonod conducted business during Jesus' Mediterranean journey.

name: Alexander of Cyrene
description: Son of Simon of Cyrene who became an effective teacher of the gospel in Africa after his father carried Jesus' crossbeam to the crucifixion.

name: Alexander the Great
description: Macedonian king who expanded Greece into an empire stretching from the Adriatic to the Indus, spreading Hellenistic civilization throughout the near-Eastern world. His cultural influence helped prepare the way for Christianity's later expansion into Greek territories.

name: Alexandria
description: Major Egyptian city and center of blended Occidental culture, home to the world's largest library containing nearly a million manuscripts and the largest Jewish synagogue governed by seventy elders. Jesus and his family lived there for two years during his infancy, and he later visited during his Mediterranean journey.

name: algae, seaweed
description: Marine plant life that played crucial roles in early Earth history, generating oxygen in the atmosphere and laying down limestone deposits. Algae represent part of the evolutionary chain from which higher life forms, including humans, eventually developed.

name: Allah
description: The one and only Deity of Islam, representing a clear-cut and well-defined concept of universal divinity that has been the source of Islam's strength.

name: allegories
description: Extended metaphorical narratives that Jesus advised against using when teaching, preferring instead simple parables that illustrated single truths rather than complex symbolic stories requiring detailed interpretation.

name: alligators
description: Reptiles descended from nonprogressive reptilian lines, representing one of the four surviving divisions of the ancient reptile kingdom along with snakes, lizards, and turtles.

name: Almighty
description: A term referring to the supreme power aspect of deity, often used in connection with the Supreme Being's evolutionary sovereignty over the grand universe.

name: Almighty Supreme
description: The power-sovereignty aspect of the Supreme Being, representing the evolving almighty power of God the Supreme as it emerges through the experiences of time and space.

name: aloes
description: A fragrant spice used along with myrrh to embalm Jesus' body for burial, applied with bandages saturated in these solutions.

name: Alpha and Omega
description: A divine title meaning "the first and the last" or "the beginning and the end," used by God the Father and claimed by Jesus to signify eternal divine nature.

name: Alpheus
description: The father of apostles James and Judas Alpheus, who were twin fishermen from Kheresa called by Jesus to be among the twelve apostles.

name: Alpheus twins
description: James and Judas Alpheus, twin brothers and fishermen from Kheresa who became two of Jesus' twelve apostles. They were 26 years old, married with children, and served as chief ushers managing crowds during Jesus' ministry. Though considered simple and mediocre by their fellow apostles, they were faithful, good-natured helpers who loved Jesus for his humility and simplicity.

name: Alpine race
description: One of the three major divisions of the white race, representing a distinct ethnic group within the broader Caucasian racial classification.

name: Alps
description: A major European mountain range where the Heidelberg race spread and where many plants and animals displaced by glaciers found refuge at high elevations. The Alps experienced significant geological activity, with limestone deposits forming the characteristic dolomite walls and peaks of the region.

name: altars
description: Sacred structures used for worship and sacrifice. Ancient peoples believed altars were polluted if built of hewn stone, preferring natural materials. Early Christian and Mithraic cults both used altars, and Andite families used their hearths as altars for religious purposes.

name: alter ego
description: A concept used in prayer technique where one relates to an imaginary companion or second self. Children naturally develop this when learning language, and mature prayer involves recognizing this alter ego concept has evolved from fiction to the truth of God's indwelling presence through the Thought Adjuster.

name: altruism
description: Unselfish concern for the welfare of others, originating from the divine spirit indwelling human minds through the Thought Adjuster. It represents the impulse to serve one's fellows rather than seeking personal gain, and is essential for social progress and spiritual development. True altruism requires the enlightenment of reason, morality, and religious consciousness.

name: Alvoring
description: One of the seven neighboring universes closest to Nebadon, located in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: Amadon
description: Outstanding human hero of the Lucifer rebellion who was an Andonite and associate of Van from Caligastia's entourage. Amadon remained steadfast in loyalty to the universe government throughout the seven-year struggle, supported by the primary midwayer 1-2-3 the first, and remained on Urantia for 150,000 years after the rebellion until Adam and Eve arrived, when he was translated to Jerusem.

name: Amadonites
description: A group derived from 144 loyal Andonites who remained faithful with Van and Amadon during the Lucifer rebellion and became known by Amadon's name. This group comprised 39 men and 105 women, with 56 of immortality status who were translated along with loyal staff members, while the remainder continued on earth under Van and Amadon's leadership as biological leaven for future generations.

name: Amatha
description: Simon Peter's mother-in-law who suffered from malarial fever. She was not miraculously healed when Jesus first comforted her at her bedside, but was cured several hours later during the extraordinary healing event that occurred at sunset in the Zebedee home's front yard.

name: Amathus
description: A town in Perea, west of Gerasa and south of Pella, near Ragaba, where Jesus and the twelve stayed for three weeks during their first public ministry tour. It was later visited during the Perean mission of the seventy, and was the location where Jesus healed ten lepers to demonstrate that all peoples were capable of accepting the gospel.

name: Amaziah
description: King of Judah who achieved victory over the revolting Edomites but was defeated when he attacked his northern neighbors. He was subsequently assassinated by revolting rural folk who then placed his sixteen-year-old son Azariah on the throne.

name: ambassadors
description: Representatives appointed to maintain diplomatic relations between different governmental levels in the universe administration. Universe ambassadors are appointed by judicial decree to represent universes to each other, while human ambassadors developed from tribal peace negotiations and represent one group to another in diplomatic relations.

name: amber
description: A fossilized resin traded by the Nordic peoples from the Baltic coast, who built extensive commerce with the Danubian broadheads through the Brenner Pass, leading to cultural exchanges including the adoption of mother worship and cremation practices.

name: ambitions
description: Personal desires and aspirations for achievement or advancement that can either motivate spiritual growth or become enslaving when focused on selfish aims. True spiritual progress requires the surrender of purely selfish ambitions while maintaining wholehearted dedication to doing God's will, as exemplified by Abraham's surrender of personal ambitions in favor of Melchizedek's larger plans.

name: Amdon
description: A Chaldean shepherd of Sumerian origin who was the first mortal to witness Melchizedek's materialization on Urantia. Melchizedek entered Amdon's tent and proclaimed his mission, sharing the first meal of his material existence with this shepherd under the stars while beginning his revelation of the truth about God.

name: amends
description: The act of making restitution or correcting wrongs committed, particularly through confession of error and restoration of ill-gotten gains. Making amends for past sins provides strength to resist future wrongdoing and is essential for spiritual progress, as taught across various religious traditions.

name: Amenemope
description: Amenemope was a great Egyptian prophet and religious teacher who greatly influenced both Hebrew religious faith and Greek philosophy. Known as the "son of man," he exalted conscience as the supreme arbiter between right and wrong, taught that sins would be punished, and proclaimed salvation through the solar deity. His teachings contributed to the Hebrew concept of El Shaddai and helped preserve Mesopotamian religious culture in Hebrew literature.

name: Amenhotep III
description: Amenhotep III was an Egyptian pharaoh whose birth is depicted on temple walls at Luxor alongside another identical child representing the ka, the Egyptian protective spirit believed to accompany distinguished individuals throughout their lives.

name: Amerinds
description: American Indians, the indigenous peoples of the Americas who are part of the red race.

name: Amida Buddha
description: A divine figure in Buddhist theology, specifically the God of Paradise in the west, believed by Amidists to show infinite mercy and loving care to all who call upon his name in true faith.

name: Amidist
description: A believer in Amida Buddha who holds that souls may choose to sojourn in Paradise before entering Nirvana, attaining salvation through faith in Amida's divine mercies and loving care.

name: Ammonites
description: An ancient people neighboring the Hebrews who were defeated by Saul when they attacked the Gileadites, and later had heavy tribute imposed on them by King David.

name: amoeba
description: Single-celled animal organisms that are typical survivors of the initial stage of animal life, having remained largely unchanged since ancient times as representatives of early evolutionary development.

name: Amorites
description: A confederation of Semitic clans who were committing cultural suicide through foolish practices, making them vulnerable to future defeat by Abraham's descendants. Chemosh was their god, whom the Hebrews acknowledged as real but subordinate to Yahweh.

name: Amos (brother of Jesus)
description: The seventh child of Joseph and Mary, born January 9, A.D. 7, who died at age five on December 3, A.D. 12, after a week-long illness with high fever. Jesus had an especially good relationship with little Amos and his sister Ruth.

name: Amos (John Mark’s friend)
description: A young friend of John Mark who believed in the gospel but was prevented by overprotective parents from joining Jesus' movement. Jesus considered him less dependable than John Mark due to his problematic home upbringing that lacked the normal, loving, and wise training necessary for developing strong character.

name: Amos (Kheresa lunatic)
description: Amos was a mentally afflicted man who lived in caves and cemeteries near Kheresa. When he encountered Jesus, his troubled mind was calmed, and he became convinced that evil spirits had left him and entered a nearby herd of swine that subsequently tumbled over a cliff. This belief in his miraculous healing helped consolidate his recovery, and he later led Aden to believe in Jesus, who dedicated himself to preaching.

name: Amos (Prophet)
description: Amos was a Hebrew prophet who emerged from the southern hills to denounce the criminality, drunkenness, and immorality of the northern tribes. He proclaimed Yahweh as the "God of all nations" and attacked the concept of the chosen people, threatening that God would abandon Israel unless justice was restored. He was eventually stoned to death for his courageous teachings that challenged the double standard of national justice and morality.

name: Amosad
description: Amosad was a leader who around 15,000 B.C. brought about a regeneration of the decadent Sethite clergy. Under his leadership, the Sethite priesthood experienced a brilliant renaissance that inaugurated a new era and sustained the survival of their faith longer than any other religious movement of that time.

name: amphibians
description: During the Devonian period, amphibians emerged as the first land animals, evolving from arthropods whose swim bladders had developed into lungs. These air-breathing creatures were able to live both on land and in water, representing a sudden and significant evolutionary development.

name: amphitheater
description: Amphitheaters are circular or oval structures designed for public gatherings, performances, and lectures. Notable examples include the amphitheater at Lake Urmia dedicated to the "spirit of religion," the massive amphitheater in Caesarea with capacity for 20,000 spectators, and the enormous broadcast-receiving amphitheaters on Jerusem and Edentia that accommodate billions of beings for universe communications.

name: anarchy
description: Anarchy augments misery and represents the absence of organized government or leadership. Jesus taught that in the universe, where two or more creatures act in cooperation, there is always provided the authority of leadership, making anarchy incompatible with divine order and effective group functioning.

name: Anaxagoras
description: Anaxagoras was a Greek philosopher and mechanist who recognized a First Cause or Initial Mind, distinguishing him from pure materialists. Though primarily mechanistic in his thinking, he acknowledged the necessity of an originating intelligence behind the universe.

name: Anaxand
description: Anaxand was a young Greek shipyard worker in Caesarea who became deeply moved by Jesus' teachings about overcoming evil with goodness. He later became quartermaster to the Roman centurion Cornelius, converted to Christianity through Philip's preaching, and perished accidentally while ministering to the wounded during the massacre of 20,000 Jews in Caesarea in 66 AD.

name: ancestor worship
description: Ancestor worship evolved from the advancing ghost cult and became the connecting link between common spirits and higher gods. While it promoted family stability and social cohesion, especially in Chinese culture, its great weakness was fostering a backward-looking philosophy that regarded the past as the exclusive source of truth, based on the belief that the dead would punish those who failed to follow ancestral customs.

name: Ancients of Days
description: The Ancients of Days are the triune rulers of the seven superuniverses, with three governing each superuniverse from its headquarters world. They are Trinity-origin beings who represent the beginning of personality records in the universe of universes and serve as supreme executive judges with the power to decree the eternal extinction of will creatures. They are the most powerful direct rulers of time-space creations and provide uniform administration across the diverse superuniverses.

name: Ancients of Days of Uversa
description: The Ancients of Days of Uversa are the supreme rulers of the superuniverse of Orvonton who serve as the highest administrative and judicial authority for all local universes within their domain. They communicated with Michael during his bestowal on Urantia, provided mandates for The Urantia Papers, and made crucial decisions regarding the Lucifer rebellion including the imprisonment of Lucifer and certification of Jesus' mortal transit.

name: Andes mountains
description: The Andes Mountains are a major mountain range in South America that began rising during the Mesozoic era, marking their emergence while most of South America was submerged under polar seas. This uplift shaped the continent's geology and prevented complete inundation of the landmass.

name: Andites
description: The Andites were the best all-round human stock to appear on Urantia since the days of the pure-line violet peoples, representing a blend of Adamite, Nodite, and superior evolutionary races. They were skilled militarists and adventurers who made extensive migrations across Eurasia, significantly influencing the development of civilization through their contributions to art, agriculture, animal domestication, and cultural advancement before eventually being absorbed into the populations they encountered.

name: Andon and Fonta
description: Andon and Fonta were the first two human beings on Urantia and the true parents of all mankind, born about one million years ago during the third glacial advance in what is now India. This remarkable pair made the first human decision of moral significance, discovered fire, developed early language, and lived to age forty-two before being killed in an earthquake, later achieving fusion with their Thought Adjusters and serving on Jerusem as counselors to ascending mortals from Urantia.

name: Andonites
description: The Andonites were the descendants of Andon and Fonta, representing the first human race on Urantia and the only race of will creatures to evolve in advance of the evolutionary races of color in the local system. They possessed swarthy complexions similar to modern Eskimos, developed basic tools and hunting techniques, and were eventually dispersed by climate changes and pressure from later races, with their purest surviving descendants being the Eskimos and other northern peoples.

name: Andovontia
description: Andovontia is the tertiary Universe Circuit Supervisor stationed in the local universe of Nebadon, responsible for spirit and morontia circuits. He isolated Urantia at the time of Caligastia's betrayal during the Lucifer rebellion and serves as a permanent member of Michael of Nebadon's personal staff with the designation number 572,842.

name: Andrew
description: Andrew was the first chosen apostle and chairman of the apostolic corps, a 33-year-old fisherman from Capernaum who served as the administrative leader of the twelve. Though lacking oratorical ability, he was an understanding executive and efficient administrator who faithfully managed the practical affairs of the apostolic group and later traveled through Armenia, Asia Minor, and Macedonia before being crucified in Patrae in Achaia.

name: Andromeda
description: Andromeda is a sun-forming nebula outside the inhabited superuniverse of Orvonton that is visible to the naked eye from Urantia. The light from this distant nebula takes nearly one million years to reach Earth, and it was the site of a giant nova that collapsed in just forty minutes about fifty years prior to the time of The Urantia Book's revelation.

name: Andronover
description: Andronover was the enormous nebula that gave origin to our sun and solar system, designated as number 876,926 in universe records. This former spiral nebula produced 1,013,628 suns and sun systems over its evolutionary cycles before its final nuclear remnant settled into a stable configuration that continues to provide light and heat to its remaining family of 165 worlds.

name: Ang
description: Ang was one of the corporeal members of Caligastia's staff who presided over the council on food and material welfare, teaching primitive humans irrigation, furriers, and weaving techniques. He remained loyal during the Lucifer rebellion along with three other members of his food council and was later transferred to Jerusem to resume his Paradise journey.

name: Angamon
description: Angamon was the leader of the Stoics in Rome who had an all-night talk with Jesus during his sojourn in Rome. He subsequently became a great friend of Paul of Tarsus and one of the strong supporters of the Christian church at Rome, and he introduced Jesus to a wealthy Roman citizen who was greatly influenced by Jesus' teachings.

name: Angelic World
description: The Angelic World is the third transitional cultural world of Jerusem, serving as the headquarters of all seraphic hosts engaged in system activities and surrounded by seven worlds of angelic training and instruction. Mortals who reach the third mansion world receive permission to visit this sphere, which houses the various system training schools for angelic orders.

name: angels
description: Angels are the ministering-spirit associates of evolutionary and ascending will creatures throughout space, serving as colleagues and working associates of higher divine personalities. They are distinct personalities registered under the Infinite Spirit, sustained directly by spiritual energy, and possess very social natures with associative capacities far transcending those of human beings.

name: anger
description: Anger is a material manifestation representing the failure of spiritual nature to gain control over the combined intellectual and physical natures. It is considered mean and despicable, weakens character, destroys happiness, and greatly interferes with spiritual progress and the work of the Thought Adjuster.

name: angiosperms
description: Angiosperms are flowering plants that emerged during the Cretaceous period and quickly spread across the continents, becoming the dominant plant life. They provided the primary food source for evolving mammals and included many modern trees such as oak, walnut, and maple, as well as fruits, grasses, and cereals.

name: Anglo-Saxons
description: Anglo-Saxons are descendants of the blended Andite and blue races who became the ancestors of the Nordic peoples. They are among the forefathers of the Scandinavian, German, and Anglo-Saxon peoples who emerged from the westward movement of Andite horsemen across the Russian plains.

name: Angona
description: Angona was a massive dark giant solar system that approached our sun 4.5 billion years ago, causing the birth of the twelve planets of our solar system through gravitational disruption. This enormous system drew off vast columns of solar material that eventually condensed into the planets, asteroids, and meteors that comprise our present solar system.

name: animals
description: Animals are material creatures that possess mind and can communicate in limited ways but lack spiritual nature, reflective thinking, and the capacity for spiritual experience. They differ from humans in their inability to know God, use symbols for meaningful communication, or experience personality survival, though they serve important roles in planetary ecosystems and human civilization.

name: animism
description: Animism is the primitive belief that all nature is alive and possessed by spirits, viewing every rock, tree, and natural object as spirit-inhabited. This crude religious concept was prevalent in Jesus' time and represents one of the earliest forms of religious thinking, though it often existed alongside other emerging religious sentiments.

name: anise
description: Anise is a plant mentioned by Jesus when criticizing the Pharisees for their meticulous attention to tithing minor herbs like mint, anise, and cumin while neglecting the weightier matters of the law such as faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: Anna
description: Anna was a Galilean poetess who, along with Simeon, frequently lingered in the temple courts and was an intimate of the priest Zacharias. She wrote a poem that was sung during Jesus' presentation in the temple as part of their hymn celebrating the redemption of the first-born son.

name: Annan
description: Annan was a loyal daughter and spokesperson for the Nodite race who served on the twelve-member Urantia reception committee that welcomed Adam and Eve upon their arrival in the Garden of Eden.

name: Annas
description: Annas was the former high priest of the Jews, deposed in A.D. 13, who remained the most powerful individual in all Jewry through his temple revenues and Roman connections. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the acting high priest, and conducted the preliminary examination of Jesus before his trial, having previously known Jesus during his earlier visits to Jerusalem.

name: Annon
description: Annon was one of the illustrious women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, representing part of the remarkable heritage that characterized Mary's diverse racial background.

name: Anova
description: Anova is the oldest inhabited world in the Satania system, designated as world number one. It is one of forty-four satellites revolving around an enormous dark planet and is exposed to the differential light of three neighboring suns, having reached an advanced stage of progressive civilization.

name: Ansie
description: Ansie was one of the illustrious women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, representing part of the remarkable heritage that characterized Mary's diverse racial background.

name: Antarctica
description: Antarctica is a continent that broke away from the ancient land masses along with Australia and Pacific islands, drifting far from its original position. It was covered by extensive ice sheets during glacial periods and continues to influence global climate patterns through its polar ice coverage.

name: Antares
description: Antares is the largest star in the local universe of Nebadon, measuring four hundred and fifty times the diameter of our sun and sixty million times its volume.

name: anteaters
description: Anteaters evolved in South America ten million years ago alongside sloths, armadillos, and primitive South American monkeys, contributing to the continent's unique fauna during the Cenozoic era.

name: antelopes
description: Antelopes migrated from Asia to North America ten million years ago during a period when all continents except Australia were connected, participating in the last great worldwide animal migration.

name: anthracite
description: A type of coal that has been subjected to more pressure and heat than other coal types, making it a hard, high-grade form of coal.

name: anthropomorphism
description: The attribution of human characteristics to God or divine beings. An exalted anthropomorphism represents the highest attainment level of purely evolutionary religion, with Christianity elevating this concept through the glorified Christ.

name: antigravity
description: A force that can neutralize or resist the effects of material gravity within a local frame by exercising equal force presence. It operates only with reference to material gravity and is possessed by certain beings and employed in various cosmic functions, including the organization of Havona worlds and the work of energy controllers.

name: Antioch
description: The capital of the Roman province of Syria and third largest city in the Roman Empire, with half a million inhabitants. Known for its wickedness and immorality, Antioch was where Jesus lived for over two months during his Mediterranean journey and later became the headquarters of Pauline Christianity.

name: Antipatris
description: A city in Palestine that Jesus passed through on his way to Jerusalem and from which people came to hear his teachings. Residents of Antipatris were among those who traveled to listen to Jesus and his apostles preach the good news of the kingdom.

name: antithesis
description: A rhetorical device effectively employed by Jesus in his teaching, comparing contrasting elements such as the minute to the infinite to illustrate spiritual truths and convey large meanings through small expressions.

name: Antonia (fortress)
description: A Roman fortress in Jerusalem that housed the praetorium where Pilate and his wife stayed during visits to the city. It was the location where Jesus' trial took place and served as headquarters for Roman forces, standing as a grim reminder of Roman rule over the Jews.

name: Anu
description: A Mesopotamian god of the sky who was part of the Babylonian triad of supreme deities, along with Bel and Ea, representing the gods of sky, earth, and sea respectively.

name: anxiety
description: A state of worry, fear, or unease that can dominate human thinking and interfere with spiritual growth. Jesus taught against anxiety, emphasizing trust in the Father's care and the importance of seeking first the kingdom of God rather than being troubled by material concerns.

name: Apennines
description: A mountain range in Italy that, forty million years ago, existed as highlands rising above water as islands when much of Europe was submerged under the expanded Mediterranean Sea.

name: Aphrodite
description: Greek goddess of beauty, sensuality and love. Aphrodite is the name given to the goddess Ishtar in Greece, and when this cult was brought to Rome, Aphrodite became known as Venus.

name: apocalyptists
description: A school of religious teachers that arose in Palestine about one hundred years before Jesus and John the Baptist. These teachers developed a belief system that attributed Jewish sufferings to the nation's sins and taught that Israel's period of discipline was nearly finished, with God's patience toward gentile foreigners almost exhausted.

name: apocrypha
description: Non-canonical biblical books mentioned in The Urantia Book, including the Book of Enoch and the Book of Wisdom. Jesus found the Book of Enoch particularly influential in his adoption of the term 'Son of Man' as a designation for his bestowal mission.

name: Apollo
description: Greek and Roman god of music and the arts who had a temple located on the Palatine Hill in Rome, along with the emperor's residence and Greek and Latin libraries.

name: apostles of Jesus
description: The twelve chosen followers of Jesus: Andrew, Simon Peter, James and John Zebedee, Philip, Nathaniel, Matthew Levi, Thomas Didymus, James and Judas Alpheus, Simon Zelotes, and Judas Iscariot. Most were married fishermen and graduates of synagogue schools who served as ambassadors of the kingdom and personal representatives of Jesus throughout his ministry.

name: apostles of John the Baptist
description: A group of twelve leaders appointed by John the Baptist as his apostles, following the precedent set by Jesus. They practiced anointing the sick with oil, maintained cooperative relations with Jesus' apostles despite occasional disagreements, and were eventually won over to Jesus' cause after John's death.

name: apostleship
description: The commission Jesus gave to those willing to dedicate everything in service to the kingdom, requiring complete devotion and the forsaking of all worldly possessions. Unlike simple discipleship, apostleship demanded wholehearted dedication to doing the Father's will and living as ambassadors of the heavenly kingdom.

name: Appalachian Mountains
description: A mountain range in eastern North America that was almost completely worn down by the Cretaceous period and later elevated during continental drift. Many arctic plants and animals were left stranded on these peaks after the retreat of the final ice age.

name: Appian Way
description: A major Roman road used for travel to Rome in Jesus' time. Travelers like Gonod and Ganid walked leisurely across Italy on this route, encountering diverse peoples including Roman citizens, Greek colonists, and descendants of slaves while pack animals carried their belongings.

name: apple
description: A fruit that became taboo among early peoples, particularly the Levantine peoples who never ate it. Every primitive tribe had its forbidden fruit tree, literally the apple, which came to represent all manner of taboos and restrictions.

name: Aquila
description: A Jewish tentmaker and Cynic philosopher whom Jesus contacted in Rome and who later lived in Corinth with his wife Priscilla, preparing the way for Paul's ministry there and providing him lodging and work.

name: Arabia
description: A desert peninsula where less progressive indigo peoples migrated from Africa, serving as a pathway to Mesopotamia and India, and where Salem missionaries established Melchizedek teachings of monotheism but failed due to overorganization.

name: Arabian Sea
description: A body of water crossed by Dravidian shipping by 7000 B.C., connecting their coastal trade routes between India and the Sumerian cities of the Persian Gulf.

name: Arabs
description: Swarthy nomadic tribes descended from a blend of blue race remnants in the Persian peninsula with yellow and Adamite races, representing one of the more recent pastoral peoples who used distinctive adoption ceremonies involving sacred oaths.

name: Aram
description: A river settlement near Erech, home to Noah the wine maker who kept written records of annual floods and warned neighboring settlements, inspiring the legend of a universal deluge.

name: Aramaic
description: One of three languages prevalent in Palestine during Jesus' time, spoken by the common people in various dialects and fluently mastered by Jesus along with Greek and Hebrew.

name: Ararat
description: A mountainous region mentioned in connection with ancient flood narratives and geographical references in Urantia Book teachings.

name: Arbela
description: One of the cities in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final three-month mission before Jesus' journey to Jerusalem.

name: archangels
description: The highest type of spirit beings produced in large numbers in local universes, numbering almost 800,000 in Nebadon, who are offspring of the Creator Son and Universe Mother Spirit and serve as angels of resurrection and creature survival.

name: archangels’ circuit
description: A special communication circuit established on Urantia due to the archangel divisional headquarters located there, used for direct Michael messages and circumventing planetary isolation caused by rebellion.

name: Archelais
description: A Greek city near the Jordan River where Jesus and the apostles preached during their journey through Samaria and the Decapolis between June and August of 27 CE. Along with Phasaelis, Archelais was inhabited primarily by gentiles rather than Jews, giving the apostles their first extensive experience preaching to non-Jewish populations.

name: Archelaus
description: A being mentioned in The Urantia Book but not further described in the available text.

name: archeology
description: The Urantia Book provides information about potential archaeological discoveries, including the location of the submerged first Eden under the eastern Mediterranean Sea, remnants of Dalamatia civilization under the Persian Gulf, fossils of human transition types in the Siwalik Hills of northern India, and vestiges of the Adamsonite headquarters in the foothills of Turkestan. The book notes that later archaeological artifacts are sometimes inferior to earlier products of the purer Andite peoples.

name: Archeozoic
description: The prelife era extending from about the time Urantia attained its present size to the establishment of life, spanning from approximately 1 billion to 550 million years ago. During the Archeozoic ages, the earth's surface was completely covered by water before land began to appear.

name: Architects of Being
description: An order of unrevealed beings who formulate the plans for life on inhabited planets. Life appears on worlds either through direct importation or through the operations of Life Carriers working according to these beings' designs.

name: Architects of the Master Universe
description: The governing corps of Paradise Transcendentalers numbering 28,011 personalities with master minds, superb spirits, and supernal absonite natures. They are organized in seven levels corresponding to different space levels, from Paradise and Havona to the four outer space levels. These beings serve as universe planners and coordinators, functioning as the veritable embodiments of the Deity concept of the universes and working closely with Paradise Creator Sons in the organization of local universes.

name: architectural spheres
description: Worlds specifically constructed according to plans and specifications for special purposes, rather than arising through natural planetary evolution. These include headquarters worlds like Salvington, Edentia, and Jerusem, which are independently lighted and heated through specialized energy systems. Architectural spheres contain one hundred physical elements and one hundred forms of morontia material, double the number found on evolutionary planets.

name: Arctic Ocean
description: During the geological past, the Arctic Ocean was connected to the Mediterranean Sea through the Ural depression, and northern seas extended southward to invade North America during major continental submergences.

name: arctic regions
description: Northern regions that enjoyed warm, uniform climates during earlier geological periods, much like the present climate of central and southern North America. Andonite peoples were scattered throughout these arctic and central Asian regions around 15,000 BCE.

name: Ardnon
description: A Chaldean priest who led a group of wise men to visit the newborn Jesus in the manger. Ardnon and his associates were informed of Michael's birth by seraphim through midway creatures, making this announcement the only supernatural event associated with Jesus's birth.

name: area and building custodians
description: Morontia Companions on Jerusem who serve as custodians of the material and morontia structures that function as abodes and meeting places on the mansion worlds.

name: Areopagus
description: The council in Athens where the Apostle Paul delivered his famous speech about "the Unknown God," marking a significant moment in the Hellenization of Christianity.

name: Areopolis
description: One of the towns in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final three-month mission before Jesus went to Jerusalem.

name: arguments
description: Disputes or debates that Jesus taught should be avoided in spiritual matters, emphasizing that the argumentative defense of any proposition is inversely proportional to the truth it contains, and that spiritual victories can only be won by spiritual power, not by force or overpowering logic.

name: Arimathea
description: A village whose citizens invited Jesus to visit during his journey through Samaria, where Jesus and the apostles spent more than two weeks teaching Jews and Samaritans.

name: aristocracy
description: The nobility class that developed from early royal assistants and shamans, which served as a restraining influence on kings and included both hereditary nobles and those who purchased their positions through service or payment.

name: Aristotle
description: Greek philosopher who taught that virtue is knowledge and goodness is health of the soul, whose philosophy was revered by Hellenized Jews and significantly influenced early Christian thought.

name: Arius
description: A priest at the Council of Nicaea who defended the subordination of the Son to the Father, whose teachings were challenged by Athanasius to preserve the truth of Jesus' divine nature and bestowal mission.

name: ark of Noah
description: The vessel built by Noah during the great flood, as referenced in Hebrew tradition and scripture.

name: ark of Yahweh
description: The sacred chest containing the law and relics that Moses created as a combined war altar and religious shrine, later brought to Jerusalem by King David and used in synagogue worship to house sacred writings.

name: armadillos
description: Ten million years ago, armadillos migrated from South America to North America during the last great worldwide animal migration, as continents were temporarily connected. Along with sloths, anteaters, and other animals, they spread across the region before the continents became isolated.

name: Armageddon
description: Armageddon was the headquarters of the orange race about 300,000 years ago, where they experienced a great revival of higher living under the wise leadership of Porshunta, the master mind who ministered to this unfortunate race.

name: Armenia
description: Armenia was one of the regions where the apostle Andrew journeyed and proclaimed the gospel after the later persecutions scattered the apostles from Jerusalem.

name: Aroer
description: Aroer was one of the towns visited during the Perean mission, the last ministry of Jesus that continued for almost three months before he went directly to Jerusalem for his final experiences in the flesh.

name: art
description: Art proves that man is not mechanistic and represents mortal morontia, the intervening field between man the material and man the spiritual. It results from man's attempt to escape from the lack of beauty in his material environment and is a gesture toward the morontia level.

name: art of living
description: The art of living is the mastery of dependable techniques for solving common problems, involving the honest recognition of difficulties and the fearless acknowledgment of their nature and gravity.

name: Artemis
description: Artemis was the famous goddess of all Asia Minor and a perpetuation of the earlier mother goddess of ancient Anatolian times, worshiped at the enormous temple in Ephesus where a crude idol reputed to have fallen from heaven was displayed.

name: arthropods
description: A group of invertebrate animals characterized by jointed legs and segmented bodies, including insects, spiders, and crustaceans.

name: artificiality
description: The quality of being artificial, contrived, or lacking in naturalness and genuineness.

name: artists of odor
description: Artists of odor are celestial artisans who create varied symphonies of spiritual fragrance for the edification and delight of advancing children of light, representing a type of spiritual grandeur that has no earthly comparison.

name: Aryans
description: Aryans were Andite conquerors who invaded India from Turkestan around 2500 BCE and became the Vedic rulers. These early invaders brought along multiple tribal deities and established the caste system to maintain racial identity. Their influence was primarily in northern India, and their Aryan language contributed to the similarity among Western tongues.

name: ascender-trinitized sons
description: Ascender-trinitized sons are beings created through trinitization by ascendant creatures, representing one category of creature-trinitized sons who have achieved this unique creative act after their long journey through the universe hierarchy.

name: ascending mortals
description: Ascending mortals are evolutionary creatures who begin their existence on material worlds and progress through the morontia spheres, spirit training worlds, Havona circuits, and finally to Paradise to join the Corps of the Finality. This ascension career represents the divine plan for creature perfection, transforming animal-origin beings into perfected spirits through seven distinct stages of universe progression.

name: ascending pilgrims
description: Ascending pilgrims are evolutionary mortals who have been accredited for the progressive ascent to Paradise. These pilgrims of time pass through the Havona circuits on their way to Paradise, representing the successful candidates in the divine plan of creature ascension from the material worlds to eternal spiritual status.

name: ascending sons of God
description: Ascending sons of God are evolutionary mortals who have achieved sonship status through faith and fusion with their Thought Adjusters. They represent creatures of ascension potential who have embarked on the eternal journey from material existence to spiritual perfection and Paradise attainment.

name: Ascendington
description: Ascendington is the seventh sacred sphere in the Father's circuit around Paradise, serving as the "bosom of the Father, Son, and Spirit." It functions as the Paradise home and rendezvous point for ascending creatures of time and space during their Havona journey, where mortals spend their "vacations" and experience thousands of unimaginable activities while developing new relationships between their human and divine selves.

name: ascension plan
aliases: Paradise plan for the progressive ascension
description: The ascension plan is the divine scheme for creature perfection that brings God down to mortals and then carries them up to God through a sublime partnership. This comprehensive plan includes progressive attainment through Thought Adjuster bestowal, the bestowal plan of Creator Sons, and mercy ministry by the Infinite Spirit, designed to elevate evolutionary creatures to Paradise perfection through graduated universe training and experiential education.

name: asceticism
description: Asceticism refers to extreme self-denial and physical deprivation practiced as religious devotion. The Urantia Book teaches against passive mysticism and colorless asceticism, noting that Jesus was not an ascetic and came to destroy such notions about approaching God through physical affliction and personal pain.

name: Ashdod
description: Ashdod was an ancient Mediterranean coastal city that Jesus passed through during his private journey along the coast in AD 25, traveling from Joppa inland toward Jamnia and Gaza.

name: Ashkhabad
description: Ashkhabad is a modern city near the location of ancient Adonia, which served as central Asia's commercial metropolis where the Andites engaged in trade of stone, metal, wood, and pottery both by land and water.

name: Ashtaroth
description: A city where a man was preaching in Jesus' name, as mentioned by John the apostle who had forbidden this unauthorized preacher.

name: Ashtoreth
description: The Palestinian name for the goddess Ishtar, part of a widespread fertility cult that also appeared as Isis in Egypt, Aphrodite in Greece, and Astarte among northern tribes.

name: Ashur
description: The most prominent god in the Assyrian pantheon, representing one of the national deities that the prophet Jeremiah declared was subordinate to Yahweh as the universal God of all nations.

name: Ashurbanipal
description: An Assyrian king who sent colonists to dwell in Samaria, contributing to the animosity between Jews and Samaritans that persisted for centuries.

name: Asia
description: The continental landmass that served as the homeland of the human race and witnessed successive civilizations including the Dalamatians, Nodites, Adamites, and Andites, from which the potentials of modern civilization spread to the world.

name: Asia Minor
description: A peninsula in western Asia, part of the broader Asian continent.

name: Asmonean dynasty
description: The ruling dynasty also known as the Maccabees, whose palace in Jerusalem served as a residence for Herod the Great.

name: Asmonean palace
description: The stately palace in Jerusalem that served as the residence of Herod the Great and later Herod Antipas, originally built by the Maccabean (Asmonean) dynasty.

name: Asoka
description: A low-caste Indian monarch who embraced Buddhism for self-protection and became one of history's most remarkable civil rulers, training and sending over seventeen thousand Buddhist missionaries throughout the known world during his twenty-five year reign.

name: assassination
description: The deliberate killing of another person, prohibited by moral and religious codes throughout history including the commandments of Dalamatia, Eden, Melchizedek, Gautama, and the Hebrew law, with primitive societies often seeking compensation rather than punishment for such acts.

name: assemblies of Paradise
description: Gatherings held on Edentia where ascending mortals first attend after graduation from world seventy, hearing the story of their career as depicted by the Faithful of Days.

name: Assigned Sentinel of Satania
description: The direct and personal representative of the Associate Inspector on Salvington and the Supreme Executive of Orvonton, serving as ex officio head of the council of 24 councilors on Jerusem.

name: Assigned Sentinels
description: Coordinating personalities and liaison representatives of the Seven Supreme Executives, personalized on Paradise by the Infinite Spirit with exactly seven billion in existence. They serve as representatives of superuniverse Supreme Executives to local systems, rotating assignments every millennium and serving as ex officio heads of the twenty-four administrators from evolutionary worlds.

name: assistant teachers
description: Seraphic helpers and associates of teaching counselors who are individually connected with educational enterprises of the local universe, especially the sevenfold training scheme on mansion worlds.

name: Associate Circuit Supervisors
description: One of four orders of Universe Circuit Supervisors created by the Infinite Spirit, responsible for directing and supervising the circuits of the seven superuniverses from their headquarters on the Paradise spheres of the Infinite Spirit.

name: Associate Inspectors
description: Personal embodiments of the authority of the Supreme Executives to local universes, being joint offspring of the Infinite Spirit and Seven Master Spirits of Paradise. Seven hundred thousand were personalized in near times of eternity, with one stationed on each local universe headquarters as a close associate of the resident Union of Days.

name: associate power directors
description: Marvelously efficient beings entrusted with assignment and dispatch of all orders of Master Physical Controllers according to the ever-shifting energy needs of the realms. Three million are assigned to each minor sector of Orvonton, serving alternating periods of executive service and inspection.

name: associate registrars
description: Morontia world recorders who serve with spirit recorders in supervising and maintaining custody of records and data indigenous to morontia creations.

name: Associate Transcendental Master Force Organizers
description: A classification of Master Force Organizers operating at transcendental levels of reality organization and energy manipulation.

name: Assuntia
description: A local system immediately neighboring Satania, part of the administrative organization of the constellation Norlatiadek.

name: assurance
description: A profound spiritual confidence and certainty experienced by God-knowing believers, characterized by unwavering trust in divine love and protection despite temporal uncertainties. This religious assurance represents the soul's recognition of its eternal kinship with God, manifesting as inner security that remains unassailable even amid earthly trials and adversities.

name: Assyria
description: An ancient Middle Eastern empire that frequently raided Palestinian tribes and eventually conquered the northern kingdom of Israel after it conspired with Egypt to refuse tribute payments. The Assyrians descended from western Nodite stock that settled northwest of Mesopotamia, with their ancestors receiving moral teachings from Van on Mount Ararat.

name: Astarte
description: A goddess worshipped by northern tribes as part of the widespread Ishtar fertility cult, which manifested under different names across various cultures including Ashtoreth in Palestine, Isis in Egypt, and Aphrodite in Greece.

name: asteroids
description: Fragments of celestial bodies created when larger space objects are disrupted by tidal gravity forces from nearby massive bodies. The present-day asteroid cluster in our solar system formed when the fifth planet was fragmentized after making increasingly closer approaches to Jupiter until it entered the critical zone of gravity-tidal disruption.

name: astrology
