description: Domesticated bovines that were among the first animals tamed by humans and became valuable sources of food, milk products, and labor. Cattle were also used as units of barter in early economic systems and sometimes regarded as sacred animals in various cultures.

name: Caucasians
description: The Caucasoid racial group representing the Andite blend of Nodite and Adamic stocks, further modified by primary Sangik admixture and Andonic crossing. This classification includes the Occidental white races along with some Indian and Turanian peoples.

name: causation
description: The cosmic mind response recognizing the reality domain of physical senses and logical uniformity, representing one of three fundamental levels of universe reality. Causation involves the relationship between effects and their antecedent causes in the material universe.

name: caves
description: Natural underground formations that served as dwelling places for early humans and various prehistoric peoples. Caves were also significant in religious practices, being associated with underworld spirits in primitive beliefs and used as places of worship by some mystery cults.

name: Cedes
description: A believer in Antioch who made a brief record of Jesus' life in A.D. 78, which later served as one of the source materials that Luke used in compiling his Gospel.

name: celestial artisans
description: Master artists and artisans of the morontia and lower spirit realms who are responsible for spiritual beautification and morontia embellishment throughout the grand universe. These beings are recruited from selected personalities and serve in seven major divisions of artistic activity.

name: celestial beings
description: General term referring to spiritual entities and personalities who exist in the celestial realms above the material universe, encompassing various orders of divine and semi-divine creatures.

name: Celestial Guardians
description: Celestial Guardians are twice-trinitized beings who serve as officers of the courts of the Ancients of Days in the superuniverse, functioning as court messengers, apprehending agents, and bearers of summonses and decisions. They are the personality embodiment of single divine ideas or universal ideals, possessed of touching affection, supreme loyalty, and exquisite intelligence regarding their one specialized concept. Nearly seven billion strong in Orvonton, they are assisted by ascending Son-fused and Spirit-fused mortals in carrying out superuniverse administrative duties.

name: celestial influences
description: Influences originating from or associated with celestial beings and realms that affect spiritual development and cosmic relationships.

name: celestial musicians
description: Celestial musicians are a specialized group of celestial artisans who create celestial harmony through the manipulation of spiritual forces including spiritual sound, light, energy impingements, color symphonies, harmony of associated spirits, melody of thought, and the music of space. They produce melodies that can be broadcast throughout the universe and represent the highest forms of morontia and spirit musical expression.

name: Celestial Overseers
description: Celestial Overseers are a recruited corps of over three million volunteers in Nebadon who serve as educational advisers and inspectors of the local universe school system. They are qualified by experience to oversee the mind training and spirit education of ascending mortals, operating from headquarters on the Salvington worlds of the Melchizedeks.

name: Celestial Recorders
description: Celestial Recorders are ascendant seraphim who execute all superuniverse records in duplicate, creating both original spirit recordings and semimaterial counterparts through their ability to manipulate both spiritual and material energy simultaneously. They are trained by Divine Counselors and Perfectors of Wisdom, with never a falsification discovered in their records nor any defection from their ranks.

name: celibate orders
description: Celibate orders are religious or social groups of men and women who abstain from marriage and sexual relations, often started and maintained by individuals lacking normal sexual urges. These orders arose in various cultures as part of religious practice, though they were sometimes influenced by negative attitudes toward women.

name: cells
description: Cells are the basic biological units of material life, consisting of protoplasmic associations of chemical, electrical, and other basic energies. When injured, cells possess the ability to elaborate chemical substances that stimulate healing processes and activate neighboring cells to facilitate wound repair and cellular reproduction.

name: Celsus
description: Celsus was a believer and host of Jesus at Caesarea Philippi who provided lodging for Jesus and the apostles on multiple occasions during their ministry. The apostles stayed overnight at his home following significant events, including after Jesus' transfiguration on Mount Hermon.

name: Celta
description: Celta was the daughter of a Roman centurion who became one of the twelve women commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom. She served as part of the women's evangelistic corps and was present at Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance in Jerusalem.

name: cemetery
description: A cemetery is a burial ground where the dead are interred. Jewish law strictly prohibited burying crucified persons in Jewish cemeteries, leading to Jesus being buried in Joseph of Arimathea's private family tomb instead of a public cemetery.

name: Cenozoic
description: The Cenozoic era, also known as the mammalian era or recent-times era, spans the last fifty million years of Earth's geological history and continues to the present day.

name: censorship
description: The practice of controlling or restricting information, with the understanding that ultimately nothing will remain hidden forever, as all things will eventually be revealed.

name: census
description: A counting or enumeration of people or possessions, which ancient peoples considered unlucky and opposed, believing it was dangerous to 'number the people.'

name: Census Directors
description: Special beings created by the Infinite Spirit who maintain perfect records of all will creatures throughout the universe, stationed on superuniverse capitals and local universe headquarters to register births and deaths of intelligent beings.

name: centaurs
description: Mythological creatures regarded as half human and half animal that were objects of worship among early peoples who revered animals for their power and cunning.

name: Center of Centers
description: A designation referring to the Universal Father as the absolute center of all reality and existence.

name: centipedes
description: Multi-legged arthropods that evolved early alongside starfish, sea urchins, and other primitive creatures, representing one of the stationary branches of the evolutionary tree that failed to progress significantly.

name: Central America
description: A region where amalgamated races migrated and established civilizations, particularly by predominantly red peoples mixed with yellow, orange, and blue racial elements.

name: central universe
description: The eternal universe of Havona, the perfect central creation that exists at the heart of all reality, surrounded by the seven superuniverses.

name: centurions
description: Roman military officers who commanded units of approximately one hundred soldiers in the Roman army.

name: cephalopods
description: Cephalopods, ancestors of modern nautiluses, octopuses, squids, and cuttlefish, were well-developed marine creatures that became dominant sea predators after the decline of trilobites. Some cephalopods grew to impressive sizes, up to fifteen feet long, and over a thousand species of ammonites evolved on the Pacific coast of California, showcasing their remarkable diversity and evolutionary success as masters of the ancient seas.

name: cereals
description: Cereals were abundant seed-bearing grasses during the Cretaceous period, representing a major evolutionary development in plant life alongside fruits and other flowering plants that would fundamentally transform the plant world.

name: ceremonies
description: Ceremonies are ritualistic religious observances that evolved from primitive placation and propitiation practices into complex forms of worship involving sacrifice, atonement, and redemption. These formal religious rituals, while intended to enhance spiritual connection, often became substitutes for genuine religious experience and were frequently dominated by priests who used ancient languages and mystifying practices to maintain their authority.

name: certainty
description: Certainty represents different levels of conviction: physical certainty based on scientific logic, moral certainty rooted in philosophical wisdom, and spiritual certainty arising from genuine religious experience. True spiritual certainty, possessed by God-knowing believers, transcends doubt and provides unshakable confidence in divine truth, while remaining tolerant of honest inquiry and criticism.

name: Ceylon
description: Ceylon was an island where Buddhism was established by the missionary efforts of Emperor Asoka and where the fundamentalist Hinayana division of Buddhism persisted, clinging to the early asocial doctrine of Siddhartha. It was also settled by some of the least progressive Saharan groups who migrated through Arabia, Mesopotamia, and India.

name: Chaldeans
description: The Chaldeans were an advanced eastern Semitic people near Ur who practiced star worship, believing themselves to be children of the sky father and earth mother. They were among the first to abandon human sacrifice for ordinary occasions, recognized women's rights in marriage contracts, and developed astronomical concepts including placing the sun at the center of seven universal circles.

name: chalk
description: Chalk formations from the Cretaceous period were created by prolific foraminifers and other marine organisms, giving this geological epoch its name. These deposits, along with limestone, reached enormous thicknesses and today can be found elevated to great heights in mountain ranges like the Alps, Himalayas, and around various coastlines worldwide.

name: challenges
description: Challenges are difficulties and uncertainties that test human character and faith, which should be accepted without complaint and faced without fear. While challenges may defeat the fearful and mediocre, they serve to stimulate and strengthen the true children of God, promoting spiritual growth and development through adversity.

name: chamber of hewn stone
description: The chamber of hewn stone was the regular meeting place of the Sanhedrin in the temple, where this Jewish governing council normally conducted its official proceedings and deliberations.

name: chance
description: Chance is a term used to describe seemingly random or accidental occurrences that primitive peoples attributed to supernatural forces or luck. However, true chance or accident is incompatible with the intelligent design evident in creation, as demonstrated by thousands of scientific facts that reveal purposeful planning rather than random occurrence in the universe.

name: Chang
description: Chang was a Chinese merchant whom Jesus encountered in Corinth during his Mediterranean journey. Jesus had many visits with Chang and, upon their farewell, advised him to worship only God as his true spiritual ancestor and to follow the leadings of the Father's spirit within him.

name: change
description: Change is a fundamental aspect of reality and growth, but should not be attempted suddenly in social or economic matters. The Urantia Book teaches that while human life consists of endless change unified by unchanging personality, great reforms require time and wisdom. Values can never be static, and change without growth or expansion of meaning is potentially evil.

name: chanting
description: Chanting was one of the primitive healing techniques used by early peoples to treat disease, along with howling, laying on of hands, and breathing on patients. These methods were employed before the development of more sophisticated medical practices.

name: character
description: Character represents the accumulated consequences of personal decisions illuminated by worship, love, and service. It consists of the correlation of factual science, moral philosophy, and genuine religious experience. Character is acquired through enlightened experience and grows through the wise utilization of life's trials and opportunities, forming the foundation for spiritual development.

name: Charax
description: Charax was a city on the Persian Gulf where Jesus bid farewell to his Indian friends Gonod and Ganid in December of A.D. 23, marking the end of their Mediterranean journey together. From Charax, Jesus returned to Palestine via Babylon and Damascus.

name: charcoal picture
description: Jesus drew a charcoal portrait of his teacher on the floor of the Nazareth synagogue schoolroom, which caused a significant controversy among the Jewish elders. This incident led to serious accusations against Jesus and ultimately resulted in restrictions on his artistic activities at home.

name: charity
description: Charity refers to the giving of alms or assistance to those in need. Jesus taught that good deeds should be done in secret, with the left hand not knowing what the right hand does. The Urantia Book warns that both private and public charity can become pernicious when extended long-term to able-bodied citizens, and emphasizes the importance of maintaining dignity and self-respect in assistance programs.

name: charms
description: Charms were magical objects believed by primitive peoples to possess supernatural powers for protection, healing, or influence. Made from diverse materials including human hair, animal parts, and personal effects, charms were thought to ward off evil and bring good fortune, though The Urantia Book identifies such beliefs as gross superstition.

name: chastity
description: Chastity originally developed as part of property customs, initially applying only to married women. Paradoxically, in ancient times chastity in unmarried girls was considered a hindrance to marriage, as proof of fertility was preferred. Later, virginity became a commercial asset demanded by fathers and eventually led to extreme practices of confining girls to ensure their purity.

name: chazan of Nazareth synagogue
description: The chazan was the synagogue officer responsible for teaching children in Nazareth. He served as Jesus' teacher and mentor, taking special interest in his promising student's progress and spending extra time helping him master Hebrew scriptures. The chazan later counseled Jesus during nationalist controversies and inaugurated a young men's philosophical discussion club.

name: Chedorlaomer
description: Chedorlaomer was a tribal chief against whom Abraham won a victory, after which Abraham offered a conventional sacrifice despite Melchizedek's teachings against such practices.

name: cheek
description: The cheek represents Jesus' teaching about nonresistance, where he instructed his followers to turn the other cheek when struck, not in passive submission but as an active, positive response to lead others away from evil toward righteousness.

name: cheese
description: Cheese was a common food item that became part of human diet during early civilization, and Jesus learned to make cheese as a young boy while caring for his family's animals.

name: chemism
description: Chemism refers to chemical processes and reactions, being one of the fundamental material realities that, along with light, heat, electricity, magnetism, energy, and matter, are essentially one and the same thing in origin, nature, and destiny.

name: chemistry
description: Chemistry is the science that evolved from ancient alchemy through honest scientific inquiry, studying chemical reactions and the behavior of matter, though it cannot alone explain the emergence of life or consciousness from material processes.

name: Chemosh
description: Chemosh was the god of the Amorites whom the Hebrews acknowledged as real but considered subordinate to Yahweh in their developing monotheistic beliefs.

name: cherubim and sanobim
description: Cherubim and sanobim are the faithful and efficient assistants of seraphic ministers, functioning as energy-positive and energy-negative pairs respectively. They are the lowest order of angels, inherently near the morontia level of existence, and may eventually attain full seraphic status through service and experience.

name: Chicago
description: Chicago is referenced as "the city of this visitation" where the Urantia Book's revelators worked, and is used to illustrate the enormous energy value of sunlight, with a hypothetical daily sunshine bill exceeding 100 million dollars.

name: chickens
description: Chickens are used metaphorically to illustrate that forms serve their purpose in learning but become valueless once lessons are learned, just as no chick can exist without its shell, yet the shell has no worth after the chick hatches.

name: chief of Mystery Monitors
description: The chief of Mystery Monitors is the being who presides over the College of Personalized Adjusters on Divinington and serves as a close associate and supernal adviser to Master Spirit Number One.

name: chief of Nebadon archangels
aliases: chief of the archangels of Nebadon
Chief of the Archangels of Nebadon
description: The chief of Nebadon archangels is a high-ranking celestial being who led the council of archangels following Jesus' burial, ultimately concluding that they could do nothing to facilitate his resurrection. This archangel is also the author of Papers 33 and 35 in The Urantia Book.

name: chief of Nebadon Voices
description: The chief of Nebadon Voices is a primary seconaphim who possesses the remarkable ability to reflect Michael's voice and attitude to the Ancients of Days on Uversa without requiring Michael's physical presence, allowing instantaneous communication across space.

name: chief of Urantia cherubim
description: The chief of Urantia cherubim served as an advisor on the council of planetary receivers that administered Urantia's affairs following the Lucifer rebellion, working alongside Melchizedek receivers and other celestial beings.

name: chief of Urantia seraphim
aliases: Chief of Seraphim stationed on Urantia
description: The chief of Urantia seraphim is a primary supernaphim who commands the seraphic hosts ministering on Urantia, a position that rotates periodically among qualified beings. This being served on the advisory council to the Melchizedek planetary receivers following the rebellion and authored Papers 82-84, 113, and 114 of The Urantia Book.

name: chief priests
description: The chief priests were high-ranking religious officials in ancient Jewish society who held positions of authority in temple worship and religious governance, often serving as members of the Sanhedrin.

name: chief recorders
description: The chief recorders are tertiary supernaphim of Havona who maintain comprehensive records in triplicate: literal files for Havona, spiritual files for their order, and formal records for Paradise, while automatically transmitting important knowledge to Paradise's living libraries.

name: chief seat
description: The chief seat refers to the place of honor at a feast or gathering. Jesus taught that one should not presume to take the chief seat but rather choose a lower position, allowing the host to invite them to a place of honor, demonstrating humility over prideful self-promotion.

name: chief supernaphim
description: The chief supernaphim is the original pattern angel of Paradise who presides over the organization of all three orders of supernaphim: primary, secondary, and tertiary. This being serves as the supreme leader of all supernaphim throughout the universe.

name: chiefs of assignment
description: The chiefs of assignment are primary supernaphim who serve as administrators on Paradise, responsible for periodically dispersing worship assemblies, overseeing the placement of defeated Deity adventure candidates, and designating supernaphim to command seraphic hosts on reclaimed rebellious worlds like Urantia.

name: child-rearing
description: Child-rearing encompasses the comprehensive process of raising and training children, involving discipline, education, moral guidance, and spiritual development. The Urantia Book emphasizes that effective child-rearing requires both love and wisdom, with parents serving as examples of divine-human relationships while fostering the child's natural positive moral impulses and preparing them for cosmic citizenship.

name: childbirth
description: The process of bringing forth offspring, which was once viewed as rendering women dangerous and unclean in primitive societies. Among unmixed tribes, childbirth was comparatively easy, occupying only two or three hours, while mixed races experienced more difficulty. Early human women like Fonta did not suffer pain in childbirth, but later mixed races developed the severe pangs associated with delivery.

name: children
description: Young human beings who are wholly dependent on their parents and home life for all early concepts of intellectual, social, moral, and even spiritual matters. Children who die before the age of spiritual choice are repersonalized on finaliter worlds, while those reaching the age of choice must make their own spiritual decisions. Jesus emphasized that entering the kingdom of heaven requires becoming like a little child in faith and trust.

name: chimpanzees
description: Primates that, along with monkeys, baboons and gorillas, descend from a common ancestor with humans called the mid-mammals. However, while the pair of twins that gave rise to chimpanzees were of an inferior species, the human pair was of a higher type, representing divergent evolutionary paths from the same ancestral source.

name: Ch’in Shih Huang Ti
description: A Chinese ruler who unsuccessfully fought against Confucianism by proclaiming a brotherhood based on love rather than ethical duty. His imperial efforts to combat Confucian doctrines were ultimately defeated by the vigorous opposition of Confucian disciples.

name: China
description: A large nation in eastern Asia that is geographically protected by mountains to the west and the Pacific Ocean to the east. China has maintained one of the most continuous civilizations in human history, with the yellow race achieving remarkable cultural stability and social development over nearly forty thousand years.

name: China Seas
description: Maritime waters around China that Buddhist missionaries bravely crossed as they spread their faith throughout Asia. These seas presented significant dangers to the religious teachers who ventured across them in their mission to bring Buddhism to distant lands.

name: Chinese
description: The people of China, primarily of the yellow race, who developed one of the most enduring civilizations in human history. The Chinese were among the first to achieve racial solidarity and large-scale cultural, social, and political organization, with their civilization persisting for nearly forty thousand years despite various retrogressions.

name: chlorine
description: A chemical element that was mostly bound with metals in Earth's early oceans, leaving only small amounts combined with hydrogen to make the primitive waters slightly acidic. This early ocean was essentially fresh water rather than the salty seas of today.

name: chlorophyll making
description: The evolutionary development of the ability to produce chlorophyll, which was the most important step in plant evolution. This capability allowed plants to harness sunlight for photosynthesis, fundamentally transforming life on the planet.

name: choice
description: The capacity for decision-making that represents a fundamental aspect of human experience and spiritual development. The ability to choose between alternatives is essential for moral growth and the development of character, particularly in relation to spiritual values and divine will.

name: Chorazin
description: A city in Galilee where Jesus taught but met with general rejection of his message. Jesus pronounced woe upon its light-rejecting inhabitants, along with those of Bethsaida-Julias and Capernaum, saying these cities did not well receive his messengers.

name: chosen people
description: A religious concept claiming divine selection of a particular group. The Urantia Book teaches there is no such thing as a chosen people, as the rule of the Most Highs is designed for the greatest good of all mortals. This doctrine has historically led to racial superiority attitudes and religious intolerance.

name: Christ
description: Christ means "the anointed one" and refers to the divine Jesus who became the central figure of Christianity. While the human Jesus had a religion, the divine Christ became a religion. Christ Michael is also the title used for the Creator Son Michael of Nebadon after his bestowal on Urantia.

name: Christian church, early
description: The Christian church founded by Peter after Pentecost and developed by Paul's missionary efforts. It evolved from a Jewish sect into an organized religious institution, spreading throughout the Roman Empire by combining Jesus' teachings with Greek philosophy and pagan elements to appeal to gentile audiences.

name: Christianity
description: A religion that developed around the person of Jesus Christ, building on his life and teachings but incorporating Greek philosophy, Persian mysteries, and pagan elements. While it contains more of Jesus' gospel than any other religion, it also includes much that Jesus did not teach and has undergone extensive evolution, institutionalization, and sectarian division.

name: Christmas
description: A Christian celebration held on December 25th, which was originally the annual festival day of Mithras. Modern Christmas gift-giving practices echo ancient customs where people would distribute wealth to gain social recognition and status.

name: chromosomes
description: Hereditary units that carry genetic patterns in living organisms. On Urantia, human sex cells contain 48 units of pattern control, while the highest life forms on planets like ours are reproduced by life-carrying bundles with 24 pattern units. The Planetary Prince's staff had their chromosomes reorganized by Satania's life circuits.

name: chronoldeks
description: A specialized type of frandalank that measures time in addition to quantitative and qualitative energy presence. They serve as living time gauges and broadcast the standard time of systems. Master chronoldeks handle the time broadcasting function for their respective systems.

name: churches
description: Religious institutions that evolved from secret societies and early religious groups. While the true church is the invisible, spiritual brotherhood of believers characterized by unity, visible churches serve as social organizations that should support but not replace the spiritual kingdom of God.

name: Chuza
description: A steward of Herod Antipas and husband of Joanna, one of the twelve women evangelists commissioned by Jesus. Chuza informed Herod that Jesus posed no political threat and was only concerned with establishing a spiritual brotherhood, helping to protect Jesus from official interference.

name: Cicero
description: The famous Roman writer and orator who is held up as an example of the intellectual maturity Rome possessed to receive Christianity.

name: Cincinnati Island
description: A geographic region that, 260 million years ago during the Devonian period, remained elevated above water while much of western Europe, including the British Isles, was submerged under seas.

name: circles
description: The seven concentric and successively elevated residential areas on Jerusem for nonnative beings, including circles for the Sons of God, angels, Universe Aids, Master Physical Controllers, ascending mortals, courtesy colonies, and finaliters, each with distinctive architectural features and inhabitants.

name: circuit regulators
description: Unique morontia beings who coordinate physical and spiritual energy and regulate its flow into the segregated channels of the morontia spheres, functioning like living dynamos to transform space energies into materials used by morontia supervisors.

name: Circuit Supervisors
description: Universe Circuit Supervisors who oversee and manage the various energy and communication circuits that connect different levels of cosmic administration.

name: circuits
description: The universal energy and communication pathways that connect all creation back to the Father's presence on Paradise, including personality gravity, spiritual gravity, mind gravity, and material gravity circuits, as well as superuniverse and local universe circuits.

name: circumcision
description: An ancient religious rite that evolved from early sacrificial practices involving partial body sacrifice, later practiced as an initiation ceremony and adopted by various cultures including the Hebrew peoples.

name: circumspection
description: The quality of careful consideration and prudent judgment in one's actions and decisions, often associated with proper conduct and wisdom.

name: circumstances
description: The conditions and situations of existence which, though sometimes difficult or mysterious, work together for good according to divine providence, with seraphic beings able to manipulate planetary conditions to favorably influence human activities.

name: citadel of the spirit
description: The absolutely unassailable inner bastion of the human soul that remains secure when dedicated to the indwelling spirit of the eternal God, providing spiritual security even when all earthly things may crash.

name: cities
description: Urban settlements that concentrate populations for commerce, manufacturing, and cultural development. Cities multiply the power of their inhabitants for either good or evil, serving as centers where fine arts, scientific progress, and spiritual culture thrive best when supported by surrounding agricultural populations.

name: citizenship
description: The formal status of membership in a political community or state. In ancient Rome, the religious initiation of youths included their solemn consecration to state service, with oaths and admissions to citizenship being conducted as religious ceremonies.

name: citizenship registrars
description: Officials on Jerusem who compute mota status and assign suffrage qualifications to citizens based on certifications from Melchizedek examiners, Brilliant Evening Stars, and the twenty-four counselors regarding their morontia, spiritual, and social achievements.

name: citron
description: A citrus fruit considered by Jews to be the "paradise apple" or "forbidden fruit." Pilgrims carried citron branches in their left hands during the Feast of Tabernacles as part of the ceremonial procession to the temple.

name: City of David
description: Another name for Bethlehem in Judea, the birthplace of Jesus and the ancestral city of King David.

name: City of Judah
description: A small village located four miles west of Jerusalem in the hills, where Elizabeth and Zacharias lived and where John the Baptist was born. Mary visited Elizabeth there for three weeks before John's birth.

name: civil servants
description: Public officials who serve temporal government. Kingdom believers called to civil service should display enhanced citizenship traits through spiritual enlightenment, with their consciousness of sonship with God quickening their entire life of service.

name: civilization
description: The advanced stage of human social development characterized by organized government, cultural institutions, and technological progress. Civilization depends on the sagacious correlation of material wealth, intellectual greatness, moral worth, social cleverness, and cosmic insight, and represents society's evolution from primitive conditions toward higher levels of cooperative achievement.

name: clams
description: Bivalve gastropod mollusks that were present in ancient seas 310 million years ago and have persisted largely unchanged through the intervening millions of years, along with mussels, oysters, and scallops.

name: clans
description: Blood-tie groups within tribes formed around common interests such as shared ancestry, religious totems, language, dwelling places, enemies, or military experiences. Clans served valuable purposes in local self-government but delayed the growth of large nations, with clan loyalty often conflicting with broader territorial sovereignty.

name: classes, social
description: Social classes refer to hierarchical divisions within society based on economic, social, or cultural status.

name: Claudia Procula
description: Claudia Procula was the wife of Pontius Pilate who became a partial convert to Judaism and later a believer in Jesus' gospel. She asked Pilate not to arrest Jesus and sent him a message warning against harming this 'innocent and just man,' having suffered dreams about Jesus. After Pilate's death, she became prominently identified with spreading the gospel.

name: Claudius
description: Claudius was a wealthy Roman slaveholder with whom Jesus spoke, and after their conversation he freed 117 of his slaves.

name: Claudus
description: Claudus was a discouraged young man contemplating suicide whom Jesus encountered on the island of Malta. After their conversation, he became enthusiastic about life, later became a Cynic preacher, and eventually joined Peter in proclaiming Christianity in Rome and Naples before going to Spain to preach the gospel.

name: claws
description: Tiger claws were among the various items used to create magical charms in ancient times, along with human flesh, crocodile teeth, and other materials believed to possess supernatural powers.

name: clay
description: Clay was an important material in ancient times, used for writing tablets, pottery, and sculptures. The belief that humans were created from clay was nearly universal in the Eastern Hemisphere, and ancient Sumerian clay tablets describe earthly paradises and civilized life.

name: cleansing the temple
description: Jesus' cleansing of the temple was a decisive act against the commercialization of worship, where he drove out animals and overturned money-changers' tables, declaring the temple should be a house of prayer rather than a den of robbers. This action challenged the profitable business that had grown around temple sacrifices and currency exchange.

name: Clement
description: Clement was a prominent Christian teacher in Alexandria who was taught by Pantaenus and later taught Origen, helping to preserve Jesus' teachings in the early Christian schools.

name: Cleopas of Emmaus
description: Cleopas was a shepherd from Emmaus and partial believer in Jesus who, along with his brother Jacob, encountered the resurrected Jesus during his seventh appearance while walking on the road from Jerusalem.

name: cleverness
description: Cleverness refers to mental skill or ingenuity, but it is not a substitute for true character and should not be used to coerce people into spiritual beliefs through overpowering arguments or mental superiority.

name: climate
description: The long-term patterns of temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric conditions that influence weather phenomena and significantly impact the development and migration of civilizations. Climate is primarily influenced by land elevation, cosmic clouds, and oceanic conditions, and has been a decisive factor in human evolution and cultural advancement throughout history.

name: Cloa
description: One of the notable ancestral women in Mary's lineage, the mother of Jesus, who was descended from a long line of remarkable women including Cloa among others such as Annon, Tamar, Ruth, and Eve.

name: cloak
description: An outer garment worn in ancient times, including by Jesus, whose cloak was taken by Roman soldiers during his crucifixion. Jesus used the concept metaphorically when teaching that offering one's cloak when another garment is taken represents doing something positive to help the wrongdoer rather than seeking retaliation.

name: clock
description: A timepiece, specifically the radium clock which is the most reliable scientific instrument for estimating the age of planets through the study of radioactive elements, though such estimates tend to be conservative since they only measure surface materials.

name: Clopas
description: The husband of Mary, who was the sister of Jesus' mother and among those who stood near the cross during the crucifixion.

name: close calls
description: Near-death experiences that almost resulted in extinction, particularly referring to the numerous occasions when prehuman ancestors of humanity barely escaped death, including incidents involving Andon and Fonta as children and earlier evolutionary ancestors who survived lightning strikes and other dangers by mere margins.

name: clothing
description: Garments and coverings for the human body, ranging from early animal skins used by Andon and Fonta for warmth to woven materials taught by the Caligastia staff. Jesus' clothing was divided among Roman soldiers at his crucifixion, and he used clothing metaphorically in teachings about not mixing old and new approaches.

name: clouds
description: Atmospheric formations of water vapor that, along with land elevation and oceanic influences, are major factors in climate fluctuation. Primitive peoples often feared and worshiped clouds, rain, and hail, with the Neanderthalers being particularly afraid of mists and fogs.

name: clubs
description: Social organizations for fellowship and learning, including the young men's philosophical discussion club that Jesus joined in Nazareth, which met at members' homes and provided a venue for intellectual discourse and community engagement.

name: co-operation
description: The ability to work together effectively with others, which is one of the most important lessons learned during mortal existence. Cooperation becomes increasingly valuable as civilizations advance, eventually proving more efficient than competition in promoting social and spiritual progress.

name: Co-ordinate Creator Sons
description: An order of divine beings related to Creator Sons who serve in universe creation and administration.

name: Co-ordinate Ministering Daughters
description: An order of divine beings related to local universe Mother Spirits who serve in universe ministry and administration.

name: Co-ordinate Trinity-origin Beings
description: An order of divine beings who originate from the Paradise Trinity and serve coordinate functions in universe administration.

name: co-ordinators and liaison directors
description: Morontia Companions who serve as instructors of social conduct and morontia progress, facilitating intercourse among ascending mortals and preventing confusion through classes and group activities.

name: coabsolute level
description: A level of reality that exists between the absolute and finite levels in the cosmic hierarchy of existence.

name: coal
description: The water-preserved and pressure-modified remains of ancient vegetation that grew in bogs and swamps, often containing gas and oil, with deposits varying greatly in thickness across different regions.

name: coat
description: An outer garment worn for warmth or protection.

name: cock
description: A male bird, particularly a male chicken or rooster.

name: cockroaches
description: Insects that first appeared around 210 million years ago, with a thousand species developing during ancient times, some reaching lengths of four inches.

name: cocoa
description: One of the earliest pharmaceutical discoveries, valued along with quinine for its medicinal properties by ancient peoples.

name: coercion
description: The use of force or compulsion to achieve compliance, particularly in religious or spiritual matters. In primitive religious evolution, coercion was used as a technique to control spirits and win divine favor, progressing from early rituals of placation and exorcism. True spiritual guidance, however, is never achieved through coercion but through willing cooperation and divine leading.

name: coinage
description: The right to mint money, which according to a well-known Sanhedrin ruling carried with it the right to levy taxes. This principle was used to guide Jews living among gentile nations and was referenced in Jesus's teaching about rendering unto Caesar what is Caesar's.

name: coins
description: Metal currency pieces that have been objects of superstitious veneration throughout history. Even in modern times, superstitious people make holes in coins, similar to ancient practices where porous stones were believed to have healing properties.

name: cold
description: The absence of heat, representing comparative energy rest and the status of the universal force-charge of space. Cold stimulates human action, invention, and resourcefulness in open lands, while in cosmic terms it works with gravity to organize matter together, contrasting with heat which disrupts matter and dissipates energy.

name: collapse
description: The complete breakdown or failure of structure or function. If God were to retire as the upholder of all creation, a universal collapse would immediately occur, demonstrating the continuous divine support required for all reality to exist.

name: college of ethics, law, and Supremacy
aliases: college of the ethics of law and the technique of Supremacy
description: A Paradise training school for perfecting Technical Advisers, where they learn the ethics of law and the technique of Supremacy. This represents the final stage of education for Technical Advisers after completing training in local universe Melchizedek colleges and superuniverse courts.

name: College of High Ethics
description: The highest educational institution for universe administration, located on the Melchizedek home world and presided over by the original Father Melchizedek. This college attracts students and observers from throughout the local universes, with Nebadon's schools being particularly renowned for their practical experience with administrative challenges.

name: college of life planning
description: An educational institution located on World Number One in Jerusem, dedicated to studying universal life in all its known phases. This college functions with teachers and advisers from Uversa, Havona, and Paradise, and houses the seven central emplacements of the adjutant mind-spirits.

name: college of revealed religion
description: An educational institution that was part of the planned curriculum for the Caligastia one hundred, designed to teach revealed religious truth and spiritual principles.

name: college of self-evaluation
description: The first of more than thirty educational centers conducted by Melchizedek Sons on Jerusem. This college begins the comprehensive training program for mortal survivors that culminates in schools of Jerusem citizenship, preparing them for the responsibilities of representative government.

name: College of Wisdom
description: The highest educational institution for Teacher Sons located on Salvington, serving as the pinnacle of an extensive training program that ranges from planetary courses to this advanced college where recognition is granted to all who complete these adventures in wisdom and truth.

name: collisions
description: Astronomical events where space bodies crash into each other, common in regions of thicker clustering. These collisions can convert enormous masses of matter into rare forms of energy and often provide material for planetary formation, though they become rare once physical systems stabilize into balanced circuits.

name: colonization
description: A practice that originated from the ancient custom of exiling first-born sons instead of sacrificing them, allowing them to grow up before being sent away to establish new settlements. The Romans incorporated this custom into their systematic approach to colonization.

name: color workers
description: Celestial artisans who work with light and shade, serving as sketchers and painters who preserve passing scenes and transient episodes for future morontia enjoyment.

name: Colorado
description: A state in North America known for its Morrison fossil beds containing abundant fresh-water fossils, mountain peaks like Pikes Peak and Longs Peak that exemplify re-elevation after erosion, and the Florissant fossil beds containing ancient insect remains.

name: Colorado River
description: A major river in North America whose Grand Canyon contains fossiliferous rocks in multiple layers that provide clear evidence of ancient geological upheavals and surface fluctuations from primitive times.

name: colored races
description: The six evolutionary races of humanity that emerged following the Sangik mutation, distinguished by their distinctive skin pigmentation and representing different branches of human development on Urantia.

name: colors
description: There are seven colors in the natural spectrum, reflecting the sevenfold nature of creation. Celestial musicians create symphonies of color using morontia color tones, which rank among their highest artistic accomplishments.

name: coma
description: A state of unconsciousness associated with disease and accidents that ancients believed allowed the soul to leave the body. Several individuals thought to be dead by Jesus' followers were actually in comatose states, including Jairus's daughter and the widow's son at Nain.

name: combined controllers
description: Highly mechanical Morontia Power Supervisors stationed at the center of each administrative unit of morontia worlds. These beings are sensitive to physical, spiritual, and morontial energies and work with associated system coordinators, circuit regulators, and other specialized personnel.

name: comets
description: Celestial bodies that are often unestablished offspring of solar systems being gradually brought under control of central governing suns. Comets have tails that point away from attracting bodies due to electrical reactions of their expanded gases and pressure from light and solar energies. Some consist of meteor swarms, while others are disrupted smaller bodies of matter.

name: Comforter
description: A term referring to the Spirit of Truth, the divine presence promised to provide comfort and guidance to believers.

name: commandments
description: Divine injunctions and moral laws given throughout history, including the seven commandments of Dalamatia and Eden, Melchizedek's seven commands, Moses' ten commandments, and Jesus' supreme commandment to love God and one's neighbor. These represent progressive revelations of divine will, culminating in the universal mandate to be perfect as the Father in heaven is perfect.

name: commendation
description: The act of expressing approval or praise, particularly in recognizing worthy achievements or character.

name: commerce
description: The evolution of trade from early barter systems to organized commercial exchange. Commerce has been a great civilizer, promoting cross-fertilization of cultures and contributing to the advancement of civilization through international trade relationships and the exchange of goods, services, and ideas.

name: commission on industry and trade
description: One of the ten councils established by the Caligastia one hundred in Dalamatia, led by Nod, which fostered primitive industry within tribes and promoted trade between peace groups, contributing to elevated standards of living and the development of early commercial credit systems.

name: common sense
description: Practical wisdom and sound judgment. Jesus exemplified sanctified common sense in all his plans and ministry, being reasonable, practical, and free from erratic or eccentric tendencies.

name: communication
description: The process of sharing meanings and ideas between intelligent beings. Higher forms of human communication are enhanced by indwelling Thought Adjusters. The ability to communicate enables the accumulation of knowledge and wisdom across generations, forming the foundation of human culture and civilization.

name: communion (mass)
description: A religious ceremony or sacrament, related to the Christian practice of communion or mass, commemorating spiritual fellowship with the divine.

name: communion (spiritual)
description: The intimate fellowship and communication between the soul and divine spirit, representing the highest form of personal religious experience and connection with God.

name: communism
description: A primitive social system characterized by communal property and automatic economic adjustment. While preventing extreme poverty and want in early societies, communism ultimately gave way to higher social orders because it discouraged individual initiative, stifled ambition, and conflicted with human desires for family inheritance, religious expression, personal liberty, and security.

name: comparisons
description: Analogies used to illustrate cosmic proportions and relationships. Examples include the comparison of atomic structure to the solar system, where electrons orbit the nucleus with similar relative spacing to planets around the sun, and the vast spaces between stars being likened to oranges circulating within a hollow Earth.

name: compass
description: A directional instrument responsive to magnetic forces and solar influences. The compass needle points north-south due to universal directional currents inherent in Nebadon, and shows slight daily variations toward east at sunrise and west at sunset, with these movements being twice as pronounced during sunspot cycles.

name: compensations
description: Divine adjustments and equalizing techniques that ensure no being is deprived of essential experiences or unfairly disadvantaged in their spiritual journey. These include the Thought Adjuster's role in bridging the distance between mortals and God, enhanced opportunities for growth through trials, and the universe's provision of alternative paths and experiences for spiritual development.

name: competition
description: A driving force essential for early evolutionary progress but gradually displaced by cooperation as civilizations advance. While competition stimulates development and determines survival of the fittest groups and individuals, it becomes increasingly wasteful and destructive unless regulated, ultimately requiring transformation into service-motivated cooperation for sustained progress.

name: complaining
description: An attitude of dissatisfaction that should be overcome through spiritual growth and acceptance of life's challenges. True spiritual development involves accepting difficulties without complaint, recognizing that many afflictions result from personal choices, and finding strength through faith rather than expressing grievances about circumstances.

name: complement of being
description: The paired angelic associate of opposite energy type that usually works together with another angel. In seraphic pairs, one angel serves as the complement of being to the other, with cherubim and sanobim being inherently associated as positive and negative energy personalities who function most effectively together.

name: complemental relationships
description: Functional associations between different types of beings that enhance their effectiveness and provide cosmic balance. These relationships, such as between Creator Sons and Mother Spirits or between evolutionary and perfect creatures, may be transient or permanent and represent the natural pairing of complementary natures in universe administration.

name: complements of rest
description: Specialized supernaphim who assist ascending mortals during their final transition rest on Havona's inner circuit. These majestic beings focus on promoting understanding and association among diverse groups rather than formal training, preparing pilgrims for their eventual Paradise residence through experiential education.

name: completion seraphim
description: Angels who have attained Paradise, been admitted to the seraphic service of completion, and achieved experiential perfection. These highly advanced seraphim serve in exalted capacities throughout the universes, often returning to their native realms to complement divine ministry with their experiential wisdom, with many seeking assignment as bestowal attendants to incarnated Paradise Sons.

name: compossibility and omnipotence
description: A philosophical concept explaining that divine omnipotence includes inherent compatibility and logical consistency. God's omnipotence means the power to create things with specific natures and to establish the fundamental nature of all things, but does not include the ability to create logical contradictions or meaningless entities.

name: compound manipulators
description: A specialized group within the Celestial Artisans who work with the functional association of the three original phases of divine energy: physical, mindal, and spiritual energies. These adventurous beings seek to discover the universe presence of God the Supreme through their manipulative work with combined energy forms.

name: compromise
description: The practice of making temporal adjustments between competing loyalties or principles while maintaining spiritual integrity. Jesus taught that while humans must become adept at continuous temporal compromise in earthly matters, they should yield spiritual allegiance to only one master and never compromise spiritual ideals or noble aspirations.

name: compulsion
description: The use of force or coercion to achieve ends, which is contrary to spiritual methods. Jesus taught that righteous causes should never be promoted by force, as spiritual victories can only be won by spiritual power, and the Spirit's domination is never tainted with coercion or compromised by compulsion.

name: concentration
description: The ability to focus one's mental, spiritual, and physical energies on a specific task or objective. Jesus possessed exceptional concentration abilities, being able to mobilize all his powers of mind, soul, and body on immediate tasks and maintain deep focus on problems he wished to solve.

name: concentric circles, three
description: The symbol of three azure blue concentric circles on a white background, representing the material emblem of Trinity government and used as the banner of Michael. These circles are emblematic of the infinity, eternity, and universality of the Paradise Trinity, and were worn by Melchizedek as his insignia during his bestowal mission.

name: Concept recorders
description: A specialized group within the Celestial Artisans responsible for preserving concept pictures and idea patterns through permanent recording methods unknown on material realms. These recorders can capture and store conceptual information in ways far more efficient than ordinary written language.

name: concepts
description: Mental frameworks and understanding patterns that serve as scaffolding for creature thought, though they are always relatively true rather than absolute. Static concepts can become potentially evil by retarding progress, while living concepts remain dynamic and adaptable to expanding cosmic comprehension.

name: conciliating commissions
description: Traveling courts composed of four Universal Conciliators that adjudicate minor disputes and misunderstandings throughout the universes. Each commission includes a Judge-Arbiter, Spirit-Advocate, Divine Executioner, and Recorder, functioning with supreme authority to gather evidence and render final, unanimous decisions on matters of temporal importance.

name: conciliation (ceremony)
description: A religious observance that evolved as part of the progression from primitive placation and avoidance rituals to more sophisticated forms of worship. Conciliation ceremonies represented an intermediate stage in religious development, focusing on reconciling relationships between mortals and spiritual forces before advancing to sacrificial and redemptive practices.

name: Conciliators, Universal
description: An order of divine beings who serve as mediators and conflict resolvers in the universe administration.

name: conclaves
description: Formal assemblies or gatherings of divine beings, Creator Sons, and other celestial personalities held on Paradise and other administrative spheres for deliberation on universe affairs and spiritual progress.

name: concubinage
description: A historical marriage practice serving as a transitional step from polygamy to monogamy, where a man maintained one legal wife but could have sexual relations with multiple concubines, often handmaidens of the wife.

name: condemnation
description: The act of expressing disapproval or judgment against someone or something, often related to moral or spiritual wrongdoing.

name: conduct
description: The manner in which one behaves or acts, particularly in relation to moral and ethical standards.

name: conductors of worship
description: The first order of primary supernaphim on Paradise who teach ascendant creatures the proper techniques of worship, enabling them to express their spiritual emotions and appreciation more effectively and completely.

name: confession
description: The sincere acknowledgment and recognition of sin or wrongdoing, which is essential for religious growth and spiritual progress, though it does not eliminate the consequences of one's actions.

name: confidence
description: A state of trust, assurance, or belief in oneself, others, or in divine guidance and support.

name: confiding
description: The act of sharing personal thoughts, feelings, or secrets with another in a trusting manner.

name: conflict
description: A state of discord or struggle, often arising from the lack of harmony between moral consciousness and intellectual consciousness, which can inhibit spiritual growth but may also lead to new religious insights when resolved constructively.

name: Confucianism
description: A Chinese ethical and moral philosophy based on the teachings of Confucius, emphasizing that earthly civilization should mirror the eternal order of heaven. Confucianism became the moral foundation for almost a third of humanity, though it subordinated the God concept to the Way of Heaven and the pattern of the cosmos.

name: Confucius
description: A sixth-century Chinese philosopher and teacher who compiled the wise sayings of ancient philosophers and established moral teachings that became the foundation of Chinese culture. Though rejected during his lifetime, Confucius replaced magic with morality among the shamans and created a new reverence for ancestral conduct and order that profoundly influenced China and Japan.

name: confusion
description: A state of mental uncertainty that naturally precedes both growth and destruction in spiritual development. While confusion does not prevent survival or indicate resistance to divine guidance, it often arises from imperfect communications with Thought Adjusters and can lead to fanaticism or intellectual upheaval when spiritual revelations are misunderstood.

name: conglomerates
description: Sedimentary rock formations consisting of matter deposited near ancient shore lines. These geological formations alternate with coal layers, shale, and stone, representing accumulations from primitive marine environments when continental shores were submerged beneath the seas.

name: conifers
description: Cone-bearing trees that, 120 million years ago during the Cretaceous period, increasingly resembled modern varieties while coexisting with persistent ferns and pines.

name: conjectures
description: Theoretical suppositions or hypotheses formed without firm evidence, representing speculative thinking about uncertain matters.

name: Conjoint Actor
description: An alternate designation for the Infinite Spirit, the Third Person of the Paradise Trinity who acts in conjunction with the Universal Father and Eternal Son.

name: Conjoint Creator
description: Another title for the Infinite Spirit, emphasizing the Third Person's role as co-creator with the Universal Father and Eternal Son in the Paradise Trinity.

name: conjurers
description: Practitioners of black arts and supernatural contact who were classified alongside sorcerers, magicians, wizards, witches, enchanters, necromancers, and soothsayers in ancient times.

name: Connecticut
description: A northeastern American state where a great geological fault appeared 150 million years ago during the Cretaceous period, with one side eventually sinking two miles and creating significant geographical changes.

name: conquest
description: The act of defeating or overcoming through military force or other means of dominance.

name: conscience
description: A human psychic reaction that represents the sum total of moral and ethical content of one's cultural development, distinct from the voice of the indwelling Thought Adjuster. Conscience admonishes one to do right but is merely the criticism of oneself by one's own value-habits and personal ideals, while the Adjuster endeavors to reveal what truly is right.

name: consciousness
description: The state of intellectual awareness that ranges from basic creature awareness to self-consciousness, which includes recognition of personality actuality and the ability to recognize other personalities. Human consciousness rests upon electrochemical mechanisms while touching spirit-morontia energy systems above, and proceeds from fact to meaning to value in mortal experience.

name: conscription
description: Mandatory military service, as contrasted with voluntary enlistment. The question of conscription versus voluntary service does not factor into maintaining world peace, as nations will continue to fight regardless of how their armies are assembled.

name: consecration of choice
description: The affectionate dedication of the human will to doing the Father's will, representing man's choicest and only possible gift of true value to God. This consecration constitutes an expansion and glorification of will that raises creature will from temporal significance to communion with the spirit Father.

name: consequences
description: The results or outcomes of actions that belong to God's domain. While humans are responsible for their acts and decisions, the consequences of these choices are in God's hands and form part of the divine experiencing of the Supreme.

name: consideration
description: The quality of being thoughtful toward others, particularly regarding the limitations and inexperience of juniors and subordinates, which represents a divine injunction for those in positions of authority.

name: consolation
description: Comfort or solace provided to those experiencing distress, grief, or disappointment.

name: conspiracy
description: In spiritual context, the beneficial confederation of divine powers and spiritual forces within each mortal whose exclusive purpose is to effect final deliverance from material bondage and finite handicaps.

name: Constantine
description: The Roman emperor who became the first ruler to be won over to Christianity, representing a significant compromise between Christian leaders and paganism.

name: Constellation Centers
description: Ten Supreme Power Centers of the fifth order stationed in each constellation, functioning as living energy projectors that provide power lines for communication, transport, and the energizing of creatures dependent upon physical energy for life maintenance.

name: Constellation Father of Norlatiadek
description: The reigning Most High ruler of Norlatiadek constellation at any given time, one of three Vorondadek Sons who govern the constellation on a rotating basis.

name: Constellation Fathers
description: The reigning Most High ruler of each constellation, one of three Vorondadek Sons who govern constellations and serve as the head of government during their term of service.

name: constellations
description: Primary divisions of a local universe, each containing 100 local systems with approximately 100,000 inhabitable worlds, administered by three Vorondadek Sons called the Most Highs and functioning as autonomous legislative units with their own governmental systems.

name: consuls (universe)
description: Representatives of constellations to one another and to the universe headquarters, appointed by legislative decree and functioning only within the confines of their local universe, often served by Vorondadek Sons.

name: Consummator of Universe Destiny
description: An unrevealed deity figure who, along with God the Supreme and God the Ultimate, constitutes the Trinity Absolute and is involved with the consummation of universe destiny through acts that encompass infinite existential-experiential attainment.

name: contact personalities
description: Fewer than twenty specially prepared members of the Urantia reserve corps of destiny who are consciously aware of their role as intermediaries between superhuman intelligences and mortal beings, with midway creatures serving as essential contact guardians during such communications.

name: contempt
description: A charge of defiance against universe government, as when Adam and Eve were absolved of standing in contempt of universe government, or when Van formally indicted Caligastia, Daligastia, and Lucifer for contempt of Nebadon's sovereignty.

name: contention
description: Disagreements and misunderstandings that naturally arise when evolving individuals with the power of choice interact, requiring wisdom and discretion to resolve and the services of conciliating commissions for fair adjustment of honest differences of viewpoint.

name: contentment
description: A state of peaceful satisfaction and acceptance, related to happiness and spiritual well-being in the context of universe relationships and personal growth.

name: continence cult
description: A religious cult that originated as a ritual among soldiers before battle and later became practiced by religious ascetics or "saints." This cult viewed marriage as only a lesser evil than fornication and promoted sexual abstinence, significantly influencing Christianity through the Apostle Paul's teachings despite not being part of Jesus' original gospel.

name: continental drift
description: The gradual movement and separation of Earth's continental land masses over geological time periods, beginning approximately 750 million years ago when the first breaks occurred in the original single continental mass.

name: continental nation
description: An advanced civilization on a neighboring planet in the Satania system, numbering about 140 million people of mixed blue, yellow, and violet races. This nation occupies a continent about the size of Australia and has developed a superior representative government, advanced educational system, and progressive social institutions, serving as an example of what human civilization might achieve.

name: continents
description: The large land masses of Urantia that began as a single continental mass 950 million years ago and gradually separated through continental drift. The continents are lighter than the ocean floors, which keeps them above water, though they tend to creep toward the oceans due to differential pressure.

name: control and overcontrol
description: A concept relating to the proper balance between exercising necessary control and avoiding excessive or counterproductive overcontrol in various aspects of life and governance.

name: controversies
description: Disputes, debates, or disagreements that arise over differing viewpoints, beliefs, or interpretations of important matters.

name: conversion
description: The spiritual transformation process by which an individual changes from one set of beliefs or spiritual state to another, often involving a fundamental reorientation of faith and values.

name: convictions
description: Firmly held beliefs or principles that guide an individual's thoughts, decisions, and actions, often based on deep personal or spiritual certainties.

name: cooking
description: The preparation of food using fire and heat, first practiced regularly by Onagar's missionaries and taught by Prince's staff as a means of avoiding sickness and reducing the energy needed for digestion. Cooking enabled early humans to preserve strength for social and cultural development.

name: copper
description: A metal brought to Earth's surface through volcanic lava flows and water deposition. Copper serves biological functions in some sea creatures for oxygen transport and was among the first metals worked by the Andites, later being mixed with tin to create the harder bronze alloy.

name: coral
description: Marine organisms that evolved from lime-secreting algae and formed extensive reef formations throughout Earth's ancient seas, particularly during the Devonian period when coral reefs characterized the clear, shallow inland seas.

name: cords, knotted
description: An early form of written communication using knotted cords, representing one of the primitive stages in the evolution of writing before the development of symbolic alphabets.

name: Corinth
description: The second city in Greece and third in the Roman Empire after Alexandria and Rome, serving as the capital of the Roman province of Achaia. A major cosmopolitan commercial center where Jesus spent two months during his Mediterranean tour, meeting people from three continents.

name: Cornelius
description: A Roman centurion from Caesarea who was converted by Peter. His subordinate Anaxand had previously met Jesus and been moved by his words, later becoming the steward to Cornelius after both men joined the early Christian church.

name: cornerstone
description: A metaphorical reference from Jewish scripture about 'the stone which the builders rejected' becoming the cornerstone, used by Jesus to warn religious leaders about rejecting the gospel message.

name: corporeal staffs of Planetary Princes
description: A corps of one hundred volunteer ascending beings from system headquarters who accompany Planetary Princes to new worlds, serving as advisers and helpers in early race improvement. These materialized beings receive new physical bodies from Life Carriers and serve as the connecting link between the prince and world races.

name: corporeality error
description: A philosophical error shown in both materialism and pantheism regarding the relationship between personality and physical form. Materialism wrongly assumes personality ceases at bodily death, while pantheism incorrectly concludes God cannot be a person without a body.

name: Corps of Conjoint Trinitized Finaliters
description: One of the seven finaliter corps with headquarters on Vicegerington. Havona natives are received into this corps along with the Corps of Transcendental Finaliters and Corps of Havona Finaliters as supreme career destinations.

name: Corps of Havona Finaliters
description: One of the seven finaliter corps that represents one of the supreme career goals for Havona natives, alongside potential admission to other finaliter corps.

name: Corps of Light and Life
description: A Paradise corps where ascendant mortals of time temporarily serve while their ultimate finality destiny remains unknown, designed to prepare them for greater tests of trust and more sublime services of responsibility.

name: Corps of Mortal Finaliters
description: The Corps of Mortal Finaliters is one of seven finaliter corps comprising primarily glorified mortals who have ascended from evolutionary worlds and achieved Paradise. This self-governing body takes an oath of allegiance only to the Paradise Trinity and serves throughout the universes in various capacities, particularly on worlds settled in light and life, while their ultimate destiny remains unrevealed.

name: Corps of Paradise Finaliters
description: The Corps of Paradise Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps composed of beings who have achieved Paradise status. This corps is part of the mysterious ongoing mobilization of perfected and ascendant beings of time and space on Paradise.

name: Corps of Perfection
description: The Corps of Perfection is a local universe organization that serves as the eternal destiny of Spirit-fused ascenders who have achieved spiritual perfection but are not destined for the Paradise Corps of the Finality.

name: corps of permanent citizens
description: The corps of permanent citizens consists of various orders of beings who serve as stable, long-term inhabitants of administrative worlds throughout the local universes and superuniverses, providing continuity amid the constant changes of transient celestial ministers and mortal inhabitants.

name: Corps of Seraphic Completion
description: The Seraphic Corps of Completion is composed of angels who have traversed the circles of Seraphington, achieved Paradise, and attained the personal embrace of the Eternal Son. These completion seraphim serve in exalted capacities throughout the universes and as associates to high Paradise orders.

name: Corps of the Finality
description: The Corps of the Finality refers to the collective organization of the seven finaliter corps, representing the highest achievement of evolutionary and perfected beings in their eternal service to the Paradise Trinity.

name: Corps of Transcendental Finaliters
description: The Corps of Transcendental Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps based on Paradise, representing one of the three supreme career goals available to Havona natives who join in the same ratio as other finaliter organizations.

name: Corps of Trinitized Finaliters
description: The Corps of Trinitized Finaliters is one of the seven Paradise Corps of the Finality, serving as the supreme destiny for most creature-trinitized sons and certain Paradise Citizens who have achieved Trinity embrace.

name: Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny
description: The Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny is one of the seven finaliter corps, though their nature, composition, and purpose remain undisclosed in current revelations.

name: correctional rest
description: Correctional rest is a purifying process that Melchizedeks undergo when they have made errors in judgment during planetary missions, requiring instruction from the Creator Son to restore harmony before reinstatement to service on the third day.

name: cosmic citizenship
description: The spiritual recognition and acceptance of one's progressive obligations to the Supreme Being and membership in the universal community. Cosmic citizenship encompasses the realization of interdependence between evolutionary mortals and evolving Deity, representing the birth of cosmic morality and universal duty. It offers faith sons of God deliverance from personal and planetary isolation through spiritual socialization on a universe scale.

name: cosmic cloud
description: The great space blanket consisting primarily of modified atoms of calcium that permeates space throughout the superuniverse of Orvonton. This cosmic cloud represents minutely pulverized stone material that serves as the basic building matter for planets and spheres of space.

name: cosmic consciousness
description: The awareness and recognition of cosmic realities and universal principles that extends beyond purely material or local perspectives to encompass the broader spiritual and intellectual dimensions of universe existence.

name: cosmic force
description: The pre-energy manifestation of space potency that represents the first step in the individuation of primordial force-charge into physical energy forms. Cosmic force is one of the common names for absoluta and proceeds from Paradise as the source of all original force-energy that circulates throughout the master universe.

name: cosmic mind
description: The sevenfold diversified mind of time and space ministered by the Seven Master Spirits to the seven superuniverses. As a subabsolute manifestation of the mind of the Third Source and Center, cosmic mind encompasses all finite-mind levels and provides the intellectual foundation for recognizing physical causation, moral duty, and spiritual worship across the grand universe.

name: cosmic reserve corps
description: A specialized group of cosmic beings or personalities designated for particular universe service or emergency functions, likely related to the broader reserve corps organizations that serve various administrative and spiritual purposes throughout the universes.

name: cosmology
description: The study of the origin, structure, and destiny of the cosmos and universe reality. True cosmology requires understanding the correlation of origin, history, and destiny rather than approaching from simple to complex, and leads ultimately to the pursuit of divine reality values and God-consciousness. The cosmology presented in The Urantia Book is acknowledged as not inspired and destined to be outgrown.

name: consumados
description: A term referring to beings or entities that have achieved completion or consummation in their development or function, likely representing a class of perfected personalities in the universe hierarchy.

name: cosmos absolute
description: A conceptual designation for the absolute level of cosmic reality, representing the ultimate expression of universe existence beyond finite and even ultimate levels of manifestation.

name: cosmos-infinite
description: An illimitable cosmos conceived to exist beyond the master universe where the Absolute Trinity may achieve final expression in the creations of infinity. This limitless, boundless, never-beginning, never-ending existence represents the absolute actualization of all potentials, though its comprehension is beyond mortal ability.

name: council of elders
description: The first real governmental body in primitive human societies, composed of distinguished older men who possessed wisdom and experience. This regulative group initially held all governmental functions - executive, legislative, and judicial - before their powers were gradually concentrated in absolute monarchs.

name: Council of Equilibrium
description: A governing body comprised of Associate Master Force Organizers dispatched by the Seven Master Spirits, responsible for directing and distributing Master Physical Controllers throughout the superuniverse. They are empowered to interpret the readings of master frandalanks and authorize the dispatch of force organizers for new material creations.

name: council of perfection
description: A Paradise council where Universal Conciliators are translated and trained before emerging as a coordinating corps evolved by the Infinite Spirit for the universe of universes.

name: council of Planetary Princes
description: A system council of world rulers presided over by the System Sovereign, consisting of Planetary Princes and resident governors general of isolated worlds. This planetary council assembles periodically at the system headquarters.

name: council of seven
description: An ancient tribal government structure of the red race that preserved the teaching of Onamonalonton, following the unanimous rule of seven members.

name: council of seventy
