description: A council of seventy members selected from evolutionary beings at constellation headquarters, analogous to the council of twenty-four counselors, that provides advice to planetary administration.

name: council on food and material welfare
description: One of the specialized councils within the Caligastia one hundred, responsible for overseeing food production and material welfare of early human civilization.

name: counsel
description: The fifth adjutant mind-spirit that provides guidance, advice, and wise judgment to evolutionary beings, enabling them to make thoughtful decisions and seek appropriate guidance.

name: counseling the rich man
description: Jesus' advice to a wealthy Roman citizen and Stoic about the proper use of material wealth, emphasizing its role as a trust for enhancing material, intellectual, social, and spiritual life for current and future generations.

name: counselors and advisers
description: Secondary supernaphim of the second Havona circle who serve as instructors for ascending mortals regarding their eternal career. They provide counsel to successful Paradise pilgrims and comfort to those who initially fail in Deity attainment, preparing both groups for their next stages of eternal progression.

name: counterirritants
description: Medicinal treatments used by ancient peoples to cure disease, involving substances that cause irritation or inflammation to counteract illness, often used alongside fasting and dieting.

name: counterpart
description: The spiritual equivalent or reflection of material experiences and wisdom, revealed by Thought Adjusters to help mortals understand cosmic truths and by Trinity Teacher Sons who demonstrate spiritual realities corresponding to material knowledge.

name: counting the cost
description: A discourse delivered by Jesus at the ford of Bethany emphasizing the importance of full dedication to God's will and being prepared to sacrifice everything, including family and life itself, to follow him as a disciple.

name: courage
description: The confidence that comes from thoroughgoing honesty about one's beliefs, representing the heart of Jesus' teachings and essential for spiritual growth, problem-solving, and the pursuit of truth.

name: court advisers
description: Supreme seraphim who serve as advisers to all orders of adjudication, ensuring that charges against mortal creatures are stated in justice and adjudicated in mercy, often serving as defenders of mortals in judicial proceedings.

name: court of gentiles
description: The ornate outer court of the Jerusalem temple where non-Jews were permitted, serving as a marketplace for sacrificial animals and money-changers, which Jesus found irreverent and inappropriate for his Father's house.

name: court of Israel
description: A courtyard in the Jerusalem temple where Jewish men could gather to observe religious ceremonies and sacrifices, positioned between the court of women and the priests' court.

name: court of priests
description: The innermost court of the Jerusalem temple located beneath a rock ledge in front of the temple where the altar stood and priests performed animal sacrifices and ritual washings.

name: court of women
description: A designated area in the Jerusalem temple where women were permitted to gather for worship, representing the furthest point women could enter in the temple complex due to gender restrictions of the time.

name: courtesans
description: High-class prostitutes or companions who provided entertainment and companionship, often associated with wealthy or influential men in ancient societies.

name: courtesy
description: A social virtue involving respectful and considerate behavior toward others. Jesus instructed his followers to avoid unnecessary waste of time in social ceremonies while enjoining courtesy toward all people they encountered. Originally, courtesy developed as an attempt to deceive envious spirits by showing humility and restraint.

name: courtesy colonies
description: Special residential communities found on headquarters worlds from local systems to superuniverse capitals, housing seven distinct groups of beings engaged in various missions and assignments. These colonies include Star Students, Celestial Artisans, Reversion Directors, Extension-school Instructors, Various Reserve Corps, Student Visitors, and Ascending Pilgrims, all working together in the furtherance of universe administration and education.

name: courts
description: Judicial institutions that evolved from primitive regulated combat between disputants to formal legal proceedings. The first courts were merely refereed fights where judges served as umpires, but they gradually developed into systems of verbal arguments and formal trials with established procedures for determining justice and settling disputes.

name: courtship
description: The process of romantic pursuit and relationship development between potential marriage partners.

name: covenants
description: Sacred agreements or contracts between divine beings and humans that represented a major advancement in religious development. In antiquity, gods were conceived as entering into real agreements with mortals, with law and covenant replacing luck, fear, and superstition as the foundation of religious relationship.

name: cowardice
description: The quality of lacking courage or being excessively fearful when facing challenges or danger.

name: coyotes
description: Wild canines that certain North American Indian tribes believed were among their ancestral origins. These primitive peoples practiced selecting animals of their supposed ancestry as totems, with some tribes teaching they descended from beavers and coyotes.

name: Crab nebula
description: An irregular nebular formation that originated from a stellar collapse approximately 900 years ago. The nebula still exhibits its mother sphere as a lone star near the center of the irregular nebular mass, representing the residual cooling sun surrounded by extensive clouds of nebular gases.

name: crabs
description: Marine crustaceans that matured and became prominent in ocean life about 110 million years ago alongside lobsters and other modern crustacean types. Despite threats from ferocious sea serpents that endangered fish families, crabs continued to thrive and evolve as part of the diverse marine ecosystem.

name: cradle
description: A metaphorical reference to the hand that rocks the cradle, symbolizing women's enduring influence as moral standard-bearers and spiritual leaders who continue to shape human destiny.

name: craftworkers of color
description: Celestial beings who create ten thousand color tones of spirit reflection that convey harmonious messages of beauty, representing activities beyond human experience in color perception.

name: creation
description: The cosmic process beginning with the personification of the Infinite Spirit and encompassing all reality differentiation from the theoretical I AM through the establishment of finite universes, local creations, and evolutionary worlds.

name: creationism
description: The belief in fiat creation of the human race that leads to discouraging outlooks predicated on retrogression rather than progression, implying a vengeful Deity who punished humanity for planetary administrative errors.

name: Creative Daughters
description: Divine beings associated with the Creative Spirits and local universe administration, serving as partners in the creative work of local universes.

name: Creative Mother Spirits
description: The local universe manifestations of the Infinite Spirit who serve as creative partners with Creator Sons in organizing and administering local universes.

name: Creative Spirits
description: Divine beings who participate in the creative and administrative functions of local universes, working in association with Creator Sons.

name: creativity
description: The divine capacity for bringing forth new realities that can be spontaneous or controlled, constructive or destructive, manifesting in both divine creation and human creative expression that aspires to be Godlike.

name: Creator Sons
description: Paradise Sons of God who are created by the Universal Father and Eternal Son to organize and rule local universes, each unique in nature and undergoing seven creature bestowals to earn supreme sovereignty over their domains.

name: creatorship
description: The aggregate of God's acting nature rather than a single divine attribute, representing the universal function of creation that is conditioned by all divine attributes and culminates in the Fatherhood of God.

name: creature-trinitized sons
description: Creature-trinitized sons are unique beings created by the trinitizing union of finaliters and Paradise-Havona personalities, endowed with personality by the Universal Father. These beings represent concepts and experiences pertaining to future universe ages and become wards of the Architects of the Master Universe, with their supreme destiny being entrance into the Corps of Trinitized Finaliters.

name: creatures
description: Creatures are the diverse forms of intelligent life throughout the universes, ranging from material beings to spiritual entities, all created and sustained by God. They include mortals, celestials, and numerous other orders of beings who inhabit the material and spiritual realms of creation.

name: creeds
description: Formal statements of religious beliefs or doctrines that systematize and codify spiritual teachings into organized sets of principles.

name: cremation
description: Cremation is the practice of burning the dead, which became widespread among certain ancient peoples. The Danubians practiced cremation as part of their mother worship rituals, while the Nodites encouraged it to combat cannibalism, and it was nearly universal in Scandinavia for thousands of years.

name: Cretaceous
description: The Cretaceous period was a geological era from 100 to 50 million years ago characterized by the dominance of flowering plants, the appearance of true birds, and the decline of reptiles. It marked the end of continental drift and witnessed significant volcanic activity and mountain building.

name: Crete
description: Crete was an island settled around 12,000 B.C. by a brilliant tribe of Andites who established an advanced civilization skilled in crafts and agriculture. It became the center of the mother cult that glorified Eve and served as a cultural hub before its inhabitants eventually migrated to Greece and other regions.

name: crickets
description: Crickets were among the early insects that first appeared about 210 million years ago, along with spiders, scorpions, cockroaches, and locusts, quickly spreading across the continents during this period of rapid insect evolution.

name: crime
description: Crime represents violations of social order and law, originally conceived as assaults upon tribal customs and taboos. Primitive peoples often used supernatural methods like ordeals to detect criminals, while modern civilized worlds have developed more rational systems of justice and rehabilitation.

name: criminals
description: Criminals are individuals who violate the laws and moral codes of society. On advanced worlds, they are often rehabilitated through agricultural colonies or detention centers, while the most dangerous habitual offenders may face capital punishment for serious crimes like murder and treason.

name: crinoids
description: Crinoids, also known as stone lilies, were ancient marine echinoderms that flourished in prehistoric seas and contributed significantly to limestone formation through their skeletal remains.

name: cripples
description: In primitive societies, physically disabled children including those who were hunchbacked and crippled were regarded as fetishes, believed to possess special spiritual significance or powers.

name: crisis
description: A critical moment or emergency situation that tests character and spiritual choosing, during which the mind's influence in spiritual decisions is demonstrated. In times of crisis, individuals may hear the Thought Adjuster's direct voice, and ideas, motives, and longings of a lifetime are openly revealed.

name: Crispus
description: The chief ruler of the Corinthian synagogue who was married with five children. He met Jesus during his travels without knowing his identity, and later when Paul preached in Corinth, Crispus embraced Christianity with his entire family and became one of the chief supports of the Christian church.

name: criticism
description: The practice of fault-finding or expressing disapproval, which can be constructive or destructive. Constructive criticism helps religion grow and develop, while habitual criticism of others can be overcome through establishing the practice of praying for the person being criticized.

name: Cro-Magnons
description: A brave and farseeing people resulting from the mixing of Andites from Eden with higher types of the blue race. They were skilled artists, hunters, and warriors who maintained efficient child culture systems, but were eventually absorbed by later Andite invasions and formed the biological foundation for modern European races.

name: crocodiles
description: Marine reptiles that evolved as a reversion from land-type reptiles. Unlike sea serpents, crocodiles always returned to land to lay their eggs and have survived as one of the remaining groups from early reptilian families.

name: cross
description: The crossbeam that condemned criminals were forced to carry to their crucifixion site. As a symbol, the cross represents the supreme devotion and love of Jesus, demonstrating God's love for humanity and serving as an eternal symbol of the triumph of love over hate and truth over evil.

name: crossbreeding
description: The mating between different racial strains, which in modern times often involves inferior strains and produces unsatisfactory offspring. Present-day prejudice against mixed races arises because contemporary crossbreeding typically occurs between the grossly inferior strains of the races concerned.

name: crossing fingers
description: A gesture or practice involving the crossing of fingers, though no specific context or significance is provided in the available source material.

name: crossroad
description: A metaphorical point of decision or choice in life's journey where the Spirit of Truth provides guidance, speaking to say 'This is the way' during moments of uncertainty or spiritual struggle.

name: crown of thorns
description: A crown made of woven thorns placed on Jesus' head during his scourging before Pilate, as part of the Roman soldiers' mockery of him as 'king of the Jews.'

name: crucifixion
description: A Roman method of execution involving nailing or binding victims to a cross, designed to provide cruel and lingering punishment. It was not used by Jews and was forbidden for Roman citizens, being reserved for slaves and subject peoples. The method was learned from the Phoenicians and could result in death taking several days.

name: cruelty
description: Brutal or barbarous treatment that Jesus opposed, particularly when manifested through pride, oppression, and hypocrisy. It represents an evil tendency in half-civilized humans who seek to harm those superior in wisdom and spiritual attainment.

name: crumbs
description: Small pieces of food that fall from a table, referenced in Jesus' parables and teachings about the beggar Lazarus desiring crumbs from the rich man's table, and in the Syrophoenician woman's humble request for healing, comparing herself to a dog seeking crumbs from the children's table.

name: crusader
description: A zealous religious warrior spirit that can emerge from true religion but becomes dangerous when not controlled by spiritual insight and loyal devotion to commonplace social obligations.

name: crust (earth’s)
description: The solid outer layer of the planet Earth.

name: crustaceans
description: Marine arthropods including crabs, lobsters, shrimps, and their prehistoric ancestors like trilobites. They were ancestors of the first vertebrates and reached their modern forms around 110 million years ago, with trilobites being particularly prominent in early seas before becoming extinct.

name: crystals
description: Crystalline materials used extensively in the construction of buildings on Jerusem and other architectural worlds, including walls made of crystal gems, gates of pearly crystals, and entire structures of transparent crystal.

name: cult of taking no life
description: A religious movement that arose in India during dark days of spiritual despair, advocating against the taking of any life, which has persisted ever since its emergence.

name: cults
description: Organized religious groups with established rituals, symbols, and ceremonies. While they can preserve sentiment and promote loyalty, cults are formed to promulgate creeds rather than discover truth, and their survival depends largely on intelligent leadership. They serve as the skeletal structure around which personal spiritual experience grows.

name: culture
description: The development and expression of human social organization, customs, knowledge, and artistic achievement within a society or group of people.

name: cumin
description: A spice mentioned by Jesus when he criticized religious leaders for meticulously tithing minor herbs like mint, anise, and cumin while neglecting weightier matters of faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: cup
description: A symbolic term frequently used by Jesus to refer to his impending suffering and crucifixion. Jesus spoke of drinking the cup of bitterness that was prepared for him, representing his willingness to accept the Father's will despite the difficulty of his mortal experience.

name: cup of remembrance
description: A symbolic cup that Jesus instituted during the Last Supper, representing the emblem of the bestowal and ministry of the divine Spirit of Truth and marking the beginning of a new dispensation of grace and truth.

name: curiosity
description: The spirit of investigation, the urge of discovery, and the drive of exploration that is part of the inborn and divine endowment of evolutionary space creatures. Curiosity represents the hunger for harmony and thirst for beauty that motivates beings throughout their eternal career of adventure and discovery.

name: cursing
description: Originally a coercive practice designed to overawe minor spirits in primitive times, cursing evolved from ritualistic repetition of holy names. Modern profanity represents a degraded form of this ancient spiritual practice.

name: curvature
description: A philosophical concept representing the essential nature of all relation concepts, requiring philosophy to account for the elliptic symmetry of reality rather than projecting interpretations in a purely linear fashion of logic.

name: custodians (Lanonandek)
description: A group of one hundred thousand Lanonandek Sons who serve as custodians and archivists in Salvington, maintaining records and administrative functions for the local universe.

name: custodians (Life Carrier)
description: The third division of Life Carriers who specialize in the conservation and protection of life. They are experts in handling basic life patterns and serve as commissioners for life-conservation plans on new worlds, with twenty-four custodians assigned to Urantia to oversee the fundamental patterns of life organization.

name: custodians of knowledge
description: Primary supernaphim who serve as living repositories of universal knowledge on Paradise. They are perfect beings in whom the facts of all universes are inherently recorded, functioning as living libraries that organize and classify knowledge into seven grand orders and serve as exalted teachers of the central universe.

name: Custodians of Records
description: Custodians of Records are senior tertiary supernaphim chosen from the chief recorders of Havona to serve as keepers of the formal archives of Paradise. They maintain the official records that contrast with the living records held in the minds of the custodians of knowledge, and some serve as Chiefs of Records directing the activities of Celestial Recorders in the superuniverses.

name: customs
description: Social practices and traditions passed down through generations within cultures and civilizations.

name: Cuthites
description: A people whose descendants were among those installed by Sargon, king of Assyria, in central Palestine to replace the twenty-five thousand Jews of the northern kingdom of Israel who were carried away into captivity around 700 B.C.

name: Cutites
description: One of the groups that occupied the first Garden of Eden after Adam vacated it, along with the Nodites and Suntites.

name: cuttlefish
description: Marine cephalopods that evolved from earlier ancestors and use copper instead of iron to oxygenate their blood, representing an alternative biological solution to oxygen transport in living organisms.

name: Cybele
description: The mother goddess in the Phrygian mystery cult, revered as the mother of Attis whose death and resurrection formed the central ritual of their religious observances.

name: Cymboyton
description: A wealthy merchant from Urmia who built a temple dedicated to universal religion and exercised unchallengeable authority over the religionists there. He lived and died without ever revealing his personal beliefs, and after his death his sons encountered great difficulties maintaining peace in the religious community.

name: cynicism (attitude)
description: A skeptical and distrustful attitude toward human nature and motives, often expressed through mockery or contempt.

name: Cynicism (religion)
description: A religio-philosophical movement that preserved remnants of Melchizedek's teachings, characterized by wandering preachers who taught simplicity, virtue, and fearless acceptance of death. They traced their philosophy to Diogenes of Athens and made their doctrine democratic and accessible to common people.

name: Cynics
description: Preachers of faith and trust in God who maintained Salem teachings in relatively pure form, functioning as wandering teachers throughout the Roman Empire. They were among the last defenders of Melchizedek's gospel and were later incorporated into the newly forming Christian religion.

name: Cyprus
description: An island in the Mediterranean Sea where Jesus and Ganid had extended conversations about the mind, and later where the apostle Thomas preached. Around 5000 B.C., progressive Mesopotamians settled there, establishing a civilization that was destroyed around 3000 B.C. by barbarian invasions from the north.

name: Cyrene
description: A city in northern Africa where Jesus and Ganid gave first aid to an injured boy named Rufus, whose father Simon later carried Jesus' cross to Golgotha by Roman order. Simon's sons Alexander and Rufus became effective teachers of the gospel in Africa.

name: Cyrus
description: A historical figure mentioned as an example of the ancient tradition of the divinely preserved disowned child, along with Sargon, Moses, and Romulus.

name: Dabaritta
description: A town in Galilee that Jesus, the twelve apostles, and 117 evangelists visited during their second preaching tour from October 3 to December 30, A.D. 28.

name: Dagon
description: The chief deity of the Philistines, described as the father of their gods in contrast to Yahweh's claim as God of all nations.

name: Dakota Black Hills
description: A region in North America that marked the eastward extent of the Pacific Ocean during an ancient geological period when much of the continent was submerged.

name: Dalamatia
description: The headquarters city of Planetary Prince Caligastia, built in the Persian Gulf region around 500,000 years ago. This beautiful walled city housed the Prince's staff and served as a center of culture and learning for nearly 300,000 years, operating under a moral code called "The Father's Way." The city was destroyed by rebellion and later submerged beneath the waters, with its legacy influencing later Mesopotamian civilizations.

name: Daligastia
description: A secondary Lanonandek Son who served as associate-assistant to Planetary Prince Caligastia on Urantia. He presided over the ten councils of Caligastia's staff and later joined the Lucifer rebellion, proclaiming Caligastia as "God of Urantia." Though deprived of much power after the rebellion, he remains active on the planet but cannot coerce normal individuals against their will.

name: Damascus
description: An ancient Syrian city where Jesus learned mathematical techniques from a teacher and spent considerable time studying numbers. It was on the road to Damascus that the Apostle Paul experienced his dramatic conversion, which significantly influenced the development of Christianity.

name: Damascus gate
description: The northern gate of Jerusalem through which Jesus was led on the direct route to Golgotha for crucifixion. It was near this gate that the panicked tomb guards fled after Jesus' resurrection, encountering the women who were coming to anoint his body.

name: Dan (city)
description: Dan, also known as Caesarea Philippi, was a northern Palestinian city that Jesus visited during his personal journey and where he and the twelve apostles stayed while fleeing north.

name: Dan (Prince’s staff)
description: Dan was the head of the council in charge of conquering predatory animals, one of the ten councils of the Planetary Prince on Urantia. Dan and all his council were lost in the Caligastia rebellion.

name: dance
description: Dance among mortals represents a crude attempt to approach celestial harmony, while among primitives it served religious purposes in worship festivals and spirit communication. Wild dancing can induce mystical states, though healthier spiritual meditation is found in reflective worship.

name: danger
description: Jesus decided to exercise normal watchcare over his human safety and take reasonable precaution to prevent untimely termination of his mission, but to refrain from superhuman intervention when facing personal danger. He warned his apostles not to expose themselves needlessly to peril, and Pentecost endowed mortals with power to remain unmoved in the face of appalling danger.

name: Daniel
description: Daniel was a Hebrew prophet who presented the drama of an impending crisis and the establishment of the Messianic kingdom, believed in the resurrection of the dead, and understood that the Most Highs rule in the kingdoms of men. His apocalyptic writings heavily influenced John the Baptist and later apocalyptists, though they caused great confusion about the nature of the Messiah.

name: Danube
description: The Danube was a major European river valley that served as a migration route for various peoples. Some Andites entered Europe by way of the Aegean islands and up the Danube valley, while the round-headed Andonites were mainly situated in the Danube valley and were never entirely displaced by later migrations.

name: Danubians
description: The Danubians were Andonites who entered Europe through the Balkan peninsula and moved northward via the Danube valley as farmers and herders. They made pottery, tilled the land, became mother worshipers through Cretan missionary influence, and were later imported as slaves by the Greeks.

name: Daphne
description: Daphne was a Greek mythical character, a naiad who was the daughter of the river god Ladon.

name: Daphne, grove of
description: The grove of Daphne was a notorious shrine of shame and immorality in Antioch that Jesus refused to visit, though Gonod and Ganid did visit this temple.

name: dark ages
description: Dark ages occur when material achievement outruns wisdom and civilization retrogresses from high but premature levels of attainment. They represent periods when the imbalance between self-liberty and self-control leads to cultural and spiritual decline, as seen in medieval Europe and ancient China.

name: dark giants of space
description: Enormous dark celestial bodies that function as powerful balance wheels in space, holding large neighboring systems in effective gravitational control.

name: dark gravity bodies
description: Enormous dark masses that encircle Havona in two elliptical belts, neither reflecting nor absorbing light. These unique bodies possess both linear and absolute gravity, serving to stabilize the central universe and maintain gravitational equilibrium between Paradise and the seven superuniverses.

name: dark islands of space
description: Dead suns and other large aggregations of matter devoid of light and heat that function as powerful balance wheels, holding large neighboring systems in effective gravitational control. Many serve as dynamos that mobilize and direct space energies, and some house power centers that regulate energy circuits throughout space.

name: darkness
description: A concept representing both physical absence of light and spiritual ignorance or separation from divine truth, contrasted with walking in the light of God's truth.

name: daughters of Aaron
description: Female descendants of Aaron the priest, representing the priestly lineage in Judaism. Elizabeth, mother of John the Baptist, was descended from this priestly line.

name: daughters of God
description: A designation for spirit beings who descend more directly from the Infinite Spirit, in contrast to sons of God who descend from the Father and Son. Angels are commonly referred to by feminine pronouns and considered daughters of God.

name: David (King)
description: The second king of Israel who established a united kingdom with Jerusalem as its capital. Despite his military successes and political achievements, his reign involved questionable alliances, corruption, and moral failures including the murder of Uriah the Hittite.

name: David Zebedee
description: Brother of apostles James and John Zebedee who organized and supervised an extensive messenger service for Jesus' ministry. Known as dependable and efficient, he maintained camps for pilgrims and coordinated communications throughout Palestine, continuing his messenger work even after Jesus' death.

name: dawn mammals
description: Small, active creatures that appeared over one million years ago in Mesopotamia as direct descendants of North American lemur-type mammals. These flesh-eating, three-foot-tall animals walked mostly on hind legs, possessed large brains for their size, and gave rise to the mid-mammals after seventy generations.

name: day of atonement
description: A significant Jewish holy day of fasting and repentance, observed annually as part of the Hebrew religious calendar and ceremonial system.

name: daydreaming
description: A practice involving religious daydreaming that carries great danger as it may become a technique for avoiding reality, though mysticism has sometimes served as a means of genuine spiritual communion.

name: Daynals, order of
description: Trinity Teacher Sons who serve as universal educators dedicated to the spiritual awakening and moral guidance of all realms throughout the universes.

name: days
description: Units of time measurement used across various worlds and contexts in the universe.

name: deaconesses
description: Women teachers and ministers in the early Christian church who were accorded general recognition for their religious work and ministry.

name: Dead, Book of the
description: An ancient Egyptian collection of magical charm texts that were inscribed on coffins and believed to protect the deceased in the afterlife.

name: dead
description: Those who have died, with various cultural practices surrounding them including taboos against speaking their names, burial customs, and beliefs about their spiritual state.

name: dead giants of space
description: Massive celestial bodies in space that have ceased active functioning.

name: Dead Sea
description: A body of water in Palestine where the Jordan River flows, located near Jerusalem and significant in the religious geography of the region.

name: deadland
description: The primitive concept of a destination realm where ghosts were believed to journey after death, representing an early form of afterlife belief.

name: death
description: The cessation of mortal life, which may be physical, intellectual, or spiritual in nature, serving as a transition point for surviving personalities to continue their eternal journey in morontia form.

name: death penalty
description: Capital punishment, which has been practiced throughout history. In ancient times, it was common for ten innocent people to be executed for one natural death due to beliefs about responsibility. Various civilizations, including those under Hammurabi and Moses, prescribed death for many crimes, particularly grave sexual offenses, often by burning. The practice continues to exist in some advanced civilizations, where it is applied to multiple crimes beyond murder and administered with certainty and swiftness.

name: debating society
description: A governing body or assembly that makes decisions through discussion and debate. Mankind has learned that neither peace nor war can effectively be managed by such deliberative groups, as they lack the decisive leadership necessary for effective action during critical times.

name: Deborah
description: A Hebrew judge and ruler who led her people to victory against Sisera at Taanach. Despite being a woman, she maintained the cruel practices of her time, ordering the death of all who surrendered after her military victories.

name: debt
description: Financial obligation or indebtedness. While modern humans are not social slaves, thousands allow ambition to enslave them to debt. In ancient times, creditors could control debtors even after death, and debt slavery was common. Jesus taught about debt through parables, including the story of the moneylender who forgave his debtors.

name: Decapolis
description: A league of ten Greco-Roman cities located east of the Sea of Galilee and the Jordan River. Jesus and his apostles preached in several of these cities, including Scythopolis, Gerasa, Abila, Gadara, Pella, Philadelphia, and Heshbon. The healed demoniac Amos proclaimed Jesus's works throughout all the cities of the Decapolis.

name: Deccan
description: The southern plateau region of India. Around 15,000 B.C., Andite peoples moved southward into the Deccan under population pressure from the north. The Salem missionaries encountered increasing caste systems as they penetrated into the Dravidian Deccan. Dravidian culture has survived in the Deccan to modern times, and the region became a source of inferior religious influences that contaminated Aryan beliefs.

name: December 25th
description: The date of the annual festival of Mithras, representing the most elaborate observance in the Mithraic cult. This winter solstice celebration was central to Mithraic worship and religious practice.

name: decimal planets
description: Special experimental worlds where Life Carriers are permitted to undertake life experiments to modify or improve standard universe life forms. About one world in ten is designated as a decimal planet, including Urantia. These worlds feature unique variations in life patterns, and midwayers always appear on them. Urantia is the sixtieth decimal planet in Satania, distinguished by the early appearance of the Andonic race and the simultaneous emergence of the six colored Sangik races.

name: decisions
description: The act of choosing between alternatives, representing the universe insignia of freewill creaturehood. Decisions constitute humanity's greatest opportunity and supreme cosmic responsibility. When mortals make and consummate decisions in action, they gain experience that becomes part of their eternal character. Moral decisions, particularly choosing to do God's will, advance spiritual status and represent the keys to the kingdom of heaven.

name: Declaration of Liberty
description: A document or manifesto associated with Lucifer's rebellion, likely referring to Lucifer's formal proclamation of his charges against universe government and his call for creature liberty from divine authority.

name: dedication, feast of
description: A Jewish religious feast commemorating the dedication of the temple or altar, likely referring to Hanukkah or similar ceremonial observance.

name: dedication, spiritual
description: The act of consecrating oneself wholly to God's will and service, representing a fundamental commitment in spiritual growth and religious experience.

name: deduction
description: A logical process of reasoning from general principles to specific conclusions, representing one method of intellectual analysis and problem-solving.

name: deer
description: Abundant game animals that appeared during Earth's evolutionary history and were hunted by early humans including the Neanderthalers around 800,000 years ago.

name: default
description: A failure to fulfill obligations or expectations, often associated with betrayal or falling short of one's duties and responsibilities.

name: defeat
description: A spiritual testing experience that, when properly understood, serves as an educational tool for acquiring wisdom and developing character. The noble soul learns to fail gracefully, viewing defeat as a mirror for honest self-examination and a stepping stone to higher spiritual achievement.

name: defectives
description: Individuals with physical, mental, or developmental impairments, often grouped with other disadvantaged populations in discussions of social welfare and human improvement.

name: defilement
description: Ceremonial or spiritual contamination according to religious law, particularly in Jewish tradition through contact with the dead, entering Gentile buildings, or other ritual impurities requiring purification ceremonies.

name: degeneracy
description: The deterioration or decline in quality, morality, or physical condition, identified as one of the major obstacles preventing Urantia from achieving its high planetary destiny of light and life.

name: degenerates
description: Individuals who have declined from a higher to a lower state of physical, mental, or moral condition, often considered among society's disadvantaged populations.

name: Deity
description: The divine reality that functions on personal, prepersonal, and superpersonal levels across seven functional levels from static to ultimate. Deity may be existential (as in the Trinity persons), experiential (as in the Supreme Being), or associative (as in God the Sevenfold), and is characterized by unity on all supermaterial levels of reality.

name: Deity Absolute
description: The causational, potentially personal possibilities of universal reality, representing the totality of all Deity potential. The Deity Absolute is the purposive qualifier of unqualified, absolute, and nondeity realities, existing as experiential in actualization but existential in potentiality.

name: Deity-trinitized Sons
description: A group of Trinitized Sons of God that remain unrevealed in The Urantia Book narratives, forming one category of beings who have undergone the experience of trinitization.

name: Deity Ultimate
description: A reference to God the Ultimate, the transcendental-experiential Deity functioning on the second level of unifying divinity expression as effective overcontroller of the master universe.

name: delay
description: The wisdom of postponement in divine administration, where apparent failures of time are never confused with the significant delays of eternity. On Paradise, postponement is never regarded as disgrace, and shortsighted mortals should be slow to criticize the time delays of farseeing administrators.

name: delegation
description: The distribution of divine authority and responsibility by the Universal Father, who delegates everything possible to his Eternal Son and other beings. This reflects God's self-distributive nature and policy of profound self-distribution throughout creation.

name: delinquency
description: The moral undoing that results when overindulgent parents conspire with foolishness to encompass the corruption of their thoughtless children. Jesus warned that the heavenly Father is not a foolishly indulgent parent who condones sin and forgives recklessness.

name: Deliverer
description: A title used to describe one who brings salvation or deliverance, particularly referring to Christ Michael as the accredited deliverer of Urantia following his bestowal mission. Jesus warned that many false deliverers would come to lead people astray.

name: dematerialization
description: The technique used by transport seraphim to transform material beings into a morontia-like state for transport between worlds. This process was used for Adam and Eve's journey to Urantia and for the immediate dissolution of Jesus' physical body after his resurrection.

name: demigods
description: Supernatural beings in Hindu theology, classified among various spirit entities including supermen, semigods, heroes, demons, and other mythological figures worshipped in later-day cults.

name: democracy
description: A form of government where power is vested in the people, either directly or through elected representatives.

name: demon possession
description: A phenomenon where disharmonious spirits were believed to control human minds, which only existed after the Caligastic rebellion and ended at Pentecost when the Spirit of Truth was poured out upon all flesh. Before Pentecost, rebel midwayers could actually influence certain inferior mortals, but since that time, no spirit can possess any human mind.

name: demons
description: Subordinate spiritual beings that monotheists kept alongside spirits, fates, fairies, and other lesser supernatural entities after advancing from polytheism to belief in a supreme deity.

name: denarii
description: Roman silver coins used as currency in Jesus' time, with a denarius representing a day's wages for a common laborer and bearing Caesar's image and inscription.

name: Denmark
description: A northern European region that arose from the sea during climatic changes and served as the military headquarters of the northern Andites for three thousand years, from which successive waves of conquest were launched.

name: density
description: The mass per unit volume of matter, which varies dramatically throughout the universe from atmospheric gases to incredibly dense dark islands of space and stellar cores that can be thousands of times denser than water.

name: denunciations
description: Strong public condemnations, particularly Jesus' criticisms of the scribes, Pharisees, and religious hypocrites, though Jesus emphasized positive teaching over negative denunciations.

name: depositions
description: Formal testimonies taken by Melchizedek Sons who serve as mobile advisory courts, receiving evidence and suggestions to help resolve difficulties in the evolutionary worlds.

name: depravity
description: Moral corruption or wickedness, relating to concepts of human sinfulness and spiritual degradation.

name: depression
description: A state of mental dejection and discouragement that believers can overcome through faith and spiritual living, as demonstrated by Thomas the apostle who struggled with depression but continued his service.

name: descending Sons of God
description: An order of divine beings who descend from Paradise to serve in the universes of time and space.

name: desertion
description: The act of abandoning one's duty or loyalty, as experienced by Van when many workers preparing the Garden of Eden left due to doubts about the delayed arrival of Adam and Eve, and by Jesus when disappointed followers ceased following him after he refused to be proclaimed king.

name: designers and embellishers
description: A division of the celestial artisans organized into seven corps who create spiritual works of beauty including color harmonies, sound designs, emotion preservation, spiritual fragrances, presence arrangements, enhanced appreciation, and culminating morontia syntheses.

name: desire of all ages
description: A title Jesus applied to himself, indicating his role as the fulfillment of humanity's eternal longing for divine connection and salvation.

name: desires
description: The longings and aspirations that motivate human behavior and spiritual development.

name: despair
description: The hopeless feeling that results from materialistic philosophy which sees death as final extinction, but which can be overcome through faith in God and recognition of eternal survival possibilities.

name: destiny
description: The ultimate purpose and goal toward which all beings and universes evolve, established by divine will yet influenced by personal choice, ranging from individual spiritual ascension to cosmic completion of God's eternal plan.

name: destiny reservists
description: A special group of reservists held in preparation for future universe assignments and purposes.

name: destruction
description: The broad and easy path that leads away from eternal life, contrasted with the narrow way of salvation that requires sincere seeking to find the Father through the Son.

name: details
description: Specific particulars or minor aspects of larger concepts or situations.

name: detention
description: A correctional system on a neighboring planet where potential criminals are sentenced to life service in detention colonies as a crime prevention measure.

name: detention planets
description: Worlds designated for the confinement or rehabilitation of certain individuals or groups.

name: detention spheres
description: Specialized worlds used as prison facilities for the confinement of rebellious or criminal beings.

name: deterioration
description: The divine nature is repugnant to any form of decline or inferior performance of personal acts.

name: determinism
description: A philosophical concept present in the universe but not alone or absolute, as it is balanced by the presence of mind and spirit which inject spontaneity into material worlds.

name: Deuteronomy
description: The fifth book of the Hebrew scriptures from which Jesus read during synagogue services and which portrayed the Great Choice between good and evil, blessing and curse.

name: devil
description: The devil is none other than Caligastia, the deposed Planetary Prince of Urantia and a secondary Lanonandek Son. While having some foundation in Caligastia's planetary presence, the doctrine of a personal devil who could influence normal human minds against their free will is wholly fictitious.

name: Devonian
description: A geological period known as the age of fishes, occurring between 270-220 million years ago, characterized by extensive sea coverage and coral reef formations.

name: devotion
description: Dedicated commitment and loyalty to a person, cause, or principle.

name: Devouress
description: In Egyptian religion, a being that consumed souls found wanting when weighed in the judgment hall of Osiris, representing their concept of hell.

name: dew point
description: The atmospheric temperature at which water vapor condenses into liquid droplets. On three-gas architectural worlds, the dew point is higher than on two-gas planets like Urantia, affecting daily moisture precipitation patterns.

name: dialects
description: Regional variations of language that develop within local groups. Primitive races often speak different dialects every forty or fifty miles, creating barriers to communication and cultural exchange. Dialects originate from language play and local word development, much like "baby talk" within family groups.

name: diamonds
description: Precious crystalline gems highly valued by modern humans. The Andonites valued flint with the same intensity that contemporary people value diamonds, traveling great distances to obtain quality materials for their tools.

name: dictation
description: The act of speaking words aloud for another person to write down or record.

name: dictators
description: Rulers who exercise absolute authority and control over their subjects. In the ancient struggle between divided authority and unified command, dictators emerged victorious. Earthly rulers who seek to exercise religious authority as dictators will cause trouble and persecution for gospel believers.

name: diet
description: The regulation or restriction of food intake, often for health or remedial purposes. In ancient times, fasting and dieting were commonly used as therapeutic measures alongside other treatments like counterirritants.

name: difficulties
description: Challenges, obstacles, and problems that arise in life and require wisdom, courage, and spiritual strength to overcome. While difficulties can be discouraging, they serve important purposes in character development and spiritual growth, teaching mortals to transform temporal struggles into eternal triumphs through faith and divine guidance.

name: digestion
description: The biological process of breaking down food to extract nutrients and energy. Cooking food reduced the vital energy required for digestion, allowing early humans to conserve strength for social and cultural development.

name: dignity
description: The quality of being worthy of honor and respect, inherent to all persons as children of God.

name: diligence
description: Careful and persistent effort or work; steady application and dedication to tasks and responsibilities.

name: Dilmat
description: Dilmat is the name by which the Egyptians referred to the Sumerian city of Dilmun in ancient times. Both were confused at that time with Dalamatia, the city of the Planetary Prince, which had long been submerged at the bottom of the Persian Gulf.

name: Dilmun
description: After the submergence of Dalamatia, the Nodites moved north and east, founding the new city of Dilmun as their racial and cultural headquarters. The Egyptians called this city of ancient glory Dilmat, while the later Adamized Sumerians confused both the first and second Nodite cities with Dalamatia and called all three Dilmun.

name: dinosaurs
description: Dinosaurs were egg-laying reptiles that became the monarchs of their age, distinguished by their small brains weighing less than one pound controlling bodies that could weigh as much as forty tons. They evolved in all sizes and dominated the land for millions of years before becoming extinct due to their inability to cope with environmental changes, lacking sufficient intelligence to adapt to the situation.

name: Diogenes of Athens
description: A Greek philosopher to whom the Cynics traced their philosophy, though they also derived much of their doctrine from the remnants of the teachings of Machiventa Melchizedek.

name: Dion
description: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during the Perea mission between January 3, 30 A.D. until the death of Jesus.

name: Dionysus
description: A Greek deity whose nature cult flourished in Greece as part of the mystery religions that spread throughout the Mediterranean lands.

name: diplomacy
description: A worldly method of gaining ends through compromise and negotiation used in politics and commerce. Jesus knew these methods but refused to utilize diplomacy in the furtherance of his mission, choosing instead to depend exclusively on the Father's will.

name: direction
description: The four points of the compass are universal and inherent in the life of Nebadon, with all living creatures possessing bodily units sensitive to directional currents. The differences in Paradise's dimensions and force-energy make it possible to establish absolute direction in the master universe.

name: directional control creatures
description: Four control creatures of Salvington that serve as living compasses of the headquarters world, maintaining directional control in Nebadon by operating over the universe currents. They are described as beings of unparalleled beauty and exquisite form.

name: directors of assignment
description: Directors of Assignment is the highest angelic council on any sphere, consisting of 144 supreme seraphim elected from angels serving on evolutionary and architectural spheres. This council coordinates the self-directed phases of seraphic service and assignment and presides over all seraphic assemblies.

name: directors of conduct
description: Primary supernaphim who serve as the fourth group of these Paradise beings, instructing new arrivals to Paradise in the proper conduct and usages of the central Isle. They guide pilgrims and visitors to avoid confusion and help them feel at ease while teaching the perfect ways of behavior among the high beings who dwell on Paradise.

name: disappointment
description: An inevitable experience of mortal existence that serves as a tool for character development and spiritual growth. While disappointment can be painful and discouraging, especially when borne alone, it helps develop courage, strength of character, and faith. The ascendant children of time learn to feast upon disappointment and transform it into spiritual progress, while on Paradise disappointment is never regarded as defeat but rather as a postponement in the eternal journey.

name: disarmament
description: The removal of weapons and military armaments from nations, which alone does not lead to peace. True peace can only be achieved when nations abandon the illusion of divine right to national sovereignty, as strong nations will continue to fight with whatever means available regardless of weapons restrictions.

name: disaster
description: Catastrophic physical events, accidents, and calamities that, while difficult and mysterious, ultimately work out for the welfare and progress of the universes. These events include planetary catastrophes, collisions that revivify burned-out suns, and various tragedies that are part of the inevitable consequences of bestowing freedom upon imperfect beings.

name: Discerners of Spirits
description: Specialized seconaphim who serve as reflective angels attached to the Universal Censors, possessing inherent spiritual insight that allows them to present naked portraits of individuals' souls with complete certainty and perfection. They can reflect the presence of Thought Adjusters and discern the true motives, purposes, and spiritual character of any being, though they cannot decipher the content of the Adjusters' minds.

name: disciples
description: Followers of Jesus who are committed to doing the Father's will and loving one another, distinguished from apostles by less rigorous requirements. Disciples may maintain their own livelihood and family relationships while serving the kingdom, whereas apostles forsake all worldly possessions and relationships for full-time gospel service. Jesus requires disciples to be willing to face persecution and bear witness for the gospel.

name: discipline
description: A loving corrective process used by wise parents and the heavenly Father to guide growth and development. True discipline is motivated by love and administered for the benefit of the recipient, helping to develop character, enforce cooperation, and maintain order. It requires wisdom, fairness, and should be understood as a blessing rather than punishment, designed to help individuals bear spiritual fruit.

name: disclosure
description: The policy of allowing full exposure and unlimited opportunity for sin and rebellion to run their natural course, rather than attempting suppression or concealment. This approach, advised by celestial beings during the Lucifer rebellion, serves as the quickest technique for achieving the complete cure of evil and sin by allowing all beings to make deliberate choices.

name: discouragement
description: A temporary emotional state that, while natural in human experience, should not lead to despair or abandonment of faith. Discouragement, along with worry and indolence, indicates moral immaturity but does not necessarily signify resistance to spiritual guidance. God-knowing individuals learn to overcome discouragement through faith and trust in divine purposes.

name: discoveries
description: New findings and innovations that advance civilization and should be shared for the benefit of all humanity. Those who gain wealth through discoveries have an obligation to remember their short earthly existence and make provision for sharing these benefits with the largest possible number of their fellow beings, recognizing their debt to ancestors, society, and circumstances that made their discoveries possible.

name: discretion
description: The virtue of careful judgment and prudent restraint in speech and action. True wisdom embraces discretion as well as courage, though when carried too far, discretion can lead to cowardice and failure.

name: discrimination
description: The practice of making unjust distinctions or showing prejudice, particularly in treatment based on group membership rather than individual merit.

name: disease
description: Physical afflictions and illnesses that affect mortal beings. While painful illnesses and scourges may work out for universal welfare over time, spiritual healing and cleansing from moral infirmities is considered even greater than physical healing.

name: disharmony
description: A state of discord or lack of agreement, representing conflict between elements that should work together in unity.

name: dishonesty
description: The practice of deception, falsehood, or lack of integrity in dealings with others, representing a fundamental breach of trust and moral character.

name: disloyalty
description: Deliberate betrayal of trust or unfaithfulness to rightful authority or confiding friends. There are degrees of disloyalty ranging from partial loyalty of indecision to complete devotion to godless ideals, with betrayal of trust being among the most destructive forms of evil to personality status.

name: dispensations
description: Distinct periods or epochs in planetary spiritual development, typically marked by major revelations or significant changes in divine administration and guidance.

name: disputation
description: Formal debate or argumentative discussion, often involving disagreement or controversy over points of doctrine, philosophy, or policy.

name: dissenters
description: Those who disagree with or oppose prevailing opinions, established doctrines, or accepted authorities, often representing minority viewpoints or reform movements.

name: dissociation, psychic
description: A psychological state involving the separation or disconnection of normally integrated mental processes, which can contribute to mystical experiences but may indicate potentially dangerous psychic conditions.

name: dissolution
description: The process of breaking down or disintegrating, often referring to the end or termination of something.

name: distances (astronomic)
description: The vast measurements of space between celestial bodies and cosmic structures.

name: distractions
description: Things that divert attention from spiritual devotion and duty, including trivial social activities, worldly concerns, and anything that detracts from wholehearted service to God and the spreading of the gospel.

name: distress
description: A state of extreme anxiety, sorrow, or pain that causes emotional or physical suffering.

name: Dium
description: A city in the Decapolis region where Jesus and his apostles preached during their second tour between August 18 and September 16 of the year 29.

name: diversity
description: The beneficial variety and differences found throughout creation, including the natural differences between individuals, races, and viewpoints that multiply versatility and overcome inherent limitations while contributing to the overall unity of God's plan.

name: Dives
description: The wealthy man in the Nazarite parable of Lazarus and Dives, used as a warning to those who love riches and covet dishonest wealth.

name: dividing the inheritance
description: A situation where a young man asked Jesus to help settle an inheritance dispute with his brother, which Jesus used as an opportunity to teach about the dangers of greed and the importance of spiritual rather than material wealth.

name: divination
description: An ancient superstitious practice of attempting to predict the future or discover hidden knowledge through various methods such as omens, oracles, and signs, which was simply an attempt to avoid trouble and is considered a relic of ignorant minds.

name: divine builders
description: One of the groups within the celestial artisans who function in seven groups and are responsible for creating spiritual cities, homes, and other structures in the celestial realms.

name: Divine Counselors
description: Trinity-origin beings who are the divine counsel of the Paradise Trinity to the seven superuniverses. There are twenty-one billion Divine Counselors in service, with three billion assigned to each superuniverse. They work alongside Perfectors of Wisdom and Universal Censors, forming tribunals whose decisions represent the collective wisdom of Deity. These high spiritual beings serve as administrators in superuniverse governments and act as truth-revealing counselors throughout the cosmos.

name: divine executioners
description: Quasi-material beings who serve as the fourth members of conciliating commissions alongside Universal Conciliators. They are almost visible to mortal races and possess great power to enforce the decisions of conciliating commissions. At higher levels of superuniverse service, they become the physical voice of spirit trios, divested of retributive power but serving as interpreters of mercy-justice.

name: Divine Ministers
description: A term referring to various orders of divine beings engaged in spiritual ministry throughout the universe of universes, including local universe Mother Spirits and other spiritual entities who serve in divine capacities.

name: divine plan
description: The eternal purpose and universal plan of the Universal Father for the creation, evolution, ascension, and perfection of will creatures. This divine plan encompasses three major aspects: the Plan of Progressive Attainment (evolutionary ascension), the Bestowal Plan (revelation of divine love through Paradise Sons), and the Plan of Mercy Ministry (comprehensive spiritual service). The plan is vast, eternal, and perfect, unfolding across all time and space to achieve the spiritual elevation of all creation.

name: divine rest
description: The concept of spiritual repose and eternal peace that characterizes the divine nature and represents the ultimate state of harmony with God's will.

name: divine sonship
description: The spiritual relationship and status achieved by mortal beings who recognize and embrace their eternal relationship with the Universal Father as his spiritual children.

name: divine voice
description: The inner spiritual guidance and communication from divine sources, particularly through the ministry of Thought Adjusters and other divine fragments that speak to the human soul.

name: divine will
description: The perfect and eternal will of God, representing the divine purpose and plan that governs all creation and provides the ultimate standard for spiritual living and cosmic harmony.

name: divining rod
description: An ancient tool used in divination practices to locate water, minerals, or other hidden objects, representing primitive attempts to discover hidden truths through supernatural means.

name: Divinington
description: One of the seven sacred spheres of Paradise, known as the "bosom of the Father" and serving as the personal-communion sphere of the Universal Father. This world is the Paradise rendezvous and administrative headquarters for Thought Adjusters and other Father fragments. Divinington is largely forbidden to most beings and holds the secrets of Adjuster bestowal and ministry. It represents the most personal and intimate sphere of the Universal Father's divine presence.

name: divinity
description: Divinity is the characteristic, unifying, and coordinating quality of Deity that creatures can comprehend as truth, beauty, and goodness. It is experienced through worship, service, and spiritual growth, manifesting as love, mercy, and ministry in personality and as justice, power, and sovereignty on impersonal levels.

name: divorce
description: Divorce is the dissolution of marriage that serves as a social safety valve when societies fail to provide adequate education and preparation for marriage. While easy divorce from lack of self-control leads back to primitive social stages, divorce remains necessary as long as immature idealism guides marriage decisions without proper training.

name: doctors
description: Medical practitioners who provide healthcare and healing services to patients.

name: doctrines
description: Religious or philosophical teachings and beliefs that can become rigid dogmas leading to sectarianism and spiritual enslavement. While doctrines may provide intellectual frameworks, they cannot transform character without spiritual power, and excessive focus on doctrinal uniformity can divide believers rather than unite them in spiritual fellowship.

name: dogmatism
description: The tendency to assert beliefs as absolute truth without question, which can enslave the spiritual nature and lead to sectarianism. Only absolute reality can consistently be dogmatic, while human beliefs should remain open to spiritual growth and experience.

name: dogs
description: The first animal domesticated by humans, originally used for food, hunting, transportation, and companionship. Dogs possess minds that can know material beings but lack spiritual nature and cannot experience spiritual reality or achieve eternal survival, as they lack the reflective will and moral discrimination that characterizes human beings.

name: doing versus being
description: The philosophical distinction between action-oriented living focused on accomplishments versus character-oriented living focused on spiritual development and becoming.

name: Doings of Kings of Israel
aliases: The Doings of the Kings of Israel
description: A historical record of Hebrew affairs that was destroyed by the priests of the Babylonian exile when they prepared their new sacred history of Israel as portrayed in the Old Testament, along with other accurate records of Hebrew history.

name: Doings of Kings of Judah
aliases: The Doings of the Kings of Judah
description: A historical record of Hebrew affairs that was destroyed by the priests of the Babylonian exile when they prepared their new sacred history of Israel as portrayed in the Old Testament, along with other accurate records of Hebrew history.

name: doll
description: A miniature human figure first employed as a magic talisman by barren wives, representing early humanity's use of imitative magic and symbolic objects to influence supernatural forces.

name: dollars (U.S.)
description: United States currency used in The Urantia Book to illustrate the enormous economic value of sunlight, with examples including Chicago's daily sunshine bill of over 100 million dollars and sunlight being economical at one million dollars per pound.

name: dolomite limestone
description: A type of limestone rock containing the mineral dolomite, composed of calcium magnesium carbonate.

name: dolphins
description: Marine mammals that are descendants of placental mammals which left the land and took up residence in the oceans, along with whales, porpoises, seals, and sea lions.

name: Don
description: A river in Russia that, along with the Volga, provided a northern migration route across grazing lands for Andite peoples entering northwestern Europe.

name: donkeys
description: Domesticated animals used for transportation in ancient times, including Mary riding to Bethlehem on Joseph's donkey and Jesus' symbolic entry into Jerusalem riding a donkey as a king on a mission of peace.

name: doom
description: A state of inevitable destruction or death, particularly referring to spiritual condemnation or final judgment.

name: door
description: A symbolic representation of spiritual access, with Jesus declaring he stands at the door of human consciousness and knocks, waiting for individuals to open their hearts to receive him.

name: doorposts
description: Vertical frames of doorways that were central to Jewish religious customs, including smearing blood for protection of firstborns and the practice of touching and kissing parchment scrolls nailed to doorposts while entering or leaving houses.

name: Dorcas
description: The owner of a house where Gadiah and Peter held a memorable religious discussion, representing one of the early Christian gathering places.

name: Dothan
description: A biblical location where Joseph's brothers sold him into Egyptian slavery, visible from the hills near Nazareth where Jesus would contemplate historical events.

name: doubt
description: Doubt is an intellectual and emotional state that challenges faith and spiritual certainty. While confusional doubts and uncertainty cannot prevent spiritual survival, doubt represents the primary battle believers face against unbelief. Honest doubts and sincere questioning are not sinful but merely delay spiritual progress, while faith consistently triumphs over doubting through its positive and living nature.

name: doubters
description: Individuals who question or challenge religious beliefs and spiritual certainties, particularly those who doubt believers' knowledge of God or challenge faith-based convictions with intellectual skepticism.

name: doves
description: Birds that became symbols of peace and love in evolutionary religion, used both as sacrificial animals and in commerce. Jesus used doves metaphorically when instructing his followers to be "as harmless as doves" while remaining "as wise as serpents." Mary maintained a dovecote in Nazareth, and temple merchants sold doves for religious sacrifices.

name: dowries
description: Property or money brought by a bride to her husband upon marriage, evolving from earlier bride-price customs. The dowry system conveyed the impression of the bride's independence and provided legal protection, as a man could not divorce a dowered wife without paying back the dowry in full.

name: dragon
description: A symbolic representation used in religious contexts, particularly in Chinese culture as a survival of snake worship cults. In Apostle John's vision, the dragon symbolically represented Lucifer and his rebel followers, with references to the great red dragon whose tail "drew a third part of the stars of heaven and cast them down in darkness."

name: dragon flies
description: Large prehistoric insects that appeared early in evolutionary history, with ancient species measuring thirty inches across during the time when many insect species first developed and spread across the continents.

name: drama
description: A form of cultural expression that originated from religious practices, specifically from acting for spirit guidance. Jesus consistently avoided dramatic presentations in his ministry, preferring quiet and undramatic approaches to his Father's business despite the enormous dramatic possibilities of his mission.

name: draught of fishes
description: A remarkable fishing event when Jesus, after teaching from Simon Peter's boat, directed the fishermen to a location where they caught so many fish their nets nearly broke. Though regarded as miraculous by his followers, this event resulted from Jesus' superior knowledge of fishing and fish behavior in the Sea of Galilee.

name: Dravidians
description: A mixed people of southern India resulting from the blending of Andite conquerors with native stock around 12,000 years ago. The Dravidians built cities, engaged in extensive trade, and founded a superior civilization with cultural centers in river valleys, though their Andite inheritance became progressively attenuated over time.

name: drawings
description: Visual representations or artistic depictions created by drawing, though specific context and usage are not detailed in the available source material.

name: dreamers
description: Humans who are naturally inclined to dream and imagine possibilities, but who must balance this trait with practical action and avoid becoming passive mystics who rely entirely on divine providence.

name: dreams
description: The disordered, disconnected parade of the uncoordinated sleeping mind that occurs during sleep. Dreams represent the Thought Adjuster's attempts to register spiritual concepts in the material mind, though most ordinary dreams are physiological and psychological phenomena rather than divine communications.

name: drifters
description: Passive individuals who drift through life supinely trusting in a fictitious Providence to provide life's necessities, rather than taking active responsibility for their spiritual and material well-being.

name: drills
description: Single-shelled gastropod mollusks that were present in ancient seas, along with periwinkles and snails, representing early forms of marine life with defensive shells.

name: drives, innate
description: Natural, inborn motivational forces that compel behavior and action in living beings.

name: dropsy
description: A medical condition characterized by abnormal accumulation of fluid in body tissues, which Jesus healed in a man on the Sabbath day at a Pharisee's dinner, demonstrating divine compassion over rigid religious law.

name: drought
description: Periods of prolonged lack of rainfall that significantly influenced human migration patterns, particularly driving the Andites from central Asia to river valleys and causing major population movements throughout ancient civilizations.

name: drudgery
description: Monotonous, routine work that can be spiritually benumbing, though religion has the power to ennoble even commonplace daily labor by transforming it into sacred service to God.

name: drugs
description: Chemical substances that alter physical or mental states, often associated with intoxication and potentially harmful effects on consciousness and spiritual development.

name: drumlins
description: Smooth mounds or gentle surface undulations of terrain created by the movement and action of glaciers during ice ages.

name: drunkenness
description: A state of intoxication from alcohol or other substances that impairs judgment and behavior.

name: dual-origin beings
description: Beings created through the collaboration of any two Paradise Deities or by any two beings of direct or indirect descent from the Paradise Deities. These beings appear in the second group of Paradise's classification system and include both descending orders like Creator Sons and ascending orders like Adjuster-fused mortals.

name: dual spiritism
description: A religious concept that evolved from early ghost beliefs, recognizing two kinds of spirits - good and bad - that influence earthly affairs. This belief system enabled humans to account for both good luck and bad luck while maintaining faith in consistent supernatural forces, though it made conceiving cosmic unity more difficult for religionists.

name: duality relationships
description: Relations that exist between the I AM as sevenfold and the Seven Absolutes of Infinity. These dualities eternalize the foundations of reality in the eternal economy of universe administration.

name: ducks
description: Modern waterfowl that, along with eagles, pigeons, and ostriches, descended from enormous reptiles of the distant past during one of evolution's greatest leaps when reptiles became birds.

name: due process
description: Legal procedures and administrative protocols that can be suspended under extraordinary circumstances, as occurred during the Lucifer rebellion when normal passage of souls to mansion worlds was interrupted and when rebellious system sovereigns were eventually barred from Edentia councils.

name: dueling
description: A formalized combat between two individuals to settle disputes, representing a modern survival of ancient trials by ordeal. Dueling evolved from early tribal warfare as a way to resolve conflicts between individuals from different tribes without involving entire groups.

name: duplication
description: Wasteful and needless repetition of function that is never encountered in the eternal economy of universe administration, as the Deities do not engage in useless duplication of universe ministry.

name: dust
description: The material substance from which mortal bodies are composed and to which they return at death, as referenced in the saying "Dust we are and to dust shall we return." This represents humanity's fundamental connection to the earth and the temporary nature of physical existence.

name: duty
description: The moral obligation to fulfill one's responsibilities faithfully, whether to God, family, or society. True duty transcends mere obligation and becomes joyful service when performed with wholehearted devotion, representing humanity's evolutionary sense of moral responsibility elevated by spiritual revelation.

name: dwarfs
description: In the evolution of religious thought, monotheists subordinate their lesser gods to various minor supernatural beings including spirits, demons, fairies, and dwarfs.

name: Dyaks
description: A modern tribe that has evolved only the most primitive religious practices, representing an early stage of religious development.

name: Dyaus pitar
description: The Vedic lord of heaven who served as part of the triune leadership of the Aryan pantheon alongside Indra and Agni.

name: Dyaus-Zeus
description: A deity figure representing the connection between the Vedic Dyaus pitar and the Greek Zeus.

name: Ea
description: A Mesopotamian god of the sea who was part of the Babylonian triad along with Bel and Anu, representing the gods of earth, sea, and sky.

name: eagles
description: Large birds of prey that descended from enormous ancient reptiles and are mentioned in Salem teachings as symbols of spiritual renewal and strength.

name: earning a living
description: The practical necessity of providing for one's material needs through work and economic activity, which ties humans to reality while they simultaneously pursue spiritual growth and eternal goals.

name: earth
description: The planet Earth, also known as Urantia in cosmic terminology.

name: earthquakes
description: Seismic disturbances caused by sliding and shifting of the solid outer crust, not by volcanoes, which were frequent during Urantia's early geological periods but have diminished to an average of fifteen daily occurrences.

name: earthworms
description: Primitive animals that evolved along with other early life forms and represent stationary types that failed to progress in the evolutionary tree of life.

name: ease
description: A state of comfort and lack of challenge that is generally counterproductive to spiritual growth and character development. According to The Urantia Book, accepting seraphic guidance rarely leads to a life of ease, and animal nature does not progress favorably in environmental ease. Ease seeking is identified as one of the animal vestigial traits that must be eradicated during the morontia career.

name: East Indies
description: A region in Southeast Asia reached by Andite-Dravidian peoples in their migrations and maritime ventures. By 7,000 B.C., Dravidian shipping was venturing across the Bay of Bengal as far as the East Indies, and small groups of Andites also made their way to this region from Central Asia.

name: Easter Island
