description: A Pacific island that served as a religious and administrative center for one group of Andite-modified Polynesians. It was part of a network of flourishing centers of civilization that grew up on now-submerged Pacific lands as a result of Andite penetration.

name: Eastern Ghats
description: A mountain range in India through which three major rivers flow toward the sea. The Eastern Ghats supported important Dravidian cultural centers in the Deccan region, with settlements located along these river valleys.

name: eating
description: The act of consuming food for nourishment and sustenance.

name: Ebal
description: Mount Ebal, a mountain visible from Nazareth that Jesus could view from the hill of Simeon. It was one of the geographical landmarks that Jesus would contemplate while recalling the historic and traditional events of his people.

name: Eber
description: An official of the Sanhedrin who was sent to arrest Jesus but refused to do so after hearing him teach. Eber defended Jesus before the Sanhedrin, saying he had never heard anyone speak like Jesus, and later attended a meeting of Jewish leaders who believed in Jesus' teachings at the home of Nicodemus.

name: eccentricity
description: An undesirable tendency toward odd or unconventional behavior that Jesus consistently avoided and counseled against. While Jesus encouraged originality, he warned his followers to shun all tendencies toward eccentricity, maintaining perfect balance and propriety in all his actions and teachings.

name: Ecclesiastes
description: A biblical book that represents a frank pessimistic and worldly wise reaction to overoptimistic beliefs in Providence. As one of the Wisdom Books, Ecclesiastes exemplifies the influence of Platonism and Stoicism on Hebrew thought.

name: ecclesiasticism
description: An organized religious system that is fundamentally incompatible with living faith and the spiritual brotherhood teachings of Jesus. Ecclesiasticism represents the institutional preservation of traditions that often becomes an obstacle to spiritual progress and the advancement of genuine gospel teachings.

name: echinoderms
description: Marine animals that increased significantly towards the end of the Silurian period, particularly sea lilies, as evidenced by crinoid limestone deposits. Two groups of echinoderms became especially well developed and serve as guide fossils of this geological era.

name: eclipses
description: Celestial phenomena where one heavenly body obscures another. Early Nordic peoples believed eclipses were caused by a wolf devouring portions of the sun or moon, while the Shawnee prophet Tenskwatawa accurately predicted a solar eclipse in 1806.

name: ecstasy
description: Religious ecstasy is permissible when resulting from sane antecedents, but such experiences are more often emotional rather than spiritual manifestations. Genuine spiritual ecstasy is characterized by outward calmness and emotional control, and should be tested by whether it produces practical improvements in physical health, mental function, social relationships, and spiritual insight.

name: Eden, Garden of
description: The Garden of Eden, the headquarters and dwelling place of Adam and Eve on Urantia, established as a center for planetary uplift and biological advancement.

name: Edenic league
description: A federation of companies established by Adam throughout Eden, representing his system of group control and his attempt to create organized governance within the Garden.

name: Edenic teachings
description: The spiritual and educational instruction provided by Adam and Eve and their followers in the Garden of Eden, aimed at uplifting human civilization.

name: Edenites
description: The inhabitants and followers of Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden, including both the Adamic staff and those who lived under their influence.

name: Edentia
description: The headquarters world of the constellation Norlatiadek, seat of administration for the Most Highs and center of morontia culture. Edentia is surrounded by 770 architectural spheres and serves as the training ground for ascending mortals learning group ethics and socialization techniques.

name: Edomites
description: A neighboring tribe subjected to tribute by King David and later involved in conflicts with the kingdom of Judah. The Edomites revolted against King Amaziah but were defeated in a decisive victory.

name: Edrei
description: A city in the Decapolis region visited by Jesus and his followers during their second tour and later during the Perea mission. At Edrei, Jesus spent time with Thomas and his associates, giving important teachings about principles for preaching truth and the gospel of the kingdom.

name: education
description: A comprehensive system of learning and development designed to foster wisdom, self-realization, and spiritual values. Education involves the enhancement of ideals, increased appreciation of values, and augmented loyalty to supreme principles. It serves as preparation for progressive universe service, with the entire cosmos functioning as one vast school for ascending beings.

name: education builders
description: Celestial artisans who construct the headquarters for morontia training and advanced spirit learning. They ensure that ascending mortals have access to facilities for acquiring knowledge about their present and future work, as well as universal cultural knowledge designed to make them more intelligent and effective citizens of the morontia and spirit worlds.

name: effects
description: The results or consequences that follow from causes in the universal system of causation and natural law.

name: efficiency
description: The optimal functioning that arises from unified systems and purposeful coordination. In universe administration, the Deities maintain perfect efficiency without wasteful duplication of function, while personal efficiency comes from the harmonious integration of physical, mental, and spiritual systems.

name: effigies
description: Images or representations of people believed to have magical properties in primitive cultures. These figures were made and treated well or poorly with the belief that the same effects would transfer to the actual person they represented.

name: effort
description: The essential exertion and determination required for spiritual growth and universe ascension. While effort does not always produce joy, there is no happiness without intelligent effort, and meaningful progress depends upon purposeful action rather than passive waiting.

name: ego
description: The self-centered aspect of personality that focuses on personal importance rather than service or truth-seeking.

name: egocentricity
description: A self-centered orientation that makes religious life circumscribed, selfish, and unsocial. True spiritual growth requires exposure to diverse religious experiences to prevent one's faith from becoming egocentric and narrow.

name: egotism
description: Excessive self-importance and self-centeredness that hinders spiritual development and meaningful relationships with others.

name: Egypt
description: An ancient civilization in the Nile valley that became a major center of culture and religious development after the decline of Mesopotamia. Egypt fostered a thoroughly blended type of religious philosophy, contributed significantly to moral and ethical development, and served as an important waystation for the spread of Melchizedek teachings throughout the ancient world.

name: Eightfold Path
description: A Buddhist philosophical concept consisting of eight elements: right views, aspirations, speech, conduct, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, and contemplation. This path was closely linked to the Buddhist doctrine of suffering and the escape from it, designed to show mortals the futility of placing all hope in temporal goals while encouraging them to look beyond material associations to eternal realities.

name: El
description: A composite Hebrew deity that comprised several diffuse gods and Bedouin nature gods. The term El evolved as devout believers attempted to worship various ideas of divinity, creating a practice of referring to this composite deity that included multiple Bedouin nature gods.

name: El Elyon
description: The Most High God preached by Melchizedek, representing the concept of one universal Deity. El Elyon was worshipped by various groups including the Kenites and was kept alive as a monotheistic doctrine by figures like the Egyptian king Akhenaten, though Moses eventually compromised by adopting the tribal god Yahweh when he realized his people would not fully comprehend El Elyon.

name: El Shaddai
description: A concept of God representing divine Providence, where material prosperity was viewed as a reward for faithful service. El Shaddai was a composite idea derived from Egyptian teachings, Ikhnaton's doctrine, and Melchizedek's teachings, becoming thoroughly colored with Yahweh beliefs among the Hebrews and representing the Egyptian concept of the God of heaven.

name: Elam
description: The eastern Nodite headquarters located in highland regions just east of the Euphrates valley. Elam served as the center for Elamite Nodites led by Enoch, son of Cain, and later became a region where Nodite culture and commerce advocates united with mixed Sangik tribes.

name: Elamites
description: A Nodite tribe from Elam led by Enoch, son of Cain and Remona. The Elamites maintained peace with the Adamites for hundreds of years and eventually became largely Sangik in nature while maintaining a civilization superior to surrounding barbarians.

name: Elealah
description: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during the Perea mission that lasted from January 3, 30 A.D. until Jesus's death.

name: elections
description: The democratic process of selecting representatives or officials through voting, related to concepts of government and suffrage in organized societies.

name: electricity
description: A secondary and subsidiary manifestation of energy that, along with light, heat, magnetism, and matter, represents the same fundamental reality in different forms. Electricity arises from electronic charge creating electric fields and electron movement producing electric current, which in turn generates magnetic fields.

name: electrons
description: Fundamental particles of matter composed of exactly one hundred ultimatons held together by mutual attraction. Electrons orbit atomic nuclei at velocities beyond human imagination and are responsible for all chemical behavior through their freely revolving activity, with their orbital shifts producing measurable light-energy particles.

name: elements (chemical)
description: Chemical elements are the basic building blocks of matter throughout the universe. Havona contains exactly 1,000 basic chemical elements, while local universes like Nebadon are limited to 100 distinguishable atomic forms consisting of 1 to 100 electrons orbiting a central nucleus. The first 27 atoms are more predictable, while those with 28 or more electrons show increasing unpredictability. Elements above 90 electrons become unstable as electrons can escape nuclear control. All architectural spheres contain 100 physical elements plus 100 forms of morontia material. The atomic world displays periodic characteristics recurring in groups of seven, reflecting its spiritual origin.

name: elements (natural)
description: Natural elements refer to the forces of nature—thunder, lightning, rain, snow, hail, ice, fire, water, and air—that early humans feared and often worshiped. The Andonites developed an early fear of these elemental forces, while the Semites regarded everything as being indwelt by spirits, including spirits of fire, water, and air. Primitive races venerated springs, worshiped rivers, and believed that moving waters contained spirit animation and supernatural power.

name: elephants
description: Elephants are among the most intelligent animals, surpassed only by humans, and possess greater emotional control than horses despite lacking agility. They were successfully domesticated by 5000 B.C. in Mesopotamia, Turkestan, and China, and the blue man was also successful in taming them, though they would not reproduce in captivity. The huge elephants of the Miocene period possessed large brains and bodies, enabling them to dominate the entire world except Australia. Straight-tusked elephants entered Europe from Africa via the Sicilian land bridge, and Neanderthals hunted elephants 800,000 years ago.

name: Eleusinian mysteries
description: The Eleusinian mysteries were Greek fertility worship cults that developed within the Olympian pantheon during the period when Mediterranean peoples sought personal salvation and gods who could hear their prayers. These mystery cults, along with Dionysus nature worship and the Orphic brotherhood, represented a departure from abstract Greek philosophy toward more emotional religious experiences promising salvation.

name: Elihu
description: Elihu was an extraordinary prophet of Ur and a Salemite priest who preached a hopeful message of salvation through divine grace and faith. He proclaimed that God would show mercy to the repentant, saying 'He shall pray to God and shall find favor with him and shall see his face with joy, for God will give to man divine righteousness.' His teachings represented the most ringing and cheering message of human salvation heard in the Levantine world since the times of Melchizedek.

name: Elijah Mark
description: Elijah Mark was the father of John Mark and husband of Mary Mark, whose Jerusalem home served as a regular meeting place for Jesus' followers. The famous Last Supper took place in an upper room of his house, and Jesus made several post-resurrection appearances there. He died suddenly of a brain hemorrhage on May 3, A.D. 30, just hours before John Mark returned home. The upper room of his house later became the gathering place for 120 believers at Pentecost.

name: Elijah (Prophet)
description: Elijah was a fearless Hebrew prophet who restored the concept of God to the northern kingdom of Israel and transformed the Yahweh-Baal controversy from a land issue into a religious and moral matter. He denounced King Ahab for murdering Naboth, tore down altars of Baal, and demolished idols while advancing Hebrew monotheism. Under his influence, Yahweh became Elohim, and polytheism gave way to monotheism. He was a translated soul who never experienced normal death and currently serves as one of the 24 counselors on Jerusem. John the Baptist was greatly influenced by Elijah's example and methods.

name: Eliphaz
description: Eliphaz was one of Job's three friends who offered misguided comfort during Job's suffering. He falsely taught that the righteous never suffer and the wicked never prosper, insisting that Job must deserve his punishment and should exhibit the same fortitude he had prescribed for others. His stern, accusing philosophy embodied the flawed concept of a God of injustice, providing no real comfort to Job's afflictions.

name: Elisha
description: Elisha was Elijah's faithful associate and successor who continued the prophet's work after Elijah was called away. Working with the assistance of the little-known prophet Micaiah, Elisha kept the light of truth alive in Palestine and was known for performing wonderful works, including those at the village of Shunem.

name: Elizabeth (mother of John the Baptist)
description: Elizabeth was the mother of John the Baptist and wife of the priest Zacharias. She was a descendant of Aaron's daughters and a distant cousin of Mary, mother of Jesus. Gabriel first appeared to her to announce her pregnancy, and Mary visited her while both were expecting. Elizabeth was well-educated for a woman of her time and helped strengthen Mary's faith in Gabriel's vision. She believed Jesus would become the Messiah and kept John informed about world affairs. She died on August 17, A.D. 22, when John was twenty-eight years old.

name: Elizabeth of Tiberias
description: Elizabeth of Tiberias was the daughter of a wealthy Jew and one of the twelve women evangelists commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom. She was present at Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance to the Jerusalem believers.

name: Ellanora
description: Ellanora was a young mortal woman who took leadership of the human races on Panoptia during Lucifer's rebellion, preventing the Planetary Prince from leading that world into rebellion and ensuring not a single soul enlisted under the Lucifer banner.

name: ellipse
description: The ellipse is a fundamental geometric pattern in cosmic reality, describing the curved path that all forms of basic energy follow around the space levels of the master universe. Paradise itself is ellipsoid in form, and the seven superuniverses traverse a great ellipse around the central universe.

name: Elman
description: Elman was a Syrian physician who supervised the field hospital established at the seaside encampment near Bethsaida, organizing what could be regarded as the kingdom's first hospital. He was the father of Nasanta, one of the twelve women evangelists commissioned by Jesus.

name: Eloah
description: Eloah is the singular form of Elohim, representing the Trinity concept of God that was taught in Egypt by Sumerian-Chaldean groups based on traditions from the days of Adam and Melchizedek.

name: Elohim
description: Elohim is the Hebrew plural term meaning 'God of gods' that represents the Trinity concept of three Gods in one, based on Salem teachings from the days of Melchizedek. Jesus accepted this concept and used this term when referring to the supreme group of beings as God, rather than using the tribal deity name Yahweh.

name: elopement
description: Elopement in primitive societies was a pseudo-capture practice where some women would connive at being captured by younger men from other tribes to escape domination by older men of their own tribe. This practice served as a transition stage between marriage by capture and marriage by contract.

name: embalming
description: Embalming was a practice developed by the Egyptians after they observed that dead bodies placed in brickless graves were naturally preserved by the action of soda-impregnated sand. The Egyptians believed that preservation of the body facilitated one's passage through the future life.

name: emergencies
description: Emergencies refer to critical situations or crises that require immediate attention and often extraordinary measures or interventions to resolve.

name: emergency council of Edentia
description: The emergency council of Edentia is composed of all personalities assigned to Norlatiadek because of the Satania uprising, presided over by a Vorondadek Son of extraordinary experience who serves as the Most High director.

name: emergent energy
description: A type of energy that emerges during certain cosmic processes, related to ultimata and fundamental energy manifestations.

name: Emmaus
description: A town about seven miles west of Jerusalem where two brothers, Cleopas and Jacob, lived as shepherds. Jesus appeared to them in his seventh morontia appearance after his resurrection.

name: emotion designers
description: A group of celestial artisans who enhance and conserve feelings, preserving the sentiments of morontia and the emotions of divinity for the study and inspiration of mortals and advancing spirits.

name: emotions
description: Human feelings that should not be the primary basis for spiritual decisions or religious teachings. While emotions cannot be wholly eliminated from human experience, emotional excitement is not the ideal spiritual stimulus and strong feelings are not equivalent to divine spirit leadings.

name: emperor worship
description: The Roman practice of deifying the emperor as a symbol of the state, which was strongly resented by Jews and early Christians and led to bitter persecutions by the Roman government.

name: employment
description: Work or labor activities that provide occupation and livelihood for individuals in society.

name: emptiness
description: A relative concept in space, as practically speaking space is not empty but contains gravity currents, power circuits, and ultimatonic activities. Even the emptiest space contains about one hundred ultimatons per cubic inch.

name: En-Gannim
description: A place in Palestine that Jesus passed through during his final private tour of the region in AD 25, located between Geba and Endor on his journey northward.

name: enchanters
description: Practitioners of magical arts or supernatural rituals, often associated with early religious and shamanic practices.

name: encouragement
description: The divine Spirit serves as the source of continual ministry and encouragement to humanity, providing spiritual renewal and strength for the ascendant experience.

name: end
description: A concept representing termination or conclusion. From a circumscribed view there are many ends and terminations of activities, but from a larger universal viewpoint, there are no true endings, merely transitions from one phase of development to another.

name: end of the world
description: A concept taught by Jewish apocalyptists who believed that the end of Roman rule would be synonymous with the end of the age and the end of the world. Many of Jesus' followers interpreted his predictions about Jerusalem's destruction as referring to the end of the world and the Messiah's return.

name: Endantum
description: The headquarters world of the fifth constellation of Nebadon, where Michael of Nebadon made his sixth bestowal as a morontia mortal of evolutionary ascension status.

name: endogamy
description: The practice of marriage within a specific social group or community, as opposed to marriage outside of it.

name: Endor
description: An ancient town in Galilee near Mount Tabor where Joseph, father of Jesus, worked and where Jesus escaped from crowds during his public ministry. It was also the location where King Saul consulted with a witch to allegedly communicate with the spirit of the prophet Samuel.

name: endowments
description: Gifts or abilities bestowed upon beings. The Spirit of Truth endows believers with the power to perform God's will joyously, while time is described as the one universal endowment given to all will creatures as their "one talent."

name: ends versus means
description: A moral and philosophical concept distinguishing between goals and the methods used to achieve them. Intelligence can discriminate between means, but moral beings can also discriminate between ends themselves, with everything nonspiritual serving as means to an end except personality and true relationships.

name: enemies
description: Those who oppose or are hostile toward others. Jesus taught his followers to love their enemies, do good to those who hate them, and pray for those who persecute them, representing one of the highest moral teachings of human brotherhood.

name: enemy
description: An appellation used by Jesus for Caligastia, the fallen Planetary Prince, when warning the apostles that this enemy of the kingdom would try to separate them from each other.

name: energy
description: The fundamental basis of all existence in the material universe. Energy denotes all phases and forms of phenomenal motion, action, and potential, with all force-energy originating from Paradise and being under the ultimate control of the Universal Father.

name: energy controllers
description: Beings responsible for controlling and directing energy flows throughout the universe systems.

name: energy manipulators
description: The fifth group of celestial artisans who specialize in manipulating physical, mindal, and spiritual energies. They are divided into seven types: physical-energy manipulators, mind-energy manipulators, spiritual-energy manipulators, compound manipulators, transport advisers, experts of communication, and teachers of rest.

name: energy-matter
description: A concept representing the fundamental relationship between energy and matter as aspects of physical reality.

name: energy shields
description: Protective barriers or devices that provide shielding against various forms of energy.

name: energy transformers
description: Powerful living beings among the Master Physical Controllers who can change the form and potential of twenty-seven of the thirty physical energies of the superuniverse. There are almost one million in Satania alone, with the usual quota being one hundred for each inhabited world.

name: energy transmitters
description: Automatic and intelligent beings among the Master Physical Controllers who can direct energy flows into new circuits and provide emergency communication lines. They function as living superconductors and can receive and transmit intelligence, though they cannot initiate communication.

name: Engedi
description: An ancient city near the Dead Sea that served as the southern headquarters of the Nazarite brotherhood and other ascetic communities. John the Baptist lived here for two and a half years, and Jesus and Abner later visited the Nazarite colony at this location.

name: England
description: A nation that, along with Scotland and Wales, was always fighting until they gave up their respective sovereignties and united under the United Kingdom.

name: English Channel land bridge
description: A land connection that existed between England and France during most of the glacial period, allowing early human migrations across what is now the English Channel.

name: English language
description: The language chosen by celestial revelators for translating the Urantia Papers, despite its limitations in conveying enlarged cosmic concepts and advanced spiritual truths.

name: enmity
description: A concept representing deep-seated hostility or hatred between individuals or groups.

name: Enoch
description: Enoch was a son of Cain and Remona who became chief of the Elamite Nodites. He was the first mortal to fuse with his Thought Adjuster during mortal life in the flesh and currently serves as one of the 24 counselors on Jerusem. The Book of Enoch influenced Jesus' adoption of the title "Son of Man" for his mission.

name: Enos
description: Enos was a son of Seth and grandson of Adam and Eve who founded a new order of worship in the second garden.

name: Ensa
description: Ensa is the minor sector containing our local universe, consisting of one hundred local universes with headquarters at Uminor the third. It is the third minor sector in the major sector of Splandon, and its rotational center is located in the dense star cloud of Sagittarius.

name: enseconaphimation
description: Enseconaphimation is the process by which an ascending pilgrim is encased in a seconaphim for the long flight from the superuniverse headquarters to Havona.

name: enseraphimation
description: The process of being transported by seraphim, one of the methods of celestial transport used in the universe.

name: Enta
description: Enta was one of the notable women ancestors in Mary's lineage, the mother of Jesus, representing part of her illustrious hereditary background.

name: enthusiasm
description: A divine quality characterized by zealous commitment to spiritual purposes. Jesus exemplified controlled enthusiasm for the gospel of the kingdom, never allowing it to become fanaticism. True enthusiasm must be balanced with wisdom and thoughtfulness to be effective in spiritual service.

name: environment
description: The material and social conditions surrounding mortal life. While environmental limitations cannot prevent individual spiritual attainment, as demonstrated by Jesus on Urantia, they significantly influence physical health, social development, and religious experience. Spiritual progress remains equally available to all regardless of environmental handicaps.

name: envy
description: A deep-seated human trait involving resentment over another's advantages or success. Envy represents one of the mental poisons that interfere with spiritual progress and Adjuster communication. It can only be prevented through intimate contact with other minds and must be transcended for higher spiritual living.

name: Eocene
description: The Eocene was the early mammalian era from 50 to 35 million years ago, during which mammalian and other related forms of life evolved with little interruption while North America was connected by land to every continent except Australia.

name: Ephesus
description: Ephesus was the capital of the Roman province of Asia, famous for its temple of Artemis. Jesus visited there with Gonod and Ganid, while Paul later resided there for over two years making tents and conducting religious lectures at the school of Tyrannus. The apostle John eventually settled in Ephesus as an aged bishop, where he directed the writing of his Gospel and died at age 101 in A.D. 103.

name: Ephraim
description: Ephraim was the hill country region where Israelitish consciousness first originated, later becoming synonymous with the northern kingdom of Israel. The oppressed elders of Ephraim anointed David king of Israel, but the northern kingdom eventually vanished when its king conspired with Egypt and refused tribute to Assyria, leading to a three-year siege and total dispersion.

name: Epicurean teacher in Corinth
description: An Epicurean teacher in Corinth with whom Jesus had a conversation about choosing the best and esteeming good, while encouraging him to also discern the greater spiritual things of mortal life through realizing God's presence in the human heart.

name: Epicureanism
description: A school of thought dedicated to the pursuit of happiness that helped deliver Romans from deadly fatalism by teaching that people could improve their earthly status. It effectively combated ignorant superstition, with the better Epicureans not given to sensual excesses.

name: epileptics
description: Individuals suffering from epilepsy, which in ancient times was often confused with demon possession. Jesus healed several epileptics, and such afflicted persons were frequently revered as priests or medicine men, with many believing they were indwelt by gods or spirits.

name: Epistle of First John
description: The epistle known as First John was written by the apostle John himself as a covering letter for the Gospel work that his associate Nathan executed under John's direction.

name: epochal angels
description: A type of seraphic angels associated with epochal events or revelations.

name: epochal revelations
description: Five major revelations of epochal significance to humanity: the Dalamatian teachings by Prince Caligastia's staff, the Edenic teachings of Adam and Eve, the revelation of Melchizedek of Salem, the teachings of Jesus of Nazareth, and The Urantia Papers. These revelations are evolutionary but progressive, each building upon previous spiritual understanding.

name: epochs
description: Significant periods or ages in planetary development and spiritual evolution.

name: equality
description: The concept that while human beings differ greatly in physical abilities and intellectual endowment, all mortals are equal in spiritual potential and cosmic worth. True equality exists only in the recognition of spiritual brotherhood under divine sovereignty, while claims of inherent material equality are erroneous and can lead to social discord.

name: equalizations
description: Cosmic adjustments and compensatory mechanisms that balance inequities and restore harmony in the universe system.

name: equation
description: A mathematical expression representing relationships between quantities. Religious experience requires a personal God rather than abstract formulas, as one cannot establish meaningful spiritual relationships with mathematical equations or other impersonal concepts.

name: equivocation
description: The practice of being deliberately ambiguous or evasive in speech or action. This animal vestigial trait, along with procrastination and insincerity, is permanently eradicated during the morontia career as mortal survivors progress toward spiritual perfection.

name: Erech
description: An ancient Mesopotamian city-state that held out for thirty years after the fall of Akkad around 2500 B.C. Erech was located near Aram, a river settlement where Noah, a wine maker, lived and kept records of annual floods.

name: error
description: Intellectual mistakes or misconceptions that result from imperfection and incomplete understanding. Error is distinguished from sin in that it represents lack of knowledge rather than willful rebellion, though persistent error can evolve into sin when consciously embraced despite better knowledge.

name: Esdraelon
description: A large, fertile valley in northern Israel, also known as the Jezreel Valley. This plain was the site of many historical battles and was visible from the hills near Nazareth where Jesus lived as a boy, serving as a place where he would contemplate the conflicts of empires.

name: Eskimos
description: A people who are descendants of the ancient Andonite and Foxhall peoples, representing the closest surviving physical type to the original human inhabitants of Urantia. They preserve much of the primitive Andonic culture and live primarily in the frigid northern regions, maintaining docile and cooperative social customs.

name: Essenes
description: A mystical Jewish religious sect that originated during the Maccabean revolt, characterized by communal living, celibacy, and ascetic practices. They adopted Persian beliefs, specialized in teachings about angels, held all possessions in common, and some tolerated theories of reincarnation.

name: Esta
description: A young woman of Nazareth who married James, the brother of Jesus. She helped support Mary's spirits during difficult times when Mary struggled with uncertainty about Jesus' messianic mission and his apparent reluctance to fulfill her expectations.

name: Esther
description: A Jewish woman from history whose courage and liberation of her people is commemorated in the Jewish festival of Purim. She is referenced alongside other notable women like Miriam and Deborah as examples of women's important work in the religious life of Israel.

name: eternal life
description: The continuation of existence beyond mortal death, achieved through fusion with the indwelling Adjuster. This immortal state represents divine progression where the mortal soul, created jointly by the material mind and Adjuster, ascends to Paradise and becomes part of the eternal adventure. Eternal life is dependent on the free will choice of the individual and cannot be coerced by others.

name: Eternal Son
description: The Second Source and Center, the perfect and final expression of the Universal Father's first personal and absolute concept. The Eternal Son is the pattern personality for all personalities throughout the universes and serves as the absolute upholder of all spirit realities and spiritual beings. He is the divine Word made manifest and the only avenue of approach to the Universal Father.

name: Eternals of Days
description: Supreme Trinity Personalities who rule the billion worlds of Havona, with exactly one Eternal of Days directing each Havona sphere. These perfect administrators are offspring of the Paradise Trinity who have ruled their exquisite worlds forever without rotation or reassignment. They are divine equals of the Ancients of Days and serve as visible rulers to all will creatures in their domains.

name: eternity
description: The timeless state of existence where all reality exists simultaneously, in contrast to the time-bound experience of finite creatures. From the perspective of eternity, all is present at once, while from the temporal viewpoint, reality appears as sequential events. Eternity represents the everlasting now and serves as the backdrop against which the temporal cycles of time unfold.

name: ether
description: A hypothetical substance once postulated by science to explain space phenomena, but which does not actually exist. The term merely represents a collective name for various force and energy activities occurring in space. The absence of this hypothetical ether enables planets to maintain their orbits and electrons to resist falling into atomic nuclei.

name: Ethical Sensitizers
description: Supervisor seraphim whose mission is to foster creature appreciation of the morality of interpersonal relationships. They serve as the seed and secret of continued societal growth, functioning as volunteer counselors to planetary rulers and exchange teachers on system training worlds, with their full guidance available in the brotherhood schools on Edentia.

name: ethics
description: The dynamic and progressive principles governing interpersonal relationships that grow out of the fundamental facts of origin. True ethics become human when they are alive with universe reality, transcending static traditional morality. Ethics develop from natural consciousness of relative right and wrong and serve as the external mirror reflecting internal spiritual and religious development.

name: etiquette
description: Social conduct and proper behavioral methods that originated from ancient taboos, particularly around eating practices. Modern table etiquette traces back to these early prohibitions. Proper social conduct is taught by Morontia Companions on tarrying worlds and by Technical Advisors who instruct in the best and right way to conduct oneself.

name: Etruscan priesthood
description: A rapidly spreading religious system in ancient Italy with a new galaxy of gods and temples that became organized into the Roman state religion. During the sixth century before Christ, this priesthood successfully resisted the influence of Salem missionaries who sought to spread Melchizedek's monotheistic gospel throughout Italy.

name: eugenics
description: The concept of improving human genetic inheritance and racial quality through selective breeding practices. The Urantia Book presents eugenics as part of normal planetary development, noting that civilizations advance through the intelligent management of biological, intellectual, and spiritual resources, including the selective elimination of inferior human strains and the encouragement of superior genetic qualities.

name: eunuchs
description: Men who have been castrated, typically for religious, social, or administrative purposes in various historical cultures.

name: Euphrates River
description: One of the two major rivers of Mesopotamia, along with the Tigris River. The second Garden of Eden was located between these rivers, and the Euphrates valley served as a cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations. Andite migrations followed the river's course, and it was home to various early peoples including Nodites and Sumerians.

name: Europe
description: A continent that experienced extensive geological changes, ice ages, and human migrations throughout its history. Europe became home to various evolutionary races including the blue man, received significant Andite and Adamite influence, and later became a center of civilization that would carry forward religious and cultural developments to the world.

name: evaluation
description: The intellectual process of assessing or judging something, which requires a degree of transcendence from the thing being evaluated. The observer cannot be the thing observed, and true evaluation demands some separation between the evaluator and the subject of evaluation.

name: evangelism
description: The practice of spreading religious teachings and gospel messages, particularly focused on proclaiming spiritual truths and converting others to faith.

name: evangelists
description: A corps of gospel preachers and teachers trained and ordained by Jesus to spread the kingdom message. Originally numbering 117, later reduced to 75 who passed the preaching test, they were organized under a selection committee of Peter, James, and Andrew. A group of 70 evangelists was later chosen and ordained under Abner's leadership, consisting of former apostles of John and other dedicated disciples.

name: evangels of destiny
description: Celestial beings who serve as messengers and teachers, proclaiming spiritual liberty and divine sonship throughout the universe. They are trained in the Melchizedek College of Spiritual Endowment and carry the gospel message even to remote worlds.

name: Evangels of Light
description: Celestial personalities temporarily assigned to serve with mortal finaliter companies. These beings do not take the finaliter oath and are transient members who may include Solitary Messengers, supernaphim, seconaphim, Paradise Citizens, or their trinitized offspring, serving specific missions before returning to their former status.

name: Eve
description: The Material Daughter and consort of Adam who came to Urantia as part of the Adamic mission to uplift the evolutionary races. Eve was deceived by Caligastia's agents into the premature commingling of life plasm, leading to the Adamic default. Despite this failure, she and Adam worked to benefit humanity and established the second garden on the Euphrates, where Eve headed a commission for race improvement before her death.

name: Evening Stars
description: An order of celestial beings mentioned in The Urantia Book, related to the Brilliant Evening Stars.

name: Eventod
description: The name adopted by Michael of Nebadon during his bestowal as an ascending mortal spirit on Uversa, where he lived and functioned for eleven years, proving worthy of trust and commanding respect from his fellow spirits.

name: eventuated transcendental beings
description: Transcendental beings who neither create nor are created but simply eventuate, existing on four ultimate levels of personality activity and functioning on seven absonite levels.

name: eventuation
description: The process by which transcendental beings are brought into existence, being neither finite nor infinite but absonite. These eventuated beings include the Architects of the Master Universe, Transcendental Recorders, and other transcendental orders who simply eventuate rather than being created or evolved.

name: eventuators
description: Another term for eventuated transcendental beings, referring to those divine children of divinity, ultimacy, and eternity who are neither creators nor creatures but simply eventuate.

name: everywhereness
description: A concept related to omnipresence, referring to the quality of being present everywhere simultaneously.

name: Eveson
description: The second son of Adam and Eve who became a masterful leader and administrator, serving as his father's great helper before predeceasing Adam.

name: evidence
description: The basis of fairness and justice in harmony with mercy, supplied by the personalities of the Third Source and Center as the conjoint representative of the Father and Son to all realms and intelligent beings of creation.

name: evil
description: The immature choosing and unthinking misstep of those resistant to goodness, rejectful of beauty, and disloyal to truth. Evil is the misadaptation of immaturity or the disruptive influence of ignorance, representing potential rather than actual reality until consciously chosen and willfully endorsed as sin.

name: evil eye
description: A widespread primitive superstition involving the belief that certain individuals could cause harm, sickness, or misfortune through malevolent gazing. This fear led to various protective practices including veiling women and wearing charms, and was thought to be used by evil spirits to retaliate against human prosperity.

name: evil one
description: A reference to Satan or a malevolent spiritual being, often associated with Caligastia in Urantia terminology.

name: evil spirits
description: Malevolent spiritual entities historically associated with demon possession and supernatural afflictions.

name: evolution
description: The purposeful, progressive development of life forms, civilizations, and spiritual beings throughout the universe, guided by divine intelligence rather than occurring by chance. Evolution encompasses biological development, cultural advancement, and spiritual progression across all levels of creation.

name: evolutionary races
description: The various human racial groups that developed through natural evolutionary processes on inhabited worlds, as distinguished from specially created or imported beings.

name: exaltation, self
description: The tendency toward excessive pride or self-importance, elevating oneself above others in vanity or arrogance.

name: examinations
description: Progressive spiritual and intellectual tests administered throughout the ascending career, from morontia worlds to Paradise, to certify readiness for advancement to higher levels of service and achievement.

name: example
description: Jesus' life served as divine inspiration for all beings rather than a literal pattern to copy. True influence comes from loyal living and spiritual demonstration rather than mere precept or imitation.

name: excitement
description: A heightened emotional state of enthusiasm or agitation, often associated with intense experiences or anticipation.

name: excommunication
description: The formal expulsion from religious communities, particularly the casting out of believers from Jewish synagogues for following Jesus' teachings, resulting in denial of religious and social privileges.

name: excreta
description: Bodily waste products, historically feared in primitive cultures as potential sources of harmful magic, leading to careful disposal practices. In morontia existence, there is no waste as all food and water are fully utilized.

name: excursion and reversion supervisors
description: A class of Morontia Companions who accompany ascending mortals on longer trips to the headquarters sphere and surrounding worlds of transition culture. They plan, conduct, and supervise all individual and group tours about the system worlds of training and culture.

name: executioners of Ancients of Days
description: Divine beings who carry out the execution orders issued by the Ancients of Days, serving as their judicial enforcement agents in the superuniverses.

name: exhaustion
description: A state of extreme physical or mental fatigue that depletes energy reserves and requires rest for recovery.

name: existential reality
description: Reality that exists eternally and inherently, without beginning or need for evolutionary development, as opposed to experiential reality which must be achieved through growth and experience.

name: Exodus
description: The second book of the Hebrew scriptures from which Jesus read during his Sabbath teaching in the synagogue, containing the law and commandments of God.

name: exogamy
description: The practice of marrying outside one's own tribe, clan, or social group, as opposed to endogamy which involves marriage within the group.

name: exorcism
description: The employment of one spirit to control or banish another, used as a primitive religious technique for frightening ghosts and spirits. It was part of early religious evolution that progressed from placation and avoidance to more complex spiritual practices.

name: expectations
description: Anticipations or beliefs about what will happen in the future, forming part of human consciousness and motivation.

name: expectoration
description: The act of coughing up and spitting out saliva, mucus, or other substances from the respiratory tract.

name: experience
description: The actual living participation in events and situations that provides knowledge, wisdom, and understanding that cannot be obtained through theory alone. Experience is essential for creature growth and spiritual development, and nothing in the universe can substitute for actual personal experience on non-existential levels.

name: experientials
description: This is earned power, demonstrated power, experiential power that stands in contrast to the eternity power, the unfathomable power, the existential power of the Paradise Deities.

name: experts of communication
description: Experts of communication are part of the Energy Manipulator group of Celestial Artisans who serve as technicians of interplanetary communication. Twelve serve on Urantia and are expert in the laws of transmittal and interference as applied to communications of the realms.

name: exploitation
description: The unfair taking advantage of the weak, unlearned, or less fortunate for selfish gain. Jesus condemned such treatment as incompatible with the ideals of the brotherhood of the kingdom of heaven and taught that no noble person should accumulate riches through the enslavement or unfair exploitation of others.

name: exploration
description: Commerce linked with adventure that led to exploration and discovery in ancient times. On an average world, the post-Adamic dispensation becomes the golden age of exploration and the final subduing of the planet, while the adventurous Andites never stopped until they had circumnavigated the globe and discovered the last remote continent.

name: extension-school instructors
description: Extension-School Instructors are teachers who maintain a strong corps on worlds just below the next higher residential world of the ascendant career. They serve as a sort of preparatory school for progressing residents as part of the ascendant scheme for advancing the pilgrims of time.

name: extinction
description: The eternal obliteration or annihilation of personality existence as the wages of sin and deliberate rebellion against God. This cessation of existence is decreed by the Ancients of Days after exhausting all provisions of mercy, resulting in the complete destruction of sin-identified beings who have wholly embraced iniquity.

name: eyes
description: The material organs of sight that are truly the windows of the spirit-born soul. The human eye can react to only one octave of wavelike energy activity - the light of ordinary sunlight - yet they serve as the means through which the soul looks out upon the world.

name: Ezda
description: Ezda is an orphan boy from Beth-zur who was adopted by John the Baptist and cared for his herds of sheep and goats when John made trips to Hebron or Engedi.

name: Ezekiel
description: Ezekiel was a Jewish prophet who was wiser than his contemporaries, combining personal moral responsibility with faithful observance of purified ritual. He proclaimed deliverance through consecrated service and spoke of a new spirit that would dwell in the soul of man, teaching that religion must become a reality in individual experiences.

name: Ezra (disciple of John)
description: Ezra is a disciple of John the Baptist who rejected Jesus as the Deliverer, separated from John with many followers, and founded a sect that believed in John but refused to accept Jesus. A remnant of this group, which became the foundation of the Mandaean religion, persists in Mesopotamia even today.

name: Ezra of Alexandria
description: Ezra of Alexandria was the father of Susanna, who was one of the twelve female evangelists and among the women who went to anoint Jesus' body at the tomb.

name: Ezra of Nazareth
description: Ezra was a wealthy merchant and trader from Nazareth, father of Rebecca who fell in love with Jesus and sought his hand in marriage when she was seventeen years old.

name: Ezra of Syracuse
description: Ezra was a backslidden Jew who kept a tavern and hospice in Syracuse, whom Jesus helped rehabilitate and return to the faith of Israel during their week-long stay in the city.

name: Ezra (Prophet)
description: Ezra the prophet proclaimed that prosperity would come through adherence to the law, offering hope to the Jews during their period of bondage under foreign rulers.

name: Ezraeon
description: Ezraeon was a friend of Joseph and Mary who owned the boat that carried them from Alexandria to Joppa in August of 4 B.C. as they returned from Egypt.

name: fables
description: Jesus advised against using fables or allegories in teaching gospel truths, instead recommending the use of parables, especially nature parables, to illustrate spiritual concepts.

name: facts
description: Facts are immutable realities that court full comprehension and scientific research, distinct from truth in that one can be technically right as to fact yet wrong in truth, and facts never quarrel with real spiritual faith though theories may.

name: faculty on dissemination and conservation of knowledge
description: One of the ten councils of the Caligastia staff on Urantia, this faculty organized and directed the educational endeavors of early ages under the leadership of Fad.

name: Fad
description: Fad was the director of the faculty in charge of disseminating and preserving knowledge, one of the ten councils of the Caligastia staff on Urantia. He formulated the first 25-character alphabet, introduced a writing system, and sponsored a method of teaching by doing. Fad and five members of his council remained loyal during the Caligastia rebellion.

name: failure
description: A concept representing unsuccessful outcomes or the inability to achieve desired goals or objectives.

name: fainting
description: A temporary loss of consciousness that ancients believed indicated the soul temporarily leaving the body.

name: Fair Havens
description: A location where Jesus intervened to protect a slave girl from assault by a drunken man, an act that left a lasting impression on his traveling companion Ganid.

name: fairies
description: Mythical beings that Oriental and near-Oriental minds delighted in including in stories, and which monotheists kept as subordinate supernatural entities alongside spirits, demons, and other lesser deities.

name: fairness
description: Justice in harmony with mercy that considers underlying motives and circumstances. True fairness requires first being just, then patient and kind, before showing genuine mercy.

name: faith
description: A living attribute of genuine religious experience that motivates life and shapes one's mode of living. Faith is the act of recognizing spiritual consciousness and represents man's trust in God's goodness and guidance, enabling entrance into the spiritual kingdom.

name: faith sons of God
description: Believers who have entered the spirit kingdom and found deliverance from personality isolation through faith. They experience comparative freedom from the bondage of the flesh through wholehearted devotion to doing the Father's will.

name: Faithful of Days of Edentia
description: The Paradise Trinity representative stationed on Edentia who serves as counselor to the Most Highs. He advised allowing Lucifer's rebels free course and presented Michael's salvation plan to Lucifer, though it was rejected.

name: faithfulness
description: The unerring measure of human trustworthiness. Those who are faithful in little things will also be faithful in much, earning the eternal verdict of 'Well done, good and faithful servant.'

name: Faithfuls of Days
description: Paradise Trinity advisors to the rulers of the one hundred constellations in each local universe. These seventy million Trinity-origin personalities serve as counselors to the Vorondadek constellation rulers and represent the final personal presence of Trinity Sons in local universes.

name: falcons
description: Birds of prey that existed among the majority of modern bird species that had developed by 30 million years ago, alongside eagles, hawks, owls, and other contemporary avian forms.

name: fall of man
description: A theological concept referring to humanity's original transgression and subsequent spiritual decline, related to the doctrine of original sin.

name: fallacy of abstraction
description: The philosophical error of focusing on only one aspect of reality and mistakenly proclaiming that isolated aspect to be the complete truth.

name: falsehood
description: Deliberate perversion of truth involving the premeditated twisting of principles for untrue purposes. It provides an opportunity to learn to love truth and differs from mere errors in narration technique.

name: familiarity
description: The principle that close acquaintance often leads to diminished respect or appreciation, as expressed in the saying that familiarity breeds contempt and a prophet is not without honor except in his own country.

name: family
description: The fundamental unit of civilization and human society, serving as the master civilizer where children learn most of life's essentials. It provides the foundation for morality, loyalty, and the understanding of divine relationships through parental love and care.

name: Family Commission of Twelve
description: A commission of twelve beings selected from higher orders of universe personalities who investigated Jewish family life and nominated three prospective families suitable for Michael's incarnation as Jesus.

name: family crests
description: Insignia used to mark private property in early times, representing the origin of family heraldic symbols and establishing ownership rights over land and possessions.

name: family of God
description: One of the terms Jesus used as a synonym for the kingdom of heaven, emphasizing the intimate relationship between God as Father and believers as his children.

name: famine
description: Periods of extreme food scarcity that historically decimated populations. Jesus taught his disciples not to be troubled by famines, and the concept appears in parables illustrating spiritual hunger and awakening.

name: fanaticism
description: Extreme and often destructive religious or ideological zeal that distorts balanced judgment and leads to bigotry, intolerance, and harmful behavior. It represents a dangerous deviation from healthy spiritual faith and must be prevented through balanced thinking.

name: fandors
description: Large passenger birds used for transportation, capable of carrying riders across distances.

name: Fanoving
description: A neighboring local universe to Nebadon, located in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: Fantad
description: Leader of the green race who delivered them from darkness and led their cultural revival 350,000 years ago, serving as one of the 24 counselors and promoting worship of "The One Source of Life."

name: farewell salutations
description: Jesus characteristically avoided formal farewell ceremonies, often departing suddenly and unceremoniously without saying goodbye to friends or associates, as demonstrated when he left Rome, Cana, and during his incarnations.

name: farm
description: Jesus dreamed of owning a farm in the country where his family could enjoy greater freedom, but this aspiration was never realized, though they maintained livestock including cows, sheep, chickens, and other animals at their Nazareth home.

name: Faroes
description: Islands used as a route by Salem missionaries traveling to reach the Andonites of Iceland as part of their efforts to spread religious teachings throughout Europe.

name: fashion
description: One of humanity's institutions of self-gratification, arising from vanity and pride emotions, which continues to unduly dominate human civilization and represents a form of slavery to custom and tradition.

name: fasting
description: An ancient religious practice of abstaining from food, often used as self-denial or purification ritual, though Jesus taught that fasting was not required in the gospel of the kingdom of heaven and could be spiritually dangerous when prolonged.

name: fat
description: One of various bodily locations where ancient peoples believed the soul resided, along with the breath, head, hair, heart, liver, and blood, with animal fat consumption being taboo among some groups.

name: fatalism
description: A philosophical belief in predetermined fate that Jesus' teachings opposed, as his instruction to trust in the heavenly Father's care was not blind or passive resignation but an active faith that empowered believers to influence their spiritual destiny.

name: Fate
description: A Greek concept that prevented the evolution of Zeus worship into true monotheism, as the Greeks believed Fate controlled even the gods rather than Zeus being the ultimate arbiter of destiny.

name: fates
description: Lesser supernatural beings that monotheists retain alongside spirits, demons, and other subordinate entities after progressing beyond polytheism to belief in one supreme deity.

name: Father-Absolute
description: A designation for the Universal Father emphasizing his absolute nature and primacy as the First Source and Center.

name: Father-Brahma
description: A Vedic concept of the source of all gods that was similar to the Salem missionaries' teaching of the one God of Melchizedek, though it remained ritualistic in nature.

name: Father fragments
description: Divine fragments of the Universal Father that indwell mortal minds, also known as Thought Adjusters.

name: Father-fused mortals
description: Ascending mortals who achieve eternal fusion with their indwelling Thought Adjusters, becoming one with a fragment of the Universal Father.

name: Father guides
description: Secondary supernaphim who serve as tutors to pilgrim souls on the third circle of Havona, being the older, highly skilled, and most experienced superaphic ministers who maintain schools of wisdom and colleges of technique.

name: Father-Infinite
description: A concept denoting the infinity and primacy of the Universal Father as the First Source and Center, representing the actual value-level of the absolute and unfettered free will of God.

name: Father Melchizedek
description: The original and firstborn Melchizedek Son of Nebadon, created through the union of the Creator Son and Creative Spirit, who serves as Gabriel's executive associate and assumes his responsibilities during his absence from Salvington.

name: fatherhood of God
description: The fundamental concept that God is the divine Father of all moral creatures, which forms the basis for recognizing the brotherhood of all humanity and represents the highest human concept of deity.

name: Fatherlikeness
description: The achievement of divine character qualities that resemble the Father's nature. Success in seeking the Infinite is directly proportional to achieving Fatherlikeness, which involves embodying the qualities of divinity by living the will of God.

name: fathers
description: According to Jesus, true fathers provide existence, security and pleasure, education and training, discipline and restraint, companionship and loyalty, love and mercy, and provision for the future. Children need fathers as well as mothers, and fathers need this parental experience equally. A child derives his first ideas of the heavenly Father from his earthly father.

name: Father’s business
description: Jesus' divine mission and work as the Son of God on earth. The phrase was first spoken by a messenger from Salvington who told Jesus 'The hour has come. It is time that you began to be about your Father's business,' marking the beginning of his public ministry.

name: Father’s house
description: A term used by Jesus to refer to the Jerusalem temple and to Paradise as the Universal Father's eternal residence. Paradise's seven zones are called 'the Father's Paradise mansions,' and each station in the Father's universe is a stopping place designed to prepare mortals for the next stage of eternal progression.

name: Father’s prayer
description: A prayer taught by Hap to the early Dalamatians that began: 'Father of all, whose Son we honor, look down upon us with favor. Deliver us from the fear of all save you. Make us a pleasure to our divine teachers and forever put truth on our lips.' It was among the first formal religious prayers given to humanity.

name: Father’s Way
description: A moral code presented by Hap to the early races in Dalamatia, consisting of seven commands including honoring only the Father of all, obeying the Father's Son, speaking truth, not killing, not stealing, not committing adultery, and respecting parents and elders. This code exemplified an ideal of creature life visualized by Paradise administrators.

name: Father’s world
description: A reference to the Universal Father's domain or sphere of influence, though specific details are not provided in the available context.

name: fatigue
description: Physical or mental exhaustion that can induce mystical states and affect spiritual progress. Self-importance, not work-importance, exhausts immature creatures. Reversion directors on Jerusem help beings achieve thought change and mind rest to recuperate depleted energies, as variety is restful while monotony wears and exhausts.

name: favoritism
description: Showing partiality or special preference. God is no respecter of persons, showing no favoritism based on spiritual or material status, loving all his children equally. Believers should not expect God to show partiality or love them more than others, and should avoid making distinctions based on race, social status, or other factors in their ministry.

name: fear
description: An emotion that has played a fundamental role in human evolution and religion, though it should be transcended through spiritual growth. While fear of the Lord can be the beginning of wisdom, mature religion replaces fear with love, reverence, and willing worship. Fear persists in present-day races due to the lack of Adamic inheritance and animal legacy, but faith can overcome fear and lead to spiritual freedom.

name: fests
description: Ancient ceremonial gatherings that concluded with communal feasting, representing the social and celebratory aspects of religious and cultural rituals, as contrasted with Jesus's teaching about spiritual feasting over physical fasting.

name: feeble-minded
description: Individuals with intellectual disabilities or mental deficiencies, often referenced in discussions of human diversity and social care.

name: feeding the 5000
description: The miraculous multiplication of food to feed five thousand people, which Jesus performed as his first and only consciously preplanned nature miracle, representing the apex of popular miracle-seeking expectations and marking a turning point in his ministry toward more spiritual teachings.

name: feelings
description: Human emotional responses and inner experiences that influence behavior, relationships, and spiritual development.

name: feet
description: The body parts that Jesus ceremonially washed for his apostles during the Last Supper, demonstrating humble service and teaching about spiritual cleansing and leadership through servanthood.

name: felines
description: Cat-like animals representing one branch of mammalian evolution, including both domestic cats and wild cat species.

name: fellowship
description: The bonds of companionship, mutual support, and shared purpose that unite individuals in friendship and common endeavors.

name: fellowship of believers
description: The spiritual brotherhood of those who follow Jesus's teachings, characterized by unity rather than uniformity, representing the true invisible church that transcends institutional boundaries and emphasizes living faith over organizational structure.

name: ferns
description: Ancient leafless plants that dominated Earth's vegetation 230 million years ago, forming vast forests up to one hundred feet tall during the geological age of ferns, later being largely replaced by conifers and other modern trees.

name: fertility
description: The capacity for reproduction and childbearing, highly valued in ancient cultures where childlessness was greatly feared and various magical or religious ceremonies were performed to ensure fecundity in marriage unions.

name: fetishes
description: Objects believed to be inhabited by spirits or ghosts, ranging from sacred stones and animals to human body parts and natural phenomena. Fetishism represents one of humanity's earliest religious practices, evolving from simple belief in spirit-indwelt objects to complex systems of taboos and worship that influenced primitive cults, trade practices, and social customs.

name: feudalism
description: A medieval European governmental system characterized by small castle groups and clan-based organization that emerged during the dark ages when centralized territorial states collapsed, representing a regression to pre-state conditions of development.

name: fields
description: Jesus' metaphorical reference to people ready to receive spiritual teaching, declaring that "the fields are already white for the harvest" when speaking of the Samaritans who came to hear his message, indicating spiritual readiness for the gospel.

name: fig tree
description: A plant species that appeared during the Cretaceous period and serves as the subject of Jesus' parables about spiritual fruitfulness and recognizing the signs of changing dispensations, including the barren fig tree parable about producing fruits of the spirit.

name: fighting
description: Physical combat or conflict, which Jesus consistently avoided despite societal expectations. He taught principles of non-resistance while acknowledging that organized society has the right to employ force for justice, and emphasized that engaging in fights often stems from a need to prove one's courage rather than defend righteousness.

name: finaliter corps
description: The organized body of perfected ascendant mortals and other beings who have completed the Paradise ascension and been mustered into eternal service, functioning as the highest order of evolutionary creatures in universe administration.

name: finaliters
description: Perfected spirit personalities who have completed the Paradise ascension and been inducted into the Corps of the Finality. These glorified beings serve throughout the universes in various capacities, are invisible to mortals except in morontia temples, and represent the ultimate destiny for Adjuster-fused ascending mortals, though their final purpose remains largely unrevealed.

name: finaliters’ world
description: The first transitional culture world of Jerusem, serving as headquarters for the finaliter corps of the local system. This sphere houses over one hundred thousand companies of finaliters and includes a probation nursery for children, while providing visualization experiences for mansion world inhabitants to glimpse their ultimate destiny.

name: fine arts
description: Creative artistic expressions and cultural achievements that represent refined aesthetic accomplishments in civilized societies.

name: fines
description: Monetary or material penalties imposed for taboo violations and crimes, representing an early advancement in justice systems. These constituted the first public revenue and evolved from the practice of paying blood money as compensation for wrongs, eventually establishing standardized punishment through financial restitution.

name: fingernails
description: Parts of the human body considered potential fetishes in early magic, particularly valued when belonging to chiefs whose long-growing fingernails and their trimmings were regarded as powerful fetishes.

name: fingers
description: Body parts associated with various superstitious practices, including crossing fingers as a modern vestige of ancient magical formulas and pointing fingers, which was once considered dangerous and is still regarded as ill-mannered.

name: finite reality
description: The level of reality characterized by limitations and boundaries, as distinguished from infinite and absolute levels of reality.

name: finites
description: Beings or entities characterized by limitations and boundaries, existing within the finite level of reality as contrasted with infinite or absolute beings.

name: Finns
description: A Nordic people who believed that most trees were occupied by kind spirits, distinguishing them from other cultures that viewed tree spirits as cruel or deceptive.

name: fire
description: A fundamental discovery made by Andon that forever separated humans from animals, serving as the first great civilizer by providing protection, light, heat, and enabling social intercourse, while also becoming an object of worship and superstition among many primitive peoples.

name: fire god
description: A deity concept associated with fire worship, where fire itself was venerated as a divine being rather than merely as a symbol of spiritual purification or divine presence.

name: first
description: A divine attribute expressing God's eternal primacy and supremacy, as declared in scripture: 'I am the first, and I am the last, and beside me there is no God,' while also referring to the paradoxical teaching that many who are first shall be last, and the last shall often be first.

name: first-born
description: The eldest child who in ancient times was often subject to ritual sacrifice during foundation ceremonies or times of great stress, a practice that Moses attempted to end by establishing a ransom system as substitute.

name: First Cause
description: The ultimate originating source of all existence, recognized by philosophers like Anaxagoras as an Initial Mind, but requiring religious faith to transform this logical First Cause into the personal God of salvation—the Universal Father of Paradise.

name: First Epistle of Peter
description: A letter written by the apostle Peter that demonstrated his vigorous style and teaching, though it was subsequently altered by a disciple of Paul.

name: first experiential Trinity
description: The first of the experiential Trinities, related to the Trinity Ultimate in the cosmic evolution of deity relationships.

name: first mansion world
description: The initial world in the mansion world progression where mortal survivors begin their morontia career after death.

name: First Person of Deity
description: The Universal Father as the first person of the Paradise Trinity and original source of all personality.

name: First Source and Center
description: The Universal Father as the absolute source and eternal center of all reality, energy, and personality in the universe of universes.

name: fish
description: Marine vertebrates that appeared as the last of fourteen phyla on Urantia and represent the first backboned animals, from which evolved frogs, salamanders, and ultimately the evolutionary line leading to humans.

name: fishermen
description: Those who catch fish for livelihood, including many of Jesus' apostles such as Peter, Andrew, James, and John, who were partners in the fishing business at Bethsaida before becoming 'fishers of men.'

name: flags
description: National symbols that evolved from ancient totems, serving as emblems of group identity and consciousness, with civilized peoples ensuring they never touch the ground as symbols of respect.

name: flamingoes
description: Large wading birds that were among the majority of modern bird species that existed 30 million years ago during a period of continued avian development.

name: flattery
description: Insincere praise that Jesus warned against as a slippery path, exemplified when Eve was influenced by Cano's flattery to participate in the scheme that led to the Adamic default.

name: Flavius
description: Flavius was a Greek Jew and proselyte of the gate who owned a beautiful home in Jerusalem adorned with priceless art treasures from his world travels. Jesus stayed at his home during visits to Jerusalem, and prominent Jews would come there secretly to interview the Master. It was also the location of Jesus' sixth morontia appearance to about forty Greek believers after his resurrection.

name: flaws
description: It requires no great depth of intellect to pick flaws, ask questions, or raise objections, but it does require brilliance of mind to answer these questions and solve difficulties; faith certainty is the greatest technique for dealing with such superficial contentions.

name: flesh
description: The flesh represents the inherent nature derived from animal-origin races that does not naturally bear the fruits of the divine Spirit. It encompasses the physical body, material appetites, and primitive instincts that often conflict with spiritual aspirations in mortal life.

name: flint
description: Flint was a crucial stone material used by early humans for making tools and weapons. Andon discovered fire by striking flint, and the Andonites traveled far and wide searching for flint deposits, fashioning it into axes, spears, and other implements that were essential for hunting and survival.

name: flogging
description: Flogging was a form of physical punishment involving whipping with knotted whips. Jesus was unjustly subjected to scourging by Roman soldiers before his crucifixion, though Roman law only permitted such treatment for those already condemned to die by crucifixion.

name: floods
description: Floods were significant geological and historical events that shaped civilizations and landscapes throughout Urantia's history. Notable floods include those that disrupted Mesopotamian settlements around 5,000 B.C., the Mediterranean flood that created the greatest loss of life in world history, and various periodic inundations during geological ages.

name: Florissant fossil beds
description: The Florissant fossil beds in Colorado contain fossils from ancient times, including many extinct insect families that were similar to those of previous geological eras.

name: flute players
description: Flute players were musicians employed in Palestinian houses of mourning to provide music during funeral proceedings and grieving rituals.

name: focus
description: The ability to concentrate attention and energy on a particular object, activity, or goal.

name: fog
description: Fog represents both a natural atmospheric phenomenon and a metaphor for uncertainty. Neanderthalers were deathly afraid of mists and fogs, while spiritually, fog symbolizes the mortal uncertainty that faith can help us see beyond to divine clarity.

name: Fonta
description: The female companion of Andon, together forming the first human pair to evolve on Urantia approximately one million years ago.

name: food
description: Essential sustenance that played a crucial role in human evolution and social development, from primitive cannibalism to the development of agriculture, food storage, and cooking methods. Food offerings were central to early religious practices, and Jesus taught about spiritual food as nourishment for the soul.

name: foolish rich man
description: A character from Jesus' parable who focused solely on accumulating earthly wealth while neglecting spiritual treasures, ultimately losing everything when his soul was required of him that very night.

name: footprints
description: Fossilized traces of land reptiles preserved in sandstone layers, providing evidence of ancient life during prehistoric periods on Urantia.

name: foraminifers
description: Prolific chalk-making marine organisms that dominated the Cretaceous seas, creating extensive limestone deposits found in mountain ranges like the Alps, Himalayas, and other regions worldwide.

name: forbidden fruit
description: A symbolic representation of taboos and restrictions, literally referring to the apple but figuratively encompassing all manner of prohibitions that primitive tribes imposed on themselves.

name: force
description: A broadly used term encompassing spiritual, mindal, and material energies, particularly referring to the pregravity stages of cosmic energy that circulate throughout the universes and originate from Paradise.

name: force-charge of space
