description: The primordial energy charge of space, also known as pure energy or segregata, representing the first stage of space potency individuation into pre-energy forms of cosmic force.

name: force-energy
description: The combined manifestation of force and energy that originally proceeded from Paradise and will eventually return there, encompassing all physical energies that circulate throughout the universe of universes.

name: foreknowledge
description: The divine attribute of knowing future events in advance, related to but distinct from omniscience, representing God's awareness of what will come to pass.

name: foreskins
description: Foreskins are mentioned in the context of ancient mutilation practices, specifically referencing Saul's demand for one hundred Philistine foreskins as David's dowry for his daughter Michal.

name: forests
description: Forests are vast woodland areas that evolved on early Earth and played a significant role in human development. The Andonites avoided forests in contrast to their nonhuman relatives, as human evolution made progress in open lands rather than in forested environments where humans tend to deteriorate.

name: Foreword
description: The Foreword to The Urantia Book is a definitive guide designed to assist readers in understanding the terminology and concepts used in papers 1-31, rather than being a finished statement within itself.

name: forgetfulness
description: Human forgetfulness refers to the mortal tendency to lose sight of spiritual truths and divine guidance, which believers must not fear as they progress on the way of life everlasting.

name: forgiveness
description: Forgiveness is the divine act of pardoning sin and wrongdoing, representing God's eternal love and mercy toward his children. It involves both receiving God's forgiveness through faith and extending forgiveness to one's fellow beings, creating a continuous cycle of divine and human mercy.

name: Formosa
description: Formosa is an island in East Asia that was reached by Andite migrations, along with Japan and the East Indies, though very few Andites entered the region by the coastal route.

name: forms
description: Forms are external structures or methods that become valueless once their underlying lessons have been learned, like a shell that serves no purpose after a chick has hatched.

name: Fortant
description: Fortant is a secondary Lanonandek Son numbered 319,847 who serves as the bestowal director among the seven Lanonandeks administering Satania. He has been attached to Lanaforge's staff for nineteen hundred years and directs all universe activities transplanted to Jerusem since Michael's bestowal on Urantia.

name: Fortune
description: Fortune was a fearful and downcast young man whom Jesus encountered in the mountains of Crete, who had grown up feeling helpless and inferior, especially after losing his father at age twelve. He later became the leader of the Christians in Crete and a close associate of Titus.

name: fortunetellers
description: Fortunetellers are practitioners who claim to predict the future, often associated with astrology and stargazing. They experienced a revival during the Babylonian worship of Ishtar and continue to be patronized by credulous people despite repeated attempts by authorities to banish such practices.

name: forty days
description: A period of retirement following Jesus's baptism during which he formulated plans and determined the technique for proclaiming the new kingdom of God in human hearts.

name: forum
description: The center of Roman political, legal, and business life where Jesus frequently spent time during his stay in Rome.

name: fossils
description: Preserved remains of ancient life forms found in rock formations that provide a record of evolutionary development throughout Earth's geological history.

name: Foundation of Spiritual Progress
description: A newly created institution on a neighboring planet that supervises spiritual teachers and pastors who examine families, supported by voluntary contributions.

name: fountain gate
description: A gate in Jerusalem located near the pool of Siloam, used during religious processions in the Feast of Tabernacles ceremonies.

name: four and twenty counselors
description: A commission of twenty-four former Urantians who serve as Michael's personal agents on Jerusem, functioning as advisory supervisors for rebellion-isolated worlds and representing the Master Son in matters concerning Satania.

name: four noble truths
description: The foundational teachings of Gautama Buddha's original gospel, consisting of the truths of suffering, the origins of suffering, the destruction of suffering, and the way to the destruction of suffering.

name: fourth creatures (cherubim)
description: Quasi-material angelic beings that are every fourth cherubim and sanobim, characterized by their morontia-like nature and efficiency in borderland work between physical, morontial, and spiritual domains.

name: fourth creatures (Havona Servitals)
aliases: fourth creatures of the Havona Servitals
description: Semiphysical beings comprising every fourth Havona Servital, more material in nature than their spirit counterparts and capable of discerning both spirit and material realities.

name: fourth creatures (Universal Conciliators)
description: Quasi-material beings who serve as Divine Executioners among the Universal Conciliators, capable of making contact with material beings and almost visible to mortal vision.

name: foxes
description: Small carnivorous mammals that Jesus referenced in his teaching about the Son of Man having nowhere to lay his head, contrasting their secure dens with his own lack of earthly dwelling.

name: Foxhall peoples
description: An early Andonite race in England who were the first human beings to live in those regions and succeeded in retaining much of the Andonic culture, including knowledge of flintworking which they transmitted to their descendants, the ancient ancestors of the Eskimos.

name: fragmentation
description: The divine process by which certain Deity beings distribute individualized portions of their nature to other entities, best exemplified by Thought Adjusters from the Universal Father, though the Eternal Son cannot fragmentize due to his exclusively personal nature.

name: France
description: A European region where early Andonites lived along the river Somme for tens of thousands of years, connected to England by a land bridge during ancient times, and later inhabited by various evolutionary races including the blue man and white races.

name: franchise
description: The right to vote or participate in governmental decisions.

name: frandalanks
description: Living and intelligent Master Physical Controllers created in thirty divisions who function as automatic presence, pressure, and velocity gauges for all forms of universe force-energy, stationed on inhabited worlds and attached to higher orders of physical controllers.

name: frankness
description: The quality of being open, honest, and straightforward in communication.

name: fraud
description: Dishonest practices involving deception or trickery to secure unfair gain, which Jesus taught should be corrected through restoration of ill-gotten wealth to rightful owners and making full amends for dishonest riches.

name: free love
description: Sexual relationships without formal marriage commitment or legal bonds.

name: free will
description: The divine endowment enabling conscious beings to make genuine choices within established limits, constituting the universe insignia of freewill creaturehood and making possible moral decisions, spiritual growth, and eternal destiny determination through voluntary cooperation with God's will.

name: friction shields
description: Friction shields are energy insulators used by transport seraphim that appear as double sets of wings extending from head to foot. These shields must be fully open to safely deposit a sleeping personality onto the transport angel for interplanetary transit, after which they are carefully closed and adjusted for the journey.

name: Fridays
description: Friday has been regarded as an unlucky day for ages, along with the number thirteen as an evil numeral, representing ancient superstitions and fetishes about certain days of the week.

name: friends of the court
description: High Commissioners serve as friends of the court in celestial tribunals, advising presiding magistrates about the background, environment, and inherent nature of those involved in legal proceedings without directly participating in the justice process.

name: friendship
description: Friendship is a fundamental aspect of personality relationships that transcends duty and creates deep bonds between beings. True friendship involves mutual self-expression, shared wisdom, enhanced defense against evil, and loving service that enriches both participants and survives even death, as personality relationships have cosmic value and persist throughout the universe career.

name: frogs
description: Frogs were crucial ancestors of land vertebrates and represent one of the most important evolutionary developments leading to humanity. They evolved from fish and later developed into prereptiles, with the ancestral frog of all humanity narrowly escaping extinction on several occasions. Frogs are the only remaining group representative of man's earlier ancestors and serve as a reminder of evolutionary progression.

name: fruit
description: Fruit represents both literal plant reproduction and spiritual development. In Jesus' teachings, bearing spiritual fruit demonstrates genuine faith and connection to God, manifesting as loving service, devotion, loyalty, and other divine qualities in the lives of spirit-born individuals.

name: fruit vendor of Messina
description: A small boy who sold fruit in Messina whose life was changed by a brief encounter with Jesus. Jesus bought fruit from him and shared spiritual wisdom, placing his hand on the boy's shoulder with words of encouragement. The lad later became a devotee of Mithraism before converting to Christianity.

name: fruits of the spirit
description: The fruits of the spirit are the observable manifestations of divine influence in the lives of spirit-led mortals, including loving service, unselfish devotion, courageous loyalty, sincere fairness, enlightened honesty, undying hope, confiding trust, merciful ministry, unfailing goodness, forgiving tolerance, and enduring peace. These spiritual fruits serve as convincing evidence of genuine religious experience and God-connection.

name: fulcrum
description: The psychic fulcrum is the mental foundation that humans provide through their decisions and choices, which enables the Thought Adjuster to apply spiritual leverage for uplifting and advancing spiritual illumination.

name: fundraising
description: Matthew conducted quiet, personal fundraising for Jesus' ministry, receiving voluntary offerings from believing disciples and immediate followers rather than openly soliciting funds from crowds. He raised most money from substantial believers while keeping his own generous contributions anonymous.

name: funerals
description: Ceremonial practices surrounding death that originated in humanity's efforts to ensure departed souls would successfully reach their afterlife destinations. Ancient funeral customs involved elaborate rituals, material provisions for the deceased, and significant expenditures of wealth and resources. On advanced worlds settled in light and life, funerals become occasions of supreme joy and inexpressible hope as mortals witness their loved ones ascend in spiritual flames during fusion with their indwelling Adjusters.

name: fungi
description: Many fungi represent a retrograde movement in evolution, being plants that have lost their chlorophyll-making ability and become parasitic in nature.

name: fusion
description: The eternal union of a mortal's immortal soul with a divine spirit fragment, enabling perfected creature existence that can be sustained throughout eternity. This can occur through fusion with fragments from the Universal Father (Adjuster fusion), Eternal Son (Son fusion), or Infinite Spirit (Spirit fusion). Fusion represents the ultimate achievement of harmony between creature will and divine will, transforming the mortal into a new order of universe citizen with unlimited potential for eternal progression.

name: future
description: The future signifies struggle and advancement, representing work, effort, and achievement for ascending beings. It embodies uncertainty as an essential element for continued growth, as knowledge of future events would be detrimental to creature development and success.

name: future eternals
description: Experiential Deities such as God the Supreme and God the Ultimate who are actualizing in the post-Havona universes through evolutionary experience. Unlike the existential Paradise Deities who have always existed, these future eternals have personality beginnings in time but will achieve unlimited eternal existence as they power-personalize through the experiential actualization of divine potentials.

name: Gabriel
description: The Bright and Morning Star of Nebadon, Gabriel serves as the chief executive of the local universe and personal representative of Creator Son Michael. As the first-born of all creatures native to the local universe, he functions as the supreme commander of the heavenly hosts and administers universe affairs during Michael's absence. Gabriel played a crucial role in Michael's earth bestowal, appearing to Mary and Elizabeth to announce the births of Jesus and John the Baptist.

name: Gad
description: One of the reputed tribes of Israel that traditionally dwelt in the regions east of the Jordan River, along with the tribes of Reuben and Gilead.

name: Gadara
description: A city in the Decapolis region where Thomas Didymus once worked as a carpenter and stone mason before becoming an apostle. Gadara was visited multiple times during Jesus' ministry, including the first work of the twelve apostles, the preaching tours of the Decapolis, the second tour of Galilee, and the final mission in Perea.

name: Gadda
description: A town in Perea that was visited during the final three-month mission conducted by the seventy messengers, the twelve apostles, and Jesus before his journey to Jerusalem for his final experiences in the flesh.

name: Gadiah
description: A Philistine interpreter who worked for Simon the tanner in Joppa and became a close friend of Jesus during their stay there. As a truth seeker who met Jesus, the great truth giver, Gadiah experienced profound enlightenment. He later became a Christian after listening to the apostle Peter, with whom he had a memorable theological controversy.

name: Gaius
description: Jesus met Gaius at the house of Crispus in Corinth; he later became a loyal supporter of Paul.

name: Galantia
description: Galantia is the ascendant associate of Gavalia, Nebadon's first Brilliant Evening Star, and is the first ascendant to enter service in Nebadon. She currently maintains headquarters on Jerusem and serves as associate chief of these superangels.

name: Galatia
description: A region where Matthew preached the gospel and baptized believers during his missionary journeys after the persecution of believers in Jerusalem.

name: galaxies
description: In The Urantia Book, galaxies are vast star formations that race around Paradise in orderly procession within space levels. The book indicates that 375 million new galaxies will soon be observed by astronomers, and describes how galaxies in outer space levels revolve in alternating directions to maintain cosmic stability.

name: Galilee
description: A northern province of Palestine where Jesus conducted his ministry and recruited most of his apostles. Galilee was more gentile than Jewish, more prosperous than Judea, and its residents were not fully favored by Jerusalem's religious leaders. Jesus conducted three major preaching tours throughout Galilee during his public ministry.

name: gall
description: According to Jewish belief, a drop of gall on the point of the sword of the angel of death began to work by the end of the third day and took full effect on the fourth day after death.

name: Gamala
description: A city visited during Jesus' second public preaching tour of Galilee and later during the second tour of the Decapolis. It was here that Jesus taught about the fear of the Lord and where John encountered a strange preacher teaching in Jesus' name.

name: Gamaliel
description: A renowned teacher and leading rabbi in Jerusalem who advised the Sanhedrin against imprisoning Jesus' followers, counseling them to let the movement alone since if it was of God, they could not overthrow it.

name: gambling
description: Primitive man was a natural-born gambler who always wanted to get something for nothing. Early humans lived in uncertainty and constant fear of chance, making existence itself a gamble that fascinated the savage mind.

name: games
description: Modern games of chance and children's counting-out rhymes are survivals of ancient shamanic practices of casting lots to determine the will of spirits and make decisions.

name: gamma rays
description: Gamma rays are electromagnetic radiations that characterize the spontaneous dissociation of atomic matter, with radium disintegration being the best illustration of this form of electronic activity.

name: Ganges
description: A major river in India where Andites migrated around 15,000 B.C., spreading over the Indus and Ganges valleys. The Ganges delta was largely formed over the last 50,000 years, and its valley became a center where Aryan and Dravidian peoples eventually mingled to produce a high culture.

name: Ganid
description: A brilliant seventeen-year-old Indian boy who traveled with Jesus and his father Gonod for nearly two years, during which Jesus served as their interpreter and Ganid's tutor. Under Jesus' direction, he compiled teachings from world religions recognizing a Universal Deity and later became an influential man in India, spreading the noble truths he learned from his beloved teacher.

name: Garden of Eden
description: The headquarters and dwelling place of Adam and Eve on Urantia, located on a Mediterranean peninsula and designed as a center of world culture and biological uplift. Named for its floral beauty patterned after Edentia, it served as a great planetary university until its submergence beneath the Mediterranean Sea following Adam and Eve's default.

name: gardeners
description: The spornagia are the landscape gardeners of the headquarters worlds, serving as both original and artistic cultivators of the open spaces of Jerusem and other administrative spheres.

name: Gardens of Eden
description: The garden homes of Material Sons and Daughters on evolutionary worlds, named in honor of Edentia and patterned after its botanic grandeur. These beautiful centers of culture serve as the second center of world civilization, working in cooperation with the Planetary Prince's headquarters city.

name: gardens of God
description: The vast natural park comprising half of Edentia, the constellation capital, featuring exquisite botanic artistry and living embellishments that represent the acme of morontia activities and serve as the most entrancing creations of the local universe.

name: gas chambers
description: Lethal execution chambers used by courts on a highly developed continental nation to sentence serious habitual criminals and the incurably insane to death.

name: gastropods
description: Ancient shell animals including single-shelled drills, periwinkles, snails, and bivalve forms like mussels, clams, oysters, and scallops that were present in primitive seas 310 million years ago and have remained largely unchanged through the geological ages.

name: gate beautiful
description: A gate in the Jerusalem temple that opened from the court of the women onto the court of the gentiles, where worshipers would turn westward during the Feast of Tabernacles ceremony to repeat their chants and continue their march for the symbolic water.

name: Gath
description: A Philistine city where David and his forces were ordered off the battlefield by Philistine commanders who feared he might switch sides and join Saul during their attack on Israel.

name: Gautama Siddhartha
description: A great religious teacher of the 6th century BC, born in Nepal as heir to a petty chieftain in the Himalayas. Known as Buddha to his followers, he experienced a sudden spiritual conversion and developed the trinitarian doctrine of Master, Law, and Brotherhood. His teachings emphasized universal salvation without sacrifice, torture, ritual, or priests, though he failed to perceive the personality of God or provide adequate spiritual driving power for lasting religious transformation.

name: Gavalia
description: The firstborn and chief of the Brilliant Evening Stars in Nebadon, assigned to ascendant mortal ministry since Michael's return from his bestowal on Urantia. His ascendant associate is Galantia, the first to attain superangel status in Nebadon. Gavalia authored Urantia Paper 119 on the seven bestowals of Michael of Nebadon.

name: Gaza
description: A coastal city in Palestine that Jesus visited during his personal tour through Syria and Palestine. It later became part of the route followed by early Christian missionaries as they spread the gospel throughout the Roman Empire.

name: Geba
description: A city in Palestine that Jesus passed through during his final tour as a private individual, traveling northward from Beersheba to Dan through the heart of the country.

name: Gehenna
description: Open burial pits located south of Jerusalem where the bodies of crucified criminals, including thieves, were typically disposed of and exposed to wild beasts. The Jewish rulers originally planned to have Jesus' body thrown there after his crucifixion.

name: genealogies
description: Living ready-reference records maintained by the Significances of Origins, a type of reflective seconaphim, containing complete ancestral information and current status data for all beings—mortals, angels, and others—throughout the seven superuniverses.

name: general council
description: A gathering of Jesus' followers held in Philadelphia where they discussed the progress of the gospel throughout Palestine and surrounding regions, and where Abner preached about Jesus' parable teachings.

name: Genesis, Book of
description: The biblical account containing the story of Eve's creation from Adam's rib, which represents a confused condensation of the Adamic arrival and the celestial surgery involving living substance interchange that occurred with the Planetary Prince's corporeal staff over 450,000 years earlier.

name: genetics
description: The biological science of heredity and inheritance. Hybridization improves species through dominant genes, and racial intermixture increases the likelihood of desirable dominant traits appearing in offspring. While hereditary factors influence physical and mental capabilities, they do not prevent spiritual achievement, as all mortals possess uniform spiritual endowment regardless of their genetic inheritance.

name: Genghis Khan
description: The Mongol military leader who represented the last great manifestation of Andite military genius in central Asia around A.D. 1200. He was a monotheist who proclaimed the existence of "one God in heaven," and his tribesmen still await his return.

name: genius
description: Exceptional intellectual or creative ability that primitive peoples often confused with insanity or divine possession. Genius owes something to both ancestors and progeny, as well as to the race and circumstances that enabled inventive discoveries. Normal humans are the backbone of civilization and the source of mutant geniuses.

name: Gennesaret
description: A region on the western shores of the Sea of Galilee where Jesus held informal conferences with the twelve apostles after the feeding of the five thousand. This is also where Jesus and the twelve ended their journey back from Phoenicia on August 1, A.D. 29.

name: gentiles
description: Non-Jewish peoples who, though morally somewhat inferior to the Jews, possessed abundant natural goodness and potential for spiritual growth. Each Jewish synagogue tolerated a fringe of gentile believers called "devout" or "God-fearing" men, among whom Paul made many early Christian converts.

name: gentleness
description: One of the fruits of the Spirit that increasingly manifests in the life reactions of spirit-led mortals, along with love, joy, peace, long-suffering, goodness, faith, meekness, and temperance.

name: geography
description: The physical features and land arrangements of continents that greatly influence the development of civilizations. Geographic barriers like mountains and oceans can protect peoples from invasion, as China was protected by mountains to the west and the Pacific to the east, allowing for unmolested cultural development.

name: geologic ages
description: The major divisions of Earth's history spanning one billion years, including the Archeozoic (prelife era), Proterozoic (life-dawn era), Paleozoic (marine-life era), Mesozoic (early land-life era), and Cenozoic (mammalian era), each marked by distinctive forms of life and geological developments.

name: geology
description: The science that studies Earth's physical structure and history through rock formations and fossils. The planet's geological story is recorded in the "stone book" of stratified rock layers, revealing alternating periods of land and sea dominance that have occurred in million-year cycles throughout Earth's history.

name: Gerar
description: A place where Abraham moved after Melchizedek's departure from Salem. There Abraham became fearful and timid, withholding his identity so that Abimelech appropriated his wife Sarah.

name: Gerasa
description: One of the principal cities of the Decapolis that Jesus visited multiple times during his ministry. It was included in his first Passover journey with his brother Simon, his preaching tours of the Decapolis, and his final Perean mission, where he gave a memorable discourse on "how many will be saved."

name: Gerizim
description: A mountain mentioned in The Urantia Book, also known as Mount Gerizim.

name: germ plasm
description: The hereditary material of an organism, related to life plasm in biological processes.

name: Germany
description: A European nation where Nordic-Danish and Danubian-Andonite cultures met and mingled on the Rhine, resulting in two racial groups that exist today. Germans are descendants of Andite and blue races, ancestors of the Nordic peoples including Scandinavians and Anglo-Saxons.

name: Geshur
description: A kingdom whose ruler Talmai was father-in-law to King David, who married Talmai's daughter as one of his political marriages.

name: gesture
description: A form of communication older than speech that was considered more holy and magical, with mimicry believed to possess strong magical power in primitive cultures.

name: Gethsemane
description: A shaded park or garden on the western slope of Mount Olivet near Jerusalem where Jesus and his apostles camped during their stays in the area. It was the site of Jesus' final prayer before his arrest and served as their headquarters during the last week of his earthly life.

name: ghosts
description: Spiritual entities believed by primitive peoples to be the souls of the dead, forming the foundation of early religion through ghost cults. These beliefs evolved as protection against bad luck and fear of the dead, with ghosts thought to influence the living and require appeasement through rituals and offerings.

name: giantism
description: Unexpected strains of unusually large physical stature that appeared among the green and orange evolutionary races on Urantia, contrary to the general trend of decreasing mortal stature from red to indigo races.

name: gibbons
description: Primates that killed the parents of Andon and Fonta, the first human beings, in raids on their forest home. The early lemurs ancestral to humans were not directly related to the pre-existing gibbon tribes of Eurasia and northern Africa.

name: Gibeon
description: A Canaanite city that had a peace treaty with the Ephraimites and was attacked by King Saul, whose defeat David attributed to Yahweh's displeasure over this violation of the treaty.

name: Gibeonites
description: A Canaanite people who had a peace treaty with the Ephraimites and whose city was attacked by King Saul, leading to Yahweh forsaking him. King David later turned over seven of Saul's descendants to the Gibeonites to be hanged.

name: Gibraltar isthmus
description: A land bridge that once connected Spain with Africa and protected the western basin of the Mediterranean Sea. The isthmus collapsed due to an earthquake, causing the Mediterranean to quickly rise to the level of the Atlantic Ocean and resulting in catastrophic flooding that caused the greatest loss of life by flood in world history.

name: Gideon
description: A biblical judge in whose days the Midianites invaded the Jordan Valley and poured into the region to overrun the land.

name: gifts
description: The affectionate dedication of human will to doing the Father's will is humanity's choicest gift to God and the only gift of true value to the Paradise Father. Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father of lights, and salvation itself is the free gift of God to all who have faith to receive sonship in the divine family.

name: Gilboa
description: A mountain where the Philistines defeated King Saul, who took his own life on its slopes. Located near Nazareth and visible from Jesus' childhood home, Mount Gilboa was a significant landmark where Jesus and his apostles established camps and held conferences, and where Mary and Joseph camped on their journey to Bethlehem for the census registration.

name: Gilead
description: One of the cities in Perea where the seventy messengers worked during Jesus' final ministry from January 30 until the end of his earthly life.

name: Gileadites
description: A biblical tribe that traditionally dwelt east of the Jordan River. King Saul rallied the northern clans to defend the Gileadites against an attack by the Ammonites, and his victory with an army of just over three thousand led the hill tribes to make him king.

name: giraffes
description: Large African animals that appeared about 10 million years ago with necks as long as they have today, during a period when the cat family dominated animal life.

name: girdle
description: A belt-like garment worn around the waist. Jesus wore a girdle which was taken by Roman soldiers during his crucifixion, and Judas used his girdle to hang himself after betraying Jesus.

name: Gischala
description: A town near the Sea of Galilee where Jesus and his apostles preached during their various ministry tours, including their first preaching tour around the lake and their first tour of Galilee, as well as on their return journey from Phoenicia.

name: glaciers
description: Massive ice formations that have significantly shaped Earth's topography throughout geological history. Glaciers first appeared regionally during the Permian period, especially in the southern hemisphere, and have continued to influence global climate and human migration patterns. These ice formations leave distinctive geological evidence including boulders, surface cleavages, potholes, lakes, and various types of moraines. The Urantia Book describes discrepancies between its glacier chronology and conventional geological dating, and notes that glacial activity has been a major factor in early human migrations, such as when the southward advance of Siberian glaciers forced early humans to migrate back toward their origins.

name: glands, ductless
description: Endocrine glands that secrete hormones directly into the bloodstream without ducts. These glands, particularly those comparable to the pituitary body, play a crucial role in spiritual receptivity and imagination across different human planetary types. Inhabited worlds contain mortals with one, two, or three such glands, with Urantians having two. The hormone output from about a dozen ductless glands can produce upward of 15 million potential chemical reactions in the human bloodstream at any given time.

name: Glantonia
description: A local system in the constellation of Norlatiadek that serves as one of Satania's immediate neighbors. Glantonia is one of the one hundred local systems that comprise the administrative organization of Norlatiadek, alongside other neighboring systems including Sandmatia, Assuntia, Porogia, Sortoria, and Rantulia.

name: glory
description: The manifestation of divine majesty, honor, and spiritual radiance associated with God and divine beings. Glory represents the visible expression of divine nature and purpose, as seen in Jesus's teachings about seeking God's glory rather than personal glorification. True glory comes from serving the Father's will and revealing divine truth, while self-glorification is contrasted with the authentic glory that flows from divine partnership and spiritual service.

name: gnats
description: Small flying insects used metaphorically by Jesus to criticize the Pharisees' tendency to focus on trivial religious details while ignoring weightier spiritual matters. The phrase "strain out the gnat and swallow the camel" illustrates the inconsistency of being scrupulous about minor ceremonial requirements while neglecting fundamental principles of faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: goals
description: Objectives or purposes that provide direction and meaning to existence across all levels of reality. Goals serve as essential motivators for progress, from material achievements to spiritual attainment, with the ultimate goal being the finding of God and achieving divine perfection. True goals should be spiritual rather than purely material, and the achievement of worthy goals brings satisfaction while serving as stepping stones to higher aspirations. The goal of human existence is described as attuning to the divinity of the indwelling Adjuster and achieving God-consciousness.

name: goats
description: Domesticated animals that were among the first livestock raised by early farmers, particularly in Mesopotamia, Turkestan, and China by 5000 B.C. Goats served as an early medium of exchange in primitive commerce and were introduced to various regions including the Nile delta by Nodite-Andonite peoples along with sheep, cattle, and other domesticated animals.

name: Gobi
description: A region that was an open grassland during the Andite period, supporting herders who were gradually transitioning to agriculture. Located east of the fertile Tarim valley, the Gobi was part of a thriving civilization that eventually perished when rain patterns shifted to the southeast.

name: God
description: The infinite, eternal, and perfect Universal Father who is the First Source and Center of all reality. God is described as pure spirit, absolute love, and the divine personality from whom all creation emanates. While God is not personally present in nature or physical forces, He indwells mortal minds through Thought Adjusters and can be known through spiritual communion. God is characterized by perfect unity of divine attributes including love, mercy, justice, and truth, and serves as both the origin and ultimate destiny of all personality beings in the universe of universes.

name: God of Action
description: A designation referring to the Infinite Spirit, the Third Person of Paradise Deity who serves as the executive agent and active administrator of divine plans. As the God of Action, the Infinite Spirit coordinates and executes the joint will of the Universal Father and Eternal Son throughout creation.

name: God the Absolute
description: The third level of experiential Deity, representing the experiential realization of absolute divinity through the unification of the second experiential Trinity, the Absolute Trinity. God the Absolute is the realization-attainment goal of all superabsonite beings and experiences identity with the Unqualified Absolute.

name: God the Father
description: The first person of the Paradise Trinity, also known as the Universal Father, who is the eternal source and center of all reality and the supreme object of worship for all created beings.

name: God the Sevenfold
description: A functional association of Deity comprising seven levels of approach to the Universal Father, established to compensate for creature limitations in approaching infinite Deity. The seven levels include Creator Sons and Creative Spirits, Ancients of Days, Seven Master Spirits, the Supreme Being, the Infinite Spirit, the Eternal Son, and the Universal Father.

name: God the Son
description: The second person of the Paradise Trinity, also known as the Eternal Son, who is the eternal word of God and the spiritual center of the Paradise Trinity.

name: God the Spirit
description: The third person of the Paradise Trinity, also known as the Infinite Spirit, who is the conjoint actor and the source of mind and spirit ministry throughout creation.

name: God the Supreme
description: The first experiential level of Deity manifestation, evolving through the grand universe as the finite synthesis of perfect Creator and perfecting creature experience, unifying all finite reality in the Supreme Being.

name: God the Ultimate
description: The second experiential level of Deity, functioning on transcendental levels beyond time and space. God the Ultimate eventuates from the potentials of divinity in the master universe and represents the transcendental unification of absonite realities through the Ultimate Trinity.

name: Godad
description: A Salemite hermit descended from a family that preserved the traditions of Melchizedek missionaries. Gautama could have transformed Buddhism by heeding Godad's instruction about the Salem gospel of salvation by faith.

name: Godheads
description: The three distinct and separate persons of the Paradise Trinity - the Universal Father, Eternal Son, and Infinite Spirit - who, while functionally one from the universe standpoint, maintain individual personalities for intimate spiritual relations with created beings.

name: Godlikeness
description: The progressive attainment of divine qualities and attributes through spiritual growth and moral development, representing the creature's evolutionary approach toward reflecting the character and nature of God.

name: gods and goddesses
description: Deities worshiped by various cultures throughout human history, evolving from glorified departed humans and ancestral spirits to more complex pantheons. These early gods were often tribal deities that reflected the limited understanding of their worshipers, including municipal gods of city-states and false gods created by human projection, distinct from true spiritual reality.

name: gold
description: The first metal sought by humans due to its ease of working and initial use as ornamentation. Gold was discovered alongside copper and tin by early civilizations, with gold-bearing quartz strata formed by ancient lava flows in mountain-building periods.

name: golden age
description: A mythical concept of a perfect past era that, while largely fictitious, has some basis in the historical realities of Dalamatia and Eden. The true golden ages of humanity lie in the future ages of light and life, rather than in any imagined perfect past.

name: golden calves
description: Symbolic representations of Yahweh used by Bedouin tribes, particularly golden and silver calves that symbolized their concept of the spirit of the Sinai volcano. These became problematic symbols when Moses tried to present higher concepts of Deity while accommodating existing tribal religious traditions.

name: golden rule
description: The fundamental ethical principle taught by Jesus to treat others as you would want to be treated, expressed in its highest form as loving others as Jesus loves us. This rule operates on multiple levels from human fairness to spiritual realization and requires spiritual insight to be properly understood and applied.

name: Golgotha
description: The official crucifixion site of Jerusalem located north of the city beyond the Damascus gate, where Jesus was crucified. The site was positioned near villas of the wealthy on one side and tombs of well-to-do Jews on the other.

name: Goliath
description: The giant warrior who fought in single combat with David, representing an ancient custom where disputes between tribes were settled by duels between chosen representatives rather than full tribal warfare.

name: Gomorrah
description: An ancient city destroyed along with Sodom due to natural causes, though later legends fabricated supernatural narratives about their destruction. The actual destruction occurred shortly before Melchizedek decided to end his emergency bestowal on Urantia.

name: Gonod
description: A wealthy Indian businessman and traveler who employed Jesus as an interpreter and tutor for his son during their journey to Rome. A citizen of India who had made three extensive trips to China, Gonod provided Jesus with valuable insights into Indian and Chinese civilization and culture.

name: good
description: A fundamental value and divine attribute representing righteousness, virtue, and moral excellence, often associated with truth and beauty as one of the supreme values that guide spiritual growth and ethical living.

name: good news
description: The message of Jesus' gospel, representing the joyful tidings of spiritual salvation and eternal life through faith in God's fatherhood and human sonship.

name: good Samaritan
description: A parable told by Jesus to clarify the meaning of 'neighbor' in response to a lawyer's question about inheriting eternal life, illustrating compassionate service regardless of social boundaries.

name: good shepherd
description: Jesus' self-designation emphasizing his role as the caring protector and guide of believers, featured prominently in his sermon to over three hundred people including Pharisees and apostles.

name: goodness
description: One of the three divine qualities that make divinity comprehensible to creatures, along with truth and beauty. Goodness represents the recognition of divine values and moral perfection, serving as both a quest for mortals and an eternal attribute of God that leads to repentance and spiritual growth.

name: gophers
description: Small gnawing rodents that appeared alongside beavers, squirrels, mice, and rabbits 35 million years ago, quickly becoming a notable form of life with little evolutionary change since.

name: Gophna
description: A village near Bethel where citizens came to hear Jesus preach at the end of June 27 A.D., along with people from Ephraim and other surrounding areas.

name: gorillas
description: Large primates that evolved from inferior mid-mammals, sharing a common extinct ancestor with primitive monkeys but not related to the superior mid-mammals from which humans descended.

name: gospel
description: The central teaching of Jesus proclaiming the fatherhood of God and brotherhood of man, offering salvation through faith and spiritual rebirth. The gospel emphasizes love, service, and the recognition that all humans are children of God, requiring personal experience rather than mere intellectual acceptance for transformation.

name: Gospel of John
description: The New Testament gospel written by John Zebedee, one of the twelve apostles and closest associates of Jesus during his earthly ministry.

name: Gospel of Luke
description: The New Testament gospel written by Luke, a physician and companion of the Apostle Paul, presenting Jesus' life and teachings with particular attention to his ministry to all people.

name: Gospel of Mark
description: One of the New Testament gospels, traditionally attributed to John Mark, which presents an account of Jesus' life and teachings.

name: Gospel of Matthew
description: One of the New Testament gospels, traditionally attributed to Matthew Levi, which presents an account of Jesus' life and teachings.

name: gossip
description: Spreading unverified information or hearsay about others, which Jesus cautioned against, advising his followers to speak only what they had heard from him directly.

name: government
description: The system of political authority and administration that regulates society, encompassing both earthly civil governance and celestial administrative structures. Jesus taught his followers to render to Caesar what is Caesar's while maintaining spiritual allegiance to God, emphasizing that believers should be good citizens without allowing earthly government to interfere with their spiritual obligations.

name: governor general
description: A high-ranking administrative official in governmental systems, typically overseeing territorial or regional governance.

name: governors of advanced tribal relations
description: Members of the Caligastia one hundred who were assigned to oversee and advance the social and political organization of early human tribal societies.

name: grace (God’s mercy)
description: God's unearned favor and mercy freely given to his creatures, enabling spiritual growth and survival. Grace is a divine gift that establishes sufficient credit for every soul who truly desires citizenship in God's kingdom, requiring no earning but only acceptance through faith.

name: grace (prayer before eating)
description: The practice of giving thanks before meals, which Jesus typically observed by offering verbal gratitude when breaking bread. The primitive form involved throwing food into fire while reciting formulas to appease spirits.

name: graciousness
description: The natural aroma of friendliness that emanates from a love-saturated soul, distinguished from mere gracefulness which can be cultivated through effort.

name: gradant
description: The standard unit of weight on Jerusem, built through the decimal system from the mature ultimaton and equivalent to almost exactly ten ounces of Urantia weight.

name: Graduate Guides
description: Graduate Guides are Higher Personalities of the Infinite Spirit who sponsor and conduct the high university of technical instruction and spiritual training in Havona. They pilot ascending mortals from time and space through the seven circuits of Havona worlds, with each guide remaining with the same pilgrim throughout their entire Havona career. These beings mysteriously appear as needed and are believed to be evolved Havona Servitals who have received the divine embrace on Paradise. Malvorian was the first Graduate Guide, assigned to Grandfanda, the first mortal to reach Havona.

name: graft
description: A metaphor describing how the nature of spiritual fruit in human life is determined by what beliefs and practices are grafted onto one's basic spiritual nature, even though spiritual sustenance comes from divine endowments of mind and spirit.

name: grain
description: Ripened wheat that the apostles gathered and rubbed in their hands while traveling on a Sabbath day, which provoked the indignation of Pharisee spies who accused them of Sabbath breaking. Grain was also thrown upon newlyweds in many Near Eastern cultures as a magical rite believed to ensure fertility.

name: Grand Canyon
description: A geological formation in the Colorado River region that contains primitive fossil-bearing rock layers from the dawn era of life, testifying to the upheavals and surface fluctuations of ancient times.

name: Grand Lama
description: The highest-ranking religious leader in the hierarchical structure of Tibetan Buddhism, which includes monks, nuns, and abbots.

name: grand universe
description: The seven evolving superuniverses in association with the central and divine universe of Havona, representing the present organized and inhabited creation. The grand universe contains approximately seven trillion inhabitable worlds plus architectural spheres and the billion spheres of Havona. It is part of the larger master universe, which also includes the uninhabited universes of outer space. The grand universe currently uses only about five percent of Paradise's gravity capability.

name: grandchildren
description: The children of one's children, representing the expanded love and planning capacity that distinguishes civilized humans from savage humans and animals. While savage humans and animals love only their immediate offspring, civilized humans extend their love and concern to their grandchildren and future generations.

name: Grandfanda
description: The first mortal pilgrim from time and space to reach Havona and attain Paradise, serving as the acting head of the Corps of the Finality and chief of the Supreme Council of Destiny. He originated from planet 341 of system 84 in constellation 62 of local universe 1,131 in superuniverse number one. His arrival at Havona inaugurated the broadcast service of the universe of universes and was guided by Malvorian, the first Graduate Guide.

name: granite
description: A rock formed from cooled lava layers of the earth's crust, with a density less than three times that of water. Granite began forming during the volcanic age approximately 1.5 billion years ago and constitutes the lighter material that formed the earth's early crust.

name: grapes
description: Fruit that appeared as decorative grape clusters on the lintel of the Capernaum synagogue alongside a pot of manna. Grapes are also referenced in Jeremiah's prophecy about sour grapes and the establishment of a new covenant where individuals are personally responsible for their own spiritual condition.

name: grass
description: Plant life comprising seed-bearing grasses that were among the most evolutionarily important developments in the plant world, second only to the appearance of man himself. Jesus used grass as a teaching metaphor about divine providence and trust in God's care.

name: gratification
description: The satisfaction or fulfillment obtained from achieving desires or meeting needs.

name: gravel
description: Small rock fragments found in early Carboniferous geological deposits, appearing alongside basin sediments and fossils of both land and marine life in strata ranging from 500 to 2,000 feet thick.

name: graves
description: Family burying grounds that represented the very first land titles granted by tribes to individuals, marking the earliest form of private land ownership in human society.

name: gravita
description: Dual-constitution material organizations in superuniverses that comprise 30 energy systems and represent the postgravity stage of Paradise gravity response. Also known as universe power, gravita includes all forms of energy that respond to Paradise gravity and are directly responsive to linear gravity, serving as working energy useful to superuniverses.

name: gravity
description: The fundamental force that holds all creation together through four absolute circuits: personality gravity of the Universal Father, spiritual gravity of the Eternal Son, mind gravity of the Conjoint Actor, and material gravity of the Isle of Paradise. These universal presence circuits pervade all realms and are independent of time and space.

name: gravity energy
description: The second phase of energy transmutation that carries the potential of universe power and becomes the active ancestor of all universe matter. This Paradise gravity-responding energy is produced through the work of Associate Transcendental Master Force Organizers and represents one of the two forms of ultimata.

name: Gravity Messengers
description: Highly personal, divine beings who are modified and personalized Thought Adjusters serving exclusively under the jurisdiction of Grandfanda and the finaliter corps. These superspirit personalities can transcend time and space, have no discoverable physical form, and serve as an invaluable messenger corps for the finaliters.

name: Great Beyond
description: Onagar's concept of the hereafter taught to remote settlements as part of his religious doctrine of one Deity, representing one of humanity's earliest formalized beliefs about life after death.

name: Great Britain
description: The largest island in the British Isles, located off the northwestern coast of continental Europe.

name: great commandment
description: Jesus stated that the greatest commandment is to love God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength, and the second is to love your neighbor as yourself, with all the law and prophets depending on these two commandments.

name: Great Lakes
description: The North American system of Great Lakes was produced during the retreat of a glacier, with these bodies of water emptying first into the Mississippi valley, then eastward into the Hudson valley, and finally by a northern route into the St. Lawrence. The connected Great Lakes system began to empty out over the present Niagara route thirty-seven thousand years ago.

name: Great Mother cult
description: A religious cult centered on the worship of a great mother figure or goddess.

name: Great Pyramid
description: A monumental pyramid structure, likely referring to the Great Pyramid of Giza in Egypt.

name: Great River
description: In Egyptian belief, the Great River was the boundary that souls crossed after death, where the ka (spirit genius) was expected to be waiting on the other side to guide the deceased.

name: Great Sources and Centers
description: The three primary divine beings of Paradise: the Universal Father, Eternal Son, and Infinite Spirit, representing the fundamental sources and centers of all reality.

name: Great Spirit
description: The supreme deity concept promoted by Onamonalonton, a leader who directed the red race from the worship of many gods to the veneration of "The Great Spirit."

name: great supper
description: A parable told by Jesus about who will be worthy to eat bread in the kingdom of God and sit at the table of the Father.

name: greater
description: A spiritual principle taught by Jesus: "Seek the greater thing, and the lesser will be found therein; ask for the heavenly, and the earthly shall be included."

name: Greater Road
description: The Mahayana division of Buddhism, also known as the "Great Road" to salvation, which developed among Chinese and north Indian followers of Gautama in contrast to the Hinayana or "Lesser Road" of the southern purists.

name: greatness
description: Greatness is synonymous with divinity and cannot be separated from goodness. True greatness lies not in possessing strength but in making wise and divine use of such strength, manifesting as self-control and unselfish service to others. The highest moral choice—choosing to do God's will—makes one great, regardless of their humble status.

name: Greece
description: Greece was an ancient Mediterranean civilization that became a major center of culture, philosophy, art, and learning. The Greeks developed advanced philosophical thought and were among the world's greatest traders and colonizers, spreading their cultural influence throughout the Mediterranean region and beyond.

name: Greeks
description: The Greeks were not only great teachers and artists but also the world's greatest traders and colonizers. They succeeded in planting numerous cultural outposts throughout the Mediterranean, and many advances of early Greek civilization persisted in later European peoples through their mixed descendants.

name: green race
description: The green race was one of the six original Sangik races and among the less able groups of primitive men. They were greatly weakened by extensive migrations and eventually split into three divisions—northern tribes were absorbed by yellow and blue races, eastern groups amalgamated with Indian peoples, and the southern nation entered Africa where they destroyed the orange race before being absorbed by the indigo race.

name: Greenland
description: Greenland is a large Arctic island that was connected to both North America and Europe during various geological periods. It served as a land bridge facilitating early human migrations and contains geological evidence of ancient life forms beneath its ice mantle. The purer Andonites lived in Greenland along with other extreme northern regions.

name: greensand marl
description: Greensand marl refers to geological deposits of chalk and green sandstone that characterize a specific geological period. These sedimentations varied in thickness from 200 feet to 10,000 feet and can be observed in locations such as the eastern foothills of the Rocky Mountains and various European regions.

name: Gregorian calendar
description: The Gregorian calendar is the present leap-year calendar system of 365¼ days per year that is used as the time reference throughout The Urantia Papers. Although the revelators possess exact chronological records, they chose to conform to this twentieth-century calendar system for presenting historical facts and dates.

name: groups
description: Groups represent associations of three or more individuals that constitute a social unit requiring leadership and coordination. Groups possess potential far greater than the simple sum of their individual members, with effective teamwork and wise leadership being essential for achieving common goals and maintaining social cooperation.

name: growth
description: Growth is indicated by progress rather than mere products, and represents the unconscious development of personality, character, and spiritual capacity. True growth cannot be forced or manufactured but must emerge naturally from within, requiring favorable conditions such as freedom from external pressure and devotion to supreme values.

name: grudges
description: Grudges refer to persistent feelings of resentment or ill will held against others. The ability to avoid holding grudges represents emotional maturity and spiritual growth, as demonstrated by apostle Thomas who was described as a good loser who did not hold grudges or nurse wounded feelings.

name: guardian angels
description: Personal angels assigned to guide and protect certain groups of human beings throughout their earthly lives and beyond. Guardian angels are seraphim who minister to mortals spiritually, serve as custodians of the surviving soul during the period between death and resurrection, and accompany their mortal associates on the Paradise ascension journey.

name: guardians of destiny
description: Personal seraphic guardians assigned to individual mortals who have achieved the third psychic circle, made the supreme decision to become Godlike, or been enrolled in the reserve corps of destiny. These angels serve as custodians of the mortal soul's survival values during the interval between death and morontia resurrection, and may accompany their human associates throughout the Paradise ascension journey.

name: guardians of health and life
description: A specialized group within the Caligastia one hundred, the superhuman staff that served on Urantia before the planetary rebellion.

name: guests
description: Individuals who are received and entertained by hosts, representing an important aspect of social relationships and hospitality customs.

name: guilt
description: The consciousness of violating moral standards or ideals, which may arise from interrupted spiritual communion or lowering of one's moral ideals. While guilt can serve as a positive stimulus toward spiritual growth, it should not be confused with sin and can be overcome through faith and divine forgiveness.

name: Guites
description: A northern people who, along with the Suites, inflicted severe military defeats on the Sumerians around 2500 B.C., contributing to the fall of Sumerian civilization in Mesopotamia.

name: gulf
description: A vast conceptual divide or separation, particularly referring to the great cosmic gulf between matter and thought, and the even greater gulf between material mind and spiritual love, as well as the vast separation between the human and divine natures.

name: Gulf of Mexico
description: A large body of water that formed when Mexico emerged from the sea 310 million years ago and has maintained its identity ever since. During ancient times, it served as one of three favorable refuges where marine animals survived periods of oceanic life destruction.

name: Gulf Stream
description: A major ocean current that in ancient times flowed over central North America before being deflected eastward to warm Greenland's shores, making that continent a tropical paradise. These great ocean currents have functioned throughout geological history, significantly affecting global climate patterns.

name: gulls
description: Seabirds that were among the modern bird species that had already evolved and existed 30 million years ago, during a period when the planet's bird life continued developing with few major evolutionary changes.

name: Guru Nanak
description: Guru Nanak was a 15th century religious leader who founded Sikhism by synthesizing teachings from Islam, Hinduism, and Buddhism into one of the most advanced religions of Asia.

name: gyroscope
description: A spinning device whose gravity-resistant phenomenon serves as a fair illustration of the effect of antigravity but provides no insight into the cause of antigravity.

name: habits
description: Religious habits that favor spiritual growth include cultivated sensitivity to divine values, recognition of religious living in others, reflective meditation on cosmic meanings, worshipful problem solving, sharing one's spiritual life, avoiding selfishness, refusing to presume on divine mercy, and living as in God's presence. When spiritual experiences are frequently repeated, they crystallize into strength-giving habits that eventually form spiritual character recognized as mature personality.

name: Hades
description: In Greek belief, Hades was conceived as a place for weak souls of weak men, who were thought to have shorter shadows. In Jesus's parable, Hades is depicted as a place of torment where the rich man found himself after death.

name: Hagar
description: Hagar was a concubine of Abraham, not a wife, similar to Keturah in her status within Abraham's household.

name: Haggai
description: Haggai was a spiritual leader and prophet who, unlike many others, was not silenced by the tyranny of the Jewish priesthood in Israel.

name: hail
description: Hail, along with clouds and rain, has been feared and worshiped by numerous primitive tribes and early nature cults as manifestations of divine power.

name: hair
description: Hair has served various purposes throughout human history: as a biological covering for early mammals, as a powerful fetish in primitive societies, and as a symbol in religious practices. In spiritual teachings, even the hairs of one's head are said to be numbered by divine knowledge.

name: half-breeds
description: The term refers to people of mixed racial heritage, whose chief troubles stem from social prejudices rather than actual biological disharmonies, as the dangers of racial crossbreeding have been greatly exaggerated.

name: Hallel
description: The Hallel is a Jewish prayer consisting of responsive reading of Psalms accompanied by waving of branches before the altar during worship services and holidays.

name: halo
description: A luminous radiance that traditionally encircles the heads of holy figures in art, originating from the actual light emanations of Adam and Eve's bodies. Their descendants portrayed spiritually advanced individuals with this glowing effect around their heads, establishing the halo as a religious symbol.

name: Hamathites
description: Descendants of Canaan who inhabited the Kingdom of Hamath in western Syria and northern Lebanon. Around 700 B.C., they were among the peoples relocated by Assyrian rulers to replace Jews carried away from the northern kingdom of Israel.

name: hammers
description: Tools that represented a significant advance in human civilization, originally created by attaching hard flint to wooden handles. The term also metaphorically describes the divine forces of necessity and anguish that forge human character and spiritual development.

name: Hammurabi
description: An ancient Mesopotamian ruler who established dominance after the fall of Sumerian civilization around 2500 B.C. Like other leaders of his era, he practiced capital punishment by burning for certain crimes, particularly those of a sexual nature.

name: Han Empire
description: The great Chinese empire that, along with Rome, was one of only two major world powers during Jesus' time. Chinese civilization under the Han represents the most continuous progression of any culture, spanning nearly forty thousand years with unbroken development through the twentieth century.

name: Hanavard
description: The 67th Primary Lanonandek Son in Nebadon who serves as a high counselor and acting chairman of Satania's executive council. He is the twelfth of his order to serve on Jerusem since the Lucifer rebellion.

name: handbreadth
description: A unit of measurement Jesus used when teaching about anxiety, asking his followers which of them could add even a handbreadth to their height or a day to their life through worry.

name: handicaps
description: Physical, mental, or environmental limitations that mortals face during their earthly existence. These deficiencies are fully compensated in the morontia career, where all deprivations are remedied and every being receives opportunity for complete spiritual development regardless of their earthly disadvantages.

name: hands
description: The human appendages that provided evolutionary advantages to early primates and became central to many religious customs. Jesus often used his hands for healing and challenged traditional ceremonial hand-washing practices, teaching that spiritual cleanliness comes from the heart rather than external rituals.

name: Hannah
description: The mother of Mary and grandmother of Jesus. Along with the rest of Mary's family, Hannah remained skeptical about Jesus' divine mission throughout his life, never fully accepting his spiritual calling.

name: Hap
description: Hap was the head of the college of revealed religion on Prince Caligastia's staff who remained loyal to Van during the Lucifer rebellion. He presented early races with a moral code called "The Father's Way," consisting of seven commands that formed the law of Dalamatia for nearly 300,000 years.

name: happiness
description: Happiness is a state of well-being that arises from the unification of physical, mental, and spiritual systems within the personality. True happiness is linked to spiritual progress, intelligent effort toward worthy goals, and the harmonious coordination of human will with divine spirit guidance.

name: happy hunting grounds
description: The "happy hunting grounds" was a term used by Andite invaders to describe a supposed afterlife destination when they ritually drowned inferior captives of the blue race who had been deemed unfit by tribal councils.

name: Haran
description: Haran was a place where Abraham's family tarried on their journey from Ur to Salem. Nahor, Abraham's brother, persuaded the family to stay there for an extended time, delaying their pilgrimage to meet Melchizedek.

name: harbors
description: Harbors were important seaports along ancient trade routes, particularly the few good harbors at the eastern end of the Mediterranean where caravan traffic from the Orient connected with maritime commerce to the western world.

name: harem
description: A harem was an institution designed to build up a strong and numerous body of blood relatives to support a ruler's throne, though the practice could create political complications when abandoned.

name: harm
description: Harm refers to injury, damage, or negative consequences that can affect physical, mental, or spiritual well-being.

name: harmony
description: Harmony is the keynote of the central universe, representing spiritual unity, proper order, and melodious association. It encompasses both musical expression and the coordination of diverse personalities working together in spiritual unity while maintaining individual originality.

name: harmony supervisors
description: Harmony Supervisors are tertiary supernaphim who coordinate activities in Havona to ensure everything moves along smoothly and expeditiously. They maintain system harmony and assist in preparing pilgrims of time for their Paradise achievements.

name: harmony workers
description: Harmony workers are celestial artisans who manipulate and organize specialized spiritual forces and energies to make them visible and comprehensible to mortals, working in a unique field of spirit achievement beyond mortal understanding.

name: harp
description: Jesus was skillful at playing harp and had an unusual liking for everything musical. He later sold his harp to defray Jude's school expenses.

name: harpoon
description: The Andonites were the discoverers of the throwing stick and harpoon, making effective use of these tools as fearless and successful hunters.

name: harps of God
description: The "harps of God" are morontia devices used for reception of space communications on worlds like Jerusem, compensating for the inability to directly adjust immature morontia sensory mechanisms.

name: harvest
description: Jesus taught that "the harvest is indeed plenteous, but the laborers are few" and told his disciples that "the fields are already white for the harvest," referring to the readiness of people to receive the gospel message.

name: haste
description: Jesus was never in a hurry and exhibited sublime composure, serving as an example of how religionists can live in effective emancipation from harrying haste and the painful stress of temporal life.

name: Hatita
description: Hatita was one of the towns visited during the Perean mission conducted by the seventy messengers from January 3, 30 AD until Jesus's death.

name: hatred
description: Jesus taught that "hate is the shadow of fear; revenge the mask of cowardice," and believers are called to overcome hatred with love, transforming enemies through acts of mercy and forbearance.

name: Havona
description: Havona is the central universe at the heart of all creation, consisting of one billion perfect worlds arranged in seven concentric circuits around Paradise. It serves as the eternal pattern universe and training ground for ascending mortals on their journey to Paradise.

name: Havona Centers
description: One million Havona Centers function in the central universe, each supervising a thousand Havona worlds and ensuring perfection of energy control that exists nowhere else in the cosmos.

name: Havona graph method
description: The Havona graph method is a technique used by Intelligence Co-ordinators to automatically assimilate vast amounts of information, enabling them to absorb in one hour what would require a thousand years using mortal communication methods.

name: Havona natives
description: The direct creation of the Paradise Trinity who inhabit the central universe and serve as eternal residents of the billion perfect spheres. These perfect beings gain experiential understanding through contact with ascending pilgrims from the evolutionary universes and may evolve in status by achieving fusion with Father fragments to qualify for membership in the Mortal Corps of the Finality.

name: Havona pilgrims
description: Ascending mortals who have successfully traversed the superuniverses and arrive on the seventh circuit of Havona with perfection of purpose. These pilgrims progress inward through the seven circuits of the central universe, accomplishing specific tasks on each world as they prepare for Paradise attainment and eventual induction into the Corps of the Finality.

name: Havona Servitals
description: Unique beings created jointly by the Seven Master Spirits and Seven Supreme Power Directors who serve in both the central universe and superuniverses. These versatile beings exist in both spiritual and semiphysical forms, assist ascending pilgrims, and may eventually transform into Graduate Guides through divine embrace experiences.

name: Havoners
description: The native inhabitants of the central universe, also known as Havona natives, who are the direct creation of the Paradise Trinity and live on the billion perfect spheres of Havona.

name: head-hunters
description: Primitive peoples who practiced capturing enemies' skulls, believing this would capture their souls. This practice was connected to ancient beliefs about skull worship, ghost servitude, and spiritual redemption, with some groups requiring possession of a head as a prerequisite for marriage.

name: headaches
description: A physical ailment treated by ancient medicine men through skull trephining, which involved opening the skull to supposedly allow headache spirits to escape as part of early surgical practices.

name: healing
description: The restoration of health through both material and spiritual means, including Jesus' ministry which combined medical treatment with prayer and faith encouragement, though true healing requires spiritual power and living faith rather than mere material manifestations.

name: healing at sundown
description: A extraordinary healing event in Capernaum where 683 people were made whole through divine power after sunset on the Sabbath, creating a spectacular display of creative healing that attracted many but provided lasting spiritual benefit to only a small number.

name: health
description: The integration of physical, mental, and spiritual systems that arises from the harmonious blending of truth, beauty, and goodness in human experience, leading to efficiency in living and true happiness through unified development.

name: hearing
description: One of the physical senses that continues to evolve and extend throughout the settled eras of planetary development, becoming enhanced along with vision as mortals progress toward the magnificent race achieved during the era of light and life.

name: hearsay
description: Information that is secondhand or unverified, which Jesus instructed his disciples to avoid when teaching, emphasizing they should speak only what they had personally heard from him.

name: hearth
description: The family fireplace that served as both a practical cooking area and a sacred altar in ancient religious practices, particularly among the Andites in India during Melchizedek's time, and later became a fetish object embodying divine presence.

name: hearts
description: The center of human moral and spiritual nature, which according to Jesus is the source of both good and evil thoughts and deeds, and the place where God's law is written under the new covenant prophesied by Jeremiah.

name: hearts of counsel
description: A group of reflective seconaphim under the supervision of Divine Counselors who possess and coordinate the wisdom and advice of the most competent minds throughout the entire superuniverse, censored and revised in light of Havona and Paradise counsel.

name: heat
description: The measure of electronic activity and energy that, along with antigravity, disrupts matter and dissipates energy in cosmic processes, reaching temperatures of 35 million degrees in stellar interiors and playing a fundamental role in atomic construction and disintegration.

name: heathen
description: Non-Jewish peoples who, according to the Psalmist quoted by Jesus, rage against God and his anointed, representing those with narrow outlook who oppose the kingdom founded on love and mercy.

name: heaven
description: The celestial realm conceived as the Great Beyond by early humans and later understood as the mansion worlds, system headquarters, and higher spiritual spheres where mortals progress after death, with the first heaven being the first mansion world and successive heavens extending through Jerusem, Edentia, Salvington, Uversa, Havona, and Paradise.

name: heavenly reproducers
description: Celestial artisans of the spirit-morontia worlds who reproduce experiences and realities of supreme value and eternal significance that are unknown on material worlds like Urantia.

name: historic pageanteers
description: A division of heavenly reproducers who dramatically reproduce and present the crucial events from universe records and history.

name: Hebrew language
description: The sacred language of the Jewish people, distinctly Andonite in origin, which Jesus mastered as a young man and used to read scriptures in the synagogue, though the Hebrews adopted their alphabet from the Philistines around 900 B.C. and had no written form during Moses' time.

name: Hebrews
description: An ancient Semitic people who became the foundational race of Judaism and later Christianity.

name: Hebrews, Book of
description: A New Testament book that recognized Melchizedek's divine mission and compared him to Jesus as a priest forever. The Book of Hebrews is noted as one of the few New Testament writings that portrays the unadulterated religious teachings of Jesus, embodying the true essence of Christianity rather than Paul's personal theological interpretations.

name: Hebron
description: An ancient city in Palestine that served as Abraham's military capital after his separation from Melchizedek, and later became King David's headquarters and the base of operations for John the Baptist's disciples under Abner. Jesus visited Hebron during his final tour through Palestine and his ministry in Judea.

name: Heidelberg race
description: Descendants of superior and prolific Andonites who spread across Europe from the northern ice to the Alps and Mediterranean in the south.

name: height
description: A physical measurement of stature. Adam and Eve were over eight feet tall, representing the superior physical form of Material Sons. Among evolutionary races, height generally decreases from the red man down to the indigo race, though unexpected strains of giantism appeared among the green and orange peoples.

name: Heldua
description: A coastal city in Phoenicia where Jesus' evangelists taught during his week-long ministry in the region, with many gentiles being added to the kingdom.

name: helium
description: An element produced in stellar nuclear reactions where hydrogen is converted into helium through complex chains involving carbon as an energy catalyst.

name: hell
description: A concept of future punishment that evolved from primitive taboo violations being seen as combined crime and sin. Various religions developed ideas of hell, from Egyptian judgment halls to Zoroastrian teachings that influenced Jewish traditions, though early humans originally had no concept of future punishment.

name: Hellenes
description: The ancient Greek people, also known as Greeks, who developed one of the most influential civilizations in human history.

name: Hellenistic Empire
description: The Greek empire and cultural sphere that emerged following Alexander the Great's conquests, spreading Greek culture and influence throughout the Mediterranean and Near East.

name: helplessness
description: A state of powerlessness and inability to act, which Jesus' teachings and example help believers overcome through faith and unity with the Father, transforming despair into courage and service.

name: hem
description: The border or edge of Jesus' garment that a woman with a hemorrhage touched in faith, believing she would be healed, though Jesus clarified that her faith rather than the physical touch brought about her cure.

name: henotheism
