description: A religious belief system that exalts one god above many others while still acknowledging their existence, representing an evolutionary stage between polytheism and monotheism that the Hebrews experienced in their spiritual development.

name: hens
description: Female chickens, part of the domesticated poultry kept for eggs and meat.

name: Henselon
description: A neighboring local universe to Nebadon in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton, notable for being the only universe that has lost more Lanonandek Sons to rebellion than Nebadon.

name: heralds of the resurrection
description: Messengers who proclaimed news of Jesus' resurrection, likely referring to members of David Zebedee's messenger corps who spread word of this pivotal event.

name: herders
description: Pastoral peoples who domesticated and raised animals for livelihood, representing one of humanity's early economic and social developments that provided capital wealth through livestock and freed time for cultural advancement, though creating social tensions with agricultural communities.

name: heredity
description: The transmission of characteristics from creators to their created beings, which can mysteriously manifest attributes in creatures that are not visible in their creators, as demonstrated in the reflectivity abilities of certain celestial beings.

name: hermit
description: A solitary religious person, specifically referring to Godad, a hermit who preserved Melchizedek traditions and whose counsel Gautama failed to heed, missing an opportunity to revive the Salem gospel.

name: Herod Agrippa
description: A ruler who executed the apostle James Zebedee with the sword, making James the first apostle to be martyred, and who particularly feared James above all the other apostles.

name: Herod Antipas
description: Herod Antipas was tetrarch of Galilee and Perea during Jesus' ministry, ruling from 4 B.C. to A.D. 39. He was responsible for the execution of John the Baptist and played a role in Jesus' trial, refusing to condemn him despite pressure from religious leaders. Known for his building projects including the reconstruction of Sepphoris and Tiberias, he was called "that fox" by Jesus due to his cunning and unjust treatment of Joseph's family regarding payment for construction work.

name: Herod Archelaus
description: Herod Archelaus was ethnarch of Judea, Samaria, and Idumea and brother of Herod Antipas. Joseph feared that Archelaus would follow his father Herod the Great's threatening policies more than Antipas would in Galilee. He was deposed by Augustus in A.D. 6 and had his palace in Jericho, which served as the basis for Jesus' parable of the pounds about his futile attempt to gain rule over Judea.

name: Herod the Great
description: Herod the Great was king of Judea, Galilee, Samaria, and Idumea, ruling until his death in 4 B.C. An Idumean by birth, he was father to Herod Antipas and Herod Archelaus. Despite professing loyalty to Hebrew ceremonial observances, he built temples for foreign gods, which made the Jews suspicious of his rule. He was a great builder who constructed the harbor at Caesarea and maintained the Maccabean palace in Jerusalem as his residence.

name: Herod the Idumean
description: Herod the Idumean refers to Herod the Great, emphasizing his ethnic origin from Idumea rather than Jewish heritage, which made his rule over the Jewish people particularly controversial and suspect.

name: Herodians
description: The Herodians were a purely political party that advocated for emancipation from direct Roman rule through the restoration of the Herodian dynasty. They united with the Pharisees and Sadducees in efforts to discredit Jesus, despite their usual political differences.

name: Herodias
description: Herodias was the unlawful wife of Herod Antipas who harbored intense and bitter hatred toward John the Baptist. She orchestrated John's execution by having her daughter dance for Herod at his birthday feast and request the Baptist's head as a reward, successfully manipulating the situation to achieve John's death.

name: Herod’s temple
description: Herod's temple refers to the Jerusalem temple as rebuilt and expanded by Herod the Great, representing one of his major construction projects that enhanced the religious center of Judaism.

name: heroes
description: Heroes are influential figures who profoundly shape human character and values. Parents should help children choose their heroes wisely, as people are deeply influenced by both earthly and divine heroes they choose to honor. Jesus represents the ideal hero who inspires courage, self-respect, and moral greatness.

name: herons
description: Herons are wading birds that existed 30 million years ago during a period when bird life continued developing on the planet with few major evolutionary changes, alongside other modern birds like gulls, flamingos, and eagles.

name: Hertzian waves
description: Hertzian waves are electromagnetic energies utilized on Urantia for broadcasting, representing the tenth group of wave-energy manifestations in the classification of energy forms.

name: Heshbon
description: A town in Perea that Jesus visited multiple times during his ministry. Jesus passed through Heshbon while bringing his brother Simon to Jerusalem in A.D. 17, and later during his tours of the Decapolis and Perea with the apostles and seventy messengers. It was at Heshbon where Jesus had his final meeting with Abner, the former chief of John the Baptist's apostles.

name: Hestia
description: Greek virgin goddess of hearth; became Vesta in Rome

name: Hezekiah
description: King of Judah in whose time the Hebrews ceased their traditional worship of serpents.

name: hidden things
description: Jesus taught that nothing is hidden that shall not be revealed, and all secrets will ultimately be made known. Our Father is not hiding from us but yearns for our association and works endlessly to reveal himself to humanity.

name: hides
description: One of the early writing materials used by ancient peoples, made from hammered animal skins to create a form of parchment. Along with tree barks, clay tablets, and stone slabs, hides were used to preserve and disseminate knowledge in the educational system established at Dalamatia.

name: Hierapolis
description: A city where Philip the apostle was crucified and buried. It was also the home of Philip's daughter Leah, who became a renowned prophetess and continued her parents' evangelistic work after their martyrdom.

name: hieroglyphics
description: An advanced form of picture writing that developed from earlier message sticks and knotted cords, representing a stage in the evolution toward symbolic alphabets.

name: High Commissioners
description: Spirit-fused ascendant mortals who serve the local universe administration as advocates for mortal races. They begin as race commissioners on planets, interpreting viewpoints and seeking mercy and justice for human races, then advance to higher levels of function. Over 1.5 billion High Commissioners serve in Nebadon, acting as friends of the court in tribunals and accompanying Paradise Sons on missions.

name: High Son Assistants
description: Twice-trinitized beings who are the personality embodiment of single supreme concepts or universal ideals. They serve as personal aids and private secretaries to high superuniverse Sons, functioning in the governments of the Ancients of Days. These touchingly affectionate and supremely wise beings provide invaluable assistance to superuniverse administrations, though they are humble seekers of knowledge outside their singular area of expertise.

name: high spirit personalities of circuit control (unrevealed)
description: Unrevealed spiritual beings who assist circuit supervisors in directing the proper circuits for transmission of spirit messages and transit of personalities throughout the universe. They work alongside staffs composed of personalities of the Infinite Spirit to maintain universal communication circuits.

name: Higher Personalities of the Infinite Spirit
description: Higher Personalities of the Infinite Spirit include seven orders: Solitary Messengers, Universe Circuit Supervisors, Census Directors, Personal Aids of the Infinite Spirit, Associate Inspectors, Assigned Sentinels, and Graduate Guides. Master Spirit Number Three directs many of these high personalities, presiding over their assemblies and speaking for the Infinite Spirit in council.

name: Hildana
description: Hildana is the name of a woman of evil repute who was brought before Jesus by her accusers during his visit to Jerusalem, in an episode that became distorted in later records.

name: hills
description: Hills are elevated landforms that are lower and less steep than mountains.

name: Himalayas
description: The Himalayas are a mighty mountain range in Asia that began forming 290 million years ago, representing one of the greatest crustal upheavals in Earth's geological history. This mountain system contains extensive fossil beds and limestone deposits that have been elevated to heights of 16,000 feet in the Himalayas and 20,000 feet in Tibet.

name: Hindu Kush
description: The Hindu Kush is a mountain range that marked one of the boundaries of the region where superior Andite tribes engaged in trade and commerce as their primary occupation.

name: Hinduism
description: Hinduism is described as the oldest and most cosmopolitan of the world's religions, having been influenced by various teachings including those of Melchizedek missionaries, Buddhism, Jainism, Islam, and Christianity. It is characterized as the most adaptive and tolerant religion, capable of unlimited change while maintaining its essential nature as an integral part of India's social fabric.

name: Hindus
description: Hindus are the followers of Hinduism, known for their devotion to physical disciplines, reverence for certain animals like cattle, and their belief in concepts such as the atman that approximate an understanding of divine presence.

name: Hinnom, valley of
description: The valley of Hinnom is a desolate valley outside Jerusalem where Judas Iscariot went in despair and took his own life by hanging himself from a tree on the steep rocks.

name: hippopotamuses
description: Hippopotamuses were large mammals that lived in Europe during prehistoric times and were hunted by Neanderthalers 800,000 years ago, but were later virtually exterminated by lions that arrived from Africa.

name: Hippos
description: Hippos was a city in the Decapolis region where Jesus and his apostles conducted preaching tours on multiple occasions, including during their preparation period and second preaching tour in Galilee. It was at Hippos where Jesus taught a lesson on forgiveness in response to a disciple's question.

name: Hiram
description: Hiram was the king of Tyre during the times of David and Solomon, whose tomb was located near where Jesus and his apostles taught and preached from July 11 to July 24, A.D. 29.

name: hirelings
description: Hired ruffians and rough men used by Jesus' enemies to cause trouble and harass him, particularly during his rejection at Nazareth. Jesus contrasted hirelings, who flee when danger comes, with true shepherds who protect their flock even unto death.

name: history
description: The record of events and experiences across time, encompassing both sacred and secular accounts. History includes the chronicles of universe development, planetary evolution, and mortal civilizations, though revelators note that finite origins alone cannot reveal eternal destinies.

name: Hittites
description: An ancient people descended from Andonite stock, characterized by pale skins and broad heads, who occupied central and southeastern Europe and Asia Minor. They were better-organized and highly civilized than many contemporary groups, often defeating Arabian Semites in northern Palestine, and some became teachers of the Salem cult.

name: Holdant
description: A Tertiary Lanonandek, number 19 of that corps, who serves as the custodian of the Satania system and holder and controller of all interned spirits above the order of mortal existence.

name: holidays
description: Days that would become mere secular celebrations without the safeguarding influence of prayer, which helps preserve the essential social, moral, and spiritual values that distinguish holy days from ordinary holidays.

name: Holocene
description: The postglacial period that began approximately 35,000 years ago, marking the end of the great ice age and roughly corresponding to the arrival of Adam and Eve and the beginning of the Adamic dispensation.

name: Holy Area
description: The outlying residential region of upper Paradise, divided into seven concentric zones that serve as the eternal dwelling places for Paradise Citizens, Havona natives, and ascending beings from the seven superuniverses. Each zone contains vast residential complexes organized into units, divisions, congregations, and assemblies to accommodate billions of glorified beings.

name: Holy City
description: A sacred city, likely referring to Jerusalem in its religious significance as a center of worship and pilgrimage.

name: Holy Mother
description: A maternal religious figure venerated by Tibetan Buddhists alongside angels, saints, and gods as part of their complex ritual practices and hierarchical religious system.

name: holy of holies
description: The most sacred inner chamber of the Jerusalem temple, accessible only to high priests and lifelong Nazarites like John the Baptist. Young Jesus spent considerable time at the temple wondering what lay behind the veil of separation that concealed this holy sanctuary.

name: Holy Spirit
description: The spirit presence of the Divine Minister, the Mother Spirit of a local universe, which serves as a spiritual circuit for divine ministry. The Holy Spirit prepares mortal minds for higher spiritual receptivity and works in coordination with the Spirit of Truth to guide and enlighten human souls toward spiritual growth and divine understanding.

name: holy water
description: Water believed to possess protective powers against ghosts and evil influences, used in various religious ceremonies. Holy water represents a survival of ancient water fetish beliefs and was commonly used for purification rituals, baptism, and spiritual protection in both ancient and modern religious practices.

name: home
description: The fundamental social institution founded on marriage and family relationships, serving as the basic unit of civilization and the primary environment for child-rearing and moral development. The home represents humanity's greatest evolutionary achievement in social organization, providing stability, love, and the foundation for spiritual and ethical growth.

name: home designers and builders
description: Divine builders among the celestial artisans who construct and remodel morontia and spirit dwellings for individuals and working groups. These skilled beings create real and beautiful spiritual abodes that accommodate the needs of their inhabitants, allowing for abundant variety and individual expression in their construction.

name: homeless
description: Those without shelter or home, representing one of humanity's fundamental needs. True spiritual fasting involves caring for the homeless by bringing them into one's house, along with feeding the hungry and clothing the naked, as expressions of genuine religious devotion.

name: homesick Phoenician
description: A young Phoenician man whom Jesus encountered who was discouraged, envious, and longing for home. Jesus offered him comfort and quoted the Hebrew proverb about how a person's gifts make room for them and bring them before great men.

name: Honan
description: A region in ancient China where progressive settlements developed through the infiltration of Andite peoples from the west about fifteen thousand years ago. Honan became a center of advanced Chinese civilization, with agricultural developments that required cooperative solutions to hydraulic problems, promoting peace among farming groups.

name: Honen Shonin
description: A Japanese Buddhist leader who, along with Ryonin and Shinran, helped develop the concept of God within Buddhism through teachings about Amida Buddha. Honen taught that souls could attain salvation through sincere faith in the divine mercies and loving care of Amida.

name: honesty
description: A fundamental virtue involving truthfulness, integrity, and sincerity in all dealings and relationships. True honesty requires spiritual discernment and discretion, as it must be balanced with wisdom and consideration for others' intellectual and spiritual capacity to receive truth.

name: honor
description: Honor is a human emotion tied to pride and ambition that requires social recognition to have meaning. It contributed to the early development of art, ceremonies, and competitive activities, as these emotions need an audience for expression and gratification.

name: hopes
description: Hope represents the grandeur of trust and serves as a divine quality that sustains mortals through life's uncertainties and disappointments. It is one of the fruits of the spirit, providing eternal perspective that transcends fear through God's love, and enables spirit-born individuals to remain calm even when their fondest ambitions fail.

name: horde
description: In the most primitive society, the horde was the fundamental social unit where everything was held in common, including children as collective property. The horde was eventually displaced by evolving families in child-rearing and by emerging clans and tribes as the primary social organization.

name: Horeb
description: Horeb is an alternative name for Mount Sinai, the sacred mountain where Moses received divine revelations and the law from God.

name: hormones
description: Hormones are chemical substances that regulate countless vital processes in living organisms through complex reactions in the bloodstream. The organization of certain hormone-producing glands, comparable to the pituitary bodies, influences spiritual receptivity and inherent imagination in different mortal races.

name: hornets
description: Hornets are used metaphorically to illustrate that anger is like throwing a stone into a hornet's nest, creating unnecessary disturbance and danger, while impatience is described as a spirit poison.

name: horsepower
description: Horsepower is a unit of energy measurement used to illustrate the incredible atomic energy potential within matter. A single drop of water contains enough energy equivalent to more than one hundred horsepower operating continuously for two years.

name: horses
description: Horses evolved over millions of years, reaching their modern form about 5 million years ago in North America before migrating worldwide and becoming extinct on their continent of origin. They were among the most important domesticated animals, serving humanity for food, transportation, agriculture, and warfare, and played a crucial role in the spread of civilization and military conquest.

name: Horus
description: Horus was the Egyptian sky god, son of Osiris and Isis, whose legendary battle with Set resulted in the loss of his eye. According to myth, the wise god Thoth restored Horus's eye by spitting on the wound and healing it, reflecting ancient beliefs in the healing power of saliva.

name: Hosea
description: Hosea was a Hebrew prophet who followed Amos and preached a gospel of divine love, forgiveness, and mercy. He emphasized repentance over sacrifice and proclaimed that God would show loving-kindness to all people, contributing to the secret writings that became the foundation of the Old Testament.

name: hospitality
description: A tradition of welcoming and caring for guests, including the ancient custom of guest friendship where departing visitors received a broken dish piece for future introductions and paid their way with travel stories. Jesus taught that hospitality should be ministered as if entertaining the children of the Most High, elevating daily service into a fine art of serving God.

name: hostility
description: A state of opposition, antagonism, or aggressive unfriendliness toward others.

name: hosts to celestial visitors
description: Gracious Morontia Companions who are dedicated to entertaining superhuman groups of student visitors and other celestial beings who sojourn on the transition worlds.

name: house of Fad
description: The library of Dalamatia that contained more than two million separate records and was presided over by Fad, who organized the educational faculty for disseminating and conserving knowledge in the early ages.

name: houses
description: Physical dwellings that Jesus frequently used in his teachings and parables, and which served as centers for apostolic ministry, including house-to-house visitation to comfort the afflicted and teach the gospel.

name: housetops
description: Flat rooftops of houses that Jesus referenced metaphorically when instructing his disciples to boldly proclaim the gospel of the kingdom, emphasizing that hidden truths would be revealed openly.

name: huddling proclivity
description: An unexplained tendency of ultimatons to cluster or group together, which contributes to the unpredictability of electronic behavior in atoms and affects the differential revolutionary velocities of these fundamental particles.

name: Hudson Bay
description: A large bay in northeastern Canada surrounded by extensive granite elevations containing ancient preoceanic rocks that have undergone intense metamorphic experiences. It was also the site where an Indian tribe and Eskimo group met and intermarried about five thousand years ago.

name: Hudson River
description: A river in eastern North America that once served as an outlet for the Great Lakes during glacial retreat, and along whose west bank the Catskill Mountains stand as geological monuments from the Devonian period.

name: human nature
description: The inherent characteristics, tendencies, and qualities that define mortal beings and their behavioral patterns.

name: human rights
description: The socially constructed rules governing human relationships and competition that are entirely social rather than natural in origin. Every human right is associated with a social duty to ensure group protection, and granting rights to individuals inevitably curtails the liberties of others. Human rights are relative and ever-changing, representing society's attempt to fairly administer varying individual rights while providing opportunities for self-maintenance, self-perpetuation, and self-gratification.

name: human sacrifice
description: The practice of sacrificing humans to appease invisible forces and spirits, which developed from primitive beliefs that spirits derived satisfaction from human suffering and humiliation. This terrible practice has been perpetuated by backward peoples throughout history and continued into the twentieth century.

name: humanism
description: A philosophical approach that emphasizes human values and concerns, often associated with secular thinking that focuses on human potential and achievement rather than divine or supernatural explanations.

name: humans
description: The mortal inhabitants of evolutionary worlds who possess the capacity for spiritual growth and eternal survival through their relationship with indwelling divine spirits.

name: humiliation
description: A state of degradation or shame that primitive peoples believed brought satisfaction to spirits and led to the development of sacrificial systems. While early humility was often an attempt to deceive envious spirits, true spiritual humility involves recognizing one's dependence on divine guidance and maintaining proper perspective before God.

name: humility
description: A spiritual attitude of recognizing one's dependence on divine guidance and maintaining proper perspective before God. True humility involves appreciating the source of salvation while avoiding ostentatious displays of false modesty. It represents a normal consciousness of mortal limitations combined with moral determination to achieve higher spiritual goals, and is essential for religious growth and spiritual progress.

name: humor
description: A universal capacity for joy and laughter that serves as an automatic safety valve against excessive self-contemplation and helps beings take themselves less seriously. Humor functions across all levels of existence, from mortal laughter to celestial mirth, and is particularly valuable for those undergoing sustained stress in spiritual development. It encompasses reminiscent jests, current situational comedy, and prophetic joy, serving as the divine antidote for ego exaltation.

name: hunchbacks
description: Physically deformed individuals who were regarded as fetishes by primitive peoples, along with other crippled children and those with mental afflictions, reflecting early humanity's inability to distinguish between various forms of abnormality and their tendency to attribute supernatural significance to physical differences.

name: hunger
description: The fundamental driving force behind early human development and social organization, ranging from basic physical need for food to spiritual longing for divine truth. Primitive hunger led to food-gathering activities and was the primary motivator for human thought and association, while spiritual hunger represents the soul's quest for divine satisfaction and eternal life.

name: hunters
description: Early human groups who developed weapon tools and hunting skills, representing a significant advancement from food-gathering societies. Hunters gained considerable freedom from food slavery and developed militant, warlike characteristics, in contrast to the more peaceful agricultural peoples. The hunting stage marked a crucial step in human civilization, though it was eventually superseded by herding and farming as climatic conditions changed.

name: hybridization
description: The biological process by which superior and dissimilar stocks create new and more vigorous strains, enhancing creativity and fertility across plants, animals, and humans. Hybridization of diverse peoples with superior inheritance factors can produce stronger races, though attempting this on Urantia under current racial conditions would be disastrous.

name: Hydra
description: An early multi-celled animal form representing one of the community organizational patterns adopted by primitive single-celled organisms during their evolutionary development.

name: hydrogen
description: The smallest and most fundamental atom, serving as the primary building block of organized matter in vast space clouds and the basic fuel for stellar energy production. In solar reactions, hydrogen is converted into helium through complex nuclear chains, with carbon acting as an energy catalyst.

name: hyenas
description: Carnivorous mammals that migrated from Africa to Europe, helping to eliminate saber-toothed tigers and hippopotamuses during the post-glacial mammalian recovery. Some African tribes believed they were descended from hyenas and adopted them as totems.

name: hygiene
description: The practice of maintaining cleanliness and health through proper sanitation and personal care habits.

name: hypnosis
description: A mental state induced through various techniques including prolonged staring, chanting, and suggestion, often used by ancient shamans and medicine men for healing rituals. Many early practitioners achieved autohypnosis by staring at their navels, creating mysterious effects that impressed tribesmen and contributed to beliefs about supernatural powers.

name: hypocrisy
description: The practice of professing beliefs, feelings, or virtues that one does not actually possess, particularly condemned by Jesus as a characteristic fault of religious leaders who say what is good but fail to practice it themselves.

name: hypotheses
description: Proposed explanations or theories put forward for consideration and testing, serving as starting points for investigation and understanding.

name: hysteria
description: A psychological condition characterized by extreme emotional outbursts and physical symptoms, often confused with demon possession in ancient times and frequently associated with shamanism. Many early medicine women were hysterics who were believed to be possessed by spirits, contributing to ancient beliefs about supernatural inspiration.

name: I AM
description: The philosophical concept representing the infinite, eternal, and absolute nature of the First Source and Center before all differentiation into the various aspects of Deity and reality. The I AM is the theoretical starting point for understanding the origin of all existence, representing unqualified infinity and the primal source of all reality.

name: ice
description: Water in its solid state, forming naturally during cold weather periods. Ancient Andonites viewed ice as a curse to be delivered from in their prayers, while Jesus contemplated the mystery of water's three forms when observing ice during an unusually cold winter in Nazareth.

name: ice ages
description: Six major glacial periods that occurred on Earth, each involving massive ice sheets covering northern regions including North America, Greenland, Iceland, the British Isles, and western Europe. These glacial invasions, occurring in roughly 200,000-year cycles, played crucial roles in human evolution and migration patterns, with primitive humans first appearing during the third ice age and the colored races evolving during the fifth glacial period.

name: Iceland
description: A northern island nation that was completely buried beneath ice flows during the glacial periods and served as a destination for early Andonite marine adventurers fleeing ice invasions. Iceland was connected to Europe via Greenland during certain geological periods and later became home to some of the purer Andonite populations, while also receiving Salem missionaries who brought religious teachings to its inhabitants.

name: ichthyosaurs
description: Ancient marine reptiles that lived during prehistoric periods, characterized by their fish-like appearance and adaptations for life in oceanic environments.

name: idealism
description: A philosophical and spiritual approach that emphasizes divine concepts and higher spiritual values over material concerns. True idealism must balance the pursuit of noble goals with practical defense against those who would exploit or destroy progressive civilizations, and represents the spiritual energy that uplifts and advances human culture.

name: ideals
description: Elevated concepts and aspirations that originate in the creative realms of the inner world and serve as the driving power for advancing human culture. Ideals require perpetuating symbolism to survive and tend to grow geometrically while human ability to achieve them progresses only arithmetically, creating ongoing challenges for moral and spiritual development.

name: ideas
description: Theoretical plans for action that represent sensations combined with reflective interpretations of the personal self. The power of ideas lies not in their reality or reasonableness but in their vividness and universal applicability, and they serve as the foundation for building personal philosophies and approaches to life.

name: identity
description: The continuity of selfhood that can be associated with both living and nonliving energy patterns, though personality is only associated with living systems. Human identity is initially a transient material manifestation but can be transferred from the temporary material-intellect system to the eternal morontia-soul system, with the Thought Adjuster serving as the custodian of ascending identity.

name: ideograph recorders
description: Celestial artisans who specialize in preserving thought through ideographic techniques and concept picturization, capable of improving one thousandfold upon the work of concept recorders in their methods of thought preservation.

name: ideographs
description: A system of written symbols representing ideas or concepts rather than specific words or sounds, used by celestial beings for thought preservation and by ancient Chinese civilization, though the cumbersome nature of ideographic writing systems limited the spread of knowledge and learning.

name: idleness
description: A state of inactivity that is destructive to self-respect and societal progress. The Urantia Book teaches that idleness breeds weakness and hinders spiritual growth, while productive activity strengthens character and prevents the soul from falling into despair or mystic tendencies.

name: idolatry
description: The worship of material objects or images rather than true spiritual reality. Originally developing from the desire to preserve memories of the dead through consecrated images, idolatry represents humanity's tendency to substitute material symbols for genuine worship of the Universal Father.

name: Idumea
description: A region south of Judea ruled by Herod the Idumean during Jesus' time. Along with Judea and Samaria, Idumea was under the administrative control of the Roman procurator Pontius Pilate during Jesus' trial in A.D. 30.

name: ignorance
description: A lack of knowledge or understanding that serves as one of the chief inhibitors of spiritual growth and social progress. While ignorance can sometimes provide opportunities for faith and prevent premature knowledge that might hinder development, it generally breeds suspicion and prevents the intellectual cross-fertilization necessary for brotherhood.

name: Ikhnaton
description: The Egyptian pharaoh Akhenaten, one of history's most remarkable figures and among the four great prophets of Egypt. He possessed an amazingly clear concept of monotheism and attempted to establish worship of one God under the guise of the sun-god Aten, though his reforms were too rapid and failed to provide lasting stability for his people.

name: illegitimacy
description: The social condition of children born outside of marriage, which became a source of disrepute early in civilization's development. Ancient societies developed various customs and institutions, including men's secret societies, partly to prevent illegitimate births through education and control of adolescent behavior.

name: Illinois
description: A North American state notable for its geological history, containing ten layers of coal beds that indicate the number of times the land rose and fell during ancient epochs. Illinois was reached by glacial ice sheets during several ice ages, with the fourth ice sheet extending as far south as southern Illinois.

name: illusions
description: False beliefs or deceptive appearances that can either hinder or help human development. While art uses illusions to foreshadow higher universe realities, personal illusions about success or optimistic self-deception create worlds of unreality that inevitably lead to disillusionment and prevent genuine wisdom.

name: Image Aids
description: Celestial beings who assist in various administrative and communicative functions throughout the universe, working in conjunction with other orders of spirit personalities to facilitate the operations of the cosmic government.

name: image of God
description: The divine likeness in which mortal humans are created, referring not to physical resemblance but to the spiritual endowment of personality and the indwelling presence of the Thought Adjuster. This divine image represents humanity's potential for spiritual growth and eternal survival through evolutionary attainment in partnership with the divine fragment within.

name: images
description: Visual representations or likenesses that have played significant roles throughout human history, from primitive monuments preserving memories of the dead to artistic depictions. The Urantia Book discusses how celestial artisans use concept pictures to preserve vast amounts of information, surpassing ordinary written language. Early human images were believed to hold spiritual power through consecration ceremonies. Moses prohibited the making of images in the second commandment to control fetish worship among the Hebrews. Jesus left no images behind to avoid contributing to idolatrous practices, and artistic depictions of him have been described as unfortunately portraying him as overly mild rather than reflecting his dignified manhood and dynamic teaching.

name: imagination
description: A creative mental faculty that enables spiritual transformation and enhances human relationships. When imagination flows freely in controlled channels, self-acting Thought Adjusters can contribute spiritual factors to the human mind, effecting deep transformations in the superconsciousness. Friendship mutually stimulates the imagination, enhancing joy, triumph, and spiritual power in human associations. The imagination of modern European races is noted as a trait derived from the Andites, despite their biological foundation being the Cro-Magnoid blue man.

name: Imhotep
description: An Andite architectural genius who served as prime minister in ancient Egypt and built the first stone pyramid, marking a significant advancement from previous brick construction. He was responsible for creating the first and most exquisite of the stone pyramids in Egypt, though the art of building steadily declined after his era.

name: imitation
description: A fundamental human tendency that influences the evolution of religious philosophy and spiritual development. The evolution of a philosophy of religion is shaped by humanity's tendency to imitate associates, requiring courage and discriminating thinking for sound philosophic conclusions. Imitating God is described as the key to perfection, while doing his will is the secret of survival and perfection in survival.

name: immanence
description: The concept of God's indwelling presence within every individual, which transcends human comprehension and creates a paradox when reconciled with divine transcendence. This essential doctrine of human realization of God requires faith to harmonize the concept of divine immanence with the idea of God's transcendent domination of the universe of universes.

name: Immanence of the Projected Incomplete
description: A manifestation of the Supreme Being's living presence that underlies the organic unity of cosmic events through seemingly fortuitous coordination of unrelated universe happenings. The Supreme anticipates future evolution as a reflection of this concept, where finite and superfinite blend and blur on supertime levels, creating a forecast of universe presence that reflects back to created levels.

name: Immanuel
description: The Union of Days numbered 611,121 assigned to Nebadon, serving as the Paradise Trinity's ambassador and the Universal Father's personal representative to Michael's court. His name means "God with us," and he functions as Michael's elder brother and adviser, providing counsel only upon request. Immanuel supervised universe affairs during each of Michael's seven bestowals and gave the pre-bestowal instructions that guided Jesus during his incarnation on Urantia. He bears the distinction of being the only personality in Nebadon who has never acknowledged subordination to Michael, maintaining his role as a liaison between Paradise and the local universe.

name: immaturity
description: A developmental stage characterized by emotional, intellectual, and spiritual limitations that hinder effective relationships and personal growth. The immature individual arouses antagonisms while the mature person earns cooperation, thereby multiplying life's contributions. Immaturity manifests in discouragement, worry, indolence, inability to forgive others, and refusal to grow up or accept responsibility. It divorces the present from its true relationship to past and future, whereas maturity reveals their coordinate connection. The pursuit of selfish desires and unwillingness to embrace spiritual growth perpetuate individual and societal immaturity.

name: immortality
description: The concept of eternal existence beyond mortal death, closely related to survival and eternal life in the cosmic scheme of spiritual progression.

name: impatience
description: A spiritual weakness described as "spirit poison" that leads to hasty decisions and disregard for proper timing and ethical obligations. Lucifer's impatience caused him to lose his precious endowments by craving immediate possession of what he desired, defying the rights and liberties of others. Adam and Eve's impatience led to their downfall, as they were unwilling to endure the long task of planetary rehabilitation. The antidote to impatience is developing patience and waiting for the majestic unfolding of progressive adventure and discovery.

name: imperfection
description: A necessary characteristic of evolutionary creation that serves as the starting point for spiritual growth and development. While God himself is eternally perfect, he shares in the experience of imperfection through all struggling evolutionary creatures, and imperfection is inherent in evolutionary growth, leading to the potential for both evil and the eventual appearance of perfected beings.

name: impersonal
description: A concept describing that which lacks personality, including the nonpersonal, subpersonal, and prepersonal levels of reality. Throughout the cosmos, the impersonal is responsive to the will and acts of existent personality, which operates on a higher level of universe reality.

name: import duties
description: A form of taxation on imported goods.

name: Imports of Time
description: A type of tertiary seconaphim angel who serves as time evaluators and the secret of prophecy. They reveal the time required for completing any undertaking and serve as dependable indicators for forecasting future events, providing invaluable counsel in assignments and ensuring that beings have adequate time for making important decisions.

name: impossibilities
description: A concept that is transcended by divine power, as expressed in the teaching that "with God all things are possible." This principle reflects the unlimited potential available through divine partnership and the perfect control of the universe creators and administrators who unfold the eternal purpose of the Universal Father.

name: improvidence
description: The lack of foresight in providing for future needs, particularly regarding family responsibilities. Jesus did not countenance improvidence, idleness, or dependence upon alms, but taught that material and temporal concerns should be subordinated to spiritual progress in the kingdom of heaven.

name: inaction
description: The state of not taking action or remaining passive when action is needed.

name: incantations
description: Magical word combinations and ritual chants used in primitive religious and magical practices. These highly magical verbal formulas were employed by enchanters, wizards, magicians, and sorcerers, and some early incantations eventually evolved into prayers as prayer gradually displaced magic in religious rituals.

name: incarnation
description: The process by which divine beings take on material form to serve and guide mortal creatures. While the Infinite Spirit does not incarnate in mortal flesh like certain Sons of God, the Spirit and coordinate Spirits undergo divinity attenuations to appear as angels and guide beings through earthly existence, revealing the combined love of the Father and Son.

name: Incas
description: Ancient South American rulers whose ancestry was established when 132 Andites crossed the Pacific from Japan and intermarried with the natives of the Andes. These Andite sailors navigated the Pacific in stages, stopping at Polynesian islands before reaching South America and founding the lineage of the later Inca rulers.

name: incense
description: Aromatic substances burned during religious ceremonies and sacrificial rituals, believed by ancient peoples to carry messages to the gods and keep sacred fire in good humor.

name: incest
description: A social taboo that originated from tribal marriage restrictions limiting unions to certain age groups, with some cultures allowing intergenerational marriages to prevent both parents from being considered foolish.

name: income
description: Financial resources or earnings received by individuals or groups.

name: incompleteness
description: A characteristic of the Supreme Being that allows for evolutionary growth in the present universes, where emptiness provides potential for experiential development.

name: inconstancy
description: Human tendency toward forgetfulness and changeability that believers are encouraged not to fear on their spiritual journey toward eternal life.

name: indecision
description: A state of uncertainty that must be overcome through wholehearted commitment to the divine will, eliminating the paralysis of indeterminate choice.

name: India
description: A large peninsula in southern Asia that served as the birthplace of humanity and became home to the most cosmopolitan population on earth, blending all the Urantia races and serving as a cultural melting pot throughout history.

name: Indian Ocean
description: A major body of water that has maintained essentially its current form for approximately 100 million years, once connected to the Mediterranean Sea through ancient waterways.

name: Indians (American)
description: Native peoples of the Americas, primarily descended from the red race.

name: indigo race
description: The sixth and least progressive of the Sangik colored races, who migrated to Africa where they have remained, becoming the dominant population of that continent and serving historically as laborers and slaves for more advanced races.

name: individual
description: A person as a distinct entity valued for their unique worth and potential. According to Jesus and the teachings of The Urantia Book, the individual human being holds sacred significance, with the progress of nations and worlds depending upon the spiritual growth and enlightenment of each person. While the Father treats each ascending soul as a cosmic individual, true development occurs through unselfish service to others and loving relationships within the community.

name: individualism
description: A philosophy or tendency emphasizing the worth and independence of the individual person. Caligastia's ultraindividualism led to his rebellious nature and eventual betrayal of Urantia. Healthy individualism, however, should promote initiative, encourage originality, and foster personal development, as progress demands the cultivation of individuality rather than standardized mediocrity.

name: Indo-China
description: A peninsula region in Southeast Asia where the cultures of India and China blended to create successive civilizations. This area retained a larger proportion of the vanished green race than anywhere else in the world and became a stronghold for Hinayana Buddhism, which preserved the early teachings of Siddhartha.

name: indolence
description: A state of laziness, inaction, or spiritual slothfulness that hinders personal and spiritual growth. Jesus taught his followers to cease useless yearning and take bold spiritual action rather than languishing in futile passivity. Religion serves as evolution's efficient scourge, driving humanity from intellectual inertia toward higher levels of wisdom and spiritual achievement.

name: Indonesia
description: A nation in Southeast Asia inhabited by peoples of Mongoloid type with secondary Sangik heritage. The Indonesian peoples, including Malays, contain a high percentage of secondary Sangik blood and represent part of the diverse racial blending that occurred in this region.

name: Indra
description: An ancient Aryan deity who served as the tempestuous lord of the atmosphere in early Vedic religion. Originally one of the major gods alongside Dyaus and Agni, Indra has survived in Hindu tradition as a secondary deity, representing the stormy forces of nature and atmospheric phenomena.

name: indulgence
description: The practice of yielding to desires or impulses without restraint, particularly in relation to child-rearing and personal discipline. Excessive indulgence can hinder proper character development and spiritual growth by failing to establish necessary boundaries and self-control.

name: Indus
description: A major river system in ancient India where significant Andite migrations occurred around 8000 B.C. The Indus valley became a center of advanced civilization and cultural development, with Dravidian centers establishing trade relationships with Sumeria. The river's delta represents the complex racial blending that occurred in India over the last 50,000 years.

name: industry
description: The organized production of goods and services, including manufacturing, commerce, and technical development. Industry represents humanity's attempt to create security against scarcity and represents a more civilized alternative to militarism. Modern industrial development should promote individual initiative, encourage creativity, and contribute to peaceful progress while avoiding the dangers of materialism and standardized slavery.

name: inequalities
description: Natural differences in human abilities, circumstances, and achievements that arise from varying hereditary endowments, environmental influences, and degrees of spiritual development. While often mistakenly attributed to social injustice alone, these differences reflect the complex interplay of natural laws and provide opportunities for altruistic service and spiritual growth through helping one's fellows.

name: inertia
description: A concept representing resistance to motion or change, often associated with physical or spiritual stagnation.

name: inevitabilitiess
description: The concept of absolute and subabsolute inevitabilities in universe reality, including the Paradise Trinity as the absolute inevitability and the Seven Master Spirits as subabsolute inevitabilities, representing those aspects of creation that were necessary and inescapable in the divine plan.

name: infallibility
description: The quality of being incapable of error or failure, typically associated with divine or absolute truth.

name: infanticide
description: The practice of killing infants, commonly practiced by primitive societies for population control, elimination of deformed children, or cultural reasons, until Christianity's teaching on Christ's love for children helped end this widespread practice.

name: inferiority
description: A psychological condition of feeling inadequate or lesser than others, which Jesus transformed in the case of Fortune from feelings of helplessness into faith, courage, and devoted service.

name: inferiors
description: Beings or individuals considered to be of lesser capability, intelligence, or moral development, whose unrestricted reproduction and dominance poses a threat to civilization's progress and stability according to Urantian teachings on racial improvement.

name: infidelity
description: Unfaithfulness or disloyalty, particularly in marital or spiritual relationships.

name: Infinite
description: The absolute and unlimited source of universal reality, representing the fullness and primacy of the First Source and Center, whose personality is revealed as the Universal Father and can only be comprehended by finite beings through the life of Jesus.

name: Infinite Capacity
description: The endless matrix and possibility for all future cosmic expansion, representing the static-reactive phase of the I AM and embodying the supergravity presence of the Unqualified Absolute.

name: Infinite Mind
description: The mental function of the Infinite Spirit, whose reality is demonstrated through the intelligence circuits and reflectivity of the seconaphim, showing the universal presence of the Conjoint Actor's infinite mind throughout creation.

name: Infinite One (The)
description: The Infinite One is the being that differentiates itself from The Infinitude at the theoretical moment of the first volitional expression within the I AM.

name: Infinite Potential
description: The Infinite Potential is the self-qualified phase of the I AM that is realized as the Deity Absolute, representing the I AM's self-limitation for the purpose of expression and self-revelation.

name: Infinite Spirit
description: The Infinite Spirit is the Third Person of Deity, also known as the Conjoint Actor, God of Action, and by many other titles. He is the divine equal and coordinate of the Universal Father and Eternal Son, serving as the source of mind, the manipulator of cosmic energies, and the universal minister who reveals the combined love of the Father and Son to all creation.

name: Infinite Upholder
description: The Infinite Upholder is the self-associative phase of the I AM, representing the primordial association of the static and potential aspects of reality and manifesting as the Universal Absolute that unifies the Deity Absolute and the Unqualified Absolute.

name: Infinitude (The)
description: The Infinitude is a theoretical postulate representing the aspect of the I AM from which The Infinite One differentiates itself at the moment of first volitional expression, part of a triune conception that includes The Infinity as the eternal continuum.

name: infinity
description: Infinity is both unity and diversity, encompassing total reality within the seven Absolutes while remaining capable of self-limitation and reality expression at all levels of universe existence. It provides the ultimate assurance for endless personality development and contains inherent finite possibility, though its complete realization remains inconceivable to finite creatures.

name: Infinity (The)
description: The Infinity represents the eternal continuum of the I AM that maintains a triune relationship with The Infinite One and The Infinitude in the theoretical portrayal of reality's genesis.

name: influences (spiritual)
description: Spiritual influences refer to the various divine and spiritual forces that affect and guide mortal beings, including the ministrations of angels, the Holy Spirit, and other spiritual agencies working for human uplift and spiritualization.

name: infrared rays
description: Infrared rays are a form of energy manifestation characterized by the slowing down of electronic activity to a stage approaching appreciable heat.

name: infraultimatonic rays
description: Infraultimatonic rays represent the first stage of emergent energy where the borderland revolutions of ultimatons begin to assume definite form, marking the initial phase where wavelike phenomena become detectable and measurable.

name: inheritance
description: The passing of wealth, property, or possessions from one generation to another, which Jesus taught should be viewed as a stewardship responsibility rather than mere material accumulation. The Urantia Book discusses both biological inheritance and the moral obligations of wealth inheritance.

name: iniquitous one
description: A being or personality that has embraced complete wickedness and defiance of divine reality, ultimately facing the loss of continued existence.

name: iniquity
description: The willful, determined, and persistent transgression of divine law and the Father's will, representing an open and persistent defiance of recognized reality that borders on cosmic insanity. Iniquity is the most severe form of spiritual rebellion, beyond sin, error, or evil.

name: initiation ceremonies
description: Ritual practices associated with secret societies and religious cults used to induct new members into their organizations.

name: initiative
description: The personal drive and creative power to act independently and originally. The Urantia Book teaches that initiative is directly proportional to one's spiritual alignment with the Father's will, and that excessive tradition or institutional control can stifle this essential quality for human progress.

name: injunctions
description: Authoritative commands or directives, often of a spiritual or moral nature, similar to commandments.

name: injury
description: Physical harm or damage to the body. The Urantia Book describes the intelligent design behind cellular healing processes, where injured cells release chemicals that stimulate neighboring healthy cells to begin healing and regeneration.

name: injured boy at Pella
description: A young boy whom Jesus ministered to after he was injured in a fall near Pella. Jesus delivered the boy safely to his parents, and this incident led to Andrew becoming Jesus' first apostle.

name: injustice
description: Unfair treatment or violation of rights and moral principles. Jesus taught his followers not to forcibly resist injustice but to suffer it willingly rather than seek revenge, while still approving of society's need to maintain justice through proper governmental authority.

name: inner life
description: The spiritual and mental realm within each individual where true creativity and civilization's advances originate. It represents the internal spiritual world as opposed to the external material world.

name: inns
description: The first inns were maintained by smiths, who were the first nonreligious group to enjoy special privileges but eventually became universally hated due to gross abuse of their privileges. After being driven out of villages, smiths maintained these first public lodginghouses on the outskirts of settlements.

name: inquiring Greeks
description: A group of believing Greeks from Alexandria, Athens, and Rome who faithfully attended Jesus' teachings in the temple. Thirty of them elected to enter the kingdom after a conference at Nicodemus' home, and they were present during many significant events of Jesus' final days, including his last temple discourse and several morontia appearances.

name: insanity
description: A mental condition distinguished by Jesus from demon possession, though this distinction was often confused in his time. Insanity is practically nonexistent in advanced worlds settled in light and life, and primitive peoples often regarded the insane as either divine or cursed beings.

name: insects
description: Arthropod creatures that evolved during early geological periods, including massive dragonflies and giant cockroaches. Unlike humans, insects are born fully educated and equipped for life, representing nonprogressive species that developed resting stages to survive climate changes.

name: insecurity
description: Feelings of uncertainty and fear of personality isolation that can be antidoted through faith contemplation of the Father. Insecurity provides opportunities for hope and trust, driving human existence to face recurrent uncertainties for spiritual growth.

name: insideness
description: The spiritual viewpoint or inner awareness of human experience that allows personality to perceive the universe as spiritual in nature. This inward perspective contrasts with the outward material viewpoint, enabling the transformation of universal understanding from material to spiritual.

name: insight
description: The capacity for understanding meanings and grasping spiritual truths, often preceding direct experience. Only personalities possess insight in advance of experience, allowing them to learn from looking as well as from doing, and to discern deeper significance in seemingly ordinary circumstances.

name: insincerity
description: A character flaw that the morontia career works to permanently eradicate from mortal survivors. Insincerity can evolve into deliberate sin, as demonstrated by Lucifer's self-deception, and represents one of the animal vestigial traits that must be overcome in spiritual development.

name: inspiration
description: Jesus' life served as spiritual inspiration rather than a detailed example for all beings throughout the universe. His earth life was designed to inspire and guide all mortal beings on their Paradise ascension journey, providing a high spiritual ideal rather than a literal pattern to copy.

name: Inspired Trinity Spirits
aliases: Inspired Spirits of the Paradise Trinity
description: One of the few wholly secret orders of beings created by the Paradise Trinity, capable of superconscious ministry throughout the universes. They appear to be almost omnipresent, operate independently of evolutionary schemes, and may function as teachers bridging gaps in spiritual enlightenment through techniques beyond conscious reception.

name: instability
description: A state of emotional imbalance that can result when virtues like sympathy are carried to extremes, demonstrating how any virtue taken too far may become a vice.

name: instigators of rest
description: Primary supernaphim who serve as Paradise inspectors on Havona's inner circuit, preparing ascending pilgrims for the final transition sleep that graduates them from time into eternity. They collaborate with complements of rest to ready pilgrims for their introduction to Paradise.

name: instincts
description: Innate drives and behavioral tendencies that influence human society and individual development, including the herd instinct, food hunger, sex urges, and self-preservation impulses. While instincts provide basic motivations, they can conflict with higher spiritual aspirations and intellectual training.

name: institutions
description: Social establishments that arise from human groups to serve collective needs and enhance security, categorized into three types: self-maintenance (industry, property), self-perpetuation (family, education), and self-gratification (customs, entertainment). While they provide social benefits, their overdevelopment can overshadow individual worth and diminish personal initiative.

name: instruction for teachers and believers
description: Jesus' principles for preaching truth and teaching the gospel, emphasizing respect for human personality and relying on spiritual power rather than coercion or fear.

name: insubordination
description: Modern tendency toward disobedience that results from civilization protecting children from the natural consequences of foolish conduct, unlike ancient times when children learned discipline through direct experience of failure or danger.

name: insult
description: An animal trait in humans that leads to verbal or physical assault against those who are spiritually or intellectually superior, representing a brutish reaction to what one cannot attain or achieve.

name: insurance
description: A concept of protection against risk that evolved from primitive ghost cults and magical practices into modern economic and social systems, representing humanity's attempt to secure safety against disasters, accidents, and future uncertainties.

name: intellectualism
description: The overdevelopment of intellectual factors in religion, which can handicap and embarrass spiritual growth despite the important role that intellectual elements play in religious experience.

name: intelligence
description: The capacity for understanding, reasoning, and learning that contributes to cultural progress and enables effective problem-solving and adaptation.

name: intelligence co-ordinators (Seraphic Corps of Completion)
description: Glorified intelligence co-ordinators in the Seraphic Corps of Completion who work with seraphic broadcasters to facilitate the exchange of essential information through sophisticated ideographic techniques.

name: intelligence co-ordinators (tertiary supernaphim)
description: Tertiary supernaphim who serve as "living newspapers" of Havona, promoting fraternal association between ascending and descending pilgrims by keeping them informed about universe affairs through advanced communication methods.

name: intelligence corps
description: Superior seraphim belonging to Gabriel's personal staff who gather information throughout the local universe for his guidance in the councils of Nebadon, reporting directly to Salvington on independent circuits.

name: intentions
description: The purposes or aims that guide conscious decisions and actions, reflecting the inner motivations of individuals in their spiritual and moral development.

name: interbreeding
description: The reproduction between different racial or biological groups, which can contribute to genetic diversity and evolutionary development within species populations.

name: intercessors
description: Intermediaries who supposedly mediate between humans and divine beings, though true communion with the loving Father requires no such mediators according to advanced religious understanding.

name: interdependence
description: The fundamental principle that all forces and personalities throughout the universe are interconnected and rely on cooperative relationships, from Creator Sons and power centers to human physical and spiritual development.

name: internationalism
description: A movement toward global cooperation and unity that represents progress beyond nationalism, though true lasting peace requires nations to surrender sovereign powers to a world government of mankind.

name: interpreters and translators
description: Morontia Companions who serve as skilled language teachers and communication facilitators, helping ascending mortals learn the tongues of Satania and Nebadon on the mansion worlds.

name: interpreters of cosmic citizenship
description: Seraphic administrators who guide new citizens of system capitals in understanding their responsibilities of universe government and help quicken their appreciation of cosmic citizenship duties.

name: interpreters of ethics
description: Primary supernaphim who serve on Paradise to help ascending mortals adjust to the numerous groups of majestic beings during their transition from residential status to induction into the Corps of Mortal Finaliters. They work in reflective liaison with the Unions of Souls to provide guidance on ethical relationships and the Paradise ideal of proper adjustment to complex interpersonal problems.

name: interregnum of wisdom
description: A period characterized by the inexorable restoration of balance between self-liberty and self-control that occurs when human civilization advances too rapidly, causing material achievement to outrun the evolution of worship-wisdom. This phenomenon serves as a vital brake on dangerous velocities of progress and leads to the "dark ages" that witness society's recession from premature levels of attainment.

name: intersonship circuit
description: A direct and exclusive communication channel between the Eternal Son and all Paradise Sons scattered throughout creation, based on their spiritual kinship. This circuit provides instantaneous communication that is independent of time though sometimes conditioned by space, and is entirely different from the universal circuit of spirit gravity.

name: intolerance
description: A mental poison and mask covering secret doubts about one's beliefs that greatly interferes with spiritual progress and human advancement. Religious intolerance arises from assumptions of superiority among faiths and is greatly multiplied by the institutionalization of religion, while being combated through the coordination of science, commerce, play, and religion.

name: intoxication
description: A state that primitive peoples believed rendered one divine, leading to the worship of plants that produced intoxicating substances. Ancient cultures viewed intoxication as a form of spirit possession, with shamans using drugs and intoxicants as fetishes to induce mystical states and impress tribesmen during religious ceremonies.

name: introspection
description: The practice of examining one's own thoughts, feelings, and motivations as part of spiritual and psychological development.

name: intuition
description: The immediate apprehension or understanding of knowledge without evident rational thought and inference, often associated with spiritual insight and the leadings of the adjutant mind-spirits.

name: invention
description: The creation of new devices, processes, or methods that drive cultural acceleration and liberate humanity from unremitting toil. Invention benefits science most of all and, combined with capital, provides economic freedom while enabling civilization to flourish through the leisure time it creates for thinking, planning, and imagining better ways of doing things.

name: invisible
description: That which cannot be seen but whose movement and trends can be detected by observing its effects on the visible, as demonstrated in the universe by detecting the movements of Supremacy through observing repercussions in the personalities and patterns of the grand universe.

name: ionization
description: The process by which atoms lose their outer electrons through electrical, thermal, X-ray activities, or solvents, creating charged particles. In Earth's atmosphere, ionization occurs in the upper levels due to solar radiation and sunspot activity, causing phenomena such as auroral displays and compass variation.

name: ionosphere
description: A region of the Earth's upper atmosphere that becomes ionized by intense heat and contains electrified conducting layers that enable long-distance radio transmission, though storms in these outer ionospheres can sometimes disrupt broadcasting.

name: Iowa
description: A North American state that was mostly covered by the central lobe of the advancing fifth glacier during the ice age.

name: Iran
description: A region in ancient times known as the Iranian plateau, which served as a corridor for Andite migrations and was home to various peoples including the followers of Zoroaster, who revived monotheistic teachings in Persia.

name: Ireland
description: An island where early mountain movements 290 million years ago contributed to the formation of the great Caledonian Mountains, which extended through Scotland to Spitzbergen.

name: Iron Age
description: A period characterized by the widespread use of iron tools and weapons, though distinct Stone, Bronze, and Iron Ages existed simultaneously in different regions rather than as sequential evolutionary stages.

name: Iron (city)
description: A city in Galilee where Jesus worked in mines, taught at the synagogue, and encountered a simple-minded Galilean man who was being ridiculed by Judeans in the Jerusalem temple.

name: iron (metal)
description: A heavy metal that settled toward Earth's center during planetary formation and serves vital biological functions, including oxygen transport in red blood cells and various industrial applications in ancient civilizations.

name: ironies
description: Paradoxical situations or contradictory outcomes that reveal deeper truths about existence, such as how ignorance can be essential for success or how national egotism, though regrettable, has been necessary for social survival.

name: Iroquois
description: A federation of six Native American nations that represented the closest approach to statehood among the northern red men, but failed to survive due to their adherence to mother-family organization and lack of essential elements like private property inheritance and definite territory.

name: irrigation
description: Advanced agricultural techniques involving water management and flood control that were developed by the Adamites in Mesopotamia and taught by Caligastia's staff, and later used by the Sumerians as part of their military canal defense systems.

name: irritation
description: A state of annoyance or agitation that can lead to negative emotional responses and complaining behavior.

name: Isaac (Nazareth moneylender)
description: Isaac was a wealthy Jewish moneylender to the Gentiles in Nazareth who offered to financially support Jesus' family if Jesus would assume leadership of the local nationalist patriots.

name: Isaac (Old Testament)
description: Isaac was the son of Abraham, born in accordance with Melchizedek's promise. After Isaac's birth, Abraham took his covenant with Melchizedek very seriously and formalized it in writing. Isaac maintained his father's teachings and promoted the gospel of the Salem colony, though it was harder for the next generation to fully grasp the significance of Melchizedek's traditions.

name: Isador
description: Isador was a disciple of the Apostle Matthew who wrote the Gospel of Matthew. He escaped from Jerusalem in 70 AD during the siege by Titus's armies and took refuge in Pella, where he wrote the gospel in 71 AD using Matthew's personal remembrances and notes, as well as portions of Mark's narrative.

name: Isaiah (first)
description: Isaiah the first was a Hebrew prophet who appeared during the awakening of Hebrew consciousness. He preached about the infinite wisdom and perfection of God, proclaimed a beneficent king-deliverer, and wrote the famous text that became Jesus' 'birthday text' at school. His teachings represented a transition from the nationalistic Yahweh to a more universal concept of God, though he prophesied that Jerusalem would never fall.

name: Isaiah (second)
description: The second Isaiah was a prophet of the Babylonian captivity who wrote chapters 40-55 of the Book of Isaiah. He effectively countered many racially selfish misconceptions about the promised Messiah and made beautiful proclamations about the heavenly Father. He preached about salvation by sacrifice and redemption, and his writings were among the most sublime presentations of the spiritual concept of God prior to Michael's arrival on Urantia.

name: Ishtar
description: Ishtar was the Mesopotamian goddess of sexual fertility and mother of gods. In ancient Mesopotamia, it was customary for all young women to submit at least once to the embrace of strangers as an act of devotion to Ishtar, as fertility was believed to depend on this sexual sacrifice.

name: Isis
description: Isis was an Egyptian goddess in the mystery cult centered around her and her son Osiris. She was worshipped by Egyptian agricultural workers and represented the Egyptian equivalent of Ishtar in Mesopotamia. The worship of Isis included elaborate rituals built around the legend of death and resurrection.

name: Islam
description: Islam is the religion founded by Mohammed in the sixth century after Christ, featuring sublime ideals superior to many creeds of his time. Its strength lies in the clear-cut presentation of Allah as the one and only Deity, while its weaknesses include the association of military force with its promulgation and the degradation of women. Islam is one of the great international, interracial faiths that provides deliverance from the rigorous moral standards of Judaism and Christianity.

name: Isle of Paradise
description: The Isle of Paradise is the eternal center of the universe and the dwelling place of the Universal Father, serving as the geographical heart of infinity and the focal point of all spiritual gravity.

name: Isle of Patmos
description: A Greek island where the apostle John was imprisoned and banished for four years, during which he wrote the Book of Revelation.

name: isms
description: Various garbled religious and philosophical movements that arise from misunderstood or distorted communications from Thought Adjusters to human minds.

name: isolation
description: The separation of individuals from social, spiritual, or cosmic connections, which can lead to various problems including personality stagnation, spiritual difficulties, and emotional distress, though it can also provide opportunities for communion with God and personal growth when used properly.

name: isolation worlds
description: Worlds that have been quarantined or cut off from normal universe circuits, typically as a consequence of rebellion, providing unique opportunities for faith development.

name: Israel
description: The northern kingdom of the Hebrew people, named after the descendants of Abraham, which fell to Assyria after conspiring with Egypt and refusing to pay tribute.

name: Israelites
description: The Hebrew people who formed the northern kingdom, consisting of only three or four tribes that settled in Palestine and intermarried with the Canaanites, with their national consciousness originating in Ephraim.

name: isthmuses
description: Narrow strips of land connecting larger landmasses, serving as natural bridges for migration and trade routes.

name: Italy
description: A Mediterranean country settled by Andites and later visited by Jesus during his journey to Rome, where he influenced religious leaders and spread his teachings.

name: Jabbok
description: A river flowing from the east into the Jordan River, historically significant as the region where Gideon fought against the invading Midianites.

name: Jacob (evangelist)
description: A disciple of Jesus who directed a group of twelve younger evangelists during the Master's teaching ministry.

name: Jacob (messenger)
description: Jacob was David Zebedee's fastest and most trusted messenger, to whom Jesus entrusted his last farewell message to Abner at Philadelphia on the night of his betrayal.

name: Jacob of Crete
description: Jacob was a wealthy Jewish merchant from Crete who met privately with Jesus during the Passover festivities in Jerusalem, seeking to understand Jesus' teachings about God as a loving Father rather than a wrathful deity.

name: Jacob of Emmaus
description: Jacob of Emmaus was a shepherd and the younger brother of Cleopas who, though not a believer in Jesus, was intrigued by the Master's teachings and witnessed Jesus' seventh morontia appearance on the road to Emmaus.

name: Jacob (Old Testament)
description: Jacob was the grandson of Abraham who had difficulty understanding the traditions of Salem and was known for sleeping on a stone which he venerated and anointed.

name: Jacob the stone mason (father)
description: Jacob the elder was a stone mason and business partner of Joseph who owned houses in partnership with him in Nazareth and served as a neighbor and adviser to the family until his death around 20 AD.

name: Jacob the stone mason (son)
description: Jacob was the son of Jacob the stone mason, Jesus' closest childhood friend and self-appointed defender who protected Jesus from bullies and later became a business partner of Jesus' brothers before marrying Jesus' sister Miriam in November 20 AD.

name: Jacob’s well
description: Jacob's well was a historic water source in Samaria where Jesus stopped during his travels and later had his famous conversation with Nalda the Samaritan woman about living water.

name: Jah
description: Jah is a Semitic name for the Deity, a shortened version of Yahweh used to avoid pronouncing the sacred name.

name: Jainism
description: Jainism arose as a significant religious movement in the sixth century BC, emphasizing righteousness, truth, self-conquest, and compassion, and has influenced Hinduism as one of the world's oldest and most cosmopolitan religions.

name: Jairus
description: Jairus was a ruler of the synagogue in Capernaum whose daughter Jesus healed from a coma, though witnesses considered it a resurrection, and who later resigned his position and openly aligned himself with Jesus despite pressure from Jerusalem religious leaders.

name: James, Epistle of
description: The Epistle of James is one of the few exceptions in the New Testament that describes the religious life of Jesus, offering a more authentic portrayal of the Master's teachings compared to other New Testament writings.

name: James Alpheus
description: James Alpheus was a fisherman and farmer from Kheresa who was selected by James Zebedee to be one of the twelve apostles.
