name: James (brother of Jesus)
description: James was Jesus' second-born brother, born April 2, 3 B.C. He became the acting head of the family at eighteen and later served as the titular head of the Christian church in Jerusalem after Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection. James was one of only four witnesses who heard the voice at Jesus' baptism and was present for Jesus' third morontia appearance.

name: James of Safed
description: James of Safed was a father from Tiberias whose fourteen-year-old epileptic son was healed by Jesus. He spoke the memorable words of faith mixed with doubt: "Lord, I believe. I pray you help my unbelief."

name: James Zebedee
description: James Zebedee, nicknamed "Son of Thunder" along with his brother John, was one of Jesus' twelve apostles. He was thirty years old when called, married with four children, and worked as a fisherman in partnership with his brother John, Andrew, and Simon Peter. James was the first apostle to be martyred, put to death by Herod Agrippa.

name: Jamnia
description: Jamnia was a town on the Mediterranean coast that Jesus passed through during his solo journey through Palestine in A.D. 25.

name: Jansad
description: Jansad was the eldest son of Eveson and grandson of Adam who became the successor of Adam as head of the Adamite tribes.

name: Japan
description: Japan is an island nation in the Pacific where Buddhism became the dominant religion within one generation under Asoka's missionary efforts. The Japanese ancestors were driven off the Chinese mainland by northern Chinese tribes around 12,000 B.C., and Japan's eastern coastline hovers precariously over oceanic depths due to geological pressures.

name: Japhia
description: Japhia was a town that Jesus visited during his second public preaching tour of Galilee, which lasted from October 3 to December 30, A.D. 28.

name: Jaram the Hittite
description: Jaram the Hittite was a student of Melchizedek who was sent to invite Abraham and his brother Nahor to come to Salem to hear the teachings about the eternal Creator.

name: Java
description: Java is an island in Southeast Asia that was connected to the Asian mainland by a land bridge during the ice age, allowing early human migrations. During the Andonic migrations 950,000 years ago, descendants of Andon and Fonta crossed this land bridge and journeyed eastward to Java. Later, Asoka established Buddhism in Java, where it became the dominant religion.

name: Java man
description: Java man refers to the fossil remains found in Java belonging to descendants of Andon and Fonta who migrated eastward 950,000 years ago during their expansion across Europe and Asia.

name: jealousy
description: Jealousy is a mental poison that interferes with spiritual progress and greatly hinders the efforts of the Adjuster to exalt the material mind. While God is jealous for man, never of him, human jealousy can turn genuine love into hate through disappointment and resentment. Modern sexual jealousy is not innate but rather a product of evolving social customs and mores.

name: Jebus
description: Jebus was the ancient name for the city that became Jerusalem, originally the site of Salem where Melchizedek established his community. David made a strategic compact with the Jebusites and established his capital of the united kingdom at Jebus, a strong-walled city positioned between Judah and Israel.

name: Jebusites
description: The Jebusites were a Canaanite people who inhabited the city of Jebus (later Jerusalem). David welcomed these non-Hebrew Palestinians into his new kingdom of Judah, where they shared the Hebrew concept of land ownership and held the ideologies of the desert clans.

name: Jehoash
description: Jehoash was a king of Israel who, along with his son Jeroboam, delivered Israel from its enemies and brought new life to the nation. Their reign preceded the period when a gangster-nobility ruled in Samaria, which led to the secret writing of Elijah, Amos, and Hosea.

name: Jehonadab
description: Jehonadab, son of Rechab, was a gypsy chieftain who joined forces with the country landlord Jehu to destroy the prophets of Baal at Samaria. He opposed the sedentary life of the cities and considered the purchase of land to be sinful.

name: Jehovah
description: Jehovah is a term used in recent times to designate the completed concept of Yahweh that evolved through the long Hebrew experience. The name Jehovah did not come into use until fifteen hundred years after the times of Jesus, around 1500 A.D.

name: Jehu
description: Jehu was a country landlord who joined forces with the gypsy chieftain Jehonadab to destroy the prophets of Baal at Samaria. He supported Elijah's reform defending the old land customs against the land-selling attitude of the Baalim and the attempt of cities to dominate the countryside.

name: jellyfish
description: Jellyfish were marine animals that existed in many varieties during ancient geological periods, particularly 310 million years ago. These early jellyfish species have since become extinct, though they were abundant during the time when lime-secreting algae were widespread and cephalopods were well developed.

name: Jephthah
description: A biblical judge of Israel who made a tragic vow to sacrifice whatever first came out of his house to greet him in exchange for victory over his enemies. When his daughter and only child came to welcome him home, he fulfilled his promise and offered her as a human sacrifice, illustrating the heartbreaking conflicts between ancient religious customs and advancing civilization.

name: Jeramy
description: A Greek Jewish proselyte from Nicopolis who hosted Jesus and his companions during their Mediterranean voyage. Paul later spent a winter with Jeramy's son during his third missionary journey.

name: Jeremiah
description: A Hebrew prophet who boldly proclaimed Yahweh as the universal God of all nations, not just the Hebrews. He fearlessly declared that righteousness comes from within the heart rather than external observances, counseled surrender to Babylon during Jerusalem's siege, and proclaimed the downfall of the city despite opposition from priests and rulers who cast him into a dungeon.

name: Jericho
description: An ancient Palestinian city near the Jordan River crossing where Joshua and the Israelites first entered the promised land. Jesus visited Jericho multiple times during his ministry, healing Bartimeus the blind beggar and encountering Zaccheus the tax collector, who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus and welcomed him into his home.

name: Jeroboam
description: Son of Joash and thirteenth king of Israel who delivered Israel from its enemies. During his reign, a gangster-nobility ruled in Samaria, and the attempts to suppress freedom of speech led prophets like Elijah, Amos, and Hosea to begin their secret writings, marking the real beginning of the Jewish and Christian Bibles.

name: Jerusalem
description: The holy city and spiritual center of Judaism, originally called Salem in Melchizedek's time and later Jebus before becoming Jerusalem. Built on the site where David established his capital, it housed the temple that served as the focal point for Jewish worship worldwide. Jesus made his triumphal entry here, cleansed the temple, taught in its courts, and was crucified outside its walls. He prophesied its destruction, which occurred in 70 A.D. when Roman armies leveled the city.

name: Jerusalem temple
description: The central place of Jewish worship in Jerusalem, where animal sacrifices were offered and religious ceremonies conducted. Built by Herod, it featured multiple courts including areas for gentiles, women, priests, and the holy of holies. The temple became commercialized with money-changers and animal sellers, leading Jesus to cleanse it twice. Jesus taught regularly in its courts, particularly Solomon's Porch, and prophesied the temple's destruction.

name: Jerusalem temple guards
description: Jewish soldiers responsible for maintaining order at the Jerusalem temple. Led by a captain, they collaborated with Roman soldiers to arrest Jesus in Gethsemane, participated in mocking and beating him during his trial, and later fled in terror when they witnessed the stone rolling away from Jesus' tomb. They were subsequently bribed to claim that disciples had stolen Jesus' body.

name: Jerusem
description: The headquarters world of the local system of Satania, consisting of a central sphere surrounded by seven major transition worlds and forty-nine subsatellites. Located about one hundred times the size of Urantia, Jerusem serves as the administrative center where ascending mortals from the mansion worlds gather before proceeding to the constellation capital of Edentia. It features various residential circles for different orders of beings, magnificent architecture, and serves as the first major destination for mortals after death.

name: Jesus
description: The Creator Son Michael of Nebadon incarnated in human form on Urantia as Joshua ben Joseph of Nazareth. Born to Mary and Joseph around 7 B.C., he lived a complete human life while gradually revealing his divine nature and mission. He established the gospel of the kingdom of heaven, demonstrating the loving character of the Paradise Father through his teachings, healings, and perfect human life. After his crucifixion and resurrection, he ascended to assume full sovereignty over his local universe of Nebadon.

name: Jethro
description: Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, played a crucial role in combining the teachings of Melchizedek with the traditions of Egypt and Palestine to guide the creation of the improved religion of the Israelites.

name: Jewelry
description: Decorative objects worn for personal adornment, including various forms of ornamental accessories.

name: Jewish priesthood
description: The religious leadership class within ancient Jewish society, responsible for temple worship, ritual ceremonies, and religious instruction.

name: Jewish religion
description: The religious system and practices of the Jewish people, based on monotheistic worship and adherence to divine law.

name: Jewish tutor
description: Jesus was known as the "Jewish tutor" in Corinth during his travels through the Roman world.

name: Jews
description: The Jewish people, descended from ancient Hebrew tribes, who developed a distinctive monotheistic religion and maintained their identity despite centuries of foreign rule and dispersion throughout the Roman Empire. They were characterized by their strong religious traditions, adherence to divine law, and expectation of a coming Messiah.

name: Jews (ancient Hebrews)
description: The ancient Hebrew people who formed the nucleus of the later Jewish nation. While Abraham's offspring were central to their formation, the Hebrews also incorporated many fellow nomads and adopted aliens, creating a diverse tribal confederation united by religious beliefs and shared traditions.

name: Jezebel
description: Jewish queen of Phoenician origin whose deeds, along with those of Ahab and Jehu, were recounted to Jesus during his journey through historic locations in Palestine.

name: Jezreel
description: A town in Palestine where Jesus and his family stopped during their journey to Jerusalem for the Passover celebration in A.D. 7, and later visited by Jesus and the twelve apostles during their second public preaching tour of Galilee in A.D. 28.

name: Joab
description: A young evangelist who served as the ringleader of a plot to make Jesus king during the feeding of the five thousand, but faced resistance and desertion when the crowds grew hungry and Jesus withdrew from public view.

name: Joachim
description: Mary's father who was always skeptical about Jesus' divine mission, along with the rest of Mary's family except her sister Salome.

name: Joanna
description: Wife of Chuza, steward of Herod Antipas, and treasurer of the women's corps that ministered to Jesus. She was one of the ten women appointed by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom and was among the five women who discovered Jesus' empty tomb on resurrection morning.

name: Joash
description: Boy king who led a monotheistic revolt and crusaded for Yahweh for thirty-five years, ending a reign of terror in Jerusalem that included a temple of Baal alongside the temple of Yahweh.

name: Job (book)
description: An Old Testament book that demonstrates human suffering is not always punishment for sin, as Job achieved salvation through a vision of God despite his innocent suffering. The book was compiled by Mesopotamian religious teachers over 300 years and preserves the Salem school teachings, presenting a variegated picture of Deity from the neighborhood of Ur of Chaldea.

name: Jogbehah
description: One of the towns visited during the Perea mission led by Abner and the seventy messengers of the kingdom.

name: Johab
description: Son of Nathan and groom at the wedding in Cana where Jesus performed his first public miracle, marrying Naomi in a celebration that Jesus and his first disciples attended.

name: John Hyrcanus
description: Maccabean leader who destroyed the Samaritan temple on Mount Gerizim, further intensifying the religious enmity between the Jews and the Samaritans.

name: John Mark
description: Son of Elijah and Mary Mark who served as the boy of all chores for Jesus and the twelve apostles. He faithfully followed Jesus during his final hours, witnessed the crucifixion, and later wrote the Gospel of Mark based on his own memories and Peter's recollections.

name: John the Baptist
description: Born March 25, 7 B.C., John was the son of Zacharias and Elizabeth and the forerunner of Jesus' mission. He preached repentance and baptism in preparation for the coming kingdom, baptized Jesus in the Jordan River, and was later executed by Herod Antipas on January 10, A.D. 28.

name: John the Revelator
description: John Zebedee in his later role as the author of the Book of Revelation, one of the original twelve apostles who became known for his mystical writings about the end times.

name: John Zebedee
description: John Zebedee was the youngest of the twelve apostles at age 24, unmarried and living with his parents in Bethsaida. A fisherman working with his brother James in partnership with Andrew and Peter, John served as Jesus' personal agent in dealing with the Master's family affairs and continued to care for Mary, Jesus' mother, throughout her life. Known for his dependability and devotion, he was one of the three apostles chosen to be Jesus' closest companions and was present at all the important events of Jesus' ministry.

name: Jonah
description: Jonah is a figure from Hebrew scripture whose story Jesus used to illustrate profound spiritual truths about God's love for all people, including those considered outsiders or heathens, regardless of whether Jonah actually existed as a historical person.

name: Joppa
description: Joppa was an important port city on the Mediterranean coast where Jesus and his traveling companions stopped several times during their journeys. It was here that Jesus met and befriended Gadiah, a Philistine interpreter, during his Mediterranean travels with Gonod and Ganid.

name: Jordan River
description: The Jordan River was a significant waterway in Palestine where John the Baptist conducted his ministry near Pella and where Jesus was baptized. The river flows through the tropical Jordan valley with winding, glistening waters that flow toward the Dead Sea, and it emerges from an underground cave near Caesarea-Philippi.

name: Jose
description: Jose was a renowned liberal teacher and rabbi in Nazareth who influenced the synagogue's progressive interpretation of religious practices. Under his influence, Jesus was able to modify certain family religious customs and practices in his community.

name: Joseph (brother of Jesus)
description: Joseph was the fourth child in Jesus' family, born March 16, A.D. 1. Unlike his brothers, he was more inclined to defend himself physically when attacked by playmates, departing from the family's teachings of nonresistance. Though a faithful worker, he was less spiritually minded than his siblings and served as head of the family household after Jesus' departure.

name: Joseph (father of Jesus)
description: Joseph was the human father of Jesus, a skilled carpenter and later a prosperous contractor from Nazareth. A Hebrew of the Hebrews with mixed ancestry, he was mild-mannered, conscientious, and faithful to Jewish religious practices. He died from injuries sustained in a construction accident at Sepphoris on September 25, 8 A.D., when Jesus was fourteen years old, leaving Jesus to become the head of the family.

name: Joseph of Arimathea
description: Joseph of Arimathea was a wealthy member of the Sanhedrin who became a secret believer in Jesus. Along with Nicodemus, he boldly claimed Jesus' body from Pilate after the crucifixion and provided his own new family tomb for Jesus' burial. He believed Jesus would rise from the dead and later hosted one of Jesus' post-resurrection appearances at his home.

name: Joseph of Tyre
description: Joseph of Tyre was a Jewish believer who lived three or four miles south of Tyre, near the ancient tomb of King Hiram. He hosted Jesus and the apostles at his home for two weeks in July, serving as their headquarters while they taught and preached throughout the busy seaport city of Tyre.

name: Joseph (Old Testament)
description: Joseph was the great-grandson of Abraham and a firm believer in Melchizedek's teachings, which his brothers considered him a dreamer for accepting. Rather than accepting military command of Egyptian armies, he chose to serve as a civil administrator in Egypt, believing he could better advance the kingdom of heaven through peaceful governance and ethical leadership.

name: Joshua ben Joseph
description: The Hebrew name of Jesus, meaning "Yahweh is salvation," which was his given name at birth.

name: Joshua (Jesus)
description: The name given to Jesus by divine instruction through Gabriel, who told Mary to call her son Joshua, announcing that he would inaugurate the kingdom of heaven on earth. On the eighth day after his birth, the child was circumcised and formally named Joshua (Jesus).

name: Joshua (Old Testament)
description: The Hebrew leader who succeeded Moses and led the Israelites into the promised land, known for his military conquests including Jericho. After Moses' death, Joshua found it necessary to preach a stern gospel emphasizing Yahweh as a holy, jealous God of power, judgment, and justice, though the common people gradually reverted to older desert concepts of deity.

name: Joshua the teacher
description: A reference to Jesus during his teaching ministry phase, representing a chapter in his life as the Son of Man that concluded his work as an educator and spiritual instructor.

name: Josiah (blind beggar)
description: A man born blind who was healed by Jesus on the Sabbath, sparking controversy with the Sanhedrin. Despite being cast out of the synagogue for boldly proclaiming Jesus as a prophet, Josiah became a lifelong preacher of the gospel and accompanied Jesus to Pella, proving to be one of the fruitful recipients of the Master's miraculous ministry.

name: Josiah (disciple of Abner)
description: One of the disciples of Abner who baptized a Pharisee lawyer that professed faith in the gospel of the kingdom after being moved by Jesus' teaching.

name: Josiah (king)
description: A righteous king of Judah who destroyed the corrupt ring of politicians in Jerusalem and attempted to restore proper worship. His reign marked a temporary recovery from corruption and idolatry, but his tragic death while intercepting Pharaoh Necho's army led to Judah falling under Egyptian tribute and the return of Baal worship.

name: Jotapata
description: A small town in Galilee where Jesus and his apostles conducted an important teaching mission. Here Jesus gave a significant discourse on prayer, thanksgiving, and worship, and Mary Magdalene and Rebecca were ordained as effective teachers of the gospel, becoming part of the twelve women evangelists.

name: Jove
description: The Roman supreme deity who, as human culture advances and monotheism develops, persists only as an exclamation while being subordinated to a higher concept of supreme deity.

name: joy
description: A spiritual fruit that emerges from trust in the divine word and faith in God's indwelling spirit, transcending material circumstances. True joy cannot be experienced in isolation but requires sharing life with others, and represents one of the characteristics exhibited by spirit-led mortals guided by divine truth and love.

name: Joys of Existence
description: A specialized order of celestial beings who serve as reflective associates of Divine Counselors, promoting reactions of joy among angelic hosts and lower will creatures. They collaborate with reversion directors and function as joy clearinghouses, seeking to demonstrate that there is inherent joy in freewill existence independent of all external influences.

name: jubilee of jubilees
description: The momentous occasion when the Universe Spirit publicly acknowledges subordination to the Creator Son before assembled hosts, pledging fidelity and obedience. This event occurs at the enthronement of the Creator Son as a Master Son following completion of his seventh and final creature bestowal.

name: jubilees
description: Significant spiritual milestones marking major transitions in the ascendant career. The seventh jubilee celebrates entrance into the mortal finaliter corps and the beginning of eternity service, while jubilee celebrations also mark the settling of local universes in light and life.

name: Judah
description: The southern Jewish kingdom and clan from which Jewish consciousness originated, as distinguished from the northern Israelite kingdom of Ephraim. David established his kingdom of Judah at Hebron and later united it with Israel under his rule from Jerusalem.

name: Judaism
description: The Hebrew religion that evolved as one of the great international faiths alongside Buddhism, Christianity, and Islam. Judaism preserved moral values and the concept of one universal God, though it was characterized by rigid traditionalism and ceremonial law that sometimes hindered spiritual progress and philosophical development.

name: Judas Alpheus
description: One of the Alpheus twins who served among Jesus' twelve apostles, known for his quiet and contemplative nature in contrast to his more outspoken brother James Alpheus.

name: Judas Iscariot
description: The twelfth apostle chosen by Nathaniel, born in Kerioth in southern Judea and the only Judean among the twelve. He served as treasurer of the apostolic group and was well-educated and cultured, but became increasingly isolated and resentful, ultimately betraying Jesus for thirty pieces of silver before taking his own life.

name: Judas Maccabee
description: A Jewish military leader whose heroic exploits in restoring Mosaic temple services and delivering the Jews from foreign oppression created vibrant expectations among the Jewish people for the appearance of an even greater deliverer, the long-expected Messiah.

name: Jude
description: The seventh child and youngest brother of Jesus, born when Jesus was twelve years old. Known for his fiery temperament and patriotic sentiments, Jude was impulsive and often got into trouble, but he eventually became a faithful supporter of the family and later believed in Jesus' mission.

name: Judea
description: The southern region of Palestine that served as the Roman province governed by Pilate, with Caesarea as its capital. This was the homeland of the Jewish religious establishment and the location of Jerusalem, making it a center of orthodox Jewish tradition and resistance to new religious teachings.

name: Judge-Arbiters
description: An order of celestial beings who serve as judicial officials on conciliating commissions, appointing Spirit-Advocates to represent and safeguard the rights of all personalities in adjudication matters.

name: judges
description: Judicial officials responsible for administering justice and maintaining law and order. Jesus emphasized that judges must demonstrate impartiality, fairness, and integrity in their pursuit of justice, noting that a nation's endurance depends upon the integrity of its courts.

name: Judges (Old Testament)
description: The tribal leaders and sheiks who ruled Israel after Moses' death, under whose successive leadership the Hebrew concept of Yahweh deteriorated from Moses' elevated understanding of an all-wise, beneficent, and almighty God back to the older desert conception of deity.

name: judgment
description: The divine process of evaluation and decision-making regarding individual survival and spiritual progression. Judgment is always a group function rather than a personal act, administered by collective bodies such as the Paradise Trinity, with mercy tempering justice in all divine verdicts.

name: Julias
description: One of Herod's residences in Perea where he maintained court, and where Joanna conducted Sabbath services for Jesus' apostolic party in the palace banquet room.

name: Juno
description: A Roman goddess honored alongside Jupiter and Minerva in the magnificent temple on the Capitolium in Rome, which Jesus often visited and contemplated during his stay in the city.

name: Jupiter (god)
description: The chief Roman god, equivalent to the Greek Zeus, honored in a magnificent temple on Rome's Capitolium. Jupiter was characterized as a reveler in contrast to the Hebrew ideal of divine labor and industry.

name: Jupiter (planet)
description: The largest planet in the solar system, formed from the central portions of superheated solar material and remaining largely gaseous since its formation. Jupiter briefly functioned as a secondary sun and captured most of the material stolen from the Angona system, as evidenced by the retrograde motion of some of its satellites.

name: Jurassic
description: A geological period beginning 120,000,000 years ago that marked the evolution and eventual decline of the dinosaurs during the later reptilian age, when land-animal life reached its greatest development in terms of size.

name: jurors
description: Celestial beings who serve in the superuniverse courts of Uversa, particularly Those without Name and Number who function as supreme jurors of Orvonton, chosen for their evolved spiritual mentality and experience in the ascendant career to render verdicts in cosmic adjudications.

name: just man
description: A person who has fulfilled all ritual obligations and spiritual duties, making their prayers highly esteemed by God. In ancient contexts, this referred to someone who had satisfied all accounts with the spirits and discharged every religious requirement.

name: Justa
description: The daughter of Bernice of Sidon who, along with her mother, hosted Jesus and the twenty-four evangelists in their home north of Sidon. Jesus taught the evangelists each morning at Justa's house during their ministry to the gentiles.

name: justice
description: A divine attribute and universal principle that ensures fair judgment and righteous administration. Justice is always a group function rather than a personal act, administered by tribunals and courts throughout the universe hierarchy to maintain cosmic order and fairness.

name: justice guides
description: Seraphim who prepare and present evidence concerning the eternal welfare of mortals and angels in universe tribunals. They ensure that unerring justice is administered with generous mercy, defending cases of doubtful survival and preparing statements for higher courts.

name: Justus (believer)
description: One of two men suggested to replace Judas Iscariot as an apostle, along with Matthias. He was considered by the apostles during their final meeting after Jesus' ascension.

name: Justus (Jewish merchant of Corinth)
description: A devout Jewish merchant in Corinth who lived near the synagogue and frequently hosted Jesus and Ganid. His home later became a place where the Apostle Paul stayed and heard stories of Jesus' earlier visits.

name: ka and ba
description: Egyptian concepts representing two aspects of human nature - the ka being a protective guardian spirit assigned at birth that guides the soul through life and into the afterlife, and the ba representing the soul itself.

name: Kaaba stone
description: A black stone fetish housed in a temple at Mecca that became a point of common reverence among Arabian tribes. This sacred stone served as a unifying factor that later contributed to the establishment of the Islamic religion.

name: Kanata
description: A location where Aden, despite being forbidden by the apostle John, successfully established a considerable company of believers before continuing his ministry into Mesopotamia.

name: kangaroos
description: Marsupial mammals whose ancestors roamed Australia 45 million years ago, evolving from small reptilian, egg-laying mammals. Their distinctive leaping form perpetuated the peculiar characteristics of their reptilian ancestors.

name: Kansas
description: A U.S. state that marked the southernmost extent of the central North American ice sheet during the first glacier's advance approximately 2 million years ago.

name: Kansu
description: A region in the upper valley of the Yellow River in ancient China where Chinese settlements received Andite migrants approximately 15,000 years ago.

name: Karahta
description: A place in northern Palestine that Jesus passed through on his final tour as a private individual, located east of the Waters of Merom on the route to Dan.

name: Karbar
description: The site of a battle where King Ahab of Ephraim and ten allied groups fought against Assyrian King Shalmaneser III, resulting in a draw that stopped the Assyrian advance but decimated the allies.

name: karma
description: The universal principle that every act receives its reward and people reap what they sow, reflecting the truth of repercussional synthesis of all time-space actions in the presence of the Supreme.

name: Karuska
description: A wealthy woman from Sidon who had been a patient at Bethsaida hospital and hosted Jesus and his associates at her home during their visit to the region.

name: Kashmir
description: A region where Buddhism was established by Emperor Asoka and became the dominant religion, contributing to Buddhism's spread across half the known world.

name: Kateri
description: A people of southern India who continue the ancient practice of stone worship, venerating stones as sacred objects.

name: Katro
description: The head of a family with whom Melchizedek lived for over thirty years, whose descendants preserved Melchizedek's higher truths down to the time of Moses.

name: Keilah
description: A Canaanite city that David defended against the Philistines, demonstrating his policy of protecting and allying with Canaanite peoples.

name: Kenan
description: Kenan, grandson of Seth, initiated foreign missionary service to surrounding tribes, continuing the spiritual work of his father and grandfather in improving the religious status of Adam's people.

name: Kenites
description: A nomadic Canaanite tribe that preserved the Salem religion of Melchizedek and maintained belief in El Elyon. The Kenites influenced Hebrew religious development through intermarriage with Abraham's descendants and their preservation of Melchizedek's teachings, which Moses later incorporated into Israelite worship.

name: Kentucky
description: The eruption of the great North American volcano in eastern Kentucky 330,000,000 years ago was one of the world's most significant geological events, covering 500 square miles with volcanic ash 15-20 feet deep.

name: Kerioth
description: A small town in southern Judea, the birthplace of Judas Iscariot, the twelfth apostle who was chosen by Nathaniel.

name: Keturah
description: A concubine of Abraham whose status in Hebrew records was deliberately altered during the Babylonian captivity, when she was incorrectly portrayed as Abraham's wife rather than his concubine.

name: keys of the kingdom
description: Jesus gave his apostles authority over temporal affairs of the kingdom, while the spiritual keys of the kingdom are sincerity and moral decisions to do God's will. All people possess these keys and can advance spiritually through choosing the highest possible values.

name: Kheresa
description: A fishing village on the shore of the Sea of Galilee, home to the twin apostles James and Judas Alpheus. Jesus and his apostles frequently visited Kheresa during their ministry, using it as a base for their travels in the region.

name: Kidron
description: A brook near Jerusalem that flows past the Mount of Olives and Gethsemane. Jesus and his apostles often crossed the Kidron valley when traveling between Jerusalem and their camp at Gethsemane.

name: kindred spirits
description: Spiritually minded persons of similar tastes and longings who are drawn together by direct spiritual attraction. The cosmic mind explains the kinship and fraternal cooperation between such kindred minds, both human and superhuman.

name: king-making episode
aliases: make a king of his Son in the flesh
description: The attempt by the multitude to proclaim Jesus king after the feeding of the five thousand, which led to widespread desertion when Jesus rejected earthly kingship. Only about five hundred of the original five thousand remained faithful followers after this episode.

name: king making war
aliases: king, when he prepares to make war
description: The parable of the king making war reminds us to carefully assess the cost before embarking on any endeavor. Used by Jesus to teach the necessity of counting the cost of discipleship before making commitments.

name: king of the Jews
description: A title applied to Jesus during his trial before Pilate, referring to accusations that he claimed temporal kingship. Jesus clarified to Pilate that his kingdom was spiritual, not of this world, and that he was indeed a king in the sense of ruling over the family of faith sons of his heavenly Father. The inscription "Jesus of Nazareth—the King of the Jews" was placed on his cross, causing outrage among Jewish authorities.

name: kingdom
description: A concept central to Jesus' teachings, referring to spiritual realities rather than earthly dominions. Jesus taught that a kingdom divided against itself cannot stand, using this principle to refute accusations that he cast out devils by Beelzebub's power.

name: kingdom of heaven
description: The central theme of Jesus' teaching, representing the spiritual brotherhood of God-knowing individuals and the realization of divine sonship. Jesus taught that the kingdom of heaven is not a temporal or political realm, but rather the reign of God's spirit in human hearts, achieved through faith, spiritual rebirth, and doing the Father's will.

name: kings
description: Temporal rulers who evolved from war chiefs and patriarchal authority, becoming hereditary positions believed to possess divine origins. Throughout history, kings were both revered as superhuman beings and constrained by various forces including mores, aristocracy, priests, and the constant fear of assassination. The concept of kingship gained universal prestige from knowledge of the Material Sons and Daughters who serve as actual planetary rulers.

name: Kirmeth of Bagdad
description: A trance prophet from Baghdad who arrived at the Bethsaida encampment with peculiar visions and fantastic dreams. Though he created considerable disturbance and his teachings were unsound according to the gospel of the kingdom, Jesus allowed him freedom to preach for several days before he departed with only a few followers.

name: Kish
description: An ancient Mesopotamian city-state where Sargon served as priest before proclaiming himself king and conquering Mesopotamia. Kish was also the location of a Salem school that made early progress in spreading Melchizedek's teachings until it failed after attempting social reforms against temple practices.

name: kiss
description: A form of greeting and betrayal with both social and spiritual significance. Early Christians greeted each other with a holy kiss as part of their fellowship, while Judas used a kiss to betray Jesus to his captors. The practice of social kissing originated from ancient adoption ceremonies involving saliva exchange.

name: knocking on wood
description: A superstitious practice representing modern man's continued disposition to bargain with Deity, despite no longer openly attempting to coerce spirits as primitive peoples did.

name: knotted cords
description: A form of superstitious belief involving the use of tied cords for magical purposes. Jesus exposed and denounced belief in knotted cords along with other forms of ignorant and enslaving superstition.

name: knowledge
description: Knowledge encompasses the accumulation of facts and information, which becomes valuable through experience and wisdom. While a little knowledge can be dangerous without proper understanding, the pursuit of knowledge through education and learning forms the foundation of civilization and culture. Knowledge must be shared and socialized with love to safeguard it with wisdom, as it represents the material or fact-discerning domain of mind that prepares individuals for spiritual truth and service to others.

name: Kopet Dagh
description: A mountain range near the southern shore of the Caspian Sea where Adamson's civilization was centered. This region served as the secondary headquarters of the violet race and was the cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations, with various Adamsonite cultures arising in the fertile foothills over different periods.

name: Koran
description: The sacred text of Islam, containing the religious teachings and revelations central to the Islamic faith.

name: Korea
description: A nation in eastern Asia where Buddhism was established by Asoka's missionaries and became the dominant religion. Buddhism later amalgamated with local religious customs and spread throughout the region along with China and Japan.

name: Kung Fu-tze
description: The Chinese name for Confucius, the ancient Chinese philosopher and teacher who founded Confucianism, one of the major philosophical and ethical systems that influenced Chinese culture and society.

name: Kyrios
description: A Semitic name for Deity meaning "Lord," used among various appellations by the Semites who preferred not to speak the actual name of their God. It was one of several titles including Adonai, The Lord of Hosts, and The Almighty.

name: labor
description: Physical and mental work that distinguishes humans from animals and represents one of humanity's paramount blessings. Labor should be approached with dignity and purpose, as even the most commonplace toil can be elevated to sacred service when performed with the right attitude. The division and specialization of labor has greatly advanced civilization, though society must ensure meaningful employment for all able-bodied citizens.

name: laborers
description: Workers who are invited by Jesus to find spiritual rest and strength in their service. The parable of laborers in the vineyard illustrates the generous nature of God's kingdom, while believers are encouraged to pray for more laborers to spread the gospel message, with the assurance that faithful workers are worthy of their hire.

name: ladder
description: A symbolic representation of how spirit personalities of the Divine and Infinite Spirit serve as a living pathway for mortal beings, providing guidance and ministry that helps humans ascend from chaos to glory through love and mercy.

name: Lagash
description: The capital city of ancient Sumeria, built on flood mounds, which fell to northern Suites and Guites around 2500 B.C. Its conquest marked the beginning of the end for Sumerian civilization, as the Sumerians became absorbed into the ranks of the northern Semites.

name: Lake Superior
description: A large lake in North America where ancient Proterozoic rocks are well-exposed and easily interpreted, containing primitive fossil-bearing layers that testify to early geological upheavals and surface fluctuations, with well-preserved examples of early marine life such as algae, coral-like plants, and protozoans.

name: Lake Urmia
description: A lake mentioned in The Urantia Book, referenced in connection with the region of Urmia.

name: Lake Van
description: A lake in the region north of Mesopotamia where followers of Van settled after the Lucifer rebellion. The lake became the center of Vanite civilization, with nearby Mount Ararat serving as their sacred mountain where they believed their moral law of seven commandments was given by the Gods. Descendants of Adamson clustered around its shores and later spread Salem teachings throughout Europe and Asia, while Nodites mingled with Amadonites in the vicinity.

name: lakes
description: Bodies of water found on various worlds including Urantia, Edentia, and Jerusem. On the architectural worlds, there are tens of thousands of sparkling lakes interconnected by streams but no great oceans, while on Urantia, glacial activity created many lakes through distinctive geological processes.

name: lambs
description: Young sheep that became typical sacrificial animals in early evolutionary religion and were used in Jewish Passover ceremonies. Jesus celebrated Passover without the paschal lamb, emphasizing spiritual truth over ritual sacrifice, and instructed Peter to "feed my lambs," meaning to minister to the weak, poor, and young.

name: lame
description: Those who are physically disabled or unable to walk properly. Jesus taught that when giving banquets, one should sometimes invite the lame along with the poor and blind, as this demonstrates humble service to others who cannot repay such kindness.

name: Lamech
description: A biblical figure whose exultation over his son Tubal-Cain's metal weapons became the scriptural basis for Jesus' teaching about forgiveness "seventy-seven" times, illustrating mercy and forbearance.

name: lamp
description: A source of light used metaphorically by Jesus to teach spiritual truths. He taught that the eye is the lamp of the body, and that one's spiritual light should not be hidden but placed on a stand where all can see it, emphasizing the importance of letting one's light shine with wisdom and discretion.

name: Lanaforge
description: The current System Sovereign of Satania, primary Lanonandek Son #2709, who succeeded the fallen Lucifer. He is the only primary Lanonandek Son in Nebadon who remained loyal when his superior fell into rebellion. Tested and proven as a capable administrator, he frequently visits Urantia and takes great interest in the isolated worlds of Satania, having given permission for various revelations and selected Adam and Eve for their mission on Urantia.

name: land
description: The foundation and stage upon which human civilization develops. The basic struggle of humanity has always been for land, as the land-man ratio underlies all social progress. Land serves as both the source of sustenance through agriculture and the basis for property rights, taxation, and economic systems, with its scarcity or abundance directly affecting population dynamics, warfare, and cultural development.

name: land bridges
description: Geological formations that temporarily connected continents and islands throughout Earth's history, allowing migration of animal species and early human populations. Major land bridges included the Bering Strait connection between Asia and North America, the Gibraltar isthmus linking Spain and Africa, and the Sicilian bridge connecting Europe to Africa. These formations rose and fell with sea level changes and geological activity, profoundly influencing the distribution of life forms and the spread of human races across the globe.

name: land-life era, early
description: The early phase of land-based life on Earth, also known as the Mesozoic era, extending over 100 million years and characterized by the evolution and dominance of early terrestrial organisms.

name: language
description: The system of communication that evolved from simple gestures and sounds to complex verbal and written forms, serving as humanity's greatest thinking tool. Language developed through group associations, with each local community creating its own word exchange system. It enables humans to communicate concepts, ideas, and spiritual meanings, distinguishing them from animals and facilitating the building of civilizations through the transmission of knowledge across generations.

name: Lanonandek Sons
description: The third order of Local Universe Sons created by the Creator Son and Universe Mother Spirit, best known as System Sovereigns who rule local systems and Planetary Princes who administer inhabited worlds. These versatile administrators are created with significant personal liberty in choosing and planning, making them excellent executives but also susceptible to rebellion. Nebadon contains exactly 12 million Lanonandek Sons divided into three classes: primary, secondary, and tertiary orders.

name: Lao-tse
description: A Chinese spiritual teacher who built upon Salem traditions by declaring Tao to be the One First Cause of all creation. He taught that humanity's eternal destiny was everlasting union with Tao, the Supreme God and Universal King, and made early presentations of returning good for evil. Though later deified by his followers, his original teachings emphasized faith in the Absolute God as the source of divine energy for spiritual transformation.

name: Laotta
description: A brilliant Nodite woman who served as head of the western schools in the Garden of Eden. After Eve's default, Adam deliberately chose to unite with Laotta rather than face a lonely existence on Urantia without Eve. Laotta died giving birth to her daughter Sansa, who was raised by Eve alongside Cain and later became the wife of Sargan, chief of the northern blue races.

name: Lapland
description: A region where the Lapps, along with Eskimos, represent a blend of Andonite and blue Sangik races. Their skeletal structures preserve the closest resemblance to the original Andonic type, and Lapland is one of the few places where older Andonites retained some semblance of their original identity after extensive racial mixing in northern Europe.

name: Larissa
description: A town in Mesopotamia through which Jesus, Ganid, and Gonod passed during their extensive journey, traveling by caravan from Damascus through Thapsacus and Larissa before reaching Babylon.

name: Lasea
description: A harbor town on the island of Crete where Jesus stopped during his journey from Jerusalem to Rome with Ganid and Gonod, sailing there from Alexandria before continuing to Carthage.

name: last
description: A spiritual principle taught by Jesus indicating that in God's kingdom, those who appear least significant may become the greatest in eternal salvation, while those who seem first may find themselves last. This reflects the Father's merciful consideration for all creatures according to their spiritual needs rather than worldly status.

name: Last Supper
description: The final meal Jesus shared with his apostles before his crucifixion, also known as the Remembrance Supper.

name: Latin
description: The Roman language that lacked a word for 'unselfishness,' making Christianity's message of spiritual comfort and liberating power particularly appealing to the spiritually hungry Roman people.

name: Lausanne
description: A province where Pontius Pilate retired in exile after being deposed as procurator of Judea, and where he subsequently committed suicide, never having recovered from his regretful condemnation of consenting to Jesus' crucifixion.

name: lava
description: Molten rock that flows from volcanic eruptions and hardens into solid rock formations.

name: law
description: A codified record of long human experience representing public opinion crystallized and legalized, evolving from negative prohibitions in primitive society to positive and directive principles in advancing civilizations. All law takes origin in the First Source and Center, with spiritual law administered by the Second Source and Center, while the revelation and interpretation of divine statutes is the function of the Third Source and Center.

name: law and the prophets
description: The traditional Jewish scriptures and teachings that Jesus came to fulfill, enlarge, and illuminate rather than to destroy or set aside.

name: law forecasters
description: Second-order supervisor seraphim who forecast how proposed constellation laws would affect the lives of freewill creatures, serving before celestial lawmakers to speak for those who cannot be present to advocate for themselves and ensuring the fair evolution of universe law while respecting moral free will.

name: lawyers
description: Legal experts among the Jews who, according to Jesus, had taken away the key of knowledge from common people and hindered others from entering the way of truth. One lawyer who asked Jesus about the greatest commandment received a response emphasizing love for God and neighbor as foundational principles.

name: Lazarus (of Bethany)
description: A wealthy resident of Bethany and one of Jesus' closest friends, along with his sisters Martha and Mary. After being raised from the dead by Jesus, he became treasurer of the church at Philadelphia, supported Abner in disputes with Paul, and eventually died at age 67 from the same sickness that had once afflicted him.

name: Lazarus (of Nazarite parable)
description: A character in a parable about a rich man and a beggar that John the Baptist used to warn against loving riches and coveting dishonest wealth, emphasizing the importance of heeding spiritual teachings.

name: laziness
description: A concept of idleness or lack of industrious effort, related to the broader theme of indolence in human behavior and character development.

name: lead
description: A heavy metal found in various geological deposits on Urantia, including pre-electronic forms from original planetary formation and forms produced through uranium disintegration. Lead appears in ancient fossil-bearing strata brought to the surface by early volcanic activity and in Silurian deposits alongside gas, oil, and zinc from sluggish water sedimentation.

name: leadership
description: The essential quality for guiding groups and civilizations, dependent on natural ability, discretion, determination, and seasoned wisdom. Effective leadership enables cooperation, maintains order, and provides direction for progress, with only about one percent of the population possessing the wisdom, insight, and foresight necessary for vital leadership roles.

name: leadings
description: The subtle spiritual guidance provided by Thought Adjusters to mortal minds, distinguished from emotional impulses or mystical experiences. True spiritual leadings require careful discernment, as many supposed divine communications actually originate from the human subconscious rather than superconscious contact with the indwelling spirit.

name: Leagues of Nations
description: International confederations that can prevent minor conflicts but cannot achieve lasting world peace. Such organizations will fail to control the most powerful governments, as true peace requires nations to surrender their sovereign powers to a unified mankind government.

name: Leah
description: The eldest daughter of the apostle Philip and his wife, who continued her parents' evangelical work after their martyrdom and later became the renowned prophetess of Hierapolis.

name: leaping
description: A method of learning through direct experience without forethought. While nonpersonal animals learn only by leaping into action, personalities can learn from both looking ahead and leaping, possessing insight that allows them to benefit from observation as well as experience.

name: least
description: A principle demonstrating how those in humble positions can be elevated to greatness. Urantia exemplifies this concept, being among the least significant worlds until Michael's bestowal elevated it to a position of honor and great universe interest.

name: leaven
description: A metaphor used by Jesus to describe both the transformative power of the kingdom of heaven and the corrupting influence of hypocrisy. The positive leaven represents how spiritual truth grows and spreads, transforming everything it touches, while the negative leaven of the Pharisees represents hypocrisy that should be avoided.

name: Lebanon, house of
description: A magnificent building constructed by King Solomon as part of his elaborate building program, which along with his lavish court and extensive projects, ultimately bankrupted the Hebrew nation despite his creation of a vast trading navy.

name: Lebbeus
description: Judas Alpheus, also called Lebbeus, was known among disciples as being among the least of all the apostles.

name: Lebonah
description: A town through which Jesus passed during his final journey through Palestine in the year 25, traveling northward from Beersheba to Dan.

name: leeches
description: Primitive animals closely related to earthworms that appeared in early evolutionary development along with starfish, sea urchins, and other invertebrates, representing nonprogressive specimens that failed to evolve further.

name: legalism
description: A system of strict adherence to religious laws and traditions that held the Jews in terrible bondage through ritualism and minute regulations of conduct, creating a more restrictive control than Roman political rule.

name: legislatures
description: Representative governing bodies that evolved from primitive councils of elders, including constellation assemblies in Nebadon that codify universe laws and the three-house system of advanced planetary governments. True legislative bodies mark significant social advancement from absolute monarchy toward representative government.

name: leisure
description: Free time essential for cultural and intellectual progress, allowing humans to think, plan, and imagine new ways of doing things. Civilization cannot flourish without leisure, though unearned leisure can become humanity's greatest affliction if not used constructively.

name: lemurs
description: Early mammalian ancestors of humans that evolved in North America and migrated to southwestern Asia via the Bering land bridge. These dawn mammals, not directly related to modern lemurs or apes, established the ancestral line leading to the human species.

name: lepers
description: Individuals afflicted with leprosy who were forbidden to attend synagogue or engage in public worship. Jesus healed several lepers during his ministry, including one at Iron and ten near Amathus, demonstrating compassion for the socially outcast.

name: Lesser Road
description: The Hinayana division of Buddhism that persisted in southern regions like Ceylon, Burma, and Indo-China, clinging to the early asocial doctrine of Siddhartha in contrast to the Mahayana or "Great Road" of northern Buddhism.

name: Levant
description: A region where Caucasoid and Negroid peoples have intermingled to create diverse human populations, and the homeland of Occidental faiths just as India was the birthplace of eastern religions.

name: Levites
description: A hereditary priestly class in ancient Judaism who officiated in religious ceremonies at the Jerusalem temple, performing sacrifices, chanting, and playing trumpets during festivals. The priests and Levites sent a delegation to John the Baptist to inquire about his authority and whether he claimed to be the Messiah.

name: liaison stabilizers
description: Morontia energy regulators that slow down energy revolutions to enable the physicalization of morontia material. They make possible the conversion of morontia energy into morontia material, and the entire morontia organization of existence depends on their stabilization work.

name: liberty
description: True liberty is defined as the progressive attainment of freedom that is related to reality and regardful of social equity, cosmic fairness, and divine obligations. It differs from false liberty, which is license without restraint, and requires the conquest of self and intelligent self-control to be genuine.

name: libraries
description: Collections of knowledge and manuscripts, including Alexandria's great library with nearly a million manuscripts from the civilized world, and the Dalamatia library known as the "house of Fad" which contained over two million records. The custodians of knowledge in Havona serve as divine living libraries, storing perfect records of truth in their minds.

name: Libya
description: A North African region that launched a military thrust against Egypt, creating an opportunity that Moses seized to lead the Hebrew exodus during a time when Egyptian forces were occupied fighting on multiple fronts.

name: license
description: False liberty that masquerades as freedom but represents unbridled self-will and unregulated self-expression, leading to unmitigated selfishness. License is the forerunner of abject bondage and stands in contrast to true liberty, which requires self-control and restraint.

name: Liege
description: The most northerly settlement of the Danubian peoples in Belgium, where these Andonite farmers and herders practiced pottery-making and agriculture before their tribes deteriorated as they moved away from their cultural center.

name: life
description: A phenomenon that represents the animation of pattern-configured energy systems, bestowed through Life Carriers but originating from the Universe Mother Spirit. Life is both mechanistic and vitalistic, involving material and spiritual elements, and serves as the foundation for mind development and spiritual growth in evolutionary creatures.

name: Life Carrier worlds
description: Seven primary spheres in the fourth Salvington circuit overseen by the Melchizedeks, dedicated to the study and development of life. World Number One serves as headquarters and houses the central emplacement of the adjutant mind-spirits, while the other worlds focus on various aspects of life conservation, evolution, and planning.

name: Life Carriers
description: Local universe Sons of God created by a Creator Son, Universe Mother Spirit, and one of the Ancients of Days, responsible for designing, carrying, and establishing life on evolutionary worlds. They are organized into three divisions: senior carriers, assistants, and custodians, and can function on physical, morontial, and semispiritual levels throughout their service to foster life development.

name: life-dawn era
description: The Proterozoic era, characterized by ancient fossil remains, marks the transition between the prelife cataclysmic age and highly developed marine life.

name: life-experiment planets
description: Planets designated for conducting special life experiments by the Life Carriers.

name: life flash
description: The translation flash marks the moment of ascension from the material domain to the morontia level, with celestial beings protecting observers from the energies released at translation.

name: life-modification planets
description: Planets where the Life Carriers modify existing life forms to create unique evolutionary patterns.

name: life plasm
description: The essential material substance containing the potential for all life manifestations, organized and catalyzed by Life Carriers into living matter through the vital spark bestowed by the Universe Mother Spirit.

name: life-story tellers
description: Celestial artisans who perpetuate the meaning and significance of life experience by projecting present personal experiences into future attainment values.

name: light
description: A fundamental energy manifestation existing in three forms: material light from physical sources, intellectual insight of the mind, and spirit luminosity characteristic of divine personality manifestation.

name: light and life
description: The settled status representing the evolutionary goal of inhabited worlds, characterized by spiritual harmony, advanced civilization, and the culmination of planetary development through seven progressive stages.

name: light picturizers
description: Celestial artisans who create real semispirit-phenomena preservations, far superior to crude motion pictures.

name: light-years
description: A unit of astronomical distance measurement used to describe vast cosmic distances, such as the 250,000 light-years from Satania to the center of Orvonton and the millions of light-years spanning outer space levels.

name: lighthouse
description: The great lighthouse of Pharos in Alexandria, one of the seven wonders of the ancient world, served as a magnificent and lifesaving device that inspired Ganid to become a beacon of light for others in darkness.

name: lighting
description: The lighting system on Jerusem projects gentle, even light similar to natural sunlight but with much less heat, emanating equally from all directions in the sky through power transformers and atmospheric energy projection.

name: lightning
description: Lightning served as both a natural phenomenon that early humans feared and worshiped as the voice of angry gods, and as a symbolic reference when Jesus declared he beheld Satan fall as lightning from heaven, marking the end of the Lucifer rebellion.

name: lignite
description: A type of inferior coal found in sedimentary deposits from 85 million years ago, particularly observed in the chalk and greensand marl formations along the eastern foothills of the Rocky Mountains.

name: lilies
description: Jesus used lilies as a teaching illustration about trusting in God's provision, noting how they grow without toil or spinning, and as a metaphor for how mortals can raise their spiritual nature from earthly origins like a white lily emerging from dark soil.

name: limestone
description: A sedimentary rock formed through chemical precipitation and marine deposition of lime-shelled life, creating extensive fossil-bearing layers throughout geological history that have been transformed by pressure into marble and other formations.

name: lions
description: Large predatory mammals that arrived in Europe from Africa via the Sicilian land bridge, virtually exterminating tigers and hippopotamuses, and were worshiped as special group gods by various Egyptian tribes.

name: lips
description: Jesus warned Peter about the need to learn to set a guard upon his lips before speaking thoughtlessly, indicating that controlling one's speech requires experience and spiritual maturity.

name: listening
description: Jesus demonstrated exceptional skill as a charming listener who made people feel comfortable and provided them opportunity to unburden their souls to a sympathetic and understanding ear.

name: literality
description: The error of interpreting Jesus' spiritual teachings in literal, concrete terms rather than discerning the spirit and deeper meaning behind his message, which the apostles frequently struggled with despite his guidance.

name: literature
description: The international exchange of literature fosters intellectual cross-fertilization, crucial for breaking down ignorance and suspicion to build brotherhood and understanding among races and nations.

name: lithographic stone
description: The lithographic stone of southern Germany was laid down during an ancient submergence when much of Europe was inundated, preserving delicate fossils such as insect wings with remarkable clarity.

name: littleness
description: Jesus does not look down upon littleness, only upon evil and sin; he welcomes simple souls with open arms, valuing faithfulness over talent or sophistication.

name: liver
description: Ancient peoples believed the soul resided in various parts of the body, including the head, heart, liver, blood, and fat.

name: Livias
description: A town in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final mission, and where the apostles secretly received and distributed swords before Jesus's betrayal.

name: living
description: Man's whole duty is summed up in loving God with all mind and soul and loving one's neighbor as oneself, rather than following the Pharisees' 613 rules of living.

name: living libraries of Paradise
description: Repositories of knowledge and wisdom maintained on Paradise for the benefit of celestial beings and ascending mortals.

name: living newspapers of Havona
description: The tertiary supernaphim Intelligence Co-ordinators of Havona serve as wise and sympathetic living newspapers, keeping all inhabitants informed of universe affairs through advanced communication methods.

name: lizards
description: Lizards are one of the four surviving divisions in the kingdom of reptiles, descended from nonprogressive reptiles along with snakes, alligators, and turtles.

name: llamas
description: Llamas originated in North America and spread to the western plains, where they coexisted with horses and giraffe-like camels before migrating to South America and becoming extinct in North America during the last glaciation.

name: loaves
description: Bread loaves, often referenced in the context of feeding multitudes and as symbols of sustenance in religious teachings.

name: lobsters
description: Lobsters are among the ancient, relatively unchanged marine life forms that have endured for millions of years, representing early crustacean life that has survived from ancient seas to the present.

name: local system centers
description: Sixth-order Supreme Power Centers permanently assigned to each local system who dispatch power circuits to inhabited worlds and coordinate with Master Physical Controllers to ensure satisfactory energy distribution throughout the system.

name: local system conclave
description: When a system achieves light and life, Planetary Sovereigns join a system conclave, a supreme governing body subject to the Constellation Fathers' veto, establishing a virtually self-governing administrative system with a legislative assembly and courts on each planet.

name: local systems
description: Administrative units consisting of approximately 1,000 inhabited evolutionary worlds, each presided over by a System Sovereign from an architectural headquarters world. Local systems are the basic units of supergovernment, with Satania being Urantia's local system containing 619 inhabited worlds.

name: local universe centers
description: Fourth-order Supreme Power Centers stationed on local universe headquarters who downstep superuniverse power circuits for distribution to constellations and systems, and provide intensified energy lanes for interplanetary communication between important inhabited points.

name: local universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon
description: The Universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon, numbered 611,121 of the sixth group of Supreme Spirits, who serves as co-director with Michael in universe administration and is known as the Divine Minister of Salvington, providing the Holy Spirit presence throughout Nebadon.

name: local universe Mother Spirits
aliases: Creative Spirits of the Local Universes
Mother Spirit of the local universe
Mother Spirit of a local universe
description: Creative Spirits of the sixth group of Supreme Spirits who serve as co-creators and administrators of local universes alongside Creator Sons, providing mind circuits and spiritual ministry while evolving through distinct stages from initial association to full personalization and sovereignty.
seeAlso: local universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon

name: local universe power centers
description: Fourth-order Supreme Power Centers assigned to local universe headquarters who modify and distribute superuniverse power circuits to constellations and systems within the local universe.

name: Local Universe Sons of God
description: Orders of divine Sons serving in local universe administration whose defection or default is never adjudicated locally but is immediately carried to superuniverse courts due to the implications for their status and authority.

name: local universes
description: Administrative divisions of the grand universe, each containing 100 constellations and approximately 10 million inhabited worlds, ruled by a Creator Son of the Michael order and blessed by a Union of Days representative. Each local universe serves as an autonomous unit within a superuniverse, with its own architectural headquarters world and distinct administrative structure governed by the combined authority of the Creator Son and local universe Mother Spirit.

name: location
description: A fundamental principle of physics stating that it is impossible to accurately determine both the exact location and velocity of a moving object simultaneously, as any attempt to measure one inevitably involves change in the other.

name: locusts
description: Insects that appeared early in evolutionary history alongside other arthropods, overspreading the continents during ancient geological periods. John the Baptist subsisted on edible locusts and wild honey during his life as a shepherd in the Jordan valley.

name: logic
description: The synthetic truth-seeking progression that unites faith and reason, founded on the innate recognition of things, meanings, and values. Logic serves as the technique of philosophy and provides a method for achieving consistency in thought, though it has limitations when applied to spiritual realities and life problems that transcend purely material considerations.

name: loneliness
description: A spiritual condition that religion effectively cures by enfranchising believers as sons of God and citizens of a meaningful universe. No God-knowing mortal need ever be lonely, for the Father walks beside each person through their cosmic journey, and ascending beings learn the importance of teamwork and fellowship in universal service.

name: long-heads
description: A physical characteristic describing the cranial shape of certain Urantia races, particularly the Adamites, Nodites, and some secondary Sangik peoples. This trait distinguished them from the broad-headed Andonites and medium-headed primary Sangik races.

name: long-suffering
description: The quality of patient endurance and forbearance, often associated with divine patience and the ability to persist through difficulties without losing faith or becoming discouraged.

name: Longs Peak
description: A mountain peak in the North American Rocky Mountain system that, along with Pikes Peak, exemplifies repeated cycles of elevation and erosion. These peaks held their heads above water during several prehistoric inundations, representing remnants of original mountain ranges that were re-elevated after being leveled by erosion.

name: Lord God of Israel
description: A Hebrew theological concept that unified three earlier religious traditions: El Shaddai, the Egyptian concept of the God of heaven, and Melchizedek's teachings about the creator Deity, forming the foundational doctrine of the creator God worshiped by the Israelites.

name: Lord’s Prayer
description: The prayer formulated by Jesus at age fifteen and later taught to his apostles, beginning with 'Our Father who is in heaven, hallowed be your name.' It evolved from Jesus's efforts to encourage individual prayer in his family and became the standard prayer adopted by both groups of apostles to teach new believers.

name: Lord’s Supper
description: A Christian sacramental meal commemorating Jesus' last supper with his disciples, also known as the Remembrance Supper.

name: losing
description: The state or experience of being defeated or failing to maintain possession or control of something.

name: lost
description: Those who have strayed from the path of spiritual truth and divine fellowship. The Father and Son actively search for the lost, employing all influences in diligent efforts to find those who stand in need of salvation. Jesus taught that God rejoices when the lost are found and return to the Father's house, as illustrated in his parables of the lost sheep, lost coin, and prodigal son.

name: Lot
description: Abraham's nephew who accompanied him to Salem after receiving Melchizedek's invitation. Lot chose to pursue trade and animal husbandry in Sodom rather than participate in Abraham's plan to conquer Canaan. Moses later gathered descendants of Abraham, Nahor, and Lot into a self-sustaining nation of pastoral warriors.

name: lotion
description: A liquid preparation applied to the skin for medicinal or cosmetic purposes, similar to ointment but in liquid form.

name: lots, casting
description: An ancient method of making decisions or determining outcomes by throwing or drawing marked objects, relying on chance or perceived divine guidance.

name: Louisville
description: A city in Kentucky where ancient coral deposits from the Devonian period are exposed along the Ohio River banks. These deposits are about one hundred feet thick and contain more than two hundred varieties of coral, indicating that inland seas of that era were clear and shallow.

name: love
description: The supreme relationship and highest motivation in the universe, representing divinity correlated in personality as truth, beauty, and goodness. Love is the experiential unification of divine law and the key to understanding personalities. God's love is outgoing and seeks to satisfy the needs of all creatures, while human love grows through understanding and unselfish service to others. True love prevents estrangements, strengthens character, and creates happiness, serving as the foundation for spiritual growth and the brotherhood of all beings.

name: Loyalatia
description: A great seraphim who reminded mortals that angels are fellow servants, not objects of worship, declaring that only God should be worshipped when a seer attempted to worship at the angel's feet.

name: loyalty
description: The fruit of intelligent appreciation of universe brotherhood and devotion to highest duty. True loyalty involves wholehearted and intelligent devotion in the face of temptation to default. It is essential for spiritual growth and character development, representing yielding intellectual allegiance to the highest dictates of spiritual consciousness. Loyalty must be exercised on behalf of great, good, true, and noble causes, and it serves as the foundation for ascending the personality scale toward divine perfection.

name: Lucifer
description: A brilliant primary Lanonandek Son who served as System Sovereign of Satania for over 500,000 years before initiating a devastating rebellion against Michael and the Universal Father. Once distinguished for wisdom and efficiency and numbered among the most able personalities of his order, Lucifer fell through pride and self-deception, eventually becoming the fallen and deposed sovereign who led 37 worlds into rebellion before being confined to prison worlds following Michael's bestowal on Urantia.

name: Lucifer rebellion
description: A massive system-wide insurrection led by Lucifer that began approximately 200,000 years ago and affected 37 worlds in the Satania system, including Urantia. The rebellion centered on Lucifer's "Declaration of Liberty" challenging the authority of Michael and the Paradise administration, promoting self-assertion and rejecting universe loyalty. This "war in heaven" was fought in terms of eternal life rather than physical warfare, resulting in the spiritual quarantine of affected worlds and profound consequences for evolutionary progress that continue to this day.

name: luck
description: A primitive concept arising from early humans' interpretation of chance events as either favorable or unfavorable supernatural influences. Evolving from simple notions of good and bad fortune controlled by spirits, luck became central to early religious beliefs and magical practices. Modern civilization has largely replaced luck-based thinking with scientific understanding and actuarial calculations, though superstitious beliefs persist in various forms among supposedly civilized peoples.

name: Luke
description: A physician from Antioch and Gentile convert of Paul who wrote the Gospel of Luke and Acts of the Apostles. Luke began following Paul in A.D. 47 and completed his Gospel in A.D. 82 in Achaia, drawing from Paul's teachings, eyewitness interviews, and other sources including Mark's Gospel. A strong believer in social equality, Luke interpreted Jesus' teachings through this lens, emphasizing themes of grace and Jesus as "the friend of publicans and sinners" until his death in A.D. 90.

name: Luminous Persons
description: A classification of beings associated with Deity, though specific details about their nature and function are not provided in the available text.

name: lunatics
description: Individuals affected by mental disorders, referenced in the context of conditions that Jesus encountered during his earthly ministry.

name: lungs
description: Respiratory organs that evolved from the swim bladders of arthropods, enabling the first amphibians to breathe air and successfully inhabit both aquatic and terrestrial environments.

name: lust
description: An intense sexual desire that, according to Jesus' teaching, constitutes adultery even when confined to thoughts and intentions rather than physical acts.

name: Lut
description: A member of Caligastia's staff of one hundred who led the council of health and life, focusing on sanitation and primitive hygiene promotion in pre-rebellion Dalamatia. Lut and his entire health council were lost to the Lucifer rebellion, joining the forces opposing the planetary administration.

name: Lutentia
description: A rebellious Lanonandek System Sovereign of system Palonia who led a widespread rebellion against Michael's authority in Nebadon. After reigning for over twenty years following his rebellion, Lutentia was deposed and replaced by Michael himself in the guise of the "Savior Sovereign." Despite being offered mercy and forgiveness, Lutentia spurned these overtures while acknowledging Michael's just and merciful administration.

name: Luxor
description: An ancient Egyptian city where temple walls depict the birth of Amenhotep III, showing the ka (guardian spirit) that Egyptians believed accompanied favored individuals throughout life.

name: luxuries
description: Material comforts and self-gratifications that can lead to spiritual and social problems when pursued excessively, including wealth, property, and intoxicants that distract from moral growth and create societal imbalances.

name: Luz
description: A town where Jesus and the apostles paused during their journey to the Phoenician coast in June 29 AD, and where Jesus delivered one of his most remarkable addresses on true religion.

name: Lydda
description: A town through which Jesus passed multiple times, including his return from Egypt as a child, his journey to Jerusalem at age twenty for Passover, and later when accompanying his brother Simon to Jerusalem.

name: lying
description: The deliberate distortion of truth, forbidden by divine commandments and moral law, which requires wisdom and discretion in truthful communication while avoiding falsehood that transgresses spiritual principles.

name: lynch law
description: Extrajudicial punishment by groups when formal justice systems fail, historically practiced by secret societies and arising from group resentment, representing the unwillingness to surrender private redress to proper authorities.

name: Lysimachia
