description: A city in Thrace where the apostle Matthew was killed by unbelieving Jews who conspired with Roman soldiers during his missionary journey preaching the gospel.

name: Macad
description: One of the cities visited during the Perean mission of the seventy messengers between January 3, 30 AD and Jesus' final journey to Jerusalem.

name: Maccabee, Judas
description: A Jewish military leader who led the Maccabean revolt against Seleucid rule, establishing Jewish independence and the Hasmonean dynasty.

name: Maccabees
description: A Jewish family that led a successful revolt against Seleucid rule, establishing the Asmonean dynasty and influencing Jewish religious and political life, with Mary's ancestors being prominently identified with their activities.

name: Macedonia
description: A region in the eastern Mediterranean where early Christian missionaries, including the apostle Andrew, proclaimed the gospel and established followers of Jesus before Paul's missionary journeys.

name: Machaerus, fortress of
description: A fortress prison in Perea where Herod Antipas maintained residence and where John the Baptist was imprisoned and executed by beheading on January 10, A.D. 28.

name: machines
description: Mechanical devices and tools that represent humanity's liberation from unremitting toil and are essential for the progress of civilization, though they lack consciousness, values, or spiritual qualities that distinguish humans from mere mechanisms.

name: Madagascar
description: An island off the coast of Africa where certain tribes in the twentieth century still practiced primitive population controls, destroying children born on unlucky days, resulting in about twenty-five percent infant mortality.

name: madman at Fair Havens
description: A drunken degenerate who attacked a slave girl at Fair Havens, whom Jesus restrained while rescuing the girl, demonstrating compassion and leaving a lasting impression on Ganid about helping the oppressed.

name: madness
description: A state of mental disorder or extreme irrational behavior, referenced in various contexts throughout The Urantia Book.

name: Madon
description: A town in northern Palestine visited by Jesus during his final tour as a private individual and later during his first preaching tour of Galilee with the apostles, where they met with general rejection of their message.

name: Magadan Park
description: A beautiful park south of Bethsaida-Julias near the Sea of Galilee that served as a frequent gathering place for Jesus and his followers, including conferences, training sessions, and the ordination of the seventy evangelists.

name: Magdala
description: A city near the Sea of Galilee where Jesus had a fisherman uncle and where he spent time during his youth, later visited during his travels, and where his brother Jude worked as a fisherman.

name: Magellanic Cloud
description: A distorted stellar aggregation in the Milky Way galaxy that appears as a gigantic luminous mass of blazing suns, formed as a result of internal catastrophes and external gravitational attraction.

name: magic
description: Primitive man's technique of attempting to manipulate the conjectured spirit environment through supernatural means, including fetishes, rituals, and formulas to coerce spirit aid or secure insight into the future. Unlike prayer, magic sought to manipulate reality without affecting the practitioner's ego, representing humanity's early attempt to control environmental forces before the development of science.

name: Magisterial Sons
description: Paradise Sons of God known as Avonals who serve as planetary ministers and judges throughout the universes. These divine beings, personalized by the Eternal Son and Infinite Spirit, conduct magisterial missions to adjudicate dispensations, preside over the awakening of sleeping survivors, and sometimes bestow themselves as mortals on evolutionary worlds to establish new epochs of planetary progress.

name: magnetism
description: A fundamental force interconnected with light, heat, electricity, and energy that traces back to the same cosmic source. Earth's magnetic poles help regulate terrestrial energy flow, while solar magnetic fields in sunspots can hurl charged particles toward Earth, creating auroral displays and affecting compass directions.

name: magnolias
description: Flowering trees that emerged around 90 million years ago during the early Cretaceous period along with fig trees and tulip trees. These angiosperms quickly spread across the continents, even growing in regions like Greenland, but gradually migrated southward as the climate cooled, largely disappearing from northern latitudes.

name: maimed
description: Jesus taught his followers to invite the poor, the maimed, and the blind to banquets, explaining that such acts of loving ministry would bring blessings to their hearts, especially since these recipients could not repay such kindness.

name: Maine
description: A region that experienced volcanic activity during the Silurian age with lava flows and fire rocks. During the ice age, enormous icebergs would slide off Maine's coast into the Atlantic Ocean, while the climate away from glacial areas remained relatively mild.

name: Majeston
description: The 50th Reflective Spirit and Paradise center of universal reflectivity, created by the Supreme Being in functional liaison with the Deity Absolute. As the only existing personality of divinity created directly by the Supreme Being, Majeston serves as the personal and infallible coordinator of all reflectivity phenomena throughout the seven superuniverses.

name: major sectors
description: Administrative divisions of superuniverses, each comprising one hundred minor sectors, ten thousand local universes, and approximately one hundred billion inhabitable worlds. Governed by three Perfections of Days, major sectors focus on intellectual development and administrative matters rather than spiritual judgment, with their headquarters worlds featuring educational satellites for ascending mortals.

name: Malach
description: A Syrian believer from Beirut who had previously been at Bethsaida and welcomed Jesus during his coastal visit to the city.

name: Malachi
description: A Hebrew prophet whose prophecy about Elijah's return significantly influenced John the Baptist's ministry. Malachi was one of the last spiritual leaders to preserve the concept of the Universal Father during a period of increasing priestly bondage and tyranny in Jewish religious life.

name: maladjustment
description: A state of poor adaptation or failure to adjust properly to circumstances or environment.

name: malaria
description: A fever-causing disease, as suffered by Amatha, Peter's mother-in-law, who was later healed by Jesus after sundown at the Zebedee home.

name: Malaysia
description: A region representing a mixture of Mongoloid and secondary Sangik racial influences, whose inhabitants include the Malay tribe that believed in their descent from the lemur.

name: Malchus
description: The Syrian bodyguard of the high priest who attempted to bind Jesus' hands during his arrest, prompting Peter to draw his sword in defense.

name: Malta
description: A Mediterranean island where Jesus encouraged the suicidal young man Claudus to embrace life, and where the apostle Thomas was later put to death by Roman authorities.

name: Maltese cross
description: The shape resembled by a vertical cross section of total space, with horizontal arms representing pervaded space and vertical arms representing unpervaded space, separated by quiescent midspace zones.

name: Malvorian
description: The first Graduate Guide who greeted the first mortal pilgrim to reach Havona, now serving as chief of the Graduate Guides and director of their educational organization that conducts pilgrims to Paradise.

name: mammals
description: A class of animals that evolved from small reptilian dinosaurs, with placental mammals suddenly appearing and developing rapidly into diverse forms including marine types like whales and air navigators like bats.

name: mammon
description: Material wealth or worldly possessions that compete with spiritual devotion, as Jesus taught that no one can serve both God and mammon simultaneously.

name: mammoths
description: Large extinct mammals that preferred open prairies and ranged from Mexico to Canada, with the Siberian variety developing woolly coats, eventually declining due to glacial climate changes.

name: Mamre
description: The plains where three celestial beings appeared to Abraham after he surrendered his personal ambitions in favor of Melchizedek's larger plans.

name: man
description: A mortal human being, distinguished from other orders of intelligent life in the universe.

name: man of sorrows
description: A portrayal of Jesus that his apostles were instructed to avoid, as they should instead emphasize his radiance of joy and the transforming power of his gospel message.

name: man who attacked wife at Tarentum
description: A man in Tarentum who publicly mistreated his wife, prompting Jesus to intervene with kindness and counsel about proper treatment of women as equal partners in marriage.

name: Manasseh
description: A boy king of Judah under whose rule a corrupt ring of politicians brought about the kingdom's undoing by favoring Baal worship over Yahweh.

name: mandates
description: Authoritative commands or directives, often referring to divine or official instructions.

name: mandrakes
description: Plants believed by some to have magical properties, which Jesus denounced along with other forms of ignorant superstition.

name: manger
description: The animal feeding trough area in the caravan stables below the Bethlehem inn where Jesus was born, providing comfortable quarters despite the overcrowded conditions.

name: Mangus
description: A Roman centurion and commanding officer of the Capernaum garrison who built a synagogue for the Jews and sought Jesus's help to heal his dying servant.

name: manitou superstitions
description: Prereligious beliefs and practices of North American Indians involving spiritual forces, similar to the mana practices of other primitive peoples.

name: manna
description: The bread of earth, not heaven, referenced by Moses as feeding the Israelites in the wilderness. Jesus contrasted this earthly sustenance with himself as the true bread of life that comes down from God. The term also relates to prereligious practices of mana observed among Melanesians and other primitive tribes, representing early evolutionary beliefs in natural wonders and mysteries.

name: Manotia
description: A supreme seraphim who has achieved all seven seraphic services and now serves as associate chief of seraphim on Urantia. Manotia displayed unparalleled courage and loyalty during the Lucifer rebellion, saving almost two-thirds of the administrator angels from falling into the rebel ranks, making her one of the seraphic heroes of that tumultuous period.

name: Mansant
description: A great teacher of the post-Planetary Prince age on Urantia who pointed his followers to the veneration of "The Great Light." He was one of 24 counselors and led cultural and spiritual revivals during the dark ages between the Caligastia rebellion and Adam's arrival.

name: mansion world students
description: All surviving mortals who reawaken on the mansion worlds and are classified as evolving spirits beginning their morontia career. They progress individually through the seven mansion spheres, receiving new morontia bodies with each advancement while retaining their personalities and worth-while memories from their previous existence.

name: Mansion World Teachers
description: A corps of glorified cherubim and sanobim who serve as instructors throughout the morontia worlds. These advanced beings are commissioned by the Melchizedeks and maintain their headquarters on the third mansion world, working in pairs to provide sympathetic instruction to ascending mortals throughout their mansion world experience.

name: mansion worlds
description: Seven worlds of progressive morontia training that serve as the initial postmortal residence for ascending mortals. These subsatellites of Jerusem's first transition world provide educational and rehabilitative experiences, allowing survivors to continue their spiritual development and correct deficiencies from their material existence before proceeding to higher universe spheres.

name: mansions
description: The many dwelling places or stopping-places in the Father's universe that Jesus referenced, including the mansion worlds and other transitional spheres. These represent the various abodes and training stations where ascending mortals progress on their journey toward Paradise and spiritual perfection.

name: mansonia worlds
description: An alternative term for the mansion worlds, referring to the seven spheres of initial morontia training for ascending mortals in the local system of Satania.

name: Mansurotia
description: The first assistant System Sovereign of Satania, number 17,841 of the tertiary Lanonandeks, who was dispatched to Satania along with Lanaforge to serve in the system administration.

name: manufacturing
description: Industrial and material production activities, including the sophisticated manufacturing sector on Jerusem that features perfected mechanical techniques and physical achievements without the pollution associated with material world industry.

name: maples
description: Maples first appeared during a period of major plant evolution when angiosperms dominated. Alongside trees like beech and oak, maples emerged as part of a rapid global spread of flowering plants. These seed-bearing trees were highly significant in evolution, second only to humans in importance within their respective domains.

name: marble
description: Marble formed from limestone that was modified through pressure and folding 300 million years ago. The Carrara marble, originating from such modified limestone, can be found in Europe, particularly Germany and Russia, as well as in Africa and Australia, but is absent in the southern regions of South America due to continuous marine deposits.

name: Marcus
description: Marcus was a Roman orator who heard Peter preach and became his successor in Rome. When Simon Peter was crucified, Marcus boldly defied Roman persecution and continued preaching the new gospel, urging people to know God as their spiritual Father.

name: Marduk
description: Marduk was one of the chief Mesopotamian deities who was exalted to supremacy in the Babylonian triad alongside Bel and Anu. In Babylonian belief, Marduk perpetuated the Adam legend as a son-of-God figure, serving as a connecting link between humanity and divinity.

name: Mardus
description: Mardus was the acknowledged leader of the Cynics of Rome who became a great friend of the scribe of Damascus. He engaged in daily conversations with Jesus and listened to his teachings, including profound discussions about good and evil.

name: marine life
description: Marine life evolved the ability to adjust to changes in ocean salinity by regulating their body fluids. The Paleozoic era was dominated by marine life for 250 million years, with creatures like trilobites, ammonites, and various mollusks flourishing before land organisms emerged.

name: marital guilt test
description: A marital guilt test described in the Old Testament involved a suspected unfaithful wife drinking a concoction of holy water and temple floor sweepings. If she showed no symptoms of physical illness after consuming the mixture, she was considered innocent of adultery.

name: Mark, Elijah
description: Elijah Mark, likely referring to the same person as John Mark, was associated with the early Christian community and the Gospel accounts.

name: Mark, Gospel of
description: The Gospel of Mark, one of the four canonical Gospels in the New Testament, traditionally attributed to John Mark who was associated with the apostle Peter.

name: Mark, John
description: John Mark was an early Christian figure associated with the apostles and traditionally credited as the author of the Gospel of Mark.

name: Mark, Mary
description: Mark, Mary appears to be an entity with no context paragraphs available, making it impossible to create a meaningful description.

name: mark of the beast
description: A symbolic term referring to the remnants of unfortunate heredity, unwholesome environment, and unspiritual tendencies that are purged during progression through the mansion worlds, with the final traces being eradicated on the seventh mansion world before residence on Jerusem.

name: market places
description: Early trading centers that served as sacred sites secured by fetishes to ensure honest commerce, eventually becoming the first places of sanctuary and cities of refuge. Jesus referenced them when criticizing the Pharisees who craved flattering recognition in public spaces rather than seeking justice and truth.

name: marriage
description: Society's institutional mechanism designed to regulate human relations arising from bisexuality, functioning both to control personal sex relations and manage descent, inheritance, and social order. Marriage has evolved from primarily property-based arrangements to increasingly romantic and spiritual partnerships, serving as the foundation for family life and the rearing of children.

name: marriage feast
description: A wedding celebration that Jesus used in parables to illustrate spiritual truths about the kingdom of heaven, where all who are willing are welcomed. Jesus also taught proper etiquette at such feasts, advising guests to take humble positions rather than presuming places of honor.

name: Mars (god)
description: The Roman god of war whose worship evolved from earlier family religious forms, making the later religion of the Latins more of a political observance than the intellectual or spiritual systems of other peoples.

name: Mars (planet)
description: A planet in the solar system with an atmosphere so thin that any intelligent beings living there would be classified as subbreathers, in contrast to the mid-breather classification of Urantia mortals.

name: martens
description: Small carnivorous mammals that, along with weasels, otters, and raccoons, thrived and developed throughout the northern latitudes during the period 15 million years ago when modern cat and dog families were also expanding worldwide.

name: Martha (sister of Andrew and Peter)
description: The elder sister of apostles Andrew and Peter, who was selected by Jesus as one of the ten women commissioned for the ministering work of the kingdom. She played a key role in the women's ministry at Magdala, helping to convert Mary Magdalene to the gospel.

name: Martha (sister of Jesus)
description: Jesus' second sister, born on September 13, A.D. 3, who was slow in thought and action but dependable and efficient. She became an expert weaver, helping to ease the family's financial pressures, and Jesus maintained fairly good relations with her throughout his youth.

name: Martha (sister of Jesus’ mother)
description: Martha, another sister of Jesus' mother, was among the women who secreted themselves near Jesus' tomb on Friday evening, waiting for the opportunity to properly prepare his body for burial with spices and ointments.

name: Martha (sister of Lazarus)
description: Martha, sister of Lazarus and Mary, first met Jesus in Bethany when their families formed a lifelong friendship. She was wealthy, respected, and inherited extensive vineyards and olive orchards. Martha was known for being distracted by household duties while her sister Mary chose to sit at Jesus' feet and listen to his teachings. She displayed unwavering faith when Jesus came to raise Lazarus, expressing belief in his divine power. After disposing of her lands at Bethany, she joined Lazarus in Philadelphia where he became treasurer of the church and supported Abner in his controversy with Paul.

name: Martha (wife of Justus)
description: Martha, wife of Justus in Corinth, welcomed Jesus and two courtesans into her home at a late hour, providing food and counsel to help the women start new lives. She rose to the occasion as Jesus had hoped, showing kindness and guidance to these strangers in need.

name: martyrs
description: Those who sacrifice their lives for their loyalty to the gospel of the kingdom, demonstrating the highest form of human courage and devotion by laying down their lives for the love of spiritual truth. Many disciples and apostles became martyrs, including Stephen, James Zebedee, and others who were put to death for their faith.

name: Mary Magdalene
description: Mary Magdalene was won for the kingdom at Magdala and became the most effective teacher among the twelve women evangelists commissioned by Jesus. She served as chief spokesman for the women's corps and was present at Jesus' crucifixion when the apostles fled. Mary discovered the empty tomb, was the first to see the risen Jesus, and witnessed four of his five morontia appearances, becoming a key witness to his resurrection.

name: Mary Mark
description: Mary Mark was the wife of Elijah Mark and mother of John Mark who welcomed Jesus and the eleven apostles into their Jerusalem home on Thursday night before they departed for the Gethsemane camp.

name: Mary (mother of Alpheus twins)
aliases: Mary the mother of the Alpheus twins
description: Mary, mother of the Alpheus twins, was among the women who went to anoint Jesus' body at the tomb on Sunday morning and was present when Jesus appeared to the women believers at Joseph of Arimathea's house, where he commissioned them to spread the gospel of sonship with God.

name: Mary (mother of Jesus)
description: Mary, the earthly mother of Jesus, was chosen by Gabriel to become the mother of the bestowal child. She married Joseph in March 8 B.C. and gave birth to Jesus in Bethlehem. Mary had a cheerful, sunny disposition and was an expert weaver and skilled homemaker. Though she initially believed Jesus was destined to become the Jewish Messiah, she struggled to understand his true mission. After Jesus left Nazareth, Mary moved to Capernaum where she lived for the rest of her life, dying less than a year after Jesus' crucifixion.

name: Mary (sister of Lazarus)
description: Mary, sister of Lazarus and Martha, first met Jesus in Bethany where their families formed a lifelong friendship. She was wealthy, inheriting extensive vineyards and olive orchards, and was deeply devoted to Jesus, loving him like a brother. Mary chose to sit at Jesus' feet and listen to his teachings rather than be distracted by household duties. She anointed Jesus with expensive spikenard ointment, believing his words that he must die, which provoked Judas Iscariot's resentment. After disposing of her Bethany lands, she joined her siblings in Philadelphia.

name: Mary (wife of Clopas)
aliases: Mary the wife of Clopas and sister of Jesus’ mother
description: Mary, wife of Clopas and sister of Jesus' mother, was among the devoted women who stood near the cross during Jesus' crucifixion and later waited by his tomb, planning to return with spices and ointments to properly prepare his body for burial.

name: Mary (wife of Ezra)
description: The wife of Ezra who, along with her husband, was deeply touched by Jesus' noble words of family devotion and loyalty to flesh and blood, remarking that Jesus was too noble for their family.

name: masks
description: Physical coverings worn by members of ancient secret societies during mysterious initiations and rituals to frighten away the curious from their mourning rites and ancestor worship. Jesus taught that revenge is the mask of cowardice, not the true nature of man as the son of God.

name: massage
description: A primitive healing method developed as part of ancient medical practices involving rubbing the body to supposedly expel evil spirits, preceding the introduction of actual medicines and often used in conjunction with incantations.

name: Master
description: The title by which Jesus' disciples and followers called him, reflecting his natural authority and leadership. Jesus was known as Master among Zebedee's employees in Capernaum and accepted this title from his followers without demanding it, commanding confidence through his gentle yet powerful personality.

name: Master Architects
description: High-level universe administrators associated with the overall architectural planning and development of the master universe.

name: Master Force Organizers
description: Transcendental beings who function throughout the master universe to manipulate primordial space-forces and organize nebulae. They comprise two orders: Primary Eventuated Master Force Organizers who transmute space potency into primary energy, and Associate Transcendental Master Force Organizers who continue the energy transmutation process, working under the supervision of the Architects of the Master Universe.

name: master frandalanks
description: Living instruments that indicate the power pressure and energy charge of entire superuniverses, whose readings are interpreted by high commissioners of power in the administration of universal energy systems.

name: Master Mathematician
description: The divine intelligence who orchestrates the vast mathematical order of the universe, representing the source and controller of all mathematical relationships and formulas that govern material creation.

name: Master Michaels
description: Creator Sons who have completed their seven creature bestowals and achieved supreme local universe sovereignty. After their final bestowal, they become Master Sons with unlimited authority derived from experienced association with the Paradise Trinity, ruling their universes with complete understanding of both Creator and creature perspectives.

name: Master Physical Controllers
description: Mobile energy-manipulating beings who serve as subordinates to Supreme Power Centers throughout the grand universe. They possess capabilities for individuality metamorphosis and can traverse space at high velocities, functioning to regulate, control, and direct the basic energies of space in local systems and on individual worlds.

name: master seraphim of planetary supervision
description: Master seraphim of planetary supervision are specialized completion seraphim from Seraphington who accompany newly commissioned Planetary Princes to inhabited worlds. They are organized into twelve specialized groups and serve under the immediate direction of the resident governor general, mobilizing the reserve corps of destiny to ensure planetary progress and guard against disaster.

name: Master Sons
description: An order of divine Sons in the celestial hierarchy.

name: Master Spirit Number One
description: Master Spirit Number One represents the Paradise Father in a special manner, being a peculiar and efficient manifestation of the Universal Father's power, love, and wisdom. He is the close associate and supernal adviser of the chief of Mystery Monitors and presides over the first superuniverse, always speaking for the Universal Father in all associations of the Seven Master Spirits.

name: Master Spirit Number Two
description: Master Spirit Number Two adequately portrays the matchless nature and charming character of the Eternal Son. He directs the destinies of superuniverse number two and is always in close association with all orders of the Sons of God, speaking for and in behalf of the Eternal Son in all assemblies of the Seven Master Spirits.

name: Master Spirit Number Three
description: Master Spirit Number Three especially resembles the Infinite Spirit and directs the movements and work of many high personalities of the Infinite Spirit. He is in charge of superuniverse number three and is closely associated with all personalities who take exclusive origin in the Third Source and Center, speaking for the Infinite Spirit when the Seven Master Spirits are in council.

name: Master Spirit Number Four
description: Master Spirit Number Four partakes of the combined natures of the Father and Son, serving as the determining influence regarding Father-Son policies and procedures in the councils of the Seven Master Spirits. He is the chief director and adviser of ascendant beings who have attained the Infinite Spirit and fosters the fourth superuniverse in accordance with his peculiar association of the Universal Father and Eternal Son's attributes.

name: Master Spirit Number Five
description: Master Spirit Number Five exquisitely blends the character of the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit, serving as adviser to the power directors, power centers, and physical controllers. He directs the welfare of the fifth superuniverse in a way that suggests the combined action of the Universal Father and Infinite Spirit, and speaks for the Father-Spirit attitude in the councils of the Seven Master Spirits.

name: Master Spirit Number Six
description: Master Spirit Number Six portrays the combined character of the Eternal Son and the Infinite Spirit. He serves as adviser to creatures jointly created by the Son and Spirit and directs the affairs of the sixth superuniverse much as would the Eternal Son and Infinite Spirit, responding when it becomes necessary to speak conjointly for these two Paradise Deities in the councils of the Seven Master Spirits.

name: Master Spirit Number Seven
description: Master Spirit Number Seven uniquely embodies the Universal Father, Eternal Son, and Infinite Spirit, serving as the presiding head of the Seven Master Spirits and functioning personally for the Supreme Being. He is the fostering adviser of all triune-origin beings and ascending pilgrims of Havona, representing the unity of Supremacy and revealing a personal and organic relationship with the evolving Supreme Being.

name: Master Spirits, Seven
description: The Seven Master Spirits are the personalized representation of the Infinite Spirit to the evolutionary universes, maintaining their personal stations on the periphery of Paradise while overseeing the seven superuniverses. They are the full representation of the Third Source and Center in relationships of energy, mind, and spirit, collectively encompassing all possible Deity functions and serving as the mind-spirit balance wheel of the universe of universes.

name: master universe
description: The master universe consists of six concentric elliptical space levels extending outward from Paradise, encompassing the grand universe (the central universe and seven superuniverses) along with the four uninhabited outer space levels. Though appearing almost infinite to finite minds, it has definite physical limits and dimensions, with all forms of energy obeying Paradise gravity as they swing around the eternal space paths in organized circuits.

name: masters
description: The principle that no person can serve two masters simultaneously, as either they will hate one and love the other, or hold to one while despising the other. This spiritual truth particularly applies to the impossibility of serving both God and material concerns like wealth, representing the fundamental choice between spiritual and material allegiances.

name: masters of philosophy
description: Primary supernaphim on Paradise who teach the philosophy of perfection to those personally present. These intellectual teachers work in synchrony with the secoraphic souls of philosophy and share divine wisdom that can only be truly understood through direct experience, serving as living mirrors reflecting the philosophy of Paradise perfection.

name: mastodons
description: Large prehistoric mammals that migrated extensively during the ice age, preferring sheltered forest regions unlike the prairie-dwelling mammoths. They persisted in North America until exterminated by early human inhabitants and represented, along with elephants, an echo of the massiveness that characterized the earlier age of reptiles.

name: Matadormus
description: A wealthy young Pharisee and member of the Jerusalem Sanhedrin who desired to become one of Jesus' seventy messengers but was unable to give up his riches as required. Though he failed to join the seventy at that time, he later became treasurer of the Jerusalem church after Jesus' death, having eventually followed the Master's injunction to part with his wealth.

name: matchmakers
description: Professional intermediaries who facilitated marriages during the transition from parental arrangement to individual choice in mate selection. Originally barbers and later priests served this role, and in ancient times they were even employed to arrange marriages for deceased individuals, as unmarried persons were believed unable to enter the spirit world.

name: Material Daughters
description: Female Material Sons who serve alongside their male counterparts as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds. Like Material Sons, they are semimaterial beings created in local universes, standing eight to ten feet tall with violet-hued radiant bodies, designed to work in pairs as physical and spiritual bridges between divinity and humanity.

name: material energy
description: All phases and forms of phenomenal motion, action, and potential that respond to the material-gravity circuit centered in nether Paradise. This encompasses energy-matter in all its metamorphic states, which obediently follows the eternal space paths of the universes under Paradise gravity control, requiring the consent of the Infinite Spirit for any new transformations or creations.

name: Material Sons
description: Dual-natured beings created by Creator Sons in local universes to serve as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds. Standing eight to ten feet tall with violet-glowing bodies, they work in male-female pairs (Adams and Eves) and serve as bridges between spirit beings and material creatures, capable of reproduction and designed to improve the physical and spiritual status of planetary races through their superior life plasm.

name: materialism
description: A philosophy that reduces human existence to merely an evolutionary accident, viewing the universe as a purposeless energy phenomenon devoid of spiritual meaning or survival beyond death. This mechanistic worldview is described as the maximation of ugliness and the finite antithesis of beauty, cheapening human life by denying spiritual realities and eternal values.

name: mathematics
description: The mathematical language and principles that science teaches to humanity, training thoughts along lines of precision while stabilizing philosophy and purifying religion. Mathematics is reliable when limited to physical things but not infallible when applied to life problems, as it cannot predict the creation of complex phenomena like water molecules or account for the superadditive nature of personality associations.

name: mathematics teacher of Damascus
description: A teacher from Damascus who met Jesus and taught him new mathematical techniques. After learning from this teacher, Jesus spent several years developing a keen sense of numbers, distances, and proportions.

name: mating instinct
description: The natural biological drive for reproduction that influences behavior in living creatures.

name: matriarchy
description: A social system in which women hold primary power and authority, particularly in family structures and governance.

name: matter
description: Organized energy subject to linear gravity, consisting of one hundred distinguishable atomic materializations in Nebadon, each with electrons revolving around a central nucleus. Matter represents the time-space shadow of Paradise energy and serves as the physical foundation for material manifestations, though spirit beings perceive it as less real than spiritual realities.

name: matter, mind, and spirit
description: The three distinct realms of cosmic reality that maintain intimate interrelations throughout the universe. These domains of physical, mental, and spiritual gravity represent different levels of existence, with matter as the physical foundation, mind as the connecting bridge, and spirit as the highest reality.

name: Matthew Levi
description: The seventh apostle, a former customs collector from Capernaum who served as the apostolic fiscal agent and treasurer. Matthew was chosen by Andrew, possessed moderate wealth which he secretly contributed to the apostolic cause, and later died as a martyr at Lysimachia while preaching the gospel. His extensive notes on Jesus' sayings formed the basis for the Gospel attributed to him.

name: Matthias
description: The apostle chosen by lot to replace Judas Iscariot in the apostolic corps. Selected from two candidates and inducted into office as the new treasurer, Matthias was declared the twelfth apostle but had little part in the subsequent activities of the apostles.

name: maturity
description: The developmental state where past, present, and future unite to reveal true meaning in the present moment. Maturity involves cooperative adjustment to change, the substitution of higher meanings for personal pleasure, and the willingness to pursue superior longings and idealistic spiritual realities rather than transient desires.

name: maximums
description: The original manifestations of finite reality that mark the beginning of universe history. Primary maximums represent constitutively perfect reality of the Havona type, secondary maximums represent evolutionally perfected reality of the superuniverse type, and tertiary maximums are coordinate manifestations resulting from relationships between the primary and secondary types.

name: May Day
description: Ancient festival day when sex regulations were traditionally suspended, seen as a form of imitative magic that appealed to the sexual energies of the plant world and contributed to early concepts of vice, crime, and sin.

name: Maypole
description: A ceremonial pole that perpetuates ancient customs of tree worship, along with the Christmas tree and the practice of rapping on wood.

name: Maza
description: A location near Jerusalem where pilgrims gathered willow branches to adorn the sacrificial altar during the early morning ceremony of the Feast of Tabernacles.

name: meals
description: Regular occasions of eating food, representing important aspects of daily sustenance and social interaction.

name: meanings
description: The appreciative consciousness of values that experience adds to perception of reality, derived from recognition and understanding rather than purely sensory experience. New meanings emerge through conflict and spiritual insight, requiring mental correlation to achieve true unity in human experience.

name: means versus ends
description: The philosophical consideration of methods and processes in relation to ultimate goals and purposes.

name: measures
description: Systems of measurement used across celestial spheres, including Jerusem's mile equivalent to seven Urantia miles and the gradant weight system. Accurate simultaneous measurement of location and velocity presents inherent paradoxes in both physical and biological analysis.

name: Mecca
description: Arabian city containing the Kaaba, a black stone fetish that served as a point of common reverence among Arabian tribes and subsequently led to the establishment of the Islamic religion.

name: mechanical controllers
description: The most powerful of all Master Physical Controllers assigned to inhabited worlds, possessing living antigravity endowment that exceeds all other beings. Ten are stationed on Urantia to facilitate seraphic transport departures and manipulate twenty-one of thirty physical energies of space.

name: mechanism (theory)
description: A mechanistic philosophy that attempts to explain existence purely through material processes, rejecting intelligent purpose in creation. This theory fails to account for consciousness, moral behavior, and the emergence of concepts like truth, beauty, and goodness from purely physical and chemical processes.

name: mechanisms
description: Complex cosmic systems that operate throughout the universe, appearing automatic but created and guided by divine mind. These mechanisms are crystallizations of Creator thought that function according to divine will, enabling finite creatures to coexist with the Infinite while maintaining perfect harmony with the eternal plan. They represent the physical and spiritual laws that govern reality, though their creative intelligence may be hidden from lower orders of mind.

name: Medeba
description: A town in Perea visited during the mission of the seventy messengers between January 3, A.D. 30 and Jesus' death, as part of the final ministry before his journey to Jerusalem.

name: Media
description: An ancient region through which Jesus traveled during his caravan journey to the Caspian Sea in A.D. 24, along with Assyria and Parthia.

name: mediators
description: Beings or concepts that serve as intermediaries between different levels of reality or consciousness, facilitating communication and understanding between various orders of existence.

name: medicine
description: The practice of healing and treating disease, which evolved from early magical and superstitious remedies involving shamanic practices to rational scientific methods first developed by the Greeks. Ancient medicine included plant remedies, human secretions, and various treatments, while modern medicine emphasizes scientific understanding over supernatural explanations.

name: medicine bag
description: A sacred pouch containing ghost-impregnated articles that symbolized a medicine man's power and was never allowed to touch the ground, similar to how modern civilized peoples treat their national flags as emblems of consciousness.

name: medicine men
description: Shamanic healers in primitive societies who practiced early forms of medicine, often combining spiritual beliefs with physical treatments and maintaining sacred medicine bags as symbols of their power.

name: mediocrity
description: The state of being ordinary or average, which poses dangers to both individuals and societies. Jesus warned against overconservative mediocrity as a vice, while political systems must guard against its dominance to prevent national downfall. Dynamic religious living transforms mediocre individuals into powerful personalities, and progress requires the development of individuality rather than standardized mediocrity.

name: meditation
description: A spiritual practice involving reflective communion with God and the indwelling spirit, which Jesus taught as essential for spiritual growth. True meditation involves silent receptivity after prayer, allowing the spirit to speak to the listening soul and transforming the worshiper to resemble the divine. It differs from mystical experiences and is most effective when combined with loving service to others, providing strength, wisdom, and energy for solving life's moral and spiritual challenges.

name: Mediterranean-Indian Ocean land bridge
description: A land connection that once existed between the Mediterranean region and the Indian Ocean, formed when the Mediterranean retreated westward. This elevated land bridge served as a migration route for various human races, particularly the orange, green, and indigo races gravitating toward Africa.

name: Mediterranean region
description: The Mediterranean region was a highly blended area where races from the north, south, and east met and mingled. Andites moved to Mediterranean islands where they thrived and advanced long after the decline of their fountainhead in Mesopotamia, but the region's civilization was disrupted by floods around 15,000 B.C. when the Gibraltar isthmus and Sicilian land bridge collapsed.

name: Mediterranean Sea
description: The Mediterranean Sea began forming 500 million years ago when Africa moved south, creating an east-west trough. A cataclysmic flood occurred when the Gibraltar isthmus collapsed, connecting the Mediterranean basin with the Atlantic Ocean and causing the greatest loss of life by flood in world history.

name: medium-heads
description: Medium-heads refers to a skull type characteristic of the Sangik races, with the yellow and blue men tending toward broad-headedness, distinguishing them from the long-headed Adamites and Nodites and the broad-headed Andonites.

name: mediumship
description: A spiritual practice associated with attempting to communicate with spirits or supernatural entities through an intermediary or medium.

name: meekness
description: Meekness is an attitude of cooperation with God that embraces patience and forbearance, motivated by unshakable faith in a lawful and friendly universe. It is of spiritual origin rather than fear-based, and represents mastery over all temptations to rebel against divine leading.

name: Meganta
description: Meganta was the location of a school in Alexandria where the philosopher Rodan taught his philosophy after returning from his encounters with Jesus and the apostles.

name: Megiddo
description: Megiddo was known as the international battlefield of Palestine, a historic site where Jesus reflected on the military victories and defeats of his people, including Egyptian conquests and the defeat of King Josiah.

name: Mek
description: Mek was the leader of the planetary council on art and science among Caligastia's staff, who improved early man's industrial techniques and elevated concepts of beauty, but remained loyal during the Lucifer rebellion.

name: Melanesians
description: Melanesians were a Pacific island people who practiced prereligious prayer and mana rituals, representing an early evolutionary stage of spiritual development similar to African Pygmies and North American Indians.

name: melanin
description: Melanin is the original Andonic skin pigment, a coloring substance found in all human skin that gave the early Andonites their swarthy complexion resembling present-day Eskimos.

name: Melchizedek, Father
description: The original Melchizedek who collaborated with the Creator Son and Creative Spirit to create the entire order of Melchizedek Sons in Nebadon.

name: Machiventa Melchizedek
description: A Melchizedek Son who incarnated as an emergency bestowal on Urantia in 1973 B.C., appearing at Salem to preserve the truth of one God and prepare the way for Michael's later bestowal. Known as the Prince of Salem, he taught Abraham and established a covenant based on faith rather than sacrifice, departing after 94 years when his followers began to regard him as supernatural. He now serves as vicegerent Planetary Prince of Urantia.

name: Melchizedek, Malavatia
description: A Melchizedek Son who sponsored Paper 43 of The Urantia Book.

name: Melchizedek, Manovandet
description: A Melchizedek Son who was once attached to Urantia's receivership and presented Paper 53 of The Urantia Book.

name: Melchizedek, Mantutia
description: A Melchizedek Son who directed the creation of Parts II and III of The Urantia Book as part of a commission of twelve Nebadon personalities and was assigned by Gabriel to supervise the restatement of Michael's life on Urantia in Paper 120.

name: Melchizedek brotherhood
description: The religious organization established by Machiventa Melchizedek at Salem, whose members committed to believing in one God and following seven commandments. At its height, the brotherhood had over 100,000 tithe payers enrolled on clay-tablet rolls, but nearly disappeared after Melchizedek's sudden departure.

name: Melchizedek College of Administration
description: An educational institution on Jerusem fostered by the Melchizedeks where representatives and electors must graduate before serving in system government, including the council of one thousand electors who select delegates to the constellation legislature.

name: Melchizedek College of Spiritual Endowment
description: A school within the Melchizedek University where all orders of Sons of God collaborate with Melchizedek and seraphic teachers to train evangels who proclaim spiritual liberty and divine sonship throughout the universe.

name: Melchizedek receivers
description: Twelve Melchizedek Sons who administered Urantia following the Caligastia rebellion, serving as planetary custodians and preserving civilization through Van's leadership until the arrival of Adam and Eve. They returned after the Adamic default and continued governing until Jesus became Planetary Prince.

name: Melchizedek Sons
description: An order of local universe Sons created by the Creator Son, Creative Spirit, and Father Melchizedek, serving as emergency ministers, educators, and administrators throughout Nebadon. They function as mobile advisory courts, maintain self-government, and are known for their wisdom and versatility in handling unusual circumstances and planetary crises.

name: Melchizedek sphere
description: The Melchizedek sphere is the pilot world of the Salvington circuit, serving as the headquarters world for the Melchizedek order and the site of Michael's first bestowal. This sphere serves as a model of self-government for all worlds in Nebadon and is considered the most interesting place in the local universe for ascending mortals.

name: Melchizedek University
description: Melchizedek University consists of seventy primary spheres and 420 tributary worlds in the Salvington circuit, totaling 490 worlds where ascending mortals from all constellations of Nebadon receive training to acquire residential status on Salvington. The university is staffed by over one hundred thousand Trinity Teacher Sons and includes specialized schools for universe administration, spiritual wisdom, and various fields of study.

name: Melchizedek worlds
description: The Melchizedek worlds comprise 490 spheres in the Salvington circuit, consisting of seventy primary worlds each encircled by six tributary spheres devoted to specialized activities. These architectural spheres serve as training grounds where ascending mortals from all constellations of Nebadon acquire the education necessary for residential status on Salvington.

name: Melkarth temple
description: The Melkarth temple in Tyre was an ancient temple where Jesus taught believers about the Father's love for all mankind during his visit in July. The temple later became the site of a Christian church built on the same location.

name: melodies
description: Musical tunes or sequences of sounds that form harmonious compositions, often associated with worship and spiritual expression.

name: memorials
description: Structures and monuments that commemorate significant events, persons, or achievements. On Jerusem, 619 planetary memorials represent the inhabited worlds of Satania, while the Michael Memorial serves as the central hub of activities related to Michael's bestowal on Urantia.

name: Memories of Mercy
description: Tertiary seconaphim who serve as living records of mercy extended to individuals and races throughout the cosmic realms. They maintain trial balances of each being's spiritual account, revealing moral debts and mercy credits established by the Sons of God, and teach ascending creatures how to truly exhibit mercy as a quality of spiritual growth.

name: memory
description: The retention and recall of past experiences, essential for personality continuity and self-consciousness. Ascending mortals retain memories of spiritually significant experiences through their Thought Adjusters, while memories of purely material events perish with the physical brain. Memory provides the foundation for humor, growth, and the recognition of former associates throughout the eternal career.

name: menstruation
description: The monthly physiological cycle in women that was heavily ritualized and feared in ancient times. Women were subjected to complete social quarantine during menstruation, which was considered defiling and associated with evil spirits, though this practice inadvertently provided women with periods of rest and reflection.

name: mental illness
description: Disorders or disturbances of the mind that affect thinking, behavior, and emotional well-being, distinguished from spiritual or moral conditions.

name: merchant of Damascus
description: A wealthy Damascus merchant who owned over four thousand caravan camels and had business interests throughout the Roman world. He developed a great liking for Jesus and Simon during their stay in Jerusalem and later invited Jesus to Damascus, offering him a position in his Oriental import business and proposing to establish a school of religious philosophy with Jesus as its head.

name: Mercury
description: The innermost planet of the solar system, whose orbit is comparable in size to the inner electron orbit of an atom. Mercury's rotation ceased due to tidal friction from the sun, leaving one hemisphere permanently facing the sun.

name: mercy
description: Divine compassion that is justice tempered by wisdom, applied love in action, and the natural offspring of goodness. Mercy ministry is the work of individuals, while justice punishment is the function of groups, and true mercy comes as the climax of justice, fairness, patience, and kindness.

name: mermaids
description: Mythical half-human, half-animal creatures that were once objects of worship by early humans who revered animals for their power and cunning.

name: Mesopotamia
description: The region between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers that served as the cradle of civilization for almost thirty-five thousand years. It was the location of the second garden of Eden, Caligastia's headquarters, and the homeland of the Andites before they were driven out by climatic changes and barbarian invasions around 2000 B.C.

name: mesotrons
description: Particles of matter 180 times heavier than electrons that hold atomic nuclei together by causing protons and neutrons to constantly exchange places. This reciprocating function prevents the electric charge of protons from disrupting the nucleus and explains the excess radiation in radioactive atoms.

name: Mesozoic
description: The early land-life era extending from 150 to 50 million years ago, also known as the premammalian era that marked a significant period of land-life evolution and brought to a close with the Cretaceous age.

name: message stick
description: An early form of written communication that evolved into symbolic alphabets, representing one of the progressive stages in the development of writing from primitive smoke signals to modern telegraph, telephone, and wireless communication.

name: messages
description: Communications transmitted between individuals or groups, serving as a fundamental aspect of human interaction and information exchange.

name: messenger corps (David Zebedee’s)
description: David Zebedee's organized communication service of twenty-six to fifty volunteer messengers who maintained contact between Jesus and believers throughout Palestine and adjacent regions. They served as heralds of Jesus' resurrection, collectors of funds for the apostles, and provided vital intelligence services during Jesus' ministry, operating from headquarters at Bethsaida.

name: messenger corps (Lanonandek)
description: The Messenger Corps is a group within the Lanonandek Sons consisting of 400,000 members.

name: Messenger Hosts of Space
description: The Messenger Hosts of Space are a group classified under the Personalities of the Infinite Spirit in the Uversa personality register.

name: messengers (tertiary supernaphim)
description: The Messengers are a group of tertiary supernaphim originating on circuit number four who serve as bearers of personal messages throughout the Paradise-Havona system, ministering to celestial personalities, Paradise pilgrims, and ascending mortals of time.

name: Messiah
description: The Messiah is the Jewish concept of an anointed deliverer, expected to establish the kingdom of God on earth. Jewish teachings varied about this figure, evolving from "servant of the Lord" to "son of David," "Son of Man," and even "Son of God," but all agreed he would be the promised deliverer who would restore Jewish national glory and rule over all nations. Jesus was recognized by some as the Messiah, though his spiritual mission differed greatly from traditional Jewish expectations of a temporal, political ruler.

name: Messina
description: Messina is a port city where Jesus stopped for one day during his Mediterranean journey, changing the life of a young fruit vendor by feeding him with the bread of life and inspiring him to feed souls as well as bodies.

name: metalworking
description: Metalworking is the craft of shaping and working with metals, practiced by various ancient civilizations including the Andites, Adamites, and Sumerians. Advanced metalworking techniques spread from centers like Syria and the Euphrates valley, with skilled artisans working iron, gold, copper, and bronze. Jesus himself acquired considerable skill at the anvil while apprenticing with a smith in Sepphoris.

name: metaphysics
description: Metaphysics is humanity's attempt to bridge the gap between science and religion through philosophical reasoning, but it ultimately fails to harmonize the physical and spiritual realms. It represents man's well-meant but futile effort to compensate for the absence of morontia insight (mota), often resulting in confusion rather than enlightenment. Only revelation can successfully unite the First Cause of science with the God of salvation in religion.

name: meteors
description: Meteors are space bodies that enter planetary atmospheres, often burning up due to friction and creating shooting stars. They played a significant role in Urantia's early development, with heavy meteoric bombardment during the volcanic age keeping atmospheric oxygen depleted. Primitive humans viewed meteors with awe, often interpreting them as signs of visiting spirits, while millions of meteorites continue to enter Urantia's atmosphere daily.

name: Methuselah
description: Methuselah is a biblical figure whose legendary longevity resulted from confusion between months and years as time periods in ancient records, with traditions of extremely long human lives arising from such chronological misunderstandings.

name: Mexico
description: Mexico is a region in North America where ancient civilizations were founded by an amalgamated race predominantly of red ancestry but mixed with yellow, orange, and blue racial elements. The area experienced significant geological changes throughout its history, including submergence beneath seas and volcanic activity, and was later the site of Mother of God cult practices involving sacramental cakes and wine.

name: Micah
description: A Hebrew prophet who followed Isaiah and confirmed his soul-satisfying gospel. Along with Obadiah, Micah boldly denounced the priest-ridden ritual of the Hebrews and fearlessly attacked the whole sacrificial system.

name: Micaiah
description: A little-known prophet who provided invaluable assistance to Elisha in keeping the light of truth alive in Palestine after Elijah was called away.

name: mice
description: Small rodents that reproduce much more rapidly than elephants, yet elephants evolve more rapidly than mice, demonstrating that rapid reproduction does not guarantee evolutionary advancement.

name: Michael, order of
description: The designation under which all Creator Michaels reign, named after the original or first-born Michael. This order encompasses all Creator Sons who go forth from Paradise to found their universes and eventually achieve Master Michael status through their bestowal experiences.

name: Michael memorial (on 1st mansion world)
description: A magnificent material-morontia structure located in the vast court of the resurrection halls of the first mansion world of Satania. Created shortly after Michael departed from Urantia and sealed by Gabriel, it bears the inscription commemorating the mortal transit of Jesus of Nazareth on Urantia.

name: Michael memorial (on Jerusem)
description: A temple at the center of the circular reservations of the Sons of God on Jerusem, completed five hundred years after Michael's bestowal on Urantia. It serves as the center of all activities in the modified management of the system since Michael's bestowal and is staffed by over one million personalities.

name: Michael of Nebadon
description: The Creator Son and sovereign ruler of the local universe of Nebadon, also known as Christ Michael. He is the 611,121st bestowal of the Eternal Son and began organizing Nebadon about four hundred billion years ago. After completing seven creature bestowals, including his final incarnation as Jesus of Nazareth on Urantia, he achieved supreme sovereignty over his universe.

name: Michael Sons
description: Creator Sons who are personalized as Michaels and go forth from Paradise to found their universes. They are known as Creator Michaels when founding universes and Master Michaels when settled in supreme authority after completing their bestowal careers.

name: Michael (the original)
description: The original or first-born Michael who has never experienced material incarnation but passed through seven spiritual creature ascent experiences on the seven circuits of Havona. He serves as the presiding head of the primary Paradise Sons when they assemble for conference at the center of all things and made the transcendent bestowal of the Original Mother Son real to the creatures of Havona.

name: Michal
description: Daughter of King Saul who became David's wife. Saul required David to pay one hundred Philistine foreskins as her dowry, an example of the brutal mutilations practiced in early human history.

name: Michigan
description: A region in North America where ancient fossil-bearing stone layers come to the surface, part of an intermittent east-west ridge extending from Pennsylvania through the Adirondack Mountains, Michigan, Wisconsin, and Minnesota.

name: mid-breathers
description: Atmospheric mortals who, like Urantians, represent the average breathing order and make up over 91% of atmospheric mortals in Satania, breathing atmospheres of moderate density.

name: mid-mammals
description: An evolutionary species that appeared during the glacial period, almost twice the size of their dawn mammal ancestors with proportionately increased brain power, representing a crucial step in the evolution toward primitive man and Primates.

name: mid-mind
description: A conceptual level of mind function intermediate between material and spiritual consciousness.

name: Middle Ages
description: A historical period of European civilization characterized by relative cultural and intellectual stagnation.

name: Midianites
description: An ancient tribal people who were victims of Hebrew warfare and who historically invaded regions including the Jordan valley during the time of Gideon.

name: midsonite world
description: A special sphere in each local system where Melchizedeks function as life carriers, mating with Material Daughters to produce midsonite offspring who eventually gather on the Salvington finaliter worlds.

name: midsoniters
description: Unique beings who are the offspring of Melchizedek life carriers and Material Daughters, existing in a state between mortality and immortality, never experiencing death, and serving mysterious purposes in universe evolution.

name: midspace zones
description: Vast elliptical regions of quiescent space activities that separate the successive space levels of the master universe, including zones averaging 400,000 light-years wide that separate the seven superuniverses from the first outer space level.

name: midway messengers
description: A small corps of named midway creatures who provide quick and reliable personal communication services on evolutionary worlds.

name: midwayers
description: Unique beings existing on a life-functioning level midway between mortals and angels, found on most inhabited worlds. They are classified into primary and secondary orders, with primary midwayers originating from the corporeal staff of Planetary Princes and secondary midwayers descended from the Adams and Eves. Midwayers serve as permanent planetary citizens, intelligence corps, and liaison beings between material and spiritual realms.

name: midwayers, chief of Urantia
description: The Chief of the Urantia Midwayers authored Paper 91 on prayer and worship. This high-ranking midway creature serves as leader among the united midwayers of Urantia.

name: midwayers, primary
description: The first order of midway creatures, numbering 50,000 on each planet, derived from the modified corporeal staff of Planetary Princes. Primary midwayers are more spiritual in nature, resembling angels more than mortals, and are energized by the angelic technique. They serve as intelligence corps and planetary historians, with their destiny being enrollment in various Paradise finaliter corps.

name: midwayers, secondary
description: The second order of midway creatures derived from the Adams and Eves or their immediate progeny. Secondary midwayers are more material in nature, resembling mortals more than angels, and are energized by the Adamic technique. They work closely with material beings and cooperate with the Adamic regime in advancing planetary civilization, with their destiny being enrollment in the Mortal Corps of Finality.

name: midwifery
description: The profession of assisting mothers during childbirth, which originated from ancient practices where old women were permitted to attend expectant mothers during labor. The shamanesses became particularly adept at midwifery as part of their healing practices.

name: might
description: Physical or political power that, while not making right in a moral sense, enforces commonly recognized rights and shapes historical outcomes. Might ensures the protection of individual and group rights through the definition and enforcement of law, though it must be distinguished from moral righteousness.

name: Mighty Messengers
description: Trinitized Sons of Attainment who are rebellion-tested, perfected ascendant mortals embraced by the Paradise Trinity. They serve as understanding ministers and messengers in superuniverse administration, maintaining constant connection with headquarters through reflectivity service. Mighty Messengers are fully conscious of their entire ascendant careers and serve as defenders in superuniverse courts.

name: Milcha
description: A cousin of Apostle Thomas who was among the ten women chosen by Jesus to minister for the kingdom. She was selected and commissioned to teach the gospel and minister to the sick as part of Jesus' revolutionary decision to include women in active ministry.

name: mile
description: A unit of measurement, with one Jerusem mile equaling approximately seven Urantia miles. The concept is also used metaphorically by Jesus to describe levels of service, contrasting the first mile of compulsory duty with the transformative second mile of free service and loving devotion.

name: militarism
description: An autocratic and cruel system that promotes social organization among conquerors but disintegrates the vanquished. Militarism conflicts with industrialism in the struggle for social advancement and directly affects women's social position, as their status varies inversely with the degree of militarism in any society.

name: military
description: Military organization originally developed to defend accumulated group wealth and property, but later evolved to include offensive operations against neighboring tribes. The survival of advanced societies depends on maintaining adequate military preparedness for defense while avoiding the temptation to use this strength for aggressive conquest. Military service in continental nations integrates technical education with voluntary peacetime service to prevent the formation of a professional military class.

name: milk
description: The use of cow's and goat's milk revolutionized child-rearing by enabling earlier weaning of babies, which reduced infant mortality and allowed mothers to bear more children. In Jesus' family, Miriam sold milk to help support the household during financially difficult times. Ancient peoples considered the milk of a black cow to be highly magical.

name: Milky Way galaxy
description: The Milky Way galaxy represents the central nucleus of the superuniverse of Orvonton and is composed of vast numbers of former spiral and other nebulae, many distorted into gigantic luminous masses of blazing suns. The physical center of Orvonton lies in the dense diameter of the Milky Way, far removed from the Satania system where Earth is located.

name: miller in Corinth
description: A miller in Corinth whom Jesus taught about grinding up the grains of truth in the mill of living experience to make divine life readily receivable by even the weak and feeble among fellow mortals.

name: millstone
description: Jesus taught that causing a child to stumble spiritually would be so serious that it would be better for the offender to have a millstone hung around their neck and be cast into the sea.

name: mind
description: Mind is the functional endowment of the Infinite Spirit, serving as the indispensable channel of communication between spiritual and material realities. It is the technique whereby spirit realities become experiential to creature personalities and acts as the universal unifier of divergent energies. Mind is always creative and dominates matter while being responsive to the overcontrol of spirit.

name: mind-energy manipulators
description: Mind-energy manipulators are celestial artisans who serve as experts in intercommunication between morontia beings and other types of intelligent entities. They promote unique adventures in intellect liaison and are keen students of the mind circuits of the Infinite Spirit.

name: mind personalities
description: Mind personalities are unrevealed orders of beings dominated by mind but always spirit-associated, as no entity has personality unless endowed with it by God who is spirit. Majeston and his associates are examples of such mind-dominated beings.

name: mind planners
description: Mind planners are seraphim devoted to organizing teamwork and groupings of morontia beings on the mansion worlds, serving as psychologists of the first heaven. Most have previous experience as guardian angels and help surviving mortals adjust to morontia existence through fair and just guidance.

name: mindal and physical energy personalities
description: Mindal- and physical-energy personalities are unrevealed creatures that are nonresponsive to spirit gravity but are nonetheless true personalities existing within the Father's circuit.

name: minerals
description: Inorganic substances that form the basic constituents of rocks and geological formations.

name: Minerva
description: Roman goddess to whom the Capitolium temple in Rome was dedicated, along with Jupiter and Juno, which Jesus regularly visited and contemplated during his time in the city.

name: mines
description: Underground excavation sites for extracting materials, mentioned as places of compulsory labor on a nearby inhabited planet and as a location Jesus worked at in Iron.

name: Ming dynasty
description: Chinese imperial dynasty whose establishment marked a culmination of political evolution and self-determination in the East, along with the Mogul dynasties.

name: ministering reserves
description: The seventh order of transition seraphim stationed on the first mansion world who serve as volunteers and kindle the divine fire of service in ascending mortals through personal friendship.

name: ministering-spirit quartettes
description: Four-member spiritual advisory groups consisting of a seraphic chief, superuniverse counselor, archangel of translations, and omniaphim representative who assist Teacher Sons and mortal executives in planetary administration.

name: ministering spirits
description: Angels who serve as spiritual associates and helpers to evolutionary and ascending will creatures throughout space, possessing highly individualized personalities and social natures.

name: ministers
description: Religious leaders who serve in spiritual and pastoral roles within various faith communities.

name: ministry
description: The act of providing spiritual service, care, and guidance to others in religious or humanitarian contexts.

name: Minnesota
description: A North American region where ancient fossil-bearing stone layers from primitive geological periods are exposed at the surface.

name: minor sectors
description: Administrative divisions of superuniverses, each consisting of 100 local universes and approximately one billion inhabitable worlds. Minor sectors are governed by three Recents of Days and serve as headquarters for Master Physical Controllers. Ascending mortals train on seven educational spheres surrounding each minor sector headquarters, focusing on physical and administrative knowledge before advancing to major sectors.

name: minorities
description: Small groups or factions within larger populations that may oppose social cooperation or progress. The Urantia Book warns against domination by vicious minorities and notes that secondary midwayers help subdue insubordinate minorities. Well-organized superior minorities have historically ruled much of the world through effective use of material resources.

name: mint
description: An aromatic plant used as an example by Jesus when criticizing the Pharisees and scribes for their misplaced religious priorities. He condemned their meticulous tithing of mint, anise, and cumin while neglecting the weightier matters of faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: Miocene
description: A geologic age during which land elevation and sea segregation gradually cooled the world's climate. During this period, subtropical plants migrated southward as warm-climate vegetation disappeared from northern latitudes.

name: miracles
description: Supernatural or extraordinary events that transcend natural law. Jesus performed miracles during his earthly ministry but consistently refused to use them merely to attract followers or prove his divinity. True religion, according to The Urantia Book, is not enhanced by appeals to the miraculous and should be rooted in personal spiritual experience rather than wonder-working.

name: Miriam (Old Testament)
description: A biblical figure referenced during Sabbath services led by the women followers of Jesus. She was cited as an example of women's important role in Israel's religious life, alongside other notable women like Deborah and Esther.

name: Miriam (sister of Jesus)
description: The eldest daughter of Joseph and Mary, born on July 11, 2 B.C. Miriam was described as well-balanced, noble, and spiritual, known as the belle of the family. She idealized Jesus and maintained a close relationship with him. She married Jacob the stone mason and helped support the family through selling milk and butter. She was present when Jesus made his fourth post-resurrection appearance to mortals.

name: mirrors
description: Reflective surfaces used both literally and metaphorically throughout The Urantia Book. Literally, primitive peoples regarded mirrors with superstitious awe, believing they could separate the soul from the body. Metaphorically, mirrors represent reflection of truth, character, and divine wisdom, with defeat described as the true mirror of one's real self.

name: misadaptation
description: A condition of imperfection or disharmony that arises naturally in evolutionary universes. Misadaptation is inherent in evolutionary growth and represents the origin of potential evil, allowing for disagreement and personality expression. The superuniverses are expected to eventually exhaust all possibilities for misadaptation as they progress toward perfection.

name: misery
description: A state of suffering or distress that believers should not indulge in through self-pity or seek comfort in through mere fellowship with others in similar circumstances. Instead, Jesus taught that sympathy should be extended to the brave and courageous while avoiding deceptive comfort that weakens character development.

name: misfortune
description: Hardship, adversity, or bad luck that may befall individuals or groups.

name: Mispeh
description: A town in Perea visited during the mission of the seventy messengers between January 3, A.D. 30 and Jesus' death.

name: missing links
description: The missing links of evolution never existed, as new species arise suddenly and fully-formed from era to era.

name: missionaries
description: Religious teachers and emissaries sent to spread spiritual teachings among different peoples, from the earliest Dalamatian and Adamic teachers to the Salem missionaries of Melchizedek and later Christian evangelists.

name: Mississippi River
description: A major North American river that once received drainage from the Great Lakes system and was displaced fifty miles westward during the fourth ice age.

name: Missouri
description: A North American state that was affected by the central glacier 150,000 years ago during the sixth ice age.

name: mistakes
description: Human errors and failures that should not discourage spiritual progress, as mistakes remembered in time will be forgotten in eternity, while God makes no mistakes and serves as the faithful source of all souls.

name: misunderstandings
description: Inevitable conflicts arising from ignorance and differing viewpoints among evolving beings with free will, which require wisdom, tolerance, and fair adjustment mechanisms to resolve harmoniously.

name: mites
description: Small copper coins given by a poor widow who, according to Jesus, contributed more than all others because she gave everything she had rather than giving from her surplus.

name: Mithraic priest from Persia
description: A Persian priest of Mithraism, educated at Alexandria, who had a memorable conversation with Jesus at Carthage about immortality, time, and eternity.

name: Mithraic priest in Corinth
description: Jesus talked to the Mithraic priest in Corinth, urging him to seek eternal salvation within his own soul rather than in man-made mysteries or human philosophies.

name: Mithraism
description: An ancient mystery religion that arose in Iran, worshipping Mithras as the savior and redeemer of sinful mankind. The cult spread throughout the Roman Empire via Roman legions, absorbing Zoroastrian teachings and becoming a major competitor to early Christianity before ultimately declining due to its exclusion of women from full fellowship.

name: Mithras
description: The central deity of Mithraism, portrayed as a militant god who emerged from a great rock and became the champion of the sun-god in his struggle against darkness. Mithras was made immortal after slaying the mythical sacred bull and was elevated to serve as an intercessor for humanity among the gods.

name: mixed commission of twelve
description: A Jerusem administrative body that completed arrangements for Urantia's planetary occupation and approved the Life Carriers' plans for their sixtieth Satania experiment to improve the Nebadon life patterns.

name: Mo Ti
description: A Chinese philosopher who combated Confucianism by proclaiming a brotherhood founded on the love of God rather than ethical duty. He attempted to rekindle the ancient quest for new truth but failed against the vigorous opposition of Confucian disciples.

name: Moab, hills of
description: Rocky hills visible to the far east from the high hill near Nazareth, where Joseph and Jesus would take their Sabbath afternoon walks and enjoy panoramic views of Galilee.

name: Moabites
