description: A neighboring tribe upon whom King David laid heavy tribute after securing the ark of Yahweh and establishing official worship for his kingdom.

name: mob
description: A disorganized crowd or mass of people, often acting emotionally or irrationally in group situations.

name: moderation
description: The virtue of restraint and balance in all things, which leads to charm, happiness, and wisdom. Action achieves strength while moderation brings charm, and restraint in all things is considered good.

name: modern civilization
description: The contemporary state of human society marked by unprecedented scientific achievement and mechanical development, yet characterized by spiritual stagnation, philosophic chaos, and widespread psychological unsettlement during the twentieth century's economic upheavals and social transitions.

name: modesty
description: A cultural concept that evolved from ancient practices of self-deprecation to prevent spiritual envy, eventually developing into civilized restraint and courtesy. In women, modesty in clothing and behavior historically originated from fears surrounding menstruation taboos.

name: Mogul dynasty
description: An Indian dynasty whose establishment, along with the Ming dynasty in China, marked a significant phase in the progressive transfer of political sovereignty from smaller to larger governmental organizations in the East.

name: Mohammed
description: The founder of Islam, a great religious teacher who created his faith as a protest against foreign religious beliefs and the incoherence of his people's religious life. Like other revered leaders, his followers anticipate his eventual return.

name: mollusks
description: A group of marine animals including oysters, octopuses, and snails that represent nonprogressive evolutionary branches. Early cephalopods, growing up to fifteen feet long, once dominated the seas before declining, while simpler mollusks remain as stationary types that failed to advance evolutionarily.

name: monastic orders
description: Religious communities of celibate men and women that arose during Christianity's decline in the dark ages, often founded by individuals lacking normal sexual urges. These orders emerged as the church became increasingly monasticized and legalized.

name: money
description: A medium of exchange that evolved from early barter systems using fish, goats, and wheat grains to become the universal language of modern trade. While money serves as a civilizing force in commerce, it cannot provide love or replace the deeper obligations of family care.

name: money-changers
description: Licensed currency exchangers who operated in the temple precincts, converting various Roman Empire currencies into Jewish shekels required for temple dues and purchases. Their commercial activities, along with animal vendors, profaned the temple courts and prompted Jesus's cleansing of the temple.

name: moneylenders
description: Ancient creditors who charged extremely high interest rates, often 100 percent annually, and created standing armies of debtors through debt slavery. While legitimate interest on fair loans is acceptable, usury that exploits others is condemned.

name: Mongolia
description: A region in Asia where an influential river cult flourishes, representing the continued worship of flowing waters and natural spirits that has persisted from ancient times.

name: Mongolian merchant
description: A Taoist merchant from Mongolia who met Jesus in Caesarea and was deeply impressed by his philosophy of living the heavenly life through daily submission to the Father's will. He became a devoted teacher of these truths, inspiring his son to become a Taoist priest and establishing a family lineage dedicated to the doctrine of the One God.

name: Mongoloids
description: The Mongoloid race is a blend of red, yellow, and blue Sangiks, encompassing the Chinese, Amerinds, Europeans modified by secondary Sangik and Andonic mixture, Andite infusion, as well as the Malayan and other Indonesian peoples with a high percentage of secondary Sangik blood.

name: monkeys
description: Primitive monkeys appeared 45 million years ago as part of mammalian evolution. The retarded mid-mammals were ancestors of monkeys, baboons, chimpanzees, and gorillas. Monkeys became fetish animals in many cultures, leading to taboos on consuming their flesh due to their resemblance to humans.

name: Monmatia
description: Monmatia is the name of Urantia's solar system, which originated 4.5 billion years ago from solar gases separated from the sun during the near approach of the massive Angona system. The solar system was registered in Nebadon's physical registry 3 billion years ago and occupies a fairly central position in one of the arms of the former Andronover nebula.

name: monogamy
description: The practice of marriage between one man and one woman, representing an evolved form of marital relationship.

name: monota
description: Monota is the living nonspirit energy of Paradise, closely related to divinity and serving as the nonspiritual energy system of the Universal Father. On Paradise, monota and spirit are indistinguishable except by name, representing the eternity counterpart of the Original Son's living spirit energy.

name: monotheism
description: The belief in one supreme God, which evolved through the inclusion, subordination, and eventual exclusion of lesser deities. Monotheism enabled humanity to understand the Father as creator and controller of all creation, though it sometimes faces the challenge of maintaining the personal nature of Deity against pantheistic tendencies.

name: monotony
description: Monotony indicates immaturity of creative imagination and inactivity of intellectual coordination with spiritual endowment. It is restful in contrast to variety, but when excessive, it wears and exhausts, multiplying perplexities and magnifying difficulties in both personal development and social relations.

name: Montana
description: A state known for the abundant fresh-water fossils found in the Morrison beds of Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming from the Jurassic period.

name: Montreal
description: A city in the St. Lawrence region of North America, home to Mount Royal, which is the eroded neck of a volcano from the great volcanic activity that occurred 230 million years ago.

name: moon (Urantia’s)
description: Urantia's natural satellite, which has stopped rotating due to tidal friction and always shows the same face to Earth. In the distant future, when the moon approaches within about eleven thousand miles of Earth, tidal gravity will cause it to shatter into fragments that may form rings or be drawn to Earth as meteors.

name: Moqui tribe
description: A Native American tribe that revered serpents and performed snake dances, representing one example of the widespread reverence for snakes found across many cultures from Arabia through India.

name: moraines
description: Geological formations left behind by glaciers, including ground, lateral, and terminal moraines that provide distinctive evidence of glacial activity and serve as telltale signs of ice movement across the landscape.

name: morality
description: The recognition of duty and awareness of right and wrong that emerges from human personality and serves as the essential foundation for spiritual consciousness. Morality represents the superanimal but subspiritual level of mind that enables moral choosing and prepares the way for religious experience.

name: mores
description: The traditional customs and folkways that govern group behavior, originating from humanity's unconscious efforts to avoid pain and seek pleasure in social living. These accumulated social practices serve as the foundation for laws and institutions while acting as a biological safety brake against overly rapid social change.

name: morontia
description: A vast level of reality intervening between the material and spiritual realms, encompassing both personal and impersonal realities, living and nonliving energies. Morontia substance has a spiritual warp and physical woof, bridging the gap between mortal material existence and advanced spiritual status.

name: morontia cherubim
description: Morontia-level beings that serve alongside other cherubim and sanobim in various capacities within the transition realms of the local universe.

name: Morontia Companions
description: Friendly and companionable beings created by the local universe Mother Spirit who serve as gracious hosts and guides for ascending mortals throughout the mansion worlds and morontia spheres. These beautiful and versatile creatures, numbering over seventy billion in Nebadon, facilitate the morontia journey from the first mansion world to Salvington.

name: morontia counselors
description: The fourth order of transition minister seraphim who teach and counsel surviving mortals on the mansion worlds, specializing in the philosophy of mota to help students integrate meanings, unify values, and achieve cosmic perspective.

name: morontia life
description: The transitional phase of existence between material and spiritual life, experienced by ascending mortals as they progress through the mansion worlds and morontia spheres of the local universe on their journey toward Paradise.

name: morontia mind
description: The transitional form of mind that functions between the material and spiritual levels of existence, enabling morontia beings to perceive and interact with both physical and spiritual realities during their ascension career.

name: morontia mota
description: A form of wisdom or knowledge that transcends material understanding, representing the higher philosophical insights gained during morontia existence.

name: morontia planners
description: Celestial beings who design and coordinate the associations of all personalities from different realms present on any sphere, working with Morontia Power Supervisors to enhance the coordination of progressive morontia life.

name: Morontia Power Supervisors
description: Self-governing beings exclusively devoted to supervising activities that combine spiritual and physical energies, creating and maintaining the morontia environment for progressing ascenders. They are offspring of the local universe Mother Spirit, directed by the joint spirit activity of the Universe Son and Universe Spirit, and provide ascending mortals with 570 successive morontia bodies during their journey through the local universe.

name: morontia progressors
description: Ascending mortals who traverse the transition spheres from mansion world graduation until attaining spirit status in the superuniverse career. These three-natured beings advance in intellect, spirit, and personality form throughout their morontia experience in the local universe, serving as the evolutionary portal to spirit life and eventual creature perfection.

name: morontia recorders
description: Celestial artisans who function as group photographers of the transition worlds, preserving vital scenes and associations of progressive morontia epochs in the archives of morontia halls of records.

name: morontia synthesizers
description: Master craftsmen among the celestial artisans who add the culminating and finishing touches to morontia ensembles, achieving inspiring portrayals of divine beauty that serve as enduring inspiration to spirit beings and their morontia associates.

name: morontia temple
description: A magnificent structure that appears when a planet enters the era of light and life, serving as the headquarters for planetary ceremonies, translation of living mortals, and cosmic contemplation. Built by Morontia Power Supervisors according to approved plans, these temples mark the admission of a sphere to the settled ages of evolutionary attainment.

name: morontia world
description: The second transitional culture world of Jerusem that serves as headquarters for the supervisors of morontia life, surrounded by seven training spheres where morontia chiefs educate their associates and ascending mortals.

name: morontia world transition ministers
description: Specialized ministers who assist with the transition processes on morontia worlds, helping ascending mortals adapt to their new environments and progressive spiritual development.

name: morontia worlds
description: Architectural spheres serving as transition phases for mortal ascension through the local universe, including the 56 system transition abodes, constellation spheres, and universe headquarters worlds. These made-to-order worlds contain 100 physical elements and 100 forms of unique morontia material, bridging the gulf between material and spiritual realms.

name: morontians
description: Morontia beings or entities progressing through the transitional stages between material and spiritual existence.

name: Morrison beds
description: Geological formations in Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming containing abundant fresh-water fossils from ancient inland lakes, with combined salt- and fresh-water deposits ranging from 2,000 to 5,000 feet in thickness and very little limestone present.

name: Mortal Corps of the Finality
description: A corps of perfected mortals who have achieved the ultimate of creature evolution and serve in the eternal purposes of the Universal Father.

name: mortal mind
description: The intellectual capacity and consciousness of evolutionary human beings, characterized by its ability to recognize divine realities, evolve spiritually through Adjuster cooperation, and serve as the foundation for morontia soul development. The mortal mind is the cosmic loom that carries the morontia fabrics on which the indwelling Thought Adjuster weaves patterns of eternal character and divine meanings.

name: mortals
description: Animal-origin evolutionary beings of ascendant potential who represent the lowest order of intelligent and personal creation in the universe. Mortals are divinely beloved creatures who may choose to accept the destiny of eternal survival and ascension to Paradise through fusion with their indwelling Thought Adjusters.

name: Moses
description: The great Hebrew leader, teacher, and organizer who emancipated a remnant of the submerged violet race from Egypt and established monotheistic worship of the Universal Father as 'The God of Israel.' Moses was an extraordinary combination of military leader, social organizer, and religious teacher who laid the foundation for the Hebrew nation and perpetuation of their race.

name: Most High
description: A divine title and designation for Deity, particularly used by ancient Semites as one of many appellations for God, referring to the supreme spiritual authority under whose shadow believers find protection and refuge.

name: Most High Assistants
description: A corps of high celestial volunteers from outside Nebadon who serve as central and superuniverse representatives and observers in the local universe. They assist Paradise personalities with advanced plans and rulings, helping to bring Nebadon into harmony with Orvonton and Paradise ideals.

name: Most High observer of Urantia
description: The current Vorondadek Son who serves as planetary observer and emergency regent of Urantia, being the twenty-third to serve in this capacity. As trustee of planetary sovereignty, he rules in the kingdoms of men and can exercise absolute authority over all celestial beings on the planet during times of crisis.

name: Most High observers
description: Vorondadek Sons who serve as planetary observers on quarantined or isolated worlds, empowered to exercise absolute authority over all celestial beings and seize planetary government during times of grave crisis. They act as the instrumentality through which the Most Highs rule in the kingdoms of men.

name: Most Highs
description: The Most Highs are Vorondadek Sons who serve as Constellation Fathers, ruling over constellations of one hundred systems each. Three Most Highs govern each constellation for 150,000 years, with the senior Most High serving as Constellation Father for 50,000 years before rotating positions. They embody the highest administrative wisdom and loyalty among all Local Universe Sons of God, focusing on group welfare and the greatest good for the greatest number over the longest period of time.

name: Most Highs of Norlatiadek
description: The Most Highs of Norlatiadek are the three Vorondadek Sons who rule the constellation of Norlatiadek from their headquarters on Edentia. They assumed special jurisdiction over the rebellious worlds of Satania during the Lucifer rebellion and continue to exercise this authority with the support of the Ancients of Days. Their rotation has been suspended since the rebellion, with the current Constellation Father being Vorondadek Son number 617,318.

name: Most Holy Sphere
description: The Most Holy Sphere is one of three grand spheres of activity on upper Paradise, reserved for worship, trinitization, and high spiritual attainment. This realm of pure spiritual reality surrounds the Deity presence and contains no material structures or intellectual creations, only divine beauty and spiritual grandeur. Trinity-origin beings have their homes on Paradise in close proximity to this sacred sphere.

name: mota
description: Mota is morontia wisdom, a superphilosophical sensitivity for truth discernment and unity perception that bridges the gap between material science and spiritual religion. It is the understanding technique of the morontia level, combining knowledge-reason with faith-insight to provide cosmic perspective and reconcile divergent reality perceptions that material personalities cannot achieve.

name: mote
description: A mote refers to a small speck or particle, used metaphorically by Jesus in his teaching about not judging others while having greater faults oneself. He taught his followers to first remove the beam from their own eye before attempting to remove the mote from their brother's eye, emphasizing mercy and self-examination over criticism of others.

name: mother cult
description: The mother cult was an ancient religious movement that glorified feminine deities and practiced mother worship, reaching its peak during the age in Crete through worship of Eve as the "great mother." This cult spread throughout the Mediterranean world, influencing various cultures including the Danubians and Greeks, and incorporated practices such as cremation of the dead and physical self-discipline including castration among its priests.

name: Mother Eves
description: Mother Eves refer to the female Material Sons, also known as Material Daughters, who serve alongside Material Sons as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds. They are created beings who function as Adams and Eves to improve the biological status of mortal races on inhabited planets.

name: mother force of space
description: The mother force of space is an alternative name for absoluta, representing the primordial space-force that serves as the foundation for all physical energy manifestations in the universe. This cosmic force is the source from which all material energy and matter ultimately derive.

name: Mother of God cult
description: The Mother of God cult was a mystery religion centered on the worship of a divine feminine figure, with its headquarters located on the site of present-day St. Peter's in Rome. This formal but emotionally powerful cult incorporated elements from various traditions including Mithraism and the Phrygian cult of Cybele, practiced sacraments of cakes and wine, and significantly influenced early Christianity through parallels with the veneration of Mary.

name: Mother-Son
description: Mother-Son appears to be an alternative designation for the Eternal Son in relation to the Universal Father, emphasizing the maternal-paternal relationship within the Paradise Trinity. The Eternal Son represents the spiritual and personal aspects of deity in conjunction with the Father's universal presence.

name: Mother Spirits
description: Divine feminine beings who serve as creative partners and administrators in local universes, working in conjunction with Creator Sons to foster spiritual development and coordinate universe activities.

name: mothers
description: Human women who bear and nurture children, providing crucial early impressions of the universe through their care and forming the foundation for children's understanding of both earthly and divine relationships. Mother love is described as a powerful, instinctive emotion that drives the establishment and maintenance of homes and families.

name: motion
description: The fundamental activity of cosmic energy throughout the master universe, with all units of energy in constant revolution around universal orbits. Motion is classified as absolute when measured in relation to Paradise, while space itself exists relative to the stationary Paradise Isle, and the chronicity of motion is determined through this Paradise relationship.

name: motivation
description: The underlying driving forces and purposes that compel action and behavior in conscious beings, representing the spiritual content that validates conduct and reveals the true character of individuals.

name: motives
description: The inner purposes and intentions behind actions that determine the moral value of conduct. God judges based on motives rather than outward acts, emphasizing that the spiritual content and sincere intentions of the heart matter more than external appearances or deeds.

name: mottos
description: Brief phrases or statements that encapsulate guiding principles or beliefs, serving as memorable expressions of fundamental truths or values.

name: Mount Ararat
description: The sacred mountain of northern Mesopotamia revered by the Vanites, where ancient traditions claim their moral law of seven commandments was given to Van by the Gods. This mountain held significance similar to Mount Sinai for the Hebrews and became woven into later flood narratives.

name: Mount Assembly
description: The most holy mount on Edentia where the Faithful of Days resides, serving as the dwelling place of the Paradise Trinity representative. Morontia mortals gather here to hear about the journey through Havona and on to Paradise.

name: Mount Carmel
description: A mountain ridge in Palestine running down to the sea, associated with the prophet Elijah who confronted the priests of Baal. John the Baptist was inspired by Elijah's connection to Mount Carmel and adopted his style of dress, wearing a hairy garment with a leather girdle.

name: Mount Gerizim
description: A sacred mountain in Samaria where the Samaritans built a temple to worship Yahweh until it was destroyed by John Hyrcanus. Jesus and his apostles camped here while preaching to the Samaritans, and it was the site where Jesus taught that true worship occurs in spirit and truth rather than at any specific location.

name: Mount Gilboa
description: A mountain referenced in The Urantia Book, likely the biblical Mount Gilboa located in northern Israel.

name: Mount Hermon
description: A majestic snow-capped mountain north of Galilee where Jesus underwent his great temptation and met Caligastia before his baptism. Mount Hermon was visible from Nazareth and Caesarea-Philippi, serving as a significant location for Jesus's ministry and the site where he established headquarters during his forty-day retirement to formulate plans for proclaiming the kingdom of God.

name: Mount Hood
description: A North American mountain that began forming ten million years ago during an age of widespread land deposits, alongside Mount Shasta and Mount Rainier.

name: Mount Horeb
description: A mountain referenced in The Urantia Book, also known as Mount Sinai in biblical tradition.

name: Mount Lebanon
description: A mountain range in the Phoenician region that twenty-five teachers of truth passed while traveling from Caesarea-Philippi to the Phoenician coast.

name: Mount of Olives
description: A mountain east of Jerusalem, also known as Mount Olivet, significant in Jesus's life and ministry.

name: mount of transfiguration
description: The location where Jesus's transfiguration occurred, associated with Mount Hermon where he took his apostles to witness his divine authority as the Son of God.

name: Mount Olivet
description: A mountain east of Jerusalem where Jesus experienced many significant moments, including his first view of the Holy City as a youth, his weeping over Jerusalem, his teachings to the apostles about the temple's destruction, his final prayers in Gethsemane, and his ascension. The Mount of Olives served as Jesus's primary residence area during his final ministry in Jerusalem.

name: Mount Olympus
description: The legendary home of the Greek gods, whom the intelligent Greeks viewed as happy-go-lucky deities more human than divine, never greatly revered or feared.

name: Mount Rainier
description: A North American mountain that began forming ten million years ago during an age of widespread land deposits, alongside Mount Shasta and Mount Hood.

name: Mount Royal
description: Mount Royal in Montreal is the eroded neck of a volcano that was active 230 million years ago during a period of great volcanic activity in the St. Lawrence region.

name: Mount Sartaba
description: Mount Sartaba is the highest mountain overlooking the Jordan valley, site of Herod's Alexandrian fortress where he imprisoned one of his wives and buried his strangled sons. Jesus took his apostles there for a three-day period of rest and relaxation during a time of great tension among the disciples.

name: Mount Seraph
description: Mount Seraph is the highest elevation on Jerusem at almost fifteen thousand feet and serves as the point of departure for all transport seraphim, with mechanical developments providing initial energy for escaping planetary gravity.

name: Mount Shasta
description: Mount Shasta began its mountain formation ten million years ago during a period when extensive land deposits and great fresh-water lakes were forming in western North America.

name: Mount Sinai
description: Mount Sinai was an intermittently active volcano up to about 2000 B.C. whose eruptions awed the Bedouins and inspired Hebrew worship of Yahweh. It was where Moses promulgated the Ten Commandments and where the Israelites received iron ores that enhanced Egyptian metalworking skills.

name: Mount Tabor
description: Mount Tabor is part of a mountain range east of Nazareth, which nine-year-old Jesus climbed with his father Joseph in late June 3 A.D., marveling at the superb view from its summit on a clear day.

name: mountains
description: Mountains are geological formations that result from the action of rain, gravity, and erosion rather than from planetary cooling and contraction. They form when lighter land areas filled with erosion deposits are subjected to crustal pressure, causing them to crumple, fold, and rise upward.

name: mourning
description: Mourning in ancient times was largely a ritual rooted in fear of ghosts rather than genuine sorrow, involving practices like self-torture, disguised clothing, and professional mourners. Jesus distinguished between sincere tenderhearted mourning, which he blessed, and forced outward exhibitions of mourning, which he resented.

name: mouth
description: The mouth represents the expression of what originates in the heart, with Jesus teaching that spiritual defilement comes not from what enters the mouth but from the evil thoughts, words, and deeds that proceed out of it.

name: multilingualism
description: The ability to speak or use multiple languages, though specific details about multilingualism are not provided in the available context.

name: multitude
description: A large gathering or crowd of people.

name: music
description: A universal language of sound and rhythm that has evolved from primitive monotony to higher forms of harmony and melody. Music serves as the speech of Havona and represents spiritual expression, with celestial music being far superior to earthly compositions.

name: musk oxen
description: Large Arctic mammals that were among the few animal species to survive the rigorous glacial periods in North America by following the advancing and retreating ice sheets.

name: mussels
description: Bivalve gastropods that are part of an ancient lineage of shell animals that have persisted through millions of years with little change from their ancient forms.

name: mustard seed
description: A tiny seed used by Jesus in parables to illustrate the kingdom of heaven, representing how something small can grow into something magnificent and how the gospel unfolds in new manifestations across generations.

name: myrrh
description: An aromatic resin used in the embalming process, notably used by Joseph and Nicodemus to prepare Jesus' body for burial in the tomb.

name: mysteries
description: Phenomena that are not fully understood or explained, ranging from cosmic forces and divine purposes to the nature of personality and spiritual communion. Mysteries stimulate religious feelings and serve as subjects for philosophical exploration.

name: mystery cults
description: Personal and fraternal religious movements from Egypt and the Levant that flourished in the Greco-Roman world, offering individual salvation through elaborate initiation ceremonies and secret rituals centered around mythical stories of gods' death and rebirth.

name: Mystery Monitors
description: Another name for Thought Adjusters, referring to their mysterious nature and function as divine fragments that monitor and guide mortal minds.

name: mysticism
description: The practice of cultivating consciousness of God's presence through meditation and spiritual communion. While praiseworthy when leading to enhanced spiritual insight and service, it becomes dangerous when used to escape reality or when resulting in fanaticism and social isolation.

name: myths
description: Traditional stories and legends that often provide supernatural explanations for natural phenomena or historical events. The Urantia Book describes how myths developed from garbled accounts of actual events, such as the arrival of celestial beings, and how ancient peoples created mythological explanations when they lacked understanding of natural causes.

name: Nabal
description: A rich Edomite whose widow David married as part of his strategy to strengthen his political position by forming alliances with powerful families.

name: Nabodad
description: Leader of the school at Kish who attempted to reform temple practices by attacking prevalent temple harlotry, but his failure led to the downfall of the Salem missionaries' more important spiritual and philosophical teachings.

name: Nabon
description: A Greek Jew who served as a high priest of the Mithraic mystery cult in Rome. Through his discussions with Jesus about truth and faith, he was profoundly influenced and became one of the early converts to the gospel of the kingdom.

name: Naboths
description: A family murdered by King Ahab in an intrigue to seize their land, which prompted the prophet Elijah to make their deaths a moral issue and launch his vigorous campaign against the Baalites.

name: Nabu
description: One of the chief Mesopotamian deities that the Salem teachers incorporated into their reduced pantheon, eventually becoming part of the Babylonian triad alongside Bel and Ea.

name: Nahor (evangelist)
description: One of the younger evangelists who, along with Simon Zelotes, made a tactical blunder at Nazareth by gathering Jesus' supporters in a belligerent stance against his enemies, which escalated the hostile crowd's anger.

name: Nahor (Old Testament)
description: Abraham's brother who was a lukewarm convert to the Salem religion and persuaded the family to delay their journey to Salem by staying in Haran. He later built up a strong city-state bearing his name rather than fully embracing Melchizedek's teachings.

name: Nahor (rabbi)
description: A Jerusalem rabbi and teacher at an academy who came to Nazareth to observe Jesus and proposed taking him to Jerusalem for education. After Jesus consulted with his parents and prayed to his heavenly Father, he decided to remain at home with his family.

name: nails
description: The metal fasteners used to secure Jesus to the cross during his crucifixion, piercing his hands to the crossbeam and his feet to the upright timber. Thomas later referenced the nail marks as evidence he would need to believe in Jesus' resurrection.

name: Nain
description: Nain was a hill town in Galilee, about the same height as Mount Tabor, where Joseph did carpentry work and Jesus gained practical knowledge of mankind. It was the site where Jesus raised the widow's son from the dead, causing great excitement among the miracle-seeking people of Galilee.

name: Nalda
description: Nalda was the Samaritan woman Jesus met at Jacob's well in Sychar, to whom he made his first direct pronouncement of his divine nature and sonship on earth. She was present at Jesus' seventeenth morontia appearance to about seventy-five Samaritan believers near the same well.

name: Nambia
description: Nambia is the original and first-born Life Carrier of Nebadon, serving as one of the life-determining trio along with Gabriel and the Father Melchizedek who direct the corps of one hundred million Life Carriers in the universe.

name: names
description: Names hold spiritual significance in the universe, with mortals receiving their real universe names after Adjuster fusion. Primitive cultures regarded names as sacred entities that could be pawned like property, and translating celestial being names into human language presents challenges for revelators.

name: Nanak, Guru
description: Guru Nanak was a religious teacher and founder of Sikhism in India.

name: Naomi
description: Naomi was the bride at the wedding in Cana where Jesus performed his first miracle, turning water into wine, marking the beginning of his public ministry and miraculous demonstrations.

name: Naples
description: Naples was a major Italian port city where Jesus, Gonod, and Ganid stayed during their Mediterranean journey. Here Jesus taught Ganid about discernment in helping others and encountered a street beggar who lacked the capacity for spiritual response.

name: Nasanta
description: Nasanta, daughter of Elman the Syrian physician, was one of the ten women commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom, marking a revolutionary step in his ministry.

name: Nathan of Busiris
description: Nathan of Busiris was the fifth messenger in the Jerusalem-Alexandria relay who carried news of Jesus' crucifixion to the believers in Alexandria, where Jesus appeared in morontia form during his report.

name: Nathan of Caesarea
description: Nathan of Caesarea was a Greek Jew who wrote the Gospel of John under the direction and inspiration of the apostle John when John was ninety-nine years old at Ephesus.

name: Nathan of Cana
description: Nathan of Cana was the father of the bridegroom Johab at the wedding feast in Cana where Jesus performed his first miracle. Despite providing ample wine for the celebration, a shortage occurred during the festivities, prompting Mary to confidently seek Jesus' help.

name: Nathan of Nazareth
description: Nathan of Nazareth was a potter who worked near a flowing spring and became a friend to young Jesus and the neighbor boy Jacob. His skillful work on the potter's wheel inspired both boys to dream of becoming potters, and he encouraged their creativity by giving them clay to play with.

name: Nathaniel (Apostle)
description: Nathaniel of Cana was the sixth and last apostle chosen by Jesus, brought to the Master by his friend Philip. At age 25, he was the youngest of seven children and the only support for his aged parents. Known as the "apostolic philosopher and dreamer," he was well-educated, honest, and sincere, though sometimes proud and prejudiced. He served as the group's controlling influence during crises and was responsible for overseeing the welfare of the apostles' families.

name: Nathaniel the Pharisee
description: Nathaniel the Pharisee was a wealthy resident of Ragaba who invited Jesus and about twenty fellow Pharisees to a Sabbath breakfast, serving as host with Jesus as the guest of honor.

name: National Council of Defense
description: The National Council of Defense is a governing body of twenty-five members on a nearby inhabited planet, empowered to assess states for money, men, and materials during wartime. Led by a president and presided over by the chief of staff of coordinated military affairs, it also commissions graduates of federal military schools as "guardians of civilization."

name: nationalism
description: Nationalism is described as both essential to social survival and the chief barrier to world peace. While it has fostered discipline, cooperation, and nation-building throughout history, it represents a delusional virus of national sovereignty that prevents lasting peace until nations surrender their sovereign powers to a unified mankind government.

name: nations
description: Nations represent consolidated sovereignty that reduces minor wars between smaller groups but increases the potential for greater conflicts as larger, more powerful nations come into contact with each other. The consolidation of sovereignty into fewer, stronger nations sets the stage for major world-wide conflicts.

name: natural events
description: Natural events refer to the ordinary occurrences and phenomena that take place according to the established laws and patterns of the physical universe, as distinguished from supernatural or miraculous interventions.

name: natural law
description: Natural laws are the established principles governing the physical universe that Jesus chose to follow consistently during his earthly ministry. These laws provide predictable patterns for physical phenomena, though their predictability decreases with distance from Paradise, and they can be accelerated under certain circumstances.

name: natural philosophy
description: Natural philosophy, like religion, tends toward dogmatism in its approach to understanding the physical world and natural phenomena.

name: natural resources
description: Natural resources are materials and energy sources found in nature that societies can utilize for various purposes. Throughout history, early wars were fought over natural resources like flint, salt, and metals, with the first formal tribal treaty concerning a salt deposit fostering intertribal communication. The extent of material civilization is largely determined by available natural resources and the inherent genius of a people.

name: natural rights
description: Natural rights refer to the concept that individuals possess inherent rights that come from their very existence as human beings, rather than being granted by governments or societies.

name: natural selection
description: Natural selection is the evolutionary process by which organisms with favorable traits survive and reproduce more successfully than those without them. Variety among organisms is indispensable to provide wide opportunity for the differential survival of superior strains.

name: naturalism
description: Naturalism is a philosophical approach that emphasizes the material world and natural causes. When philosophy leans heavily toward the world of matter, it tends to become rationalistic or naturalistic in its perspective.

name: nature
description: Nature is described as the physical habit of God in a limited sense, representing the material manifestation of divine laws as they operate throughout the universe. While nature exhibits divine perfection from Paradise, it is also modified and sometimes marred by the evolutionary processes, imperfections, and rebellions of creatures in time and space. Nature provides no inherent rights to humans and should not be worshiped, though it can serve as an analogy for spiritual truths and inspire contemplation of the Creator.

name: navels
description: Many shrewd shamans induced autohypnosis by prolonged staring at their navels, using this technique to impress and mystify tribesmen with their mysterious conduct and seemingly supernatural feats.

name: Nazareth
description: Nazareth was a liberal center of Jewish thought and culture where Jesus received his education and moral training. Located at a major caravan crossroads with a largely gentile population, it was known for liberal interpretation of Jewish law, leading to the common saying "Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?" The town had a mild climate and served as Jesus's home base, though its people later became resentful of his fame and refused to accept his teachings.

name: Nazarites
description: The Nazarites were a religious brotherhood with headquarters at Engedi, known for their ascetic lifestyle and strict vows of abstinence from intoxicants, growing their hair, and avoiding contact with the dead. John the Baptist was a lifelong Nazarite who took formal vows at age fourteen, following in the tradition of Samson and Samuel. While some Nazarites became believers in Jesus, the majority of this eccentric group refused to accept him as a teacher from heaven.

name: Neanderthalers
description: The Neanderthalers were descendants of Andon who dominated the world for almost half a million years, spreading from northwest India to France, China, and northern Africa. They were excellent fighters and travelers but had little culture beyond improved flint work and lived in fear of darkness, practicing human sacrifice to bring back the moon's light. These peoples gradually retrogressed spiritually and were eventually absorbed or displaced by the evolutionary races of color.

name: Nebadon
description: Nebadon is the local universe in which Urantia is located, consisting of one hundred constellations with headquarters at Salvington. Created by Michael of Nebadon, it belongs to the superuniverse of Orvonton and contains over three million inhabited worlds. The universe has experienced three rebellions by System Sovereigns and is still considered a young creation not yet settled in light and life.

name: Nebadon Corps of Completion
description: A celestial order mentioned in connection with Jesus's soul-identity and the future destiny of finaliters, though specific details about their function and composition are not provided.

name: Nebadon Corps of Perfection
description: Spirit-fused ascenders who attain the Nebadon Corps of Perfection may become Universe Aids and interpret the viewpoints of evolving creatures to celestial authorities. Members acquire authoritative wisdom about Nebadon from witnessing the ascending processional of planetary ages on ten million inhabited worlds.

name: Nebadon Corps of the Finality
description: A celestial corps that Jesus's soul-identity, built up through divine guidance and perfect alignment with the Father's will, may have been destined to lead in connection with their undisclosed destiny in outer space universes.

name: Nebadon Power Centers
description: A local universe order of power centers operating within Nebadon, likely involved in energy control and distribution functions similar to other power centers in the grand universe.

name: Nebadon Revelatory Corps
description: The Nebadon Revelatory Corps, directed by Mantutia Melchizedek, created Part II of the Urantia Papers in 1934. This commission of twelve Nebadon personalities sponsored various presentations and authored narratives for the revelation.

name: Nebuchadnezzar
description: The Babylonian king who overthrew Necho, destroyed Jerusalem, and carried the Jewish population into captivity, marking the end of Judah and the Yahweh-Baal struggle. Jeremiah counseled surrendering Jerusalem to Nebuchadnezzar during the siege.

name: nebulae
description: Cosmic formations that serve as the mother wheels of suns and planetary systems, initiated by Paradise force organizers and existing in various forms including spiral, concentric, and other configurations. They vary greatly in size and can produce up to one hundred million suns, playing a crucial role in the material evolution of the universes.

name: necessities (economic)
description: The fundamental material requirements that tie humanity to reality and drive social development. Early civilization was forged upon the anvils of necessity and fear, while primitive society evolved through cycles founded on the reciprocity of necessity for survival and cooperation.

name: Necho
description: The Egyptian pharaoh who defeated and killed King Josiah, leading Judah into Egyptian bondage under the Baal political party's control. Necho was later overthrown by Nebuchadnezzar, causing Judah to fall under Babylonian rule.

name: necklaces
description: Personal ornaments worn around the neck, part of humanity's early development of decorative accessories and social status symbols.

name: necromancy
description: Necromancy is a form of black art practiced by those who claimed supernatural contact with the dead, later classified as witchcraft or shamancraft alongside other occult practices.

name: needle
description: A needle is referenced in Jesus's teaching that it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for the self-satisfied rich to enter the kingdom of heaven.

name: needs
description: Human needs encompass both genuine requirements that the Father knows before we ask, and the excessive desires that burden modern civilization with luxury and materialism beyond basic necessities.

name: negativism
description: Negativism is a major error of modern religions that emphasizes repression rather than positive growth, while children naturally possess positive moral impulses that move toward righteousness and social ministry.

name: Negroids
description: The Negroid race is a secondary Sangik type originally composed of the orange, green, and indigo races, found primarily in Africa, India, and Indonesia.

name: neighbor
description: A neighbor, in Jesus's expanded teaching, includes all of humanity rather than just those close to us, with the commandment to love one's neighbor as oneself representing the highest ethics.

name: neighboring planet
description: A neighboring planet in the Satania system displays an advanced civilization on one continental nation, though it suffered defaults by both its Planetary Prince and Material Son like Urantia, and has never received a Magisterial Son mission.

name: Nepal
description: Nepal is the north Indian province where Gautama Siddhartha was born in the sixth century before Christ, in a small mountain valley in the southern Himalayas.

name: Nephilim
description: The Nephilim were the Nodites of the postrebellion era, regarded as the "mighty men of old" and "men of renown" born from the union of Caligastia's staff with mortal women.

name: Neptune, god
description: Neptune was the Roman god of the sea who had a temple in Alexandria, one of the city's chief attractions alongside the library and other cultural landmarks.

name: Neptune
description: The temple of Neptune was one of the chief attractions in Alexandria, a cosmopolitan city of one million people where Jesus and Ganid explored during their Mediterranean journey.

name: Nereids
description: Sea nymphs from Greek mythology that evolving monotheists kept as subordinate spiritual beings along with other lesser deities like spirits, demons, fates, and fairies.

name: Nerites
description: The final Andite group to emerge from the Caspian region, representing the last eruption of Mesopotamian descendants of blended Andonite and Andite races during the nomadic rampage from 2500 to 2000 B.C.

name: net cast into sea
aliases: sweep net which was cast into the sea
description: A parable told by Jesus comparing the kingdom of heaven to a fishing net that gathers all kinds of fish, which are later sorted with the good kept and the bad discarded.

name: nether Paradise
description: A region or aspect of Paradise, the central universe location and dwelling place of the Universal Father.

name: neutrons
description: Electrically inactive particles in the atomic nucleus that determine atomic stability, held together with protons by the reciprocating function of mesotrons.

name: new commandment
description: A divine directive or teaching given by Jesus, representing an elevated spiritual principle for human conduct.

name: New Guinea
description: An island region where primitive tribes believed they descended from parrots, illustrating ancient peoples' totemic understanding of human evolution.

name: New Jerusalem
description: The prophetic holy city that many believed the Messiah would establish as the world's capital, and which John the Revelator saw descending from God as the culmination of the Teacher Sons' mission.

name: New Red Sandstone
description: Geological deposits from a specific epoch found across Europe, Africa, and Australia, with notable formations in Germany, Russia, England, and the southern Alps where modified limestone became Carrara marble.

name: New Stone Age
description: A prehistoric period in Europe around 3000 B.C. that overlapped with the Bronze Age, characterized by sun worship, agriculture, and the construction of circular, roofless sun temples, with superstitions from this era persisting in regions like Brittany.

name: New Testament
description: The collection of Christian scriptures written in Greek, primarily based on the gospels of Mark, Matthew, Luke, and John, which present honest but imperfect accounts of Jesus' life as influenced by Paul's theology, changing the course of Urantian history for nearly two thousand years.

name: new things
description: Novel practices or ideas that should be evaluated on their merit rather than embraced simply because they are new or rejected merely because they are old, as ancient civilizations often viewed new practices like writing and printing with suspicion due to their supposed magical nature.

name: New Zealand
description: A location where a tribe's priests nominally accepted Christianity but claimed to receive revelations from Gabriel allowing them to maintain their traditional loose sexual practices and other customs, illustrating how races superficially adopt new religions while adapting them to their existing beliefs.

name: Newfoundland
description: Part of an ancient geological formation of primitive fossil-bearing stone that extends across eastern North America, from Pennsylvania through various Canadian regions to Mexico.

name: newlyweds
description: Newly married couples who are traditionally subjected to teasing and pranks, customs that originated from ancient beliefs about appearing miserable to avoid arousing the envy of jealous spirits.

name: Niagara Falls
description: A waterfall flowing over Niagara limestone that became the outlet for the Great Lakes system 37,000 years ago, after the lakes had previously emptied through different routes including the Mississippi and Hudson valleys.

name: Nicaea
description: The location of a crucial church council where Athanasius, a Greek from Egypt, courageously defended the true nature of Jesus against the teachings of Arius, preserving essential Christian doctrine from dangerous compromise.

name: Nicodemus
description: A wealthy, elderly member of the Jewish Sanhedrin who secretly met with Jesus at night to avoid public detection, later became a bold follower who helped claim Jesus' body for burial, and whose home served as a meeting place for Jerusalem believers.

name: Nicopolis
description: A Greek city founded by Augustus around 31 B.C. as the "city of victory" to commemorate the battle of Actium, where Jesus and his companions lodged with a Greek proselyte named Jeramy, and where the Apostle Paul later spent a winter during his third missionary journey.

name: night police
description: Secret societies functioned as night police and contributed to the building up of social castes through their mysterious initiations and rituals.

name: Nile god
description: The legendary Nile god of ancient Egypt was a deity who died and was resurrected, inspiring the refined rituals of Isis and Osiris worship. The Nile god was believed to bestow a protecting spirit called the ka upon favored individuals at birth.

name: Nile valley
description: The valley of the Nile in Egypt served as a major center of ancient civilization, receiving migrations of Adamites and Andites who contributed to its cultural advancement. It was the destination for various peoples including Abraham and Lot during drought, and later became the successor to Mesopotamia as headquarters of the most advanced earthly civilization.

name: Nineveh
description: An ancient city referenced in the story of Jonah, representing God's love for all peoples including the so-called heathen.

name: Nirvana
description: In Buddhist philosophy, a state of supreme enlightenment and supernal bliss representing freedom from material desires and deliverance from reincarnation. Amidist believers teach that souls may enjoy Paradise before entering Nirvana, the ultimate existence.

name: nitrogen
description: A chemical element that was absent as free nitrogen in the ancient planetary atmosphere, which instead contained water vapor, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen chloride.

name: Noah (Garden dweller)
description: Son of the architect and builder of the Garden of Eden who served on the reception committee that welcomed Adam and Eve as rulers of the world.

name: Noah (wine maker of Aram)
description: A wine maker from Aram who kept records of annual river floods and advocated building houses like boats to survive flooding. He became the basis for later flood legends, including the Biblical story of Noah and the ark.

name: Nod (land of)
description: The dwelling place of the Nodites, descendants of the rebellious planetary staff, located east of the second Eden. Cain departed to this land after leaving Eden, where he married his cousin Remona and became a leader promoting peace between Nodites and Adamites.

name: Nod (Prince’s corporeal staff)
description: Former chairman of the commission on industry and trade who led the sixty members of the planetary staff into rebellion against Prince Caligastia. After the fall of Dalamatia, Nod and his followers became known as the Nodites and settled in the land of Nod.

name: Nodites
description: The Nodites were descendants of the rebel members of Prince Caligastia's staff who participated in the planetary rebellion. Named after their leader Nod, they constituted the eighth race to appear on Urantia. Despite being degraded from superhuman to mortal status after the rebellion, the Nodites displayed superior physical, intellectual, and spiritual qualities compared to other contemporary races. They established the cities of Dalamatia and later Dilmun, built the tower of Babel, and eventually dispersed into various groups that influenced the development of many ancient civilizations including the Sumerians, Assyrians, and Elamites.

name: Nog
description: Nog was a false god of light and fire worshipped by the lowest types of Sangik races who had converted the Father's temple in Dalamatia into a shrine dedicated to this deity before the city was engulfed.

name: noise
description: Noise was believed by ancient peoples to disturb and frighten away ghosts and spirits. Methods such as shouting, ringing bells, and beating drums were used to drive spirits away from the living, practices that continue in modern funeral wakes.

name: nomadism
description: Nomadism represents the wandering lifestyle that has progressively declined as human society evolved from hunting through herding to agricultural stages, with each advancement resulting in more settled, home-based living.

name: nonbreathers
description: Nonbreathers are a type of mortal beings who do not require atmospheric respiration, representing one of the planetary variations in mortal life forms found throughout the universes.

name: nonconformists
description: Nonconformists are individuals who dissent from established social norms and practices. Civilization imposes heavy penalties on such dissenters as it strives to maintain order and create a better world for future generations.

name: nonexistence
description: Nonexistence refers to the state of complete cessation of being that awaits those personalities who definitively reject eternal survival and choose against participation in the divine plan of spiritual progression.

name: nonreligionists
description: Nonreligionists are individuals who do not embrace or practice religious beliefs, representing those who remain outside organized spiritual movements and faith traditions.

name: nonresistance
description: Nonresistance is the spiritual philosophy taught by Jesus advocating the conquest of evil through positive spiritual means rather than physical retaliation. This teaching emphasizes active love, mercy, and the aggressive pursuit of righteous spiritual values while refusing selfish reactions to personal injury or injustice. Jesus demonstrated that true nonresistance involves dynamic spiritual response rather than passive submission to evil.

name: nonsurvivors
description: Nonsurvivors are mortal beings who choose to reject eternal life and spiritual progression after death. Though their personalities cease to exist as individuals, any spiritual values from their earthly experience are preserved by their Thought Adjusters and absorbed into the Supreme Being. Their choice against survival delays the Supreme's full expression in the grand universe, as each personality decision affects the cosmic evolution of deity.

name: nonviolence
description: The concept of avoiding or rejecting violence as a means of resolving conflicts or achieving goals.

name: Norana
description: A Syrian woman whose great faith in Jesus led to her twelve-year-old daughter being healed of a grievous nervous disorder characterized by convulsions and other distressing manifestations.

name: Nordan the Kenite
description: A brilliant disciple of Melchizedek who, along with his band of earnest students, received advanced teachings about the truths of the superuniverse and even of Havona.

name: Nordic race
description: A division of the white race characterized by northern European ancestry and physical traits.

name: Norlatiadek
description: Constellation number 70 in the local universe of Nebadon, with headquarters on Edentia and consisting of one hundred local systems including Satania. Currently administered by twelve Vorondadek Sons during the ongoing effects of the Lucifer rebellion, with ten Supreme Power Centers stationed at the center of its enormous stellar system.

name: north
description: The absolute direction established by the axis of Paradise, due to dimensional differences and greater force-energy out-pressure at the north end of the Isle.

name: North America
description: A continent that served as one of two extensive fertile hunting areas during the Andite era, overspread by the Amerinds. Its favorable geographic configuration, protected by vast oceans, provided unique opportunities for continuous peaceful development, with its future depending on the quality of racial factors entering its population.

name: North Pole
description: The northern polar region where vast ice sheets began forming 100,000 years ago during the retreat of the last glacier, with ice accumulation centers moving northward and preventing future glacial ages as long as polar regions remain ice-covered.

name: North Sea
description: A body of water whose then-warm shores provided settlement locations for the descendants of Andon and Fonta, who established over one thousand separate settlements along great rivers leading to these waters before ice sheets reached France and the British Isles.

name: northern lights
description: Auroral phenomena on Urantia produced by gases in the upper air belts that reflect light-energy, similar to gases in Jerusem's upper ionosphere, though the causes differ between the two worlds.

name: Norway
description: A Scandinavian country whose coastwise climate varied greatly between times of glacial inaction and periods when enormous icebergs thundered down Norwegian fiords into the North Sea.

name: nostalgia
description: A human emotion experienced by ascending mortals on Salvington as they stand on the brink of their spirit adventure in the vast superuniverse of Orvonton, looking back at familiar realms of achievement while facing the challenging mystery ahead.

name: numbers
description: Certain numbers held as fetishes by primitive peoples, with Friday being an unlucky day, 13 considered evil, and 3, 4, and 7 regarded as lucky numbers derived from early revelations and recognition of natural patterns.

name: numerology
description: The belief in magic numbers that founded the science of mathematics and led to scientific development, though the belief in magic numbers and omens of good or bad luck is pure unfounded superstition.

name: nutrition
description: The science and practice of nourishing the body through proper food intake and dietary management.

name: oaks
description: Trees that first appeared during a period of great plant-life evolution dominated by angiosperms, which were second in evolutionary importance only to the appearance of man himself.

name: oaths
description: Sacred pledges or vows of allegiance, ranging from primitive self-cursing oaths in Dalamatia to the Trinity oath of eternity taken by finaliters, including the oaths of loyalty taken by Adam and Eve to Michael and the Most Highs.

name: oats
description: A grain crop referenced in the ancient custom of granting young men brief freedom before marriage, which continued as the modern notion of "sowing wild oats."

name: Obadiah
description: A Hebrew prophet who followed Isaiah and, along with Micah, confirmed and embellished Isaiah's gospel while boldly denouncing the priest-ridden ritual and sacrificial system of the Hebrews.

name: Oban
description: Onagar's headquarters settlement on the northern shores of the ancient Mediterranean in the region of the present Caspian Sea, from which he sent out the world's first missionaries to spread his doctrines of one Deity and the Great Beyond.

name: obligations
description: Duties or responsibilities that individuals are bound to fulfill.

name: observer
description: The observer transcends the thing observed in the evaluation of reality, representing a fundamental principle that evaluation demands some degree of transcendence of what is being evaluated.

name: observers (universe)
description: Observers are commissioned by executive decree of a System Sovereign to represent their system to other systems and at the constellation capital, functioning only within the confines of the local universe.

name: oceans
description: Large bodies of water that have played crucial roles in planetary development, originally formed as fresh water but becoming increasingly salty over time through geological processes. These vast waters support marine life and influence climate patterns through their currents and seasonal interactions with atmospheric systems.

name: octopuses
description: Descendants of ancient cephalopods that have survived from early evolutionary periods alongside corals, sponges, and other primitive sea creatures, representing one of the stationary types that failed to progress evolutionarily.

name: Og
description: An Amorite king of Bashan who was defeated in a brutal Hebrew raid that exemplified the merciless treatment of war captives, contrasting with the more compassionate practices of adopting superior captives practiced by contemporary tribes.

name: Ohio
description: A region that was covered by the eastern ice sheet during the sixth and final glacier period 150,000 years ago.

name: Ohio River
description: A river whose banks near Louisville, Kentucky display thick coral deposits from 260 million years ago, indicating the presence of clear and shallow inland seas during the Devonian period.

name: oil (petroleum)
description: Fossil fuel deposits formed from enormous collections of vegetable and animal matter carried down during previous land submergences, often found in sedimentary rocks including chalk, sandstone, limestone, and coal beds.

name: oil (vegetable)
description: Plant-derived oils used in ancient medicine for treating wounds, often combined with wine, representing medical knowledge passed down from early civilizations including the Greeks, Egyptians, and Sumerians.

name: ointment
description: Ointments, perfumed anointing lotions, and precious embalming substances were used in various significant events during Jesus' ministry. A reformed woman anointed Jesus' feet with perfumed lotion at Simon's house in gratitude, Mary of Bethany poured costly spikenard on Jesus' head and feet, and women prepared spices and ointments for Jesus' burial preparation.

name: Okhban
description: Okhban was one of four great prophets who arose among the Egyptians over six thousand years, but was ultimately murdered by the people who had once followed his teachings.

name: Old Testament
description: The collection of Hebrew sacred writings that Jesus recognized as containing valuable religious wisdom and spiritual truth, while also acknowledging their human origin and the need to discern the better teachings within them. Jesus taught that these scriptures represent the views and enlightenment of their times, containing both truth and human error.

name: old things
description: The former ways and ancient traditions that are passing away as spiritual transformation occurs. Jesus taught that in the Father's kingdom, believers become new creatures and old things pass away, while emphasizing that ancient truths should not be despised merely for being old if they remain valid.

name: Oligocene
description: A geological period from 35 to 25 million years ago characterized by the rapid evolution of placental mammals and the development of more progressive forms of mammalian life.

name: olive press
description: The location near the entrance to Gethsemane where Jesus sat alone awaiting his arrest, and where the apostles gathered to deliberate their response to the Master's capture.

name: Olivet
description: A location referenced in The Urantia Book, likely referring to the Mount of Olives area near Jerusalem.

name: Olympian religion
description: The religious system associated with ancient Greek worship of the Olympian gods.

name: omens
description: Signs and portents used by ancient peoples to interpret the intentions and will of spirits, forming part of primitive religious practices. Medicine men and shamans placed great trust in omens, though The Urantia Book characterizes belief in omens as pure superstition.

name: omniaphim
description: A celestial order created by the Infinite Spirit in liaison with the Seven Supreme Executives, serving as their exclusive servants and messengers. They are assigned to grand universe service, maintain headquarters in Orvonton, and serve as personal representatives of Assigned Sentinels at system headquarters.

name: omnificence
description: The belief that God personally does all that is done in the universe, which would disenfranchise all other personalities and lead to pantheism. The Urantia Book teaches that God is omnipotent but not omnificent—he does not personally do everything that occurs.

name: omnipotence
description: The divine attribute of unlimited power held by Deity, though constrained by God's nature, will, and law. True omnipotence does not include the power to do ungodlike things or logical contradictions, and God's omnipotence is perfectly coordinated with his other divine attributes.

name: Omnipotent
description: A transcendental manifestation of God the Ultimate as the overcontroller of time and space, exercising administrative functions beyond those of the Almighty Supreme.

name: omnipresence
description: The ability of the Universal Father to be everywhere present simultaneously, existing in numberless places at the same time. This divine attribute allows God to transcend spatial limitations while maintaining personal presence throughout all creation.

name: omniscience
description: The divine attribute of unlimited knowledge possessed by God, who knows all things universally and perfectly. God's omniscience encompasses awareness of all thoughts and events throughout creation, though this foreknowledge does not abridge creature free will.

name: Omri
description: A king of Israel who attempted to purchase Shemer's estate during a period of civil wars and resistance against Assyrian forces under Shalmaneser III.

name: Onagar
description: The first great spiritual leader of primitive humanity, born 983,323 years ago. He organized early tribes into the first high spiritual civilization on Earth and led them in worship of "The Breath Giver," establishing efficient tribal government that lasted until the arrival of the Planetary Prince.

name: Onamonalonton
description: A spiritual leader of the American red men who lived 65,000 years ago and brought temporary peace among the tribes while reviving their worship of "The Great Spirit." He lived to be ninety-six years old and established his headquarters among the redwood trees of California.

name: one-brained mortals
description: A classification of mortal beings with a single brain structure, representing one of the planetary types of evolutionary mortals described in the cosmic scheme of intelligent life.

name: One God
description: A book of thirty-one chapters written by Ikhnaton promoting monotheistic worship, which was later destroyed by Egyptian priests when they returned to power after his reign.

name: One High in Authority
description: A celestial being of high administrative rank and authority.

name: one hundred councils of supreme sanction
description: High advisory councils situated on Salvington whose presidents constitute Gabriel's working cabinet. These councils function as universe superlegislatures but operate without authority of enactment or power of execution, referring their findings to judicial bodies or legislative assemblies.

name: One Truth
description: The spiritual concept proclaimed by Singlangton to the yellow race about one hundred thousand years ago, representing the worship of truth as the supreme Deity. This progressive religion provided spiritual stimulus that advanced Chinese civilization, though later became overshadowed by reverence for established traditions.

name: ONE UNCAUSED
description: A designation referring to the absolute, self-existent nature of the ultimate source of all existence.

name: One without Name and Number
description: A celestial being belonging to the class of Those without Name and Number.

name: ooze
description: The primordial slime and ooze of ocean beds in ancient inland seas from which human life originally evolved. Man's primordial ancestors were literally this organic matter found in sluggish, warm-water bays and lagoons where the Life Carriers established life on Urantia.

name: Ophel
description: A location in Jerusalem through which religious processions passed during feast day ceremonies. Priests carrying golden water pitchers would march through Ophel to the fountain gate near Siloam as part of sacred temple rituals.

name: opiate
description: A deadening drug or influence that provides escape from reality. Jesus refused such substances and taught that true religion is not an opiate to the people but rather a powerful spiritual force that leads humanity upward and forward in growth and enlightenment.

name: opinions
description: Personal viewpoints or judgments formed about matters, often distinguished from established facts or revealed truth.

name: opium
description: A narcotic substance used medicinally by ancient peoples, particularly the Sumerians. Along with other secret remedies, opium lost its perceived power once its properties became widely known and understood.

name: opossums
description: Small, primitive mammals that flourished 45 million years ago during the rapid evolution of mammalian life on elevated continental backbones.

name: opportunities
description: Chances for growth, service, and achievement that arise through faithful living and alignment with divine will. Seeking God with a whole heart leads to deliverance from captivity and new opportunities for better living, while disappointments often become the foundation for greater blessings and renewed service.

name: oppression
description: The unfair exploitation and cruel treatment of the weak by the strong, which Jesus consistently condemned as incompatible with the ideals of the kingdom of heaven. While historical oppression like slavery taught industry and contributed to social development, only love and brotherhood can prevent the strong from oppressing the weak.

name: optimism
description: A positive outlook that, while serviceable for earthly living, differs from the divine peace that comes from knowing and doing God's will. Jesus displayed a controlled, divine optimism based on unwavering trust in the Father, but warned against blind, unreasoning optimism that hides failure under deceptive cheerfulness.

name: oracles
description: Prophetic revelations or divine messages believed to reveal the intentions of spirits, used alongside omens and divination in ancient religious practices. The Greeks believed in oracular advice, while Latin peoples consulted oracles during crises as part of their deeply rooted religious traditions.

name: oral law
description: Traditional Jewish legal interpretations and teachings passed down verbally alongside the written Torah, forming part of rabbinic Judaism's comprehensive legal system.

name: orange race
description: One of the six colored races characterized by their peculiar urge to build massive structures, including vast mounds of stone. They believed in transmigration and reincarnation, practiced naming children after ancestors, and experienced a great cultural revival under Porshunta's leadership 300,000 years ago at Armageddon before eventually being absorbed by other races.

name: oratory
description: The art of public speaking, which in the spirit world represents one of the rare treats with harmony of music and euphony of expression that inspires beyond description. Celestial artisans preserve thought for reproduction in the harmonious orations of higher worlds, far superior to the crude and stumbling speeches of earthly speakers.

name: ordeals
description: Primitive methods of detecting crime or determining guilt through trials of poison, fire, and pain, believed to be administered by ghosts through medicine men and priests. These crude techniques of arbitration were practiced by early tribes as a form of divine justice, though Jesus denounced such superstitious practices.

name: order
description: The harmonious arrangement and proper conduct that prevails throughout creation, from Paradise to earthly kingdoms. Jesus taught that maintaining temporal order in spiritual communities requires group wisdom and definite leadership, distinguishing between the spiritual methods of the kingdom of heaven and the physical force sometimes employed by material governments.

name: Ordovician
description: The Ordovician era was a geological period characterized by gradual land fluctuations and multiple major floods, during which various marine organisms evolved.

name: organisms
description: Organisms are inherently active participants in the dynamic relationship between selfhood and environment, functioning as active agents rather than passive mechanisms.

name: organs
description: The ancients believed the soul resided in various physical organs including the eye, liver, kidney, heart, and later the brain.

name: orientation
description: The sense of orientation in living beings is maintained by four directional control creatures at Salvington who operate over universe currents, working with microscopic direction cells in animal organisms that point north and south.

name: origin
description: The significance of origin is the paramount question regarding all relationships, ethics, and advancement in the cosmic realm, serving as the basis for the relational reaction of the Gods.

name: Original
description: The Original is the unqualified concept of the First Source and Center, that source manifestation of the I AM from which all reality originates, making possible the coexistence of actual, potential, and eternal aspects of existence.

name: Original Mother Son
description: A designation related to the Eternal Son in their original relationship with the Universal Father.

name: original sin doctrine
description: The doctrine of original sin teaches that Adam's transgression cursed the human race with inherited guilt, but The Urantia Book rejects this concept, teaching instead that guilt is purely a matter of personal sin and that mankind has no racial guilt before God.

name: originality
description: Society should foster originality to cultivate individualism and encourage initiative in a more civilized manner than militarism, while individuals should strive for originality while avoiding eccentricity.

name: Orlandof
description: Orlandof was a prince and teacher of the blue race who lived about 200,000 years ago and led a revival of learning and religion, guiding many tribes back to worshiping the true God as the "Supreme Chief."

name: ornamentation
description: Decorative items and artistic embellishments used by early humans, including the first necklaces made from preserved umbilical cords set with pearls, gold ornaments as humanity's earliest use of metal, and animal god engravings on various objects by the Andonites.

name: orphans
description: Children adopted into the fifty pattern homes of Dalamatia by Prince's corporeal staff, who were raised and trained for three years before being commissioned as emissaries to their respective tribes.

name: Orphic brotherhood
description: The best of the mystery cults in the Mediterranean region, distinguished by its moral preachments and promises of salvation, offering a respectable alternative to the orgiastic follies of other mystery religions.

name: Orvonon
description: A spiritual leader who brought enlightenment to the isolated indigo race in Africa, teaching them about "The God of Gods" and now serving as one of the twenty-four special counselors of Jerusem designated by Jesus.

name: Orvonton (Paradise satellite)
description: The seventh Paradise satellite of the Spirit, named Orvonton, whose executive facilities are dedicated to the efficient governance and administration of the seventh superuniverse.

name: Orvonton (superuniverse)
description: The seventh superuniverse of the grand universe, containing approximately one trillion inhabited or inhabitable worlds including Urantia. Orvonton encompasses the local universe of Nebadon and is characterized by its lavish bestowal of merciful ministry, representing a unique demonstration of love and mercy in cosmic evolution.

name: Osiris
description: Egyptian deity in mystery cult worship, depicted as the son of Isis who experienced death and resurrection. Souls of the departed were believed to pass through Osiris's judgment hall to be weighed in balances for admission to realms of bliss or consignment to hell.

name: ostriches
