description: Modern birds descended from enormous ancient reptiles, representing one of the greatest evolutionary leaps in prehuman history. Ancient ostrich-like birds grew to ten feet in height and laid eggs nine by thirteen inches, serving as ancestors to later gigantic passenger birds.

name: otters
description: Members of the weasel family that thrived and developed throughout the northern latitudes alongside martens, raccoons, and other small carnivorous mammals.

name: oudah
description: Religious beliefs and prayer practices of African Pygmies, representing an early evolutionary stage of spiritual development alongside the mana practices of Melanesians and manitou superstitions of North American Indians.

name: outer space
description: The regions beyond the seven superuniverses where future universes are expected to develop and evolve.

name: outerspacers
description: The future inhabitants of the outer space universes who may eventually become citizens of Havona and contribute to the maintenance of experiential equilibrium in the perfected superuniverses.

name: overcontrol
description: The universal oversight and balanced coordination of all creation by the experiential Deities and Absolutes, manifested through integrated energies, minds, and spirit influence.

name: overplanning
description: Excessive planning for the future that sometimes defeats its own high purpose by becoming an end in itself rather than a means to achieve worthy goals.

name: overpopulation
description: The condition of having too many inhabitants relative to available resources or living space.

name: overprotection
description: Excessive parental protection that injuriously exalts a child's concept of self-importance and prevents the development of independence, wisdom, and dependable character.

name: overrevelation
description: The provision of too much revealed truth beyond what recipients can properly understand or effectively utilize.

name: oversoul of creation
description: A designation referring to the Supreme Being as the experiential soul of all creation.

name: overspecialization
description: Excessive focus on a narrow field that breeds monotony, narrow-mindedness, and circumscribed thinking, threatening both individual development and civilizational progress.

name: Ovid
description: A Phoenician teacher who preached the Salem doctrines in Ur and converted Terah's family, including Abraham, to the Salem religion.

name: owls
description: Nocturnal birds of prey that were among the diverse modern bird species existing during the early development of avian life on the planet.

name: oxen
description: Large domesticated cattle used as beasts of burden and for agricultural work, first domesticated by Mesopotamian, Turkestan, and Chinese farmers around 5000 B.C. alongside other animals like camels, horses, and yaks.

name: oxygen
description: A vital atmospheric gas first generated by seaweed and other plant life, essential for respiration and life on Earth, initially absent from the primitive planetary atmosphere which contained mainly water vapor, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen chloride.

name: oysters
description: Bivalve mollusks that evolved millions of years ago and survived the long struggle of marine life development, continuing to exist much as they did in ancient seas alongside mussels, clams, and scallops.

name: ozone
description: A thin atmospheric layer about ten miles above Earth's surface that protects inhabitants from dangerous ultraviolet radiation while allowing beneficial ultraviolet rays to reach the surface, essential for vitamin production.

name: Pacific Ocean
description: The world's largest body of water, originally formed as a fresh-water ocean covering the entire planet before becoming salty, and later covering nine-tenths of Earth's surface after meteoric accumulation on the ocean floor.

name: pacifism
description: The doctrine or practice of rejecting violence and warfare as means of resolving conflicts.

name: pack
description: A burden or load to be carried, referenced in Jesus' teaching about going beyond the minimum required service by willingly carrying someone's pack a second mile to challenge unrighteousness.

name: paddle
description: A steering implement for boats, which Jesus helped construct during his stay in Caesarea when the vessel's original steering paddle needed replacement.

name: paganism
description: Religious practices combining Hellenic and Latin mythology with patriotism and tradition, representing spiritual systems that Jesus recognized required readiness and spiritual hunger before higher teachings could be effectively received.

name: pain
description: Pain is a universal human experience that serves essential evolutionary purposes. It drives the development of social mores and institutions as groups seek to avoid suffering while pursuing pleasure and power. Pain and suffering are necessary for progressive evolution, prompting biological adaptations and behavioral changes. While early humans were less sensitive to pain than later mortals, the experience remains fundamental to mortal existence until reaching Paradise, where eternal joy replaces all pain and sorrow.

name: Palatine Hill
description: One of the seven hills of Rome where the emperor's residence, the temple of Apollo, and the Greek and Latin libraries were located. Jesus regularly visited Palatine Hill during his stay in Rome, along with the forum and Capitolium, as part of his exploration of the city.

name: palavers
description: Primitive debate councils or discussion groups that were seldom effective for conducting affairs of war or peace. History demonstrated that armies led by groups of clan heads could not compete with those commanded by a single strong leader.

name: Paleozoic
description: The Paleozoic era, also known as the marine-life era, lasted 250 million years and ended with the Permian period. This era represents one quarter of planetary history and was characterized by the dominance of marine life forms.

name: Palestine
description: Palestine was the crossroads of three continents where multiple ancient civilizations met through trade and travel routes. During Jesus's time, it enjoyed peace and prosperity under Roman rule, with three prevalent languages: Aramaic among common people, Hebrew among priests, and Greek among educated classes. The region's central location and diverse climate, ranging from frigid mountains to torrid valleys, made it an ideal setting for both Melchizedek's and Michael's earthly missions.

name: Palisades of the Hudson
description: A geological formation along the Hudson River created by the extrusion of basalt lava between Triassic rock layers during a period of extensive worldwide volcanic activity.

name: Palm Sunday
description: The Christian commemoration of Jesus's triumphal entry into Jerusalem, when he was acclaimed by crowds waving palm branches. This event occurred on a Sunday when Jesus chose to perform his mightiest earthly work in nearby Bethany, the village that had accepted and believed in him.

name: palms
description: Palm trees that appeared during the Cretaceous period alongside other angiosperms and became abundant across Europe and North America. Palm branches were also used by crowds to express joy and fellowship when greeting Jesus during his approach to Jerusalem.

name: Palonia
description: A local system in Nebadon where the first system rebellion occurred under System Sovereign Lutentia. Michael served there for over seventeen years as a temporary ruler known as the Savior Sovereign, bringing order and justice to the confused system while offering mercy even to the rebellious predecessor.

name: Panama Isthmus
description: The Isthmus of Panama, which rose and fell twice during the Cenozoic age, alternately connecting and separating the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans and significantly affecting global geography and ocean circulation patterns.

name: Pandora
description: A mythological figure from ancient Greek tradition who, according to various cultural narratives, was blamed for bringing evil upon mankind, representing the historical tendency to distrust women and complicate understanding between the sexes.

name: Panoptia
description: A world in the system of Satania that remained loyal during the Lucifer rebellion under the guidance of Ellanora, a young mortal woman who led her people to support Michael. The loyal Panoptians now serve as caretakers and builders on the seventh Jerusem transition world and surrounding detention spheres.

name: panorama, exhibit (Jerusem)
description: A vast circular exhibit on Jerusem, five thousand standard miles in circumference, that presents an up-to-date depiction of the advancing status of all peopled worlds in the Satania system. The exhibit includes panoramic reign-records of planetary history, with Caligastia's records having been obliterated as part of Urantia's rehabilitation process.

name: Pantaenus
description: An early Christian teacher who instructed Clement at the Alexandria school and later followed Nathaniel to India, where he proclaimed Christ's teachings free from compromise.

name: Pantheism
description: A philosophical and religious concept that identifies God with the universe itself, which The Urantia Book considers problematic because it denies the personality of God and leads to difficulties in understanding prayer and divine relationship. During Europe's "dark ages," mysticism akin to pantheism thrived alongside declining institutional Christianity.

name: panthers
description: Large cats that evolved as part of the cat family tribe alongside saber-toothed tigers in North America during the mammalian age, representing one branch of the diversifying carnivorous species of that era.

name: Paphos
description: A coastal city in Cyprus where Jesus and Gonod landed during their Mediterranean tour, serving as their base for gathering supplies before retreating to the nearby mountains for several weeks of rest.

name: parable prayers
description: Additional forms of prayer that Jesus taught secretly to his apostles during his earthly ministry, many of which originated from other inhabited planets, though Jesus did not reveal this fact to the twelve.

name: parables
description: Jesus's primary method of teaching the masses, with the stories of the good Samaritan and the prodigal son being his favorites for illustrating the Father's love and human neighborliness. Parables enabled Jesus to present new spiritual truths while avoiding controversy, allowing listeners to find meaning according to their spiritual capacity and willingness to understand.

name: Paradise
description: The eternal, stationary Isle at the geographic center of infinity and the dwelling place of the Universal Father, Eternal Son, and Infinite Spirit. Paradise serves as the source and center of all physical gravity, spiritual energy, and personality circuits throughout the universe of universes, representing the absolute pattern of material reality and the ultimate destination for ascending mortals seeking divine perfection.

name: paradise apple
description: The paradise apple, or citron, was carried by pilgrims in their left hand during a morning ceremony at the temple, symbolizing the forbidden fruit.

name: Paradise arrivals
description: Ascendant mortals who reach Paradise with residential status and begin the progressive course in divinity and absonity, signifying they have found God and are to be mustered into the Mortal Corps of the Finality.

name: Paradise Central Shining
description: A being or manifestation associated with the central regions of Paradise.

name: Paradise Citizens
description: Over three thousand orders of superb beings resident on Paradise who are not directly concerned with perfecting ascending will creatures. They acquire capacity for Supremacy comprehension through observation and discovery, and many serve in various capacities including training creature-trinitized sons and assisting in finaliter assignments.

name: Paradise Companions
description: A composite group of angels recruited from seraphim, seconaphim, supernaphim, and omniaphim who are assigned as companions to beings who arrive alone on Paradise, particularly ascendant mortals. They serve temporarily until the individual joins associates or the Corps of Finality.

name: Paradise Corps of the Finality
description: One of seven finaliter corps overseen by the senior Master Architect, representing a mobilization of potentials, personalities, minds, spirits, and experiential actualities in preparation for future developments in outer space universes.

name: Paradise Creator Sons
description: Creator Sons who originate from or are associated with Paradise.

name: Paradise Deities
description: The three primary Deities of Paradise: the Universal Father, the Eternal Son, and the Infinite Spirit.

name: Paradise energy
description: The fundamental energy manifestations originating from or associated with Paradise.

name: Paradise force organizers
description: Force organizers who operate from or in association with Paradise in the organization of cosmic energies.

name: Paradise gravity
description: A fundamental cosmic force originating from Paradise that governs the attraction and control of matter and energy throughout the universe of universes.

name: Paradise-Havona system
description: The eternal Paradise-Havona System stands as the perfect nucleus of the master universe, with all seven superuniverses and regions of outer space revolving around this gigantic central aggregation of Paradise satellites and Havona spheres.

name: Paradise-Havona-trinitized Sons
description: Unique trinitized beings created by the collaborative efforts of Paradise Citizens and Havoners, representing high spiritual aspects of the Supreme Being that they acquired through supersupreme experience. These sons of destiny embody concepts, ideals, and experience that pertain to future universe ages and are held in reserve on Vicegerington under the care of the Architects of the Master Universe.

name: Paradise incarnation commission
description: A commission of seven unidentified Paradise personalities who facilitated Jesus' resurrection on April 9, A.D. 30, arriving at the tomb at two forty-five Sunday morning and overseeing his morontia awakening without any creature participation in the process.

name: Paradise Master Spirits
description: The Seven Master Spirits who originate from Paradise and serve as the primary expressions of the combined nature and functions of the Paradise Trinity, representing all possible combinations of the three Paradise Deities.

name: Paradise Sons of God
description: Divine beings of Paradise origin who serve as ministers, creators, bestowers, judges, teachers, and truth revealers throughout the universe of universes. They include three orders: Creator Sons, Magisterial Sons, and Trinity Teacher Sons, all dedicated to descending ministry and facilitating the Paradise climb of evolutionary creatures.

name: Paradise Source and Center
description: An alternative designation for the Universal Father, emphasizing his role as the original source and central focus of all reality emanating from Paradise.

name: Paradise Trinity
description: The eternal Deity union of the Universal Father, the Eternal Son, and the Infinite Spirit, constituting the only Deity reality embracing infinity. This existential Trinity serves as original Deity while functioning in absolute, transcendental, and finite capacities, providing the source from which all other divine reality springs.

name: paradox
description: A seemingly contradictory or impossible situation that reveals deeper truths about reality, particularly regarding the relationship between finite and infinite, unity and diversity, or God's simultaneous immanence and transcendence.

name: paralysis
description: The debilitating effect of constant fear of bad luck that prevented primitive peoples from working hard or taking initiative, as they became immobilized by dread of unfortunate outcomes.

name: paralytic at Bethsaida
description: A paralytic at Bethsaida sought healing from Jesus with unwavering faith and determination.

name: paranoids
description: A classification applied to some early priests who were deemed paranoiac due to mental and physical abnormalities that ancient peoples associated with spirit and devil possession.

name: parchment
description: A small piece of parchment nailed to Jewish doorposts containing religious text, which was customarily touched and kissed as part of a ritual when entering or leaving the house. Jesus objected to this practice at age twelve, pointing out its essentially idolatrous nature to his father Joseph.

name: parental commission
description: A commission of twelve parental couples on the first mansion world who assess whether survivors have adequate parental experience for continued ascension. Those who fail must gain this experience through service in the homes of Material Sons on Jerusem or in the probationary nursery.

name: parents
description: Intelligent and affectionate parents are never called upon to forgive their average and normal child, thanks to understanding relationships associated with love. Family life instills true morality and loyalty, stabilizing personality and nurturing growth through necessary adjustments, while also revealing the Creator's love to parents and children alike. No ascender can reach Paradise and attain the Father without experiencing parenthood, for it is fundamental to the concept of the Universal Father and vital for the training of all ascenders.

name: parrots
description: According to a New Guinea tribe's beliefs, the parrot is one of the animals from which they claim descent, reflecting ancient understanding of the evolutionary character of human progress.

name: Parsees
description: Followers of Zoroastrianism who consider the dog their sacred animal due to fetishistic beliefs, maintain fire worship symbolism from ancient times, and whose modern religious practices represent a significant departure from Zoroaster's original exalted teachings and noble psalms.

name: Parthia
description: An ancient kingdom that engaged in struggles with expanding Rome, ultimately leaving Syria under Roman control. Parthia was known for its manuscripts housed in Alexandria's great library and its rising power that contributed to the weakening of the Seleucids.

name: partiality
description: The practice of showing favoritism or bias toward certain individuals or groups over others.

name: partnership
description: Partnership quadruples achievement possibilities by enhancing personal potentials intellectually, socially, and spiritually. When mortal man enters into partnership with God through Adjuster fusion, no limit can be placed upon the destiny of such an unprecedented union.

name: parts and the whole
aliases: sum of two or more things
description: A fundamental cosmic principle describing the reciprocal relationship between individual components and their collective totality. The progress of the whole depends on the evolution of its parts, while the parts reflect the purposive growth of the whole. Every individual benefits from and suffers consequences of actions by others in their group, as survival depends on identification with and cooperation with the whole in fulfilling divine will.

name: passenger birds
description: Large, intelligent flying creatures used for transportation by early evolutionary humans on various planets. These enormous birds could carry one or two people nonstop for over 500 miles, possessed high intelligence, could speak many words, and were described as obedient and affectionate. On Urantia, the fandors served this purpose but became extinct over 30,000 years ago.

name: passivity
description: A negative approach to dealing with life situations that Jesus explicitly rejected in favor of active, positive responses. Jesus taught his followers to overcome evil with good through vigorous action rather than passive submission, emphasizing that righteousness comes from actively engaging with challenges rather than merely enduring them.

name: Passover
description: A Jewish religious observance and feast commemorating the Israelites' liberation from Egypt, when the angel of death 'passed over' their homes during the final plague.

name: passport
description: Attainment of the Universal Father serves as the passport to eternity, marking a momentous occasion on the pilot world of circle number three as creatures of space seek entry to Paradise through the portals of eternity.

name: past
description: While valuable for gleaning wisdom and providing restful contemplation of mastered achievements, the past should not be regarded as the exclusive source of truth. Truth is relative and expanding, living in the present moment. Spiritual progress requires the willingness to disown and move beyond past limitations to embrace greater realities.

name: pastoral living
description: A way of life centered around the herding and care of livestock, typically involving nomadic or semi-nomadic movement in search of grazing lands for animals such as sheep, goats, or cattle.

name: Patagonians
description: An indigenous people of Patagonia who, like the early Semites, practiced tree worship as part of their spiritual beliefs and customs.

name: patents
description: Legal protections for inventions and original creations. On a nearby inhabited planet, the federal government protects patents while taking 50% of profits from all inventions and creations as royalties to support and incentivize continued creativity and innovation.

name: paternalism
description: A practice or attitude of acting in a way that limits a person's liberty or rights, ostensibly for their own good, similar to how a father might act toward his children.

name: patience
description: A divine virtue that involves waiting with understanding and forbearance, especially in difficult circumstances. Patience is one of the fruits of the spirit manifested by spirit-led mortals, functioning as a prerequisite for mercy and spiritual growth. It requires time to develop and enables mortals to endure trials while trusting in divine timing and wisdom.

name: Patmos, Isle of
description: The island where the Apostle John was banished for four years, during which he learned wise conciliation and taught loving service over ruling power. While in exile on Patmos, John wrote the Book of Revelation, though it survives only in greatly abridged and distorted form.

name: Patrae
description: A city in Achaia where the Apostle Andrew was finally apprehended and crucified after journeying through Armenia, Asia Minor, and Macedonia, bringing many thousands into the kingdom. He continued proclaiming the gospel throughout two days on the cross before dying.

name: patriarchy
description: A family organizational system in which the father serves as the head and primary authority figure, representing one of the traditional forms of family structure in human social development.

name: patriotism
description: Devotion and loyalty to one's nation or homeland. Intelligent patriotism and national devotion are essential for lasting national civilization, but patriotism can also become problematic when it involves extreme nationalism or conflicts with spiritual principles, as demonstrated by the difficulties Jesus faced with his brother Jude's patriotic outbursts.

name: pattern
description: A master design or configuration of reality from which copies are made. Pattern can pervade personalities, entities, or matter but remains unchanged while only copies are multiplied. Paradise is the absolute of patterns, the Eternal Son is the pattern personality, and Havona serves as the pattern universe for all other creations.

name: Paul
description: The Apostle Paul of Tarsus, a Roman citizen who became the primary missionary to the gentiles and a major architect of early Christianity. Originally Saul, he was converted on the Damascus road and subsequently transformed Jesus' teachings into a new religion that blended Hebrew, Greek, and Roman elements, though his personal theological interpretations sometimes diverged from Jesus' original gospel message.

name: peace
description: A state of harmony and tranquility that exists in multiple forms: personal peace that integrates personality, social peace that prevents fear and conflict, and spiritual peace that comes from knowing God's love. True peace is not merely the absence of conflict but represents the positive presence of harmony, understanding, and divine assurance.

name: peacemakers
description: Those who actively work to create and maintain peace in personal, social, and political realms, whom Jesus declared would be called sons of God. Peacemaking involves preventing ruinous conflicts and serves as the cure for distrust and suspicion among individuals and groups.

name: pearl of great price
description: A parable teaching that represents the supreme value of the kingdom of heaven, for which one should be willing to sacrifice everything else. The pearl symbolizes the spiritual treasure of divine sonship and eternal life, which is worth giving up all material possessions and worldly attachments to obtain.

name: pearls
description: Pearls are mentioned metaphorically in Jesus' teaching about not casting pearls before swine, meaning not to offer sacred truths to those who would despise or trample them. Historically, the first necklace worn by man was made with a preserved umbilical cord set with pearls.

name: pearly nautilus
description: The modern pearly nautilus is a descendant of ancient cephalopods that evolved during prehistoric times and survived to the present day.

name: peat
description: Peat consists of the remains of past vegetable growth that could be converted into coal if subjected to proper pressure and heat.

name: peccaries
description: Peccaries are descendants of a small hoglike creature that evolved in North America during prehistoric times, becoming ancestors to many species of swine, peccaries, and hippopotamuses.

name: Pella
description: Pella was a significant location in Jesus' ministry, where John the Baptist baptized Jesus in the Jordan River. It later served as Jesus' final headquarters in Perea, where thousands gathered to hear him preach, and became a place of safety for believers who fled Jerusalem before its destruction.

name: penance
description: Penance represents the negative form of ritual renunciation that became incorporated into many religions. While often excessive, it taught early humans valuable self-control, contributing to social evolution through self-denial and discipline.

name: Pennsylvania
description: Pennsylvania contains ancient geological formations and coal beds that reveal Earth's prehistoric history, including evidence of multiple land movements and various fossils indicating past environmental conditions.

name: pensions
description: A system of financial support provided to individuals, typically upon retirement or in certain circumstances.

name: Pentecost (bestowal of Spirit of Truth)
description: Pentecost marked the bestowal of the Spirit of Truth upon all flesh, transforming the apostles and believers through intelligent self-surrender and consecration. This event ended religious discrimination based on race, gender, or social status, made demon possession impossible, and established a new spiritual era where the Spirit of Truth would dwell within and guide all truth-seeking believers.

name: Penuel
description: Penuel was one of the cities visited during the Perean mission conducted by the seventy messengers between January 3, A.D. 30 and Jesus' final journey to Jerusalem.

name: Pepi
description: An ancient Egyptian king mentioned in sun-worship texts, where his radiance symbolically acts as a celestial stairway for ascending souls to reach their divine mother.

name: Perea
description: A province in Palestine east of the Jordan River, equally populated by Jews and gentiles during Jesus' time. Known as the most beautiful and picturesque province of Palestine, it was called 'the land beyond the Jordan' and ruled by Tetrarch Philip, who offered Jesus protection from arrest there.

name: perfection
description: The ultimate goal of all creation, representing the divine mandate 'Be you perfect, even as I am perfect.' It encompasses both existential perfection found in Paradise-Havona and experiential perfection achieved through evolutionary ascension by will creatures.

name: Perfections of Days
description: Trinity-origin beings who govern the major sectors of each superuniverse as personal representatives of the Ancients of Days. There are exactly 210 of these rulers, with three assigned to each major sector to oversee intellectual and administrative matters.

name: Perfectors of Wisdom
description: Trinity-created beings who personify divine wisdom in the superuniverses, serving as advisors to the Ancients of Days. Exactly seven billion exist, with one billion assigned to each superuniverse to provide divine insight and administrative wisdom.

name: perfume
description: Fragrant substances used in various contexts throughout The Urantia Book, often associated with ceremonial or religious practices.

name: periodic table
description: The arrangement of chemical elements that displays a sevenfold pattern of recurring properties, reflecting the spiritual origin and sevenfold constitution of the material universe's ancestral energy.

name: periwinkles
description: Ancient gastropod mollusks that evolved alongside other shell animals in the early seas, representing part of the diverse marine life that has persisted through millions of years of evolution.

name: perjurers
description: False witnesses who gave testimony against Jesus during his trial, whose hatred and fanaticism caused their accusations to fall apart in their own contradictions when faced with Jesus' dignified silence.

name: permanent citizens
description: Residents of various spheres who maintain stable citizenship rather than progressing through the ascending career, serving important administrative and support functions in the universe hierarchy.

name: Permian
description: The Permian period, from 170 to 150 million years ago, marked the end of pivotal evolutionary development in marine life and the beginning of the transition to subsequent ages of land animals, characterized by great life impoverishment and near extinction of many species.

name: Perpetua
description: Perpetua was Peter's able wife who accompanied him on all his missionary journeys and excursions. She served as a leader in the enlarged women's corps, ministering fearlessly to the sick and afflicted throughout Perea, and ultimately met a tragic end when she was fed to wild beasts in the arena at Rome on the same day her husband was crucified.

name: persecution
description: Persecution refers to hostility and suffering faced by believers for their faith. Jesus warned his disciples they would face persecution, imprisonment, and even death for proclaiming the gospel. Early Christian persecution in Rome was often due to misunderstanding of the term 'kingdom' as a political threat, though such persecution ultimately strengthened and spread Christianity throughout the empire.

name: perseverance
description: The quality of continuing steadfastly in a course of action despite difficulties, opposition, or discouragement.

name: Persia
description: An ancient empire and region in what is now Iran, historically significant in Middle Eastern civilization and politics.

name: Persian Gulf
description: A body of water between Arabia and Iran where ancient Dravidian traders conducted commerce with Sumerian cities, importing alphabets and writing arts. The Persian Gulf region contained early settlements including Ur, which prospered during flood times and became a major pottery center, and holds submerged remnants of Dalamatia civilization beneath its waters.

name: Persians
description: The Persians freed the Jews from Babylonian captivity, though the Jews subsequently fell back into bondage under their own priestly laws and rituals. Like many ancient civilizations including Hindus, Greeks, Egyptians, and Romans, the Persians developed concepts of triad gods long before the revelation of the Paradise Trinity.

name: persistence
description: Persistence is the steadfast continuation of effort despite obstacles or discouragement. Achievement is predicated upon persistence in accordance with the Father's plan for finite progress and personality development. True believers persist in well-doing, undaunted by challenges, and persistence in prayer helps change one's earth attitude and enlarge the soul's capacity for spirit receptivity.

name: Personal Aids of the Infinite Spirit
description: The Personal Aids of the Infinite Spirit are beings created in trillions to assist exclusively the Paradise presence of the Third Person of Deity. Though stationed on Paradise, they flash throughout creation to execute the bidding of the Infinite Spirit, traversing space like Solitary Messengers but lacking discoverable personality form, making them all equal and identical in the eyes of the Conjoint Actor.

name: personality
description: Personality is a unique divine gift bestowed by the Universal Father that unifies all factors of individuality and reality. It is the one changeless reality in an otherwise ever-changing creature experience, functioning on multiple cosmic levels from finite to absolute. Personality is eternal, absolutely unique, and possesses the prerogative of choice, enabling beings to exercise free will in their relationship with God and the universe.

name: personality circuit
description: The divine circuit centered in the Universal Father through which all personalities throughout the universe maintain direct contact with God. This circuit enables the Father to have personal knowledge of all thoughts and acts across creation, transmits worship directly to the Father, and provides the means by which God maintains parental contact with all personal beings regardless of their location in the universe.

name: Personalized Adjusters
description: Thought Adjusters who have been granted personality by the Universal Father, typically after serving with incarnated Paradise Sons or achieving unusual distinction during mortal indwelling. These beings serve as planetary supervisors of Adjusters, directors of their kind on Divinington, and personal agents of the Universal Father's ministry throughout the universes.

name: persons
description: Individual beings to whom the Universal Father reveals his gracious and divine nature without discrimination. God is no respecter of persons, treating all spirit beings and mortal creatures equally, with his love extending to each individual regardless of their status, race, or circumstances.

name: perspective
description: A cosmic viewpoint that enables understanding of reality by considering origin, history, and destiny together rather than viewing events in isolation. True perspective reveals the eternal context of temporal experiences and provides the broader understanding necessary for spiritual growth and wisdom.

name: perspiration
description: The physical manifestation of Jesus's great anguish in Gethsemane, where perspiration rolled off his face in great drops as he faced his impending crucifixion and accepted that natural events would take their course.

name: persuasion
description: The preferred method for influencing minds and establishing truth, which Jesus taught should be used instead of compulsion or force. True persuasion respects human free will and follows the principle of treating others as you would want to be treated.

name: Peru
description: A South American region where traces of Andite blood reached the indigenous populations, representing one of the few places in the Western Hemisphere where contact with the more advanced racial strains occurred before the end of the first millennium after Christ.

name: pervaded space
description: The regions of space that are occupied by and subject to the presence and influence of various cosmic forces and energies, in contrast to unpervaded or empty space.

name: pessimism
description: A negative outlook that paradoxically proves the universe is not wholly material, since consciousness of values contradicts pure materialism. Pessimism often results from the pursuit of mere knowledge without the wisdom and spiritual insight that provide proper understanding.

name: pet evil
description: A cherished personal failing or attachment that nearly every human being must relinquish as part of entering the kingdom of heaven. This represents the individual sacrifice or surrender required for spiritual advancement, as illustrated by Matadormus's attachment to his wealth.

name: Peter
description: Peter was one of Jesus' twelve apostles, also known as Simon Peter, who played a prominent role in the early Christian movement.

name: petition
description: The inherent privilege of petition allows citizens to be heard in representative government and must progress to actual participation in the management of government.

name: petrified forests
description: Petrified forests formed when ancient land depressions were filled with erosion deposits and later elevated by underground lava flows during geological epochs.

name: phallic cult
description: The phallic cult emerged as a defense against the evil eye, with reproductive organs viewed as the only fetish capable of warding off its harmful influence.

name: Pharisee and publican
aliases: parable of the Pharisee and the publican
story of the Pharisee and the publican
description: The parable of the Pharisee and publican teaches that true happiness comes from humility and seeking spiritual wealth, contrasting self-righteousness with genuine repentance and faith.

name: Pharisees
description: A Jewish religious sect known for strict observance of ritual law, tithing, and purification practices, who often opposed Jesus' teachings due to their adherence to tradition and ceremonial requirements.

name: Pharos, lighthouse of
description: The lighthouse of Pharos in Alexandria was one of the seven wonders of the world and served as a metaphor for spiritual guidance when Jesus told Ganid he would be like this lighthouse, showing others the way to salvation.

name: Phasaelis
description: A Greek city where Jesus and the apostles gained valuable experience preaching to predominantly gentile populations including Greeks, Romans, and Syrians.

name: Phenix
description: A destination in the mountains that Jesus asked directions to while encountering a troubled youth, using the inquiry as an opportunity to offer spiritual guidance.

name: Philadelphia
description: A city in Perea where Abner led the largest group of believers who embraced Jesus' teachings, serving as the center of the Abnerian version of the kingdom of heaven and maintaining closer adherence to Jesus' original teachings than other early Christian communities.

name: philanthropy
description: The practice of charitable giving and benevolent acts toward humanity, aimed at promoting the welfare of others through generous donations or selfless service.

name: Philip (Apostle)
description: Philip was one of the twelve apostles, serving as the group's steward responsible for provisions and food. Known for his methodical reliability but lacking imagination, he was a successful personal worker who effectively used the invitation "Come and see" to bring others to Jesus. Despite his literal-mindedness and tendency to ask seemingly foolish questions, Philip remained loyal and was eventually martyred for his faith.

name: Philip (Tetrarch)
description: Philip the Tetrarch was the brother of Herod Antipas who ruled over territories including Caesarea Philippi. Unlike his brother, Philip became a reluctant believer in Jesus and allowed him to minister freely in his domain, even sending word that Jesus was welcome to live and work there.

name: Philistines
description: The Philistines were a people who frequently raided Palestine and engaged in conflicts with the Hebrews. Abraham made converts among them and was held in reverence as a spiritual leader. The Hebrews learned the alphabet from Philistine political refugees from Crete, and David later defeated them to establish his kingdom and gain control of caravan trade routes.

name: Philo of Alexandria
description: Philo of Alexandria was a wealthy Jewish philosopher who attempted to harmonize Greek philosophy with Hebrew theology. His teachings influenced Paul's Christian theology, particularly in restoring concepts of the Trinity, though Paul wisely eliminated many of Philo's inconsistencies. Philo was considered one of the greatest teachers of his era, ranking alongside figures like Moses and Buddha in his influence on ethical and religious thought.

name: philosopher in Ephesus
description: A learned Greek philosopher in Ephesus who engaged in profitable discussions with Jesus about the nature of the soul. Through their conversations, Jesus explained that the soul is the self-reflective, truth-discerning, and spirit-perceiving part of man that elevates humans above the animal world and represents the potential survival value of human experience.

name: philosopher’s stone
description: A legendary substance sought by ancient alchemists that was believed to have the power to transmute base metals into gold. The belief in the philosopher's stone, though mythical, led to the mastery of metals and contributed to scientific advancement.

name: philosophy
description: Philosophy is the search for wisdom and the systematic study of fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, values, and reality. It serves as a bridge between science and religion, helping to coordinate ideas and unify human understanding. True philosophy seeks to integrate the insights of material science, moral reasoning, and spiritual experience into a coherent worldview.

name: Phoenicians
description: The Phoenicians were a superior and well-organized group of mixed Semites who controlled the western Mediterranean coast of Palestine. They were skilled traders and seafarers who developed the alphabet system that the Hebrews later adopted. The Phoenicians practiced crucifixion as a form of execution and were among the peoples who received Jesus's teachings favorably during his ministry in their coastal regions.

name: photographers
description: A term used metaphorically to describe morontia recorders who function like group photographers of the transition worlds, preserving vital scenes and associations of progressive epochs in the archives of morontia halls of records.

name: Phrygian mystery cults
description: The Phrygian cult of Cybele and Attis conducted imposing but degrading ceremonies, culminating in the bloody Black Friday festival to honor the death and resurrection of Attis.

name: phyla
description: Only fourteen phyla have appeared on Urantia, with fishes being the most recent, and no new classes have emerged since birds and mammals.

name: phylacteries
description: Religious accessories worn by Jewish leaders that were broadened to display piety and seek honor from men, while the wearers secretly exploited widows and profited from the temple.

name: physical controllers
description: Celestial beings who manipulate and control physical energies and forces throughout the universe.

name: physical energy
description: The material force that underlies all physical phenomena and manifestations in the universe.

name: physical-energy manipulators
description: Physical-energy manipulators are celestial artisans who assist Morontia Power Supervisors in manipulating and controlling various phases of physical energy, knowledgeable about the three basic currents and thirty energy segregations of the superuniverses.

name: physical matter
description: The tangible, material substance that constitutes the physical universe and all its manifestations.

name: physical nature
description: The material or bodily aspects of existence, as distinguished from spiritual or mental aspects.

name: physicians
description: Medical practitioners who treat the sick, including Elman the Syrian physician who supervised the Bethsaida hospital and Luke who wrote a Gospel about Jesus.

name: physics
description: The science of matter and energy, whose laws are immutable but cannot predict emergent qualities or explain the concepts of truth, beauty, and goodness that transcend material phenomena.

name: piety
description: True piety is characterized by genuine spiritual devotion without sanctimoniousness or self-torture. Jesus exemplified pious humility perfectly balanced with practical loving service, demonstrating that cooperation with the divine spirit requires sincere dedication rather than mock religiosity.

name: pigeons
description: Pigeons are descendants of enormous ancient reptiles, representing one of the greatest evolutionary leaps in prehuman history when reptiles became birds. Joseph offered two young pigeons as a temple sacrifice for Mary's purification, following the prescribed offering for the poor.

name: Pikes Peak
description: Pikes Peak is an outstanding example of North American Rocky Mountain geological activity, representing remnants of original mountain ranges that were re-elevated after being leveled by erosion. The peak held its head above water during several prehistoric inundations, extending over multiple generations of mountain formation.

name: Pilate, Pontius
description: Pontius Pilate was the Roman governor of Judea who presided over the trial and crucifixion of Jesus.

name: pilgrim guardians
description: Pilgrim Guardians are morontia beings responsible for the entire morontia career of ascending mortals, serving as coordinators of all other morontia and transition ministers rather than being assigned to specific duties.

name: pilgrim helpers
description: Pilgrim helpers are secondary supernaphim who function on the seventh circle of Havona worlds, teaching ascending mortals about the Paradise Trinity, the Father-Son partnership, and the Infinite Spirit through comprehensive educational programs. They are beings of quick understanding and broad sympathy who welcome space ascenders to the central universe.

name: pilgrim receivers and free associators
description: Pilgrim Receivers and Free Associators are morontia companions who welcome new arrivals upon awakening on the mansion worlds and serve as social companions throughout the morontia journey. They remain with ascending mortals from initial resurrection until departure from the local universe as first-stage spirits.

name: pilgrimages
description: Pilgrimages to sacred shrines are ancient religious rituals that evolved into elaborate ceremonies of purification and sanctification, eventually developing into modern forms of religious worship and social ceremonials.

name: pilot
description: In the spiritual metaphor of human existence, the mind is the ship, the Thought Adjuster serves as the pilot, and the human will acts as captain. The divine pilot guides the ascending soul toward eternal survival when trusted by the mortal vessel's master.

name: pilot light
description: The pilot light refers to the characteristic spirit luminosity that accompanies the divine presence of Thought Adjusters. This Paradise luminosity is known by different names throughout the universes and represents the divine light that illuminates every person.

name: Pindar
description: A Greek poet of the fifth and sixth centuries who, along with other poets, attempted to reform Greek religion by elevating its ideals, but failed to develop a technique for fostering and conserving supreme values.

name: pines
description: Coniferous trees that became more like present-day varieties during the Jurassic age, largely replacing fern forests as part of an eventful biological transition toward modern tree varieties.

name: pint
description: A unit of measurement used metaphorically to illustrate that inherent capacities cannot be exceeded, just as a spirit concept cannot be mechanically forced into a material memory mold.

name: pirates
description: Maritime raiders who were cleared from the Mediterranean seas by Jesus' time, contributing to a great era of trade and travel throughout the unified Roman Empire.

name: Pisidia
description: A region in Asia Minor where Antioch was located, the home of Luke the physician who wrote his Gospel in A.D. 82 after learning about Jesus from Paul and others.

name: Pitcairn experiment
description: A successful racial blending of white men and Polynesian women that demonstrated the potential for creating new and biologically effective characteristics when good racial strains intermix.

name: pituitary glands
description: Endocrine glands that vary in number among different mortal races across worlds, with their chemical organization significantly influencing inherent imagination and spiritual receptivity.

name: pity
description: An emotional response involving compassion for another's suffering or misfortune.

name: placation
description: An early religious practice of appeasing ghosts and spirits to avoid their displeasure, which evolved from defensive worship techniques into more complex forms of religious ritual including coercion, propitiation, and eventually sacrifice and redemption.

name: Planetary Adams
description: Material Sons who serve as biological uplifters on evolutionary worlds, working to improve the genetic heritage of mortal races.

name: planetary commission of seventy
description: A commission of seventy beings on Edentia that approved the planetary life-initiation plans for Urantia proposed by the Life Carriers.

name: planetary council on art and science
description: A specialized council that was part of the Caligastia one hundred, dedicated to advancing artistic and scientific development on evolutionary worlds.

name: planetary custodians
description: Seventy guardians on each morontia world who constitute the local council of supreme morontia authority, granting material forms to ascending creatures and authorizing transitions between morontia phases without loss of consciousness.

name: planetary epochs
description: Seven successive ages of human development on evolutionary worlds, determined by the arrival of divine Sons: pre-Planetary Prince, post-Planetary Prince, post-Adamic, post-Magisterial Son, post-Bestowal Son, post-Teacher Son, and the era of light and life.

name: planetary helper seraphim
description: Seraphim assigned to accompany Planetary Adams on evolutionary worlds, typically numbering about 100,000, who assist in the physical and intellectual upliftment of material races and maintain headquarters on system capitals.

name: planetary physical types
description: Classifications of mortals based on their physical characteristics and environmental adaptations across different planetary conditions and atmospheres.

name: Planetary Prince of Urantia
description: Originally Caligastia, who was deposed after joining the Lucifer rebellion; now Michael of Nebadon holds this title, with Machiventa Melchizedek serving as vicegerent Planetary Prince.

name: Planetary Princes
description: Secondary Lanonandek Sons who serve as rulers of evolutionary worlds, commissioned by System Sovereigns to guide primitive mortals through early civilization and prepare planets for subsequent divine Sons.

name: planetary sentinels
description: Midwayers who serve as guardians and observers of the invisible spirit realm of planets, patrolling and monitoring supernatural phenomena and communications.

name: Planetary Sovereigns
description: The elevated status of Planetary Princes upon the inauguration of the era of light and life, marking their advancement to supreme planetary authority after successfully guiding their worlds through all evolutionary epochs.

name: planets
description: Material spheres that orbit suns and serve as dwelling places for diverse orders of intelligent life throughout the universe. All mortal-inhabited worlds are evolutionary in origin and serve as training schools for ascending beings on their journey toward Paradise. Each inhabited planet is presided over by a Planetary Prince and governed within a local system under a System Sovereign, with ideal planets being cold accretion worlds in proper relationship to their sun.

name: plans
description: Theoretical or validated schemes for action that guide personal and cosmic development. While positive decisions represent validated plans of action, mere ideas serve as theoretical plans, and stereotypes function as unvalidated accepted plans. True satisfaction comes from skillful execution rather than overplanning, and spiritual growth involves learning to make fewer personal plans concerning others while faithfully performing one's duties.

name: plants
description: Living organisms that played a crucial role in planetary evolution and human civilization development. The most significant evolutionary advances included the development of chlorophyll-making ability and the evolution from spores to complex seeds. Association with plants through agriculture fostered peace-loving cultures, instilling patience, quiet, and peace in contrast to the militant nature of hunting and herding societies.

name: plasm
description: The fundamental life substance that contains the full potential for all future evolutionary developments and variations on inhabited worlds.

name: platinum
description: A precious metal that the early Andonites actively sought and valued, traveling great distances to find it much as modern humans journey to find gold and diamonds.

name: Plato
description: Greek philosopher who, along with Socrates and Aristotle, taught that virtue is knowledge and that it is better to suffer injustice than to be guilty of it. Like Confucius, Plato was a philosopher rather than a religious teacher, though his idealistic concepts significantly influenced later religious thought.

name: Platonism
description: Greek philosophical system that profoundly influenced the gentile world and early Christianity through its emphasis on ideal spiritual patterns underlying material reality. Platonic concepts of the eternal order and the material world as shadows of spiritual realities were incorporated into Christian doctrine, making the teachings more acceptable to diverse audiences throughout the Mediterranean world.

name: play
description: A form of recreation and spiritual recharging that continues throughout the ascending career from mortal worlds to Paradise. The principles of play provide philosophical soundness and serve as a basis for humor, diversion, and the recalling of past experiences that enrich present existence.

name: play builders
description: Celestial artisans who construct enormous edifices used during periods of rest and recreation on the morontia worlds, providing suitable settings for the training and spiritual recharging of ascendant beings.

name: pleasures
description: Physical and spiritual satisfactions that serve legitimate purposes when properly balanced but become destructive when elevated to absolute values. While mortals are entitled to physical pleasures and human affections, spiritual maturity involves substituting higher meanings for personal pleasure and recognizing that lasting satisfaction comes from divine rather than material realities.

name: Pleistocene
description: The Pleistocene ice age occurred from 2 million to 100,000 years ago, shaping the landscape and climate of northern regions.

name: Pliocene
description: The Pliocene era, spanning 10 to 1 million years ago, was marked by preglacial land elevation in North America, Europe, and Asia, with significant topographical changes including the birth of mountain ranges, altered stream courses, and volcanic activity worldwide.

name: plough
description: A farming implement used metaphorically by Jesus to illustrate the commitment required for discipleship, teaching that one cannot turn back once they have put their hand to the plough and committed to serving the kingdom.

name: plumbing
description: An advanced technology used by skilled Andite emigrants who migrated to Crete around 12,000 B.C., demonstrating their superior craftsmanship alongside textiles, metals, pottery, and stone building.

name: poetry
description: A form of artistic expression that serves as mortal morontia, representing humanity's attempt to transcend material realities and embrace spiritual values. Poetry can discern beauty in commonplace existence and provides rhythmic relief to profound philosophy, balancing the strain of living through its connection to spiritual contemplation.

name: poets
description: Celestial artisans known as rhythm recorders who transcend earthly poets, creating rhythmic forms that capture beauty and harmony while increasing efficiency and pleasure for morontia and spirit beings.

name: pointing bow
description: A magical weapon believed by primitives to cause sickness and death through bewitchment, along with the evil eye, making it dangerous and ill-mannered to point at someone.

name: poise
description: A quality of tranquil stability and mysterious composure displayed by believers, characterized by emancipation from stress and the ability to maintain tranquility despite physical suffering, injustice, and life's vicissitudes.

name: poisons
description: Substances that were revered as fetishes in ancient times and used in primitive ordeals for crime detection, early warfare with poisoned weapons, and court intrigue. Both physical and mental poisons hinder the Thought Adjuster's efforts to elevate the material mind and impede spiritual progress of the soul.

name: polarity
description: A spiritual characteristic that requires certain ministering spirits to work in complemental pairs to function effectively, enabling them to synchronize with specific spiritual circuits and perform coordinated actions.

name: Pole Star
description: The North Star that served as a celestial guide in ancient Egyptian beliefs, where the sloping entrance passage of the great pyramid pointed directly toward it so that the soul of the dead king could travel straight to the stationary constellations of the fixed stars.

name: poles, magnetic
description: The magnetic poles of a planet or celestial body, relating to the planet's magnetic field properties.

name: poles of space communication
aliases: place of the spiritual polarity of the planet
universe energy pole of the planet
description: Designated planetary locations that serve as communication centers for celestial beings and interplanetary transport, functioning as the spiritual polarity points where space reports are received at noon and seraphic transports depart at midnight.

name: police
description: Law enforcement organizations that evolved from primitive shamans and secret societies into organized state forces responsible for maintaining internal peace, preventing violence, and preserving social order through organized cooperation and regulation.

name: politics
description: The activities, processes, and structures related to governance and the exercise of authority in organized societies.

name: polyandry
description: A form of marriage in which one woman has multiple husbands simultaneously, practiced among some primitive societies.

name: polygamy
description: A marriage system in which one person has multiple spouses simultaneously, including both polygyny and polyandry.

name: polygyny
description: A form of marriage in which one man has multiple wives simultaneously, commonly practiced among primitive peoples.

name: Polynesia
description: A Pacific Ocean region consisting of numerous islands that were more extensive in ancient times, inhabited by racially diverse peoples including descendants of Andite sailors who traveled from Japan and established flourishing centers of civilization.

name: polytheism
description: The worship of multiple gods, which represents an evolutionary stage in religious development that serves as a transitional step toward monotheism and the eventual recognition of one universal Father.

name: Pontius Pilate
description: Roman procurator who governed Judea, Samaria, and Idumea for ten years under Tiberius Caesar. Known as a moral coward and weak administrator who feared the Jewish leaders due to past confrontations over religious matters. He presided over Jesus' trial and reluctantly ordered his crucifixion despite finding no fault in him, later falling into disfavor and committing suicide.

name: pool of Bethesda
description: A natural hot spring in Jerusalem with reddish-tinged water surrounded by five porches where many sick people gathered seeking healing. The pool's periodic bubbling, caused by underground gas accumulations, was believed to have supernatural healing properties.

name: poor
description: Those lacking material wealth who Jesus blessed because they were usually sincere and pious, and who readily accepted the gospel of the kingdom. Jesus taught that followers should minister to and share with the poor, noting that 'the poor are always with us.'

name: poor in spirit
description: A spiritual condition of humility and recognition of one's need for God, representing those who are spiritually receptive and open to divine guidance rather than self-reliant or proud.

name: poor man falsely accused
description: An unnamed individual whom Jesus defended before a Roman magistrate, making a powerful speech about justice and the sacred duty to protect even the humblest citizens. The man was subsequently released due to Jesus' intervention.

name: populace
description: The common people or masses, often described as easily led and unthinking. In Jerusalem, they initially welcomed Jesus but later, influenced by religious leaders, clamored for his crucifixion and the release of Barabbas during his trial before Pilate.

name: population
description: The number of inhabitants in a given area, governed by the law that it varies directly with land resources and inversely with standard of living. Optimum population stabilization enhances culture and prevents war, while overpopulation can lead to social problems and conflict.

name: pork
description: Meat from swine, which was considered unclean and forbidden under Jewish dietary laws, making it a significant cultural and religious issue in Jesus' time.

name: Porogia
description: One of the local systems neighboring Satania in the constellation of Norlatiadek. Like other systems in the constellation, Porogia is evolutionary and progressive in nature.

name: Porphyreon
description: A coastal town where Jesus and the apostles stayed during their ministry in the Phoenician region. The gentile population there received them well, with many accepting the gospel message during their week of teaching.

name: porpoises
description: Marine mammals that evolved from placental mammals that returned to the sea, along with whales, dolphins, seals, and sea lions.

name: Porshunta
description: Oracle and master mind of the extinct orange race who led a great revival of higher living at Armageddon some three hundred thousand years ago and serves as one of 24 counselors in the worship of "The Great Teacher."

name: Portalon
description: A neighboring universe to Nebadon in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: porters
description: Human burden bearers used for carrying loads, with one ruler of the blue race having had a colony of one hundred thousand such workers.

name: position
description: A location, status, or role held by an individual or entity.

name: positive
description: A constructive and affirmative approach to life and faith that always triumphs over doubt, fear, and negative thinking through spiritual certainty and action-oriented love.

name: possession
description: The state of being controlled or owned by another entity, whether material ownership or spiritual domination.

name: possessions
description: Material goods and property owned by individuals, which disciples must renounce to follow Jesus, as true life consists not in abundance of things but in spiritual realities and eternal values.

name: post-Adamic era
description: The planetary epoch that follows the arrival and ministry of the Adamic Sons, representing an advanced stage of planetary spiritual and biological development.

name: post-Bestowal Son era
description: The planetary epoch that follows the completion of a Creator Son's bestowal mission, marking an advanced stage of spiritual enlightenment and planetary progress.

name: post-Magisterial Son era
description: A planetary epoch that follows the dispensation of a Magisterial Son, representing an advanced stage in a world's spiritual and administrative development.

name: post-Planetary Prince era
description: A planetary epoch that follows the dispensation of a Planetary Prince, marking the transition to a more advanced stage of world administration and development.

name: post-Teacher Son era
description: A planetary epoch that follows the dispensation of Trinity Teacher Sons, representing the final stage of a world's evolutionary development before the age of light and life.

name: postulates
description: Basic assumptions or principles taken as given without proof, serving as foundational premises for theoretical frameworks or logical reasoning.

name: Potential
description: That which is becoming and will be, representing the unrealized possibilities inherent in existence, contrasted with the Actual which was and is.

name: potentials
description: Unrealized possibilities that exist within the Deity Absolutes and cosmic reality, which become actualized through the influence of transcendental agencies and the actions of the Paradise Trinity. In evolving universes, potential is always supreme over the actual, representing what is to be as the unfolding of Deity's purposive mandates.

name: potholes
description: Distinctive geological features created by glacial action, representing one of the characteristic pieces of evidence left by ice movement that distinguishes glacial phenomena from other terrestrial activities.

name: potters
description: Craftspeople who specialized in making pottery, representing some of the first industrial specialists in human society. Women typically made plain pottery while men created fancy pottery, though these roles varied among different tribes.

name: pottery
description: Clay vessels and decorative objects that were among humanity's earliest manufactured goods, first introduced by the Prince's staff about 500,000 years ago and later revived during Adam's time. Pottery making became a significant cultural indicator and trade commodity, spreading across civilizations and helping drive urban development.

name: poultry
description: Domesticated birds, particularly chickens and roosters, which played significant roles in ancient superstitions and religious symbolism. Roosters were believed to ward off spirits with their crowing and were used as weather vanes, while Jesus used the metaphor of a hen gathering her brood to describe his protective care for Jerusalem.

name: pounds
description: The parable of the pounds, spoken exclusively to apostles, emphasized the importance of faithfulness and diligence in handling one's entrusted responsibilities.

name: poverty
description: Poverty is humanity's natural and tyrannical estate that can only be overcome through labor, knowledge, and organization. In advanced civilizations settled in light and life, poverty and social inequality have virtually vanished, while culture never develops under conditions of poverty as leisure is essential for civilization's progress.

name: power
description: Power is ordinarily limited to the designation of the electronic level of material or linear-gravity-responsive matter in the grand universe, though it is also employed to designate sovereignty. The term encompasses physical energy systems, human authority, and divine endowments used for spiritual purposes rather than self-glorification.

name: power centers
description: Celestial beings responsible for regulating and controlling various forms of energy and power throughout the universe systems.

name: power supervisors
description: Celestial beings who oversee and direct the operations of power and energy systems in universe administration.

name: power transformers
description: Specialized celestial beings who modify and convert various forms of energy and power for specific universe functions.

name: Powers of Paradise
description: Divine powers and authorities emanating from Paradise that govern and sustain the universe of universes.

name: practicalities
description: The practical affairs and details of daily earthly life that are managed through cooperation with spirit personalities originating from the Third Source and Center, the Infinite Spirit.

name: praetorium
description: An addition to the fortress of Antonia where Pilate and his wife made their headquarters when in Jerusalem, and the location where Jesus' trial before Pilate took place.

name: praise
description: Recognition and commendation that should be given judiciously to encourage others, while avoiding flattery and insincerity. Jesus remained uninfluenced by praise, living independently above human opinion, and taught his followers to seek only honest recognition that belongs to them.

name: Prajapati
description: The deity-father principle in Vedic religion, also known as Prajapati, was a key figure in henotheistic developments leading towards evolved monotheism. Sometimes termed Brahma, Prajapati was often exalted as the father-head of the entire pantheon but was later submerged in theological battles between Brahman priests and Salem teachers.

name: prayer
description: Prayer is sincere and trusting communication between the spiritual nature of the creature and the spirit of the Creator. It is the technique whereby mortals can effectively approach the threshold of communion with their Maker, ranging from childlike pleas to mature entreaties for moral growth and spiritual power. True prayer is both moral and religious, serving as a stimulus to spiritual growth and a means of aligning human will with divine will.

name: prayer of identification
description: The prayer of identification is expressed by a Master Spirit in the presence of the Infinite Spirit during the stage of physical creation. This prayer first differentiates the entity of the subsequent Creative Spirit from the person of the Infinite Spirit, marking the beginning of the Creative Spirit's distinct identity before being assigned to accompany a Creator Son.

name: pre-Sumerians
description: An ancient people who preceded the Sumerians in Mesopotamia.

name: preaching principles
description: Guidelines and methods for proclaiming the gospel and spiritual teachings.

name: precedent
description: Judges use precedent to adapt laws to evolving societal conditions, balancing tradition with progress. This legal principle represents the effort of judges to adapt written laws to changing conditions of society, providing for progressive adaptation while maintaining the impressiveness of traditional continuity.

name: precipice
description: Jesus would have effectively disarmed his violent enemies, but a tactical blunder by one of his own apostles led to hirelings trying to shove him off a precipice. Jesus also decided to exercise normal watchcare over his human safety and refrain from superhuman intervention, even when faced with the temptation to cast himself off the precipice before him during his wilderness contemplations.

name: predestination
description: While mortals are subjects of predestination, they have the freedom to reject any part of the divine plan. Man's eternal personality is bestowed by God's choice, with the option of participating in the foreordained cycle of attaining Deity destiny. The supreme correlation of all past, present, and future choice does not negate free will but rather reveals the predetermined course of the universe while respecting human sovereignty of choice.

name: predictability
description: The universe is highly predictable in quantitative terms but becomes increasingly unpredictable as we encounter complex interassociations of physical, intellectual, and spiritual energies unified by personality. While the reactions of the spirit-gravity circuit are predictable, the actions of mind and personality are not, especially when influenced by the presence and performance of the Ultimate and Absolutes.

name: preference
description: The apostles' desire for positions of greatness in the kingdom of heaven caused Jesus to repeatedly remind them of the importance of humility and service over worldly preferment. Jesus taught that spiritual greatness consists in understanding love rather than enjoying the exercise of material power, and urged his followers to refrain from seeking preference between groups and to strive to be as brethren in the kingdom of believers.

name: pregnancy
description: Primitive peoples believed pregnancy was the result of spirits entering women, not understanding the connection between sexual relations and childbirth.

name: prejudice
description: Deep-seated attitudes that blind the soul to truth and hinder spiritual progress. Prejudice is linked to selfishness and can only be eliminated through sincere devotion to causes greater than oneself and service to others.

name: prelife era
description: The Archeozoic era, extending over the initial 450 million years of Earth's development, from the time the planet attained its present size until life was established, before land had begun to appear.

name: prepersonal entities
description: Entities that exist before achieving personality status, representing a stage in the development toward full personhood.

name: presence circuits
description: Spiritual communication pathways that facilitate divine presence and connection throughout the universe.

name: presence embellishers
description: Celestial artisans who arrange supermaterial beings as living musical notes, odors, and sights to create anthems of glory, dramatizing the significance of relationships through positional values.

name: presence of God
description: The divine consciousness and spiritual awareness that permeates existence and can be experienced by mortal beings.

name: present
description: The current moment that gains true meaning only when interpreted in light of both past and future, and becomes less trivial when viewed within the circle of eternity.

name: presentiments
description: Intuitive feelings or premonitions about future events, often serving as spiritual leadings or guidance.

name: pressure
description: External forces that can develop stability and certainty when properly managed, but do not save or stimulate spiritual growth, which springs from within the evolving soul.

name: prestige
description: The concept of prestige relates to respect and admiration based on achievements, status, or reputation.

name: presumption
description: Presumption is dangerous spiritual arrogance that leads to recklessness and self-destructive behavior, characterized by overconfidence without proper foundation. Jesus warned against presuming upon the Father's love, as God does not condone reckless or sinful practices, and true faith must be free from such presumptuous attitudes.

name: pretense
description: The concept of pretense involves false claims or deceptive appearances intended to create a misleading impression.

name: preventive detention
description: Preventive detention is a judicial practice used on a nearby inhabited planet where individuals believed to be potential murderers and major criminals are sentenced to life service in detention colonies. Those who demonstrate improvement may be paroled or pardoned, resulting in a remarkably low homicide rate of only one percent compared to other nations.

name: pride
description: Pride is a dangerous spiritual attitude characterized by self-importance and arrogance that obscures one's relationship with God. While some forms of pride in family, reputation, or nation can be commendable if not excessive, spiritual pride is deceitful, intoxicating, and leads to downfall. It represents the greatest threat to spiritual integrity and prevents proper recognition of one's place in the divine order.

name: priests
description: Priests are religious leaders who evolved from shamans to become directors of worship and guardians of religious traditions. They serve as intermediaries between worshippers and the divine, conducting rituals and maintaining religious institutions. While they have contributed to civilization's stability and cultural enhancement, they can also become obstacles to spiritual progress when ritual replaces genuine religious devotion.

name: priest’s title
description: A priest's title refers to a form of early property ownership where land was consecrated by priests and protected by religious taboos. Owners of such consecrated property were said to have a "priest's title," with stone markers and initialed trees serving as boundary indicators under spiritual protection.

name: primary associators
description: Primary Associators are Master Physical Controllers who function as living catalytic agents, converting space energies into physical states and producing primitive units of material existence. They operate at extreme temperatures incompatible with ordinary life and serve as masterly energy conservators, storing energy during periods of abundance and releasing it when needed.

name: primary eruption
description: The primary eruption is a tremendous spiritual flash that occurs during the personalization of a local universe Mother Spirit. This Paradise phenomenon marks the transformation of the Creator Son's impersonal Spirit consort into a personal creative partner and is accompanied by a marked change in the creative spirit presence throughout the local universe.

name: Primary Eventuated Master Force Organizers
description: Primary Eventuated Master Force Organizers are a specialized order of Master Force Organizers involved in the fundamental organization and manipulation of cosmic forces and energies.

name: Primates
description: The Primates were the direct animal ancestors of the human race who emerged as the third vital mutation from mid-mammal stock approximately one million years ago. More human than animal, they possessed skeletal proportions similar to primitive humans, walked upright, and had developed hands and feet, though they remained subject to fear and did not yet possess truly human minds. The Primates became extinct within five thousand years after the first humans appeared, serving as the last vital link in human evolution.

name: primitive mortals
description: Early human beings in the initial stages of mortal development and civilization.

name: primordial force
description: The original or fundamental force from which other forms of energy and matter derive.

name: prince of darkness
description: A title referring to Caligastia, the fallen Planetary Prince of Urantia, who along with Lucifer and Satan worked to orchestrate Jesus's betrayal through Judas Iscariot.

name: prince of devils
description: A title associated with evil spiritual beings or fallen celestial personalities.

name: Prince of Peace
