description: A title for Jesus Christ Michael, emphasizing his mission to reveal a God of love and establish a kingdom of spiritual understanding rather than earthly dominion. Though the bestowal Son traditionally brings peace to worlds, Urantia did not experience the usual reign of peace due to its irregular planetary status.

name: Prince of Salem
description: The local title for Machiventa Melchizedek during his incarnation on Urantia, who presided over a small colony of truth seekers at Salem and served as a teacher to Abraham during a period of spiritual darkness.

name: Prince of this world
description: A title referring to the planetary ruler or dominant spiritual authority over a world, typically associated with Caligastia in the context of Urantia.

name: Prince’s corporeal staff
description: The physical, material assistants who served alongside a Planetary Prince, likely referring to the one hundred modified beings who accompanied Caligastia to Urantia.

name: printing
description: The art and technology of reproducing text and images, which revolutionized the dissemination of knowledge and greatly accelerated cultural development. Though printing appeared early in China, its impact was limited by the cumbersome ideographic writing system, while its later widespread adoption dramatically increased the pace of human progress and civilization.

name: Priscilla
description: A Jewish tentmaker who, along with her husband Aquila, embraced Paul's teachings and provided him with lodging and work during his extended stay in Corinth.

name: prison worlds
description: Seven detention spheres surrounding Jerusem in Satania, serving as confinement for Lucifer and his followers from the rebellion. These worlds stand as a solemn warning throughout Nebadon that sin carries the seeds of its own destruction.

name: prisoners
description: Individuals held in captivity or confinement. The refinement of warfare included taking prisoners rather than killing all enemies, with exemptions eventually extended to women and noncombatants. Jesus offered compassionate words of hope and divine forgiveness to condemned prisoners.

name: privacy
description: The concept of personal space and confidentiality. The Paradise Creators respect the privacy and sanctity of personality even in their lowly creatures, while primitive societies had no privacy as public opinion regulated all behavior.

name: private property
description: The right to own and control individual possessions. Private property originally consisted of personally touched items and evolved from communal ownership, bringing increased liberty and stability but also creating social classes of slaves, serfs, and landless people.

name: privilege
description: Special rights or advantages granted to individuals or groups. To possess and uphold privilege without abuse is a mark of high civilization.

name: probationary nursery
description: Educational facilities on the finaliter worlds where children who died before making spiritual choices are raised and trained. These nurseries provide opportunities for young souls to develop and eventually choose whether to pursue the Paradise path of perfection.

name: problem solving
aliases: more difficult problems to be solved
description: The process of identifying and resolving difficulties through systematic analysis and action. Effective problem solving requires freeing the mind from bias and prejudice, often benefiting from periods of rest and worship to gain fresh perspective and clearer insight.

name: Proclamation of Equality
description: Michael of Nebadon's final declaration acknowledging the Creative Mother Spirit as his equal co-ruler, establishing the transcendent pattern for family organization and voluntary marriage throughout his universe.

name: procrastination
description: The tendency to delay or postpone action. The morontia career works to permanently eradicate this animal vestige, teaching that postponement cannot be used to avoid disagreeable obligations after mortal life.

name: prodigal son
description: A parable told by Jesus about a wayward son who leaves his father's house, wastes his inheritance, and returns home to find complete forgiveness and restoration. The story illustrates the Father's willingness to receive all who seek entrance into the kingdom of heaven and demonstrates how a loving father can long wait for the repentance of an erring child.

name: profit
description: The legitimate gain earned through fair trade and commerce. Jesus endorsed honest profit as the rightful reward for merchants and traders, but condemned unfair profiteering and exploitation of the vulnerable. Present-day profit-motivated economics must be augmented by service motives to avoid the destructive effects of ruthless, self-serving competition.

name: progress
description: The universal watchword reflecting divine providence's consistent support for advancement in both temporal and spiritual realms. Progress is characterized by increasing personality freedom through self-understanding, voluntary self-restraint, and faith. It occurs gradually through evolutionary stages rather than sudden revolutionary changes, with the certainty of Godward direction being more important than rapidity.

name: progress angels
description: A category of celestial beings dedicated to fostering advancement and development, though specific details about their functions are not provided in the available source material.

name: progress helpers
description: Spiritual midway creatures who serve as assistants to various orders of seraphim functioning in special groups on planets, representing the more spiritually advanced among the midway beings.

name: proletariat
description: The working class or laboring population, referenced in discussions of social and economic structures though not extensively detailed in the available source material.

name: Prometheus
description: A figure from ancient mythology who allegedly stole fire from heaven. This legend replaced the simpler historical account of Andon's discovery of fire through flint, representing humanity's tendency to create supernatural explanations for natural phenomena beyond their understanding.

name: Promised Land
description: A concept referring to the land promised by God to his chosen people, though specific details are not provided in the available source material.

name: promises
description: Commitments or pledges that should be honored once made. Jesus taught that promises should be kept even at personal cost, while ancient cultures often required tangible guarantees beyond mere promises, particularly in marriage arrangements, due to their distrust of love and verbal commitments alone.

name: promoters of oratory
description: Celestial artisans who specialize in preserving and reproducing thoughts through the art of speech. They possess the remarkable ability to condense a lifetime of mortal experience into a brief address in the language of Nebadon, demonstrating advanced techniques of thought compression and communication.

name: prophecies
description: Divine revelations or predictions about future events. The Imports of Time serve as the secret to prophecy by revealing the necessary time element for any endeavor. Prophecies include John the Baptist's pronouncements about Jesus, Melchizedek's covenant promises to Abraham about Canaan, and various predictions about humanity's future spiritual development.

name: prophetic artists
description: Celestial artisans and heavenly reproducers who project the meanings of history into the future as part of their divine creative work.

name: prophets
description: Individuals who receive and proclaim divine revelations or teachings from God. Early humans worshiped prophets as beings indwelt by the gods, while Jesus taught his followers to aspire to become living prophets of the Most High. Throughout history, prophets have been persecuted and killed for their messages, yet they serve as essential messengers who reveal God's will and righteousness to their generations.

name: propitiation
description: Early religious practices aimed at appeasing or winning favor from spirits and gods through offerings, sacrifices, or ceremonies. These primitive attempts evolved from negative avoidance rituals to positive efforts involving praise, gifts, and entertainment. The concept of propitiating an angry God through blood sacrifice represents a barbarous and primitive religious philosophy unworthy of an enlightened age.

name: proportions
description: Mathematical or comparative relationships between different quantities, sizes, or elements used to understand scale and relative measurements.

name: propriety
description: The quality of being proper, appropriate, or socially acceptable in behavior and conduct. Jesus demonstrated an extraordinary sense of propriety that guided all his actions and teachings with exquisite discrimination, while Mary Magdalene showed great circumspection and propriety despite her bold proclamation of the Master's resurrection.

name: proselyte
description: A person who converts from one religion to another, particularly referring to Gentiles who adopt Jewish practices. Flavius was described as a proselyte of the gate who had not undergone full conversion rituals, while the Pharisees were criticized for their zealous but ultimately harmful efforts to make converts.

name: prostitution
description: The commercialization of sexual relations for monetary gain. In ancient times, temple prostitution was often connected to religious rituals and human sacrifice customs. Jesus showed compassion toward women engaged in prostitution, recognizing their circumstances and offering redemption, while teaching that publicans and harlots would enter the kingdom before self-righteous religious leaders.

name: Proterozoic
description: The life-dawn geological era spanning from approximately 550 to 400 million years ago, characterized by rock systems containing fossil remains of early marine life and primitive vegetable organisms.

name: Protestant Reformation
aliases: disruption of the unity of Christianity
description: A religious movement in 15th-century Europe that disrupted the unity of Christianity due to the institutional church's inelasticity and inability to accommodate further growth while maintaining unity.

name: protons
description: Protons are positively charged particles that form the nucleus of atoms alongside neutrons. They are emitted from carbon atoms during solar reactions when hydrogen is converted into helium. A proton may be no larger than an electron but weighs almost two thousand times more. The charged protons in atomic nuclei are held together with uncharged neutrons by the reciprocal function of the mesotron, preventing disruption.

name: protoplasm
description: Protoplasm is the biologic unit of material life, consisting of a communal association of chemical, electrical, and other basic energies. The Life Carriers initiate the primordial reactions of material life within the protoplasmic cell. A paradox exists in studying protoplasm: to analyze it accurately, it must be killed, yet dead protoplasm differs from living protoplasm despite weighing the same.

name: Protozoa
description: Protozoa are primitive single-celled animal organisms that appeared early in evolutionary history. The amoeba, a typical single-celled animal organism, represents this higher protozoan type of animal life that has remained largely unchanged since ancient times. Protozoa are to the animal creation what bacteria are to the plant kingdom, representing early evolutionary steps with failure of subsequent development.

name: Proverbs
description: The Book of Proverbs is a Hebrew wisdom text that draws heavily from Amenemope's philosophy. Chapters fifteen, seventeen, twenty, and portions of chapters twenty-two through twenty-four are taken almost verbatim from Amenemope's Book of Wisdom. These teachings emphasize the ephemeral nature of earthly possessions and the importance of turning to divine guidance rather than human words.

name: providence
description: Providence represents God's loving overcare and the harmonious coordination of cosmic events for the ultimate good of all creation. It is the slow and sure emergence of the Supreme Being, manifesting through the interlocking activities of celestial beings who labor for God's honor and the spiritual advancement of his children. Providence does not mean God decides all things in advance, as that would constitute cosmic tyranny, but rather ensures all things work together for good to those who love God.

name: prudence
description: Prudence is a virtue of careful judgment and discretion, but when carried too far, can lead to cowardice and failure. Jesus taught that while prudence and discretion have value, excessive caution can prevent proper action and spiritual growth.

name: Psalms
description: The Book of Psalms is a collection of Hebrew religious writings that expresses the greatest wealth of devotional and inspirational ideas of God found in religious literature. It traces the evolution of beliefs about God from Amenemope to Isaiah, containing hymns written by Egyptian and Mesopotamian teachers as well as Hebrew authors. Many psalms were originally composed by Salem missionaries and later incorporated into Hebrew scripture during the Babylonian captivity.

name: psychic circles
description: The psychic circles are seven levels of cosmic progression that measure mortal personality development and spiritual attainment. Starting from the seventh circle, mortals advance through successive levels of intellectual, social, spiritual, and cosmic-insight achievement. Entrance upon the seventh circle marks the beginning of true human personality function, while completion of the first circle denotes relative maturity and the nearest possible approach of material mind and spirit Adjuster in human experience.

name: psychic organization
description: Psychic organization refers to the fundamental patterns of mental and spiritual structure in living beings. There are twenty-four basic orders of human psychic organization that correspond to the twenty-four pattern units of the life-carrying material, since intellectual life grows out of and upon the foundation of physical life.

name: psychology
description: Psychology is the study of mental phenomena and religious reactions to social environments, but it cannot penetrate the true inner motives and workings of religion. While psychology can examine religious behavior and personal expression, only theology, rooted in faith and revelation, can provide an intelligent account of the nature and content of religious experience.

name: pterosaurs
description: Pterosaurs were flying reptiles that evolved from hollow-boned leaping dinosaurs, featuring batlike wings with a twenty to twenty-five foot wingspan and separable jaws like snakes. These ancient creatures grew to ten feet long but failed to survive as they could not evolve the necessary characteristics for successful air navigation.

name: Ptolemais
description: Ptolemais was a coastal city in Palestine that Jesus passed through several times during his journeys, including visits during his second public preaching tour of Galilee and his travels to and from various regions of Palestine.

name: Ptolemies
description: The Ptolemies were the Greek ruling dynasty of Egypt following Alexander the Great's conquest.

name: Ptolemy
description: Ptolemy was the Greek king of Egypt who commissioned seventy scholars to translate a spurious document attributed to Moses into Greek for his library at Alexandria. This translation later became part of Hebrew and Christian sacred scriptures, profoundly influencing Western philosophical thought.

name: puberty
description: Puberty was a crucial transition period in early societies, often marked by initiation ceremonies involving self-torture and cutting that granted social aristocracy within the tribe. Failing these puberty tests was considered the supreme humiliation, forcing individuals to remain with women and children rather than joining the men's society.

name: public baths
description: Public baths were Roman social institutions that Jesus refused to visit due to the sexual promiscuity that prevailed there, despite his otherwise open engagement with all walks of Roman society.

name: public builders
description: The public builders are divine artisans who plan and construct magnificent places of assembly for celestial beings. These supermaterial structures are invisible to mortal perception but are very real and greatly enjoyed by spiritual beings.

name: public opinion
description: Public opinion represents the collective ideas of common people that, while often delaying social progress, serves to preserve civilization. Education of public opinion through nonviolent means is the only safe method for accelerating civilization, as force provides only temporary solutions.

name: public women
description: Public women were prostitutes or courtesans who solicited clients in public places, as encountered by Jesus and Ganid in Corinth.

name: public work
description: Public work refers to Jesus' ministry and teaching activities that began after his period of retreat in the hills, where he formulated plans for his public labors on behalf of his people and all inhabited worlds throughout his universe.

name: publicans
description: Tax collectors in ancient Palestine who were often despised by religious leaders but welcomed by Jesus. Matthew Levi was a former publican who became an apostle, and Jesus taught that publicans would enter the kingdom of heaven before the self-righteous religious rulers, as illustrated in his parable of the Pharisee and the publican.

name: publicans and sinners
description: A derogatory term used by Pharisees to describe businessmen and tax gatherers who were considered religiously unclean or morally compromised, yet were among those who welcomed Jesus's teachings.

name: Puget Sound
description: A body of water in the Pacific Northwest that served as an outlet for enormous icebergs during glacial periods, remaining relatively stable despite the varying climates and ice invasions throughout Earth's history.

name: puissant energy
description: The first phase of energy transmutation created by Primary Master Force Organizers from primordial force. This powerful, directional energy forms the initial stage of universe power development and eventually yields to Paradise gravity, becoming the foundation for ultimata formation.

name: punishment
description: Divine justice administered as a natural consequence of wrongdoing rather than arbitrary retribution. True punishment is a group function rather than personal vengeance, with the greatest punishment being loss of existence for those who persistently rebel against God's laws.

name: Punjab
description: A region in northern India that experienced gradual Andite infiltration from the Turkestan highlands and continuous influx of Aryan blood, resulting in greater racial persistence compared to other parts of India.

name: pure energy
description: A fundamental form of cosmic energy representing matter-energy in its most basic state, distinct from the various stages of energy transmutation in universe power development.

name: pure in heart
description: Those who possess spiritual purity characterized by faith, love, and freedom from suspicion and revenge. They embody fatherly love that seeks the best in others and shall see God through their sincere devotion.

name: purging
description: An ancient medical practice using foul-smelling and bad-tasting medicines to drive out disease spirits from the body, often employed by shamans as a routine treatment alongside other primitive remedies.

name: purification ceremonies
description: Religious or spiritual rituals designed to cleanse individuals or groups from contamination, sin, or spiritual impurity, commonly practiced in various cultures and religious traditions.

name: Purim
description: A Jewish feast or festival mentioned in The Urantia Book's discussion of religious observances.

name: purse
description: A container or pouch used for carrying money and personal items.

name: Pygmies
description: Ancient nonsocial peoples who represent primitive social conditions and are at an early stage of religious evolution. These backward groups, including African Pygmies, have simple mating customs without formal marriage institutions and lack organized religious reactions as a class, illustrating humanity's early antisocial tendencies before social progression.

name: pyramids
description: The first stone pyramid in Egypt was erected by the Andite architect Imhotep, representing the peak of Egyptian building artistry before subsequent decline. The great pyramid's sloping entrance passage pointed directly toward the Pole Star, symbolically directing the souls of worthy dead kings to the eternal constellations.

name: Pyrenees
description: A mountain range that, along with the Alps, Carpathians, and Apennines, emerged as islands above the submerged European continent during a geological epoch when most of Europe was covered by lakes and bays.

name: Qualified Absolute
description: An aspect of absolute reality that is conditioned or limited in some manner, related to the Deity Absolute in cosmic function.

name: Qualified Vicegerents of the Ultimate
description: Representatives or deputies of the Ultimate who are present in Havona in an absonite and superpersonal sense. They are influential beings in superuniverse governments whose current whereabouts and specific functions remain undisclosed, though they are represented by Reflective Image Aids.

name: quality
description: A matter of mind interpretation that represents an estimate of values and must remain an experience of the individual. Quality is felt by the soul through the mutual creation of mind and spirit, transcending mere quantity and representing the value aspect of reality.

name: quanta
description: Specific, exact amounts of energy that can be absorbed or released by matter, designated by scientists to maintain the relative integrity of matter in the universes. Energy is always taken in or given out in these precise quantum amounts or multiples thereof.

name: quantum behavior
description: The wavelike behavior of energy units that is determined by the dimensions of material structures involved. The never-ending confusion in observing wave mechanics of quantum behavior results from the superimposition of energy waves, where crests can combine or cancel each other out.

name: quarantine
description: A state of spiritual isolation imposed on rebellious worlds or systems, preventing normal interplanetary and interuniversal communication. Satania system remains under quarantine by Norlatiadek constellation due to the Lucifer rebellion, while Urantia faces additional isolation within Satania due to Caligastia's betrayal.

name: quartz
description: A mineral formed through geological processes including pressure, folding, and volcanic activity. Gold-bearing quartz strata were produced by lava flows during the Sierra mountain-building epoch, while sandstone was transformed into quartz through pressure and folding during Earth's geological history.

name: Quebec
description: A region characterized by lava flows and extensive water deposition during an ancient geological period when volcanic activity was largely past and mountain building was minimal.

name: Queensland
description: A region where primitive cannibalistic practices included mothers killing and eating their first child, supposedly to renew strength lost in childbearing.

name: questionings
description: Mental processes involving inquiry, doubt, or uncertainty about beliefs, concepts, or circumstances.

name: quickeners of morality
description: A group of seraphic teachers who instruct ascending mortals about cosmic morality, the interactions of liberty and loyalty, and the importance of sincere decision-making in spiritual progress. They teach on mansion worlds and portray mortal life as part of an unbroken chain extending across universes.

name: quinine
description: An early pharmaceutical discovery valued by primitive shamans as medicine, along with raw cocoa, representing one of humanity's earliest therapeutic remedies.

name: rabbis
description: Jewish religious teachers and authorities who were ordained by the Sanhedrin and qualified to act as judges in religious matters. Jesus was often addressed as Rabbi, though he taught without formal rabbinical authority, which concerned temple rulers who valued proper credentials and ordination.

name: rabbits
description: Small gnawing rodents that appeared during Earth's mammalian evolution, originating from an extinct carnivorous ancestor and becoming a notable form of life that has undergone little change since its emergence.

name: raccoons
description: Carnivorous mammals that thrived and developed throughout the northern latitudes during Earth's evolutionary history, belonging to a family that included weasels, martens, and otters.

name: race commissioners
description: High Commissioners who begin their service on planets as race commissioners, devoted to interpreting viewpoints and advocating for the needs of various human races. They function as spokesmen for mortal races, seeking mercy, justice, and fair treatment in planetary crises, and work closely with racial interpreter seraphim to harmonize varied viewpoints and social tendencies among different racial types.

name: races
description: The various human racial groups that have evolved on Urantia, including the six evolutionary Sangik races (red, yellow, blue, orange, green, and indigo), as well as the Andonites, Nodites, and Adamites. These distinct racial types possess different physical, mental, and spiritual characteristics and have undergone extensive interbreeding and blending throughout human history.

name: Rachel (Old Testament)
description: The wife of Jacob who concealed sacred stones in her tent, following an ancient tradition of stone worship that dates back to humanity's earliest days.

name: Rachel (sister-in-law of Jude)
description: Sister-in-law of Jude, Jesus' brother, who was selected as one of the ten women commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom. She helped convert Mary Magdalene at Magdala and asked Jesus about salvation for women during his teachings in Shunem.

name: racial interpreters
description: Seraphic beings who work to harmonize the varied viewpoints and social tendencies of different racial types among mortal beings. These skilled sociologists and ethnic advisers function on both inhabited worlds and the mansion worlds, furthering the efforts of race commissioners to promote understanding between diverse racial groups.

name: radio broadcasting
description: The use of Hertzian waves for transmitting broadcasts on Urantia. Radio broadcasting is made possible by upper air belts that reflect terrestrial broadcast waves back to the ground, enabling long-distance transmission, though it is occasionally disturbed by storms in the outer ionospheres.

name: radioactivity
description: The disorganizing tendency and activity of heavier atomic elements, particularly demonstrated in their spontaneous emission of energy and particles. Radioactive elements brought to Urantia by meteors help scientists estimate the planet's age at over one billion years, though such estimates are limited by the surface-only availability of these materials for study.

name: radium
description: A radioactive element that exemplifies atomic disintegration through gamma ray emissions and the ejection of positive particles at velocities of 10,000 miles per second. Radium serves as the most reliable timepiece for estimating planetary age, and its behavior illustrates the tendency of heavier elements to spontaneously dissociate.

name: Ragaba
description: A town in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final three-month mission before Jesus went to Jerusalem. It was home to Nathaniel, a wealthy Pharisee who hosted a Sabbath breakfast for Jesus and fellow Pharisees.

name: rage
description: An intense form of anger or fury that represents an extreme emotional state, often associated with loss of self-control and potential for destructive behavior.

name: rain
description: Precipitation of water that has played important roles in planetary history, religious symbolism, and human experience. Early Urantia experienced thousands of years of continuous rain when the atmosphere first cooled, and rain is used metaphorically to represent God's universal love falling on both the just and unjust.

name: rainbow
description: A celestial phenomenon holding diverse symbolic meanings across cultures, worshipped by hill tribes in India and Africa as a gigantic celestial snake and regarded by Hebrews and Christians as the bow of promise from God.

name: Ramah
description: A town where Jesus had a memorable discussion with an aged Greek philosopher, teaching him the saving truths of the gospel of the kingdom and demonstrating how religion reveals spiritual realities beyond what science and philosophy alone can discover.

name: Ramath
description: One of the towns visited during the Perean mission led by Abner and the seventy messengers, which lasted almost three months and represented Jesus's final ministry before going to Jerusalem.

name: Ramman
description: A Babylonian god of the air who was worshipped in the city of Rimmon, where Jesus and the twenty-four worked to distinguish these ancient beliefs from the gospel of the kingdom.

name: ransom doctrine
description: A theological concept related to atonement doctrine, though specific details are not provided in the available context.

name: Rantowoc
description: The first mortal on Urantia to secure a personal guardian of destiny, described as a wise man of the red race from long ago.

name: Rantulia
description: A neighboring system to Satania that provided crucial support during Lucifer's rebellion, forwarding calls for help to Edentia and helping ensure the triumph of loyalty and truth over rebellion.

name: rapture
description: A dematerialization technique used to transport planetary populations to safety in the event of physical catastrophes that threaten an entire world, allowing evolutionary races to continue their development on new worlds.

name: rationalism
description: A philosophical approach that leans heavily toward the material world and naturalistic explanations, contrasting with idealism's spiritual inclinations. Rationalism incorrectly assumes that primitive beliefs precede the pursuit of religious values, when in fact religion primarily pursues values first before formulating interpretative beliefs.

name: rationalization
description: The mental process of creating logical justifications for actions or beliefs that may be driven by other motives, often involving self-deception about one's true motivations.

name: Ratta
description: A superhuman 20-year-old Nodite woman who became the second wife of Adamson, the first son of Adam and Eve. Claiming to be the last pure-line descendant of Prince Caligastia's staff, she and Adamson had 67 children who gave origin to a great line of world leadership. Their unique offspring included secondary midwayers who appeared every fourth birth. Ratta's lineage contributed to the cultural legacy of the Greeks and formed part of the ancestry of Mary, the mother of Jesus.

name: ravens
description: Birds used by Jesus as an example of divine providence, illustrating how the Father provides food for every creature that seeks it, encouraging his apostles not to be anxious about material needs but to focus on spiritual progress.

name: raw eaters
description: A term of derision that emerged after the discovery of fire and cooking, referring to those who still ate uncooked food. The advent of cooking reduced energy expenditure for digestion and allowed early humans more strength for social culture and activities.

name: reality
description: The fundamental nature of existence that encompasses all levels of universal manifestation, from material and morontia to spiritual realms. Reality originates in the infinite volition of the Universal Father and actualizes through diverse universe levels. It includes both actual and potential aspects, finite and infinite dimensions, and serves as the foundation for all cosmic relationships and divine purposes.

name: realization of identity transition
description: The immediate transition that occurs when a Thought Adjuster, after registering at Divinington and receiving recognition from Personalized Monitors, rejoins with a resurrected mortal subject, marking the beginning of their unified morontia existence.

name: reaping
description: The principle that consequences follow actions in accordance with divine law, as Jesus taught that the reaping is in accordance with the sowing. This concept emphasizes that cultivating patience and trust will sustain believers through trials and tribulations.

name: Rebecca (daughter of Ezra)
description: The eldest daughter of Ezra, a wealthy merchant of Nazareth, who fell in love with Jesus but was gently rejected as he prioritized his divine mission. Heartbroken, she moved to Sepphoris and devoted her life to awaiting Jesus' ministry. She remained a faithful follower throughout his public career, witnessing his crucifixion and being present at his tomb on resurrection morning.

name: Rebecca (daughter of Joseph of Arimathea)
description: The daughter of Joseph of Arimathea who was commissioned by Jesus as one of ten women for ministering work in the kingdom. She played a role in reporting the resurrection to the apostles and was present during Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance to the Jerusalem believers.

name: rebellion (spiritual)
description: Conscious defiance against divine authority and universal order, exemplified by the Lucifer rebellion in Satania. Spiritual rebellion isolates worlds from normal universe circuits until proper rehabilitation occurs. While preventing rebellion is valued more highly than loyalty during actual revolt, the ascension plan proves the surest defense against such uprisings, as demonstrated by the triumphant survival of ascending mortals during the Lucifer rebellion.

name: Recents of Days
description: The youngest of the supreme Trinity directors, numbering 21,000 total, who preside over the minor sectors of superuniverses in groups of three. They are coordinate in nature with the Perfections of Days but subordinate in administrative authority, overseeing the affairs of component local universes within each minor sector.

name: recession of galaxies
description: The apparent outward movement of distant galaxies as observed from Earth, creating the impression that the universe is expanding.

name: recklessness
description: Acting without proper consideration of consequences or dangers, often driven by impulse rather than wisdom.

name: recognition
description: The acknowledgment of worth, achievement, or service in others, essential for healthy character development and spiritual growth. True recognition should be earned through genuine accomplishment rather than sought for selfish purposes, and the Universal Father never imposes arbitrary recognition but seeks voluntary worship from intelligent creatures.

name: recorder-teachers
description: Sixth-order transitional ministry seraphim who serve as recorders of borderland transactions between spiritual and physical realms, and as instructors in efficient fact-recording techniques. They demonstrate artistry in assembling and coordinating related data, often collaborating with celestial artisans and ascending mortals.

name: recorders
description: Seraphic beings who serve as custodians and keepers of records throughout the universe hierarchies, maintaining communication networks and preserving both formal archives and living records of universal transactions and individual progress.

name: records
description: Essential documentation systems maintained throughout the universes, including both formal written archives and living records preserved in the minds of suitable personalities. These records enable efficient universe administration and provide testimony for individual adjudication proceedings.

name: recreation
description: Periods of rest, play, and diversion essential for the well-being of ascending beings throughout their universe careers. Recreation serves as rejuvenation from serious endeavors and helps prevent harmful tension, with humor and play acting as healthy backward glances that refresh the mind and spirit.

name: rectangles (Jerusem)
description: Designated areas on the system capital of Jerusem that serve as meeting places and residential zones for lower native life forms, part of the diverse administrative and residential arrangements of the system headquarters.

name: red race
description: The first and most superior of the six primary Sangik races, numbering about 85,000 when they migrated to North America across the Bering land bridge. They were remarkable specimens with advanced intelligence, tribal government, and monogamous practices, but suffered from internecine warfare that weakened their potential for building a great civilization in the Americas.

name: red shift
description: The apparent displacement of spectral lines toward the red end of the spectrum when observing distant astronomical objects, which astronomers interpret as evidence of recession. According to The Urantia Book, this phenomenon results from multiple observational errors and time-space distortions rather than actual recession, with the greatest distortion arising from opposite rotational directions between outer space universes and the grand universe.

name: redemption
description: The eternal rehabilitation and restoration offered through divine love, which not only forgives but actually destroys sin and evil. Jesus brought this new method of redemption to Urantia, representing love that transforms and makes wrongs right rather than merely overlooking them. In primitive religions, redemption evolved from concepts of debt to spirits and the need for sacrificial payment.

name: redwoods
description: Large coniferous trees that grew during geological periods and served as the headquarters location for Onamonalonton, the great spiritual leader and deliverer of the American red men around 65,000 years ago.

name: reflections
description: The mirror images of oneself seen in water or other reflective surfaces, which early humans viewed with superstitious fear and reverence, believing they provided proof of a double self or soul.

name: Reflectivator Liaisons of Majeston
description: An unrevealed order of beings that serve as liaisons for Majeston, classified among the unclassified orders of the universe whose nature and function remain undisclosed.

name: Reflective Image Aids
description: Forty-nine beings created by the Reflective Spirits, with seven stationed on each superuniverse headquarters. They serve as perfect reproductions of their Reflective Mother Spirits, functioning as communication channels and representatives to the Ancients of Days, though they lack the attribute of reflectivity and possess no will of their own.

name: Reflective Mother Spirits
description: The term referring to the Reflective Spirits in their role as creators of the Reflective Image Aids, emphasizing their maternal relationship to these beings who serve as their perfect reproductions in all attributes except reflectivity.

name: Reflective Spirits
description: Forty-nine unique beings of Trinity origin stationed on superuniverse headquarters, with seven of each type representing different combinations of the divine characteristics of the Paradise Deities. They conduct the vast reflectivity operations that enable instantaneous communication and intelligence gathering throughout the superuniverses, creating the seconaphim as their offspring and serving as the mother-makers of the angelic hosts.

name: reflectivity
description: The extraordinary phenomenon of universe-wide intelligence gathering and communication that enables instantaneous knowledge of all mental, material, and spiritual transactions throughout the superuniverses. This unique power of the Infinite Spirit operates through the Reflective Spirits and their associates, allowing universe rulers to know about remote conditions simultaneously with their occurrence.

name: Reformation
description: The fifteenth-century religious movement that disrupted the unity of Christianity in Europe, making institutionalized Christianity so inflexible that further growth became incompatible with unity.

name: Refuge
description: A formal statement of Buddhist faith recited by new converts, consisting of taking refuge in the Buddha, the Doctrine, and the Brotherhood.

name: regulation (governmental)
description: Governmental control and oversight mechanisms that ideally should define rights, prevent violence, and ensure fairness while avoiding oppressive interference with individual liberties or becoming parasitical to society.

name: rehabilitation
description: The process of spiritual restoration and recovery, particularly referring to divine efforts to win loyalty from rebellious creatures through endless mercy and patience before justice must ultimately prevail.

name: reincarnation
description: The belief that souls return to earth in successive lives, which originated from observing hereditary resemblances and ancestor worship but conflicts with revealed truth that human spirits do not return to their planet of birth after death.

name: reindeer
description: Large Arctic mammals that served multiple purposes for Neanderthal peoples as sources of food, clothing, and materials for tools made from their horns and bones.

name: relationships
description: Connections and associations between beings that form the basis of cosmic organization, where two beings create partnerships, three or more form groups or systems, and all true relationships with persons lead to fellowship with Deity as the eternal goal of universe ascension.

name: relativity
description: The principle that all finite knowledge and creature understanding are relative, varying in completeness and accuracy based on personal experience and perspective, while divine truth remains uniform and universal across creation.

name: relaxation
description: A state of rest and mental ease that determines capacity for spiritual receptivity, often involving carefree reflection on past experiences and serving as a necessary counterbalance to intense activity and emotional tension.

name: relics
description: Objects believed to possess spiritual power due to their association with deceased holy persons, representing a rationalized form of ancient fetish worship that modern religions have elevated to respectability despite being rooted in superstition.

name: religion
description: A living and dynamic experience of spiritual communion with divine influences that transcends mere belief systems, providing meaning, values, and purpose while fostering moral growth and social service through personal relationship with God.

name: religionists
description: Individuals who practice religion and have faith in God. They are characterized by God-knowing certainty, spiritual unity beyond theological conformity, dedication to universe service, and the manifestation of spirit fruits such as love, joy, peace, and service to others. Religionists seek to identify themselves with the universe and serve the brotherhood of all beings through personal spiritual experience and living faith.

name: religious guardians
description: A celestial order of beings, likely seraphic, assigned to supervise and protect religious activities and spiritual development on inhabited worlds.

name: religious leaders in Rome
description: Thirty-two religious leaders from various philosophical and mystery schools in Rome whom Jesus personally instructed during his six-month stay. Jesus prepared them by enhancing truth in their existing teachings, and thirty of them became pivotal in establishing Christianity in Rome and throughout the empire.

name: religious leaders (Jerusalem)
description: The religious authorities and teachers in Jerusalem, including members of the Sanhedrin, who held positions of spiritual leadership among the Jewish people during Jesus' time.

name: Remembrance Supper
description: The sacred meal instituted by Jesus at the Last Supper to commemorate his bestowal life and the gift of the Spirit of Truth. This symbolic communion service represents the new dispensation of grace and truth, where believers gather to remember Jesus' teachings and experience spiritual fellowship with the Master who is truly present during the ceremony.

name: Remona
description: The wife of Cain and mother of Enoch, who became the head of the Elamite Nodites in the land of Nod east of the second Eden.

name: renaissance (European)
description: The period of cultural and religious revival in Europe during which Christianity was rehabilitated after the dark ages, resulting in the emergence of numerous Christian sects and religious movements that brought new intellectual, emotional, and spiritual expressions to the faith.

name: renunciation
description: The practice of giving up physical pleasures and material comforts as a form of religious devotion. It developed as the negative form of propitiation in ancient religions, leading to practices like fasting and celibacy that became common elements in many religious traditions.

name: repentance
description: The sincere sorrow for wrongdoing and genuine change of heart that leads to spiritual transformation. True repentance involves recognition of error, desire for divine forgiveness, and faith in God's mercy, ultimately resulting in the bearing of spiritual fruits and alignment with the Father's will.

name: repersonalization
description: The process by which a deceased personality is restored to conscious existence, typically referring to the resurrection and reconstitution of an individual's identity and consciousness after physical death.

name: repression
description: A psychological concept involving the suppression or restraint of thoughts, feelings, or impulses.

name: reptilians
description: Ancient creatures that gave rise to both birds and mammals, with modern descendants including snakes, lizards, alligators, and turtles. The reptilian kingdom evolved from frogs and represents one of the greatest evolutionary leaps when reptiles became birds.

name: research
description: The systematic investigation and study of phenomena to establish facts and reach new conclusions.

name: resentment
description: A destructive emotion that can transform love into hate through accumulated disappointments and wounds. Resentment inhibits creative imagination and can be overcome by understanding others' viewpoints and discovering their true values.

name: reserve corps
description: Vast divisions of beings maintained on universe capitals as mobilized reserves for various duties and emergency assignments. These corps include seventy primary divisions on Uversa and similar reserves throughout the universe hierarchy, serving when requisitioned by their respective group directors.

name: reserve corps of destiny
description: A special service group of living mortals chosen to assist in the superhuman administration of world affairs during planetary emergencies. Urantia has twelve such corps totaling 962 persons who are trained unconsciously and held in readiness to prevent the breakdown of evolutionary progress.

name: reservists
description: Mortal members of the reserve corps of destiny who are chosen for their mental capacity, moral status, and spiritual potential. Most are unconscious of their training and preparation, serving as human liaisons to prevent cultural breakdown or the extinction of truth during planetary crises.

name: resident governors general
description: Executive representatives of the twenty-four Jerusem counselors who serve one hundred-year terms on Urantia as coordinators of superhuman administration. They function as advisory chief executives and respected leaders of all celestial beings on the planet.

name: resistance
description: The tendency to follow paths of least resistance, which led primitive humans to organize society and develop specialization of labor. However, taking the path of least resistance can also lead to compromise and difficulty in correcting wrong courses of action.

name: resolutions
description: Weak substitutes for action, as the strong act decisively while the weak merely make resolutions. True accomplishment comes from doing rather than resolving.

name: respect
description: A fundamental attitude of honoring the dignity and worth of personality, particularly human personality. Respect involves treating others with consideration for their experience, opinions, and inherent value as children of God. True respect cannot be compelled by force but must be freely given, and it forms the basis for healthy relationships and spiritual growth.

name: respiration
description: The process of breathing, which varies across different orders of beings and worlds. Architectural worlds like Edentia feature a three-gas atmosphere suitable for both material and morontial life forms, while evolutionary worlds like Urantia typically have two-gas atmospheres.

name: responsibility
description: The capacity to bear trust and fulfill duties, which develops gradually with character growth. Responsibility represents humanity's supreme cosmic obligation through the power of choice, as mortal decisions affect eternal destiny and contribute to the experiential evolution of the Supreme Being.

name: rest
description: A sevenfold concept encompassing sleep, play, discovery, worship, energy intake, transition sleep, and divine rest. Rest serves different purposes across various orders of beings and includes both physical recuperation and spiritual recharging, ultimately culminating in the eternal rest experienced upon reaching Paradise.

name: resurrection
description: The process by which mortal survivors are repersonalized on the mansion worlds through the reassociation of the immortal soul with the returned Thought Adjuster in a new morontia form. Resurrection involves the fabrication of a suitable morontia body, the return of the Adjuster, and the bestowal of personality consciousness, typically occurring on the third day for those with personal guardians or at dispensational judgments for others.

name: resurrection halls
description: Specialized facilities on the first mansion world and other morontia receiving planets where the repersonalization of sleeping survivors takes place. These enormous temples serve as assembly chambers where seraphic guardians, Thought Adjusters, and archangels collaborate to reconstruct personality through the reunion of soul and Adjuster in new morontia forms.

name: retaliation
description: The act of responding to harm or injury with corresponding punishment or revenge, which Jesus consistently taught against through his emphasis on forgiveness, turning the other cheek, and loving one's enemies.

name: retirement
description: The cessation of gainful employment, typically occurring at age 65 in advanced planetary civilizations. Progressive worlds implement dignified retirement systems with government-insurance guarantees funded through various means to ensure security in old age without destroying self-respect.

name: retrograde motion
description: The backward orbital movement of celestial bodies that occurs when foreign space objects collide with or influence existing astronomical systems. This phenomenon results from the intrusion of bodies with different directional origins, as seen with Venus and Uranus, which likely were originally satellites of the visiting dark giant Angona.

name: revelation
description: The divine disclosure of truth that supplements and elevates evolutionary religion by providing expanded spiritual insight and cosmic perspective. Revelation bridges the gap between material and spiritual understanding, offering assured knowledge of eternal realities, personality survival, and God's nature while being validated through human experience and spiritual transformation.

name: Revelations, Book of
description: The Book of Revelation, written by the Apostle John during his exile on Patmos, contains surviving fragments of a great revelation in greatly abridged and distorted form. John's confused vision blends symbols of the seven spirits of God with the majestic seats of the twenty-four elders on Jerusem, representing a confusion of presentations from different celestial locations.

name: revenge
description: The desire to inflict harm in return for perceived wrongs or injuries. Jesus taught his followers to reject revenge and retaliation, advocating instead for forgiveness, love, and returning good for evil. Revenge is described as the mask of cowardice and a shadow of fear, representing spiritual immaturity and inability to trust and love.

name: reversion
description: The process of returning to or recalling past experiences and states of being for purposes of rest, relaxation, and mental rejuvenation.

name: reversion directors
description: Celestial beings who promote relaxation and humor by guiding memory back to past experiences for rest and rejuvenation. They are recruited volunteers ranging from Havona natives to morontia progressors who serve as ministers of exalted humor in the morontia and spirit realms, helping prevent overdevelopment of self-importance and promoting smoother group interactions.

name: revolution (abrupt change)
description: Sudden, radical transformation of social, political, or cultural systems. The Urantia Book teaches that revolution disrupts society and should be avoided in favor of gradual evolution, as sudden wholesale changes are nearly fatal to civilization and cause confusion and disorder.

name: revolution (motion)
description: The circular or orbital movement of cosmic bodies and energy units around centers of gravity. All units of cosmic energy are in perpetual revolution, executing their mission while moving along the endless circuits of the master universe space levels.

name: reward
description: Spiritual benefits and personal satisfaction that follow righteous living and unselfish service. True rewards are primarily spiritual rather than material, including divine communion, eternal life, and consciousness of achievement that transcends any conceivable material compensation.

name: Rhine River
description: A major European river where Nordic-Danish and Danubian-Andonite cultures met and mingled, creating the two distinct racial groups that exist in Germany today.

name: rhinoceroses
description: Large mammals that underwent their greatest expansion after 45 million years ago, migrating over the whole world except Australia and South America before becoming extinct in the Western Hemisphere by the end of the Cenozoic era.

name: Rhodes
description: A Greek island where Jesus and his companions stopped during their Mediterranean journey, enjoying rest and refreshment during their travels from Ephesus to Cyprus.

name: rhythm
description: Rhythm enhances efficiency and pleasure for morontia and spirit beings, with transcendent artisans executing functions in rhythmic form. Ancient peoples practiced rhythm to influence spirits in the treatment of ailments, utilizing methods like vapor baths and tom-toms.

name: rhythm recorders
description: Celestial artisans who would be denominated poets by Urantians, though their work transcends earthly poetic productions. They use rhythm to increase efficiency and augment pleasure for morontia and spirit beings, mastering an exquisite form of self-expression and social harmonization.

name: rice
description: A grain that became part of the omnivorous diet of ancient peoples when combined with wheat, vegetables, and meat, marking a great forward step in health and vigor. Rice was also used by certain Oriental peoples in magical wedding rites, thrown on newlyweds to ensure fecundity.

name: rich
description: Those possessing wealth and material abundance, often referenced in Jesus's teachings about the proper stewardship of resources and the dangers of loving riches above spiritual values.

name: rich man and beggar
aliases: the rich man and the beggar
description: A Nazarite parable referenced by Simon Peter that serves as a warning about the dangers of loving riches and coveting dishonest wealth, representing an older teaching tradition before the new gospel of the kingdom.

name: rich man of Rome
description: A wealthy Roman citizen and Stoic who became interested in Jesus's teaching and received counsel about administering material wealth as a wise trustee for the benefit of future generations. Jesus taught him that having wealth was not wrong, but emphasized the responsibility to help others and condemned exploitation of the weak.

name: rich widow of Tyre
description: A wealthy woman who sought healing for her many infirmities and followed Jesus through Galilee, continually offering more money as if God's power could be purchased. She showed no interest in the gospel of the kingdom, seeking only physical cures.

name: rich young ruler
description: A wealthy young man who encountered Jesus, representing those who struggle to prioritize spiritual values over material possessions and worldly attachments.

name: ridicule
description: A form of mockery that Jesus warned against as poisonous darts that can wound self-respect and hurt the simple-minded. Ridicule proved particularly devastating to both Judas, who was influenced by his Sadducean friends' mockery, and Peter, who withered before it despite his great devotion to Jesus.

name: Rig-Veda
description: One of the most ancient sacred books, compiled by Brahmans from earlier sacred writings in an effort to combat Salem teachers. It represents both some of the highest concepts of Deity ever conceived and contains the quote "My mind speaks to my heart," illustrating the distinction between mind and soul.

name: right
description: A concept encompassing moral correctness, legal entitlements, and just authority. Rights are associated with social duties and regulated by law to ensure fairness and equality of opportunity. While ancient times operated on the principle that "might was still right," civilization evolved toward representative government based on ability rather than physical power.

name: right hand of the Father
aliases: right hand of the Universal Father
description: A symbolic position of honor and authority at the Universal Father's side. Creator Sons proceed to the right hand of the Father after each bestowal to receive acceptance and instructions for their next universe service. Jesus ascended to the right hand of the Father to receive completed sovereignty over Nebadon, and this position represents the place where divine authority and recognition are conferred.

name: righteousness
description: Moral virtue and conformity with divine will, representing the progressive character development that comes through faith and spiritual growth. Righteousness involves actively choosing good over evil and is both a gift received through sonship with God and a quality developed through living according to spiritual principles. It encompasses love, mercy, truth, and the sincere desire to do the Father's will.

name: rights
description: Legal and moral entitlements that individuals and groups possess within society, typically balanced with corresponding social duties and responsibilities.

name: Rimmon
description: A small city once dedicated to the worship of Ramman, a Babylonian god of the air. Jesus and the twenty-four devoted significant time there to clarify the differences between older Babylonian and Zoroastrian beliefs and the new gospel of the kingdom.

name: ring
description: A circular ornament or symbol of status and belonging. Rings have historically served as markers of married status for women and symbols of familial acceptance, as illustrated in the parable of the prodigal son where the father places a ring on his returning son's hand as a sign of forgiveness and restoration to the family.

name: rivalry
description: Competition or contest between individuals or groups, often involving opposing interests or goals.

name: river bed
description: The channel or course through which a river flows. Used metaphorically to illustrate that socialized religion serves as a channel for spiritual growth, though the channel itself is not the essence of the spiritual experience, just as the river bed is not the river.

name: rivers
description: Natural watercourses that played a crucial role in early human civilization, with the earliest cultures arising along rivers in the Eastern Hemisphere. While architectural worlds like Jerusem and Edentia have interconnecting streams and sparkling lakes, they lack the raging rivers found on evolutionary worlds like Urantia.

name: road of robbers
description: A dangerous route between Jericho and Bethany known for criminal activity. Jesus and his followers traveled this road on their way to Jerusalem for the Passover, and it was the location where the notorious robber Barabbas had been apprehended for robbery and murder.

name: roads
description: Transportation routes that facilitated travel and trade throughout the ancient world. During Jesus' time, interconnected good roads linked major centers in the unified Mediterranean world, enabling commerce and communication. Notable roads mentioned include the Appian Way where Gonod and Ganid traveled across Italy, and the Damascus road used by Jesus and his apostles for journeys to places like Caesarea-Philippi and Beit Jenn.

name: robes
description: Long garments worn as symbols of status and authority. Self-centered religious rulers delighted in wearing long robes to seek honor from men, though inwardly they were spiritually unclean. In the parable of the prodigal son, the father adorned his returning son with the best robe as a symbol of restoration and honor.

name: rocks and minerals
description: The geological materials that form the Earth's crust, including various types of stones, sedimentary layers, and mineral deposits. These materials provide evidence of glacial action, crustal distortions, and geological processes spanning millions of years. The Urantia Book references numerous specific types including boulders, granite, limestone, sandstone, shale, coal, and various crystalline formations that record the planet's evolutionary history.

name: Rocky Mountains
description: A major mountain range in western North America formed through geological processes spanning millions of years. The Rocky Mountains reached their maximum elevation around 70 million years ago during crustal distortions that included significant rock overthrusts. These mountains contain fossils of dinosaurs and other ancient life forms, serving as geological records of Earth's evolutionary history.

name: Rodan of Alexandria
description: A Greek philosopher from Alexandria who became a disciple of Jesus through the teaching of one of John the Baptist's former apostles. Rodan sought to harmonize his philosophy with Jesus' teachings and engaged in extensive discussions with apostles Nathaniel and Thomas at Magadan. He later taught philosophy in the school of Meganta in Alexandria and was eventually martyred in Greece during the persecutions, remaining faithful to the gospel until his death.

name: rodents
description: A family of gnawing mammals including beavers, squirrels, gophers, mice, and rabbits that appeared during the mammalian evolution period. These creatures evolved quickly and became a notable form of life, with very little change occurring in this family since their early appearance.

name: roll call
description: The formal celestial procedure for accounting of personalities during dispensational resurrections and judgments. All Adjuster-indwelt beings are on the roll calls of justice, while guardian angels respond on behalf of nonsurviving personalities during these proceedings. Jesus reminded his followers that their names were written on the rolls of heaven for eternal spiritual conquest.

name: Roman Catholic Church
description: A major Christian denomination that developed from early Christianity and became a dominant religious institution in Western civilization. The church was influenced by ancient continence cults that promoted celibate priesthood and faced challenges during the medieval period when it became highly institutionalized. The Urantia Book discusses how the church's totalitarian medieval structure contributed to the rise of modern secularism, while noting both its positive role in preserving Christianity and its tendency toward ecclesiastical authority that hindered religious progress.

name: Roman centurion in Corinth
description: A Roman military officer whom Jesus encountered during his Mediterranean journey. Jesus counseled him about the compatibility of loyal service to both God and Caesar, explaining that sincere service to God would make him even more faithful in his devotion to a worthy emperor.

name: Roman centurion of Jerusalem
description: The Roman military captain who was present during Jesus' arrest, crucifixion, and burial. He led the procession to Golgotha carrying the inscription "Jesus of Nazareth—the King of the Jews," showed kindness to Jesus by moistening his lips with wine, and after witnessing the crucifixion became a believer, declaring Jesus to be a Son of God. He also helped protect Jesus' body and assisted in placing it in the tomb.

name: Roman Empire
description: The Roman Empire was a vast political entity that encompassed southern Europe, Asia Minor, Syria, Egypt, and northwest Africa. Built on foundations including the father-family structure, agriculture, urban centers, private property, slavery, conquest, territorial roads, and strong leadership, the empire provided political unity and tolerance for diverse cultures and religions. Its decline was attributed to moral decay, rapid social changes, overtaxation, and spiritual emptiness, despite later adoption of Christianity.

name: Roman judge in Corinth
description: A Roman judge in Corinth whom Jesus counseled about fair judgment and universal accountability. Jesus reminded him to judge with justice and mercy, as he himself would one day face judgment before the Supreme Arbiter.

name: Roman magistrate in Rome
description: A Roman magistrate in Rome before whom Jesus appeared to defend a falsely accused poor man. Jesus delivered a powerful address emphasizing that justice makes a nation great and that courts must protect even the most humble citizens, leading the magistrate to reopen the case and discharge the prisoner.

name: Roman senator
description: A Roman senator who conversed with Jesus about politics and statesmanship. This encounter made such a profound impression that the senator spent the rest of his life trying to convince his colleagues to change government policy from supporting the people to having the people support the government.

name: Roman soldier in Rome
description: A Roman soldier in Rome whom Jesus counseled while walking along the Tiber River. Jesus urged him to be brave in heart and hand, to seek justice tempered with mercy, to control his lower nature, and to reach out for God with a whole heart as his Father in heaven.

name: Roman soldiers in Jerusalem
description: Roman soldiers stationed in Jerusalem who witnessed the stone rolling away from Jesus' tomb and fled in panic to report to their centurion. They were also present during various incidents involving Jewish citizens, including the arrest of Jude for expressing anti-Roman sentiment after a guard made improper remarks.

name: romance
description: The emotional and imaginative aspects of courtship and love relationships. Romance, along with personal selection and idealized concepts of attraction, represents an Andite contribution to human civilization, gradually replacing purely utilitarian motives in mate selection, though excessive romantic fantasy in modern courtship contributes to increasing divorce rates.

name: Romans
description: The people of Rome and the Roman Empire, characterized by political devotion, honesty, and strong self-control. They were natural lawyers who revered law and order, making them receptive to Christianity's moral teachings, though their original religion was primarily political rather than spiritual in nature.

name: Rome
description: The capital city of the Roman Empire, home to two million inhabitants and the largest, most cosmopolitan city of the ancient world. Jesus spent considerable time in Rome during his Mediterranean journey, teaching individuals from all walks of life and gaining intimate knowledge of diverse races and classes while preparing future disciples for the kingdom.

name: Romulus
description: The legendary founder of Rome, preserved in Roman tradition as an example of a child who was put away but saved by divine intervention. His story represents the ancient custom of abandoning infants who, if they survived, were believed to be divinely protected.

name: roof
description: Most mansion world structures are roofless due to perfect climatic conditions on architectural worlds. On Urantia, roofs serve practical purposes, such as providing summer bedrooms for study and requiring removal of tiles to lower the paralytic to Jesus for healing.

name: roosters
description: Male chickens, part of domestic poultry.

name: round-heads
description: By 5000 B.C., round-headed Andonites were dominant in central Europe, particularly the Danube valley, alongside the blue man, and were never entirely displaced by the Andites.

name: royalties
description: The federal government on a nearby inhabited planet collects royalties by taking half the profits from all inventions and creations produced in regional laboratories, using these funds to support inventors and creators.

name: Ruben
description: A reputed tribe that traditionally dwelt in regions east of the Jordan River, whose stories Jesus shared with Joseph during their journey to Jerusalem for Passover.

name: ruffians at Nazareth
description: Rough and uncouth men hired by Jesus' enemies to harass him and make trouble during his visit to the Nazareth synagogue, but who ultimately failed in their attempt to harm him.

name: Rufus
description: Son of Simon of Cyrene who became an effective teacher of the gospel in Africa. Jesus had earlier aided Rufus after he was injured by an oxcart, unknowingly helping the son of the man who would later carry his cross.

name: rule of living
description: The old way of suppression and conformance to rules of living, contrasted with the new way of transformation by the Spirit of Truth that leads to personal faith in God's promises and makes believers temples of God.

name: rules
description: Established principles or guidelines that govern conduct or procedure.

name: ruminants
description: Cud-chewing mammals including deer, oxen, camels, bison, and rhinoceroses that appeared and soon overran North America during the early mammalian age.

name: runaway lad in Corinth
description: A young man who ran away to Corinth and encountered Jesus during his Mediterranean tour. Jesus counseled him that one cannot run away from God or oneself, and urged him to courageously face life with the spirit of the heavenly Father within his heart.

name: Russia
description: A vast northern land where the blue race absorbed considerable Andonite blood and where tree spirits are regarded as cruel. During various geological periods, much of Russia was covered by seas, and it later became occupied by mixed populations of Adamites, Nodites, Andonites, and Sangik peoples.

name: Ruth (daughter of Matthew)
description: Ruth, eldest daughter of Matthew Levi, was one of ten women chosen by Jesus on January 16 to serve in the ministering work of the kingdom. She was present at Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance to the Jerusalem believers.

name: Ruth (Old Testament)
description: A biblical figure who was among the remarkable women in Mary's ancestral lineage. Ruth was one of the well-known women in the racial history of Urantia who contributed to the illustrious heritage of Jesus' mother.

name: Ruth (sister of Jesus)
description: The youngest sister of Jesus, born April 17, A.D. 9. Ruth was unswervingly loyal to Jesus throughout his ministry, remaining the only family member who consistently believed in the divinity of his mission. She later married David Zebedee and moved to Philadelphia with him.

name: Ryonin
description: A Japanese Buddhist teacher who, along with Honen Shonin and Shinran, helped evolve the God concept in Buddhism that culminated in belief in Amida Buddha. He taught faith in Amida Buddha as part of the development from Buddhism's dual conception of God and the Absolute.

name: Sabbath
description: The seventh day of the week, originating from Adam and Eve's first day of rest in Eden. Originally devoted to physical improvement, spiritual worship, mind culture, and social rejoicing, it later became heavily regulated by Jewish law. Jesus frequently challenged restrictive Sabbath interpretations, declaring that the Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

name: saber-toothed tigers
description: Large prehistoric cats that first evolved in North America and spread to other continents. They became extinct during the ice ages, being virtually exterminated in Europe by lions following glacial retreat. These fierce predators represented one of the dangerous threats faced by early humans.

name: sachems
description: Peace chiefs of the red race who were chosen for outstanding personal abilities rather than military prowess. Unlike hereditary war chiefs, sachems served as judges and teachers, representing a more civilized form of leadership based on wisdom and capability.

name: sackcloth and ashes
description: A traditional expression of deep repentance and mourning. Jesus used this phrase when declaring that if the mighty works done in Chorazin, Bethsaida-Julias, and Capernaum had been performed in the heathen cities of Tyre and Sidon, their people would have repented in sackcloth and ashes.

name: sacraments
description: Religious ceremonies that evolved from early sacrificial rituals, representing symbolic spiritual observances. The concept encompasses both primitive rituals involving human sacrifice that developed into higher symbolic levels, and modern ceremonial services that emerged from ancient ideas of ransom, redemption, and covenants. Sacraments include practices like the Lord's Supper, baptism, and the partaking of bread and wine, though Jesus warned against attaching precise interpretations to such symbolic observances.

name: sacred books
description: Religious writings regarded as divinely inspired by various faiths, including the Hebrew scriptures, Christian texts, and other religious literature. The Urantia Book teaches that while these texts contain valuable spiritual wisdom and moral teachings, they are human productions reflecting the understanding of their times and should not be viewed as infallible or the exclusive source of divine truth. True spiritual insight comes from recognizing God's ongoing revelation rather than limiting divine truth to ancient writings.

name: sacred spheres of Paradise
description: Twenty-one eternal worlds arranged in three circuits between Paradise and Havona, consisting of seven secret spheres of the Universal Father, seven luminous worlds of the Eternal Son, and seven executive spheres of the Infinite Spirit. These spheres of unexcelled grandeur serve diverse functions in both central and superuniverses, containing potentials of master universe function and serving as headquarters for various orders of celestial beings.

name: sacredness
description: The quality of being sacred or holy, often attributed to ancient traditions and customs in religious practice. True sacredness lies in heartfelt loyalty to one's highest spiritual convictions rather than in attachment to nonsacred objects or traditions. The concept warns against false reverence that prevents acceptance of new spiritual truth and emphasizes that all things become sacred when performed in service to God.

name: sacrifice
description: Religious practices ranging from primitive human and animal offerings to symbolic ceremonies, evolving from early attempts to appease spirits and gods. These practices progressed from crude physical sacrifices to more refined spiritual concepts of self-abnegation and service. True spiritual progress involves loyalty and wholehearted service rather than sacrifice, as Jesus taught that genuine devotion transcends duty and becomes joyful friendship with God.

name: Sadducees
description: A Jewish religious party composed primarily of the priesthood and wealthy Jews, known for their acceptance of only the five Books of Moses as legitimate scripture and their denial of angels and resurrection. They controlled the temple and were less strict about law enforcement than the Pharisees. The Sadducees eventually joined with the Pharisees in opposition to Jesus, particularly after his cleansing of the temple threatened their economic interests.

name: Sadib
description: The second assistant System Sovereign of Satania, a tertiary Lanonandek Son numbered 271,402 who arrived in the system with Lanaforge, the System Sovereign.

name: Safed
description: A town in Galilee where Jesus preached and the apostles conducted personal evangelistic work during their ministry. It was one of the many towns and villages where the twelve apostles labored to spread the teachings of the kingdom.

name: safeguard
description: A spiritual protection or assurance for beings on their journey toward God. The surest safeguard for creatures struggling to attain the Universal Father is maintaining faith in the Father's presence within his Creator Sons, recognizing that the Father and Sons are spiritually one.

name: Sage of Salem
description: A title referring to Machiventa Melchizedek, the emergency Son who incarnated on Urantia and established his teaching center at Salem. As a wise teacher and spiritual leader, he preserved monotheistic concepts and prepared the way for later spiritual revelations.

name: Sagittarius
description: The center of our minor sector, around which our local universe Nebadon orbits in an increasingly settled path. Sagittarius is part of a vast subgalactic system and serves as the rotational center for the minor sector containing our local creation.

name: Sahara
description: Once an open grazing land inhabited by herders and agriculturists, the Sahara became a barren desert due to sudden climatic changes when water-laden winds shifted north around 15,000 years ago.

name: Saharans
description: An indigo-black group of herders and agriculturists who inhabited the Sahara when it was open grazing land. They carried extensive strains of the extinct green and orange races, never engaged in manufacturing or city-building, and dispersed to various regions including Spain, Egypt, and Ceylon when drought transformed their homeland into desert.

name: saints
