description: Distinguished souls who were elevated to sainthood in evolutionary religions, often created as intermediaries between mortals and divine courts. Their veneration included the superstitious regard for their bones and relics, and their cult contributed to the formation of celibate priesthoods in various religions.

name: Sakyamuni Buddha
description: The historical Buddha whose followers later taught that his spirit returned periodically to earth as a living Buddha, leading to the perpetuation of Buddha images, temples, rituals, and impostor living Buddhas that contradicted his original teachings against ceremonial practices.

name: salamanders
description: Unique evolutionary modifications that sprang from the fish family, along with frogs, representing important steps in the progressive differentiations of animal life that eventually culminated in human beings.

name: Salamis
description: A port city where Jesus and his companions embarked for Antioch on the Syrian coast during their Mediterranean journey.

name: salary
description: A modern word derived from salt, which was precious in Jesus's time and used for money. Salt serves by flavoring food, preserving it, and enhancing taste, thus illustrating how value comes through being spent in service.

name: Salem
description: The community established by Melchizedek that later became Jerusalem. At its height under Abraham's leadership, Salem had over 100,000 tithe payers on the rolls of the Melchizedek brotherhood and served as the center for missionary activities.

name: Salem teachings
description: The monotheistic gospel of Machiventa Melchizedek proclaiming faith in one universal God, salvation through faith rather than ritual, and the concept of Deity based on creative power. These teachings spread worldwide through missionaries and became a fundamental element in religions across the Orient and Occident over the past 4,000 years.

name: Salome (sister of Mary mother of Jesus)
description: Sister of Mary, the mother of Jesus. Mary confided in Salome about her belief that her son Jesus was destined for greatness.

name: Salome (wife of Zebedee)
description: Wife of Zebedee and mother of apostles James and John. She was a relative of Annas, the former high priest, and introduced Jesus to him during his Jerusalem visit. Salome loved Jesus as her own son and was present at the crucifixion. She discovered the empty tomb and was among the women who witnessed Jesus' resurrection appearance.

name: Salsatia
description: The Census Director of Nebadon, number 81,412 of Orvonton, stationed on Salvington. Salsatia is automatically cognizant of the birth and death of will creatures in the local universe, recording only the fact of will function without concern for individual personalities.

name: salt
description: A precious substance used for money, flavoring, and preservation in Jesus' time, from which the word 'salary' derives. Jesus used salt as a metaphor for his disciples, calling them 'the salt of the earth' with a saving savor. Salt was among the earliest medicines and trade commodities, and primitive humans obtained it by dipping food in ashes.

name: salutations
description: Elaborate ceremonial greetings common in oriental culture. Jesus instructed his seventy messengers to 'salute no man by the way' to avoid wasting time on lengthy social ceremonies and focus on their gospel mission.

name: salvage plan
description: A provisional feature of the fundamental bestowal plan of the Paradise Sons of God, unaffected by sin and rebellion.

name: salvation
description: The gift of God bestowed through faith, comprising seven levels of spiritual enlightenment leading to eternal life and unity with God. Jesus taught that salvation is not earned by works but received by believing in divine sonship, and believers should focus on loving and serving others rather than seeking personal glory.

name: Salvington
description: The architectural headquarters sphere of Nebadon, the local universe, situated at its exact energy-mass center. Salvington is surrounded by ten university clusters of forty-nine spheres each and serves as the supreme judicial and coordinating authority. It houses Christ Michael's threefold mansion of light and functions as the center of universe administration and education.

name: Salvington University
description: The educational complex surrounding Salvington, consisting of ten university clusters of forty-nine spheres each, where ascending mortals are spiritualized following their constellation socialization.

name: Samaria
description: A region in central Palestine between Judea and Galilee. Jesus traveled through Samaria during his final tour as a private individual and appeared to Nalda and seventy-five Samaritan believers near Jacob's well at Sychar following his resurrection.

name: Samaritans
description: A people living in central Palestine who had long-standing religious and political enmity with the Jews, dating from the return from Babylonian captivity when Samaritans worked to prevent Jerusalem's rebuilding. They built a temple on Mount Gerizim where they worshiped Yahweh and tribal gods until it was destroyed by John Hyrcanus, and despite mutual hostility with Jews, Jesus ministered among them and Philip later conducted successful evangelistic work in their cities.

name: Samson
description: A revered Hebrew Nazarite whose example of lifelong consecration was followed by John the Baptist. Nazarites like Samson were regarded as sanctified and holy personalities, receiving almost the same respect as high priests.

name: Samuel
description: The first Hebrew prophet who centralized tribal organizations and functioned as a teacher and reformer. A virile and resolute man from a long line of Salem teachers, Samuel proclaimed Yahweh as the unchanging, all-powerful Creator and made significant contributions to the development of Hebrew monotheism, being the first to declare that God was changeless and did not repent like humans.

name: Sanctities of Service
description: One of seven types of tertiary seconaphim collectively assigned to Trinitized Sons of Attainment. These secoraphic service indicators are mind readers and soul revealers who fully analyze the true and hidden motives of any service, revealing the real nature of service rendered by both mortals and angels.

name: sanctuary
description: Places of refuge that evolved from sacred market squares where fugitives could find safety from attack. Sanctuary provided a means for escaping sudden group anger and lynch law when society failed to punish crimes, representing an early form of legal protection.

name: sand
description: Fine particles carried by hot winds during sandstorms, notably mentioned in connection with Jesus' childhood accident when he fell down stone stairs during an unexpected July sandstorm, and during the crucifixion when Jesus wrote in the sand to dismiss the woman's accusers and when fierce sandstorms darkened the sky as he died on the cross.

name: sandals
description: Footwear worn by Jesus that was divided among the Roman soldiers at his crucifixion. John the Baptist also referenced sandals metaphorically when declaring his unworthiness to unloose the sandal straps of the one coming after him.

name: Sandmatia
description: An evolutionary and progressive local system neighboring Satania in the constellation of Norlatiadek, one of the immediate neighboring systems in the administrative organization of the constellation.

name: sandstone
description: Sedimentary rock formed from deposits made in shallow water where waves prevented mud settling. Red sandstone deposits found across continents suggest ancient arid or semiarid conditions, and pressure and folding have transformed sandstone into quartz in various regions, with some formations containing trilobite fossils from primitive marine life.

name: sandstorms
description: Fierce storms carrying fine sand particles, typically occurring during the rainy season but sometimes at unexpected times. Notable sandstorms include the July storm that caused Jesus' childhood accident and the sandstorm during the crucifixion that darkened the sky as Jesus died.

name: Sangik family
description: The Sangik family consisted of 19 intelligent children born to Badonite parents who became the ancestors of all six colored races of Urantia. Their skins manifested a unique tendency to turn various colors upon exposure to sunlight, ultimately giving rise to the red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and indigo races.

name: Sangiks
description: The Sangiks were the six colored races of Urantia that originated from the Sangik family 500,000 years ago. Divided into primary Sangiks (red, blue, and yellow) and secondary Sangiks (orange, green, and indigo), they were all cannibalistic and belligerent, unlike the peaceful Andonites. The Sangiks originated in the highlands northwest of India and became the dominant racial influence on Urantia.

name: Sanhedrin
description: The Sanhedrin was the supreme Jewish religious council and court, consisting of at least 23 members who could ordain teachers and rabbis and had authority over Jewish law. Only the Sanhedrin could legally condemn someone to death, though they required Roman approval for execution. They were strongly opposed to Jesus and ultimately orchestrated his arrest and trial.

name: sanitation
description: Sanitation practices on Urantia evolved from primitive fear-based customs to more advanced health measures. The Garden of Eden maintained superior sanitary arrangements with wholesome drinking water and proper waste disposal. The guardians of health from the Prince's staff taught early hygiene principles including cooking food to prevent disease, though many teachings were lost until the twentieth century.

name: sanity
description: Sanity, along with health and happiness, results from the integration of truth, beauty, and goodness in human experience through the unification of energy systems, idea systems, and spirit systems.

name: sanobim
description: The sanobim are an order of celestial beings, typically mentioned alongside the cherubim as cherubim and sanobim.

name: Sansa
description: Sansa was the daughter of Adam and Laotta who grew up to become a woman of great ability. She became the wife of Sargan, chief of the northern blue races, and contributed to the advancement of the blue men of her time.

name: Sanselon
description: Sanselon is one of the neighboring universes to Nebadon in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: Sarah
description: Sarah was Abraham's brilliant and beautiful wife who lived at the Egyptian court and was nearly taken by Abimelech due to her attractiveness and intelligence. She was much younger than the Hebrew records indicate, and these ages were deliberately altered to provide for the alleged miraculous birth of Isaac.

name: sarcasm
description: Sarcasm is a form of verbal irony used to express contempt or ridicule, often employed in human communication.

name: Sarepta
description: A location in Phoenicia through which Jesus and the apostles passed during their journey from Sidon to Tyre in July of the year 29 CE.

name: Sargan
description: Chief of the northern blue races who became the husband of Sansa, the daughter of Laotta who was raised by Eve alongside Cain. Sargan's union with Sansa contributed to the advancement of the blue races of that time.

name: Sargon (King of Assyria)
description: King of Assyria who around 700 B.C. carried over 25,000 Jews from the northern kingdom of Israel into captivity during his suppression of a revolt in central Palestine, replacing them with descendants of the Cuthites, Sepharvites, and Hamathites, which sparked centuries of enmity between Jews and Samaritans.

name: Sargon (priest of Kish)
description: A priest of Kish who proclaimed himself king and conquered all of Mesopotamia and adjoining lands, ending the long period of weak rule by city priests and their priest-ridden city-states, each with their own municipal gods and ceremonial practices.

name: Sarid
description: A location several miles south of Nazareth on the Megiddo road where Mary's brother had a country farm that served as a safe refuge for Jesus and his family during an outbreak of malignant intestinal disease in Nazareth when Jesus was nearly four years old.

name: Sartaba (mount)
description: A mountain location mentioned in The Urantia Book.

name: Sasta
description: The title given to Gautama by his followers, meaning "master" or "teacher," before he became known as Buddha or "the enlightened one."

name: Satan
description: A primary Lanonandek Son of great brilliance who served as Lucifer's first lieutenant and was assigned to advocate Lucifer's cause on Urantia during the rebellion. Satan was a member of the same primary group of Lanonandeks as Lucifer but had never functioned as a System Sovereign, and ultimately failed in his mission to thwart Michael's bestowal.

name: Satania
description: The local system containing 619 inhabited worlds, including Urantia (world number 606), with its headquarters on Jerusem. Satania is one of 100 local systems that make up the constellation of Norlatiadek and was the site of the Lucifer rebellion approximately 200,000 years ago.

name: Satania Power Center
description: A Supreme Power Center of the sixth order stationed at the exact gravity focus of the Satania system, occupying a dark island of space at the astronomic center and coordinating with Master Physical Controllers to provide light, heat, water, and energy to the fifty-seven architectural worlds of Jerusem.

name: Satisfactions of Service
description: A class of secondary seconaphim angels who reflect the attitude of Paradise conduct directors and work to enhance the value of unselfish service while augmenting the satisfactions derived from it. They illuminate the deferred rewards inherent in service for the extension of truth's kingdom and help promote a wholesome competitive spirit among seraphic hosts by circulating information about exemplary service across worlds.

name: Sato
description: A direct descendant of Adamson and Ratta who led a group of 375 superior Adamsonite descendants westward from their highland home north of Mesopotamia to Greece almost two thousand years after the settlement of Crete. These tall, intellectually advanced people represented the most valuable strains of the emerging white races and were physically the most beautiful of men since the days of the first Eden.

name: Saturn
description: One of the two largest planets in our solar system, formed from the massive central portions of matter disgorged during the solar system's origin. Saturn briefly acted as a secondary sun due to its concentration of superheated solar gases and has remained largely gaseous, never cooling to complete condensation. Its rings are fragments of a disrupted satellite, broken apart by tidal gravity forces.

name: Saul (king)
description: The first king of Israel, made ruler by popular election of his troops after successfully rallying the northern clans against an Ammonite attack on the Gileadites. Saul was defeated and killed by the Philistines at Mount Gilboa, with his downfall attributed by some to attacking the Canaanite city of Gibeon, which had a peace treaty with the Ephraimites, causing Yahweh to forsake him.

name: Saul of Tarsus
description: The original name of the apostle Paul before his conversion to Christianity, also known as Paul of Tarsus.

name: savior
description: Jesus in his role as one who makes the way of salvation more clear and certain for all mortals. As a savior, Jesus brought eternal and loving salvation rather than condemnation, showing a love so strong that it awakens responsive love in human hearts and destroys sin through divine forgiveness that transcends mere mercy.

name: scaffolding
description: A metaphor used throughout The Urantia Book to describe temporary structures, concepts, or stages that serve essential purposes in development but must eventually give way to higher realities. Examples include primitive communism in early society, conceptual frameworks for understanding reality, and transitional forms in biological and spiritual evolution that bridge toward more advanced states.

name: scallops
description: Bivalve mollusks that have existed for millions of years with protective valve arrangements. These shell animals, along with mussels, clams, and oysters, have remained largely unchanged from ancient seas to the present day.

name: scalping
description: An ancient practice, along with head hunting, used as a method of capturing an enemy's soul, based on early beliefs that the soul resided in various parts of the head including the hair. This reflects primitive concepts about the soul's location before eyes came to be regarded as the windows of the soul.

name: Scandinavians
description: The Nordic peoples descended from Andite and blue race ancestors who practiced mother worship and cremation for thousands of years during the Bronze Age. They were energetic builders who engaged in amber trade from the Baltic coast and, like many cultures, worshipped triad gods long before the revelation of the Paradise Trinity.

name: scapemen
description: In primitive religious belief, a substitute person offered to the spirits to redeem one's soul from original sin. The headhunter, who practiced skull worship, could provide such a scapeman as a substitute for his own life.

name: schooling
description: The process of formal education and instruction.

name: schools of the seven circles
description: Training institutions located on the pilot worlds of the Havona circuits where Technical Advisers continue their education after superuniverse training, before proceeding to the Paradise training school for the ethics of law and the technique of Supremacy.

name: science
description: The systematic study of the material universe that approaches reality from the outside, examining physical phenomena through observation and reason. Science seeks to identify, analyze, and classify the segmented parts of the cosmos, teaching humanity the language of mathematics while purifying religion by destroying superstition and stabilizing philosophy through the elimination of error.

name: science, philosophy, and religion
description: Three fundamental approaches to understanding universal reality that together constitute the basis of human cosmic consciousness. Science deals with facts and physical causation, philosophy coordinates ideas and seeks wisdom through reason, and religion grasps values and spiritual experience through faith.

name: scientists
description: Researchers who study the material universe and often conceive of God as the First Cause or a God of force. While they may measure energy manifestations of natural phenomena, scientists must guard against mathematical pride and spiritual blindness, recognizing their limitations in understanding what these universe phenomena truly are.

name: scorpions
description: The first air-breathing animals that appeared suddenly during the Silurian age. These primitive water scorpions evolved in favorable locations and were among the early arthropods to make the transition from aquatic to terrestrial life.

name: Scotland
description: A nation that exemplified the path to peace by relinquishing its sovereignty to unite with England and Wales under the United Kingdom. Scotland was also the site of significant geological activity, including the great Caledonian Mountains and extensive volcanic eruptions in ancient times.

name: scribe of Damascus
description: The identity by which Jesus was known during his tour of the Roman world. This mysterious figure prepared the way for later Christian teachers like Peter and Paul, though they never realized his true identity as Jesus himself.

name: scribes
description: Jewish religious scholars and teachers who, along with the Pharisees and priesthood, maintained strict adherence to religious law and tradition. Though many were honest of heart and thoroughly moral, they held the Jews in bondage to ritualism and legalism that often prevented acceptance of new spiritual teachings.

name: scriptural infallibility doctrine
description: A religious doctrine asserting that sacred scriptures are without error or fault in their teachings and content.

name: sculpture
description: An art form that originated in Dalamatia where Andonite-blue man blends became master sculptors working with baked clay, and which developed from idol making as part of religion's cultural contributions to civilization.

name: Scythopolis
description: The chief Greek city of the Decapolis, located at the ancient Hebrew site of Beth-shean, featuring marble temples, an open-air theater, and amphitheater where Jesus marveled at its beauty and athletic competitions despite his father Joseph's disapproval.

name: sea commerce
description: Maritime trade and commercial activities conducted by ship across bodies of water.

name: sea cucumbers
description: Early marine animals that evolved from single-celled organisms and survived the long evolutionary struggle, representing one of the stationary types of lower animals that failed to progress further.

name: sea lions
description: Marine mammals descended from placental mammals that abandoned land for ocean life, remaining permanently in the sea along with whales, dolphins, porpoises, and seals.

name: Sea of Galilee
description: A body of water nearly 700 feet below sea level, surrounded by high banks and steep gorges, known for sudden violent windstorms and where Jesus built boats with Zebedee's family and later calmed storms during his ministry.

name: sea serpents
description: Massive marine reptiles descended from land reptiles that represented a backward evolutionary step, growing up to 75 feet long but eventually perishing due to inadequately small brains weighing less than two ounces.

name: sea squirts
description: Marine organisms that utilize vanadium instead of iron to oxygenate their blood, demonstrating how evolving creatures adapt their functions to varying environmental conditions.

name: sea urchins
description: Marine animals that were one of the outstanding evolutionary mutations 110 million years ago, increasing in numbers while other species like corals and crinoids declined during an active period of marine life development.

name: sea worms
description: Marine invertebrates that were abundant during an ancient era alongside lime-secreting algae, early coral ancestors, and jellyfish.

name: seals
description: Marine mammals that evolved from placental mammals which descended from agile reptilian dinosaurs and later adapted to ocean life.

name: seances
description: Ritualistic gatherings conducted by secret societies where ghosts were purported to appear, developed from earlier mourning rites and ancestor worship practices.

name: seaports
description: Coastal facilities and harbors that serve as ports for maritime activities and commerce.

name: seas of glass
description: Crystal receiving fields found on architectural worlds like Jerusem and Edentia that serve as landing areas for transport seraphim and as broadcast receiving stations for universe communications.

name: seasons
description: Periodic climate variations that occur on sun-heated planets, enabling agriculture which is universal among advancing atmospheric worlds.

name: Sebaste
description: The burial place of John the Baptist and the home of Abner, where John's disciples laid him to rest after his execution by Herod Antipas.

name: seconaphim
description: Ministering spirits of the superuniverses who are the offspring of the Reflective Spirits, classified into primary, secondary, and tertiary orders and serving various functions including transport, communication, and assistance to ascending mortals.

name: second coming
description: The anticipated return of revered religious leaders, a concept found across many cultures and races throughout history.

name: second experiential Trinity
description: A future experiential Trinity that will emerge in the ultimate ages, also known as the Trinity Absolute.

name: second garden
description: The second garden was Adam and Eve's home in Mesopotamia between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers after their default and departure from Eden. Protected by a 56-mile defense wall, it served as the cradle of civilization for almost thirty thousand years, where the Adamic peoples developed and later became the Andites who spread cultural progress throughout Urantia.

name: second mile
description: The concept of carrying a pack a second mile beyond what is required, which Jesus taught as a way to challenge unrighteous strangers through positive righteousness rather than passive resentment.

name: Second Person of Deity
description: The Second Person of Deity, referring to the Eternal Son within the Paradise Trinity.

name: Second Source and Center
description: The Second Source and Center, another designation for the Eternal Son as the second person of the Paradise Trinity.

name: Secondary Circuit Supervisors
description: Beings who supervise spirit-energy circuits for local universes from superuniverse headquarters. There are 84,691 stationed on Uversa, and they are created in groups of seventy on the worlds of the Supreme Executives as new universes require separate energy circuits.

name: secondary dissociators
description: Master Physical Controllers with enormous antigravity endowment who work in reverse to primary associators, evolving limitless supplies of energy from unknown forms of matter. Though they appear automatic and mechanical, they demonstrate differential intelligence and serve as wonder-workers of time within cosmic mandates.

name: secrecy
description: A concept that appeals to vanity and confers power through mystery, historically used in secret societies to preserve valuable knowledge, maintain social hierarchy, and control groups. While secrecy has been essential to fraud and superstition, divine truth seeks full illumination and understanding.

name: secret police
description: Early law enforcement agents employed by secret societies during peacetime to maintain order and surveillance, historically used by kings as a counterbalance to the power of secret societies.

name: secret societies
description: Ancient social organizations that evolved from religious cults and political clubs, using mysterious initiations, oaths, and secrecy to build social castes and control tribal behavior. They served various functions including vigilance, law enforcement, education, and preparation for adulthood, eventually dividing into sociopolitical and religio-mystical types.

name: secrets
description: Hidden knowledge or information, often associated with mystery and power in human societies, though divine truth ultimately seeks full revelation and understanding.

name: Secrets of Greatness
description: A type of tertiary seconaphim assigned to Trinitized Sons of Attainment that works inseparably with the Soul of Goodness to reflect and measure divine greatness. These angels demonstrate that true greatness is synonymous with divinity and is manifested through unselfish service and self-control.

name: Secrets of Supremacy
description: A classification of beings or order mentioned in The Urantia Book, though specific details about their nature and function are not provided in the available context.

name: sects
description: Religious divisions or denominations that have separated from larger religious bodies, often developing distinct practices, beliefs, or organizational structures.

name: secularism
description: A philosophical approach that emphasizes material and worldly concerns while ignoring or denying the reality of God and spiritual values. While secularism contributed to human liberties and social progress by breaking ecclesiastical control, it risks establishing godless domination and lacks the spiritual foundations necessary for lasting moral advancement and social harmony.

name: See Fuch
description: The Salem missionaries established their headquarters at See Fuch in China, training Chinese teachers to spread the doctrine of the Most High God across Asia.

name: seeds
description: Featured prominently in Jesus' parables about the kingdom of heaven, including the parable of the sower and the mustard seed. Seeds represent the living truth of the gospel that grows and develops across generations, as well as the evolutionary development of plant life that provided better food supply for animal life.

name: seers
description: Individuals gifted with spiritual insight or the ability to perceive divine truths and future events, often serving as religious guides or prophetic figures.

name: segregata
description: The primordial form of pure energy that Primary Master Force Organizers transmute from space potency. This basic force represents the first stage in the creation of physical reality and serves as the foundation for all subsequent energy manifestations in the universe.

name: Segub
description: Segub, youngest son of a petty king in Palestine, was sacrificed in building the walls of Jericho, according to the word of the Lord.

name: selective assorters
description: A type of Morontia Power Supervisor that re-keys or advance-tunes ascending mortals as they progress between morontia worlds and phases. They certify beings for advanced standing based on spiritual achievement and group morontia personalities for study, teaching, and temporary collaborative projects.

name: Seleucids
description: A Hellenistic dynasty that ruled parts of the ancient Near East following the conquests of Alexander the Great.

name: self
description: The individual identity and personality of a being, encompassing both material and spiritual aspects. The self is a cosmic reality that can achieve eternal survival through alignment with divine will and cooperation with the indwelling Thought Adjuster. True success comes from focusing on important work rather than self-importance, as self-glorification exhausts energy needed for meaningful achievement.

name: self-abasement
description: Ostentatious displays of humility or self-deprecation. True spiritual achievement comes through loving service and sincere consecration to divine aims, not through hypocritical or exaggerated acts of self-abasement.

name: self-acting Adjusters
description: A classification of Thought Adjusters that possess enhanced autonomy and experience in guiding mortal subjects toward spiritual development.

name: self-admiration
description: Excessive pride in oneself that leads to the exploitation of others and the assumption of unjust power over fellow beings, contrasted with true liberty that comes from genuine self-respect and self-control.

name: self-aggrandizement
description: The pursuit of personal power, glory, or advancement at the expense of others. A mark of high civilization is possessing power while refusing to use it for self-aggrandizement, instead choosing altruistic service over selfish gain.

name: self-analysis
description: The practice of examining one's own character, motivations, and spiritual progress. While useful for preventing conceited egotism and understanding one's true spiritual capacity, Jesus taught self-forgetfulness and character growth rather than introspective self-examination.

name: self-assertion
description: The aggressive pursuit of one's own interests and rights, often at the expense of others. Self-assertion was the battle cry of Lucifer's rebellion, advocating equality of mind and brotherhood of intelligence, but when unchecked, it leads to destructive conflicts and separation from divine communion.

name: self-confidence
description: Assurance in one's abilities and decisions, particularly when founded on doing the Father's will. Reasonable self-confidence distinguishes humans from animals and enables transcendence, but it must be balanced to avoid presumptuous behavior that creates difficulties.

name: self-consciousness
description: The awareness of oneself as a distinct individual entity, representing a fundamental aspect of personality development and spiritual identity formation in conscious beings.

name: self-contemplation
description: The practice of thoughtful introspection and reflection upon oneself, one's actions, and inner nature.

name: self-control
description: The ability to regulate one's impulses, emotions, and behaviors, considered the acme of all human virtues and the supreme test of civilization's advancement. True liberty is the fruit of self-control, which leads to altruistic service and represents the real measure of planetary greatness.

name: self-criticism
description: The practice of evaluating and finding fault with oneself, one's actions, or beliefs through critical examination.

name: self-deception
description: The act of misleading oneself through false beliefs or denial of truth, often manifesting as the desire to exercise power over others or the justification of one's errors and harmful actions. Self-deception can lead to fears, enslaving pleasures, and continued wrongdoing despite one's better knowledge.

name: self-defense
description: The right and practice of protecting oneself from harm or aggression. While individuals may defend themselves against aggressors lacking moral judgment, mercy ministry remains the work of individuals while justice and punishment are responsibilities of social groups and governments.

name: self-denial
description: The practice of sacrificing immediate desires or pleasures for future benefit or spiritual development. While primitive religions emphasized extreme self-denial through fasting and self-torture, Jesus taught that self-control is better than extreme self-denial for balanced living.

name: self-determination
description: The concept of individual or group autonomy in making decisions about one's own affairs. The fallacy of unlimited self-determination will be overcome under global government, while Lucifer's rebellion included advocating excessive liberty of individual self-determination against universal training plans.

name: self-discipline
description: The practice of training oneself to act in accordance with rules, standards, or goals through controlled behavior and restraint of impulses.

name: self-distribution
description: The practice or concept of spreading or allocating oneself across multiple areas, tasks, or responsibilities.

name: self-exaltation
description: The act of elevating or glorifying oneself, often associated with pride and the pursuit of personal aggrandizement over righteous service.

name: self-examination
description: The practice of introspectively analyzing one's thoughts, motives, actions, and character for personal growth and spiritual development.

name: self-expression
description: The manifestation of one's inner thoughts, feelings, personality, and creative abilities through various forms of communication and action.

name: self-forgetfulness
description: A spiritual attitude where one focuses on serving others and doing God's will rather than dwelling on personal concerns or seeking self-aggrandizement. This quality characterized Jesus's life and represents the ideal of the new religion, which teaches self-forgetfulness and enhanced self-realization rather than self-sacrifice.

name: self-government
description: The ability to govern oneself through personal discipline and self-control, exercising autonomous authority over one's actions and decisions.

name: self-gratification
description: The pursuit of personal pleasure and satisfaction through various means including food, play, humor, and sex. While natural and serving as an incentive for marriage and social cooperation, excessive self-gratification poses the greatest threat to family life and civilization when it becomes the primary goal rather than self-maintenance and self-perpetuation.

name: self-importance
description: An exaggerated sense of one's own significance or worth that can lead to spiritual blindness and failure. This attitude prevents proper understanding of divine relationships and can result in serious spiritual consequences, as demonstrated by Lucifer and Caligastia's downfall. Humor serves as a divine antidote to such ego exaltation.

name: self-justification
description: The tendency to rationalize or defend one's actions, beliefs, or behavior as morally or logically correct, often to avoid acknowledging fault or responsibility.

name: self-maintenance
description: The fundamental drive for self-preservation and survival that builds society and encompasses practices rooted in food hunger and self-preservation instincts, including industry, property, and social regulations. While essential for civilization, it must be balanced with self-perpetuation to avoid the destructive effects of excessive self-gratification.

name: self-mastery
description: The achievement of complete control over one's impulses, emotions, and desires through disciplined effort and spiritual growth.

name: self-perpetuation
description: The biological and social drive to ensure species survival through reproduction and family formation. This fundamental instinct, along with self-maintenance, forms the basis of marriage and family institutions, with individual impulse securing what society deems necessary for racial continuation.

name: self-pity
description: Jesus taught that one cannot develop strong characters by indulging in self-pity, advising believers to avoid the deceptive influence of false sympathy and mere fellowship in misery.

name: self-realization
description: The progressive fulfillment of one's potential through environmental adaptation, artistic expression, and spiritual growth. True self-realization involves identifying with cosmic reality and supreme values while maintaining social responsibility, as antisocial self-realization is potentially evil since no being exists in isolation.

name: self-respect
description: True self-respect is rooted in spiritual living and coordinate with love and service of others, not self-admiration. It is essential for moral development, as its loss can paralyze the will, while excessive self-respect can lead to pride and arrogance.

name: self-righteousness
description: A form of spiritual pride characterized by an inflated sense of one's own moral superiority or religious standing.

name: self-satisfaction
description: A state of contentment with one's achievements or circumstances that can lead to spiritual complacency and hinder further growth.

name: self-torture
description: Physical self-infliction of pain practiced in ancient mourning rituals to please ghosts, initiation ceremonies to harden youth, and religious disciplines. True spiritual cooperation with the Thought Adjuster does not require such practices but rather loving service toward eternal destiny.

name: selfhood
description: The state or quality of being an individual self, encompassing one's unique identity, consciousness, and personal existence as distinct from others.

name: selfishness
description: The tendency to prioritize one's own interests over those of others, which leads to spiritual darkness and ultimately destroys even the highest forms of government and social progress. Religious experience transforms selfish motivations into unselfish acts of service, as the Thought Adjuster places preference on altruistic impulses over egoistic ones.

name: selflessness
description: The quality of putting others' needs and interests before one's own, characterized by self-forgetfulness and altruistic service that reflects spiritual maturity and divine values.

name: Selta
description: A Jewish writer attached to Emperor Caligula's court who authored an apocalyptic work about the Messiah in the early second century. His writings, including the parable of the ten virgins, were later copied into the Gospel of Matthew and parts added to Mark and Luke, contributing to confusion about Jesus' teachings on Jerusalem's destruction and his second coming.

name: Semites
description: The Semites were among the most blended of Urantia peoples, containing hereditary factors from almost all nine world races. They included the Babylonians, Chaldeans, Phoenicians, Jews, Carthaginians, and Canaanites, occupying strategic positions in the fertile crescent and along Mediterranean trade routes. Early Semites practiced animistic religions with pantheons of nature spirits before evolving toward monotheism.

name: seniority
description: A hierarchical system based on order of creation or achievement, establishing leadership and precedence among spiritual beings and orders. Examples include the original Michael leading all Creator Sons, Grandfanda as the first mortal to reach Havona serving as head of the Finality Corps, and the first-born seraphim presiding over training schools in local universes.

name: senses
description: Physical and spiritual perception capabilities that vary by being type and evolutionary level. Mortals typically possess twelve special physical senses, while three-brained mortals have enhanced abilities beyond Urantia races. Havona natives possess forty-nine specialized sensation forms, morontia beings have seventy, and high spiritual orders may have up to 210 specialized forms of sensation.

name: sentiment
description: Emotional appeal or feeling that can either advance or hinder spiritual progress. While divine sentiment governs God's relationship with creatures as the Universal Father, false sentiment that perpetuates defective human strains impedes racial improvement. True spiritual work should appeal to the divine spirit within rather than manipulating human emotions or sentiment.

name: separation
description: The act of setting apart or creating distinct boundaries, particularly in religious contexts. Religious groups function best when separated from secular associations to focus on spiritual membership in the kingdom of heaven. Jesus demonstrated the necessity of open separation from established religious authorities when their teachings conflicted with spiritual truth.

name: Sepharvites
description: A people group installed in northern Israel by Sargon, king of Assyria, around 700 B.C. as part of his colonization policy. Along with Cuthites and Hamathites, they replaced over 25,000 Jews who had been carried into captivity, contributing to the longstanding enmity between Jews and Samaritans that lasted over six centuries.

name: Sepphoris
description: An important trade center and former capital of Galilee, rebuilt by Herod Antipas during Jesus' youth. Located about three miles northwest of Nazareth, it served as one of Herod's residences until the capital was moved to Tiberias. Jesus worked there as a carpenter and smith, gaining exposure to Gentile culture, though he later advised his apostles to avoid the city due to its low moral standards.

name: sepulchres
description: Burial places or tombs used for interring the dead, serving as monuments to the deceased and often reflecting cultural beliefs about death and the afterlife.

name: sequentiality
description: The ordered progression of events and experiences in time, representing the temporal framework within which finite beings experience reality and spiritual growth.

name: Serapatatia
description: A brilliant brown-tinted leader of the western Nodite Syrian confederation who became Adam's lieutenant and Eve's confidant. Despite being entirely honest and sincere, he was unknowingly manipulated by Caligastia and became impatient with the long-term plan for world uplift. His private meetings with Eve led to her default, after which he drowned himself in remorse, sparking warfare between Adamites and Nodites.

name: seraphic, secoraphic, and superaphic transport
description: A celestial transportation system utilizing the energy-manipulating abilities of seraphim, seconaphim, and supernaphim to carry personalities across space. Transport seraphim can carry all beings except material flesh and blood bodies, which must first undergo dematerialization. The transported being enters transit sleep while enclosed in energy shields, traveling at speeds approaching 559,000 miles per second.

name: Seraphic Corps of Completion
description: An order of seraphim who have achieved Paradise, received the personal embrace of the Eternal Son, and attained perfection of existence and supremacy of service. These completion seraphim serve as associates to superuniverse seconaphim and assistants to Paradise-Havona supernaphim, having finished their career of time and become eternal servants of God.

name: seraphic evangels
description: First-order transition minister seraphim who serve as teachers and guides for surviving mortals on the mansion worlds. They help ascenders choose optimal routes to higher destinations, proclaim the gospel of eternal progression, and counsel adherence to preaching God's goodness and love. These seraphim whispered the 23rd Psalm to the shepherd boy and continue to inspire spiritual growth.

name: seraphic guardians of destiny
description: Personal seraphic attendants assigned to guide and protect individual mortals throughout their earthly careers and beyond. These guardian angels accompany their human wards through the mansion worlds and may continue the relationship through Paradise ascension, achieving their own spiritual advancement through devoted service to ascending mortals.

name: seraphic hosts
description: The collective assemblies of seraphim organized for various celestial functions and ministries. These angelic hosts serve throughout the local universe in organized groups, participating in worship, transport, education, and planetary supervision under the direction of their superiors.

name: seraphim
description: Angels of the local universes who serve as ministering spirits to evolutionary mortals and ascending beings. Created by the Universe Mother Spirit, seraphim function in pairs and are organized into multiple orders including planetary helpers, transition ministers, and guardian angels. They provide guidance, protection, and spiritual ministry while progressing toward their own destiny goal of Seraphington and eventual Paradise attainment.

name: seraphim of the future
description: Planetary helper seraphim held in reserve near Salvington who minister to mortals surviving through modified orders of ascension. These angels are being prepared for extensive service during the future age of light and life in Nebadon, when they will serve on the more advanced and spiritually mature worlds.

name: Seraphington
description: The sacred Paradise sphere that serves as the destiny world and eternal home for all orders of ministering angels including seraphim, seconaphim, and supernaphim. Known as the "bosom of the Son and the Spirit," Seraphington is the angelic threshold to Paradise and Deity attainment, where angels achieve completion and become forever immune to sin.

name: serfdom
description: A historical system of feudal bondage where peasants were bound to the land and subject to their lord's authority. Serfdom represented a form of economic and social control that restricted personal freedom and mobility, eventually evolving toward more democratic and equitable social arrangements.

name: Sermon on the Mount
description: Jesus' ordination charge to his twelve apostles, containing the beatitudes and instruction for their ministry. While containing helpful spiritual teachings founded on faith and love rather than law and duty, this discourse was specifically directed to the apostles as representatives of the kingdom, not intended as the general gospel message for all humanity.

name: servant
description: A servant is one who provides service to others, with the understanding that no servant is greater than their master. In the spiritual realm, few duties exist for the lone servant, as teamwork becomes increasingly important at higher levels of existence. The concept emphasizes humble service and accountability in discipleship.

name: service
description: In the spiritual world, all service is sacred and exhilarating, with the ascension plan preparing mortals for transcendent service throughout the grand universe. True service involves wholehearted devotion to the welfare of others, intelligent ministry to real needs, and loving social service that flows naturally from spiritual growth. Service is both the cornerstone of spiritual greatness and the practical expression of divine love in action.

name: Set
description: Set was the Egyptian god of darkness and evil who was believed to have cruelly killed Osiris, the consort of Isis. In Egyptian mythology, Set lost his eye in a legendary battle with Horus, which was later restored by the god Thoth using spittle.

name: Seth
description: Seth was the eldest surviving son of Adam and Eve born in the second garden, born 129 years after Adam's arrival on Urantia. He became absorbed in improving the spiritual status of his people and originated the priesthood, becoming the head of the new priesthood of the second garden.

name: Sethard
description: Sethard was one of seven outstanding human teachers who combined the best elements of ethical and religious teachings, alongside Moses, Zoroaster, Lao-tse, Buddha, Philo, and Paul.

name: Sethites
description: The Sethites were the priesthood founded by Seth that embraced religion, health, and education as a threefold undertaking. They were great post-Adamic teachers who spread their influence among the Greeks, Sumerians, and Hindus, where their descendants became the Brahmans. The Sethite priests maintained advanced concepts of Deity and the Trinity, and their cultural and religious influence persisted for thousands of years, particularly in India.

name: seven
description: A numerical concept representing completeness and spiritual significance throughout The Urantia Book, appearing in various contexts such as the Seven Master Spirits, seven psychic circles, and other divine organizational structures.

name: Seven Absolutes of Infinity
description: The seven fundamental reality levels that constitute the totality of infinity, representing the complete spectrum of absolute existence and divine reality.

name: Seven Central Supervisors
description: Divine beings who serve in supervisory capacities in the central universe, working in coordination with the Seven Master Spirits in the administration of cosmic affairs.

name: seven psychic circles
description: The seven levels of human psychic achievement that mortals must master in their spiritual development, representing progressive attainment in the coordination of personality growth and Adjuster harmony.

name: seven sacred worlds of Eternal Son
description: Seven sacred spheres belonging to the Eternal Son, which are among the sacred spheres of Paradise.

name: seven secret spheres of Universal Father
description: Seven secret spheres belonging to the Universal Father, which are among the sacred spheres of Paradise.

name: seven spirits of God
description: Seven spirits seen by the prophet in symbolic vision, described as "like lamps burning before the throne," which the prophet observed but without seeing the seats of the twenty-four sentinels that represent universe headquarters and system capitals.

name: Seven Spirits of Havona Circuits
description: The joint impersonal representation of the Infinite Spirit and the Seven Master Spirits to the seven circuits of the central universe. These spirits are the servants of the Master Spirits and provide unified spiritual supervision for Havona, with each spirit permeating and coordinating celestial affairs throughout a single circuit.

name: seven superuniverses
description: The seven major administrative divisions of the grand universe, each overseen by specific administrative beings and representing the primary organizational structure of post-Havona creation.

name: Seven Supreme Executives
description: The administrative coordinators of the grand universe who function as the board of managing directors for post-Havona creation. These seven beings were trinitized by the Father, Son, and Spirit to serve as universal representatives of the Seven Master Spirits, overseeing all physical, intellectual, and spiritual matters in the seven superuniverses and Havona.

name: seven Supreme Power Directors
description: The physical-energy regulators of the grand universe, created by the Seven Master Spirits as the first recorded instance of semimaterial progeny from true spirit ancestry. These seven directors regulate power and energy throughout the superuniverses while maintaining force-focal headquarters on peripheral Paradise.

name: seven Supreme Spirit groups
description: A classification of supreme spiritual orders that serve in various capacities throughout the universe hierarchy.

name: seven worlds of Infinite Spirit
description: Seven sacred spheres belonging to the Infinite Spirit, which are among the sacred spheres of Paradise.

name: Sevenfold
description: A fundamental organizational principle in the universe administration, most notably manifested in God the Sevenfold and various seven-fold administrative structures throughout creation.

name: sevenfold controllers
description: The seven groups of controllers that comprise the physical control level of God the Sevenfold, being functionally inseparable from this Deity association and encompassing the grand universe alongside its creators.

name: sewers
description: Urban drainage systems that were flushed by tides in ancient cities like Caesarea, where Jesus and his companions stayed and Ganid learned about the city's water management techniques.

name: sex
description: The biological and social aspects of gender relations that have always involved choice and cooperation, serving as both a civilizing force that compels altruistic behavior and a source of ongoing social regulation throughout human evolution.

name: sexes
description: The complementary male and female variations of humanity that, despite their differences in viewpoint and personality trends, remain mutually dependent throughout their mortal, morontial, and spiritual careers, with their cooperation essential for civilization's continuation and reproduction of the species.

name: Shabattum
description: The Babylonian name for the seventh day that was considered taboo-ridden and unlucky in ancient Mesopotamia, with its associated prohibitions later carried back to Palestine by the Jews and incorporated into their Sabbath observances.

name: shadows
description: Primitive humans feared and revered shadows as proof of a double self or soul, while philosophically they represent the material reflection cast by divine reality, serving as imperfect representations that should be interpreted in terms of their true spiritual substance.

name: shale
description: A sedimentary rock formed from deposits in deeper, quieter waters that has been transformed by pressure and folding into slate in many regions, commonly found alternating with coal and limestone in various geological formations.

name: Shalmaneser III
description: An Assyrian king whose decision to control the Mediterranean coast led to the battle of Karkar, where King Ahab of Ephraim and allied groups fought him to a draw, though this significant conflict went unrecorded in the Old Testament.

name: shamans
description: The earliest professional class of medicine men and spiritual intermediaries who served as priests, healers, and ceremonial leaders in primitive societies, often wielding significant political power despite frequently resorting to fraud and trickery to maintain their authority.

name: Shamash
description: One of the chief Mesopotamian deities that the Salem teachers elevated alongside Bel and Ea, later incorporated into the Babylonian triad as part of their effort to reduce the number of gods in ancient Mesopotamian religion.

name: shame
description: A feeling of humiliation or distress caused by consciousness of wrong or foolish behavior. Religion warns against shame as an unworthy motivation for religious living, emphasizing that faith should be motivated by God's love rather than fear and shame.

name: Shang-ti
description: The Taoist God of Heaven who was the focal point of Singlangton's teachings, leading the Chinese people toward monotheistic belief in the Spirit of Heaven. Imperial worship of Shang-ti preserved vestiges of a real concept of God even as the Chinese ideographic writing system limited the spread of religious knowledge.

name: sharing
description: A divine pattern of participation and distribution that characterizes God's nature, seen in the Father's sharing with the Son and Spirit, and exemplified by creatures who willingly share life, knowledge, and spiritual experiences with others. Knowledge is possessed only by sharing, safeguarded by wisdom, and socialized by love.

name: sharks
description: Large predatory fish that are survivors of ancient teeth-bearing varieties from earlier geological epochs, some of which reached twenty-five to thirty feet in length. The shell-feeding sharks were highly evolved and dominated the oceans for more than five million years.

name: Shawnee Tenskwatawa
description: A Shawnee religious leader and prophet, also known as Tenskwatawa, who played a significant role in Native American religious and cultural movements.

name: Shechem
description: An ancient Palestinian city that Jesus passed through during his final tour as a private individual through Palestine. It was also one of the destinations where David's messengers carried news of Jesus' crucifixion.

name: sheep
description: Domesticated animals that were among the first livestock raised by ancient farmers around 5000 B.C. Used frequently by Jesus in parables, particularly the lost sheep parable illustrating God's active search for repentant sinners, and in his teaching about being the Good Shepherd who knows and cares for his flock.

name: shekels
description: Ancient Jewish silver coins used for temple taxes and religious obligations. Every first-born son was redeemed for five shekels according to Mosaic law, and the temple tax was one-half shekel. Money-changers in the temple exchanged various currencies for the required Jewish shekels.

name: Shekinah
description: A Jewish symbol of the Divine Presence that was believed to have once resided in the temple. The Jews hoped that the coming Messiah would restore the Shekinah and redeem humanity from its inherent evil nature.

name: shell
description: Used metaphorically to represent forms or structures that serve a purpose but become valueless once their function is fulfilled. Just as a shell protects the developing chick but has no worth after hatching, forms are valuable only until the lessons they contain are learned.

name: Shema
description: The Jewish creed of faith consisting of numerous passages from the law, recited to indicate that worshipers took upon themselves the yoke of the kingdom of heaven and the commandments as applied to day and night.

name: Shemer
description: A landowner whose estate King Omri attempted to purchase during a period of civil wars and political instability in Israel, before the Assyrian threat under Shalmaneser III changed the region's history.

name: Shensi
description: A Chinese province where Andite immigrants stimulated rapid development of advanced civilization through metalworking and manufacture, and where hydraulic problems in agriculture led to group cooperation and peaceful relations among farming communities.

name: Sheol
description: In Hebrew belief, the destination for the deceased soul, conceived as a place where a phantom replica of the individual went down and could not return to the land of the living.

name: shepherd
description: A pastoral figure used extensively as a metaphor for divine care and leadership, with God portrayed as the shepherd who feeds his flock, leads them to green pastures and still waters, and seeks out the lost sheep with loving dedication.

name: Shiloh
description: A location where Yahweh's temple was situated but later became discredited, leading to the centralization of worship at Jebus in the northern kingdom while Judah fell under tribute to the Pharaohs.

name: Shinran
description: A Japanese Buddhist teacher who, along with Ryonin and Honen Shonin, helped develop the mature God concept in Buddhism that culminated in belief in Amida Buddha.

name: Shinto
description: A Japanese religion that affected Buddhism and contains remnants of earlier Melchizedek teachings, representing a form of proto-Taoism that influenced religio-philosophic thought and included worship of the state through the imperial family.

name: ship
description: A metaphor for the human spiritual journey where mind serves as the vessel, the Thought Adjuster as the pilot, and human will as the captain navigating toward eternal survival.

name: Shittim
description: One of the cities and towns in Perea where Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers conducted their final ministry mission from January to March in A.D. 30.

name: shock
description: A psychological reaction mentioned in contexts of spectacle or trauma. In ancient Greece, lower classes made ceremonial entertainment from executions of condemned criminals. During Jesus' crucifixion, curious onlookers followed the procession to Golgotha seeking the shock of witnessing the event.

name: shooting stars
description: Meteoric phenomena visible in the night sky, appearing as brief streaks of light as small celestial objects burn up in Earth's atmosphere.

name: short cuts
description: Attempted bypasses of the established divine plan for spiritual progression. There are no shortcuts, royal roads, or easy paths to Paradise; spiritual growth requires mastering each lesson and sphere in the proper sequence on the ascension journey.

name: short space rays
description: A type of space radiation with shorter wavelength characteristics, part of the broader spectrum of cosmic energy phenomena.

name: showbread
description: Sacred bread placed in the temple that was lawfully eaten only by priests. Jesus referenced David eating showbread when hungry to defend his apostles' action of eating grain on the Sabbath, arguing that human needs take precedence over rigid ceremonial law.

name: shrewd steward
description: The central figure in Jesus' parable teaching that the children of the world are often wiser in their generation than the children of the kingdom, emphasizing the importance of wise preparation for both earthly and spiritual matters.

name: shrimps
description: Marine crustaceans that, along with crabs and lobsters, have remained virtually unchanged since ancient geological periods. They represent early life forms that have survived to the present time practically unaltered.

name: shrines
description: Sacred places or structures dedicated to religious worship or commemoration, typically smaller and more intimate than temples.

name: shrub of Edentia
description: A plant species native to Edentia, the constellation headquarters world, possibly related to or similar in function to the tree of life.

name: Shunem
description: An ancient village where Jesus heard stories of the beautiful maiden who once lived there and the wonderful works performed by the prophet Elisha. Jesus later taught about salvation to a group of evangelists and women in this historic location.

name: shunning
description: The primitive emotional response of avoiding or rejecting something based on feeling rather than rational thought, which along with fearing, honoring, and worshiping, guided early evolutionary development.

name: Siam
description: A region in eastern Asia where Buddhism became established through the missionary efforts of Asoka, who built a great Indian empire by spreading Buddhist teachings to the farthest frontiers of the known world.

name: Siberia
description: A vast northern region of Asia that served as a corridor for human migration, where Andonites were driven by expanding Chinese populations and where the red race left genetic imprints before migrating to North America. The region was connected to Alaska by the Bering Strait land bridge and experienced significant glacial activity during ice ages.

name: Sibmah
description: One of the cities and towns in Perea where the seventy messengers, along with Jesus and the twelve apostles, conducted their final ministry mission from January 3, A.D. 30 until Jesus' departure for Jerusalem.

name: Sicilian land bridge
description: An ancient land connection between Europe and Africa that allowed early human migration and the movement of mammalian species, including straight-tusked elephants and African lions into Europe. The bridge eventually submerged, creating the unified Mediterranean Sea and connecting it with the Atlantic Ocean.

name: Sicily
description: An island in the Mediterranean that served as both a refuge for marine life during oceanic cooling periods and a destination for Thomas's missionary work, where he preached the gospel before his eventual martyrdom in Malta.

name: sick
description: Those afflicted with physical or mental ailments who became a focus of Jesus' ministry and apostolic work. The apostles were instructed to minister to the sick while preaching the kingdom, visiting homes to comfort the afflicted and provide healing, though they were cautioned against teaching expectations of miraculous cures.

name: Siddhartha
description: The given name of Gautama Buddha, the founder of Buddhism who became known as the enlightened one.

name: Siddim
description: The location of a significant battle where Abraham achieved victory, after which he became leader of a confederation of eleven tribes and established a powerful state in Palestine, paying tithes to Melchizedek and consolidating his influence in the region.

name: Sidon
description: A Phoenician coastal city where Jesus and his associates stayed for almost two and a half weeks during their northern journey, resting at the home of a former Bethsaida hospital patient. Jesus used the city as an example of a place that would have repented if it had witnessed the mighty works performed in Chorazin, Bethsaida, and Capernaum.

name: Sierras
description: The Sierras are a mountain range on the Pacific coast that began forming during the modern mountain-building stage of geologic history. Their gold-bearing quartz strata are the product of lava flows from this epoch, and they have been continuously rising for millions of years.

name: sifting
description: A metaphorical process of separation and testing. Amos warned that Yahweh would sift the house of Israel like wheat among all nations, while Jesus taught that the sifting of the kingdom, though distressing, was necessary for the spiritual teachings of heaven to shine forth in new glory.

name: Significances of Origins
description: A type of tertiary seconaphim who serve as living ready-reference genealogies of the vast hosts of beings throughout the seven superuniverses. They provide up-to-date estimates of ancestral factors and current status of any individual, teaching ascending creatures about spirit ethics and the paramount importance of origin in cosmic relationships.

name: signs
description: Omens, tokens, or supernatural manifestations sought to reveal divine will or predict the future. Ancient peoples interpreted spirit messages through signs, while Jesus refused to give predetermined signs to faithless generations, stating that only one sign would be given - his resurrection after three days.

name: Sikhism
description: A religion founded by Guru Nanak in the fifteenth century that synthesized teachings from Islam, Hinduism, and Buddhism. Described as one of the most advanced religions of Asia and the most recent of the major world religions.

name: Silas
description: A companion who joined Paul and Timothy in Corinth during the final months of Paul's preaching ministry there, where they encountered many who had been taught by Jesus during his earlier visit.

name: silence
description: A state of receptivity recommended by Jesus for prayer and worship. After prayer, believers should remain in silent receptivity to allow the indwelling spirit to speak to the listening soul, while Jesus himself demonstrated the power of calm and majestic silence when facing false accusations.

name: Siloam (pool)
description: A pool in Jerusalem used for ceremonial purposes and healing. Jesus directed the blind man Josiah to wash clay from his eyes there to receive sight, and over 2,000 believers were baptized there by the apostles. The pool was also used in temple ceremonies, with priests filling golden pitchers from it during feast days.

name: Siloam (tower)
description: A tower in Jerusalem that collapsed and killed eighteen men. Jesus used this tragedy as an example to teach that such accidents do not indicate greater sin in the victims, but rather show that innocent people can suffer from the accidents of time.

name: Silurian
description: An ancient geologic period from 300 million years ago when vast seas covered most of Europe and North America. These Silurian seas teemed with lime-shelled life that fell to the sea bottom and gradually built up thick layers of limestone, creating the first widespread limestone deposits.

name: Simeon (reputed holy man)
description: A reputed holy man of Israel whose tomb stood on a hill in Nazareth where Jesus often went to meditate. This hill had previously been a high place of Baal, and from this location Jesus would contemplate the historic and traditional events of his people.

name: Simeon (singer)
description: A Judean singer who, along with Anna the poetess, lingered in the temple courts and recognized the infant Jesus as the promised Messiah at his presentation. Simeon sang a hymn of redemption written by Anna, proclaiming Jesus as the salvation prepared for all peoples.

name: simians
description: Modern apes and monkeys that descended from inferior mid-mammal stock alongside humans, but from separate lineages. While humans descended from superior mid-mammal strains, simians originated from retarded couples of the same tribe who survived in hiding and became the ancestors of modern chimpanzees, gorillas, and other apes.

name: Simon (assistant of David Zebedee)
description: An assistant of David Zebedee who experienced a miraculous catch of fish when Jesus taught from their boat. Amazed by the miracle, Simon fell at Jesus' knees declaring himself a sinful man, and from that day forsook his nets to follow Jesus.

name: Simon (boat)
description: A fishing boat named after Simon Peter that had been built by Jesus' own hands. Jesus used this boat as a platform for teaching the multitudes gathered by the seaside, including when he delivered the parable of the sower.

name: Simon (brother of Jesus)
description: The third son of Joseph and Mary, born April 14, A.D. 2. Simon was a well-meaning but dreamy boy who graduated from school and worked as a stone mason with Jacob. He traveled with Jesus to Jerusalem for Passover, married his longtime sweetheart in A.D. 24, and witnessed Jesus' fourth resurrection appearance before his family.

name: Simon (brother of Jesus’ mother)
description: Mary's brother who became an officer in the Galilean division of the Zealots. His involvement in the nationalist cause created an estrangement between himself and Jesus, who refused to join the revolutionary movement despite family pressure.

name: Simon (father of Lazarus)
description: The father of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus who lived in Bethany and welcomed Jesus' family during their visits to Jerusalem. Simon was a well-to-do and honorable Jew who served as the leading resident of Bethany and hosted Passover celebrations, developing a lifelong friendship with Jesus.

name: Simon of Bethany
description: A leading citizen of Bethany who became Jesus' host during his final visits to Jerusalem. Simon gave a public banquet for Jesus and Lazarus in defiance of the Sanhedrin, provided lodging for Jesus and his apostles, and owned the ground in Gethsemane where they established their camp.

name: Simon of Bethphage
description: A believer in Bethphage who provided shelter to the apostle Thomas. Thomas stayed at Simon's home for a week after Jesus' crucifixion, brooding in solitude and refusing to believe in the resurrection until his fellow apostles finally brought him back to rejoin them.

name: Simon of Cyrene
description: Simon of Cyrene was compelled by Roman soldiers to carry Jesus' cross after the Master collapsed under its weight. A Passover visitor from northern Africa, Simon became a valiant believer after witnessing the crucifixion and led his family into the kingdom upon returning home, though he never knew that Jesus was the same man who had once befriended his injured son Rufus.

name: Simon Peter
description: Simon Peter, originally a fisherman from Bethsaida, was the first apostle chosen by Jesus and became the recognized spokesman and leader of the twelve. Despite his impulsive nature and tendency to speak before thinking, Peter's wholehearted belief in Jesus' divinity and his passionate loyalty made him the chief preacher among the apostles and the real founder of the early Christian church.

name: Simon (Sanhedrin member)
description: Simon was a Sanhedrin member who publicly espoused Jesus' teachings, causing renewed agitation among the Jewish rulers for Jesus' arrest and prompting the Master to withdraw into Samaria and the Decapolis.

name: Simon the Pharisee
description: Simon the Pharisee was an influential Jerusalem Pharisee and halfhearted believer who invited Jesus and his personal associates to his home for a social meal, during which Jesus forgave a repentant woman much to Simon's surprise.

name: Simon the tanner
description: Simon the tanner was a wealthy leather merchant in Joppa who embraced Christianity after being influenced by the profound impact Jesus had on his interpreter Gadiah, a Philistine who worked for Simon and became a devoted follower of the Master.

name: Simon Zelotes
description: Simon Zelotes was the eleventh apostle, a former fiery Zealot agitator and merchant from Capernaum who was chosen by Peter to join Jesus' followers. Despite his patriotic nationalism and struggles to reconcile his political views with spiritual teachings, he became an efficient organizer of the apostles' recreational activities and later preached the gospel throughout Africa until his death.

name: sin
description: Sin is the conscious, knowing, and deliberate transgression of divine law and the Father's will. It represents purposeful resistance to divine reality and cosmic truth, distinguished from error (misconception) and evil (maladjustment to reality) by its willful nature and personal moral guilt.

name: Sin (god)
description: Sin was one of the chief Mesopotamian deities and a member of the Babylonian triad alongside Bel and Anu, representing one of the seven principal gods that Salem teachers recognized in their efforts to reduce the pantheon of Mesopotamian religion.

name: sincerity
description: Sincerity is one of the keys to the kingdom of heaven and becomes sublime when perfected through faith and spiritual testing. It represents genuine trustfulness and honest intention, most serviceable in the work of the kingdom when combined with discretion and spiritual wisdom.

name: singers
description: Singers are celestial artisans and heavenly reproducers who serve as harmonists, reiterating the specific harmonies of the past and interpreting present melodies on the morontia level as part of the spiritual ministry in the universe.

name: Singlangton
description: Singlangton was a Chinese spiritual leader who lived approximately 100,000 years ago and was the first of the yellow men to teach and lead his people in the worship of "One Truth." His teachings on monotheism kept the Chinese among the more spiritual peoples of earth and influenced their cultural development, with the concept later evolving into Shang-ti, the God of Heaven.

name: single-origin beings
description: Single-origin beings are those of origin in any one of the Paradise Deities or otherwise created by any one being of direct or indirect descent from the Paradise Deities. This classification includes the Supreme Spirits, Ascending Orders, and the Family of the Infinite Spirit, encompassing numerous orders of celestial beings.

name: Sinkiang
description: Sinkiang (Eastern Turkestan) was an ancient gateway through which Andite peoples from Mesopotamia penetrated the mountains to reach the northern lands of the yellow men. For almost fifteen thousand years, centers of mixed Andite culture persisted in the Tarim River basin of Sinkiang, serving as the easternmost outpost of true Andite civilization.

name: sinners
description: Sinners are those who commit sin and become bond servants of sin, yet God loves sinners unconditionally and actively seeks their redemption. Jesus came specifically to call sinners to repentance, and there is great joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, with divine mercy providing sufficient time for each wrongdoer to choose their eternal destiny.

name: Sisera
description: Sisera was a military commander who was defeated by Deborah and Barak at Taanach, a historic battle that Jesus reflected upon while overlooking the surrounding countryside from the hill of Nazareth.

name: Siva
description: Siva is one of the principal deities in the Hindu trinitarian concept, alongside Brahma and Vishnu. Known as the lord of life and death, god of fertility, and master of destruction, Siva's worship arose in the first millennium after Christ and represents one aspect of the later Indian development of Trinity concepts.

name: Siwalik Hills
description: The Siwalik Hills of northern India contain fossils that approach nearer to transition types between man and prehuman groups than any other location on earth.

name: skeletal structures
description: Skeletal structures are more dependable than skull dimensions in deciphering racial origins and help identify the ancestry of present-day races. Originally, there were five distinct types of skeletal structure in Urantia's racial development, though extensive intermingling has now reduced human remains to approximately three main classifications: Caucasoid, Mongoloid, and Negroid.

name: skepticism (philosophy)
description: Skepticism is a philosophical position asserting that knowledge is fallacious and that conviction and assurance are impossible. It represents a purely negative attitude that becomes prevalent when philosophy leans too heavily upon metaphysics, resulting in confusion and doubt.

name: Skepticism (religion)
description: Skepticism was one of the philosophical schools that emerged during a period when Occidental religion languished, along with the Cynics, Epicureans, and Stoics, preceding the great contest between Mithraism and Christianity.

name: skies
description: The heavens above, which represent the misconception of God as distant rather than dwelling within through the indwelling Mystery Monitor.

name: skulls
description: Bony head structures that became powerful fetishes in primitive cultures and served as key evidence for racial identification and blending patterns throughout human history. Ancient medicine men performed trephining surgeries on skulls to release headache spirits, while archaeologists use skull types to trace the mixing of different racial groups across civilizations.

name: slate
