description: A metamorphic rock formed when shale is transformed under pressure, found in transition rock layers containing organic carbon that indicates ancestral plant life from the Carboniferous age.

name: slavery
description: An institution that compelled backward peoples to work, serving as an indispensable bridge from chaos to civilized society while providing the foundation for primitive government and social organization. Though oppressive, slavery enabled cultural advancement and eventually evolved into modified forms of industrial servitude in modern times.

name: slaves
description: Individuals held in bondage, beginning with women as the first slaves due to decreased male dependence, and later including war captives forced into agricultural labor. Many superior slaves earned wages, purchased freedom, and rose to prominent positions in society, influencing the early Christian church's tolerance of this modified form of servitude.

name: sleep
description: A natural state of rest during which Adjusters may detach and attempt spiritual transformations, while ancient peoples believed the soul temporarily left the body, experiencing dreams as the absent soul's adventures.

name: sleeping survivors
description: Mortals without personal guardians who must rest in unconscious sleep until judgment day of a new dispensation, when they are repersonalized on mansion worlds through the reunion of their morontia soul and Adjuster elements.

name: slime
description: Primitive organic matter from ocean beds that represents humanity's earliest ancestral forms and the transitional stages between vegetable and animal life 450 million years ago.

name: slogans
description: Motivational phrases or mottos, exemplified by the United Midwayers' determination expressed in their saying and the need for religion to develop modern calls to spiritual battle.

name: sloth
description: A state of laziness or reluctance to work, representing one of the challenges that slavery helped overcome in primitive societies by compelling backward peoples to engage in productive labor.

name: slothful servant
description: In Jesus's parable of the slothful servant, the lord will cast out those unfaithful servants who mistreat others and do not prepare for his unexpected return, while those who endure trials will be exalted in the affairs of the kingdom.

name: sloths
description: Asiatic sloths entered North America during ancient continental connections, while ground sloths as large as elephants lived in North America during the ice age. South American sloths, armadillos, and anteaters evolved as distinctive species on that continent.

name: smiths
description: Skilled craftsmen who work with metals, forging tools, weapons, and other implements.

name: smoke signals
description: An early form of long-distance communication that evolved from primitive message systems, eventually leading to more advanced technologies like telegraph, telephone, and wireless communication.

name: snails
description: Ancient gastropods that crawled from the briny seas onto land millions of years ago, surviving as one of the early animal types alongside bivalves, starfish, and other primitive creatures.

name: snakes
description: Reptiles descended from the saurian family that survived the extinction of dinosaurs and became objects of worship in many cultures. Jesus taught his followers to be "wise as serpents but harmless as doves," and snakes were considered sacred by various peoples including Hebrews, Hindus, and Chinese.

name: snares
description: Ingenious traps used by primitive hunters and blue men to catch game and fish, representing early technological advances in hunting techniques.

name: sneezing
description: Primitive peoples viewed sneezing as the soul's failed attempt to escape from the body, later leading to religious expressions like "God bless you!" to accompany sneezing.

name: snow
description: Frozen precipitation that played a significant role in geological changes and climate patterns. Increased snowfall in northern highlands led to glacier formation during the ice age, and Jesus experienced a record-breaking two-foot snowfall in Galilee during his lifetime.

name: social architects
description: A class of seraphim who work to enhance sincere social contacts and promote genuine relationships among intelligent beings by removing artificiality from associations. They bring together suitable individuals to form efficient working groups and continue their ministry on mansion and morontia worlds.

name: social classes
description: Divisions in society based on mental and physical inequalities that naturally emerge in evolving civilizations. Flexible and shifting social classes are essential for civilization's progress, but when classes become rigid castes, they diminish personal initiative and prevent social cooperation.

name: socialism
description: An economic and political system where local governments operate socialistic enterprises in areas like health, sanitation, and recreation. Some modern socialist teachings are criticized as contradicting religion's role as a conservator of morals and stabilizer of society.

name: society
description: The collective organization of human beings living together in communities, characterized by shared institutions, relationships, and patterns of behavior that facilitate cooperation and mutual support.

name: sociology
description: The study of society and social behavior, identified as one of the essential fields that youth must not neglect for civilization's welfare. Materialistic sociologists are contrasted with those who can inspire transformative social change through spiritual insight.

name: Socrates
description: Ancient Greek philosopher who, along with Plato and Aristotle, taught that virtue is knowledge and that it is better to suffer injustice than to commit it. His ideals became foundational to Western philosophy and influenced the development of Christian religious thought.

name: sodium
description: A chemical element capable of light and energy locomotion that permeates space as wandering atomic remnants from solar elemental battles. Sodium atoms can literally ride light beams across vast distances throughout space.

name: Sodom
description: An ancient city where Lot engaged in trade and animal husbandry, and whose king joined Abraham's military confederation. The city's destruction became associated with fabricated narratives linked to the appearance of three celestial beings to Abraham.

name: Sol Invictus
description: The sun-god of Mithraic religion, representing a degeneration of the Zoroastrian Ahura-Mazda deity concept. Sol Invictus was associated with Mithras, who was believed to be the surviving champion of the sun-god in his struggle against the god of darkness.

name: solar energy
description: Light and energy streams that flow from suns across millions of miles through space to energize, light, and heat distant planets. The steady escape of these light-energies proves that suns are not very dense.

name: solar radiation
description: Energy emitted by suns in the form of light and other electromagnetic radiation that travels through space to provide energy, light, and heat to planets across vast distances.

name: solar supergases
description: A concept related to stellar formation and composition, mentioned in connection with solar phenomena.

name: solar systems
description: Astronomical arrangements consisting of a central sun with orbiting planets and other celestial bodies, such as the Monmatia system containing Urantia.

name: Solemnities of Trust
description: A specialized order of tertiary seconaphim who evaluate and measure trustworthiness of individuals, serving as living scales of character assessment for superuniverse administrators. These beings weigh candidates in the secoraphic balances to determine their capacity for increased responsibilities and trust.

name: solicitations
description: Requests or appeals for support, assistance, or participation in various activities or causes.

name: Solitarington
description: One of the seven sacred spheres of Paradise, serving as the divine rendezvous of unrevealed beings born from the conjoint acts of the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit. This world holds the secrets of trinitization and houses Solitary Messengers, Universe Power Directors, and numerous unrevealed orders of spirit personalities.

name: Solitary Messengers
description: The first and senior order of Higher Personalities of the Infinite Spirit, serving as the personal and universal messenger corps of the Conjoint Creator. These formless spirit beings possess tremendous transit velocities and function alone throughout the universes, serving in roles ranging from Trinity messengers to explorers of undirected assignment and revelators of truth.

name: Solomon
description: Son of King David and Bathsheba who continued his father's tyrannical rule over Israel. Solomon bankrupted the nation through lavish spending on building projects including a temple, palace complexes, and maintaining a navy and harem of nearly a thousand members.

name: Solomon’s Porch
description: A section of the Jerusalem temple where Jesus frequently taught during his visits to the city. This location served as the setting for his official announcement of his divinity to the Jewish people and the world.

name: Solonia
description: The seraphic chief of Urantia's planetary helper seraphim who served during the Adamic mission. Known as the "voice in the Garden," Solonia reproved Adam and Eve for their disobedience and proclaimed the miscarriage of the divine plan following their default.

name: Soma
description: A secondary Hindu deity from among the ancient gods of the Aryans, persisting in the Hindu pantheon alongside other Vedic deities such as Agni and Indra.

name: Somme River
description: A river in northern France that flows in the same preglacial channel as it did tens of thousands of years ago, where evidence of the ancient Andonic tribes can still be found. The Somme was the site of decisive struggles between the blue man and white invaders, where the Cro-Magnoids made their final stand before succumbing to superior military strategy.

name: Son finders
description: Secondary supernaphim of Havona's fourth circuit who guide ascending mortals in understanding the Eternal Son, helping pilgrims achieve understanding contact with the Eternal Son on their journey to Paradise.

name: Son-fused mortals
description: Evolutionary mortals who fuse with an individualized gift of the spirit of their Creator Son rather than with their Thought Adjusters. These beings are primarily confined to their superuniverse of origin, numbering less than one million in Orvonton, and serve as permanent ascendant citizens in superuniverse administration.

name: son of commandment
description: A Jewish term for a young man who has graduated from synagogue school at age thirteen, becoming a responsible citizen of Israel with the privilege of attending Passover in Jerusalem. Jesus received this designation upon completing his education at the Nazareth synagogue.

name: Son of God
description: A Jewish Messianic concept that evolved from earlier expectations of "the servant of the Lord" and "son of David" to include the idea of a divine heavenly Prince who would rule the earth made new as part of God's kingdom.

name: Son of Man
description: The title Jesus chose for himself based on passages in the Book of Enoch, referring to a divine being who walked through heavenly glory with the Father but came to earth to bring salvation to mankind. Jesus adopted this as his inaugural title, emphasizing both his human nature and divine mission.

name: Son seizure
description: A form of translation from among the living whereby mortals who have fused with their Adjusters during mortal life are taken directly to the presence of the Sovereign Son at universe headquarters, bypassing death and the usual mansion world experiences.

name: Son-Spirit ministers
description: Supreme seraphim assigned to serve the high Sons and Spirit-origin beings in the local universe, closely affiliated with the Bright and Morning Star's intelligence corps and responsible for organizing dispensational transitions on evolutionary worlds.

name: Sonarington
description: One of the seven sacred spheres of Paradise, serving as the personal receiving world and headquarters of the Eternal Son. This sphere is the Paradise home for all Sons of God and contains the secrets of divine incarnation, accessible only to those who have personally experienced the bestowal mystery.

name: sons of Annas
description: The descendants or followers of Annas, the former Jewish high priest who played a role in Jesus's trial and crucifixion.

name: Sons of God
description: Sons of God encompass all beings of divine sonship including descending orders (Creator Sons, Magisterial Sons, Trinity Teacher Sons, Melchizedek Sons, and others), ascending orders (mortals fused with Thought Adjusters, evolutionary seraphim, and other ascending beings), and trinitized sons. This classification represents the broad category of divine beings who share spiritual kinship with the Paradise Father, with Sonarington serving as their Paradise headquarters.

name: sons of thunder
description: James and John, the apostle sons of Zebedee, were nicknamed "sons of thunder" by Jesus due to their headstrong and sometimes impetuous nature. Despite their vehement temperaments, they developed great affection for one another and eventually became devoted followers who learned to channel their passionate natures into spiritual service.

name: sonship with God
description: Sonship with God is the supreme relationship between creature and Creator, realized through spiritual insight, faith acceptance, and cooperation with the indwelling Thought Adjuster. This status transforms mortals from material beings into spiritual sons of God, enabling divine fellowship and eternal survival through the recognition of their divine kinship.

name: Sonta-an
description: Sonta-an, meaning "loved by mother," was the name Fonta used for Andon during their mortal life on Urantia. These were the personal names they gave each other, signifying their mutual regard and affection before receiving their official Nebadon names upon fusion with their Thought Adjusters.

name: Sonta-en
description: Sonta-en, meaning "loved by father," was the name Andon used for Fonta during their mortal life on Urantia. These were the personal names they gave each other, signifying their mutual regard and affection before receiving their official Nebadon names upon fusion with their Thought Adjusters.

name: Sontad
description: Sontad was the first child of Andon and Fonta, notable as the first creature born on Urantia to be wrapped in protective coverings at birth. He assumed leadership of the clan after his parents' death and was later fused with his Thought Adjuster on Jerusem, like his parents.

name: soothsaying
description: Soothsaying was a form of divination practiced to interpret spirit messages and determine the will of spirits. Along with omens, oracles, and signs, it was part of ancient religious practices designed to communicate with supernatural forces, later classified as either witchcraft or shamancraft.

name: sop
description: A sop is a piece of bread dipped in a dish, which Jesus gave to Judas during the Last Supper. This act served as a subtle indication to John of Judas's identity as the betrayer, though the other apostles did not understand its significance at the time.

name: sophistries
description: Sophistries refer to clever but fallacious arguments, particularly those of materialistic philosophy that deny spiritual reality. True faith successfully withstands all intellectual sophistries, while practical endeavors like international trade organizations often achieve more real progress than abstract philosophical arguments.

name: sorrow
description: Sorrow often stems from disappointed ambitions and wounded pride, but can be transcended through faithful duty performance and acceptance of one's lot. While sorrow is a natural human emotional response to difficult circumstances, spiritual beings find that the joy of divine service eclipses such feelings, and ultimate reality promises freedom from all sorrow.

name: Sortoria
description: Sortoria is one of Satania's neighboring local systems within the Norlatiadek constellation, along with Sandmatia, Assuntia, Porogia, Rantulia, and Glantonia.

name: soul
description: The soul is the evolving, immortal reality jointly created by the material mind and the indwelling Thought Adjuster through moral choices and spiritual decisions. This morontia entity represents the survival potential of human personality and serves as the vehicle for eternal ascension, growing through spiritual experiences and divine cooperation until it achieves fusion with the Adjuster or other destiny assignments.

name: Souls of Goodness
description: The Soul of Goodness are tertiary seconaphim created in seven types and collectively assigned to Trinitized Sons of Attainment for interchangeable use. They work inseparably with the Secret of Greatness to reflect the divine qualities of greatness and goodness, demonstrating that true greatness is measured by unselfishness and goodness rather than worldly achievements.

name: souls of peace
description: The Souls of Peace are planetary helper seraphim who minister to evolutionary mortals, working to establish peace on earth and good will among men. Led by Vevona, who was left on Urantia after Adam's time and later heralded Michael's birth, these angels help replace primitive atonement concepts with divine attunement philosophy.

name: Souls of Philosophy
description: The Soul of Philosophy are secondary seconaphim who reflect the wisdom of divinity and philosophy of Paradise. Attached to the Perfectors of Wisdom, they remain in focal synchrony with the masters of philosophy on Paradise and can adapt divine philosophy for practical application on lower worlds.

name: sound designers
description: Sound designers are celestial artisans who work with spirit waves of diverse identity and morontia appreciation, creating what mortals would understand as sound. These impulses reflect the glorious spirit-souls of the celestial hosts.

name: South Africa
description: South Africa is a continent that has been connected to other landmasses at various geological periods and contains some of the largest dinosaur fossils in the world, which originated from western North America.

name: South America
description: South America is a continent that has undergone extensive geological changes throughout Earth's history, rising from Pacific depths and connecting with other continents at various periods. It was populated by Polynesian Andites who established the ancestry of the Incan rulers, and developed advanced civilizations predominantly from red race peoples with admixtures of yellow, orange, and blue races.

name: Sovereign Michaels
description: Sovereign Michaels are Creator Sons who have completed their seven bestowal experiences and achieved full sovereignty over their local universes.

name: Sovereign Sons
description: Sovereign Sons are Creator Sons who have attained full sovereignty over their local universes through completion of their required bestowal experiences.

name: sovereignty
description: The supreme ruling authority and power that characterizes different levels of cosmic governance. Creator Sons earn experiential sovereignty over their local universes through bestowal service, while the Ancients of Days possess perfect but non-experiential sovereignty over superuniverses. The Supreme Being is achieving sovereignty through experiential service and will eventually supersede the Ancients of Days. Political sovereignty evolves from family authority to larger territorial groupings, but ultimate spiritual sovereignty belongs to God.

name: sower
description: A central figure in Jesus' parable teaching, first delivered from Andrew and Peter's fishing boat by the seaside. The parable of the sower illustrated both Jesus' ministry and the varying success that future messengers of the kingdom would experience in spreading the gospel.

name: sowing
description: The act of planting seeds, both literally and metaphorically. Used to describe a period of youthful freedom before marriage in ancient customs, and spiritually represents the principle that reaping is in accordance with what one sows, emphasizing that consequences follow actions.

name: space
description: A fundamental cosmic reality that serves as the arena for all material existence and motion. Space is not empty but contains various energy manifestations and responds to the presence of matter. It exhibits complex motions including respiration cycles and serves as a pathway for cosmic forces. Space is pervaded by the Unqualified Absolute and originates from Paradise, functioning as both a container for and participant in universal activities.

name: space bodies
description: Various cosmic objects and spheres that exist throughout space, including suns, planets, dark islands, comets, and architectural worlds. These bodies form through diverse processes such as gravitational condensation, stellar disruption, meteoric accumulation, and deliberate construction. They range from naturally evolved celestial objects to purpose-built administrative spheres, all participating in the endless circuits of the master universe.

name: space energy
description: One of the usual names for absoluta, representing the primal force-energy that emanates from Paradise and pervades all space.

name: space-force
description: One of the usual names for absoluta, referring to the fundamental cosmic force that originates from Paradise and operates throughout the master universe.

name: space levels
description: Elliptical regions of motion separated by zones of relative quiescence that constitute the major divisions of the master universe. These six concentric levels include the central universe, seven superuniverses, and four outer space levels. They provide curved paths of lessened resistance for cosmic energies and exhibit alternating clockwise and counterclockwise motions that help stabilize physical gravity throughout the cosmos.

name: space potency
description: One of the usual names for absoluta, representing the ancestral potencies and potentials that exist within space and emanate from Paradise.

name: space rays
description: High-energy radiations that pervade space, including ultimatonic rays produced by ultimaton assembly, short space rays from atomic processes, and other forms of radiant energy. These rays represent the dominant form of energy in the universe and are produced through stellar processes, atomic disruption, and various cosmic phenomena, with short space rays being four hundred times more abundant than all other forms of radiant energy.

name: space reports
description: Communications or broadcasts that provide information about celestial activities and space-related matters, likely transmitted through the universe communication systems.

name: Spain
description: A European peninsula where Andite peoples entered and mixed with earlier populations, and where later Christian preaching occurred through disciples inspired by Jesus, including Claudus who proclaimed the gospel there after Peter's death.

name: sparrows
description: Small birds that Jesus used in his teachings to illustrate God's knowledge and care for all creation, noting that not one sparrow falls to the ground without the Father's awareness, demonstrating that humans are of much greater value than many sparrows.

name: speaker at forum
description: A man named Marcus who spoke at a Roman forum, whom Jesus told that eloquence and logic were admirable but true teaching comes from knowing God as spiritual Father; he later heard Peter preach, became his successor, and boldly continued preaching after Peter's crucifixion.

name: spear
description: A weapon invented by the blue man and used by Roman soldiers, one of whom pierced Jesus' left side with a spear while he hung on the cross to ensure his death.

name: specialists
description: Individuals with specialized skills and knowledge whose coordination is essential for civilization's continued advancement, as society's growing complexity requires effective methods to unite various technical, social, artistic, and industrial experts.

name: spectral lines
description: Observable features in stellar spectra that reveal surface compositions of stars and indicate the presence of shattered elements floating in space, with some unknown lines resulting from atomic casualties of solar battles and energy modifications in organized space.

name: spectrum
description: The natural light spectrum containing seven colors that demonstrates the fundamental sevenfold constitution of ancestral energy and reflects the basic reality of creation's sevenfold diversity in time and space.

name: speculations
description: Theoretical considerations and conjectures about divine mysteries, future destinies, and cosmic purposes that celestial beings engage in regarding topics beyond current revelation, though such speculation can be detrimental to religious experience when it falsifies spiritual realities.

name: speech, freedom of
description: The essential liberty allowing all forms of human expression and opinion that makes representative government possible, as democratic governance cannot function without the free exchange of ideas and aspirations.

name: spells
description: Jesus exposed and denounced belief in spells and all forms of ignorant superstition, teaching against these enslaving practices that included bewitching, cursing, signs, and other forms of primitive magical thinking.

name: spheres of space
description: Celestial bodies distributed throughout the cosmos, representing the physical worlds and astronomical structures that exist within the vast expanse of space.

name: spheres of the Father
description: Sacred worlds associated with the Universal Father, likely referring to the divine spheres that surround Paradise and serve special functions in the cosmic hierarchy.

name: spicules
description: Sharp pieces of stone, flint, and bone used by the Andonites and early human ancestors as tools and weapons for hunting and survival, representing humanity's first steps in tool-making technology.

name: spiders
description: Arthropods that evolved early in planetary history alongside insects and other creatures, becoming part of the stationary types of early and lower animals that failed to progress further in evolutionary development.

name: spies
description: Early practitioners of espionage and surveillance who emerged from secret societies and evolved into various roles including vigilance committees, secret police, and eventually merchant intermediaries in early trade systems.

name: spikenard
description: A costly and rare ointment that Mary used to anoint Jesus at Bethany, an act of devotion that Jesus defended against criticism, declaring it a preparation for his burial and a demonstration of faith.

name: spirit
description: The fundamental divine reality that originates from God and represents the highest form of personal existence, transcending matter and mind while serving as the eternal goal of all personality development and cosmic evolution.

name: spirit, new-name
description: One of seven higher spirit influences that progressively contact and dwell within ascending mortals as they advance in intelligence and spiritual perception beyond the initial adjutant mind-spirits.

name: Spirit-Advocate
description: A celestial being appointed by a judge-arbiter to present evidence and safeguard the rights of all personalities involved in matters before conciliating commissions, ensuring fair representation in universe tribunals.

name: Spirit co-ordinators
description: The third group of superior seraphim based on Salvington who prepare ascending sojourners for their final transition in the local universe from the highest morontia level to the status of newborn spirit beings.

name: spirit conjuring
description: A precise and highly complicated procedure used by shamans, comparable to present-day church rituals, to control the hazards of living through spiritual manipulation and revelatory practices.

name: Spirit-fused mortals
description: Ascendant mortals who achieve immortality through eternal fusion with individualized fragments of the local universe Mother Spirit rather than with Thought Adjusters, serving permanently within their native local universe.

name: spirit mortals
description: Mortals who have achieved spirit status through their ascension journey, representing an advanced stage of evolutionary development beyond their material origins.

name: Spirit Mother
description: A reference to the Divine Minister or Universe Mother Spirit, the local universe representative of the Infinite Spirit who serves as the spiritual mother figure for the local creation.

name: spirit of God
description: The spirit presence of the Infinite Spirit functioning as the Conjoint Actor, representing both the Father and the Son as well as himself, and encompassing the liaison of all spiritual ministry from God the Father, Son, Spirit, and Sevenfold.

name: Spirit of Truth
description: The combined spirit of the Creator Son and Universal Father poured out upon all flesh following a bestowal Son's mission, serving as the divine teacher and guide that leads believers into all truth and spiritual understanding.

name: Spiritington
description: The Paradise home of high spirit beings representing the Infinite Spirit, including the Seven Master Spirits, where the impenetrable mysteries of reflectivity are centered.

name: spiritism, dual
description: A form of spiritual belief or practice involving dual aspects of spirit influence or manifestation, though specific details are not provided in the available context.

name: spirits
description: Advanced evolutionary beings who have progressed through various stages of spirit development, with mortal finaliters currently achieving sixth-stage spirit status while potentially advancing to a mysterious seventh stage.

name: spirits of brotherhood
description: Planetary helper seraphim who work to achieve racial harmony and social cooperation among diverse races on evolutionary worlds, fostering the wisdom of peaceful interassociation and human brotherhood.

name: Spirits of the Havona Circuits
description: An order of celestial beings associated with the Seven Spirits of Havona Circuits.

name: spirits of trust
description: Planetary helper seraphim who inculcate trust into the minds of evolving humans, fostering a civilization that embodies confidence and the understanding that uncertainty is essential for spiritual growth and contentment.

name: spiritual assurance
description: The confidence and certainty that comes from spiritual communion and relationship with God.

name: spiritual attainment
description: The achievement of higher levels of spiritual development and God-consciousness through progressive spiritual growth.

name: spiritual communion
description: The intimate, personal fellowship and communication between the human soul and God, fostered through prayer, worship, and meditation, which provides spiritual refreshment, guidance, and assurance of divine companionship.

name: spiritual consecration
description: The dedicated commitment and wholehearted devotion of one's will and life to doing God's will and serving divine purposes.

name: spiritual-energy manipulators
description: Celestial artisans who study and manipulate spiritual energy according to established laws, developing improved techniques for the intake and application of spirit force among morontia and celestial beings.

name: spiritual growth
description: The progressive development of the soul characterized by awakening to spiritual needs, discernment of divine meanings, discovery of eternal values, and increasing God-consciousness, demonstrated through love, unselfish service, and wholehearted worship.

name: spiritual influences
description: The various divine spiritual forces and ministries that surround and guide mortals, including the Thought Adjuster, Spirit of Truth, Holy Spirit, and other celestial agencies that work in unified cooperation to foster spiritual development and lead souls toward divine perfection.

name: spiritual insight
description: The faculty of human personality that develops as a consequence of the indwelling Thought Adjuster, enabling the recognition of moral values, discernment of spiritual meanings, and perception of cosmic realities. Spiritual insight represents the individual's ability to choose between good and evil, truth and error, and distinguishes humans from the animal world through divine endowment of cosmic mind.

name: spiritual peace
description: The tranquil state of harmony that arises from spiritual attunement and alignment with divine will, representing inner serenity derived from spiritual communion and God-consciousness.

name: spiritual presence
description: The conscious awareness and experiential reality of divine spiritual forces, particularly the indwelling presence of God through the Thought Adjuster and other spiritual influences that guide and uplift human consciousness.

name: spiritual progress
description: The evolutionary advancement of the soul through increasing levels of spiritual insight, cosmic understanding, and divine attunement, achieved independently of material circumstances and leading toward eternal spiritual goals.

name: spiritual rebirth
description: The transformative experience of being born of the spirit, marking the transition from material-minded existence to spiritual consciousness as a child of God. This rebirth involves a fundamental reorientation of personality values and the beginning of eternal life through living faith.

name: spiritual unity
description: The harmonious fellowship among believers derived from the common indwelling of the Father's spirit, creating bonds of shared spiritual purpose and divine dedication. Unlike uniformity, spiritual unity allows for diversity of thought and experience while maintaining fundamental spiritual harmony.

name: spiritualism
description: The mistaken belief that spirits of the dead can communicate with the living. The departed do not return to establish communication after death, and such phenomena are not connected with legitimate spiritual ministry or the work of celestial beings like midwayers.

name: spirituality
description: The quality or state of being spiritually minded and attuned to divine realities, characterized by the progressive development of spiritual insight, God-consciousness, and alignment with eternal values rather than material concerns.

name: spironga
description: Spirit beings created by Gabriel and Father Melchizedek who serve as spirit helpers in the local universe of Nebadon, executing routine spirit tasks and functioning as transition servants between material and spiritual realms. They work from mansion world number two onward in the morontia progression.

name: spittle
description: Saliva, which primitive peoples regarded as a potent healing agent and magical substance. Ancient superstitions attributed curative powers to the spittle of great or holy persons, leading to various ceremonial and protective practices in early human cultures.

name: Spitzbergen
description: An Arctic archipelago to which the Caledonian Mountains extended 290 million years ago, forming part of the great crustal upheavals that included the Himalayas of Asia.

name: Splandon
description: The fifth major sector of the superuniverse of Orvonton, consisting of one hundred minor sectors with its headquarters world called Umajor the fifth, which is surrounded by seventy spheres of advancing intellectual training administered by the Perfections of Days.

name: sponges
description: Survivors of early transition organisms that represented the gradual evolutionary bridge from vegetable to animal forms of life, serving as true borderline organisms that eventually led to the development of true animal forms.

name: spontaneity
description: A divine quality that prevents uniformity and determinism in creation, injected into the physical universe by the agencies of Infinite Spirit and cosmic mind, and manifested in personalities as freewill action and natural expression of spiritual devotion.

name: spore-bearing plants
description: Early forms of plant life that reproduced through spores rather than seeds, representing an important stage in plant evolution.

name: spornagia
description: Animal caretakers and landscape gardeners of architectural spheres in the local universe, combining traits of horses, dogs, and chimpanzees, who are devoted to the physical care and beautification of headquarters worlds from Jerusem to Salvington and can experience reincarnation despite lacking survival souls.

name: sports
description: Athletic games and physical contests that serve as wholesome recreation and better accomplish the purpose of hardening youth for life's realities, replacing earlier painful initiation ceremonies involving self-torture.

name: squares (Jerusem)
description: Administrative districts on Jerusem designated for the necessary functions involving supervision of the affairs of 619 inhabited spheres, 56 transitional-culture worlds, and the system capital itself.

name: squid
description: Modern cephalopods that evolved from well-developed early ancestors and have survived alongside other marine life forms such as the pearly nautilus, octopus, and cuttlefish.

name: squirrels
description: Small mammals that evolved 45 million years ago during a period of rapid mammalian evolution, emerging as part of the gnawing rodent family that has undergone very little change since that time.

name: St. Croix valley
description: A valley in Wisconsin where geological history is graphically displayed through 127 successive lava flows followed by water submergence and rock deposition, with about 65-70 of these stratified records of past ages still visible today.

name: St. Lawrence River
description: A major river system in North America that was the final drainage route for the Great Lakes system, which began emptying through this northern route 37,000 years ago after previously draining through the Mississippi valley and Hudson valley.

name: St. Peter’s Church
description: A church in Rome built on the exact site where the headquarters of the Mother of God mystery religion sect once stood during the decline of formal Roman patriotic worship.

name: stability
description: A divine quality that provides assurance and dependability, achieved through balanced energies and cooperative minds rather than inertia, and is always proportional to divinity in the universe.

name: stabilization corps of local universe
aliases: stabilization corps of the local universe
description: A corps comprised of all Assigned Sentinels in a local universe that has achieved light and life, mobilized by the Associate Inspector and supervised jointly with the Father Melchizedek, with support from Inspired Trinity Spirits.

name: stagnation
description: A condition of inactivity or lack of growth that leads to spiritual death and decay, contrasting with the dynamic progress required for true religion, healthy societies, and personal development in the eternal career.

name: stake
description: A method of execution by burning alive that was commonly used as punishment for serious crimes, particularly sexual offenses, as prescribed by ancient rulers including Hammurabi and Moses.

name: stamina
description: The capacity for sustained physical or mental effort and endurance in facing challenges and difficulties.

name: standard time
description: The universal time system used throughout the local universe, based on Salvington time, which corresponds to the day length on Jerusem that is slightly less than three Urantia days.

name: standards of living
description: The level of material comfort and quality of life achieved by societies, which has evolved through agriculture, industry, and manufacture, but can become suicidal when too complicated or luxurious.

name: star clouds
description: Vast aggregations of matter in Orvonton that are comparable to separate nebulae observable outside the Milky Way galaxy. Many of these star clouds consist of gaseous material with an unbelievably enormous energy potential, some of which is taken up by nearby suns and redistributed in space as solar emanations.

name: star students
description: Celestial astronomers who serve on Uversa and other headquarters worlds, drawn from beings throughout the universes who aspire to study space evolution and astronomical phenomena. Over one million star students reside on Uversa, working with mechanical instruments and spirit helpers to observe and forecast stellar behavior, energy disturbances, and cosmic phenomena throughout space.

name: starfish
description: An ancient animal type that survived the long evolutionary struggle, representing one of the nonprogressive specimens that failed to advance beyond early developmental stages.

name: stars
description: Celestial bodies that are best discerned from experiential depths rather than illuminated heights. God knows all stars by name, and they represent divine handiwork in the universe. Ancient peoples regarded stars as the glorified souls of great departed men, leading to early forms of ancestor worship.

name: stationary orders (dual origin)
description: A classification of beings that originate from dual sources, representing one category within the broader organizational structure of divine personalities.

name: Stationary Sons of the Trinity
description: A grand corps of Trinity administrators totaling over thirty-seven billion beings who serve as rulers, executives, advisers, counselors, and judges throughout Havona and the superuniverses. They represent the collective attitude of the Trinity in executive judgment and include ten orders such as Ancients of Days, Perfections of Days, and Universal Censors.

name: statisticians
description: Those who announce laws governing large groups of atoms or persons, but cannot predict individual behavior due to various unpredictable influences affecting single entities.

name: status
description: A condition of standing or achievement that, with God the Supreme, requires both being and doing something. True spiritual status is determined by service and the direction of one's progress toward God, rather than current material circumstances or assumptions.

name: steam
description: Water vapor that primitive humans feared to harness as power due to its explosive potential when confined. Young Jesus observed steam escaping from boiling pots during cold Nazareth winters, pondering the physical constitution of matter in its various states.

name: stellar gas clouds
description: Gaseous aggregations in space that contain enormous energy potential, with some energy being absorbed by nearby suns and redistributed as solar emanations.

name: Stephen
description: Stephen was a Hellenistic leader among Greek believers in Jerusalem who became the first Christian martyr. After a four-hour conversation with Jesus during Passover week, Stephen was profoundly influenced, though he never realized he had spoken with the future subject of his preaching. His bold proclamation of the gospel led to his stoning by Jewish authorities, and his martyrdom inspired Paul's conversion and became pivotal in Christianity's early spread.

name: stereotype
description: A stereotype is an unvalidated plan of action accepted without proper examination or testing, contrasting with validated decisions based on careful consideration.

name: steward
description: A steward is a manager or caretaker of resources, featured prominently in Jesus' parables teaching about forgiveness, mercy, and wise management of both earthly and spiritual responsibilities.

name: stimulus-response
description: Stimulus-response is not merely a mechanical process but involves personality as an active factor, making organisms inherently dynamic rather than passive in their environmental interactions.

name: stoicism (attitude)
description: Stoicism as an attitude involves accepting disappointments and maintaining composure, but falls short of true spiritual realization compared to the dynamic peace and progressive outlook that comes from knowing God's will.

name: Stoicism (religion)
description: Stoicism was a prominent Greek philosophy that taught virtue as its own reward and living in harmony with a controlling Reason-Fate. While it achieved sublime morality and prepared Rome intellectually for Christianity, it remained a philosophy rather than a religion, failing to recognize God as a loving Father and unable to provide the spiritual satisfaction that Christianity offered.

name: stone
description: Stone is the basic building material of planets and space, with calcium being its chief element. Calcium atoms can literally ride light beams through space after escaping solar photospheres, making stone the most prevalent matter throughout the universe. On Earth, stone has been used for construction, worship, and as the foundation for early human civilization.

name: Stone Age (Old)
description: The Old Stone Age roughly corresponds to the first half of human history, beginning almost one million years ago with the appearance of the six colored races 500,000 years ago. European research focuses on uncovering tools and artifacts of ancient blue men, ancestors of the white races.

name: stone book
description: The stone book refers to the geological record preserved in rock strata, containing fossilized remains that tell the story of life's evolution on Urantia. These stratified layers, laid down by ancient seas, provide a methodical record of life's development through successive ages.

name: stone lilies
description: Stone lilies are early marine organisms (crinoids) that evolved during the Silurian period, becoming prominent echinoderms that left extensive limestone deposits as evidence of their abundance in ancient seas.

name: stone masons
description: Early specialists in industry who worked alongside flint flakers as among the first craftsmen to develop specialized skills in shaping and working with stone materials.

name: Stone of Scone
description: A sacred fetish stone that has survived through time as one of the few ancient sacred stones still revered, along with the Kaaba.

name: Stonehenge
description: An ancient stone monument built by European white races during the New Stone Age as a circular, roofless sun temple, representing their tradition of setting up great stones as tokens to the sun.

name: stonings
description: A form of execution by throwing stones, practiced in ancient times and mentioned in various contexts including the martyrdom of Stephen and threats against Jesus by unbelieving Jews.

name: storms
description: Violent weather phenomena including windstorms and sandstorms that occur on Urantia, ranging from the early planetary storms during crustal cooling to specific events like the sandstorm during Jesus' crucifixion and the storm that prompted Andon and Fonta to flee their treetop homes.

name: storytelling
description: An ancient tradition where travelers and guests would share tales of their adventures as payment for hospitality, with skilled storytellers becoming highly valued in their communities.

name: strain
description: Physical, mental, or emotional stress and tension experienced by individuals under difficult circumstances.

name: strange preacher
description: An unidentified religious teacher or evangelist mentioned in The Urantia Book.

name: strangers
description: Persons unknown or foreign to a particular tribe or group, who in early times were often feared, killed, or enslaved, but whom Jesus taught should be welcomed as brothers when one knows God.

name: stratosphere
description: The atmospheric layer above the troposphere where temperature remains constant at about 65 to 70 degrees below zero Fahrenheit for approximately forty miles of altitude.

name: strength
description: Greatness lies not so much in possessing strength as in making a wise and divine use of such strength. Those who wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength and mount up with wings as eagles.

name: stress
description: A concept related to psychological or physical pressure and strain experienced by individuals.

name: strong
description: National survival demands military preparedness, but only religious idealism and brotherhood can prevent the strong from oppressing the weak.

name: structures
description: Physical constructions or organizational frameworks that provide form and support.

name: struggle
description: The effort and conflict involved in overcoming difficulties or challenges in personal or evolutionary development.

name: student visitors
description: Celestial beings who visit various worlds for educational purposes and experiential learning. Student visitors are allowed on all inhabited planets, even those in isolation, and are registered in the Uversa personality register as part of the constant stream of celestial visitors flowing through universe headquarters.

name: study
description: The pursuit of knowledge and understanding through systematic investigation and learning.

name: sturgeon
description: A type of fish that first appeared during the marine life developments of 110 million years ago, representing one of the marked changes that occurred in the fish family during that epoch.

name: subbreathers
description: Atmospheric mortals who inhabit planets with thin atmospheres similar to Mars, comprising about 2.5% of atmospheric mortals in Satania.

name: subconscious mind
description: The part of mortal mind that operates below the level of conscious awareness, processing thoughts and memories outside of direct conscious control.

name: submission
description: The active and alert surrender to God's will, emphasizing living in accordance with divine guidance rather than passive acceptance. Jesus taught that submission to the Father's will does not diminish but rather augments and enriches the individual personality, leading to spiritual transformation and eternal existence in unity with God.

name: subnormals
description: Individuals with below-normal intellectual, physical, or social capabilities who require special consideration in social organization and governance.

name: Succoth
description: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during the Perea mission between January 3, A.D. 30 and Jesus' death.

name: Sudna
description: A Graduate Guide from Havona who entered the divine embrace of the Father on Paradise and ceased to exist as an individual being, becoming one with the Universal Father.

name: Suduanism
description: A religious philosophy or movement, likely related to or influenced by Jainism in its teachings and practices.

name: Suez region
description: A geographical area that was elevated during ancient times, temporarily separating the Mediterranean from the Indian Ocean and making the Mediterranean an inland salt sea.

name: suffering
description: An inevitable aspect of mortal existence that serves to cultivate courage, character, and spiritual growth. While not sent as divine punishment, suffering results from the accidents of time, imperfections of existence, and consequences of choices, ultimately contributing to the soul's development and the appreciation of eternal values.

name: suffrage
description: The right to vote in governmental elections, which should ideally be universal but exercised by an intelligent and educated electorate. The effectiveness of representative government depends on the character and wisdom of those who possess voting privileges.

name: suicide
description: The deliberate taking of one's own life, which among humans indicates failure to attain the artistic levels of mortal experience and represents a rejection of life's spiritual possibilities. Ancient cultures sometimes practiced suicide as retaliation, but evolved teachings have made life more valued and desirable.

name: Suites
description: A northern people who, along with the Guites, conquered the Sumerians around 2500 B.C., absorbing the defeated Sumerians into their ranks and contributing to the end of Sumerian civilization.

name: Sumer
description: Ancient Mesopotamian city-state that was part of a confederation including Akkad, Kish, Erech, Ur, and Susa. After the breakup of the Kish confederation, constant warfare ensued between these valley cities for supremacy, with rulership shifting among them over time.

name: Sumerians
description: Ancient people of Mesopotamia who appeared suddenly with a fully developed, superior culture around 200,000 years ago after the submergence of Dalamatia. Of Nodite and Adamite origin, they were peace-loving teachers and administrators greatly respected from Egypt to India. The Sumerians were eventually absorbed into the northern Semites around 2500 B.C. following severe military reverses.

name: sun (Urantia’s)
description: Urantia's sun is 6 billion years old and currently in its period of greatest efficiency, destined to shine for more than 25 billion years. It has a surface temperature of 6,000°F with a core temperature of 35,000,000°F, and is one of 1,013,572 offspring from the Andronover nebula with a mass of two octillion tons.

name: Sunday
description: The first day of the week, named in honor of the sun by later civilizations as sun worship became prominent following the earlier era of moon worship.

name: sunrise
description: The daily rising of the sun, which some backward tribes still greet with howling and lamenting, a reaction born of primitive fear and mystery in their struggle for existence.

name: suns
description: Stars of space existing in over a thousand different states and stages, ranging from solitary evolving systems to double stars and contracting planetary systems. They serve as local accelerators of energy circulation and power-control stations, with the superuniverse of Orvonton containing more than ten trillion blazing suns.

name: sunspots
description: Solar cyclones that whirl in opposite directions above and below the solar equator, functioning as enormous magnets capable of hurling charged particles through space. These solar storm centers can alter light frequencies and are directly related to auroral phenomena on planets.

name: Suntites
description: A group that occupied the Garden of Eden after Adam's departure, along with the Nodites and Cutites, until the peninsula was eventually submerged beneath the Mediterranean Sea.

name: superangel world
description: The fourth transitional culture world of Jerusem, serving as the Satania home of the Brilliant Evening Stars and a vast concourse of coordinate and near-coordinate beings. Its seven satellites are assigned to the seven major groups of these celestial beings.

name: superangels
description: Celestial beings associated with the Brilliant Evening Stars who reside on the fourth transitional culture world of Jerusem, organized into seven major groups corresponding to the seven satellites of their world.

name: superbreathers
description: A type of atmospheric mortal comprising five percent of beings in Satania, adapted to dense atmospheric conditions similar to Venus, representing one of three breathing classifications along with mid-breathers and subbreathers.

name: superconscious mind
description: A level of consciousness beyond normal mortal awareness, representing higher mental functions and spiritual perception.

name: superghosts
description: An advanced or enhanced form of ghost phenomenon, representing a higher category within spirit manifestations.

name: superior seraphim
description: The second order of seraphim serving in local universes, including seven specialized groups such as transporters and recorders. A considerable number were deceived in the Lucifer rebellion, though they normally serve faithfully in system capitals and universe administration.

name: supermen
description: Enhanced human beings, particularly referring to Prince Caligastia's staff who were repersonalized on Urantia as superhuman entities. Early humans regarded all unusual persons as supermen, worshiping them in reverential awe and believing them to be indwelt by gods.

name: supernaphim
description: The highest order of angelic beings serving in Paradise and the central universe, created in three orders: primary supernaphim who serve on Paradise, secondary supernaphim who minister on Havona's seven circuits, and tertiary supernaphim who assist ascending pilgrims. They are highly individualized personalities with exceptional ministering capabilities.

name: supernatural
description: The tendency to ascribe unexplained phenomena to spiritual or mystical causes rather than seeking natural explanations. Jesus warned against this practice, encouraging his apostles to seek true causes of all phenomena rather than taking the lazy approach of invoking supernatural explanations.

name: superpersonalities
description: Divine beings who represent the Eternal Son in the superuniverses but exist beyond the personality circuit of the Universal Father. These finite beings of divine origin serve various functions throughout the universe of universes.

name: superstition
description: Unfounded beliefs based on ignorance, fear, and primitive thinking that ascribe magical or supernatural causes to natural phenomena. Jesus denounced all forms of superstition, teaching instead spiritual freedom and divine sonship, while science works to destroy superstition through the elimination of error and the pursuit of truth.

name: superstratosphere
description: The atmospheric region containing two different levels of electrified conducting regions that enable long-distance transmission of radio broadcasts, though broadcasting can be disturbed by storms in these outer ionospheres.

name: superultimate
description: The superultimate Absolute remains an unpredictable force within the complex reality of the universe, representing presences whose responses to demands in complex reality situations cannot be predicted with certainty by physicists, philosophers, or religionists.

name: Superuniverse Centers
description: Superuniverse Centers are one thousand power centers of the third order located on the capital sphere of each superuniverse. They receive three currents of primary energy and emit seven specialized circuits of electronic universe power, serving as the electronic organization of universe power and directing energy movements for specific purposes.

name: superuniverse wards
description: Ascending mortals who arrive on the training worlds of the superuniverses become wards of the Ancients of Days. These accredited spirits have traversed the morontia life of the local universe and embark on the superuniverse system of training and culture, extending from minor sector receiving spheres through the ten major sectors to the cultural spheres of superuniverse headquarters.

name: superuniverses
description: The seven superuniverses are finite, evolutionary space segments of the grand universe, each containing approximately one-seventh of the organized post-Havona creation. Each superuniverse is ruled by three Ancients of Days and encompasses ten major sectors, one hundred minor sectors, and seven million local universes with about one trillion inhabitable planets.

name: supervising assistants
description: The first order of supervising seraphim assigned to the collective work of the Constellation Fathers, serving as efficient helpers to the Most Highs. These seraphim are primarily concerned with the unification and stabilization of entire constellations.

name: supervisor seraphim
description: The third order of local universe angels assigned to constellation service, making their headquarters on constellation capital spheres and ministering extensively throughout Nebadon. They include specialized orders such as transporters, recorders, and reserves, serving the morontia-training worlds and constellation administration.

name: suppression
description: An emergency council of ex-mortals advised Gabriel that the summary suppression of the Lucifer rebellion would have tripled the number of beings led astray, leading to the decision to allow the rebellion to take its natural course.

name: Supremacy
description: The experiential synthesis and unification of finite reality through the evolution of the Supreme Being. Supremacy encompasses the circle of experiential insights, the unity of power and personality in the Supreme, and represents the completion of finite evolution toward divine perfection and cosmic citizenship.

name: supremacy guides
description: Supernaphim who supervise ascending mortals designated as "spiritual graduates" when translated from the seventh to the sixth circle of Havona. These guides function exclusively on the sixth circle and are distinct from the Graduate Guides of the Infinite Spirit.

name: Supreme
description: God the Supreme is the actualizing, evolving God of time and space who personally experiences the achievement of Deity unity as the experiential God of evolutionary creatures. The Supreme Being represents the unification of spirit person, almighty power, and supreme mind, synthesizing all finite experience and serving as the finite approach to transcendent divinity.

name: Supreme Center Supervisors
description: Seven beings who coordinate and regulate the master energy circuits of the grand universe, each headquartered on a special world of the Seven Supreme Executives and working in close association with them as regulators of universal energy distribution.

name: Supreme Chief
description: The name used by Orlandof to teach the blue race to worship the true God, leading a widespread religious revival about five hundred years after Caligastia's downfall that brought many tribes back to proper worship.

name: Supreme Circuit Supervisors
description: Seven completed beings created exclusively by the Infinite Spirit who serve as agents regulating the circuits of the seven Havona circuits, stationed on the pilot worlds and working in coordination with Paradise spheres.

name: Supreme Council of Destiny
description: A Paradise council comprised of the presiding heads of all seven finaliter corps, led by Grandfanda as chief, representing the mobilization of universe personalities and potentials for future outer space activities and inconceivable cosmic developments.

name: supreme council of Salvington
description: An advisory local universe council consisting of three members from each system and seven representatives from each constellation, functioning as a superlegislature that makes recommendations based on fundamental universe laws but lacks enforcement authority.

name: Supreme Council of Universe Adjustments
description: The reserve corps of seven hundred thousand Unions of Days that functions on Paradise as the Supreme Council of Universe Adjustments, handling cosmic administrative matters requiring high-level coordination.

name: supreme council of unlimited authority
description: A governing body established when a universe becomes settled in light and life, consisting of one hundred Faithfuls of Days presided over by the Union of Days, representing the first extra-Paradise authority acknowledged by the finaliter corps.

name: supreme court of tribal co-ordination and racial co-operation
description: A judicial body that coordinated tribal affairs and promoted racial cooperation among early human societies.

name: Supreme Creators
description: The collective designation for the first three levels of God the Sevenfold, including the local universe Creator Sons and Spirits, superuniverse Ancients of Days, and Seven Master Spirits of Paradise, who actively manifest divinity in the superuniverses and contribute to the evolution of the Supreme Being.

name: Supreme Executive #7 (of Orvonton)
description: One of the Seven Supreme Executives who functions from executive sphere number seven on Paradise and is wholly devoted to managing the affairs of Orvonton, the seventh superuniverse, receiving reports from omniaphim and other representatives throughout the superuniverse.

name: Supreme Executives
description: One of the seven groups of Supreme Spirits, derived from the Paradise Trinity and serving as universal coordinating directors alongside the Master Spirits and Reflective Spirits in the administration of the grand universe.

name: Supreme Lanonandek councils
description: Governing bodies stationed on constellation headquarters that select System Sovereigns and assistants from reserves of the Lanonandek order, presided over by senior and junior Most High associates of the Constellation Father.

name: Supreme Mind
description: The experiential mind of the Supreme Being, bestowed by the Infinite Spirit to unify the spirit person of God the Supreme with the power of the Almighty Supreme, serving as the individual contribution of the Third Source and Center to the reality of the Supreme Being.

name: Supreme Power Centers
description: Beings created by the Seven Supreme Power Directors in association with the Seven Master Spirits who function as living mechanisms to control, modify, and directionize the physical energies of space throughout the grand universe, operating in seven orders numbering over ten billion.

name: Supreme Power Directors
description: Seven beings personalized by the Seven Master Spirits who serve as the Paradise progenitors and directors of the Supreme Power Centers, responsible for the power control of the grand universe.

name: supreme reaction of complement
description: An inherent modification that occurs within the person of the Infinite Spirit when a Creator Son is personalized, designating the birth of the potential for the future local universe Creative Mother Spirit.

name: Supreme Rulers
description: A conceptual reference to the highest governing authorities in the universe, encompassing the supreme administrative powers that rule over creation.

name: supreme seraphim
description: The highest of the seven revealed orders of local universe angels, functioning in seven groups closely associated with the Seraphic Corps of Completion and serving as teaching counselors and advisers to morontia mortals.

name: supreme service
description: The highest honor conferred in the morontia temple, granted to citizens who have distinguished themselves in supermaterial discovery or planetary social service.

name: Supreme Spirits
description: Seven groups of beings that serve as the universal coordinating directors of the grand universe administration, including the Master Spirits, Supreme Executives, Reflective Spirits, and four other orders created by the Infinite Spirit or associates.

name: Supreme Trinity Personalities
description: Supreme Trinity Personalities are beings of administrative perfection created in definite and final numbers by the Paradise Trinity for specific duties throughout the grand universe. They represent the administrative policies, justice, and executive judgment of the Paradise Trinity, serving with perfection and devotion in seven distinct orders. These beings have never strayed from the divine path and form an interrelated line of administrative perfection extending from Paradise to local universe headquarters.

name: Supreme-Ultimate
description: The Supreme-Ultimate is a postulated future realization that may appear through the potential full unification of the Absolute Trinity. This being represents the possible trinitization-factualization of God the Absolute, though the complete unification of the Absolute Trinity is considered doubtful by celestial observers.

name: surgery
description: Surgery had early origins in primitive medicine when shamans performed trephining operations to allow supposed headache spirits to escape from patients' skulls. Later surgical practices included treating fractures and dislocations, opening boils and abscesses, and midwifery. Advanced surgical techniques were also used by Avalon surgeons who transplanted life plasm from Andonite descendants to the Jerusemite members of the Prince's staff.

name: surprise
description: God is never subjected to surprise because He inhabits the circle of eternity and foresees all cosmic events. To God there is no past, present, or future; all time is present at any given moment, and His power and wisdom are wholly adequate to cope with any universe exigencies in accordance with eternal wisdom and infinite judgment.

name: survival (of mortal death)
description: Survival of mortal death is the continuation of personality existence beyond physical death through the evolution of an immortal soul in association with the indwelling Thought Adjuster. This survival depends on the mortal's choice to do the Father's will and seek God-likeness, not on knowledge or wisdom. The Adjuster preserves all experiences and memories of survival value throughout the ascendant career, while purely material and animalistic associations perish with the physical brain.

name: survival value
description: Survival value refers to the worth-while qualities, experiences, and meanings that persist beyond mortal death and contribute to eternal existence. These values include moral decisions, spiritual insights, and meaningful experiences that the Thought Adjuster counterparts and preserves throughout the ascendant career. Every meaningful value in every will creature is assured of eternal survival, as nothing of true worth is ever lost in the universe.

name: survivors
description: Survivors are mortals who achieve continuation of personality existence beyond physical death through fusion with their Thought Adjusters or other spirit entities, thereby gaining access to the ascending career toward Paradise and eternal service.

name: Susa
description: Susa was an ancient city that greatly prospered during flood times, serving as headquarters for distinctive artcrafts of its era. Jesus and Ganid were fascinated by its early history and ruins, spending three weeks there during their Mediterranean journey to allow Jesus time to conduct investigations of this historically significant location.

name: Susanna (daughter of Ezra of Alexandria)
description: Susanna, daughter of Ezra of Alexandria, was one of the five women who discovered Jesus' empty tomb on resurrection morning. She was among the women who encountered the risen Jesus and received his instruction to tell the apostles of his resurrection. She later witnessed Jesus' fifth morontia appearance to the assembled women believers at Joseph of Arimathea's house.

name: Susanna (daughter of Nazareth chazan)
description: Susanna, daughter of the former chazan of the Nazareth synagogue, was appointed by Jesus as chief of the women's corps on January 16. She was one of ten women commissioned to teach the gospel of the kingdom and minister to the sick, organizing their own administration and serving as their leader throughout Jesus' ministry.

name: Susatia
description: The Susatia are marvelous beings who reside as permanent citizens on Salvington, the headquarters of the local universe. They are the brilliant offspring of the Creator Son and Creative Spirit and are closely associated with the Spirit-fused mortals of Nebadon.

name: suspicion
description: Suspicion is the inherent reaction of primitive beings that hinders intellectual cross-fertilization essential for brotherhood. This mental poison tremendously interferes with spiritual progress and is incompatible with sympathy and love, while trust represents a new human acquisition brought about by planetary seraphim.

name: Susquehanna River
description: The Susquehanna River carved through layers of volcanic deposits in the Appalachian Mountains, revealing successive geological layers over 13,000 feet thick from 230 million years ago.

name: sweat
description: A bodily secretion produced through perspiration, mentioned in various contexts throughout the revelations.

name: swine
description: Swine became fetish animals in ancient times, leading to taboos on eating their flesh. Jesus cast pearls before swine as an example of not presenting sacred things to those unable to appreciate them, and swine were involved in the legendary account of Jesus healing the lunatic Amos.

name: Switzerland
description: Switzerland was visited by Jesus during his Mediterranean journey, where he engaged in profound discussions about Buddhism. The region was inhabited by tribes dwelling in houses on piles over lakes and was later where Pontius Pilate retired and committed suicide after his condemnation of Jesus.

name: swords
description: Swords were carried by some of Jesus' apostles, particularly Simon Peter, during the events leading to the Master's betrayal. Jesus taught that those who fight with swords perish with swords, while those who labor in the spirit achieve life everlasting.

name: sycamores
description: Sycamore trees were among the flowering plants that appeared during significant plant evolution. Notably, Zaccheus climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus as he passed through Jericho, leading to Jesus' decision to abide at his house.

name: Sychar
description: Sychar was a Samaritan city near Jacob's well where Jesus made his 17th appearance to about seventy-five believers. Jesus and his apostles preached there for two days before establishing camp on Mount Gerizim, and it was where Jesus first encountered Nalda, the Samaritan woman at the well.

name: symbolism
description: Symbolism in religion can be either beneficial or detrimental depending on whether it preserves or replaces the original worshipful idea. It provides a means for perpetuating moral sentiments and religious loyalties, and those with greater spiritual insight should be tolerant of those who require symbols to mobilize their spiritual understanding.

name: symmetry
description: A fundamental principle of divine reality characterized by perfect balance and harmonious integration. All real beauty embodies symmetry through the unified blending of truth, goodness, and spiritual elements in experience. Divine law and divine love reveal the Father's infinite symmetry, while the presence of spiritual endowments in mortals signifies spiritual symmetry that enables conscious realization of sonship with God.

name: sympathy
description: A genuine emotional response and compassionate understanding toward others that should be practical, personal, and constructive. Jesus taught against false sympathy that breeds self-pity and weakness, instead advocating for sincere compassion that encourages courage and strength. True sympathy should be balanced and restrained, as excessive sympathy can degenerate into emotional instability or fanaticism.

name: synagogues
description: Jewish houses of worship and community gathering where Jesus initially taught during Sabbath services and evening assemblies. Following opposition from Jerusalem religious leaders, the Sanhedrin issued a decree in A.D. 29 closing all synagogues in Palestine to Jesus and his followers, with only the Hebron synagogue refusing to comply before being destroyed by fire. Believers in Jesus were subsequently excommunicated from synagogues throughout Judea.

name: syncopation
description: A musical technique or rhythmic pattern mentioned in The Urantia Book's discussions of celestial music and harmonious arrangements.

name: Syracuse
description: A Mediterranean port city where Jesus and his companions spent a full week during their journey to Rome. The notable event of their stay was Jesus helping Ezra, a backslidden Jewish tavern keeper, find God and return to faith, which later led to the establishment of the first Christian church in Syracuse through Ezra's partnership with a Greek proselyte.

name: Syria
description: A region in the eastern Mediterranean that served as an important center for early civilization, metalworking, pottery, and agriculture. The broad-headed Nodite-Andonite Syrians introduced advanced techniques and domesticated animals to surrounding areas. During Jesus' time, Syria enjoyed peace and prosperity under Roman rule and was the destination of Jesus' final journey outside Palestine, where he visited Antioch, Sidon, and other cities.

name: system
description: An organized arrangement of three or more interconnected parts that creates something greater than the sum of its components. Unlike simple relationships between two objects, systems involve positional values and cosmic organization where individual members are connected through their relationship to the whole. In human systems, personality serves as the unifying factor that coordinates all activities and imparts identity and creativity.

name: system centers
description: Administrative or organizational centers within local systems that coordinate various functions and activities across the inhabited worlds of a system.

name: system co-ordinators
description: Specialized beings belonging to the Morontia Power Supervisors who harmonize and blend the differing energy systems of various morontia worlds into working units. They enable ascending mortals to progress smoothly from physical to spiritual existence by coordinating the individualized energy systems of each successive transition sphere in a local system.

name: System Sovereigns
description: Primary Lanonandek Sons who serve as chief executives of local systems of inhabited worlds. They rule in commissions of two or three from system headquarters, exercising wide powers of personal discretion in directing system affairs. System Sovereigns are appointed by Constellation Fathers and serve for dekamillennium periods, supervising Planetary Princes and enforcing legislative mandates throughout their systems.

name: Taanach
description: An ancient city where Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera, visible from the hill of Simeon near Nazareth where Jesus would often gaze upon the surrounding landscape and recall historical events.

name: Tabamantia
description: A veteran finaliter of agondonter status who serves as sovereign supervisor of all decimal or experimental worlds in the universe of Nebadon. He inspected Urantia and recommended that the planet be granted Material Sons, leading to the arrival of Adam and Eve.

name: tabernacles, feast of
description: A Jewish religious festival, one of the major feasts observed in Judaism.

name: taboos
description: The earliest form of societal regulation, originating as man's effort to avoid offending spirit ghosts. Taboos became the source of ceremonial standards and the ancestor of primitive self-control, serving as lawmakers and institution builders that helped organize early society despite being burdensome and restrictive.

name: tact
description: The fulcrum of social leverage that enables individuals to win support and cooperation from others. Tact allows wise souls to avoid unnecessary social misunderstandings and navigate difficult situations gracefully, as demonstrated by Jesus in his dealings with others.

name: tactics
