description: Methods or strategies employed in dealing with adversaries or challenges. Jesus never resorted to ignoble tactics when facing his enemies, always responding with enlightening, dignified, and conclusive answers rather than unfair or deceptive methods.

name: tadpoles
description: The aquatic larval stage of frogs, representing a phase of development and growth. Used metaphorically to illustrate the importance of living loyally in one's current stage of development to achieve future growth and transformation.

name: talents
description: Natural endowments, abilities, or resources entrusted to individuals for development and service. Featured prominently in Jesus' parable teaching about faithful stewardship, where servants are given different amounts of talents to invest and are expected to produce gains for their master.

name: Talmai
description: The king of Geshur whose daughter David married as part of his political strategy to build himself up through advantageous marriages.

name: Tamar
description: One of the remarkable women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who descended from a long line of notable women in Urantia's racial history.

name: Taoism
description: An ancient Chinese religion and philosophy that arose from the teachings of Singlangton, Salem missionaries, and Indian philosophers, greatly different from modern Taoism. Taoism influenced Buddhism in China, Shinto in Japan, and Christianity in Tibet, and was one of the longest-persisting Chinese religions containing monotheistic truth, with Lao-tse envisioning God in Tao.

name: tapirs
description: Primitive mammals with proboscises that flourished in forested mountain regions 45,000,000 years ago alongside early horses, rhinoceroses, and other small mammals. Tapirs became extinct in North America during the last glaciation.

name: Tarentum
description: An Italian port city where Jesus, Gonod, and Ganid stayed during their Mediterranean journey, setting sail from there to Athens. At Tarentum, Jesus intervened to help an assaulted youth and counseled a man mistreating his wife, demonstrating mercy and wisdom in human relations.

name: Tarichea
description: A fishing village on the west bank of the Jordan River where it flows out of the Sea of Galilee, home to Thomas the apostle and where he and Judas Iscariot joined Jesus and the twelve. The village was known for its fish-drying establishments and merchants who bought fresh catches from local fishermen.

name: tariffs
description: Trade duties or taxes imposed on imported goods, mentioned in the context of economic systems and taxation policies.

name: Tarim basin
description: A fertile valley in Sinkiang that served as the easternmost outpost of Andite culture for nearly fifteen thousand years. The Tarim basin was home to mixed Andite-Andonite settlements that engaged in trade with the Chinese to the east and Andonites to the north, rivaling Mesopotamia in its civilization before perishing when rainfall patterns shifted.

name: Tarshish
description: A distant port city mentioned in Hebrew traditions as the destination of Jonah's attempted voyage to escape his divine calling. Jesus used the story of Jonah's flight to Tarshish to teach a young Philistine about the importance of not running away from duty and service.

name: Tarsus
description: The birthplace of Paul the apostle, where Mithraism was the dominant religion during his adolescence, influencing the religious environment of his formative years.

name: Tasmania
description: An island reached by early human descendants of Andon and Fonta during their eastward migration 950,000 years ago, representing one of the farthest points of early human expansion from Europe through Java.

name: taste designers
description: A division of celestial artisans who work to improve morontia taste and enhance the appreciation of beauty by sharpening the evolving spirit senses of ascending mortals.

name: tattoo
description: The tattoo originated as an ancient badge of tribal membership, marking the hardships and endurance required for initiation ceremonies. Tattooing is an artistic evolution of earlier crude scarring practices.

name: tax collectors
description: Officials responsible for collecting taxes and debts, historically using secret clubs for enforcement. Early forms of taxation included tithes and temple offerings. Jesus interacted with several tax collectors, including Matthew Levi who became an apostle, and Zaccheus, a chief publican who encountered Jesus in Jericho.

name: taxation
description: The system of levying taxes to support government operations, historically beginning with tithes and temple offerings. Excessive taxation can enslave citizenry and obstruct individual freedoms, while proper taxation under citizen control is essential for maintaining state functions without hampering industry.

name: taxpayers
description: Citizens who pay taxes to support government functions. The improvident often depend on thrifty taxpayers for support, creating a form of modern dependency. In some governmental systems, heavy taxpayers receive additional voting privileges based on their tax contributions.

name: Teacher Sons
description: Celestial beings who serve in educational and instructional capacities throughout the universe. These Sons are part of the divine plan for teaching and guiding ascending mortals.

name: teachers
description: Individuals who instruct others in truth and wisdom, playing a crucial role in spiritual and educational development. True teachers maintain intellectual integrity by remaining learners themselves, and effective teaching involves giving out newly acquired truth and experience to those behind them in progression.

name: teachers of rest
description: Celestial artisans and energy manipulators who guide seraphim and other spiritual beings in the technique of divine rest. They counsel on efficient energy utilization and help maintain proper balance between active and passive spiritual forces.

name: teaching counselors
description: A specialized order of seraphim who serve as invaluable assistants to the spiritual teaching corps of the local universe. They function as secretaries and helpers to all orders of teachers, from Melchizedeks to mortals, and are particularly active in educational enterprises on mansion worlds and training spheres.

name: teamwork
description: The collaborative effort of individuals working together toward common goals. Teamwork represents a fundamental principle of effective organization and achievement in both material and spiritual endeavors.

name: tears
description: Expressions of deep emotion, often representing gratitude, sorrow, or joy. In the eternal realms, God will wipe away all tears, as the former things of pain and suffering will pass away.

name: Technical Advisers
description: Technical Advisers are celestial beings devoted to interpreting universal laws and guiding all creatures in proper spiritual techniques and procedures. Originally chosen by the Infinite Spirit from one million of the most orderly minds in the spirit world, they serve as living law libraries throughout the universes, preventing delays and facilitating progress by teaching the divine methods of perfection. They are recruited from various angelic orders and ascending mortals, maintain headquarters throughout the universe systems, and have never been known to go astray in their divine interpretations.

name: technicians
description: Technicians are the fifth order of transition minister seraphim who help ascending mortals adjust to life on the morontia spheres. They serve as liaisons with Morontia Power Supervisors and Master Physical Controllers, act as instructors about morontia energies, and function as emergency space traversers to assist pilgrims in their transition from material to spiritual existence.

name: teeth
description: Early mammals of the Cenozoic developed two sets of teeth along with other distinctive features like mammary glands and hair-covered bodies. Human teeth are slowly evolving from thirty-six in remote ancestors toward twenty-eight as part of an ongoing adaptive evolutionary process. Ancient peoples used teeth from various animals like crocodiles and tigers as magical charms, believing they held special powers.

name: Teherma the Persian
description: Teherma the Persian was a businessman from Damascus who sought out Jesus after hearing of his teachings. Despite being mistakenly labeled a fire worshipper by Simon Zelotes, Teherma explained that fire was merely a symbol of the Pure and Holy One, and after speaking with Jesus, he became convinced of the kingdom teachings and joined the followers.

name: telepathy
description: Adam and Eve possessed the ability to communicate telepathically through thought oscillations transmitted via delicate gas chambers near their brain structures, with a range of about fifty miles. This power was instantly lost when their minds surrendered to the discord and disruption of evil.

name: telescopes
description: Future telescopes will reveal approximately 375 million new galaxies in outer space, greatly expanding human understanding of the universe. Current spectroscopic observations show red-shifted spectral lines from distant systems, creating the appearance of incredible recession speeds, though this apparent motion results from various observational errors and time-space distortions rather than actual velocities.

name: temperament
description: Inherent temperament significantly influences the formation of one's personal religious philosophy and spiritual experience. Along with social status, education, and other environmental factors, temperament helps determine the pattern of religious thought and practice, while also affecting one's disposition toward material accumulation and generosity throughout life.

name: temperance
description: Temperance is one of the fruits of the Spirit that characterizes spirit-led mortals, along with love, joy, peace, and other divine qualities. Jesus preached temperance and taught consistency through proportionate adjustment of life problems, warning that even virtues carried to extremes may become vices and emphasizing the importance of well-balanced characters.

name: temperature
description: Temperature plays a crucial role in cosmic processes, from the earth's core which exceeds the sun's surface temperature due to immense pressure, to stellar interiors reaching 35 million degrees. In earth's atmosphere, temperature drops to 70 degrees below zero in the stratosphere before rising to 1200 degrees at auroral levels, while gravity prevents absolute zero from existing in interstellar space.

name: Temple of New Life
description: The Temple of New Life on mansonia number one contains seven radial wings serving as resurrection halls for the seven races of time. Each wing houses one hundred thousand personal resurrection chambers leading to circular assembly halls that can accommodate one million individuals, surrounded by personality assembly chambers where the real and conscious reassembly of complete personality takes place during resurrection.

name: temple
description: Sacred structures for worship and spiritual fellowship found throughout the universes. Temples range from the exquisite morontia temples on mansion worlds to the stone temple of the Universal Father in the Garden of Eden. Jesus spoke of building a "living temple of spiritual fellowship" based on spiritual reality, and taught that human bodies themselves are "temples of God" where the divine spirit dwells.

name: temptation
description: A spiritual challenge that cannot be overcome through mere human will alone. True victory over temptation comes through developing genuine love for higher ideals and spiritual transformation, redirecting energies into more noble channels. Jesus faced his great temptation on Mount Hermon, where he encountered Satan and Caligastia in a universe trial that established his sovereignty.

name: ten
description: The number representing completeness or a full measure in various spiritual and administrative contexts throughout The Urantia Book.

name: ten-cent piece
description: A reference coin used to describe the size of the half-shekel temple tax that Jewish worshippers were required to pay. Money-changers in the temple courts charged excessive commissions for exchanging various currencies for this orthodox Jewish coin, which was about the size of a ten-cent piece but twice as thick.

name: Ten Commandments
description: The divine laws given by Moses that evolved from earlier, more primitive commandments to reflect advancing concepts of God. Jesus noted how these ten negative commandments were later transformed in Isaiah's time into the positive law of love - to love God supremely and one's neighbor as oneself. Jesus once wrote the Ten Commandments in Greek on boards, which he later destroyed.

name: ten virgins
description: A parable from Jesus' discourse about the destruction of Jerusalem and his second coming that caused great confusion among the apostles. This parable was later incorporated into gospel records through the writings of Selta, a Jewish apocalyptist attached to Emperor Caligula's court.

name: tenderness
description: A divine quality manifested through mercy and compassion toward the weak and needy, indicating that humans are indwelt by God's spirit. True courage includes the ability to show tenderness and emotional sensitivity to human needs, and mature individuals look upon others with feelings of tenderness and tolerance.

name: tension
description: A state of strain or stress that arises in various spiritual, emotional, or social contexts as beings navigate challenges and growth.

name: Tenskwatawa
description: A Shawnee prophet who predicted the 1806 solar eclipse and denounced both shamanism and the vices of white civilization. He exemplified the true teachers who periodically arise to challenge religious shamans and priests who oppose education and scientific progress.

name: tentmaker
description: An occupation Jesus practiced for three weeks while living in Antioch for over two months, during which he studied human nature and prepared the way for later Christian teachers. Paul, who was also a tentmaker, unknowingly followed in Jesus' footsteps and developed teachings about celibacy that were influenced by ancient continence cults.

name: Terah
description: Father of Abraham who, along with his family, were halfhearted converts to the Salem religion and met the Melchizedek receivers' expectations for intelligence, initiative, sagacity, and sincerity.

name: terminal rest of time
description: A final rest period that ascendant souls undergo after attaining the Universal Father and receiving final clearance of the Havona circuits, preceding their achievement of residential status on Paradise.

name: terrestrial escape
description: The liberation of mortals from physical existence through natural death or translation, enabling their transition to the morontia worlds of spiritual evolution and mind progress.

name: tertiaphim
description: High angels who are children of the Infinite Spirit, personalized on Paradise in groups of one thousand and given as companions to Creator Sons during universe organization, later serving as liaison ministers between Creator Sons and the Ancients of Days.

name: Tertiary Circuit Supervisors
description: Circuit supervisors created in groups of seven hundred who function on local universe headquarters, directing spirit messages and personalities across universe circuits and capable of isolating rebellious worlds from higher spiritual circuits.

name: testimonies
description: Authoritative declarations of truth, particularly those given by Divine Counselors based on their direct experience and knowledge of divine realities, serving as reliable witness to spiritual facts.

name: tests
description: Spiritual challenges and trials that measure character and faith, exemplified by Jesus' test of refusing to use divine power for selfish purposes and his standard of evaluating institutions by what they do for the human soul.

name: Thaddeus
description: An alternative name for James Alpheus, one of Jesus' twelve apostles, who along with his brother Judas was known for his humble acceptance of being among "the least of all the apostles."

name: Thailand
description: A Southeast Asian nation, historically known as Siam.

name: Thamna
description: A village where citizens invited Jesus to preach during his ministry, located in the region between Jerusalem and Samaria.

name: thanksgiving
description: The expression of gratitude and appreciation, particularly in religious contexts, representing a fundamental aspect of worship and spiritual relationship with the divine.

name: Thapsacus
description: An ancient city in Mesopotamia through which Jesus and his traveling companions passed during their extensive journey through the Mediterranean and Near Eastern regions.

name: Thebes
description: The ancient Egyptian capital city to which Tutankhamen returned the seat of government, where the priests subsequently gained significant power and eventually seized control of the throne.

name: theft
description: The act of stealing, which among primitive peoples was considered acceptable only against outsiders due to beliefs about personal property containing the owner's essence. As civilization advanced and private property rights developed, theft became recognized as a great crime, with commandments against stealing forming an integral part of religious and moral codes.

name: theology
description: The systematic study and interpretation of religious experience and divine truth, which serves to facilitate self-consciousness of spiritual experience and validate religious claims through living faith. While theology can help define and clarify religious concepts, it becomes lifeless when it masters religion rather than serving it, and must remain grounded in genuine spiritual experience rather than mere intellectual formulation.

name: theories
description: Intellectual formulations and hypothetical explanations that, while useful for understanding, remain powerless to transform human character unless animated by divine spirit. Experience always holds advantage over theory, and living spiritual truth cannot be imprisoned in theoretical formulations without losing its dynamic power.

name: thieves
description: Criminals who engage in robbery and theft, exemplified in parables such as the Good Samaritan story where brigands attacked a traveler, and in the account of thieves who killed a rich man, demonstrating the temporal nature of material wealth versus spiritual treasures.

name: thieves crucified with Jesus
description: Two brigands and associates of Barabbas who were crucified alongside Jesus, having been properly scourged before execution. While both initially mocked Jesus, one later repented and sought forgiveness, receiving Jesus's promise of salvation in Paradise, demonstrating the possibility of redemption even in the final moments of life.

name: thing, meaning, and value
description: A conceptual framework representing the three fundamental aspects of reality that correspond to the domains of science, philosophy, and religion respectively, forming the basis for comprehensive understanding of existence.

name: third day
description: A significant time period in divine plans, most notably when bestowal Sons reappear after passing through death, and specifically when Jesus rose from the dead as he had repeatedly foretold his disciples. Among the Jews, the third day held special meaning as they believed the soul would not linger beyond this time, with decay being well advanced by the fourth day.

name: Third Person of Deity
description: The Third Person of Deity is another designation for the Infinite Spirit, the third member of the Paradise Trinity who serves as the God of Action and the source of mind in creation.

name: Third Source and Center
description: The Third Source and Center is another name for the Infinite Spirit, the third person of the Paradise Trinity who functions as the universal source of mind ministry and spiritual administration throughout creation.

name: Third Source personalities
description: Third Source personalities are beings personal to the Infinite Spirit but not included in the Father's personality circuit. These diverse personalities include power directors, Creative Spirits, and other spiritual beings who are contactable by those in the Father's personality circuit and possess minds with memory, reason, judgment, and creative imagination.

name: thirst
description: Jesus expressed physical thirst while on the cross, prompting the Roman captain to offer him wine. In spiritual terms, those who believe in Jesus will never thirst spiritually, as the divine Spirit provides eternal satisfaction and serves as a well of living water springing up to everlasting life.

name: Thomas Didymus
description: Thomas Didymus was the eighth apostle chosen by Philip, a former fisherman, carpenter, and stone mason from Tarichea. Known for his analytical mind and initial skepticism, he served as manager of the apostolic itinerary and became famous for doubting Jesus' resurrection until seeing him personally, after which he became a courageous and loyal supporter who eventually died for his faith in Malta.

name: Thor (Andite)
description: Thor was the victorious Andite commander who led the white invaders to final victory over the Cro-Magnoid blue race in the decisive battle of the Somme, ending a five-hundred-year struggle and becoming a hero to the northern white tribes.

name: Thor (god)
description: Thor was originally a ghost hero who became revered as a god by northern white tribes after his military victories, later evolving into a nature deity known as the master of lightning, illustrating how human heroes can be deified in religious systems.

name: thorns
description: A crown of thorns was placed on Jesus' head during his scourging as part of the mockery before his crucifixion, symbolizing his suffering. In Jesus' parable of the sower, thorns represent worldly cares and the deceitfulness of riches that choke spiritual growth and prevent fruitfulness.

name: Those High in Authority
description: Those High in Authority are Adjuster-fused mortals who demonstrated superior administrative ability during their ascendant careers and were embraced by the Paradise Trinity to become executives in superuniverse government. They serve as everywhere-present administrators for the Ancients of Days, representing the cream of governing ability from surviving mortals of space.

name: Those without Name and Number
description: Those without Name and Number are ascendant souls who developed exceptional worship ability and spiritual comprehension of the Universal Father's eternal purpose that transcends ordinary evolutionary understanding. As Trinitized Sons of Attainment, they serve as supreme jurors in superuniverse courts and constitute the highest type of spiritual mentality for rendering verdicts in cosmic adjudication.

name: Thoth
description: Egyptian god who healed Set's eye with his spittle in the legendary battle of Horus with Set, illustrating the ancient belief in spittle as a healing agent that spread from Egypt to Arabia and Mesopotamia.

name: thought
description: The mental activity that leads mortals Godward through the highest and most spiritualized thinking. While feelings guide evolutionary development, thoughts provide the realm where the divine spirit makes contact with human beings, enabling spiritual growth through the combined operations of the Thought Adjuster and the Spirit of Truth.

name: Thought Adjuster of Jesus
aliases: the Personalized Adjuster of his indwelling
description: The Personalized Adjuster who guided Jesus throughout his human life, having previously served with Machiventa Melchizedek. This extraordinary Monitor became chief of all Adjusters in Nebadon after Jesus' baptism, when it was personalized and returned to direct the celestial hosts during Jesus' remaining earthly ministry.

name: Thought Adjusters
description: Divine spirit fragments of the Universal Father that indwell the minds of mortal beings, serving as guides toward spiritual perfection and eternal survival. These Mystery Monitors are organized as an independent unit administered from Divinington and function throughout all local universes, preparing mortals for eventual fusion and the Paradise ascent.

name: Thought Changers
description: The designation given to Mystery Monitors during the early phase of mortal indwelling, from their arrival until the human subject reaches comparative full growth at about twenty years of age on Urantia.

name: Thought Controllers
description: The designation given to Mystery Monitors during the final phase of mortal indwelling, from the attainment of discretion at about forty years of age until deliverance from the flesh.

name: thought preservers
description: Celestial artisans dedicated to preserving the higher thoughts and noble ideas of the realms, treasuring gems of mentation on morontia worlds and recording the ideation of great minds in the language of Uversa.

name: thought recorders
description: Celestial artisans who preserve and reproduce the superior thoughts of the realms, functioning in seven specialized groups including thought preservers, concept recorders, and ideograph recorders.

name: thoughtless pagan
description: A man encountered by Jesus, Gonod, and Ganid during their journey to the northern Italian lakes, whom Jesus chose not to engage because the man was not hungry for truth or ready to receive spiritual teaching.

name: Thrace
description: Ancient region where the apostle Matthew met his death at Lysimachia, conspired against by unbelieving Jews and Roman soldiers. The Thracians were notable for their belief in monotheism and immortality, representing advanced religious development.

name: three
description: The number three, which has significance in various cosmic and spiritual contexts throughout The Urantia Book.

name: three-brained mortals
description: Three-brained mortals exhibit a higher level of spiritual, ethical, and worshipful attributes compared to two-brained and one-brained mortals. Their third brain specializes in controlling physical activities, freeing the two superior brains for intellectual functions and spiritual activities.

name: three concentric circles
description: A symbolic pattern of three circles arranged concentrically, appearing in various spiritual and cosmic contexts.

name: threefold fluctuation
description: The threefold fluctuation of light from the Deity abode indicates complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents.

name: threshold
description: A point or level at which something begins or changes, often referring to spiritual or developmental transitions.

name: throwing stick
description: The Andonites crafted the throwing stick for successful hunting expeditions, representing an early tool-making advancement that worked in conjunction with their implement-using abilities.

name: thumbs
description: One million years ago, Mesopotamian dawn mammals emerged with primitive opposable thumbs, descendants of the North American lemur type of placental mammal. The mid-mammals had perfectly opposable thumbs, just about as well adapted for diversified work as the present human thumb.

name: thunder
description: James and John, the sons of thunder, were transformed by Jesus into apostles of love. Thunder was regarded by early humans as the voice of an angry god and was feared and worshiped by primitive tribes and nature cults.

name: thunderstones
description: Bretons protect against lightning with ancient thunderstones, a tradition passed down from the original Andonite inhabitants of western Europe.

name: Thursday
description: Thursday was considered a lucky day for weddings and was believed to bring the most favorable outcomes when celebrating under the full moon, with the custom of throwing grain to ensure fecundity.

name: Ti Tao
description: A mountain pass through which Andites traveled about fifteen thousand years ago to enter China, eventually spreading throughout the upper Yellow River valley and progressing eastward to Honan.

name: Tiberias
description: A new city built by Herod Antipas that became the capital of Galilee, succeeding Sepphoris. Jesus spent a week there during his tour around the Sea of Galilee, and it later became a site where Herod and Sanhedrin representatives held conferences to plot Jesus's arrest, though Herod initially refused to take action against him.

name: Tiberius
description: Roman emperor who was coemperor with Augustus beginning in A.D. 11 and whose fifteenth year of actual rule was A.D. 26, the year of Jesus's baptism. He allowed Pontius Pilate to serve as procurator of Judea for ten years and admired Jesus's kingly bearing when meeting him through Gonod's introduction.

name: Tibet
description: A highland region where Andonites lived in crude stone huts and grottoes, and which served as an ancient gateway for Andite migrations into China. Tibet developed mixed religious practices combining elements of Buddhism, Hinduism, Taoism, and Christianity, though notably lacking the simple gospel teachings of sonship with God and brotherhood with man.

name: tidal friction
description: A gravitational force that gradually slows planetary revolutions, eventually causing planets to always turn the same hemisphere toward their sun or larger body, as illustrated by Mercury and Earth's moon.

name: tidal wave
description: A massive wave that swept over Dalamatia one hundred and sixty-two years after the rebellion, causing the planetary headquarters to sink beneath the sea and obliterating nearly every vestige of its noble culture.

name: tigers
description: Large predatory cats used as an example of nature's indifference to human rights, illustrating that an unarmed person meeting a hungry tiger would demonstrate that nature confers no rights on humans, only life and a world to live in.

name: Tiglath
description: A young lad who assisted Jesus during his sojourn on Mount Hermon, carrying supplies and depositing food at a designated point 6,000 feet above sea level. Jesus requested guardian seraphim to be with Tiglath and gave him a donkey as a gift when departing.

name: Tigris River
description: One of two major rivers, along with the Euphrates, that formed the cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations in Mesopotamia. The second garden was located in the triangle between these rivers, and various ancient peoples including Sumerians and Adamites established important centers along its course.

name: time
description: A bestowal of Paradise that comes by virtue of motion and mind's inherent awareness of sequentiality. Time is the moving image of eternity, essential for finite creatures to coexist with the Infinite, and serves as both an opportunity for spiritual growth and a limitation that must eventually be transcended in the ascension career.

name: timidity
description: A dangerous weakness occasionally manifested by Andrew, one of Jesus's apostles, despite his otherwise faithful leadership and commitment to justice and fairness. More broadly, the fear that God-knowing individuals may experience when asserting their beliefs in the face of shallow objections, despite their inner certainty about spiritual truths.

name: Timothy
description: A companion of Paul and Silas who joined them during their eighteen-month ministry in Corinth, where they encountered students who had been taught by a Jewish tutor.

name: tin
description: A metal that was mixed with copper by the Adamsonites to create bronze, revolutionizing metalworking when one of their number discovered this combination at a highland copper mine located alongside a tin deposit in Turkestan.

name: tithing
description: The practice of giving one-tenth of one's income or possessions, originating as an early form of taxation and later adopted by religious communities. The system was used by ancient Sethite priests, introduced to Abraham by Melchizedek, and continued through Jesus's time and into advanced civilizations as a method of public financing.

name: Titus (associate of Paul)
description: A close associate of Paul who was sent to reorganize the churches in Crete and worked alongside Fortune, a former youth who became the leader of Christians in Crete, in uplifting the Cretan believers.

name: Titus (Emperor)
description: The Roman Emperor whose armies besieged and destroyed Jerusalem in A.D. 70, covering Golgotha with thousands of crosses and bringing terrible destruction upon the Jewish people forty years after Jesus's crucifixion.

name: Titus of Capernaum
description: A prominent nobleman of Capernaum whose critically ill son was healed through Jesus's word while Jesus was in Cana. This experience led Titus and his entire household to believe with whole hearts, and his son later became a minister of the kingdom and was trained in Peter's school for gospel service.

name: Toda tribe
description: A tribe of India whose religious practices represent a regression to primitive levels, characterized by prayers addressed to no one in particular and rituals that contribute nothing to social, moral, or spiritual values, though their prayer forms are gradually evolving toward more intelligent and ethical communication.

name: Todan
description: An early convert who first heard the gospel message at Rimmon and later carried it into Mesopotamia and far beyond, being among the first to preach the good news to those dwelling beyond the Euphrates.

name: tokens
description: Superstitious signs and omens that civilized races still believe in as remnants of ancient ghost cults, used in attempts to avoid ill luck and trouble, representing humanity's slow abandonment of primitive methods of understanding the world.

name: tolerance
description: The quality of understanding and accepting others despite their differences, which Jesus taught leads to friendship and ultimately to love. Tolerance develops through seeking to understand others' viewpoints and motivations, and it represents one of the fruits of the spirit that mature individuals demonstrate toward their fellows.

name: tom-toms
description: Universal primitive drums used rhythmically in early spiritual practices, believed to influence spirits and aid in healing ceremonies alongside other methods like sweating and vapor baths.

name: tombs
description: Burial places and monuments for the dead, ranging from simple stone cairns to elaborate sepulchres. Jesus criticized religious leaders who built elaborate tombs for prophets while planning to kill contemporary truth-bearers, and his own resurrection took place at the tomb of Lazarus in Bethany.

name: tomorrow
description: The future day that Jesus taught is wholly in the hands of the heavenly Father, counseling his followers not to be anxiously concerned about tomorrow since each day has sufficient trouble of its own.

name: tongue
description: The human tongue as a symbol of speech and communication, which Jesus taught is a member that few can tame but which the spirit can transform into a voice of tolerance and mercy. Control of one's tongue represents a true measure of greatness.

name: tools
description: Implements and devices created by humans to extend their capabilities, beginning with the Andonites' stone axes and clubs. Tools enabled the transition from primitive hunting to advanced civilization, with domesticated animals serving as living tools and mechanical devices determining the survival of advancing groups.

name: torture
description: Physical suffering inflicted as part of ancient initiation ceremonies or religious practices, including self-torture used to harden youth and physical humiliation taught by various religious traditions as a form of spiritual discipline.

name: Total Deity
description: A conceptual designation referring to the complete and undifferentiated aspect of deity reality, representing the absolute unity of all divine attributes and functions before their differentiation into the various expressions of divinity.

name: totalitarianism
description: A form of authoritarian government that seeks total control over all aspects of society, mentioned as one of the influences contributing to twentieth-century secularism that blights spiritual experience.

name: totems
description: Sacred symbols representing animals or objects that primitive tribes believed were their ancestral origins, serving as both group identity markers and religious deities. Totemism combined social and religious practices, with totems eventually evolving into modern national symbols and flags.

name: tower of Babel
description: A legendary tower mentioned in ancient traditions, associated with human attempts to reach heaven and subsequent divine intervention causing confusion of languages.

name: traditions
description: Cultural and religious practices passed down through generations that provide stability and continuity but can also stifle spiritual growth and creative freedom. While traditions can bridge the gap between past and present, they may become oppressive when they blind people to new spiritual truths and keep them enslaved to outdated customs and ceremonies.

name: traitors
description: Individuals who betray trust or allegiance, violating loyalty to their commitments, causes, or communities.

name: tranosta
description: The transcendental energy system that operates on and from the upper level of Paradise, functioning exclusively in connection with absonite beings and known by this name on Uversa.

name: transcendence
description: The divine quality of God existing beyond and above the material universe while simultaneously dwelling within each individual. This paradox of divine transcendence and immanence challenges human comprehension but must be unified through faith to justify intelligent worship and validate the hope of personality survival.

name: transcendental Almighty
description: A manifestation of God the Ultimate that will occur when He begins to function as supercontrol of the universes, exercising overcontrol of supertime and transcended space over the administrative functions of the Supreme Almighty.

name: transcendental energy
description: The energy system that operates on and from the upper level of Paradise, functioning exclusively in connection with absonite beings and designated as tranosta on Uversa.

name: transcendental level
description: The absonite level of reality characterized by beings and phenomena that transcend time and space, existing beyond finite limitations but below absolute reality.

name: Transcendental Recorders
description: Personal gravity traversers who exist without discoverable form, unlike most beings in the seven superuniverses. They function as transcendental beings capable of instantaneous transit but are not available to superuniverse and local universe administrators.

name: Transcendentalers
description: Eventuated beings of absonite attributes who exist beyond time and space, neither created nor creators, but simply eventuated. They reside exclusively in the west of Paradise and are concerned solely with the superadministration of the master universe, serving as transcendental supercitizens loyal to the Paradise Trinity and obedient to God the Ultimate.

name: transcription, celestial
description: The process or method by which celestial beings record information and events.

name: transfiguration
description: A significant transformative event in the life of Jesus, representing a moment of divine revelation and spiritual manifestation.

name: transformation
description: The spiritual process by which mortals are changed through the Spirit of Truth, enabling them to perform God's will and transition from material to spiritual existence. This transformation occurs in expectant, faith-dominated persons and leads to loving service, intellectual communion, and social ministry as natural byproducts of inner spiritual renewal.

name: transformers
description: Celestial beings or mechanisms involved in the transformation and manipulation of energy within the universe.

name: transit sleep
description: A state of rest or suspended consciousness experienced during spiritual transportation or transition between celestial realms.

name: transit trio
description: The transit trio are celestial beings who assist Havona pilgrims in their final journey to attain the Universal Father and enter eternity, announcing when the last venture of time is about to ensue.

name: transition
description: The process of change from one state or level to another, particularly from material to spiritual existence. Transition is always accompanied by confusion and requires careful guidance, whether in religious, social, or individual contexts. It represents a critical period requiring wisdom and stability to successfully navigate from established methods to new and better procedures.

name: transition ministers
description: The sixth order of seraphic servers who facilitate the transit of material and mortal creatures from temporal life in the flesh into the early stages of morontia existence on the seven mansion worlds. These angels serve in seven specialized orders including seraphic evangels, racial interpreters, mind planners, morontia counselors, technicians, recorder-teachers, and ministering reserves.

name: transition rest
description: A period of rest or recuperation associated with transitional phases in spiritual progression or celestial transport.

name: transition world #1
description: The first of the transition worlds, likely associated with the finaliters and their ongoing universe service.

name: transitional culture worlds
description: Worlds that serve as cultural transition points, mentioned in connection with Jerusem.

name: translation
description: The process by which mortals who achieve Adjuster fusion during their mortal lifetime are translated directly from material existence to morontia life without experiencing natural death. These translated beings bypass the mansion worlds and appear immediately in the presence of the Sovereign Son on the local universe capital, becoming increasingly common as worlds advance in light and life.

name: translation sleep
description: A ten-day rest period that morontia progressors enter as they advance between mansion worlds on their ascension journey.

name: translators
description: Various beings who facilitate communication across different languages and superuniverses, including broadcast directors who adapt Ancients of Days transmissions for local universes, and Morontia Companions who serve as interpreters and language teachers for ascending mortals on the mansion worlds.

name: transmigration
description: The belief in the reincarnation of souls into successive incarnations as humans, animals, or plants. This doctrine, which originated from the Dravidian Deccan, contaminated emerging monotheistic beliefs and robbed struggling mortals of hope for spiritual advancement and deliverance in death.

name: transport advisers
description: A corps of seventy technical advisers on Urantia who collaborate with star students and transport seraphim to work out routings and serve as traffic supervisors for celestial transportation on inhabited planets.

name: transport seraphim
description: Seraphic beings responsible for celestial transportation services, working with transport advisers to facilitate movement of personalities between worlds and spheres.

name: transport supernaphim
description: Superangelic beings involved in transportation services at higher levels of universe administration.

name: transportation
description: The various methods and systems used for movement across space and between worlds, including mechanical transport for material beings, energy-allied systems for morontia beings, and direct Adjuster transit for fused mortals. Transportation systems facilitate commerce, promote peace, and enable the movement of ascending personalities throughout the universe.

name: transportation personalities
description: Seconaphim responsible for transporting time pilgrims from superuniverse headquarters worlds to the outer circle of Havona, operating as the transport corps of the superuniverses for inward and outward travel.

name: travel
description: Travel in the universe encompasses both physical transportation and spiritual progression. Celestial visitors may travel to any realm they have experienced, with seraphic transporters facilitating interplanetary travel, though mortals never return to their native planet during their current existence. The human desire for travel will be fully satisfied during the long journey to Paradise through local, super, and central universes, while earthly travel promotes civilization and brotherhood through cultural exchange, development of common languages, and international contact.

name: treasure
description: Material wealth becomes treasure when it absorbs one's interests and diverts affections from spiritual pursuits of the kingdom. Jesus taught that disciples should lay up treasures in heaven rather than on earth, for where one's treasure is, there will the heart be also. The parable of the treasure in the field illustrates that the kingdom of heaven is worth sacrificing everything to obtain.

name: treaties
description: Early treaties were sealed through ritualistic exchanges including maidens and blood drinking, with the first formal tribal treaty concerning shared access to a salt deposit. While treaties historically served to establish peace between groups, world peace cannot be maintained by treaties, diplomacy, or other makeshift arrangements with national sovereignties—only world law enforced by world government can achieve lasting global peace.

name: tree of life
description: A real shrub from Edentia that sustained the material beings of Caligastia's staff and later Adam and Eve with life-extending energy. The tree grew in the central courtyard of temples on Urantia, first at Dalamatia and later in the Garden of Eden. Its fruit contained antidotal complements to aging that were useless to ordinary evolutionary beings but essential for maintaining the extended lives of materialized celestial staff and the Material Son and Daughter.

name: trees
description: During the Carboniferous period, trees reached heights of 125 feet with trunks seven feet in diameter, spreading spores by wind. Tree worship was one of the oldest religious practices, with varying cultural beliefs about tree spirits ranging from kindly to cruel among different tribes and races. Jesus taught that trees are known by their fruits, using this metaphor to illustrate moral and spiritual principles about judging character and bearing good works.

name: triad beliefs
description: Evolutionary religious concepts arising from natural human experiences like three-jointed fingers, three-legged stools, and temporal sequences (yesterday, today, tomorrow). These primitive triad beliefs, found among Persians, Hindus, Greeks, and other cultures, differ fundamentally from the revealed Trinity concept and stem from humanity's natural tendency to think in threes rather than divine revelation.

name: triangles
description: Administrative divisions on celestial worlds, including the seventy triangular sections on Edentia whose boundaries converge at sector headquarters buildings, and the one hundred triangles on Jerusem that direct local administrative affairs. These geometric organizational units facilitate governance and provide residential areas for various orders of celestial beings and ascending mortals.

name: Triassic
description: An early reptilian geological period from 150 to 125 million years ago characterized by extensive erosion deposits of conglomerates, shale, and sandstone. The period featured arid climates across America and Europe, evidenced by gypsum and red sedimentary layers, with these dry regions experiencing significant erosion from violent periodic cloudbursts from surrounding highlands.

name: triata
description: Threefold organizations of energy-matter in Havona that respond to linear gravity and serve as the superpower mother systems of time and space creations. These triune energy systems of the central universe, coupled with antigravity effects, enable perfect balance and harmony within Havona's immense spheres, representing the existential energy domain of the Conjoint Actor functioning for the Paradise Trinity.

name: tribal mark
description: Identifying marks cut or tattooed on the body during puberty initiation ceremonies that signified group membership and provided protection in ancient societies. Without a tribal mark proving group association, individuals faced certain death when encountered by neighboring tribes, as these marks served as essential insurance against violent assault and testified to belonging to a group that would avenge harm done to its members.

name: tribes
description: Social groups that evolved from clans but remained smaller than territorial states, serving as intermediate stages in political development from families to nations. Tribal war chiefs established patrilineal leadership while clan peace chiefs ruled through the mother line, fostering local self-government but hindering the growth of unified nations.

name: tribute money
description: The denarius coin bearing Caesar's image that Jesus used to illustrate his teaching about rendering to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God when questioned about paying tribute to Rome.

name: trifles
description: Minor matters or trivial concerns of little importance or significance.

name: trilobites
description: Dominant marine animals of early geological periods that were sexed creatures existing in many forms, including carnivorous, herbivorous, omnivorous, and mud-eating varieties. These segmented arthropods, predecessors of modern crustaceans, eventually declined and nearly disappeared as larger mollusks and cephalopods assumed dominance of the seas.

name: Trimurti
description: The supreme trinity of Hinduism consisting of Brahma (the self-created first member identified with fate), Vishnu (who periodically incarnates in human form and becomes real in Indian imagination), and Siva (lord of life and death, master of destruction and fertility).

name: Trinities
description: Deity reality associations that are always collective manifestations of coordinated Deity, never individual personalities, encompassing and correlating personal aspects within a unified whole. Trinities include the original Paradise Trinity and experiential Trinities such as the Trinity Ultimate and Trinity Absolute, representing truths of relationship and facts of coordinate Deity manifestation.

name: trinitization
description: The supreme creative performance whereby perfect spiritual beings unite to bring forth a new creature being, accomplished by both Deities and qualified creatures including finaliters and Paradise-Havona personalities. This extraordinary process involves unique spiritual techniques and results in the bi-unification of the trinitizing parents, who become spiritually as one while producing offspring that embody concepts unsuited to comprehension by either eternal or temporal creatures alone.

name: Trinitized Ambassadors
description: The second order of Trinitized Sons of Selection, recruited from Spirit-fused and Son-fused ascendant mortals who have reached Paradise and been Trinity-embraced in classes of seven thousand. They serve as emissaries of the Ancients of Days throughout the superuniverses, particularly in minor sectors, representing superior minds selected to assist superuniverse rulers in understanding and administering the interests of their respective groups.

name: Trinitized Custodians
description: Trinity-embraced ascendant seraphim and translated midway creatures who have traversed Havona and attained Paradise, then been assigned to superuniverse service. Numbering about ten million in Orvonton, they serve as officers in the courts of superuniverse governments, administering group affairs and fostering collective projects rather than dealing with individuals.

name: Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy
description: The highest order of Stationary Sons of the Trinity, consisting of seventy supreme administrators who direct the seven sacred worlds nearest Paradise in the personal circuit of the Universal Father. Each world is presided over by ten of these beings, who serve as personal agents of specialized Divine presences and are related to the secrets of the Supreme, Ultimate, and Supreme-Ultimate.

name: Trinitized Sons of Attainment
description: The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including the Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number, are ascendant mortals who achieved Paradise, were embraced by the Paradise Trinity, and were subsequently assigned to superuniverse service under the Ancients of Days.

name: trinitized sons of destiny
description: Trinitized Sons of Destiny are beings produced by the joint trinitization efforts of finaliters and Paradise-Havoners, embodying certain aspects of the unrevealed master universe function of the Supreme-Ultimate and representing actualities of Supreme-Ultimate Deity not otherwise experientially attained.

name: Trinitized Sons of God
description: The Trinitized Sons of God comprise three primary divisions: Deity-trinitized Sons, Trinity-embraced Sons, and Creature-trinitized Sons. All have experienced trinitization either as part of their origin or through subsequent Trinity embrace, and they serve faithfully in various capacities throughout the superuniverses, never having gone astray in their service.

name: Trinitized Sons of Perfection
description: Trinitized Sons of Perfection are twice-trinitized beings who are creature-trinitized sons of Paradise-Havona personalities or perfected ascendant mortals, subsequently embraced by the Paradise Trinity. They serve as Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants to the Ancients of Days, each personifying a single supreme concept or universal ideal, though they are limited by time-space inhibited experiential capacity.

name: Trinitized Sons of Selection
description: The Trinitized Sons of Selection, including Trinitized Custodians and Trinitized Ambassadors, are recruited from evolutionary seraphim, translated midway creatures, and Spirit-fused or Son-fused mortals who have traversed Havona and attained Paradise, then embraced by the Paradise Trinity and assigned to the courts of the Ancients of Days.

name: Trinity
description: The Trinity refers to the Paradise Trinity, the eternal union of the Universal Father, Eternal Son, and Infinite Spirit as a corporate entity distinct from but inclusive of the three Paradise Deities.

name: Trinity Absolute
description: The Trinity Absolute is the second experiential Trinity consisting of God the Supreme, God the Ultimate, and the unrevealed Consummator of Universe Destiny. This Trinity functions on personal, superpersonal, and nonpersonal levels toward the experientialization of Absolute Deity, though its full function transcends the master universe and could only be realized after the entire master universe evolved to completion.

name: Trinity ambassadors
description: Trinity ambassadors are Paradise Trinity representatives who serve as liaisons to local universe rulers, coordinating administrative activities of universal government. They include the Unions of Days, such as Immanuel of Salvington, who serves as the Trinity ambassador to the local universe of Nebadon.

name: Trinity-embraced Sons
description: Trinity-embraced Sons are beings of dual or single origin who have been embraced by the Paradise Trinity and are forever devoted to Trinity service and assignment, comprising seven orders of personalities organized for superuniverse service.

name: Trinity guides
description: Trinity guides are secondary supernaphim who serve as tireless ministers on the fifth circle of Havona, providing each ascending pilgrim with undivided attention and advanced instruction concerning the divine Trinity in preparation for the Deity adventure and personality recognition of the Infinite Spirit.

name: Trinity Infinite
description: The Trinity Infinite represents the conceptual unlimited integration of all triunity relationships within the First Source and Center, encompassing both deified and undeified aspects. Philosophers postulate this existential-experiential Trinity Infinite as a theoretical culmination, though its personalization remains beyond current understanding.

name: Trinity of Supremacy
description: The Trinity of Supremacy represents the Paradise Trinity's relationship to finite existence and serves as the threefold Deity domain of the grand universe. It encompasses the coordinated sovereignty involving the Supreme Being's evolutionary actualization through Creator Sons, Ancients of Days, and Master Spirits, reflecting the Trinity's attitude toward finite reality.

name: Trinity of Trinities
description: The Trinity of Trinities represents the ultimate culmination of all triunities and triodities, providing unlimited integration of reality through three levels of Trinity association. This existential-experiential Trinity completes the cycle of reality, unifying experiential and existential infinity in the pre-experiential I AM.

name: Trinity of Ultimacy
description: The Trinity of Ultimacy represents the Paradise Trinity's transcendental function in relation to absonite levels of existence that are more than finite but less than absolute. This aspect of the Trinity operates on transcendental levels beyond finite reality.

name: Trinity-origin beings
description: Trinity-origin beings are personalities brought into existence directly by the Paradise Trinity, all endowed with freewill and none having ever gone astray. They possess special transit prerogatives and serve vital roles throughout superuniverse administrations, including orders such as Trinity Teacher Sons and various administrative beings.

name: Trinity Teacher Sons
description: Trinity Teacher Sons are beings of Trinity origin who serve as supernal teachers throughout the grand universe to all personalities, human and divine. These Daynals initiate spiritual ages on evolutionary worlds, working in groups to facilitate the transition to the era of light and life, and serve as the educational ministry to mortal creatures and lower orders of spiritual beings.

name: Trinity Ultimate
description: The Trinity Ultimate is the first experiential Trinity consisting of the Supreme Being, the Supreme Creator Personalities, and the absonite Architects of the Master Universe. This Trinity coordinates transcendental reality and mobilizes forces for inconceivable developments in outer space universes.

name: triodities
description: Triodities are non-Father triune relationships directly concerned with the cosmic appearance of experiential Deities such as the Supreme, Ultimate, and Absolute. These associations of absolute actuals and potentials, including the triodity of actuality and the triodity of potentiality, contribute to the functional unification of infinity and serve as the foundation for experiential Deity emergence.

name: triumph
description: A concept representing ultimate victory or success, particularly in spiritual or cosmic contexts within the universal plan of divine progression and achievement.

name: triune-origin beings
description: Triune-origin beings are personalities created by all three Paradise Deities, either individually or as the Trinity, along with the Trinitized Corps. This classification includes various orders of beings such as the Supreme Spirits, Stationary Sons of the Trinity, and Trinity-origin beings, representing those with inherent relationship to all three Paradise Deities.

name: triunities
description: Functional associations of the Seven Absolutes of Infinity that eternalize the potential of all reality, both deified and undeified. Seven major triunities are revealed, each serving specific universal functions: the first triunity (personal-purposive) consists of the Father, Son, and Spirit; the second (power-pattern) includes the Father-Son, Paradise Isle, and Conjoint Actor; and others govern energy, spirit evolution, and cosmic relationships. Unlike the Paradise Trinity which is an organic entity of undivided Deity, triunities are functional partnerships that serve as the balance wheel of infinity.

name: trivialities
description: Minor matters that can distract from essential spiritual realities and meaningful work. Jesus counseled his followers to avoid wasting time on trivialities that detract from wholehearted devotion to preaching the gospel, while he himself focused on the essentials of any undertaking rather than spending time on trifles. Many people become preoccupied with trivial concerns of daily life while overlooking the more important task of attuning to their divine Adjuster and achieving consecration to eternal aims.

name: Troas
description: An ancient city through which Jesus and his companions traveled on their journey from Athens to Ephesus during their Mediterranean tour. At Troas, they engaged in discussions about the worship of objects crafted by human hands, continuing their philosophical and religious conversations as they made their way across the region.

name: tropics
description: Warm climate regions that, while offering easier living conditions, historically hindered human progress and civilization. Races that sought ease in tropical climates seldom advanced significantly, as the comfortable environment reduced the stimulus for action, invention, and resourcefulness that cold and hunger provided in higher latitudes and open lands where human evolution made greater progress.

name: troposphere
description: The lower five or six miles of Earth's atmosphere where weather phenomena occur, including winds and air currents. In this region, temperature steadily falls with altitude, reaching around 70 degrees below zero Fahrenheit at six to eight miles above the surface, before entering the stratosphere where temperature remains constant for the next forty miles of ascent.

name: trumpets
description: Musical instruments referenced both literally and metaphorically in spiritual contexts. Religious leaders are cautioned against trying to call modern people to spiritual battle using outdated approaches like medieval trumpet blasts, needing instead new and current methods. Wealthy Pharisees would sometimes literally blow trumpets when giving charity to publicize their philanthropy, even during banquets where they invited street beggars to receive food portions.

name: trust
description: A fundamental spiritual quality and crucial test of character for will creatures, representing the true measure of self-mastery and trustworthiness. Trust is both a human acquisition fostered by planetary seraphim and a divine attribute, as the Universal Father freely trusts humanity by placing Thought Adjusters within mortal minds. Childlike trust secures entrance into the kingdom of heavenly ascent, while the development of trustworthiness allows for increased responsibility and spiritual advancement.

name: trust funds
description: Financial arrangements for managing and preserving assets, though this concept receives minimal specific treatment in the spiritual teachings of The Urantia Book, which focuses more on spiritual trusts and stewardships rather than material financial instruments.

name: truth
description: A spiritual reality and living value that exists on high spiritual levels and can only be truly known through divine spirit discernment. Truth is beautiful, coherent, and always a revelation—either through the work of the indwelling Adjuster or celestial agencies. While truth is uniform and universal in its divine nature, it cannot be imprisoned in formulas or creeds but must be lived and experienced. Jesus declared himself to be "the truth" and promised to send the Spirit of Truth to guide believers into all truth, emphasizing that divine truth is best known by its spiritual flavor rather than intellectual concepts alone.

name: truth, beauty, and goodness
description: The threefold revelation of divinity reality that represents the intellectual, aesthetic, and moral approach to understanding the universe of mind, matter, and spirit. These divine qualities must be unified into a supreme ideal and can only be fully realized in spiritual experience. Truth embodies the special ministry of Paradise Sons, beauty reflects the Isle of Paradise in material creation, and goodness manifests through the loving ministry of the Infinite Spirit's personalities. Together, they constitute love—humanity's perception of God as spiritual Father—and their integration produces health, sanity, and happiness in human experience.

name: Truth-Justice-Righteousness
description: The Egyptian triad of Truth-Justice-Righteousness was preached by pre-Salemite moral leaders in the Nile valley thousands of years before Hebrew scriptures were written, representing one of the highest expressions of purely human religion on Urantia.

name: Tubal-Cain
description: Son of Lamech who created superior metal weapons, inspiring his father's exultation that became the scriptural basis for the concept of mercy and forbearance illustrated by the term seventy-seven.

name: tulip trees
description: Flowering trees that emerged alongside fig trees and magnolias 90,000,000 years ago when angiosperms suddenly appeared and overran the continents during the Cretaceous period.

name: tunic
description: Jesus's seamless vestment that reached down to near the knees, which the soldiers at his crucifixion decided to cast lots for rather than cut into pieces due to its unusual quality.

name: Turanians
description: Central Asian peoples whose ancestors were upstepped by the Andites, possessing significant Caucasoid inheritance through Andite blending and Andonic crossing, with traces of Andite blood still evident in blond types found among Turanian and Tibetan peoples.

name: turban
description: A head covering worn by Jesus that was taken by one of the four soldiers who divided his garments at the crucifixion.

name: Turkestan
description: A fertile region of central Asia that served as a major crossroads of ancient civilization, where Adamites and Nodites blended with superior inhabitants to extend the Andite race northward, and from which numerous migrations spread Andite influence across Eurasia, India, and China.

name: turtles
description: Reptilian descendants that first appeared in North America via the northern land bridge from Asia, becoming one of the four surviving divisions of the saurian family along with snakes, crocodiles, and frogs.

name: Tut
description: Leader of the governors of advanced tribal relations among the Caligastia one hundred, who promoted peaceful group associations and improved tribal governments but was lost to rebellion along with his entire commission on tribal government.

name: Tutankhamen
description: Son-in-law of Ikhnaton who reverted to the worship of old gods under priestly pressure, changing his name from its original form and moving the capital back to Thebes, leading to increased priestly power in Egypt.

name: twelve
description: The number twelve, which appears frequently throughout The Urantia Book in various contexts and organizational structures.

name: twelvefold adjutants of the Havona circuits
description: Specialized beings who serve as adjutants to the circuits of Havona, the central universe.

name: twentieth century
description: A period characterized by psychological instability, economic upheavals, moral confusion, and sociological turbulence, during which thousands of people became dislocated and needed the stabilization of sound religion amid unprecedented scientific achievement but spiritual stagnation.

name: twins
description: The birth of twins has occurred at significant evolutionary moments, including the first Primates who stood upright from birth, and has been regarded by various cultures as either very lucky or very unlucky, sometimes resulting in the killing of one twin due to beliefs about magic or infidelity.

name: two-and-two
description: The practice of sending disciples and evangelists out in pairs, as established by Jesus to enable the union of souls and mobilization of wisdom, prevent isolation, and provide mutual support in spreading the gospel of the kingdom.

name: two-brained mortals
description: Mortals with a two-hemisphere brain type, like Urantians, who are more imaginative, adventurous, and philosophical than one-brained mortals but somewhat less spiritual, ethical, and worshipful than three-brained orders.

name: two sons
description: A parable told by Jesus about a father with two sons, one who initially refused to work in the vineyard but later repented and went, and another who agreed to go but did not, illustrating the true nature of obedience and faithfulness.

name: Tyrannus
description: The owner of a school in Ephesus where Paul conducted nightly lectures on religion and philosophy in the main audience chamber during his two-year residence, helping to establish Christianity in that city.

name: Tyre
description: An ancient Phoenician seaport city where Jesus and his apostles taught from July 11-24, establishing a strong Christian community among the polyglot population, with Jesus speaking at the Melkarth temple where a Christian church was later built on the same site.

name: Tyrian purple
description: A valuable dye made from sea animals that made Tyre and Sidon famous worldwide, whose manufacturers became believers in the kingdom and spread the gospel of the fatherhood of God and brotherhood of man as they migrated to find new sources of the shellfish.

name: Tyrus
description: A city or town in Perea visited by the seventy messengers during their mission from January 3, A.D. 30 until Jesus' death.

name: ubiquity
description: The quality of being present everywhere at all times, relating to the omnipresent nature of divine reality.

name: ugliness
description: The antithesis of beauty, with materialism and atheism representing the maximum expression of ugliness in contrast to the unifying beauty of harmonious reality.

name: Ukraine
description: A region in Europe where Andonites entered the continent around 3000 B.C., having been pushed northward by Andites for thousands of years before penetrating Europe via the Balkans and Ukraine.

name: ultimacy
description: The highest level of divine realization that evolving mortals may eventually attain, representing the comprehension of God's ultimate nature beyond supremacy.

name: ultimata
description: A collective term for puissant and gravity energies in their emergent forms, representing pre-electronic energy that responds to Paradise gravity and serves as the ancestor of all universe matter.

name: Ultimate
description: A reference to God the Ultimate, the deity functioning on the absonite level of reality beyond time and space.

name: Ultimate Deity
description: Another designation for God the Ultimate, representing deity function on the absonite level that transcends finite time-space limitations.

name: ultimate level
description: The absonite level of reality that transcends finite time and space, associated with God the Ultimate's function and eventual mortal realization.

name: ultimatons
description: The first measurable form of energy and prime physical units of material existence that make up electrons, consisting of energy particles that respond to Paradise gravity and mutual attraction but not to local linear gravity.

name: ultraspecialization
description: A concept related to excessive or extreme specialization, connected to the broader idea of overspecialization.

name: ultraviolet rays
description: The ultraviolet or chemical rays of sunlight and various mechanical productions that may cause harm.

name: Umajor the fifth
description: The headquarters world of the major sector Splandon, surrounded by seventy satellites devoted to superuniverse intellectual training and development for ascending mortals.

name: umbilical cord
description: A highly prized fetish in primitive cultures, particularly in Africa, where preserved umbilical cords set with pearls served as mankind's first necklaces and toys.

name: Uminor the third
description: The headquarters world of the minor sector Ensa, surrounded by seven spheres dedicated to the higher physical studies of ascending life.

name: unanimity
description: A state of complete agreement or unity of opinion, related to the broader concept of unity.

name: unbelievers
description: Those who lack faith in God or spiritual realities, yet may still exhibit religious traits and loyalty to causes, though without the spiritual roots necessary for eternal survival value.

name: uncertainty
description: A fundamental aspect of mortal existence that serves divine purposes, teaching creatures to develop faith, courage, and trust while providing the essential element of adventure and growth in the cosmic journey.

name: Unclassified Centers
description: The seventh order of Supreme Power Centers that function in special local situations with extraordinary energy relationships, serving as universe balance wheels or energy governors on select spheres.

name: Uncle Joshua
description: The affectionate name children used for Jesus when he worked at the repair shop, where he would gather them around to tell captivating stories and share moments of laughter and wonder.

name: unclean spirits
description: Malevolent spiritual entities associated with demon possession and mental affliction.

name: undershepherds
description: True spiritual leaders who guide their followers with worthiness, providing good spiritual nourishment and leading them to peaceful, life-sustaining places.

name: understanding
description: A concept related to comprehension, knowledge, sympathy, tolerance, and wisdom, particularly associated with mental and spiritual development.

name: unemployment
description: The condition of being without work, which civilizations must address through efficient job placement and training programs to prevent long-term economic instability and social problems.

name: unfortunates
description: Those who suffer misfortune or disadvantage, toward whom the favored majority should show kindness and consideration, as divine mercy naturally extends to the weak and needy.

name: unhappiness
description: A state of discontent arising from deficient personality unification or lack of worthy goals, which can be overcome through spiritual insight and divine guidance.

name: uniformity
description: A rigid standardization that differs from true spiritual unity, as Jesus prayed for unity among believers while allowing for diversity of personal interpretation and experience.

name: Unions of Days
description: Paradise Trinity personalities who serve as counseling ambassadors to local universes, with exactly seven hundred thousand in existence, acting as observers and advisers to Creator Sons.

name: Unions of Souls
description: Reflective associates of the Perfectors of Wisdom who promote ethical relationships and teamwork, helping resolve problems arising from associations between intelligent beings throughout the universe.

name: United Kingdom
description: A nation located in the British Isles, referenced in geographical contexts within The Urantia Book.

name: United Midwayers of Urantia
description: A unified organization of both primary and secondary midway creatures serving on Urantia, combining the ancient primary midwayers with the secondary midwayers descended from the Adamic staff.

name: United States of North America
description: The United States of North America exemplifies the creative potential of race mixtures combining superior strata, leading to increased vigor and fertility. The American states surrendered sovereignty to the federal government, promoting peace and unity within the nation through federal governance rather than individual state sovereignty.

name: unity
description: The fundamental principle of divine coordination and integration throughout creation, manifested in the eternal interassociation of the Father, Son, and Spirit in universe administration. Unity represents the underlying oneness that connects all levels of reality, from the Paradise Trinity to the evolving universes, and is essential for spiritual brotherhood and cosmic harmony.

name: Universal Absolute
description: The third of the three Absolutes of Infinity that functions as the associative coordinator between the Deity Absolute and Unqualified Absolute, resolving the tension created by the differentiation of universe reality into deified and undeified values. The Universal Absolute enables the coexistence of finite and infinite reality and serves as the unifier of static and dynamic potentials.

name: Universal Censors
description: There are exactly eight billion Universal Censors in existence who represent the judgment of the Paradise Trinity. These unique beings are not merely reflective of decisions but actually embody divine judgment itself, serving throughout all levels of universe administration from Havona to the evolutionary worlds, always functioning in association with other Trinity-origin personalities.

name: Universal Conciliators
description: Beings created in groups of four for each Havona Servital through a reflective response technique involving the Seven Master Spirits and Reflective Spirits. These conciliating commissions consist of a Judge-Arbiter, Spirit-Advocate, Divine Executioner, and Recorder who serve at various universe levels to resolve disputes and interpret cosmic law, advancing through progressive stages of universe service.

name: Universal Controller
description: The aspect of the I AM that represents God-as-pattern, establishing the master pattern of impersonal and nonspiritual relationships and serving as the primal cause of Paradise's existence and configuration.

name: Universal Creator
description: The aspect of the I AM representing the union of the Father and Eternal Son that initiates the creative cycle, resulting in the appearance of the Conjoint Actor and the eternal universe of Havona, characterized by divine unity in all creative acts.

name: Universal Father
description: The First Person of Deity, the First Source and Center of all reality, creator and controller of the universe of universes. The Universal Father is the infinite and eternal God who bestows personality, indwells mortals through Thought Adjusters, and serves as the loving Father of all personalities throughout creation, being both transcendent in Paradise and immanent in the hearts of his children.

name: Universal Intelligence
description: A designation referring to the cosmic intelligence that pervades creation, often associated with the mind ministry of the Infinite Spirit and the universal coordination of intellectual realities throughout the universes.

name: Universal Mind
description: A conceptual aspect of deity associated with the Infinite Spirit.

name: Universal Mother
description: The Supreme Being in its nurturing aspect, serving as the divine Mother who sustains all universe creatures throughout their lives, complementing the Universal Father as divine parents of the cosmic family.
