name: Universal One of Infinity
description: The eternal self-relationship of Infinity expressing the I AM, revealed as the Universal Father in personality, as pure energy and spirit coherence in impersonal manifestation, and as the primacy of the First Source and Center.

name: Universal Oversoul
description: A Brahmanic doctrine that conceived the totality of all creature existence as one universal entity, bringing Indian philosophers close to the truth of the Supreme Being but failing to provide a rational approach to monotheistic Deity attainment.

name: universe
description: The vast integrated mechanism of physical, intellectual, and spiritual creation that is absolutely controlled by one infinite mind and serves as a gigantic training school for ascending creatures progressing toward Paradise perfection.

name: Universe Aids
description: Beings engaged in specific missions for Creator Sons on headquarters worlds of local and superuniverses, carrying out observations and work under the authority of the Creator Sons.

name: Universe Circuit Supervisors
description: Beings created exclusively by the Infinite Spirit who direct and manipulate all spirit-energy circuits outside Paradise, serving eternally in their original assignments to supervise the transmission of spirit messages and transit of personalities.

name: Universe Co-ordinators and Constellation Counselors
description: An order of 100,000 beings involved in the classification and administration of Lanonandek Sons.

name: universe of universes
description: The totality of all creation including the central universe, seven superuniverses, and outer space levels, constituting the master universe under divine administration.

name: universe orientators
description: Supreme seraphim who serve as counselors to ascending creatures pausing on Salvington before their superuniverse adventure, helping them adjust to the transition from attained to unattained levels of universe service.

name: universe power
description: The fourth stage of cosmic energy where space-energy has been transformed into universe power that can be directed into channels and made to serve the manifold purposes of the universe Creators.

name: Universe Power Directors
description: A vast order of living energy-control beings created by the Seven Master Spirits who regulate, stabilize, and manipulate physical energies throughout the grand universe. They include the Seven Supreme Power Directors, Supreme Power Centers, Master Physical Controllers, and Morontia Power Supervisors.

name: Universe Sons
description: A general designation for divine Sons who function in universe creation and administration, primarily referring to Creator Sons who establish and govern local universes.

name: Universe Spirits
description: A general designation for divine Spirits who partner with Universe Sons in local universe administration, primarily referring to local universe Mother Spirits who work with Creator Sons.

name: univitatia
description: Permanent residents of constellation headquarters worlds who exist on a plane between the material and spiritual but are not morontia beings. Created by the Creator Son and Creative Spirit in seventy different forms, they serve ascending mortals during their constellation sphere training much like Havona natives serve pilgrims in the central universe.

name: unjust judge
description: The parable of the unjust judge teaches the importance of persistence in prayer, not to change God's mind, but to transform ourselves and enlarge our soul's capacity for spirit receptivity.

name: unknown
description: That which is unfamiliar or undiscovered, often causing fear in mortals who tend to crystallize science and dogmatize truth due to mental laziness in adjusting to progressive living.

name: unpervaded space
description: Space regions that are not infiltrated by the space presence of the Unqualified Absolute, contrasting with pervaded space that contains this cosmic overcontrol.

name: Unqualified Absolute
description: One of the three Absolutes of Infinity, being nonpersonal, extradivine, and undeified. This positive reality pervades all space and serves as the repository of uncreated universes while functioning as the cosmic overcontrol of physical force and energy throughout the master universe.

name: Unqualified Supervisors of the Supreme
description: Advisory personalities who represent God the Supreme and are destined to become high administrative bodies in superuniverses settled in light and life. They are the personalization of time-space supremacy and can contact directly with absonite administrators.

name: unreality
description: The philosophical concept of unreality, associated with illusions and false perceptions of existence.

name: unwritten law
description: Informal customs, traditions, or rules that operate without formal legal codification, sometimes associated with vigilante justice.

name: Upanishads
description: Later Hindu scriptures compiled along with the Brahmanas, representing the Brahmanic priesthood's effort to find true reality by deanthropomorphizing the Indian concept of deity, but inadvertently depersonalizing God into a distant, metaphysical Absolute.

name: Ur
description: An ancient Mesopotamian city that prospered during flood times and became a pottery industry center about seven thousand years ago. The idea of God was best preserved at Ur of Chaldea during Palestine's dark days, where divine righteousness and salvation by faith were preached, influencing Abraham's family and later religious teachers like Zoroaster.

name: Ural mountains
description: A mountain range that began forming during the Carboniferous age along with the Vosges and Black Forest mountains.

name: Ural Straits
description: A waterway that connected the Arctic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea during the Ice Age, forming part of a larger network of water passages when much of Europe was submerged.

name: uranium
description: A heavy radioactive element that spontaneously disrupts due to the independence and free movement of its outermost group of electrons. The lead produced by uranium disintegration reveals the actual loss of energy in atomic breakup, indicating the existence of pre-electronic forms of matter.

name: Urantia
description: The name of our planet in the universe, designated as world number 606 in the Satania system of Norlatiadek constellation in the local universe of Nebadon. Urantia is an experimental decimal planet that has experienced unusual difficulties including planetary rebellion and Adamic default, but was honored by Michael's terminal bestowal as Jesus of Nazareth.

name: Urantia advisory council
description: An administrative body that provides counsel and guidance regarding planetary affairs on Urantia.

name: Urantia Brotherhood of Urantia Midwayers
description: An organization of the midway creatures serving on Urantia, functioning as intermediary beings between material and spiritual realms.

name: Urantia Papers
description: A collection of 196 revelatory papers constituting the most recent presentation of truth to the mortals of Urantia, authored by various celestial personalities and presented as a composite revelation from many beings rather than a single universe personality.

name: Uranus
description: One of three major planets originally from the Angona system that were captured by our solar system, with its retrograde motion proving it was once a satellite of the dark giant Angona before being gravitationally detached and incorporated into our solar family.

name: Uriah the Hittite
description: A Hittite soldier whose wife Bathsheba was taken by King David, leading to a series of political atrocities that culminated in Uriah's murder as David's corrupt political machine gained power in violation of Hebrew customs.

name: urine
description: Along with blood, one of the earliest medicines used by primitive peoples who believed these human secretions possessed magical properties that could drive out disease spirits when combined with foul-smelling remedies.

name: Urmia
description: An ancient Persian city on the western shores of Lake Urmia where Jesus stopped during his caravan journey to the Caspian Sea, delivering systematic lectures on the brotherhood of men at a unique school of religions founded by the wealthy merchant Cymboyton.

name: Usatia
description: The superuniverse chief of all Census Directors in Orvonton, serving as a magnificent totaling personality who coordinates reports from his subordinates stationed throughout the local universes rather than directly registering intelligent will.

name: usury
description: The practice of charging excessive interest on loans, which Jesus condemned as an unfair use of money-power to exploit struggling individuals in financial distress.

name: Uversa
description: The spiritual and administrative headquarters of the seventh superuniverse Orvonton, governing approximately one trillion inhabited worlds and serving as the capital from which celestial administrators oversee vast cosmic territories with glory and perfection surpassing all time-space creations.

name: Uzziah
description: Also known as Azariah, a sixteen-year-old who became king of Judah after his father Amaziah was assassinated by rural rebels, but during his reign the kingdom declined and was forced to pay tribute to the Assyrian kings.

name: vacations
description: Periods of rest and recreation, such as the three-day retreats Jesus organized for his apostles on Mount Sartaba to relieve emotional tension, and systematic vacation programs on advanced worlds where citizens take annual month-long breaks for travel and adult education.

name: vale of tears
description: A traditional religious concept describing earthly life as sorrowful and filled with suffering. Jesus rejected this pessimistic view, instead regarding the world as a sphere where immortal spirits are born and begin their eternal journey to Paradise.

name: values
description: Spiritual and moral realities that represent worth, meaning, and quality in existence. Values are primarily spiritual in nature, are retained by divine beings, and form the foundation of religious experience. All meaningful values in will creatures are certain of survival, regardless of the survival of the personality that discovers them.

name: Van (lake)
description: A lake referenced in The Urantia Book, likely corresponding to the geographical Lake Van in eastern Turkey.

name: Van (Prince’s staff)
description: A loyal member of Caligastia's staff who served as chairman of the supreme council of coordination. Van remained steadfast during the Caligastia rebellion, leading the loyal forces for over 150,000 years until Adam and Eve's arrival, demonstrating exceptional spiritual insight and unwavering dedication to the universe government.

name: vanadium
description: A metallic element used by sea squirts to oxygenate their blood, demonstrating how evolving organisms can adapt their biological functions using different materials to serve the same purpose as iron does in higher animals.

name: Vanites
description: A group descended from Nodites and Amadonites who forsook the leadership of Nod to follow Van and Amadon. They emerged as a distinct people prior to the Bablot conflict in the northern regions.

name: vanity
description: An early emotional drive that contributed to human socialization and the formation of societies. While vanity helped bring people together and motivated achievements, excessive vanity threatens civilization by replacing legitimate social aims with selfish gratification.

name: variety
description: An essential element for beauty, natural selection, and balanced living. Variety enables the unification of contrasts that creates cosmic beauty, provides opportunities for evolutionary advancement, and prevents the exhaustion that comes from monotony.

name: Vedas
description: Ancient Hindu sacred texts and scriptures that form part of the religious tradition of India.

name: Vedic priesthood
description: The priestly class associated with the Vedic religious traditions of ancient India, connected to the development of Brahmanism.

name: vegetables
description: Plant foods including herbs and vegetables that became part of human diet, first consumed by Adam and Eve on their way to Mesopotamia after leaving the first garden.

name: vegetarianism
description: A nonflesh diet practiced by Adam, Eve, and their first generation children, who subsisted wholly on fruits, cereals, and nuts. This dietary practice originated with the Caligastia 100 and spread to influence many surrounding tribes, though most descendants eventually became omnivorous.

name: veils
description: Coverings worn by women to protect them from the evil eye, a widespread superstitious fear that led to the practice of veiling pretty women and eventually became adopted by many women who desired to be considered beautiful.

name: velocities
description: Various speeds achieved by different classes of beings in universe travel, ranging from 186,280 miles per second for basic energy lines to nearly 559,000 miles per second for seraphic transports, with instantaneous travel possible for certain gravity-traversing spirit personalities.

name: Veluntia
description: A location with a massive sun that is one of the largest in the superuniverse of Orvonton, having a density only one one-thousandth that of Urantia's atmosphere.

name: venom
description: Snake venom was one of many substances used by ancient peoples in creating magical charms, along with human flesh, tiger claws, crocodile teeth, and other materials believed to possess supernatural powers.

name: ventriloquism
description: A technique first used by shrewd shamans and priests to mystify and impress tribesmen, making them believe they were witnessing supernatural phenomena through this vocal deception.

name: Venus (god)
description: The Roman goddess of love, originally the Greek goddess Aphrodite who was transplanted to Rome as Venus when Roman religion was greatly influenced by extensive cultural importations from Greece.

name: Venus (planet)
description: A planet in the solar system with a thick atmosphere that would classify any potential inhabitants as superbreathers. Venus exhibits retrograde motion, proving it was originally a satellite of the visiting system Angona.

name: verdict
description: The eternal judgment pronounced by the Supreme Judge that declares "Well done, good and faithful servant" based not on material successes but on faithful performance of divine duty.

name: Vergil
description: Vergil was a Roman poet whose Stoic appeal to nature and conscience helped prepare Rome intellectually to receive Christianity and the laws of God.

name: Veronica of Caesarea Philippi
description: Veronica of Caesarea Philippi was a woman who suffered from a hemorrhage for years and was healed when she touched the hem of Jesus' garment while he passed through a crowd. Jesus clarified that it was her faith, not the physical touch, that made her whole.

name: vertebrates
description: Animals with backbones or spinal columns, representing a major classification of life forms.

name: Vesta
description: Vesta was the Roman goddess of the home and hearth, derived from the Greek goddess Hestia as part of the cultural exchange between Greece and Rome.

name: veto
description: A power of rejection or prohibition. The Universal Father wields an all-powerful veto over creation's destiny, while in settled systems the Constellation Fathers hold veto power over system conclaves, and on Urantia the Most High observer has veto authority over planetary government.

name: Vevona
description: Vevona is chief of the souls of peace, a type of seraphim who remained on Urantia from Adam's day and later heralded Jesus' birth to the worlds as leader of the angelic host.

name: Vicegerington
description: Vicegerington is one of the sacred spheres of Paradise, known as the "bosom of the Father and the Son," where beings who originate from acts of the Father and Son reside. It is the secret sphere containing the mysteries of trinitization and serves as home to glorified beings of complex ancestry and creature-trinitized sons held in reserve for future universe ages.

name: vices
description: Moral failings or bad habits. Jesus taught that virtues carried to extremes can become vices, and he focused on teaching positive virtue rather than condemning social vices or moral delinquency.

name: vicissitudes
description: The natural changes, variations, and ups and downs that occur in life and experience.

name: victory
description: Ultimate triumph achieved through faith and divine partnership. True victory comes through sacrifice of pride and selfishness, maintaining trust in divine power despite apparent obstacles, and recognizing that the indwelling spirit can overcome human limitations.

name: viewpoints
description: Perspectives or ways of looking at and understanding various subjects or situations.

name: Vilton
description: Vilton is a local system recorder, tertiary Lanonandek number 374 and secretary of the Lanonandek ministry of Satania, who was a member of the original Lanaforge group.

name: vine and branches
aliases: the vine, and you are the branches
description: The parable of the vine and branches teaches that believers must abide in Jesus to bear much spiritual fruit and experience the supreme joy of spiritual harvest. Jesus declares himself the true vine with believers as the living branches who can only be fruitful through maintaining their spiritual connection with him.

name: vinegar
description: Jesus' final mortal request was for vinegar, a sour wine commonly used in those days, to quench his thirst during crucifixion.

name: vineyard
description: The parable of laborers in the vineyard teaches that the kingdom of heaven is characterized by divine fairness, generosity, and mercy, where God's grace is given equally regardless of when one enters his service.

name: violence
description: Violence is described as the natural law of evolving man and the automatic reaction of primitive humanity. While society secures freedom from violence through police regulation and legal systems, true religion opposes violence as a technique of social evolution, promoting nonviolence and peaceful development instead.

name: violet race
description: A racial designation associated with the Adamites and their descendants.

name: vipers
description: John the Baptist accurately referred to hypocritical religious leaders as the offspring of vipers, condemning their evil actions and character.

name: virgin birth doctrine
description: The belief that divine or heroic figures are born of virgins, which became intertwined with Christian legends about Jesus' birth through influence from mystery religions like Mithraism. However, Jesus was conceived and born naturally like all other babies, with the mystery being the incarnation of the divine Son of Paradise within him.

name: virgins
description: In ancient cultures, virgins held special significance in religious practices, often serving as temple attendants tending sacred fires. Societal attitudes toward virginity evolved from early times when bearing children before marriage increased a woman's desirability, to later periods where chastity became highly valued and commercially important in marriage arrangements.

name: virtue
description: Virtue is the consistent choosing of good over evil through the daily exercise of moral will, representing righteousness and conformity with cosmic purpose. It requires a sense of proportion in distinguishing between good and evil, and is influenced by moral nature, wisdom, and spiritual insight. Supreme virtue is wholeheartedly choosing to do the Father's will in heaven.

name: viruses
description: Most disease-causing bacteria and their auxiliary viruses are renegade parasitic fungi that represent a retrograde movement in evolution.

name: Vishnu
description: Vishnu is one of the three primary deities in the Hindu trinitarian concept, alongside Brahma and Siva. He is extremely popular due to the belief that he periodically incarnates in human form, making him real and living in the imaginations of Indians. Hinduism claims both Buddha and Christ as incarnations of Vishnu, demonstrating its tolerant adaptability to other religions.

name: vision (sight)
description: Vision ranges from material sight limited to physical objects to extended morontia and spirit vision capable of perceiving both material and spiritual realities. Celestial beings with exclusive spirit vision cannot see material buildings but can discern spirit counterparts, while ascending mortals gain progressively expanded vision ranges as they advance through morontia and spirit levels.

name: visions
description: Visions should be carefully evaluated as potential divine communications, with genuine spiritual visions distinguished from mystic trances, dreams, or emotional experiences. True prophetic vision occurs at a superconscious level of contact with the indwelling spirit entity, while cultivating trancelike states as religious practice can become dangerous reality avoidance.

name: visiting
description: Evangelists should avoid excessive social visiting and trivial activities that could detract from wholehearted devotion to preaching the gospel and lead to loss of time.

name: vitamins
description: Essential vitamins are produced through the health-giving ultraviolet rays from sunlight that reach Earth's surface, protected by the ozone layer which filters out dangerous radiation while allowing beneficial rays to pass through.

name: vocabulary
description: True character development is not achieved merely through expanding vocabulary, but rather through genuine understanding of values and ideals, along with personal growth in religious experience and spiritual progress.

name: vocation builders
description: Vocation builders are celestial artisans who design and assemble the abodes and workshops of spirit and morontia workers, contributing to both better working environments and the vocational enhancement of the workers themselves.

name: voices
description: Jesus possessed a musical, fascinating, and authoritative voice that captivated all who heard it. In his religious life, he achieved communion with God through intelligent prayer and sincere worship rather than relying on supernatural voices, visions, or extraordinary religious practices.

name: Voices of Angelic Hosts
aliases: The Voice of the Angelic Hosts
description: The primary seconaphim, representing the voices of the angelic hosts, provide insightful counsel to the Ancients of Days.

name: Voices of Conjoint Actor
aliases: The Voice of the Conjoint Actor
description: The primary seconaphim who serve as the personal representatives of the Infinite Spirit on the superuniverse headquarters, being the nearest equivalent to a specialized personalization of the Third Source and Center.

name: Voices of Creator Sons
aliases: The Voice of the Creator Sons
description: Primary seconaphim who possess the remarkable gift of reflecting the minds of the Creator Sons, allowing the Ancients of Days to perceive their voice unfailingly and thus know the attitude of Michael regarding any matter under consideration.

name: Voices of Mercy
description: Superior seraphim who ensure that unerring justice is delivered with generous mercy, often appearing before referee trios and serving as mercy ministers who foster higher impulses and holier emotions of men and angels.

name: Voices of Seven Master Spirits
aliases: The Voice of the Seven Master Spirits
description: Primary seconaphim who represent the collective natures and reactions of the Seven Master Spirits before the Ancients of Days, providing collective rather than individual representation.

name: voices of the Garden
description: The personal seraphim of Adam and Eve, known as the voices of the Garden, who serve as planetary helpers in the evolution of human races and accompany Planetary Adams on their world missions.

name: Voices of Wisdom
description: Seconaphim in perpetual liaison with the living libraries of Paradise who transmit the essence of wisdom to the Perfectors of Wisdom, serving as living concentrations of the coordinated wisdom of the universe of universes.

name: volcanoes
description: Geological formations that played a crucial role in Earth's early development, equalizing internal heat pressure and crustal contraction while bringing heavy metals to the surface through lava flows and contributing to the planet's stabilization.

name: Volga
description: A river region through which the earlier and purer strains of Andites migrated to northwestern Europe via the northern route through the grazing lands.

name: volunteers
description: Individuals who offer their services freely without compensation, including the volunteer laborers who prepared the Garden of Eden and seraphim who volunteer for assignments as destiny guardians based on human needs and angelic qualifications.

name: Volvox
description: Early single-celled animal types that evolved into communities, serving as a foundational step in the development of more complex marine creatures like Hydra and jellyfish.

name: voodoo doctors
description: Twentieth-century practitioners of magic who are typical of ancient magicians, representing the continuation of primitive magical practices involving mystery rather than medical treatment.

name: Vorondadek Sons
description: Local universe Sons of God created by the Creator Son and Creative Spirit, known as Constellation Fathers when commissioned as Most Highs to rule constellation governments. They serve as reliable administrators, historians of local universes, and observers on quarantined worlds, with exactly one million existing in Nebadon.

name: Vorondadek worlds
description: Seven planets in the circuit of seventy primary spheres surrounding Salvington, each with six satellites, where ascending mortals receive the highest level of education in universe legislation under the guidance of senior Vorondadeks.

name: Vosges mountains
description: Mountain range that began forming during the Devonian epoch, along with the Black Forest and Ural mountains, as part of the geological development of Europe.

name: voting
description: Democratic process referenced in the context of political sovereignty and governance among nation-states.

name: vows
description: Sacred pledges made to gods that evolved from self-denial practices into contractual arrangements, often involving self-torture or sacrifice, with Moses later establishing a ransom system to help people escape the worst consequences of rash promises.

name: wakes for dead
description: Funeral rituals continuing ancient ghost cult customs, featuring noisy ceremonies and foul-smelling concoctions believed to drive away disturbing spirits from the deceased.

name: Wales
description: A nation that, along with England and Scotland, engaged in constant warfare until they surrendered their individual sovereignties to form the United Kingdom, illustrating how political unification can end conflict.

name: walls
description: Protective barriers built around ancient cities to defend against ferocious beasts and surprise attacks, enabling human progress in dwelling construction and animal domestication.

name: walnuts
description: Walnuts were among the many present-day trees that first appeared during a period of great plant-life evolution, second in importance only to the appearance of man himself.

name: wampum belts
description: Wampum belts were one of the developmental stages in the evolution of writing systems, progressing from message sticks and knotted cords through picture writing and hieroglyphics to early symbolic alphabets.

name: wands
description: Wands were ritual implements used by primitive magicians in their practice of magic, typically employed by unclothed practitioners, with women outnumbering men among ancient magicians.

name: war
description: War is the natural state and heritage of evolving man, serving as a kingmaker throughout history. While war has had evolutionary value in promoting organization, efficiency, and cultural exchange, it becomes increasingly destructive as civilization advances and must eventually be abandoned in favor of peaceful solutions to international conflicts.

name: warnings
description: Warnings from divine sources, while well-intentioned, often prove ineffective when dealing with embittered human nature or those whose love has died. Adam and Eve were warned of consequences for departing from the divine plan, and Lucifer and Caligastia received loving warnings about their pride, but these were misunderstood as interference with personal liberty.

name: washing
description: Jesus washed the apostles' feet as a demonstration of true service and greatness, and practiced washing only for cleanliness rather than observing the ceremonial hand-washing rituals favored by the Pharisees.

name: wasps’ nests
description: Wasps' nests provided crude paperlike writing material for early peoples under Fad's educational system, who also used tree barks, clay tablets, stone slabs, and hammered hide parchment for recording information.

name: watchful householder
description: The parable of the watchful householder teaches vigilance for the sudden and unexpected departure of the Son of Man, comparing spiritual preparedness to a homeowner guarding against thieves.

name: watchful servant
description: The parable of the watchful servant teaches disciples to remain vigilant and prepared for trials ahead, like servants keeping their lamps burning while waiting for their master's return from a feast.

name: watchtower
description: A watchtower represents the importance of counting the cost before undertaking discipleship, as one would calculate expenses before building such a structure to avoid mockery from neighbors for leaving it unfinished.

name: water
description: Water is a fundamental substance present on all evolutionary planets that are not too small, playing a crucial role in shaping their physical aspects. The energy contained within one drop of water equals 100 horsepower for two years due to the vast number of atoms it contains. Understanding water's unique properties should have prevented materialistic philosophy, as the union of hydrogen and oxygen creates something qualitatively different from the predictable sum of its parts.

name: water gate
description: A gate in the Jerusalem temple through which the sacred procession entered after collecting symbolic water from the pool of Siloam during the Feast of Tabernacles. On the last day of the feast, priests carrying golden pitchers of water would enter through the water gate to perform the ritual pouring at the altar.

name: water hogs
description: Large mammals that migrated from South America to North America during the last glaciation, replacing extinct species such as horses, tapirs, llamas, and saber-toothed tigers. They arrived along with sloths and armadillos as part of the faunal exchange between the continents.

name: water of life
description: A spiritual metaphor used by Jesus to describe the living Spirit of Truth that quenches spiritual thirst and brings eternal life. Jesus declared himself to be the living water that springs up to everlasting life in believers, representing divine sustenance that prevents spiritual discontent and provides lasting satisfaction.

name: Waters of Merom
description: A body of water in northern Palestine near Dan, passed by Jesus and the apostles during their travels through Galilee. The location served as a geographical landmark on their journeys between Caesarea-Philippi and other destinations in the region.

name: watersnake
description: A serpentine creature mentioned by Jesus in a parable about prayer and divine wisdom. He asked rhetorically whether a father would give his child a watersnake if the child foolishly requested one, illustrating how the heavenly Father gives appropriate gifts according to divine wisdom rather than unwise petitions.

name: wave phenomena
description: Physical manifestations involving wave motion and energy transmission, likely referring to various forms of electromagnetic radiation and other wave-based energy patterns in the universe.

name: wax board bond
description: A type of debt record written on wax tablets used for commercial transactions in ancient times. In Jesus' parable of the unfaithful steward, these bonds documented the amounts owed by the master's debtors and could be altered to reduce the recorded debts.

name: way
description: A spiritual path or method of living, often referring to God's way versus human ways. Jesus declared himself to be 'the way, the truth, and the life,' establishing a new and living way for mortals to find the Father and achieve spiritual growth. The concept encompasses both the path of divine will and the means by which creatures can ascend from humanity to divinity.

name: Way of Heaven
description: A Confucian concept teaching that earthly morality reflects the eternal cosmic order. According to Confucius, temporal civilization mirrors the heavenly pattern, with earthly ways being distorted shadows of the divine order in the cosmos.

name: weak
description: Those lacking strength, courage, or resources, often contrasted with the strong in social and spiritual contexts. According to Jesus, the weak should not be dismissed, as his gospel was not intended only for weaklings, and God gives power to the faint while showing divine mercy and tenderness toward them.

name: wealth
description: Material riches and possessions that can be accumulated through various means including inheritance, trade, genius, or labor. Jesus taught that wealth itself is not sinful, but the love of riches can become spiritually dangerous, emphasizing that material prosperity should be held as a trust and used for the benefit of others.

name: weaning
description: The process of transitioning infants from breast milk to other foods. Early weaning was facilitated by cooking food and the availability of animal milk, which reduced infant mortality and allowed mothers to bear more children, with some ancient practices involving weaning at one year versus the traditional four to five years.

name: weapons
description: Tools of warfare and combat, including metal implements that represented technological advancement. The term "seventy-seven" originated from Lamech's boasting about his son Tubal-Cain's superior metal weapons, and weapon-making was traditionally assigned to older men and those unable to perform other labor.

name: weasels
description: Small carnivorous mammals that evolved from an extinct ancestor resembling a cross between a cat and a seal. Weasels, along with martens, otters, and raccoons, thrived and developed throughout the northern latitudes as part of the diversifying mammalian fauna.

name: weaving
description: The craft of creating textiles by interlacing threads or fibers. This skill was taught by the Caligastia one hundred and practiced in advanced civilizations including Eden, with Jesus becoming an expert weaver by age ten and skilled Andite migrants bringing weaving expertise to various regions.

name: weddings
description: Marriage ceremonies that evolved from simple community affairs to elaborate celebrations involving ritual, magic, and public witness. Ancient weddings included various customs for ensuring fertility and warding off evil spirits, with Jesus attending several weddings including the famous one at Cana where he performed his first miracle.

name: Wednesday
description: The fourth day of the week, traditionally used for Jewish wedding celebrations and designated by Jesus as a mid-week holiday for rest and recreation for his apostles. This weekly day of relaxation allowed time for reflection and spiritual growth away from regular ministry duties.

name: weeds
description: Unwanted plants that grow among crops, used by Jesus in a parable to illustrate how good and evil coexist in the kingdom of heaven. In the parable, weeds sown by an enemy among wheat represent how righteousness and wickedness must grow together until the final harvest and judgment.

name: week
description: A seven-day period of time measurement introduced by the Dalamatia teachers, derived from one-fourth of the 28-day lunar month. The weekly cycle gained sacred significance when Adam and Eve spent six days surveying the Garden of Eden, establishing the tradition of the seventh day for worship.

name: weights
description: The first weights were grains of wheat and other cereals, while the first medium of exchange was a fish or a goat, later transitioning to the cow as a unit of barter. On Jerusem, one gradant equals almost exactly ten Urantia ounces. The weight disparity between subatomic particles is immense, with an electron weighing over two thousand times less than a proton despite being almost as small.

name: welfare (government)
description: Government-administered welfare programs designed to provide social support and assistance to citizens in need.

name: well-doing
description: The practice of doing good works and maintaining righteous conduct. Jesus encouraged his followers to be not weary in well-doing, emphasizing perseverance in service to others and faithfulness to spiritual duties even in difficult circumstances.

name: wells
description: Water sources that were among the first private possessions, initially guarded by fetish practices before laws evolved to protect belongings. The Caligastia 100 taught well digging to promote material welfare and human advancement. Jesus met the Samaritan woman Nalda at Jacob's well, where he offered her living water.

name: werewolves
description: Ancient beliefs in souls entering animals culminated in the werewolf concept of animal identification, allowing a person to transform into a wolf or other creature for nocturnal exploits.

name: West Indies
description: A region where high elevations of land rose during various geological periods, with the entire Caribbean region being highly elevated. The West Indies contains some of the mid-age mountains and chalk deposits from ancient periods.

name: Western civilization
description: Christianity suffers as part of the guilt-ridden society of Western civilization, which staggers under the burden of tolerating science without idealism, politics without principles, wealth without work, and industry without morality.

name: Western Ghats
description: A mountain range in India where Dravidian settlements along the seacoast maintained maritime relationships with Sumeria, owing their prominence to these sea-based trade connections.

name: whales
description: Marine mammals that evolved from placental mammals that returned to the sea. These creatures, along with dolphins, porpoises, seals, and sea lions, represent a whole tribe of mammals that deserted the land to take up permanent residence in the oceans.

name: wheat
description: A grain crop that served as one of the first weights in ancient commerce and was cultivated by Adam's descendants in Mesopotamia. Jesus used wheat in parables to teach about spiritual growth, sacrifice, and the coexistence of good and evil until final judgment. The apostles' act of rubbing wheat on the Sabbath sparked controversy about religious law observance.

name: wheels
description: Prince's staff first taught the use of the wheel for facilitating traction, and by 5000 B.C., wheeled vehicles had become common in spreading cultural civilization through trade channels.

name: whirlwind
description: While ancient scriptures spoke of God revealing himself in the whirlwind, The Urantia Book teaches that the Father does not reveal himself in such dramatic ways, but rather speaks within the human heart as a still, small voice.

name: white race
description: The modern white races are descendants of the blue man as modified by mixture with yellow and red peoples, and later greatly enhanced by assimilating much of the violet Adamic race. These races are predominantly descended from ancient blue men but admixed with all other races, with no pure racial divisions remaining in the modern world.

name: white smiths
description: Skilled craftsmen who worked with metals other than iron, in contrast to blacksmiths who worked primarily with iron and steel.

name: whole
description: The unified totality or complete entity, often contrasted with its constituent parts in discussions of cosmic relationships and universal organization.

name: wickedness
description: Deliberate evil behavior or moral corruption that violates divine principles and spiritual values.

name: widows
description: Women who had lost their husbands faced varied circumstances throughout history, from ancient customs requiring suicide on husbands' graves to social restrictions and poverty. Jesus showed compassion to widows, raising the son of the widow of Nain and commending the poor widow's sacrificial offering in the temple.

name: wilderness of Judea
description: The region where John the Baptist tended sheep along tributaries leading to the Dead Sea at Engedi, home to Nazarites and ascetic herdsmen who supported themselves through sheep raising and gifts from wealthy Jews.

name: will
description: The faculty of conscious choice and decision-making that characterizes personality, ranging from mortal free will to the absolute volition of the Universal Father.

name: will of God
description: The divine purpose and plan of the Universal Father, which mortals are called to discover and follow. Doing God's will involves progressive spiritual communion, choosing the highest values, and seeking to become perfect as the Father is perfect. It represents the way of God and partnership with divine choice in all circumstances.

name: wind
description: A natural phenomenon that ancient peoples believed was produced by the wings of birds, leading them to fear and worship winged creatures. Different cultures regarded wind as either beneficial or malignant, with some regions viewing it as divine while others saw it as demonic. Jesus used wind as a metaphor for those born of the spirit, whose effects are visible though the spirit itself remains unseen.

name: wine
description: A fermented beverage that held deep religious significance in various cultures and ceremonies. Jesus transformed it into a symbol of divine ministry when he blessed the cup at the Last Supper, declaring it the emblem of the new dispensation of grace and truth. Wine featured prominently in adoption ceremonies, healing practices, and religious sacraments, including Melchizedek's substitution of bread and wine for blood sacrifice, and Jesus's miraculous transformation of water into wine at Cana.

name: wings
description: The erroneous belief that angels possess wings originated from human observations of transport seraphim being prepared for interplanetary travel. What appeared to be wings were actually energy insulators or friction shields that extended from head to foot, serving as protective barriers during transit through universe energy currents.

name: Wisconsin
description: A North American state notable for containing ancient fossil-bearing stone layers that showcase Earth's geological history. The St. Croix valley of Wisconsin graphically displays one hundred and twenty-seven successive lava flows with water submergence and rock deposition, with about sixty-five to seventy stratified records of past ages currently exposed to view.

name: wisdom
description: The highest achievement of intellectual and spiritual development, representing the coordination of knowledge, experience, and divine insight. True wisdom encompasses the consciousness of meaning elevated by cosmic mind and activated by the adjutant spirit of wisdom. It progresses through ascending levels from knowledge of things to God-consciousness, ultimately leading to worship and spiritual understanding.

name: Wisdom, Book of
description: An ancient wisdom text by Amenemope that significantly influenced Hebrew scripture, with chapters from Proverbs taken almost verbatim from this work. The book taught that earthly riches are fleeting and emphasized turning from human words to divine acts, with the philosophy that while humans plan, God ultimately decides outcomes.

name: Wisdom of Solomon
description: A Hellenistic Jewish text that exemplified the influence of both Platonic philosophy and Stoic doctrines on Jewish religious thought. The work reflects themes of fate and chance governing human affairs, including the famous passage that 'the race is not to the swift' but to those whom fate and chance favor.

name: wise men (Magi)
description: Chaldean priests from Mesopotamia, led by Ardnon, who were informed by seraphim of Michael's birth and traveled to Bethlehem to find the infant Jesus. These wise men, seeking the prophesied 'light of life,' found the three-week-old babe and left gifts with Mary. Their visit was the only supernatural event associated with Jesus's birth, though later legend created the appealing myth of the star of Bethlehem.

name: wish fulfillment
description: The tendency to interpret events and teachings according to one's intense desires rather than objective reality. The apostles exemplified this when they interpreted Jesus's promise to return as meaning he would establish an earthly kingdom after a brief visit with the Father. True moral values and wisdom cannot grow from such wishful thinking but require honest acknowledgment of facts and adjustment to ideals.

name: witchcraft
description: The practice of magic performed by irregular, unrecognized spirits, distinguished from shamancraft which involved miracles by recognized tribal gods. Ancient peoples attributed many deaths and illnesses to witchcraft, leading to the execution of accused witches. Practitioners included sorcerers, wizards, and witches who were believed to dispense private magic for personal gain, while shamans performed public magic for tribal welfare.

name: withered hand
description: The withered hand refers to a physical condition that was healed by Jesus on the Sabbath day, demonstrating the importance of doing good to others.

name: witnesses
description: Jewish law required at least two witnesses to agree before charges could be laid against a prisoner. This legal requirement prevented Judas from testifying against Jesus as a traitor, and the contradictory testimony of false witnesses disconcerted even the perjurers themselves.

name: wives
description: Throughout history, wives have been subject to more restrictive taboos than husbands, rooted in concepts of marital fidelity and the protection of lineage and inheritance. In primitive societies, a wife's value was based on her ability to work hard and bear children, and husbands could escape punishment for killing their wives if they had paid for them.

name: wizards
description: Wizards were practitioners of the black art along with sorcerers, magicians, witches, enchanters, necromancers, conjurers, and soothsayers. When practicing what was considered white art, these same individuals were known by titles such as priests, seers, shamans, or medicine men.

name: Wolvering
description: Wolvering is one of the seven nearest neighboring universes to Nebadon in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: wolves
description: Wolves were abundant throughout Europe 800,000 years ago and were actively hunted by the Neanderthalers. They belonged to the dog family along with foxes and thrived in northern latitudes during this period.

name: women
description: Women throughout history have been subject to more restrictive taboos and social limitations than men, often treated as property rather than equals. Jesus revolutionized women's status by recognizing them as spiritual equals, commissioning female evangelists, and teaching that in the kingdom of heaven there is no distinction between male and female as sons and daughters of God.

name: women’s corps
description: The women's corps was a group of female evangelists commissioned by Jesus to teach the gospel and minister to the sick. Originally consisting of ten women selected by Jesus, it later expanded to include twelve and eventually sixty-two women who worked alongside the male apostles and evangelists, with Mary Magdalene serving as their chief spokesman.

name: women’s gallery
description: The women's gallery was a separate section in Jewish synagogues and the Jerusalem temple where women were confined during religious services, reflecting the restrictive social customs that prevented women from participating alongside men in worship.

name: wood
description: Wood was an important material in ancient commerce and craftsmanship. Jesus, as a skilled carpenter and boatbuilder, revolutionized boatmaking techniques in Galilee with innovative wood-working methods, and the improved economic conditions of his family were reflected in their use of smooth white wooden boards for writing.

name: woolly mammoths
description: Large, prehistoric elephants covered in thick fur that roamed from Mexico to Canada during the ice age. They preferred open prairies and migrated with the advancing and retreating glaciers before becoming extinct in North America.

name: word of God
description: The divine expression of God's truth and nature, primarily manifested through the Eternal Son as the perfect Word of the Father. In spiritual context, it represents divine sustenance for the soul that can be discerned regardless of human origin or traditional religious records.

name: words
description: Verbal expressions or language used for communication.

name: workmanship
description: Jesus was a painstaking workman who took great care with his handiwork, focusing on the essentials of any undertaking rather than wasting time on trifles, and experiencing satisfaction in completing commendable work.

name: works
description: Self-righteous deeds or outward actions that cannot earn God's favor or salvation. Salvation is a free gift based on faith, not on works or blaming external influences for one's failings.

name: world (earth)
description: The planet Earth, also known as Urantia in cosmic terminology.

name: world government
description: A unified planetary governmental system.

name: world of the cross
description: Urantia's designation throughout the local universe due to the tragic crucifixion of Jesus, making it locally known as the world where a Creator Son met an unusually cruel death, unlike the more considerate reception given to bestowal Sons on most other worlds.

name: world of the Father
description: The silent seventh transitional culture world orbiting Jerusem, featuring a great temple of light where all system beings are welcomed as worshipers. It currently serves as headquarters for detention spheres housing Lucifer rebellion internees and is maintained by loyal Panoptians under Michael's guidance.

name: world of the Sons
description: The fifth transitional culture world orbiting Jerusem that serves as headquarters for divine Sons of all orders, including creature-trinitized sons, with seven surrounding worlds devoted to specific groupings of these divinely related sons.

name: world of the Spirit
description: Transitional culture world number 6 that serves as the system rendezvous for high personalities of the Infinite Spirit, with no representation of the Spirit present.

name: World War I
description: A major conflict that unfortunately re-established submerged political sovereignty of numerous small groups in Europe, hindering the normal progression of political sovereignty from smaller to larger organizations.

name: worlds
description: Celestial spheres and planetary bodies throughout the universe, including both inhabited and architectural worlds.

name: worm of the dust
description: A humble description of man's mortal nature and origin, contrasted with his divine destiny when indwelt by the Father's spirit.

name: worship
description: The highest privilege and first duty of all created intelligences, being the conscious and joyous recognition of the intimate personal relationships between Creators and creatures. Worship is the act of acknowledging divine truth and reality, ranging from primitive fear-based reverence to the supreme spiritual communion experienced by Paradise beings.

name: worship builders
description: Divine builders and celestial artisans who construct exquisite temples of worship in the spirit and morontia realms.

name: wounds
description: Physical injuries that were effectively treated in ancient times with oil and wine, a medical practice that lost its effectiveness when the secrets of healing remedies became known.

name: wrath
description: Divine or human anger, often associated with justice and judgment in religious contexts.

name: writing
description: The art of recording thoughts and language in permanent form, evolving from primitive methods like message sticks and picture writing to alphabetic systems. The Dalamatians developed the first alphabet of twenty-five characters, while modern writing originated from early trade records.

name: Wyoming
description: A western American state containing Morrison beds with abundant fresh-water fossils that reveal a past era of inland lakes during the Mesozoic period.

name: X rays
description: High-energy electromagnetic radiation produced when electrons are suddenly stopped, forming part of ten octaves of wave energy above visible light. X rays serve as great levelers of atomic existence within stars, enable light to escape from suns by penetrating space, and activate preatomic matter to become slightly gravity-responsive.

name: Xenophanes
description: Greek philosopher who taught the concept of one God, though his deity concept was too pantheistic to represent a personal Father to mortal humanity.

name: Yahweh
description: The evolving Hebrew concept of God that began as a tribal deity of Mount Horeb and gradually developed into the supreme concept of a universal Creator Father. This progressive transformation from a jealous, anthropomorphic god to a loving, merciful Father represents one of the greatest achievements in religious evolution, influencing nearly half the world's population.

name: Yahwehites
description: Southern Arabian tribes who believed in Yahweh and viewed land as an inalienable gift from God that could not be sold or mortgaged. Their conflict with the Baalites was primarily a socioeconomic clash over land ownership ideologies rather than religious differences.

name: yaks
description: Large, long-haired bovine animals domesticated by 5000 B.C. and used as beasts of burden alongside oxen, camels, and horses by ancient Mesopotamian, Turkestan, and Chinese farmers.

name: yang
description: One half of the Chinese philosophical concept of complementary forces in nature, representing the active, masculine, light principle that balances with yin.

name: Yangtze River
description: Major river in China where advanced Chinese culture and learning centers developed, with ancestors of the Chinese establishing strong cultural foundations along its banks by 20,000 years ago. Cultural union between Yangtze and Yellow River centers was achieved between 4000 and 500 B.C.

name: yawn
description: A natural bodily function that early ancestor worshipers feared because they believed malignant ghosts could enter their bodies through their open mouths during yawning.

name: yearning
description: A chronic attitude of longing that Jesus criticized in his followers, urging them instead to take the kingdom of heaven by spiritual assault rather than regretting the past, whining about the present, and vainly hoping for the future.

name: years
description: Units of time that represent but a moment in God's eternal perspective, as a thousand years in the Universal Father's sight are like yesterday when it is past.

name: yellow race
description: One of the six original Sangik races of humans on Urantia, characterized by their peaceful nature, agricultural settlements, and fraternal spirit. The yellow race consolidated control of central Asia around 15,000 B.C. and was the first Sangik people to abandon hunting for settled agriculture. They experienced spiritual leadership under Singlangton about 100,000 years ago, who proclaimed the worship of the "One Truth." The yellow race successfully drove the red race from Asia into North America through superior social organization and collective cooperation, though they fought the red race for over 200,000 years in an epic struggle. They largely avoided mixture with debased human stocks and received beneficial infusions of Andite blood, particularly in northern China, contributing to their advanced civilization along the Yellow River.

name: Yellow River
description: A major river in China where advanced Chinese settlements flourished, particularly along its upper valley near Kansu. About 15,000 years ago, Andites traversed the pass of Ti Tao and spread throughout the Yellow River valley, bringing superior blood strains to the Chinese settlements. The Yellow River centers of culture were consistently more progressive than the southern Yangtze settlements, achieving political reunification of the yellow race between 4000 and 500 B.C.

name: yin and yang
description: Chinese philosophical concepts representing the dual aspects of human nature - the soul and the spirit. The Chinese recognized these two factors within a human being, distinguishing between the material and spiritual components of personality, similar to how other cultures identified dual human nature through concepts like the Hindu atman or the Egyptian ka and ba.

name: Yoga
description: A spiritual practice that Gautama (Buddha) pursued futilely for six years before formulating Buddhism. After finding yoga ineffective for achieving enlightenment, Gautama developed his Buddhist philosophy and discouraged seeking salvation through physical affliction and personal pain.

name: yoke
description: Both a literal wooden implement that Jesus became expert at crafting as a young carpenter, and a spiritual metaphor used by Jesus to describe discipleship. Jesus invited followers to "take my yoke upon you and learn of me," promising that his spiritual yoke is easy and the burden light, offering rest and strength to those who willingly accept the responsibilities of the kingdom.

name: young man who was afraid
aliases: young man who was fearful and downcast
description: A fearful and downcast youth whom Jesus encountered in the mountains, struggling with feelings of helplessness and inferiority after losing his father at age twelve. Through Jesus' guidance and encouragement, this young man overcame his despair and later became a leader of Christians in Crete.

name: Zaccheus
description: The wealthy chief publican (tax collector) of Jericho who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus as he passed through the city. Jesus surprised Zaccheus by choosing to stay at his house, where he taught the gospel of the kingdom to Zaccheus and his family, declaring him a son of Abraham and bringing salvation to his household.

name: Zacharias
description: John the Baptist's father, a Jewish priest who was married to Elizabeth, a relative of Mary. Initially skeptical of Gabriel's announcement about their son's destiny, Zacharias became convinced through a dream and helped arrange for wise men to visit the infant Jesus in Bethlehem. He provided education for John and warned Joseph of Herod's massacre, enabling the holy family's escape to Egypt.

name: Zadoc
description: A direct descendant of King David who adopted Joseph's orphaned paternal ancestor six generations before Jesus' birth. This adoption linked Joseph to the Davidic lineage, accounting for why Joseph was considered to be of the "house of David" despite not being a direct blood descendant of the king.

name: Zaphon
description: A city in the Decapolis region visited during Jesus' ministry tours. It was included in both the four-week Decapolis mission of the twelve apostles and evangelists, and later in the three-month Perean mission of the seventy messengers that concluded Jesus' public ministry before his final journey to Jerusalem.

name: zeal
description: Intense enthusiasm and passionate devotion to a cause or belief.

name: Zealots
description: A strong Jewish nationalist party that advocated rebellion against Roman rule in Judea, opposing Roman taxes and seeking political revolution to escape Roman bondage. Unlike the Pharisees, the Zealots were not willing to await the coming of the Messiah and believed any methods were justified in their struggle for liberation.

name: Zebedee
description: A moderately well-to-do boatbuilder who owned shops on the lake near Capernaum and became Jesus' business partner and host. Zebedee was the only person entrusted with the secret of Jesus' mysterious Mediterranean journey, and his home served as headquarters for the kingdom during Jesus' ministry.

name: Zebedee, David
description: The youngest son of Zebedee and Salome, who loved Jesus as an elder brother and often went fishing with him.

name: Zebedee, James
description: The eldest son of Zebedee and Salome, who became one of Jesus' twelve apostles and was a fisherman by trade.

name: Zebedee, John
description: The son of Zebedee who managed Jesus' financial affairs and later became one of the twelve apostles.

name: Zebedee, Salome
description: Wife of Zebedee and relative of Annas, the former high priest, who loved Jesus as much as her own sons and whose daughters regarded him as their elder brother.

name: Zebulun
description: A village along the Nazareth-Mount Lebanon trail where Jesus and his followers stopped during their travels, holding meetings and providing instruction on the mutual obligations of the kingdom and the differences between personal religious experience and social religious obligations.

name: Zechariah
description: A prophet who was slain between the sanctuary and the altar, representing the historical pattern of rejecting and destroying heavenly messengers. Jesus referenced his prophecy about the Messiah coming humbly, riding upon an ass, as guidance for his entry into Jerusalem.

name: Zeus
description: The head of the Greek pantheon, a man-god imposed by the Greeks on the Mediterranean world that was previously dominated by mother cults. Zeus later became Jupiter in Roman religion through extensive cultural importation from Greece.

name: zinc
description: Zinc is a mineral found in ancient geological deposits alongside gas, oil, and lead, formed from organic material and sedimentation in sluggish bodies of water.

name: Zone of Infinity
description: The Zone of Infinity is a mysterious, unknown and unrevealed region located directly beneath the Trinity in the central portion of nether Paradise.

name: Zophar
description: Zophar was one of Job's three friends who offered uncomforting counsel, suggesting there might be hidden purpose in Job's miseries and reiterating melancholy advice during Job's trials.

name: Zoroaster
description: Zoroaster was an outstanding 6th century BC religious teacher in Iran who revived monotheistic teachings influenced by Salem missionaries, using flame as a symbol of the pure and wise Spirit rather than promoting fire worship.

name: Zoroastrianism
description: Zoroastrianism was a religion of action founded by Zoroaster in Persia, emphasizing work over rituals and centered on a god of supreme wisdom who battles evil, darkness, and inaction.

name: Zulus
description: The Zulus are an African people who, as of the twentieth century, were evolving a religion centered on ghost fear and sacrifice as part of their religious development.
